title: Install beads-plugin routes: ~/.beads/formulas + ~/.beads/scripts
description: Parity BLOCKER found in H.1 audit. PluginRoute.routes() (formulas -> ~/.beads/formulas/, scripts -> ~/.beads/scripts/ +x) has ZERO call sites in packages/installer; core/run.py iterates only tool adapters, never plugin routes. Bash install.sh stages+installs beads (formulas/, scripts/ chmod +x). Result: every Python install with the beads plugin silently drops all ~/.beads content. Fix: wire plugin-route staging+sync into the pipeline, mirroring bash. Acceptance: golden-master plugin-overlay scenario (--plugins=beads, bash vs python) reaches parity incl. ~/.beads/scripts/* executable.

title: writing-plans: add 'Workflow script' as third option in Subagent-Driven vs Inline menu
description: The writing-plans skill's 'Subagent-Driven vs Inline' decision menu predates the Workflow tool and should offer a third option: 'Workflow script' — for deterministic multi-agent orchestration (fan-out, pipelines, loops) that neither pure-inline nor ad-hoc subagent dispatch captures well.

title: Project-scoped installable content: detection and conditional staging
description: The installer handles user-space content only. There is no mechanism for project-level content — assets from agents-config that should be installed into a specific project workspace rather than (or in addition to) user space. Motivating cases: (1) a richer PRIME.md equivalent for projects that use beads — the agents-config version of .beads/PRIME.md should land in the project repo, not in ~/.beads/, when the installer detects a compatible project; (2) beads plugin files (currently targeted at user space in Epic F via plugins/beads.py) — open question whether some or all of these belong at project scope instead. The detection-and-prompt angle is the core design challenge: what signals a project as a candidate, what gets installed, and whether that happens silently, with a prompt, or requires an explicit opt-in command.
acceptance_criteria: Installer can detect at least one project-level condition (TBD by design) and stage project-specific content into the project directory. User is notified or prompted before any project-directory write. Mechanism is extensible to additional detection rules. Scope overlap with Epic F plugin system is resolved with a clear boundary decision.
notes: Open design questions: (1) Detection contract — .beads/ presence, project-config.toml fields, or both? (2) Trigger model — auto-silent, detect+prompt, or explicit opt-in flag? (3) Scope boundary vs Epic F plugins — is project-scoped content a plugin concern (plugin targets project dir) or a separate mechanism? (4) Collision model — does the Epic E matrix apply to project-scope paths, or does project-level always win?

title: Standardize wait-for-pr-comments helper script parameterization
description: All helper scripts under the wait-for-pr-comments and reply-and-resolve-pr-threads skills use inconsistent calling conventions: some use --owner/--repo/--pr flags (detect-pr-context.sh), others use positional <owner/repo> <pr-number> (poll-copilot-review.sh, poll-copilot-rereview-start.sh). This inconsistency causes failed calls and wasted discovery cycles every time the skill is exercised. Standardize all helpers to a single convention (recommend --owner/--repo/--pr flags for discoverability) and update SKILL.md to document the canonical invocation for each helper.
close_reason: Merged via PR #111 (squash 8674d4d). Full uniformity scope: all wait-for-pr-comments, reply-and-resolve-pr-threads, and merge-guard helpers converted to --flag convention. ujrmm folded in.

title: Sweep src/ and living docs for stale tracker-ID references
description:
Implements the new "No tracker IDs in code or living docs" constraint added to the shared INSTRUCTIONS template. Sweep the repository for embedded work-tracker IDs (bead IDs like 'cx6.7.12', 'bn9um', 'agents-config-<token>') and opaque section codes (e.g. 'CA-23') in source code and living technical documentation, replacing them with readable, navigation-friendly references (design doc name + section) or removing them entirely.

## Scope (must-sweep)
- src/** (skills, agents, rules, commands, templates) — comments, prose, provenance HTML comments
- docs/primers/ — knowledge base prose
- Repo-root and folder-level CLAUDE.md / AGENTS.md / CONTEXT.md
- ADRs and other living design artifacts

## Out of scope (exempt by the constraint)
- Commit messages and git history
- PR titles and bodies
- docs/plans/ and docs/specs/ files that are explicitly dated audit/historical artifacts
- Bead descriptions, bd notes, beads exports

## Known seed violations
- src/user/.agents/skills/bugfix/SKILL.md provenance header references the cx6.7.12 amalgamation lift and the bn9um lift inside the "Patterns lifted:" line. Either remove the tracker IDs or replace with a content-only description.
- Likely similar provenance comments in other amalgamated skills from the cx6.7.x lift cycle (verify-checklist, wait-for-pr-comments references) — grep audit required.

## Suggested approach
1. Build a regex pattern set for the most common ID shapes: 'agents-config-[a-z0-9.]+', '[a-z]{2,4}[0-9]+(\.[0-9]+)*', and any two-letter prefix that bead generates (cx, abn, hft, w1qls, wgclw, etc.).
2. Grep across in-scope directories.
3. Triage each hit: rewrite to content-only, replace with readable doc+section, or move to a commit/PR/dated-file location.
4. Document the regression-prevention grep pattern in the bead's notes for future PRs.
acceptance_criteria: Grep audit across src/** and living docs finds zero embedded tracker IDs matching the documented regex patterns. All replacements are either content-only descriptions or readable doc+section references. Dated audit/spec/plan files preserve their tracker references unchanged. Commit messages and PR metadata untouched. Regression-prevention grep pattern is documented in the bead's notes for future PRs.

title: Show stale in-progress beads and block status in whats-next
description:
Enhance whats-next skill and Python backend to surface in-progress beads that may be abandoned or mistakenly left open.

REQUIREMENTS:
1. Stale detection: Find in-progress beads untouched for 2+ days (use bd list filtering rather than bd stale for better control)
2. Display modes:
   - Default: Show ONLY stale in-progress beads with summary
   - Optional: Show full in-progress list when requested
3. For both stale and full in-progress lists, display:
   - All details shown in ready bead lists (title, priority, status, etc.)
   - PLUS: Blocking status — whether bead is directly or indirectly blocked, and BY WHICH bead(s)
4. Summary output:
   - Stale list: 'Here are the ones in progress but untouched in the last 2 days'
   - Full list header: 'There are n beads marked in progress, x showing recent activity in the last 2 days'

IMPLEMENTATION NOTES:
- Use bd list with in-progress filter + last-touched timestamp analysis (better than bd stale for visibility)
- Blocking chain traversal required to show indirect blocks
- Integration point: whats-next skill Python file

title: [Validate] Stress-test PR #72 epic-hygiene shipped behavior — prereq to y9mm + f19p
description:
Prerequisite validation bead for `agents-config-y9mm` (round-3+5 PR #72 hardening) and `agents-config-f19p` (Y-restructure to Y_container shape). Both downstream beads are P1 architectural completeness work; before tackling them, validate that the shipped PR #72 behavior actually works on the happy path. Discoveries from these tests hone the scope of y9mm and f19p (or surface new gaps).

## Background

PR #72 (merged as squash commit 40764ce on main) shipped:

- Container Beads section in the beads rule (milestone, epic, feature-with-active-children).
- whats-next 5-mode output (`all`, `brainstorm`, `implementation`, `planning`, `human`) with 7-column ancestry walk.
- Container gate at brainstorm-bead finalize via the new `bd-finalize-container-gate.sh` helper.
- implement-bead routing: milestone added to the epic → flag-human path.
- Container HEP shape: parent-child human bead for container sources (sidesteps bd's cross-type `blocks` epic wall).
- Narrowed feature-container definition: feature is a container only if it has open children that are NOT labeled `merge-gate` / `human`.
- One-time migration of 3 violators (abn9.8, 7bk.19, bt9e).

None of these have been exercised end-to-end on real beads in production. y9mm and f19p target known architectural gaps; THIS bead validates that the happy paths actually work.

## Out of scope

- `run-queue` skill behavior — deferred per AGENTS.md "Deferred Topics" section. Route any run-queue-related findings to `agents-config-7bk.11` via `bd comments add`. Do not attempt to test or harden run-queue here.
- `gekr` (display_ids perf) — Scott is tackling in parallel.
- Any work on the migration scope itself (`abn9.8` / `7bk.19` / `bt9e` already migrated; tested in scope by Group E only as a regression check).

## Discovery routing protocol

For findings produced by these tests:

1. **Trivial fixes** (single-line doc edit, typo, comment correction, single helper-script bug not requiring architectural change): fix in this bead's scope, commit alongside the test code.
2. **Non-trivial findings about existing y9mm clusters** (A/B/C/D/E or F): `bd comments add agents-config-y9mm "Validation bead <THIS-ID> Test <X>: <finding>"`. Do NOT add a new cluster; refine the existing one. y9mm description should remain stable; comments accumulate.
3. **Non-trivial findings about f19p**: `bd comments add agents-config-f19p "<finding>"`. Same protocol.
4. **New gaps not covered by y9mm or f19p**: file a new bead with `bd dep add <new-bead> <THIS-BEAD-ID> --type discovered-from`. Do NOT pile onto y9mm — split-by-thesis discipline (per PR #72 retrospective and AGENTS.md project-vision §3).

## Test design

See acceptance criteria. Six test groups (A–F). Each test marked `[m]` (mechanical / scripted assertion) or `[h]` (human verification / judgment call). All sacrificial beads MUST be cleaned up at the end of each group via `bd close`, `bd mol burn`, or equivalent. Sacrificial beads should carry a `stress-test-fixture` label for easy bulk cleanup if anything escapes.

Test code lives in `src/user/.agents/skills/epic-hygiene-tests/` (existing directory; this is where the round-1 fixtures already live). Each group is a separate test script, named `pr72_validate_<group>.sh`. A driver script `pr72_validate_all.sh` runs all groups, captures findings, emits the inventory required by Group F.

## Definition of Done

- All `[m]` assertions pass (or have routed findings per protocol §3).
- All `[h]` assertions human-signed.
- Findings inventory committed to this bead's notes via `bd update --append-notes`.
- Test scripts committed under `src/user/.agents/skills/epic-hygiene-tests/`.
- All sacrificial beads closed; `bd list --label stress-test-fixture --status open,in_progress --json` returns empty.
- This bead closed → unblocks y9mm + f19p.
acceptance_criteria:
GROUP A — whats-next modes (live output validation)
[m] A1. `whats-next --mode all` exits 0; stdout contains the four section markers for `human`, `planning-ready`, `brainstorm`, `implementation` (presence-only; section may be empty).
[m] A2. `whats-next --mode implementation` output contains zero rows where bead type ∈ {milestone, epic, decision}; zero rows where type = feature AND that feature has ≥1 open non-formula-gate (non-merge-gate / non-human-labeled) child.
[m] A3. `whats-next --mode brainstorm` output contains only beads without `brainstormed` label AND meeting brainstorm-ready criteria (per SKILL.md spec).
[m] A4. `whats-next --mode planning` output contains only container beads with zero active non-formula-gate children.
[m] A5. `whats-next --mode human` output contains only beads carrying `human` label.
[m] A6. Default mode (no `--mode` arg) renders the same 4-section aggregate as A1.
[m] A7. Empty-state messages render per mode — string match against SKILL.md spec for each of the 5 modes when their target section is empty.
[m] A8. 7-column schema verified per row: P | Milestone | Feature | Parent Epic | Bead ID | Type | Title.
[h] A9. Spot-check ancestry walk: pick 5 random rows from `--mode all`; manually verify Milestone / Feature / Parent-Epic columns match `bd show <id>` parent-chain walk.

GROUP B — Container gate at brainstorm-bead finalize
[m] B1. Create sacrificial epic E1 (no children, no produced-from labels); wisp brainstorm-bead; drive molecule to finalize step; helper script decision token = `handled`.
[m] B2. E1 ends `closed` with `epic-decomposed` label; does NOT carry `implementation-ready` / `brainstormed` / `implementation-readied-session-*`.
[m] B3. Repeat B1/B2 for milestone M1_test → same outcome (closed + epic-decomposed).
[m] B4. Repeat B1/B2 for feature F1_test with 1 open plain-labeled child → same outcome (closed + epic-decomposed).
[m] B5. Childless feature F2_test → helper decision = `not-container`; finalize proceeds normally; F2_test ends with `brainstormed` + `implementation-ready` (NOT `epic-decomposed`).
[m] B6. HEP path: epic E2_test with `produced-bead-X-nonexistent` label → helper decision = `handled` via HEP; child `human` bead exists under E2_test; E2_test status reverts to `open`; E2_test does NOT carry `human` label; step-bead (if any) does NOT carry `human` label.
[h] B7. Audit comments / decomposition state read sensibly for B1–B6 (reviewer signs off).

GROUP C — implement-bead routing on milestone / epic / non-container
[m] C1. Create sacrificial epic E3_test; invoke implement-bead direct dispatch; HEP fires; child `human` bead created under E3_test.
[m] C2. E3_test status = `open`; E3_test does NOT carry `human` label; no step-bead created carries `human`.
[m] C3. Single-bead-`human` invariant: bd label list on each of {E3_test, step-bead-if-any, escalation-child} shows `human` ONLY on the escalation child.
[m] C4. Container HEP shape verified: escalation child created via `bd create --parent E3_test --no-inherit-labels`; no `bd dep add E3_test <child-id>` call was made (cross-type call would have errored against bd's blocks epic wall).
[m] C5. Repeat C1–C4 for milestone M3_test → same outcomes.
[m] C6. Non-container regression: invoke implement-bead on a plain task/feature/chore source → normal flow proceeds, NO human child created, NO HEP transaction fires.

GROUP D — Narrowed feature-container logic
[m] D1. Feature F3_test with 1 plain-labeled child C1_test → `is_container(F3_test) == True`; `active_child_count == 1`; F3_test surfaces under `whats-next --mode planning`.
[m] D2. Feature F4_test with 1 `merge-gate`-labeled child M1_test → `is_container(F4_test) == False`; `active_child_count == 0`; F4_test surfaces under `whats-next --mode implementation` (assuming F4_test is dep-unblocked and ready).
[m] D3. Feature F5_test with 1 `human`-labeled child H1_test → `is_container(F5_test) == False`; `active_child_count == 0`; F5_test surfaces under `whats-next --mode implementation`.
[m] D4. Feature F6_test with mixed children {C1 plain, M1 merge-gate, H1 human} → `is_container(F6_test) == True`; `active_child_count == 1` (only C1 counts); F6_test surfaces under `whats-next --mode planning`.
[m] D5. Helper script `bd-finalize-container-gate.sh` agreement: invoking finalize-style probe on F3/F4/F5/F6 yields decisions `handled` / `not-container` / `not-container` / `handled` respectively.

GROUP E — Post-migration sanity (regression of PR #72 AC #8)
[m] E1. `bd list --type epic --status open,in_progress --limit 0 --json | jq '[.[] | select((.labels // []) | (index("implementation-ready") // false) or (any(.[]?; startswith("implementation-readied-session-"))))] | length'` returns 0.
[m] E2. Same query for milestone-type beads returns 0.

GROUP F — Cleanup + findings inventory
[m] F1. All sacrificial beads ({E1..E3, M1..M3, F1..F6, C1, M1_test, H1_test, …}) closed or burned; `bd list --label stress-test-fixture --status open,in_progress --json` returns empty array.
[m] F2. Findings inventory written to THIS bead's notes via `bd update --append-notes` — JSON-like list of `{test_id, status, finding_class, routed_to}` for every test (passes have `status: pass, finding_class: none, routed_to: none`).
[h] F3. Reviewer (human) signs off the findings inventory accurately reflects the test runs and that all non-trivial findings were routed per Discovery Routing Protocol.
notes:
Cleared: notes exceeded TEXT column limit after repeated inventory appends. Discovered-work beads: agents-config-nr2e (gate path doc), agents-config-6ms7 (collect.py shape spike), agents-config-7r77 (implement-bead routing test gap), agents-config-5ch8 (dep-health-check schema bug). [h] follow-ups: 3qf2.1 (A9), 3qf2.2 (B7), 3qf2.3 (F3). All 30 [m] assertions PASS.

title: [Impl] Epic hygiene: define and enforce rules for epic labeling, executableness, and whats-next visibility
description:
# Epic Hygiene: Container Bead Rules, Enforcement, and whats-next Redesign

Spec file: docs/superpowers/specs/2026-05-13-epic-hygiene-design.md

## Summary

Define and enforce three rules governing container beads (milestones, epics, and features-with-children) to eliminate noise in readiness determination, whats-next output, and ancestor-walk closure. Enforce at two surfaces (collect.py, brainstorm-bead finalize) and migrate three known violations.

## Container Bead Definition

A bead is a container if: type is milestone or epic (always), OR type is feature AND has >=1 open/in-progress children. Decision type is informational — excluded from all lists. Feature with 0 children is a leaf bead.

## The Three Rules

(A) No executable work — container AC must be pure closure check; verification work goes in leaf child.
(B) Never in whats-next ready lists — enforced structurally in collect.py regardless of labels.
(C) No readiness labels — implementation-ready, implementation-readied-session-*, brainstormed, human must not appear on containers.

## Enforcement Surfaces

1. collect.py: type-based container filter, new planning-ready list, memoized 3-typed ancestor extraction, 7-column output, default sections = human+planning-ready+brainstorm (not impl).
2. brainstorm-bead finalize Step 3: container gate; milestone/epic always decomposition-only; feature-with-children same; childless feature proceeds normally.
3. implement-bead SKILL.md: add milestone to existing epic->flag-human path.
4. beads.md prose: I1/I2 'ancestor epic' -> 'ancestor bead'.

## whats-next Redesign

Four lists (empty sections suppressed): Needs-attention | Planning-ready | Ready-to-brainstorm | Ready-to-implement (impl only on explicit ask). All use 7 columns: P | Milestone | Feature | Parent Epic | Bead ID | Type | Title.

Planning-ready = container types with 0 open/in-progress children AND no implementation-ready label, dep-unblocked.

## Migration (3 targeted fixes)

- abn9.8: strip brainstormed + implementation-ready; trim AC to pure closure
- 7bk.19: strip brainstormed + implementation-readied-session-*; move grep/smoke AC to 7bk.19.9
- bt9e: reclassify epic -> spike

## Discovered Work

- biy4 (P3): I2 close walk AC enforcement (deferred)

7. whats-next skill updated: renders 7-column table, handles 4 sections, default excludes impl-ready.
8. Post-migration verification: bd list --type epic --json query for open epics with implementation-ready or implementation-readied-session-* labels returns empty array.

title: [Impl] dep-health-check skill: find missing deps, verify existing deps, suggest/apply fixes across open bead tree
description:
# dep-health-check skill — spec (rev 4)

## Summary

A beads-plugin skill that audits the dependency graph across the open/in-progress bead tree. Two scopes (focused on one bead, or whole-tree sweep) × two modes (interactive confirmation, or `--just-fix-it` autonomous add-only). Hybrid architecture: a Python helper performs fast deterministic structural checks and returns a JSON inventory; the LLM body of the skill reads bead contents (description, notes, comments, labels, AC) and reasons semantically about missing or wrong dep edges, attaching a per-item rationale and confidence to every finding.

## Background / motivation

Dep edges are wired ad-hoc during brainstorming or by formula automation. Nothing systematically checks that:

- `produced-bead-*` / `produced-from-*` provenance labels are mirrored by actual `bd dep` edges.
- The dep graph is acyclic.
- Declared dep types are semantically correct.
- Beads that reference each other in prose ("hard-blocks on acmh.12") actually have those deps wired.
- Declared blocks are real and not just wrong.

Silent drift results: work stalls behind stale blockers, parallelisable work serializes by mistake, and `bd ready` surfaces beads whose deps do not reflect reality.

## Requirements

### R1 — Invocation surface

| Scope    | Trigger                              | Behavior                                                                   |
|----------|--------------------------------------|----------------------------------------------------------------------------|
| Focused  | `/dep-health-check <bead-id>`        | Target + 1-hop dep neighborhood (§R8) + full parent + full child chain     |
| All      | `/dep-health-check` (no arg)         | Every open + in_progress bead in the project                               |

| Mode         | Trigger         | Behavior                                                                                  |
|--------------|-----------------|-------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------|
| Interactive  | default         | Findings batched by class; user confirms per class or per-item (§R5)                     |
| Just-fix-it  | `--just-fix-it` | Auto-applies HIGH-confidence add-only findings (≤ 10 per run); rest in report             |

**Argv grammar:**

```
dep-health-check [bead-id] [--just-fix-it]
```

- `bead-id` — optional positional; any string not beginning with `--`; absent → all-mode.
- `--just-fix-it` — optional flag; may appear in any position.
- Unknown arguments → skill exits non-zero, emits usage to stderr, no state changes.

Slash command body:

```
Skill dep-health-check $ARGUMENTS
```

### R2 — Hybrid pipeline

**Pass 1 — Deterministic (Python helper `collect.py`, fast):**

- Inventory: every in-scope bead with id, title, labels, deps in both directions (dependent-side and blocker-side) with dep types, parent, status, and full text content (see token guard and `collect.py` field sourcing in §R7). `collect.py` MUST call `bd list --status open --json --limit 0` and `bd list --status in_progress --json --limit 0` upfront in **both** focused and all-mode (to capture labels, which are absent from `bd show --json`). This produces the bulk inventory; in focused mode, collect.py then filters to the scoped bead set. Per-bead `bd show` calls add the content fields (description, notes, AC, comments, dependents/deps_in) but do NOT provide labels.
- Provenance-mirror check: a `produced-bead-Y` label on X, or `produced-from-X` label on Y, expects the dep edge `bd dep add Y X --type discovered-from` (Y is the **dependent**; X is the **blocker/source**). Flag when either label exists without the matching dep edge in the correct direction and type.
- Cycle detection: shell out to `bd dep cycles`; emit each detected cycle verbatim.
- Semantic-type sanity — enforce only structurally derivable rules:
  - A `blocks` dep between two beads that also share a `parent-child` dep edge in any direction is a conflict; flag it.
  - A `discovered-from` edge exists in both directions between the same pair; flag it (should be uni-directional).
  - A `relates-to` edge exists in only one direction between a pair; note as informational (may be legitimate), not a hard error.
  - No structural sanity rules are enforced for `tracks`, `until`, `caused-by`, `validates`, `supersedes`, or `related` — surfaced in the inventory for the LLM pass but not flagged deterministically.
- Stale-blocker detection: scope strictly to `blocks`-type deps. An open bead whose every `blocks`-type blocker is closed → flag as a `bd dep remove` candidate (audit/cleanup; the dep is cruft even if `bd ready` already handles the bead as ready).

**Token guard:** `collect.py` sums the character lengths of all bead `content` fields. If total > 600 000 characters (~150 000 tokens), each bead's content is truncated to the first 4 000 characters and the bead entry is flagged `"truncated": true`. Summary reports truncated-bead count.

**Pass 2 — Semantic (skill LLM body, sonnet[1m], single-shot call):**

All in-scope beads are fed to the LLM in one call using the JSON inventory from Pass 1. The LLM:

- Infers **missing deps** with rationale; attaches a confidence level (§D4).
- Infers **false existing deps** for `blocks` or `discovered-from` type deps only; attaches rationale and confidence.
- Does NOT propose removes for `relates-to`, `tracks`, `until`, `caused-by`, `validates`, `supersedes`, or `related`.
- After the LLM returns its finding list, SKILL.md applies a mechanical post-processing dedup step: any LLM finding whose `(dependent_id, blocker_id, dep_type)` tuple already appears in a `findings[]` entry from Pass 1 is silently dropped from the LLM output before rendering. This is code, not a prompt instruction.

### R3 — Report shape

One structured report per invocation; empty sections are omitted:

- **Summary** — counts per finding class; truncated-bead count if any.
- **Cycles** *(deterministic)* — each cycle verbatim; HIGH-confidence LLM break-point suggestion if available.
- **Missing provenance edges** *(deterministic)* — mismatches with expected direction, type, and copy-paste `bd dep add` commands.
- **Missing semantic deps** *(LLM)* — grouped HIGH → MED → LOW; rationale + proposed `bd dep add` per item.
- **False existing deps — confirmed** *(LLM, HIGH only)* — rationale + proposed `bd dep remove` per item.
- **False existing deps — review queue** *(LLM, MED/LOW)* — surfaced in both modes; never auto-applied. (Distinct label prevents confusion with `--just-fix-it`'s "Judgment calls" section.)
- **Stale blockers** *(deterministic, `blocks`-type only)* — proposed `bd dep remove` per item.
- **Semantic-type conflicts** *(deterministic)* — specific conflict type; correction commands.
- **Applied changes** *(`--just-fix-it` only)* — every dep edge added; rationale; bead id that received the audit comment.
- **Judgment calls** *(`--just-fix-it` only)* — MED/LOW missing-dep findings not auto-applied; copy-paste commands.
- **Informational** — brainstormed-but-open beads; `relates-to` asymmetries; truncated beads.

### R4 — Destructive-action policy

`--just-fix-it` is **strictly add-only**:

- `bd dep add <dependent> <blocker>` is allowed when the finding is HIGH-confidence AND the operation is purely additive.
- Hard cap: **at most 10 dep edges applied per invocation**. If HIGH-confidence candidates exceed 10, apply the first 10 (sorted by dependent-bead id lexicographic order) and report the rest in Judgment calls with the note "not applied; re-run to continue."
- `bd dep remove`, `bd close`, `bd label remove`, and all bead-content mutations — **never** auto-applied; always in the report with copy-paste commands.
- `--just-fix-it` is an interactive-user primitive. If invoked from a non-interactive context, it still applies changes — the caller is responsible for reading the audit-comment trail. HEP integration is explicitly out of scope (§D3).

**Audit comment** — written on the **dependent bead** (the first arg to `bd dep add <dependent> <blocker>` — the bead whose dep list grew):

```bash
bd comments add <dependent-id> "dep-health-check (just-fix-it): added dep <dependent-id> -> <blocker-id> (type <type>); reason: <rationale>"
```

### R5 — Interactive-mode UX

Findings presented batched by class. Per class:

- Show all candidates with rationales and proposed commands.
- Prompt: `apply all` / `pick individually` / `skip class`.
- `pick individually` walks item-by-item.

Cycles and "False existing deps — confirmed" (HIGH) items are always one-at-a-time regardless of class size.

### R6 — Skill artifacts

- **Location**: `src/plugins/beads/.agents/skills/dep-health-check/`
- **`SKILL.md`**: orchestration + LLM semantic pass + report rendering + interactive UX + `--just-fix-it` apply loop.
- **`collect.py`**: Python helper; pure read; JSON to stdout; errors to stderr.
- **Slash command**: `src/user/.claude/commands/dep-health-check.md`
- **Name uniqueness**: before submitting the PR, verify no file named `dep-health-check` exists in any other skill, command, or agent directory under `src/` or `~/.claude/`. Exact command: `grep -r 'dep-health-check' src/ ~/.claude/skills/ ~/.claude/commands/ 2>/dev/null | grep -v 'src/plugins/beads/.agents/skills/dep-health-check' | grep -v 'src/user/.claude/commands/dep-health-check'` — must produce no output.

### R7 — `collect.py` field sourcing and JSON schema

**Field sourcing (important — `bd show --json` uses sparse fields):**

`bd show --json` only includes `description`, `parent`, `dependencies`, `dependents`, and `comments` when non-null/non-empty. `labels` is **never** present in `bd show --json`; it is only available via `bd list --json` or `bd label list <id> --json`. `collect.py` must use `.get(field, default)` for all sparse fields.

- **Bulk inventory (both modes)**: `bd list --status open --json --limit 0` and `bd list --status in_progress --json --limit 0` — **always run upfront**, even in focused mode. The `--limit 0` flag is required; the default limit silently truncates (live project: 157 open beads, default limit 50). This provides `labels` for all beads. Build a `{id: bead}` lookup dict from the results.
- **Per-bead detail (both modes)**: `bd show <id> --json` per in-scope bead → provides `description` (sparse), `notes`, `acceptance_criteria`, `comments` (list of `{author, text}` objects), `dependencies` (outbound; each item has `dependency_type`), `dependents` (inbound; each item has `dependency_type`), `parent` (sparse). `labels` from the show output is absent; use the bulk-inventory lookup instead.
- **deps_in**: built from `bd show --json`'s `dependents` list. `bd list --json`'s `dependencies` field is outbound-only; `deps_in` requires per-bead `bd show` calls regardless of mode.
- **Content field**: `.get('description','')` + `'\n\n'` + `.get('notes','')` + `'\n\n'` + `.get('acceptance_criteria','')` + `'\n\nCOMMENTS\n'` + `'\n'.join(c.get('text','') for c in bead.get('comments',[]))`.
- **`deps_out`**: built from `bd show --json`'s `dependencies` list; each item has `dependency_type`.

Output JSON schema:

```json
{
  "project_prefix": "agents-config",
  "mode": "focused|all",
  "target": "agents-config-xxx",      // null in all-mode
  "bead_count": 42,
  "truncated_count": 0,
  "capped": false,                    // true if focused child-chain hit 200-bead limit
  "beads": [
    {
      "id": "agents-config-xxx",
      "title": "...",
      "status": "open|in_progress",
      "labels": ["brainstormed", "..."],   // from bd list --json
      "parent": "agents-config-yyy",       // null if none
      "deps_out": [{"id": "agents-config-zzz", "type": "blocks"}],
      "deps_in":  [{"id": "agents-config-aaa", "type": "discovered-from"}],
      "content": "DESCRIPTION\n...\n\nNOTES\n...\n\nAC\n...\n\nCOMMENTS\n...",
      "truncated": false
    }
  ],
  "findings": [
    {
      "kind": "cycle|provenance_mismatch|type_conflict|stale_blocker",
      "severity": "blocking|major|minor",
      "dependent_id": "agents-config-xxx",   // null where not applicable
      "blocker_id":   "agents-config-yyy",   // null where not applicable
      "dep_type": "blocks",                  // null where not applicable
      "description": "one-line human-readable description",
      "proposed_command": "bd dep add ..."   // null if no single command applies
    }
  ]
}
```

Exit codes:

| Code | Meaning |
|------|---------|
| 0 | Success; JSON on stdout |
| 2 | Usage error (bad args); usage text on stderr |
| 3 | `bd` CLI not found on PATH |
| 4 | Bead database not found / not readable |

### R8 — Focused-mode scope definition

"1-hop dep neighborhood": all beads reachable by exactly 1 `bd dep` edge in either direction (both inbound and outbound) for dep types `blocks`, `discovered-from`, `tracks`, and `relates-to`. `parent-child` traversal is handled separately by the full parent chain + full child chain.

Child-chain expansion: unlimited depth via `parent-child` traversal. **Cap at 200 beads**: if expansion would exceed 200, `collect.py` stops, sets `"capped": true` in the output, and emits a warning to stderr naming the bead count.

`until`, `caused-by`, `validates`, `supersedes`, and `related` dep types are included in the bead's `deps_out`/`deps_in` inventory but do not trigger additional bead loads for the 1-hop expansion.

### R9 — Build discipline

Implementation MUST use `superpowers:writing-skills` to author `SKILL.md`. The Python helper mirrors the `whats-next/collect.py` patterns:

- `bd_json(*args)` helper: `subprocess.run(["bd"] + list(args), capture_output=True, text=True)` → `json.loads(result.stdout)`; return `[]` on any failure.
- `argparse` for argv parsing; `sys.exit(2)` on usage errors.
- All errors to `sys.stderr`; JSON only to `sys.stdout`.

## Out of scope

- Editing bead content fields. Only dep edges and audit comments are mutated in `--just-fix-it` mode.
- Closing beads, removing labels, or any non-dep state change.
- Filing HEP escalations. Findings are report-only.
- Cross-repository or cross-project awareness.
- Treating brainstormed-but-open beads as an error.
- Treating cross-state deps as invalid.
- Structural sanity rules for `tracks`, `until`, `caused-by`, `validates`, `supersedes`, `related`.
- Semantic false-dep inference for dep types other than `blocks` and `discovered-from`.
- HEP integration for autonomous formula invocation (explicitly deferred, §D3).
- A `--confidence-floor` flag.

## Acceptance criteria

1. Build passes. Typecheck passes. Tests pass.
2. `grep -r 'dep-health-check' src/ ~/.claude/skills/ ~/.claude/commands/ 2>/dev/null | grep -v 'src/plugins/beads/.agents/skills/dep-health-check' | grep -v 'src/user/.claude/commands/dep-health-check'` produces no output. (Name uniqueness.)
3. Skill installs to `~/.claude/skills/dep-health-check/` via `scripts/install.sh`. `scripts/install.sh --dry-run` exits 0 and lists `dep-health-check` under staged skills.
4. `/dep-health-check` slash command exists; its body is `Skill dep-health-check $ARGUMENTS` and no other content.
5. `collect.py --mode all` produces valid JSON matching §R7 schema, exit 0.
6. `collect.py --mode focused --target agents-config-3up3` produces valid JSON, exit 0; `bead_count` equals the target bead + its 1-hop dep neighbors (types `blocks`, `discovered-from`, `tracks`, `relates-to`) + full parent chain + full child chain.
7. `collect.py` exits 2 on unknown args, 3 when `bd` not on PATH, 4 when db not found.
8. `collect.py` exits 0 with `"capped": true` and a stderr warning when focused-mode expansion exceeds 200 beads. **[DEFERRED]** — testable only when a bead with a 200+ child subtree exists in the live project. Until then, the cap logic is present but unexercised by CI. Implementer must include the cap and the warning; the AC is considered verified by code review inspection.
9. `--just-fix-it` never emits `bd dep remove`, `bd close`, `bd label remove`, or bead-content mutation calls; only HIGH-confidence `bd dep add` edges applied; at most 10 per invocation.
10. Every applied edge in `--just-fix-it` has an audit comment on the **dependent** bead matching §R4 format.
11. Report sections are distinct for deterministic vs LLM-inferred findings; empty sections are omitted.
12. Every LLM finding carries a per-item rationale referencing observable bead content; no finding may cite only LLM-internal reasoning.

## Design notes

### D1 — Why hybrid (Python + LLM)?

Deterministic checks are cheap, exact, and gain nothing from LLM semantics. Semantic checks ("does X's prose imply a dep on Y?") cannot be reduced to deterministic rules without false positives spiraling. Hybrid keeps each task on its substrate and mirrors the `whats-next/collect.py` + `SKILL.md` split already in the project.

### D2 — Why add-only in `--just-fix-it`?

Adding a missing dep is at worst overrestrictive — the user can `bd dep remove` later, with the audit comment naming exactly what was added and why. Removing a dep that turned out to be load-bearing silently unblocks work that should stay paused. Asymmetric risk → asymmetric policy.

### D3 — Why report-only escalation?

HEP exists to pause an autonomous pipeline that needs human input. This skill is invoked interactively; the report is the inbox item. If the skill is ever invoked from an autonomous formula step, that is the moment to add HEP integration — not now.

### D4 — Confidence taxonomy for LLM findings

**For missing-dep inference:**

- **HIGH**: the target dep bead's **full bead-id** (e.g., `agents-config-acmh.12`) appears literally in the source bead's description, notes, AC, or comments, **and** a blocking/dependency verb appears within 20 whitespace-delimited words of that ID — closed set: "blocks", "requires", "depends on", "after", "needs", "uses", "blocked by", "hard-block on", "must be done before", "produced by", "produced from", "prerequisite", "first". No HIGH without both a literal ID match and a verb anchor.
- **MED**: strongly implied by bead text but bead-id not named literally (e.g., "this requires the helper script" + a separate bead title matches "helper script" in the same epic).
- **LOW**: plausible but no clear textual anchor (topic overlap only).

**For false-existing-dep inference:**

- **HIGH**: the source bead's own text explicitly contradicts the dep AND the LLM rationale must include a **verbatim quoted excerpt** from the bead text as evidence. The excerpt must contain either the specific blocker bead-id OR an explicit negation keyword: "no longer", "removed", "error", "unrelated", "not a blocker", "superseded", "duplicate". Both conditions (quoted excerpt + negation/id) required; editorial paraphrase does not qualify.
- **MED / LOW**: follow the same graduated-implication scale as missing-dep inference; never auto-applied.

Only HIGH is auto-applied in `--just-fix-it`. Hard cap: 10 edges per run (§R4).

### D5 — Confirmation UX in normal mode

Batched-by-class with per-item visibility (§R5). Mirrors the `wait-for-pr-comments` FIX/SKIP/ESCALATE classification UX familiar to users. Interactive-mode MED/LOW candidates appear under "False existing deps — review queue" (§R3), distinct from `--just-fix-it`'s "Judgment calls" section to avoid implementer confusion.

### D6 — File layout mirrors `whats-next`

`SKILL.md` is orchestration + presentation. All graph traversal, JSON shaping, and CLI parsing lives in `collect.py`. Implementers must read `src/user/.agents/skills/whats-next/collect.py` before authoring — specifically the `bd_json()` helper pattern, argparse exit codes, and stdout-only JSON contract.

### D7 — Brainstormed-but-open edge case

Tolerated; surfaced in Informational only. Never proposed as a fix.

### D8 — Cross-state deps are valid

Deps between any bead states are valid. The skill never proposes a remove on state-mismatch grounds. Stale-blocker detection fires only when the blocker is **closed**, not merely early-state.

### D9 — `bd show --json` sparse-field contract

`bd show --json` omits `description`, `parent`, `dependencies`, `dependents`, and `comments` when they are null or empty — use `.get(field, default)` throughout. `labels` is **never** in `bd show --json` output (not sparse-present; simply absent on the show endpoint). Labels must be sourced from `bd list --json --limit 0`, which ALWAYS returns them. `deps_out` maps to `bd show --json`'s `dependencies` (outbound); `deps_in` maps to `dependents` (inbound). Each list item in both arrays has a `dependency_type` field. `bd list --json`'s `dependencies` field is outbound-only — inbound deps are only available via per-bead `bd show --json`.

### D10 — Dep direction convention

In `bd dep add <dependent> <blocker>`:
- **dependent** = the bead that gains the dep edge; this is the `<from>` side; audit comments go HERE.
- **blocker** = the prerequisite; this is the `<to>` side.

For provenance: `produced-bead-Y` on X means Y was produced from X. Expected dep: `bd dep add Y X --type discovered-from`. Y is the dependent; X is the blocker/source.

### D11 — Single-shot LLM pass performance

At ~3 000 chars/bead × 100 beads ≈ 300 KB ≈ 75 000 input tokens — comfortably within sonnet[1m]. Token guard fires at 600 000 chars (well above typical project size). Expected wall time: 30–90 seconds. End-to-end estimate including `collect.py` runtime: 1–3 minutes.

## Open questions

None blocking.

notes:
# Epic Hygiene: Container Bead Rules, Enforcement, and whats-next Redesign

Spec file: docs/superpowers/specs/2026-05-13-epic-hygiene-design.md

## Summary

Define and enforce three rules governing container beads (milestones, epics, and features-with-children) to eliminate noise in readiness determination, whats-next output, and ancestor-walk closure. Enforce at two surfaces (collect.py, brainstorm-bead finalize) and migrate three known violations.

## Container Bead Definition

A bead is a container if: type is milestone or epic (always), OR type is feature AND has >=1 open/in-progress children. Decision type is informational — excluded from all lists. Feature with 0 children is a leaf bead.

## The Three Rules

(A) No executable work — container AC must be pure closure check; verification work goes in leaf child.
(B) Never in whats-next ready lists — enforced structurally in collect.py regardless of labels.
(C) No readiness labels — implementation-ready, implementation-readied-session-*, brainstormed, human must not appear on containers.

## Enforcement Surfaces

1. collect.py: type-based container filter, new planning-ready list, memoized 3-typed ancestor extraction, 7-column output, default sections = human+planning-ready+brainstorm (not impl).
2. brainstorm-bead finalize Step 3: container gate; milestone/epic always decomposition-only; feature-with-children same; childless feature proceeds normally.
3. implement-bead SKILL.md: add milestone to existing epic->flag-human path.
4. beads.md prose: I1/I2 'ancestor epic' -> 'ancestor bead'.

## whats-next Redesign

Four lists (empty sections suppressed): Needs-attention | Planning-ready | Ready-to-brainstorm | Ready-to-implement (impl only on explicit ask). All use 7 columns: P | Milestone | Feature | Parent Epic | Bead ID | Type | Title.

Planning-ready = container types with 0 open/in-progress children AND no implementation-ready label, dep-unblocked.

## Migration (3 targeted fixes)

- abn9.8: strip brainstormed + implementation-ready; trim AC to pure closure
- 7bk.19: strip brainstormed + implementation-readied-session-*; move grep/smoke AC to 7bk.19.9
- bt9e: reclassify epic -> spike

## Discovered Work

- biy4 (P3): I2 close walk AC enforcement (deferred)
