notes:
# dep-health-check skill — spec (rev 4)

## Summary

A beads-plugin skill that audits the dependency graph across the open/in-progress bead tree. Two scopes (focused on one bead, or whole-tree sweep) × two modes (interactive confirmation, or `--just-fix-it` autonomous add-only). Hybrid architecture: a Python helper performs fast deterministic structural checks and returns a JSON inventory; the LLM body of the skill reads bead contents (description, notes, comments, labels, AC) and reasons semantically about missing or wrong dep edges, attaching a per-item rationale and confidence to every finding.

## Background / motivation

Dep edges are wired ad-hoc during brainstorming or by formula automation. Nothing systematically checks that:

- `produced-bead-*` / `produced-from-*` provenance labels are mirrored by actual `bd dep` edges.
- The dep graph is acyclic.
- Declared dep types are semantically correct.
- Beads that reference each other in prose ("hard-blocks on acmh.12") actually have those deps wired.
- Declared blocks are real and not just wrong.

Silent drift results: work stalls behind stale blockers, parallelisable work serializes by mistake, and `bd ready` surfaces beads whose deps do not reflect reality.

## Requirements

### R1 — Invocation surface

| Scope    | Trigger                              | Behavior                                                                   |
|----------|--------------------------------------|----------------------------------------------------------------------------|
| Focused  | `/dep-health-check <bead-id>`        | Target + 1-hop dep neighborhood (§R8) + full parent + full child chain     |
| All      | `/dep-health-check` (no arg)         | Every open + in_progress bead in the project                               |

| Mode         | Trigger         | Behavior                                                                                  |
|--------------|-----------------|-------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------|
| Interactive  | default         | Findings batched by class; user confirms per class or per-item (§R5)                     |
| Just-fix-it  | `--just-fix-it` | Auto-applies HIGH-confidence add-only findings (≤ 10 per run); rest in report             |

**Argv grammar:**

```
dep-health-check [bead-id] [--just-fix-it]
```

- `bead-id` — optional positional; any string not beginning with `--`; absent → all-mode.
- `--just-fix-it` — optional flag; may appear in any position.
- Unknown arguments → skill exits non-zero, emits usage to stderr, no state changes.

Slash command body:

```
Skill dep-health-check $ARGUMENTS
```

### R2 — Hybrid pipeline

**Pass 1 — Deterministic (Python helper `collect.py`, fast):**

- Inventory: every in-scope bead with id, title, labels, deps in both directions (dependent-side and blocker-side) with dep types, parent, status, and full text content (see token guard and `collect.py` field sourcing in §R7). `collect.py` MUST call `bd list --status open --json --limit 0` and `bd list --status in_progress --json --limit 0` upfront in **both** focused and all-mode (to capture labels, which are absent from `bd show --json`). This produces the bulk inventory; in focused mode, collect.py then filters to the scoped bead set. Per-bead `bd show` calls add the content fields (description, notes, AC, comments, dependents/deps_in) but do NOT provide labels.
- Provenance-mirror check: a `produced-bead-Y` label on X, or `produced-from-X` label on Y, expects the dep edge `bd dep add Y X --type discovered-from` (Y is the **dependent**; X is the **blocker/source**). Flag when either label exists without the matching dep edge in the correct direction and type.
- Cycle detection: shell out to `bd dep cycles`; emit each detected cycle verbatim.
- Semantic-type sanity — enforce only structurally derivable rules:
  - A `blocks` dep between two beads that also share a `parent-child` dep edge in any direction is a conflict; flag it.
  - A `discovered-from` edge exists in both directions between the same pair; flag it (should be uni-directional).
  - A `relates-to` edge exists in only one direction between a pair; note as informational (may be legitimate), not a hard error.
  - No structural sanity rules are enforced for `tracks`, `until`, `caused-by`, `validates`, `supersedes`, or `related` — surfaced in the inventory for the LLM pass but not flagged deterministically.
- Stale-blocker detection: scope strictly to `blocks`-type deps. An open bead whose every `blocks`-type blocker is closed → flag as a `bd dep remove` candidate (audit/cleanup; the dep is cruft even if `bd ready` already handles the bead as ready).

**Token guard:** `collect.py` sums the character lengths of all bead `content` fields. If total > 600 000 characters (~150 000 tokens), each bead's content is truncated to the first 4 000 characters and the bead entry is flagged `"truncated": true`. Summary reports truncated-bead count.

**Pass 2 — Semantic (skill LLM body, sonnet[1m], single-shot call):**

All in-scope beads are fed to the LLM in one call using the JSON inventory from Pass 1. The LLM:

- Infers **missing deps** with rationale; attaches a confidence level (§D4).
- Infers **false existing deps** for `blocks` or `discovered-from` type deps only; attaches rationale and confidence.
- Does NOT propose removes for `relates-to`, `tracks`, `until`, `caused-by`, `validates`, `supersedes`, or `related`.
- After the LLM returns its finding list, SKILL.md applies a mechanical post-processing dedup step: any LLM finding whose `(dependent_id, blocker_id, dep_type)` tuple already appears in a `findings[]` entry from Pass 1 is silently dropped from the LLM output before rendering. This is code, not a prompt instruction.

### R3 — Report shape

One structured report per invocation; empty sections are omitted:

- **Summary** — counts per finding class; truncated-bead count if any.
- **Cycles** *(deterministic)* — each cycle verbatim; HIGH-confidence LLM break-point suggestion if available.
- **Missing provenance edges** *(deterministic)* — mismatches with expected direction, type, and copy-paste `bd dep add` commands.
- **Missing semantic deps** *(LLM)* — grouped HIGH → MED → LOW; rationale + proposed `bd dep add` per item.
- **False existing deps — confirmed** *(LLM, HIGH only)* — rationale + proposed `bd dep remove` per item.
- **False existing deps — review queue** *(LLM, MED/LOW)* — surfaced in both modes; never auto-applied. (Distinct label prevents confusion with `--just-fix-it`'s "Judgment calls" section.)
- **Stale blockers** *(deterministic, `blocks`-type only)* — proposed `bd dep remove` per item.
- **Semantic-type conflicts** *(deterministic)* — specific conflict type; correction commands.
- **Applied changes** *(`--just-fix-it` only)* — every dep edge added; rationale; bead id that received the audit comment.
- **Judgment calls** *(`--just-fix-it` only)* — MED/LOW missing-dep findings not auto-applied; copy-paste commands.
- **Informational** — brainstormed-but-open beads; `relates-to` asymmetries; truncated beads.

### R4 — Destructive-action policy

`--just-fix-it` is **strictly add-only**:

- `bd dep add <dependent> <blocker>` is allowed when the finding is HIGH-confidence AND the operation is purely additive.
- Hard cap: **at most 10 dep edges applied per invocation**. If HIGH-confidence candidates exceed 10, apply the first 10 (sorted by dependent-bead id lexicographic order) and report the rest in Judgment calls with the note "not applied; re-run to continue."
- `bd dep remove`, `bd close`, `bd label remove`, and all bead-content mutations — **never** auto-applied; always in the report with copy-paste commands.
- `--just-fix-it` is an interactive-user primitive. If invoked from a non-interactive context, it still applies changes — the caller is responsible for reading the audit-comment trail. HEP integration is explicitly out of scope (§D3).

**Audit comment** — written on the **dependent bead** (the first arg to `bd dep add <dependent> <blocker>` — the bead whose dep list grew):

```bash
bd comments add <dependent-id> "dep-health-check (just-fix-it): added dep <dependent-id> -> <blocker-id> (type <type>); reason: <rationale>"
```

### R5 — Interactive-mode UX

Findings presented batched by class. Per class:

- Show all candidates with rationales and proposed commands.
- Prompt: `apply all` / `pick individually` / `skip class`.
- `pick individually` walks item-by-item.

Cycles and "False existing deps — confirmed" (HIGH) items are always one-at-a-time regardless of class size.

### R6 — Skill artifacts

- **Location**: `src/plugins/beads/.agents/skills/dep-health-check/`
- **`SKILL.md`**: orchestration + LLM semantic pass + report rendering + interactive UX + `--just-fix-it` apply loop.
- **`collect.py`**: Python helper; pure read; JSON to stdout; errors to stderr.
- **Slash command**: `src/user/.claude/commands/dep-health-check.md`
- **Name uniqueness**: before submitting the PR, verify no file named `dep-health-check` exists in any other skill, command, or agent directory under `src/` or `~/.claude/`. Exact command: `grep -r 'dep-health-check' src/ ~/.claude/skills/ ~/.claude/commands/ 2>/dev/null | grep -v 'src/plugins/beads/.agents/skills/dep-health-check' | grep -v 'src/user/.claude/commands/dep-health-check'` — must produce no output.

### R7 — `collect.py` field sourcing and JSON schema

**Field sourcing (important — `bd show --json` uses sparse fields):**

`bd show --json` only includes `description`, `parent`, `dependencies`, `dependents`, and `comments` when non-null/non-empty. `labels` is **never** present in `bd show --json`; it is only available via `bd list --json` or `bd label list <id> --json`. `collect.py` must use `.get(field, default)` for all sparse fields.

- **Bulk inventory (both modes)**: `bd list --status open --json --limit 0` and `bd list --status in_progress --json --limit 0` — **always run upfront**, even in focused mode. The `--limit 0` flag is required; the default limit silently truncates (live project: 157 open beads, default limit 50). This provides `labels` for all beads. Build a `{id: bead}` lookup dict from the results.
- **Per-bead detail (both modes)**: `bd show <id> --json` per in-scope bead → provides `description` (sparse), `notes`, `acceptance_criteria`, `comments` (list of `{author, text}` objects), `dependencies` (outbound; each item has `dependency_type`), `dependents` (inbound; each item has `dependency_type`), `parent` (sparse). `labels` from the show output is absent; use the bulk-inventory lookup instead.
- **deps_in**: built from `bd show --json`'s `dependents` list. `bd list --json`'s `dependencies` field is outbound-only; `deps_in` requires per-bead `bd show` calls regardless of mode.
- **Content field**: `.get('description','')` + `'\n\n'` + `.get('notes','')` + `'\n\n'` + `.get('acceptance_criteria','')` + `'\n\nCOMMENTS\n'` + `'\n'.join(c.get('text','') for c in bead.get('comments',[]))`.
- **`deps_out`**: built from `bd show --json`'s `dependencies` list; each item has `dependency_type`.

Output JSON schema:

```json
{
  "project_prefix": "agents-config",
  "mode": "focused|all",
  "target": "agents-config-xxx",      // null in all-mode
  "bead_count": 42,
  "truncated_count": 0,
  "capped": false,                    // true if focused child-chain hit 200-bead limit
  "beads": [
    {
      "id": "agents-config-xxx",
      "title": "...",
      "status": "open|in_progress",
      "labels": ["brainstormed", "..."],   // from bd list --json
      "parent": "agents-config-yyy",       // null if none
      "deps_out": [{"id": "agents-config-zzz", "type": "blocks"}],
      "deps_in":  [{"id": "agents-config-aaa", "type": "discovered-from"}],
      "content": "DESCRIPTION\n...\n\nNOTES\n...\n\nAC\n...\n\nCOMMENTS\n...",
      "truncated": false
    }
  ],
  "findings": [
    {
      "kind": "cycle|provenance_mismatch|type_conflict|stale_blocker",
      "severity": "blocking|major|minor",
      "dependent_id": "agents-config-xxx",   // null where not applicable
      "blocker_id":   "agents-config-yyy",   // null where not applicable
      "dep_type": "blocks",                  // null where not applicable
      "description": "one-line human-readable description",
      "proposed_command": "bd dep add ..."   // null if no single command applies
    }
  ]
}
```

Exit codes:

| Code | Meaning |
|------|---------|
| 0 | Success; JSON on stdout |
| 2 | Usage error (bad args); usage text on stderr |
| 3 | `bd` CLI not found on PATH |
| 4 | Bead database not found / not readable |

### R8 — Focused-mode scope definition

"1-hop dep neighborhood": all beads reachable by exactly 1 `bd dep` edge in either direction (both inbound and outbound) for dep types `blocks`, `discovered-from`, `tracks`, and `relates-to`. `parent-child` traversal is handled separately by the full parent chain + full child chain.

Child-chain expansion: unlimited depth via `parent-child` traversal. **Cap at 200 beads**: if expansion would exceed 200, `collect.py` stops, sets `"capped": true` in the output, and emits a warning to stderr naming the bead count.

`until`, `caused-by`, `validates`, `supersedes`, and `related` dep types are included in the bead's `deps_out`/`deps_in` inventory but do not trigger additional bead loads for the 1-hop expansion.

### R9 — Build discipline

Implementation MUST use `superpowers:writing-skills` to author `SKILL.md`. The Python helper mirrors the `whats-next/collect.py` patterns:

- `bd_json(*args)` helper: `subprocess.run(["bd"] + list(args), capture_output=True, text=True)` → `json.loads(result.stdout)`; return `[]` on any failure.
- `argparse` for argv parsing; `sys.exit(2)` on usage errors.
- All errors to `sys.stderr`; JSON only to `sys.stdout`.

## Out of scope

- Editing bead content fields. Only dep edges and audit comments are mutated in `--just-fix-it` mode.
- Closing beads, removing labels, or any non-dep state change.
- Filing HEP escalations. Findings are report-only.
- Cross-repository or cross-project awareness.
- Treating brainstormed-but-open beads as an error.
- Treating cross-state deps as invalid.
- Structural sanity rules for `tracks`, `until`, `caused-by`, `validates`, `supersedes`, `related`.
- Semantic false-dep inference for dep types other than `blocks` and `discovered-from`.
- HEP integration for autonomous formula invocation (explicitly deferred, §D3).
- A `--confidence-floor` flag.

## Acceptance criteria

1. Build passes. Typecheck passes. Tests pass.
2. `grep -r 'dep-health-check' src/ ~/.claude/skills/ ~/.claude/commands/ 2>/dev/null | grep -v 'src/plugins/beads/.agents/skills/dep-health-check' | grep -v 'src/user/.claude/commands/dep-health-check'` produces no output. (Name uniqueness.)
3. Skill installs to `~/.claude/skills/dep-health-check/` via `scripts/install.sh`. `scripts/install.sh --dry-run` exits 0 and lists `dep-health-check` under staged skills.
4. `/dep-health-check` slash command exists; its body is `Skill dep-health-check $ARGUMENTS` and no other content.
5. `collect.py --mode all` produces valid JSON matching §R7 schema, exit 0.
6. `collect.py --mode focused --target agents-config-3up3` produces valid JSON, exit 0; `bead_count` equals the target bead + its 1-hop dep neighbors (types `blocks`, `discovered-from`, `tracks`, `relates-to`) + full parent chain + full child chain.
7. `collect.py` exits 2 on unknown args, 3 when `bd` not on PATH, 4 when db not found.
8. `collect.py` exits 0 with `"capped": true` and a stderr warning when focused-mode expansion exceeds 200 beads. **[DEFERRED]** — testable only when a bead with a 200+ child subtree exists in the live project. Until then, the cap logic is present but unexercised by CI. Implementer must include the cap and the warning; the AC is considered verified by code review inspection.
9. `--just-fix-it` never emits `bd dep remove`, `bd close`, `bd label remove`, or bead-content mutation calls; only HIGH-confidence `bd dep add` edges applied; at most 10 per invocation.
10. Every applied edge in `--just-fix-it` has an audit comment on the **dependent** bead matching §R4 format.
11. Report sections are distinct for deterministic vs LLM-inferred findings; empty sections are omitted.
12. Every LLM finding carries a per-item rationale referencing observable bead content; no finding may cite only LLM-internal reasoning.

## Design notes

### D1 — Why hybrid (Python + LLM)?

Deterministic checks are cheap, exact, and gain nothing from LLM semantics. Semantic checks ("does X's prose imply a dep on Y?") cannot be reduced to deterministic rules without false positives spiraling. Hybrid keeps each task on its substrate and mirrors the `whats-next/collect.py` + `SKILL.md` split already in the project.

### D2 — Why add-only in `--just-fix-it`?

Adding a missing dep is at worst overrestrictive — the user can `bd dep remove` later, with the audit comment naming exactly what was added and why. Removing a dep that turned out to be load-bearing silently unblocks work that should stay paused. Asymmetric risk → asymmetric policy.

### D3 — Why report-only escalation?

HEP exists to pause an autonomous pipeline that needs human input. This skill is invoked interactively; the report is the inbox item. If the skill is ever invoked from an autonomous formula step, that is the moment to add HEP integration — not now.

### D4 — Confidence taxonomy for LLM findings

**For missing-dep inference:**

- **HIGH**: the target dep bead's **full bead-id** (e.g., `agents-config-acmh.12`) appears literally in the source bead's description, notes, AC, or comments, **and** a blocking/dependency verb appears within 20 whitespace-delimited words of that ID — closed set: "blocks", "requires", "depends on", "after", "needs", "uses", "blocked by", "hard-block on", "must be done before", "produced by", "produced from", "prerequisite", "first". No HIGH without both a literal ID match and a verb anchor.
- **MED**: strongly implied by bead text but bead-id not named literally (e.g., "this requires the helper script" + a separate bead title matches "helper script" in the same epic).
- **LOW**: plausible but no clear textual anchor (topic overlap only).

**For false-existing-dep inference:**

- **HIGH**: the source bead's own text explicitly contradicts the dep AND the LLM rationale must include a **verbatim quoted excerpt** from the bead text as evidence. The excerpt must contain either the specific blocker bead-id OR an explicit negation keyword: "no longer", "removed", "error", "unrelated", "not a blocker", "superseded", "duplicate". Both conditions (quoted excerpt + negation/id) required; editorial paraphrase does not qualify.
- **MED / LOW**: follow the same graduated-implication scale as missing-dep inference; never auto-applied.

Only HIGH is auto-applied in `--just-fix-it`. Hard cap: 10 edges per run (§R4).

### D5 — Confirmation UX in normal mode

Batched-by-class with per-item visibility (§R5). Mirrors the `wait-for-pr-comments` FIX/SKIP/ESCALATE classification UX familiar to users. Interactive-mode MED/LOW candidates appear under "False existing deps — review queue" (§R3), distinct from `--just-fix-it`'s "Judgment calls" section to avoid implementer confusion.

### D6 — File layout mirrors `whats-next`

`SKILL.md` is orchestration + presentation. All graph traversal, JSON shaping, and CLI parsing lives in `collect.py`. Implementers must read `src/user/.agents/skills/whats-next/collect.py` before authoring — specifically the `bd_json()` helper pattern, argparse exit codes, and stdout-only JSON contract.

### D7 — Brainstormed-but-open edge case

Tolerated; surfaced in Informational only. Never proposed as a fix.

### D8 — Cross-state deps are valid

Deps between any bead states are valid. The skill never proposes a remove on state-mismatch grounds. Stale-blocker detection fires only when the blocker is **closed**, not merely early-state.

### D9 — `bd show --json` sparse-field contract

`bd show --json` omits `description`, `parent`, `dependencies`, `dependents`, and `comments` when they are null or empty — use `.get(field, default)` throughout. `labels` is **never** in `bd show --json` output (not sparse-present; simply absent on the show endpoint). Labels must be sourced from `bd list --json --limit 0`, which ALWAYS returns them. `deps_out` maps to `bd show --json`'s `dependencies` (outbound); `deps_in` maps to `dependents` (inbound). Each list item in both arrays has a `dependency_type` field. `bd list --json`'s `dependencies` field is outbound-only — inbound deps are only available via per-bead `bd show --json`.

### D10 — Dep direction convention

In `bd dep add <dependent> <blocker>`:
- **dependent** = the bead that gains the dep edge; this is the `<from>` side; audit comments go HERE.
- **blocker** = the prerequisite; this is the `<to>` side.

For provenance: `produced-bead-Y` on X means Y was produced from X. Expected dep: `bd dep add Y X --type discovered-from`. Y is the dependent; X is the blocker/source.

### D11 — Single-shot LLM pass performance

At ~3 000 chars/bead × 100 beads ≈ 300 KB ≈ 75 000 input tokens — comfortably within sonnet[1m]. Token guard fires at 600 000 chars (well above typical project size). Expected wall time: 30–90 seconds. End-to-end estimate including `collect.py` runtime: 1–3 minutes.

## Open questions

None blocking.

comments.author: Scott Hamilton
comments.text:
Original brainstorm notes (preserved before write-spec overwrote --notes):

Skill requirements:
- Two modes: (1) focus on specific bead to find its place in dep tree; (2) general health check across all open/in-progress beads
- Label-aware: understands brainstormed, implementation-ready, human, formula-*, ralf:* labels to interpret bead purpose and impact
- Cross-state deps are valid: not-yet-brainstormed | brainstormed | implementation-ready deps between each other are acceptable
- Edge case: brainstormed beads may be open/in-progress (technically should be closed - tolerate, do not treat as hard error)
- Implementation: most logic in Python (dep graph traversal, cycle detection, missing-dep heuristics, label parsing); skill file is orchestration + presentation only
- Must use writing-skills or skill-creator skill (whichever is current at implement time) to build the skill
- Two invocation modes: (a) normal - ask user about conflicts with recommendations before applying; (b) just-fix-it - skip confirmation, best-judgment choices, flag decisions in report
- Conflict handling: circular deps -> ask user or flag in report; ambiguous relationships -> same
- Target outcome: sensible dep chain across entire open/in-progress bead tree (user non-objecting)
- Report must include: changes made/proposed, conflicts flagged, judgment calls in just-fix-it mode

comments.author: Scott Hamilton
comments.text: Brainstormed; produced agents-config-he9o on 2026-05-13

title: [Impl] Update ralf-* skills and formulas to consume worker-report-v1 and dispatch new workers
description:
## Summary

Wire the new worker-agent architecture (7bk.19.1 / 19.2 / 19.3) into `ralf-implement`. Update `ralf-implement` to consume `worker-report-v1` via `implement-bead`'s per-dispatch primitive; rename `ralf-implement` prompt-template files using the `subagent-*` / `foreign-cli-*` scheme; wire concrete template-path references into `ralf-implement/SKILL.md`; close two remaining formula-file gaps not addressed by PR #66 (header pointer comment + `subagent_type` plumbing in green-loop dispatch prose). `ralf-review` foreign-eyes and template renames are deferred to `agents-config-abn9.13` (`ralf-spec`).

## Background / Motivation

7bk.19.1 defined the `worker-report-v1` contract. 7bk.19.2 created three focused worker agents (`tdd-red-team`, `tdd-green-team`, `bug-diagnoser`). 7bk.19.3 rewrote `implement-bead` as a metadata-driven dispatcher with the per-dispatch primitive at `implement-bead/SKILL.md` §4 and the upstream-report retrieval at §6. PR #66 (commit `11c5bd1`, 2026-05-12 — "feat(agents): migrate bead-implementor to five role-named workers (acmh.13)") then migrated both formula files (`implement-feature.formula.toml`, `fix-bug.formula.toml`) to stage-declarative dispatch language and deleted `bead-implementor.md`.

This bead closes the remaining 19.4 work that PR #66 did **not** address:

1. The `ralf-implement` SKILL.md itself still has no `worker-report-v1` wiring, no explicit convergence predicate, and no reference to the per-dispatch primitive.
2. Prompt-template files in `ralf-implement/` are unreferenced by their SKILL.md (S1, from superseded `abn9.9`). The `ralf-review` equivalent (S2 foreign-eyes option) is deferred to `agents-config-abn9.13` (`ralf-spec`), which replaces `ralf-review` entirely.
3. Two narrow formula-file gaps remain post-PR-#66: (a) no `ralf:required` header pointer comment in either formula file; (b) the green-loop dispatch prose does not pass `subagent_type: tdd-green-team` to the new ralf-implement contract (introduced in this bead), which is now a fail-fast input under R1.11.

## Requirements

### R1 — `ralf-implement/SKILL.md` rewrite

R1.1. Consume `worker-report-v1` evidence blocks and derive gate roll-up (`pass` / `fail` / `partial` / `n/a`) per `worker-report-v1.md` §1.1. Workers do NOT emit `gate_status`; the orchestrator derives it.

R1.2. Dispatch inner doer via Agent tool with caller-provided `subagent_type`. ralf-implement is intended for work whose success state is `gate=pass` (machine gates apply) OR `gate=n/a` (no machine gates apply, e.g. docs/config repos); it is NOT intended to orchestrate red-tests (success = intentionally-failing tests, gate-shape = `fail`) or diagnose (single-shot, no iteration).
- The canonical recommendation in the interactive prompt is `tdd-green-team` (the only named worker whose success state matches the convergence predicate at R1.6).
- `general-purpose` is the fallback when no project-specific worker fits. `general-purpose` is a Claude Code builtin `subagent_type`, NOT an agent file in the repo.
- Other workers (`tdd-red-team`, `bug-diagnoser`) are dispatched directly via the per-dispatch primitive from formula steps (not via ralf-implement) because their gate shape does not match the convergence predicate; do NOT offer them in the interactive prompt.
- If an interactive caller declines both options, ralf-implement aborts with a clear diagnostic naming `subagent_type` as the missing input. No silent fallback.

R1.3. Reference the per-dispatch primitive at `implement-bead/SKILL.md` §4 via informal section reference (`see implement-bead/SKILL.md §4 — Per-dispatch primitive`). Do NOT inline the procedure.

R1.4. Synthesize `status: failed` on crash/malformed worker output per `worker-report-v1.md` §4: empty `evidence`, `escalations[].reason` of `"Worker crashed"` or `"Worker emitted malformed report"`, `discovered_work: []`, `commits: []`.

R1.4.1. **Synthesize on schema violation in evidence sub-blocks** (cycle-4 F-malformed-evidence resolution). If the worker-report parses as YAML and carries `status: complete` but any present `evidence` sub-block violates the `worker-report-v1.md` §1.1 schema (e.g., `evidence.tests` is missing required `command` / `exit_code` / `skipped`; or a field is the wrong type — string where int expected, etc.), treat as malformed and synthesize `status: failed` with `escalations[].reason: "Worker emitted malformed report"` wrapping the schema violation in `escalations[].detail`. Without this, R1.6's status-first gate has a tunnel: a `status: complete` report with corrupt sub-fields would compute a misleading derived gate and route to a convergence-candidate path.

R1.5. Stamp `worker-audit-<agent-name>[-iter<N>]` label per dispatch ONLY when the dispatched doer is a named worker covered by `implement-bead/SKILL.md` "Audit-label scope" (`tdd-red-team`, `tdd-green-team`, `bug-diagnoser`). For `general-purpose` or any other non-worker doer, do NOT stamp `worker-audit-*`.

ralf-implement OWNS this conditional in policy. **In practice it is documentation-only until upstream `agents-config-go1w` lands** (cycle-4 F-unenforceable-conditional resolution): §4 step 5 currently stamps `worker-audit-<agent-name>[-iter<N>]` unconditionally and §4 accepts no "skip step 5" directive. Stamping a label after §4 returns cannot retract a label already applied. Therefore, in 19.7's deliverable, `general-purpose` dispatches via §4 WILL receive a `worker-audit-general-purpose-iter<N>` label — accepting this is the explicit cost of NOT modifying §4 in this PR (per D3).

The 19.7 deliverable: `ralf-implement/SKILL.md` documents the policy ("non-named-worker doers SHOULD NOT carry `worker-audit-*`"), names `go1w` as the upstream tracking item, and notes that current behavior over-stamps in the `general-purpose` case until `go1w` lands. No active filtering or post-hoc label removal in ralf-implement — that would be an integration tangent for this bead.

R1.6. Explicit convergence predicate. The predicate branches on TWO signals from the worker-report: (a) `status` (verbatim from the report or synthesized per `worker-report-v1.md` §4), and (b) derived gate roll-up per `worker-report-v1.md` §1.1.

- **`status = failed` (real or synthesized crash/malformed report)**: NOT a convergence candidate, regardless of derived gate. Iterate until cap. At cap: `FAIL`.
- **`status = complete` AND `gate = pass`**: convergence candidate; converge if fresh-eyes finds no blocking/critical. At cap with no blocking/critical: `PASS_WITH_RESERVATIONS`.
- **`status = complete` AND `gate = n/a`** (no machine gates apply — docs/config repos; not a crash report): convergence candidate; fresh-eyes alone decides convergence. Converge when fresh-eyes finds no blocking/critical. At cap with no blocking/critical: `PASS_WITH_RESERVATIONS`.
- **`status = complete` AND `gate = fail` or `gate = partial`**: NOT a convergence candidate; iterate until cap. At cap: `FAIL`.
- **Otherwise at cap** (blocking/critical present after fresh-eyes, regardless of status/gate): `FAIL`.

The status check is the first gate. Without it, a synthesized crash report (which has `evidence: {}` → derived gate `n/a` per `worker-report-v1.md` §4) would silently pass through as a convergence candidate.

R1.7. Convergence-candidate definition: an iteration where `status = complete` AND derived gate roll-up ∈ {`pass`, `n/a`}. Any `status: failed` iteration is excluded from convergence-candidate-hood by definition.

R1.8. Per-cycle loop:
- Default cycle cap remains `RALF_IMPLEMENT_DEFAULT_CYCLES=3`. The cycle range is `1..20` per the existing skill contract.
- Cycle sequence: cycle 1 = foreign-eyes via Codex (`gpt-5.5`); cycle 2 = foreign-eyes via Gemini; cycle 3+ = pure-Claude fresh-eyes (matches existing skill semantics — extended cycles 4–20 are pure-Claude). NOTE: this sequence matches the current `ralf-implement/SKILL.md`; R1.8 is documentation alignment for this bullet, not a behavior change.
- **Degradation cascade** (cycle-4 F-cascade-asymmetry resolution): On Codex degradation at any cycle: dispatch Gemini as the fallback for that cycle index (not pure-Claude). On Gemini degradation when Gemini was the fallback OR the planned cycle: fall back to pure-Claude. Cycle counts as consumed regardless. Evidence-store canonicalization (per D26) applies: both attempt artifacts persist under `.ralf/<session-id>/`; cycle status reports both attempts (e.g. `cycle-N=Codex:<STATUS>,Gemini:COMPLETED`); fallback output is canonical input. The existing `ralf-implement/SKILL.md` "degrade cleanly to pure fresh-eyes" line is superseded by this cascade.
- `quality-reviewer` + `simplify` run ONLY on convergence candidates (per R1.7). They do NOT run on `status: failed` iterations or on `gate ∈ {fail, partial}` iterations.
- `simplify` is advisory-only. Trigger criterion (binary, measurable): after `simplify` lands its edits, re-run the project test command from the worker-report's `evidence.tests.command`. If any previously-passing test now fails, restart the loop via a new worker dispatch — `simplify`'s output is rejected. If tests still pass, accept `simplify` and converge. This eliminates judgment calls about what counts as "test-affecting." **N/A case** (cycle-4 F-simplify-na resolution): if `evidence.tests.command` is empty or absent (gate=n/a docs/config repos, or a worker that did not run tests), there is no test command to re-run — accept `simplify`'s output without a re-run and converge. The regression check is N/A; fresh-eyes review remains the convergence signal in that branch.
- `verify-checklist` is removed as a separate per-iteration step. Its inputs are split: tests/build/lint/typecheck evidence is now derived from each worker's `worker-report-v1` `evidence` blocks (the gate roll-up at R1.1); the quality-reviewer + simplify portion runs on convergence candidates (preceding bullet). The historical "run verify-checklist every iteration" behavior is intentionally retired.

R1.9. Iteration-routing section names each prompt template by concrete relative path. The paths are **documentation references** for human readers navigating the skill directory; they are NOT resolved at runtime by the dispatched Claude subagent (which receives a prompt string, not a `cwd` rooted at the skill directory).

At dispatch time, ralf-implement (the markdown skill interpreted by the orchestrator LLM) performs these **operational steps** (cycle-4 F-inline-paste-procedure resolution):

1. **`Read`** the relevant template file by its relative path inside the installed skill directory (the orchestrator resolves the path against the loaded skill's location).
2. **Substitute** every placeholder in the template body (e.g., `[PASTE ORIGINAL DOD]`, `[PASTE ORIGINAL SPEC]`, `[PASTE CONTEXT]`) with the corresponding caller-supplied input string. Placeholders are uppercase-bracket tokens by convention.
3. **Dispatch** via `Agent({subagent_type: <doer>, prompt: <substituted-body>})`. Templates are read fresh on each dispatch — do NOT assume cached content.

This matches the existing convention in `implementer-prompt.md` (which embeds `[PASTE ORIGINAL DOD]` placeholders the orchestrator fills before dispatch).

Templates:
- `./subagent-implementer.md` — initial-cycle dispatch (Claude subagent body)
- `./subagent-fresh-eyes.md` — pure-Claude fresh-eyes cycles (Claude subagent body)
- `./subagent-foreign-cycle.md` — foreign-augmented cycles (Claude subagent body, which then invokes the foreign CLI)
- `./foreign-cli-instructions.md` — instruction file piped to Codex / Gemini via stdin (NOT inline-pasted into an Agent-tool prompt; the dispatched Claude foreign-cycle subagent — itself the product of inline-pasting `subagent-foreign-cycle.md` — reads `foreign-cli-instructions.md` from its repo-relative path inside the worktree and pipes the body to `codex-companion.mjs task` / `gemini` on stdin).

R1.10. `ralf:required` label semantics documented in `ralf-implement/SKILL.md` as the canonical home (the label is its trigger signal).

R1.11. Non-interactive context detection is **caller-supplied**, not shell-evaluated. ralf-implement is a markdown skill interpreted by an LLM, not a shell script — it cannot reliably evaluate `[ -t 0 ]` from inside the model. The caller indicates non-interactive context by EITHER:
- passing an explicit argument (e.g. `non_interactive: true`) in the skill invocation context, OR
- setting `RALF_NONINTERACTIVE=1` in the harness environment (a value the model can read via a single `bash` call if needed).

**Argument syntax canonicalization** (cycle-4 F-syntax resolution): all arguments passed to ralf-implement in invocation prose use `<key>: <value>` form (colon-space-value, YAML-style). Examples: `subagent_type: tdd-green-team`, `non_interactive: true`, `max_cycles: 5`. The orchestrator LLM is the parser; consistent shape across all callers prevents drift. Form variants (`key=value`, `--key value`, `key:value` without space) are NOT canonical and SHOULD NOT be used in caller invocation prose.

In non-interactive context with `subagent_type` missing, ralf-implement fails fast with a diagnostic naming `subagent_type` as the missing input and explaining that orchestration callers (e.g. `implement-bead`, formula green-loop steps) must pass it explicitly. No prompt is offered.

Orchestration callers (`implement-bead`, `run-queue`, formula green-loop steps) MUST pass `subagent_type` explicitly; they are the canonical non-interactive consumers. R4.2 makes this concrete for the two formula files.


### R3 — Template-file renames (Scheme A)

R3.1. `ralf-implement/`: rename `implementer-prompt.md` → `subagent-implementer.md`; `fresh-eyes-prompt.md` → `subagent-fresh-eyes.md`; `foreign-eyes-prompt.md` → `subagent-foreign-cycle.md`; `foreign-agent-prompt.md` → `foreign-cli-instructions.md`. The renames use `git mv` (or equivalent); old filenames MUST NOT exist on disk after the rename.

R3.2. `ralf-review/` template renames — **deferred to `agents-config-abn9.13` (`ralf-spec`)**, which replaces `ralf-review/` entirely.

R3.3. `ralf-implement/` SKILL.md path references updated to new names. Intra-template references updated: the existing `foreign-eyes-prompt.md` body references `./foreign-agent-prompt.md`; after rename, `subagent-foreign-cycle.md` must reference `./foreign-cli-instructions.md`. No active `ralf-implement/` template, `ralf-implement/SKILL.md`, or formula file may reference any of the four old `ralf-implement/` filenames after the rename.

### R4 — Formula-file gaps not addressed by PR #66

R4.1. Add a `ralf:required` header pointer comment near the top of both `implement-feature.formula.toml` and `fix-bug.formula.toml`:
```
# ralf:required label semantics: see src/user/.agents/skills/ralf-implement/SKILL.md
```

R4.2. Update the green-loop step's `ralf:required` dispatch prose in BOTH `implement-feature.formula.toml` and `fix-bug.formula.toml` to pass `subagent_type: tdd-green-team` explicitly to ralf-implement. The two files have **different current prose** (fix-bug also propagates `the root-cause note`), so the prescriptive replacement is split per-file (cycle-4 F-critical-fix-bug-prose resolution).

**For `implement-feature.formula.toml`** (current prose, verbatim):
> "If label `ralf:required` is present: invoke `ralf-implement` with the bead spec, Definition of Done, relevant context, quality commands, and optional max cycle count from `ralf:cycles=N`"

becomes:

> "If label `ralf:required` is present: invoke `ralf-implement` with the bead spec, Definition of Done, relevant context, quality commands, `subagent_type: tdd-green-team`, and optional max cycle count from `ralf:cycles=N`"

**For `fix-bug.formula.toml`** (current prose, verbatim):
> "If label `ralf:required` is present: invoke `ralf-implement` with the root-cause note, bead spec, Definition of Done, relevant context, quality commands, and optional max cycle count from `ralf:cycles=N`"

becomes:

> "If label `ralf:required` is present: invoke `ralf-implement` with the root-cause note, bead spec, Definition of Done, relevant context, quality commands, `subagent_type: tdd-green-team`, and optional max cycle count from `ralf:cycles=N`"

**The root-cause note argument MUST be preserved** in the fix-bug form — it is the §6 propagation chain from the upstream diagnose step's worker-report. Dropping it would break fix-bug's downstream test-writing dispatch.

Rationale: R1.11 makes `subagent_type` a fail-fast input in non-interactive context. Formula green-loop steps run non-interactively. Without this update, every autonomous formula-driven ralf-implement run trips the new fail-fast contract.

The previously-listed (and later-dropped) `R4.2: root_cause_note propagation` is NOT re-added — that case is handled by `implement-bead/SKILL.md` §6's upstream-report retrieval primitive shipped in 19.3. The `reproduction_steps` schema-vs-§6.3 mismatch remains filed as upstream discovered work (out of 19.4 scope).

Cross-shell note for both R5.1 and R5.2: the commands above are shell-ready as written (single-quoted regex literals; `-E` extended-regex for R5.2). When copy-pasting from `bd show` output, verify quoting did not get double-escaped before asserting zero matches.

## Design Notes

**D1 — subagent_type: required input with interactive fallback (interactive contexts only).**
ralf-implement requires `subagent_type` from the orchestration caller. In interactive contexts, a missing `subagent_type` triggers an interactive prompt offering `tdd-green-team` (canonical) and `general-purpose` (fallback). User declining both → abort with diagnostic. In non-interactive contexts (caller-supplied flag or `RALF_NONINTERACTIVE=1` env), missing input fails fast with the same diagnostic. Rationale: keeps the contract clean (no hardcoded defaults) while preserving UX for human callers and refusing to hang in autonomous run-queue / implement-bead / formula orchestration.

**D2 — ralf-review: entirely deferred to abn9.13 (ralf-spec).**
Originally D2 was a minimal worker-report-v1-N/A documentation note; scope was expanded 2026-05-12 to add the foreign-eyes option (S2). Both the N/A note and the foreign-eyes work are now deferred to `agents-config-abn9.13` (`ralf-spec`), which replaces `ralf-review/` entirely with a tiered-pipeline architecture that subsumes all R2/R3.2 requirements. This bead (abn9.10) no longer touches `ralf-review/`.

**D3 — per-dispatch primitive: anchor reference, not duplication.**
ralf-implement references `implement-bead/SKILL.md`'s per-dispatch primitive via an informal section reference (`see implement-bead/SKILL.md §4 — Per-dispatch primitive`) rather than inlining the procedure; `implement-bead/SKILL.md` is NOT modified in this PR. Rationale: single canonical home; avoids drift between the two files.

**D4 — ralf:required documentation home.**
Canonical doc in `ralf-implement/SKILL.md` (label is its trigger signal). Short pointer comment in each formula file header (R4.1).

**D5 — bead-implementor.md deletion (SUPERSEDED — landed in PR #66).**
PR #66 (2026-05-12, commit `11c5bd1`) delivered the deletion ahead of this bead. The invariant grep AC remains in this bead for regression protection.

**D6 — Three follow-up beads filed.**
7bk.21: inline-execution directive for `implement-bead`. 7bk.22: explicit `agent:*` / `skill:*` dispatch label overrides. 7bk.23: `stage:<role>` label stamping for robust upstream step lookup. All three depend on 7bk.19.4 and are P4/backlog, unbrainstormed.

**D7 — Convergence predicate: status + gate + fresh-eyes combined.**
Converge when: `status = complete` AND derived gate ∈ {`pass`, `n/a`} AND fresh-eyes finds no blocking/critical. At cycle cap with `status = complete` + gate ∈ {`pass`, `n/a`} + no blocking/critical → `PASS_WITH_RESERVATIONS`; blocking/critical or gate ∈ {`fail`, `partial`} or `status = failed` → `FAIL`. Scope: this predicate applies to ralf-implement orchestration of gate=pass-shaped OR gate=n/a-shaped workers (canonically `tdd-green-team` in code repos; `general-purpose` in docs/config repos). It does NOT apply to red-tests (gate-shape `fail` is success) or diagnose (single-shot, no iteration); those stages dispatch directly via the per-dispatch primitive from formula steps.

**D8 — Per-cycle loop: quality-reviewer + simplify on convergence candidate only.**
Convergence candidate = iteration where `status = complete` AND derived gate ∈ {`pass`, `n/a`} (per R1.7). quality-reviewer + simplify run on convergence candidates only, not on `status: failed` iterations or on `gate ∈ {fail, partial}` iterations. The legacy "run verify-checklist every iteration" step is removed: per-iteration test/build/lint/typecheck evidence is now derived from each worker's `worker-report-v1` evidence blocks (R1.1 gate roll-up), so a separate verify-checklist iteration is redundant.

**D9 — Formula step language: stage-declarative (SUPERSEDED — landed in PR #66).**
PR #66 (2026-05-12) rewrote both formula step descriptions to stage-declarative language and removed MAX_ITERATIONS literals and loop-prose. No further formula step-description rewrite needed in this bead beyond R4.1 (header pointer) and R4.2 (green-loop `subagent_type` plumbing).

**D10 — Convergence candidate definition (extended for n/a, gated by status).**
Cycle-2 review fix (F12): convergence candidate includes iterations where derived gate is `pass` OR `n/a`. Cycle-3 review fix (F3): adds the `status = complete` gate so synthesized crash reports (which have `evidence: {}` → derived gate `n/a` per `worker-report-v1.md` §4) are NOT mis-classified as convergence candidates. The `n/a` branch covers docs/config repos and any work where no machine-gate evidence applies AND the worker completed successfully — fresh-eyes alone is the convergence signal in that case. quality-reviewer + simplify still run on each convergence candidate.

**D11 — simplify is advisory; correctness changes restart the loop.**
Cycle-3 review fix (F8): `simplify`'s trigger criterion is now measurable. After `simplify` lands its edits, re-run the project test command from the worker-report's `evidence.tests.command`. Tests still pass → accept `simplify` and converge. Any previously-passing test now fails → reject `simplify`'s output and restart the loop with a new worker dispatch. This replaces the older "test-affecting production changes" phrasing, which required judgment about what counted.

**D12 — general-purpose fallback is a Claude Code builtin subagent_type.**
`subagent_type: 'general-purpose'` is a Claude Code builtin, not an agent file in the repo. Clarified in spec to avoid confusion with agent files.

**D13 — per-dispatch primitive reference is informal (not a formal markdown anchor).**
`implement-bead/SKILL.md` has no formal anchors and is not modified in this PR. `ralf-implement` uses an informal section reference (`see implement-bead/SKILL.md §4 — Per-dispatch primitive`).

**D14 — ralf-review foreign-eyes policy: deferred to abn9.13.**
Added 2026-05-12 brainstorm; deferred 2026-05-15 scope reduction. The Q2 resolution (always-on foreign-eyes for ralf-review) is deferred to `agents-config-abn9.13` (`ralf-spec`), which subsumes this design in a more sophisticated tiered pipeline (T1=Gemini, T2=Claude+Codex, T3=all-three). The Codex/Gemini/pure-Claude cycle sequencing originally documented here no longer applies to abn9.10.

**D15 — Template-filename naming scheme: subagent-* / foreign-cli-* (Scheme A), ralf-implement only.**
Added 2026-05-12 brainstorm. Old filenames confused audience: "foreign-eyes" sounded like the foreign model's prompt (it is actually the Claude subagent's mission), "foreign-agent" was ambiguous. New scheme: `subagent-*` = prompt body inline-pasted into a Claude subagent's Agent-tool dispatch; `foreign-cli-*` = template body piped on stdin to Codex/Gemini. Audience explicit at filename level. This bead applies Scheme A to `ralf-implement/` only; `ralf-review/` (now `ralf-spec/`) is handled by `agents-config-abn9.13`.

**D16 — PR #66 supersedence + abn9.13 scope reduction.**
Added 2026-05-12 brainstorm; updated 2026-05-15. PR #66 (commit `11c5bd1`, 2026-05-12) landed the bulk of the original 19.4 formula-side scope ahead of this bead. Subsequent analysis (2026-05-15) found that `agents-config-abn9.13` (`ralf-spec`) replaces `ralf-review/` entirely, fully subsuming R2 + R3.2. Remaining 19.4 work this bead closes: (a) `ralf-implement` SKILL.md rewrite (R1), (b) `ralf-implement/` prompt-template renames, Scheme A (R3.1), (c) two narrow formula gaps (R4.1: `ralf:required` header pointer; R4.2: green-loop `subagent_type` plumbing). R2 (`ralf-review` SKILL.md) and R3.2 (`ralf-review/` rename) deferred to abn9.13.

**D17 — ralf-implement gate-shape scope.**
Added 2026-05-12 cycle-1 review (F2), extended cycle-2 review (F12), extended cycle-3 review (F3 — status check added). ralf-implement orchestrates work whose success state is `status: complete` AND gate ∈ {`pass`, `n/a`}. `status: failed` (real or synthesized crash) is never a convergence candidate. red-tests (success = gate=fail by design) and diagnose (single-shot, no iteration) dispatch directly via the per-dispatch primitive from formula steps. The interactive subagent_type prompt offers only `tdd-green-team` and `general-purpose` (R1.2).

**D18 — verify-checklist retirement scope.**
Added 2026-05-12 cycle-1 review (F4). "verify-checklist subsumed by gate roll-up" specifically means: per-iteration test/build/lint/typecheck checks are now derived from worker-report-v1 evidence (no separate run); the quality-reviewer + simplify portion of verify-checklist runs on convergence candidates only (D8/D10). Historical per-iteration verify-checklist is intentionally retired, not silently dropped.

**D19 — Non-interactive context: caller-supplied flag, not shell test.**
Added 2026-05-12 cycle-1 review (F6), corrected cycle-2 review (F14). The original D19 used `[ -t 0 ]` shell test; that does not work for an LLM-interpreted markdown skill. Replaced with caller-supplied indicator: explicit `non_interactive: true` argument OR `RALF_NONINTERACTIVE=1` env. Orchestration callers (`implement-bead`, `run-queue`, formula green-loop steps) MUST pass `subagent_type` explicitly and are the canonical non-interactive consumers.

**D20 — Docs-only repo validation: build/typecheck/tests AC replaced.**
Added 2026-05-12 cycle-1 review (F7). This repo has no build / typecheck / test infrastructure (per project AGENTS.md). The historical "Build passes. Typecheck passes. Tests pass." AC is replaced by docs-validation: filesystem-level rename checks (new files exist; old filenames absent), SKILL.md and intra-template path-reference greps, and the invariant grep at R5.1 / old-filename grep at R5.2.

**D21 — worker-audit label scope (R1.5 constraint).**
Added 2026-05-12 cycle-2 review (F19), refined cycle-3 review (F5). ralf-implement stamps `worker-audit-<agent-name>[-iter<N>]` only when the dispatched doer is a named worker covered by `implement-bead/SKILL.md` "Audit-label scope" (`tdd-red-team`, `tdd-green-team`, `bug-diagnoser`). For `general-purpose` or other non-worker doers, ralf-implement does NOT stamp `worker-audit-*`. The conditional lives in ralf-implement, not in §4 — an upstream §4 contradiction (step 5 stamps unconditionally) is filed as `agents-config-go1w`.

**D22 — Degradation cascade preserves foreign-eyes value (F17 resolution + F2 evidence-store canonicalization).**
Added 2026-05-12 cycle-2 review (F17), extended cycle-3 review (F2). On Codex degradation at any cycle, dispatch Gemini as the fallback for that cycle index (not pure-Claude). Only when Gemini also degrades — or Gemini was the planned cycle and degrades — fall back to pure-Claude. Cycle counts as consumed regardless. Both attempt artifacts persist under `.ralf/<session-id>/`; cycle status reports both attempts (`cycle-N=Codex:<STATUS>,Gemini:COMPLETED`); fallback's output is the canonical findings input.

**D23 — R4.2 (root_cause_note) dropped; R4.2 (subagent_type) re-added.**
Added 2026-05-12 cycle-2 review (F16). The originally-scoped R4.2 ("propagate root_cause_note from diagnose to red-tests in fix-bug formula") is dropped: `implement-bead/SKILL.md` §6 already handles this via the upstream-report retrieval primitive shipped in 19.3. Cycle-3 review (F4) re-introduced an R4.2 slot for a DIFFERENT concern: formula green-loop dispatch prose now must pass `subagent_type: tdd-green-team` to ralf-implement, given R1.11's non-interactive fail-fast contract. The `reproduction_steps` schema-vs-§6.3 mismatch remains upstream discovered work.

**D24 — Template-path resolution mechanism (cycle-3 F1 resolution).**
Added 2026-05-12 cycle-3 review. The "concrete relative path" language in R1.9 / R3.3 is a documentation-reference convention for human readers; templates are NOT resolved at runtime by the dispatched Claude subagent. At dispatch time, ralf-implement (the markdown skill) **inline-pastes the relevant template body** into the Agent-tool prompt argument, matching the existing `[PASTE ORIGINAL DOD]` convention in `implementer-prompt.md`. The exception is `foreign-cli-instructions.md`, which is read by the foreign-cycle Claude subagent from its repo-relative path (inside the worktree) and piped to `codex-companion.mjs task` / `gemini` on stdin. Considered: a `${SKILL_DIR}` resolution convention with a defined lookup mechanism (rejected: requires harness-level support not present today; deferred until tracer-bullet work in 7bk.19.5).

**D25 — Formula green-loop subagent_type plumbing (cycle-3 F4 resolution).**
Added 2026-05-12 cycle-3 review. R1.11 makes `subagent_type` a fail-fast input in non-interactive context. Formula green-loop steps run non-interactively. Without R4.2's update to both formula files' dispatch prose, every autonomous formula-driven ralf-implement run would trip the fail-fast contract. The canonical green-loop worker is `tdd-green-team`, so the formula prose hardcodes that as the passed argument. Future stage/worker variants would need their own formula-step prose.

**D26 — Cascade evidence-store canonicalization (cycle-3 F2 resolution).**
Added 2026-05-12 cycle-3 review. When the degradation cascade runs (Codex degraded → Gemini fallback for the same cycle index), both attempt artifacts are preserved under `.ralf/<session-id>/{agent_lower}-review-{timestamp}.md`. The cycle's foreign-eyes status string reports both attempts (machine-parseable format), and the fallback's output is the canonical findings input for the cycle. The failed attempt's artifact is forensic only — it does not contribute to the convergence decision.

**D27 — F5 ralf-implement owns audit-label conditional (cycle-3 resolution).**
Added 2026-05-12 cycle-3 review. The "Audit-label scope" policy lives 50 lines below §4 in `implement-bead/SKILL.md`; §4 step 5 stamps unconditionally. R1.5 places the conditional in ralf-implement: when ralf-implement dispatches a non-named-worker doer (e.g. `general-purpose`), it either instructs §4 to skip step 5 or stamps the label itself. The upstream §4 contradiction is filed as `agents-config-go1w`. Once `go1w` lands, ralf-implement's conditional becomes documentation-only for the named-worker case (since §4 would itself condition).

**D28 — F7 R5.2 dated-design-doc exclusion (cycle-3 resolution).**
Added 2026-05-12 cycle-3 review. R5.2's grep matched 4 lines in `docs/specs/2026-04-19-ralf-it-form-reevaluation-design.md` (the predecessor design doc for the ralf-* skills) — that file is HISTORICAL and predates this rename. Excluding `docs/specs/` wholesale would mis-exclude active specs (e.g. `worker-report-v1.md`). The R5.2 exclusion is narrowed to the dated-design-doc pattern `^docs/specs/YYYY-MM-DD-*-design.md`. R5.1 is unchanged (it already passes against the current tree).

**D29 — F10 `<agent-name>` identifier normalization (cycle-3 resolution).**
Added 2026-05-12 cycle-3 review. R1.5, D21, and the AC bullet now uniformly use `<agent-name>` (matching `worker-report-v1.md` §3 and `implement-bead/SKILL.md` §4) rather than `<agent>`. Trivial but prevents copy-paste drift.

**D30 — CRITICAL R4.2 fix-bug prose mismatch (cycle-4 resolution).**
Added 2026-05-12 cycle-4 review. The cycle-3 R4.2 quoted the implement-feature prose verbatim and prescribed it for BOTH formula files. But fix-bug.formula.toml's prose differs — it propagates `the root-cause note` as a leading argument (per the §6 upstream-report retrieval chain shipped in 19.3). Applying R4.2 literally to fix-bug would silently delete the root-cause-note plumbing. R4.2 is now split into two per-file prescriptive replacements that preserve the root-cause-note argument in the fix-bug form.

**D31 — R1.5 audit-label conditional is documentation-only until `go1w` (cycle-4 resolution).**
Added 2026-05-12 cycle-4 review. The cycle-3 spec said ralf-implement "either invokes §4 with a directive to skip step 5 OR stamps the label itself after §4 returns" — but §4 has no skip directive and post-return stamping cannot retract an already-applied label. Both options are unimplementable today without modifying §4 (which D3 forbids in this PR). Resolution: in 19.7's deliverable, ralf-implement DOCUMENTS the policy and names `go1w` as the upstream tracking item. `general-purpose` dispatches via §4 WILL receive `worker-audit-general-purpose-iter<N>` until `go1w` lands — accepting this is the explicit cost of not modifying §4 in this PR.

**D32 — Argument syntax canonicalization (cycle-4 resolution).**
Added 2026-05-12 cycle-4 review. The cycle-3 spec used `subagent_type:tdd-green-team` (colon-no-space) in R4.2 but `non_interactive: true` (colon-space-value) in R1.11. The orchestrator LLM is the parser — consistent shape prevents drift. R1.11 now canonicalizes on `<key>: <value>` (colon-space-value, YAML-style) for all caller invocation prose, and R4.2's prescriptive replacements use the canonical form.

**D33 — Inline-paste mechanism operational procedure (cycle-4 resolution).**
Added 2026-05-12 cycle-4 review. The cycle-3 D24 described the inline-paste OUTCOME ("ralf-implement inline-pastes the template body") but not the PROCEDURE. R1.9 now spells out the three operational steps: (1) `Read` the template by relative path; (2) substitute uppercase-bracket placeholders with caller-supplied inputs; (3) dispatch via `Agent({subagent_type, prompt: <substituted-body>})`. Templates are read fresh on each dispatch.

**D34 — `simplify` trigger N/A case (cycle-4 resolution).**
Added 2026-05-12 cycle-4 review. The cycle-3 D11 made the simplify trigger criterion measurable (re-run test command after simplify's edits). But the gate=n/a branch — docs/config repos where `evidence.tests.command` is empty or absent — had no defined behavior. Resolution: when no test command exists, accept simplify's output without a re-run and converge. The regression check is N/A; fresh-eyes review remains the convergence signal.

**D35 — Malformed evidence sub-blocks (cycle-4 resolution).**
Added 2026-05-12 cycle-4 review. R1.6's status-first gate (cycle-3 fix for F3) is sound, but `worker-report-v1.md` §4's existing synthesis rules only fire on (i) crash, (ii) unparseable YAML, (iii) missing `status`, (iv) missing per-agent runtime-required field. A `status: complete` report with corrupt `evidence` sub-blocks (e.g., `evidence.tests` missing required `command`) parses cleanly and routes to R1.6's convergence path. New R1.4.1 synthesizes `status: failed` on §1.1 schema violations within `evidence` sub-blocks, closing the tunnel under the status-first gate.

**D36 — Cascade applied to ralf-implement (cycle-4 resolution).**
Added 2026-05-12 cycle-4 review. The cycle-3 spec added the degradation cascade (Codex→Gemini→pure-Claude) in R2.2.1 context; ralf-implement retained the older "degrade cleanly to pure fresh-eyes" line. R1.8 now adopts the cascade for ralf-implement; evidence-store canonicalization applies (D26). The existing ralf-implement/SKILL.md "degrade cleanly to pure fresh-eyes" line is superseded. (ralf-review cascade handling is deferred to abn9.13.)

**D37 — Grep run-from-where preamble (cycle-4 resolution).**
Added 2026-05-12 cycle-4 review. R5.1 / R5.2 path-anchored exclusions (`^docs/plans/`, etc.) are relative to grep's start directory. R5 now states explicitly: run from the repository root. Worktrees still work (they are their own root) but the exclusion patterns assume top-level path structure.

## Open Questions

None. All design decisions resolved. Scope reduced 2026-05-15: R2 (`ralf-review` SKILL.md) and R3.2 (`ralf-review/` template rename) deferred to `agents-config-abn9.13` (`ralf-spec`); see D2, D14, D16 for rationale.
acceptance_criteria:
- ralf-implement/SKILL.md updated to consume worker-report-v1 (evidence blocks + derived gate roll-up per worker-report-v1.md §1.1); dispatches inner doer via Agent tool with required subagent_type; references implement-bead/SKILL.md §4 per-dispatch primitive via informal section reference (not inlined); synthesizes status:failed on crash or malformed worker output per worker-report-v1.md §4.
- ralf-implement/SKILL.md interactive subagent_type prompt offers tdd-green-team (canonical) and general-purpose (Claude Code builtin fallback) only; tdd-red-team and bug-diagnoser are NOT offered because their gate shapes (fail / single-shot) do not match the convergence predicate. User declining both prompt options causes ralf-implement to abort with a diagnostic. Non-interactive context (caller-supplied non_interactive:true argument OR RALF_NONINTERACTIVE=1 env) with missing subagent_type fails fast with the same diagnostic — no prompt.
- ralf-implement/SKILL.md documents the worker-audit-<agent-name>[-iter<N>] policy: the label SHOULD apply only to named workers covered by implement-bead/SKILL.md "Audit-label scope" (tdd-red-team, tdd-green-team, bug-diagnoser); general-purpose and other non-worker doers SHOULD NOT carry worker-audit-*. The policy is documentation-only in 19.7's deliverable — §4 currently stamps unconditionally and ralf-implement does not retract labels post-§4-return; this is filed as upstream tracking item agents-config-go1w. Until go1w lands, general-purpose dispatches via §4 WILL receive worker-audit-general-purpose-iter<N> labels (acknowledged in spec).
- ralf-implement/SKILL.md explicit convergence predicate enumerates BOTH status and derived-gate signals: status=failed (real or synthesized crash/malformed) → NOT a convergence candidate, regardless of derived gate; status=complete + gate=pass → convergence candidate if fresh-eyes finds no blocking/critical; status=complete + gate=n/a → convergence candidate; fresh-eyes alone decides convergence; status=complete + gate=fail|partial → not a candidate, iterate. PASS_WITH_RESERVATIONS at cycle cap with status=complete AND gate∈{pass,n/a} AND no blocking/critical; FAIL otherwise. Convergence-candidate definition: iteration where status=complete AND gate ∈ {pass, n/a}. R1.4.1 extends the synthesis path: malformed evidence sub-blocks (e.g. evidence.tests missing required command/exit_code/skipped per worker-report-v1.md §1.1) cause synthesized status=failed with escalations[].reason "Worker emitted malformed report" — closes the tunnel under the status-first gate.
- ralf-implement/SKILL.md per-cycle loop: quality-reviewer + simplify run ONLY on convergence candidates (status=complete AND gate ∈ {pass, n/a}), not on status=failed iterations or on gate∈{fail,partial} iterations. simplify advisory-only with measurable trigger: after simplify lands edits, re-run the worker-report's evidence.tests.command — any previously-passing test now failing causes simplify's output to be rejected and the loop restarts via a new worker dispatch; tests still passing causes simplify's output to be accepted and the iteration converges. N/A case: if evidence.tests.command is empty or absent (gate=n/a docs/config repo), accept simplify's output without re-run and converge — fresh-eyes review is the convergence signal in that branch. verify-checklist removed as a per-iteration step (test/build/lint/typecheck evidence derived from worker-report-v1 evidence blocks; quality-reviewer + simplify on convergence candidate only); default cycle cap stays at RALF_IMPLEMENT_DEFAULT_CYCLES=3; cycle sequence: 1=Codex(gpt-5.5), 2=Gemini, 3+=pure-Claude (matches existing skill semantics for extended cycles). Degradation cascade (D26): Codex degraded → Gemini fallback for that cycle index → pure-Claude only if Gemini also degrades; evidence-store canonicalization applies (both attempt artifacts persist; fallback output is canonical).
- ralf-implement/SKILL.md iteration-routing section references each prompt template by concrete relative path AS DOCUMENTATION REFERENCES. At dispatch time ralf-implement performs the operational procedure: (1) Read the named template file by its relative path inside the installed skill directory; (2) substitute uppercase-bracket placeholders (e.g. [PASTE ORIGINAL DOD], [PASTE ORIGINAL SPEC], [PASTE CONTEXT]) with caller-supplied input strings; (3) dispatch via Agent({subagent_type: <doer>, prompt: <substituted-body>}). Templates are read fresh on each dispatch — no caching. Templates: ./subagent-implementer.md (initial-cycle dispatch body), ./subagent-fresh-eyes.md (pure-Claude cycles body), ./subagent-foreign-cycle.md (foreign-augmented cycles body), ./foreign-cli-instructions.md (instruction file read by the foreign-cycle Claude subagent from its repo-relative path inside the worktree and piped to codex-companion.mjs task / gemini on stdin — NOT inline-pasted into an Agent-tool prompt).
- ralf-implement/SKILL.md documents ralf:required label semantics as the canonical home.
- Implementation ordering: R3.1 renames complete first (ralf-implement/). R3.2 (ralf-review/ rename) and R2.3 (new ralf-review/ files) are deferred to agents-config-abn9.13.
- ralf-implement/ prompt-template renames per Scheme A: implementer-prompt.md → subagent-implementer.md; fresh-eyes-prompt.md → subagent-fresh-eyes.md; foreign-eyes-prompt.md → subagent-foreign-cycle.md; foreign-agent-prompt.md → foreign-cli-instructions.md. Old filenames do NOT exist on disk after the rename (git mv or equivalent; not copy).
- After rename, no active template, skill file, or formula file references the four old template filenames (implementer-prompt.md, fresh-eyes-prompt.md, foreign-eyes-prompt.md, foreign-agent-prompt.md). Intra-template references are updated (e.g. subagent-foreign-cycle.md's body references ./foreign-cli-instructions.md, not ./foreign-agent-prompt.md).
- implement-feature.formula.toml and fix-bug.formula.toml header pointer comment added: `# ralf:required label semantics: see src/user/.agents/skills/ralf-implement/SKILL.md`.
- Both formula files' green-loop dispatch prose updated PER-FILE to include `subagent_type: tdd-green-team` in the ralf-implement invocation argument list (canonical YAML-style `<key>: <value>` syntax per R1.11). implement-feature.formula.toml: "invoke ralf-implement with the bead spec, Definition of Done, relevant context, quality commands, and optional max cycle count from ralf:cycles=N" becomes "invoke ralf-implement with the bead spec, Definition of Done, relevant context, quality commands, `subagent_type: tdd-green-team`, and optional max cycle count from ralf:cycles=N". fix-bug.formula.toml: "invoke ralf-implement with the root-cause note, bead spec, Definition of Done, relevant context, quality commands, and optional max cycle count from ralf:cycles=N" becomes "invoke ralf-implement with the root-cause note, bead spec, Definition of Done, relevant context, quality commands, `subagent_type: tdd-green-team`, and optional max cycle count from ralf:cycles=N". The root-cause-note argument MUST be preserved in the fix-bug form (it is the §6 propagation chain from the upstream diagnose worker-report).
- bead-implementor.md no longer exists at src/plugins/beads/.agents/agents/bead-implementor.md (regression check — delivered by PR #66).

title: [Impl] fix: bd-record-decision.sh jq parse fails on bd create --json object shape
description:
# Spec — Fix bd-record-decision.sh jq parse on bd create object shape

## Summary

`bd create --json` returns a JSON object `{id, ...}` (with array-form variation by `bd` version per the inline comment at `bd-finalize-create-impl-bead.sh:185-186`). Two scripts use the brittle `jq -r '(.[0].id // .id) // empty'` pattern which throws `Cannot index object with number` because jq's `//` only catches null/false, not exceptions. Three additional sites in `brainstorm-bead.formula.toml` use `jq -r '.id'` on `bd create --json` output (single-shape; would fail if the array variant is emitted). One SKILL.md doc snippet teaches the same fragile single-shape pattern. Replace all six with explicit type-aware extraction (Option A: `if type == "array" then .[0].id else .id end // empty`).

## Background / motivation

Discovered while running step `[write-spec]` of brainstorm-bead for agents-config-fca6.1. `bd-record-decision.sh` aborted on the `bd create --type decision --json | jq ...` pipeline; manual workaround required (raw `bd create --json` + Python parse + separate `bd dep add` + `bd close`). The inline comment at `bd-finalize-create-impl-bead.sh:185-186` already documents the version-dependent dual-shape — confirming the array form is a real historical variant the fix must continue to handle.

## Requirements

R1. Replace the broken jq expression in `src/plugins/beads/.beads/scripts/bd-record-decision.sh` (the `DEC_ID=$(bd create ... --json | jq -r '(.[0].id // .id) // empty')` line) with the Option A pattern: `jq -r 'if type == "array" then .[0].id else .id end // empty'`.

R2. Apply the same Option A fix to `src/plugins/beads/.beads/scripts/bd-finalize-create-impl-bead.sh` (the `Y_ID=$(printf '%s' "$CREATE_JSON" | jq -r '(.[0].id // .id) // empty' 2>/dev/null)` line). **Retain** the dual-shape contract comment immediately above the expression. **Drop** the `2>/dev/null` redirect — Option A no longer throws on dual-shape input, so the redirect becomes a footgun masking unrelated jq errors during development.

R3. Replace the three `jq -r '.id'` extractions of `bd create --json` output in `src/plugins/beads/.beads/formulas/brainstorm-bead.formula.toml` with the same Option A pattern. Locate the sites by their opening `bd create --type task --priority "$X_PRIORITY_ESC" --title "Manual triage: ..."` invocations (three call-sites; each followed by `--json | jq -r '.id'`); patch the `jq -r '.id'` continuation on each. Do NOT modify the fire-and-forget `bd create` calls that omit `--json` or do not capture an ID.

R4. Update the `bd create --json | jq -r '.id'` example snippet in `src/plugins/beads/.agents/skills/create-bead/SKILL.md` (near L86) to teach the Option A type-aware pattern. **Add an adjacent one-line comment** in the snippet (e.g., `# dual-shape contract: bd create --json may emit either {id:...} or [{id:...}]`) so future authors understand why the extractor is verbose.

R5. Manual repro evidence in the PR description: before/after `bd create --type decision --json | <expression>` invocation output demonstrating the exception → clean ID extraction. Plus an isolation test of the patched expression against both fixtures: `printf '{"id":"x"}' | jq -r '<expression>'` and `printf '[{"id":"x"}]' | jq -r '<expression>'`. **Both must emit `x` on stdout and exit 0** — that pair locks the test against accidental regression to single-shape behavior.

R6. Both `--implemented` and `--needs-approval` branches of `bd-record-decision.sh` exercised end-to-end in manual repro (PR description captures output).

R7. Exercise the patched `bd-finalize-create-impl-bead.sh` path: either an end-to-end molecule wisp/pour invocation that hits L187, OR the isolation jq-expression test from R5 (treated as sufficient because the surrounding `[[ -z "$Y_ID" || "$Y_ID" == "null" ]]` guard is unchanged).

## Out of scope

- Adding bats / shell test framework — explicit decision: lightest tooling weight; rely on manual repro + reviewer validation.
- Auditing scripts outside `src/plugins/beads/` and the SKILL.md hit. Within `src/plugins/beads/`, the inventory below is exhaustive for the root-cause family.
- Investigating whether upstream `bd create --json` should normalize on a single shape — out of scope; the L185 comment documents callers must handle both.

## Antipattern inventory (root-cause family, all in scope)

- `src/plugins/beads/.beads/scripts/bd-record-decision.sh` — `.[0].id // .id` form (the reported bug)
- `src/plugins/beads/.beads/scripts/bd-finalize-create-impl-bead.sh` — `.[0].id // .id` form (identical)
- `src/plugins/beads/.beads/formulas/brainstorm-bead.formula.toml` (3 sites near L261/L305/L341) — `jq -r '.id'` single-shape form on `bd create --json`
- `src/plugins/beads/.agents/skills/create-bead/SKILL.md` — `jq -r '.id'` doc-snippet teaching the single-shape form

## Acceptance criteria

- `bd-record-decision.sh` runs to completion with valid inputs (no jq exception)
- Both `--implemented` and `--needs-approval` branches exercised in manual repro, output captured in PR description
- `bd-finalize-create-impl-bead.sh` jq expression patched; exercised end-to-end OR via the R5 isolation jq-expression test against object + array fixtures
- All three `bd create --json | jq -r '.id'` sites in `brainstorm-bead.formula.toml` patched to Option A
- `create-bead/SKILL.md` example snippet patched to teach Option A
- Negative grep AC: `grep -rn '\.\[0\]\.id // \.id' src/plugins/beads/` returns **no matches**
- Allow-list audit AC: every `bd create --json` capture site in `src/plugins/beads/` is enumerated in the PR description and confirmed to use the Option A type-aware extractor. Capture sites (exhaustive at spec time): `bd-record-decision.sh` (1), `bd-finalize-create-impl-bead.sh` (1), `brainstorm-bead.formula.toml` (3), `create-bead/SKILL.md` (1) = **6 total**. PR description must confirm each. (Line-oriented grep cannot pair multi-line `bd create | jq` pipelines, so an allow-list audit replaces a second negative grep.)
- The dual-shape contract comment at `bd-finalize-create-impl-bead.sh:185-186` is retained (or moved/updated to remain adjacent to the new expression)
- Build passes. Typecheck passes. Tests pass.

## Design notes

- **Option A (if/else) chosen over Option B (try/catch).** Rationale: `bd-finalize-create-impl-bead.sh:185-186` already carries a comment documenting the dual-shape contract — explicit `if type == "array"` reads as the contract enforcement; `try/catch` reads as defensive fallback. Repo style values explicit dual-shape handling.
- **Comment retention (R2 / final AC):** the L185-186 comment is evidence the dual-shape variant is real, not myth. Spec keeps Option A's dual-shape handling AND retains the comment.
- **Dead-code guard at `bd-finalize-create-impl-bead.sh:189`:** the `[[ -z "$Y_ID" || "$Y_ID" == "null" ]]` belt-and-suspenders guard becomes partially dead under `// empty` (the `"null"` branch is unreachable). **Retain the guard unchanged** — defensive, harmless, eliminates a coin-flip review question.
- **Test-approach decision:** manual repro in PR description + isolation jq-expression test against object/array fixtures. No bash test framework exists in repo; adding one for this fix is disproportionate.
- **Scope decisions:** SKILL.md doc-snippet and formula.toml runtime config both in scope for this PR, after the cycle-1 review surfaced the formula.toml sites as the same root-cause family. Keeps the antipattern family closed in one patch; avoids leaving land mines in molecule-pour paths.
- **Line numbers in this spec are nearest-anchor pointers** (the implementer should locate by expression/context, not by exact line, because edits within the same file will shift lines).

## Follow-up (housekeeping bead to file)

- Repo-wide audit for any further `jq -r '.id'` or `.[0].id // .id` extractions of `bd create --json` output outside `src/plugins/beads/`, plus a lint/CI guard so the pattern cannot reappear. Track as a separate P3 bead; not in this PR.

## Open questions

None.
acceptance_criteria: bd-record-decision.sh runs to completion with valid inputs (no jq exception). Both --implemented and --needs-approval branches exercised in manual repro, output captured in PR description. bd-finalize-create-impl-bead.sh jq expression patched (2>/dev/null redirect dropped); exercised end-to-end OR via R5 isolation jq-expression test against object + array fixtures. All three bd create --json | jq -r '.id' sites in brainstorm-bead.formula.toml patched to Option A. create-bead/SKILL.md example snippet patched to teach Option A with an adjacent dual-shape comment. Negative grep AC: '\.\[0\]\.id // \.id' returns no matches under src/plugins/beads/. Allow-list audit AC: PR description enumerates all 6 bd create --json capture sites in src/plugins/beads/ and confirms each uses Option A. Dual-shape contract comment at bd-finalize-create-impl-bead.sh:185-186 retained. R5 isolation tests emit 'x' on stdout and exit 0 for both object and array fixtures. Build passes. Typecheck passes. Tests pass.
notes:
verify-ac complete — all 7 mechanical AC met. Allow-list audit AC deferred to PR description at create-pr.
review-cycle round 3 hard cap reached. One round-3 Copilot comment flipped to ESCALATE (cap-exceeded). Comment 3228680861 (bd-finalize-create-impl-bead.sh:189): jq error handler suppresses stderr (2>/dev/null) and dumps full CREATE_JSON; consider trimmed diagnostic. Deferred to human reviewer for follow-up. Three other round-3 comments (3228680939 project-config vs spec doc, 3228680975 caveman persistence inconsistency, 3228681017 formula cycle-cap scope) classified SKIP — pre-existing scope or repeats of round 1/2 objections already addressed in PR description.
Round-4 follow-up: addressed the previously cap-exceeded ESCALATE (Copilot comment 3228680861, bd-finalize noisy stderr). Commit 005dd894 drops 2>/dev/null so jq's parse error surfaces, replaces full-CREATE_JSON dump with terse pointer. Replied to thread + resolved via GraphQL. All 12 actionable comments now committed; 9 SKIP threads remain intentionally open (pre-existing-scope rationale).
