title: Codify post-merge cleanup rules + audit memory for redundancy
description:
During PR #44 squash merge, agent hit three avoidable git/gh noise points beyond the expected admin override:

1. `gh pr merge --delete-branch` failed local branch-delete because shell was inside the worktree using that branch.
2. `git push` rejected from origin/main divergence after squash merge (origin had the squash commit, local main did not).
3. `git pull --rebase` aborted from unstaged `.beads/issues.jsonl` working-tree changes (auto-exported by bd operations).

Existing memory `feedback_pr_merge_worktree_cleanup.md` anticipated #1 but wasn't surfaced as a rule — agents don't proactively recall memories before action. Promoting these into instruction files prevents the noise at the source.

Bonus rule violation: agent committed beads update directly to main and pushed (succeeded only via admin bypass). Need carve-out language or redirect to `bd dolt push` for mechanical beads sync.
acceptance_criteria:
1) Worktree-merge-ordering rule added to src/user/.claude/rules/worktrees.md template, covering both orderings: (a) pre-emptive — before 'gh pr merge --delete-branch', agent checks worktree cleanliness ('git -C .claude/worktrees/<branch> status --porcelain'); if clean, runs 'git worktree remove .claude/worktrees/<branch>'; if dirty, ESCALATES to user (do not use --force without explicit user authorization — that destroys uncommitted work); THEN runs gh pr merge; (b) recovery — if error already happened (merge succeeded, local branch deletion failed), sequence: worktree remove (after confirming clean or stashing) → 'git branch -D <branch>' → pull main; both sequences documented with exact command shapes

2) Post-squash-merge sync rule added to src/user/.claude/rules/delivery.md template: after a GitHub squash-merge, local main is behind origin by exactly the squash commit; before ANY further commits or pushes (including beads reconciliation), agent runs 'git pull --ff-only'; if ff-only fails (local main has diverging commits — which is itself a sign something went wrong earlier), use 'git pull --rebase'; explicitly states committing FIRST and pulling SECOND is wrong and causes push-rejection; notes that --ff-only fails gracefully (non-zero exit, no state change) and is safe to run speculatively; non-squash-merge cases (rebase-merge, regular merge) also use 'git pull --ff-only' as the safe default

3) Beads config recommendation rule added to src/plugins/beads/.claude/rules/beads.md with two parts: (i) AFFIRMATIVE POLICY — for any project using beads with dolt server mode AND sync.remote configured, recommended baseline is 'export.auto = false' AND 'import.auto = false'; implementer must verify import.auto is a valid key via 'bd config show | grep import.auto' before documenting (export.auto is stored in config.yaml; import.auto is stored in the beads DB via 'bd config set import.auto false'); (ii) FRICTION DETECTION + OFFER — when concrete signals appear (rebase abort citing .beads/issues.jsonl; git status shows unstaged .beads/issues.jsonl blocking pull/merge; post-merge JSONL diff with no semantic bead changes), agent verifies dolt+sync.remote ('bd config show | grep sync.remote'), then offers to set the flags, applies only on user authorization

4) Manual post-merge export workflow documented in src/plugins/beads/.claude/rules/beads.md (with one-line cross-reference from delivery.md's post-merge section): executed OUTSIDE merged PR's worktree, from main branch: (a) 'bd export -o .beads/issues.jsonl'; (b) 'git add .beads/issues.jsonl'; (c) commit with 'chore(beads):' prefix — NORMATIVE (the carve-out gate); wording after prefix is EXEMPLARY; (d) verify with 'git diff --name-only HEAD~1..HEAD' that ALL changed paths are under .beads/ and do NOT include .beads/config.yaml; (e) push directly to main per AC#5 carve-out; (f) smoke-test result documented in a comment in the rule file itself (not only in jp9w notes, which will be compacted)

5) chore(beads): direct-push-to-main carve-out documented in BOTH src/plugins/beads/.claude/rules/beads.md (full conditions) AND src/user/.claude/rules/delivery.md (explicit exception note in 'REQUIRES EXPLICIT AUTHORIZATION' section, pointing to beads.md for conditions); carve-out conditions — BOTH must hold: (1) ALL changed paths are under .beads/ AND .beads/config.yaml is NOT among the changed paths (config changes require PR review); verify with 'git diff --name-only origin/main..HEAD'; AND (2) commit message begins 'chore(beads):'; one condition alone is not sufficient

6) Memory feedback_pr_merge_worktree_cleanup.md disposed per criterion: if worktrees.md rule (AC#1) fully captures prescriptive content including recovery sequence → DELETE; if memory contains unique incident context not in the rule → DEMOTE with note directing to worktrees.md; implementer picks one outcome and documents rationale in jp9w notes

7) Upstream beads assessment documented in jp9w notes: either (a) at least one follow-on bead filed (clear bug or non-trivial enhancement, not preference), linked from jp9w; or (b) documented determination that no filing is warranted, with rationale; candidates: 'export.auto=false default for dolt+remote setups'; 'bd doctor warning for friction-prone configs'

8) install.sh propagation verified via 'scripts/install.sh --dry-run': confirms (a) src/user/.claude/rules/ changes appear; (b) src/plugins/beads/.claude/rules/beads.md changes appear with '---' append-separator; flag confirmed to exist in AGENTS.md; actual ~/.claude/ installation is user's gate

9) Scope: files changed limited to exactly: src/user/.claude/rules/worktrees.md, src/user/.claude/rules/delivery.md, src/plugins/beads/.claude/rules/beads.md, feedback_pr_merge_worktree_cleanup.md (deleted or demoted per AC#6); no other files
notes:
## Summary

Codify post-merge cleanup, beads/git interaction, and direct-push-to-main carve-out rules into the project's rule templates so agents reach for the canonical pattern automatically. Eliminate three recurring git/gh friction points encountered during PR squash merges, and route the JSONL auto-export issue through a config change rather than a workaround. Investigate beads upstream first; file enhancement/bug beads where the friction reflects beads ergonomics.

## Background

PR #44's squash merge surfaced four avoidable friction points:

1. 'gh pr merge --delete-branch' failed local branch deletion because shell sat inside the worktree using that branch.
2. 'git push' rejected post-squash because origin/main had the squash commit while local main did not.
3. 'git pull --rebase' aborted because '.beads/issues.jsonl' was unstaged from prior bd auto-export.
4. Beads reconciliation commit was pushed directly to main, succeeded only via admin bypass — rule technically violated, correct shape of the carve-out undocumented.

Existing memory feedback_pr_merge_worktree_cleanup.md anticipated #1 but lived as backing context only — agents do not proactively consult memory before action. Memories must be promoted to rule files (auto-loaded into agent context).

## Architectural pivot for the JSONL friction

Decision (user-confirmed during brainstorming): for any project using beads with dolt server mode + sync.remote configured, the canonical configuration is 'export.auto=false' AND 'import.auto=false'. With dolt remote configured, dolt itself provides version tracking; the JSONL becomes a periodic snapshot artifact rather than a live mirror, regenerated manually after merge events. This eliminates the auto-export/git contention entirely.

Verified during brainstorming: this project already has both flags set; probes confirmed bd write ops do NOT touch the JSONL during a session. The remaining staged diff at session start was historical residue from before the flag flip.

Downsides accepted: offline consumers without Dolt see stale JSONL between manual exports. Acceptable for current usage model.

## Plugin architecture finding

beads.md lives in src/plugins/beads/.claude/rules/, not src/user/.claude/rules/. install.sh appends plugin rule content to base rule content (same filename, '---' separator) when staging to ~/.claude/rules/. Therefore:
- Beads-coupled rules (config flags, manual export workflow, chore(beads) carve-out) → plugin tree
- Tool-base rules (worktree merge ordering, post-squash pull) → user tree

This refines the original bead's blanket 'rules land in src/user/.claude/rules/' constraint.

## Out of scope

- Implementing the upstream beads enhancements (filed as follow-on beads, not built here)
- Migrating .beads/issues.jsonl out of git tracking (would break offline visibility for non-Dolt consumers)
- Modifying superpowers skills (using-git-worktrees, finishing-a-development-branch) — those are external; we add complementary rules
- Codex/Gemini/OpenCode rule equivalents — current rules live in src/user/.claude/rules/ only; cross-tool propagation is a separate concern

## Design notes

- AC#3 (config recommendation) and AC#4 (manual export workflow) are complementary, not redundant: AC#3 is the agent's recognition + offer pattern (when to flip the flags); AC#4 is the workflow that runs even with the flags flipped (because the JSONL still needs periodic refresh).
- AC#5 carve-out is path-restricted (.beads/ only) AND message-prefix-restricted (chore(beads):) so it is automation-checkable. This matches the existing git history pattern (commits 0f8e604, 0431762, 16c2b11, d412dd4 all follow this shape).
- AC#7 (file upstream first) is honored by making upstream filings a deliverable of jp9w itself: investigate during implementation, file beads, link from jp9w notes. Implementation of those upstream-fixes is NOT in jp9w's scope.
- 'Exit worktree before --delete-branch' rule covers two cases: pre-emptive ordering (preferred) and post-error recovery (worktree remove + branch -D + pull).
- For AC#3 friction-detection, signals include: 'git pull --rebase' aborts on .beads/issues.jsonl; pre-rebase shows .beads/ in unstaged changes; agent observes JSONL diff after a sequence of bd write ops.

## Investigation list (do during implementation)

- Confirm 'bd config set export.auto false --update-config' is the right CLI invocation (vs editing config.yaml directly)
- Confirm the same for 'import.auto'
- Read src/plugins/beads/.claude/rules/beads.md to see current content shape and append/edit appropriately
- Verify install.sh handles both src/user/ and src/plugins/beads/ rules paths correctly (the existing install.sh-backup-is-now-path-aware bd-memory suggests the namespace is registered)
- Check if 'beads.md' is already among install.sh's known rule files (it is, given current ~/.claude/rules/beads.md exists)
- Identify upstream filing candidates that pass the bar of 'a non-trivial enhancement or a clear bug, not a personal-preference change'

## Open questions

None — all material decisions resolved during the discuss step.
## RALF Review Outcomes (2 cycles)

Cycle 1 (1 critical, 5 major, 4 minor) → revised spec. Cycle 2 (1 critical, 3 major, 3 minor) → final.

### Fixed before finalize
- AC#5 'BOTH must hold' self-contradiction resolved: .beads/config.yaml exclusion is now stated as a modifier inside condition (1), not a third gate
- delivery.md cross-reference to carve-out now explicitly required
- Trigger signals for AC#3 made concrete (three named signals, not 'friction observed')
- Dirty-worktree-before-merge escalation added to AC#1
- AC#4/AC#5 git-diff commands reconciled: HEAD~1..HEAD for post-commit, origin/main..HEAD for pre-push

### Implementation guidance (from cycle 2 M-findings — address during implementation, not spec revision)
- M1: AC#4 uses HEAD~1..HEAD; AC#5 uses origin/main..HEAD; both are intentionally different moments — document this in the rule so implementers don't conflate them
- M2: Before removing a worktree, run 'git -C .claude/worktrees/<branch> status --porcelain'; if non-empty, escalate to user rather than using --force
- M3: Verify import.auto semantics before writing AC#3(i) into the rule — run 'bd config show | grep import' and confirm the flag is valid and where it's stored (likely Dolt DB, not config.yaml)
- m2 (smoke-test): document the smoke-test result in a comment in the rule file itself, not only in jp9w notes (jp9w will be compacted)

title: start-bead and implement-bead must treat implementation-ready + human as 'paused, do not run'
description:
A bead can legitimately carry both `implementation-ready` AND `human` labels simultaneously — this happens when a previously-ready bead is escalated mid-flight. The combination is valid state. Today, neither start-bead nor implement-bead (and by extension run-queue) explicitly handles this combination. Risk: the run-queue picks up the bead via implementation-ready and an agent resumes work while the blocker is still unresolved, defeating the point of the human escalation.

## Two flag mechanisms to check

Human escalation uses two distinct mechanisms — both must be detected:
1. **Label**: `bd label list <id>` shows human
2. **Gate**: `bd gate list --bead <id>` shows an unresolved human gate

Either condition means the bead is paused. Checking only for the label is insufficient.

## Required behavior

`start-bead` and `implement-bead` MUST detect BOTH mechanisms at the routing/dispatch decision point and refuse to proceed with implementation work. Concretely:

1. **Detection** — check for (a) human label AND (b) unresolved human gate simultaneously, regardless of implementation-ready status.
2. **Behavior** — do NOT dispatch worker agents. Do NOT advance molecule steps. Surface the bead's human notes / blocker context and direct the operator to `/resolve-human-bead <id>` (skill 7bk.13).
3. **Run-queue** — skips beads carrying human label or unresolved human gate, regardless of other labels.
4. **Resumption contract** — work may resume only after BOTH conditions clear: (a) blocker resolved (via `bd human respond`, `bd human dismiss`, or `bd gate resolve` as appropriate) AND (b) no remaining human label or unresolved gate.

## Acceptance Criteria

- `start-bead` skill explicitly documents and handles the `implementation-ready` + human combination (label OR gate), refusing to route to implement-bead while either is present. Surfaces `/resolve-human-bead` as the resolution path.
- `implement-bead` skill (and its pour/wisp probes) refuses to start or continue when human label is present or an unresolved human gate exists, even if implementation-ready is also present.
- Run-queue scan logic skips beads carrying human label or unresolved human gate, regardless of other labels.
- Documented in CLAUDE rules and/or skill bodies.
- Build passes. Typecheck passes. Tests pass.

## Why P0

A run-queue that ignores human-escalation flags is a pipeline that overrides human judgment silently — exactly the failure mode the human flag exists to prevent.
acceptance_criteria:
start-bead probes self-and-ancestor chain via structural parent field only (dep-edges not walked) for human label OR unresolved human gate before routing
start-bead refuses all routes (A/B/C) while any ancestor is human-paused; surface message names the flagged ancestor and gives /resolve-human-bead <flagged-ancestor-id> as primary path plus bd human respond/bd gate resolve as fallback if 7bk.13 is unavailable
implement-bead probes at pour time and before each worker dispatch / after worker returns; refuses to continue while any ancestor is human-paused; parks cleanly (molecule stays in_progress, note appended, parent-chain claims not reversed)
implement-bead and start-bead surface messages include manual escape primitives (bd human respond / bd human dismiss / bd gate resolve) for when /resolve-human-bead is unavailable
run-queue scan precomputes human-flagged set once per loop iteration (one bd list --label human + one bd gate list call), not O(N*D) individual CLI calls; skips beads whose self-and-ancestor chain intersects the set
run-queue logs one line per skipped bead in format: [run-queue] SKIP <bead-id> human-paused at <flagged-ancestor-id> (reason: label|gate)
Implementation Step 0: schema discovery — bd gate list --json --all run against live DB or test fixture; jq filter field names documented inline in SKILL files with schema evidence in PR description; if gate typing unconfirmable, label-only detection ships and a new bead tracks gate detection
SKILL files contain the literal jq filter used for gate detection with inline comment showing verified field names
All three trigger conditions verified independently: (a) human label only, (b) unresolved human gate only, (c) both present
Depends on 7bk.13 (/resolve-human-bead) landing first; surface message references the skill by name
Build passes. Typecheck passes. Tests pass.
notes:
## Summary

Add a human-pause gate to start-bead, implement-bead, and run-queue that detects the human label OR an unresolved human gate on the target bead OR any of its self-and-ancestors (via structural parent chain only), and refuses to proceed with implementation work while either condition holds. Surfaces which ancestor is flagged and directs the operator to `/resolve-human-bead <flagged-ancestor-id>` (7bk.13) as the resolution path.

## Background / Motivation

A bead can legitimately carry both `implementation-ready` AND `human` simultaneously — this happens when a previously-ready bead is escalated mid-flight. Without an explicit gate, run-queue picks up the bead via `implementation-ready` and resumes work while the blocker is unresolved, silently overriding human judgment.

## Requirements

1. **Self-and-ancestor probe** — Walk the target bead, then its parent, then grandparent, up to root via the structural `parent` field only. Dep-edges (blocks, discovered-from, etc.) are NOT walked — those are enforced by `bd ready`'s readiness check and are out of scope here. At each node check:
   - (a) `bd label list <id>` contains `human`
   - (b) `bd gate list --json` filtered to any human-type gate in non-terminal status for that bead id
   Either condition at any level = paused. Report which bead triggered.

   **Schema discovery (Step 0 for implementer):** Before writing the jq filter for (b), run `bd gate list --json --all` and document the actual field names (bead reference, type, status values). If no gates exist in the current DB, create a test human gate to inspect the schema. If gate typing (a `type=human` or equivalent taxonomy) cannot be confirmed, restrict detection to label-only and file a discovered-work bead for gate-based detection. Do not ship an unverified jq filter.

2. **start-bead gate** — Add probe between Step 2 (molecule probe) and Step 3 (routing). If paused: refuse all routes (A, B, C). Do NOT create a molecule. Print message:
   ```
   PAUSED: <bead-id> is blocked — <flagged-ancestor-id> has human flag (label|gate).
   Resolve via: /resolve-human-bead <flagged-ancestor-id>
   If /resolve-human-bead is unavailable: bd human respond <flagged-ancestor-id> --response "..."
   or bd gate resolve <gate-id> as appropriate. Do NOT use bare bd label remove.
   ```

3. **implement-bead gate** — Add probe (a) at pour time before dispatching any workers, AND (b) before dispatching each worker / before resuming after any worker returns. Mid-worker interruption is explicitly out of scope — in-flight subagent work completes before the pause takes effect (race accepted; see Design Decisions). If paused:
   - Do NOT dispatch next worker.
   - Append to bead notes: `Paused at <step-id> due to human flag on <flagged-id> at <timestamp>`
   - Molecule status stays `in_progress`. Parent-chain claims are NOT reversed.
   - Print same surface message as start-bead gate above.

4. **run-queue pre-filter** — Precompute the human-flagged set once per scan loop:
   ```
   HUMAN_BEADS=
   GATE_BEADS=
   ```
   For each implementation-ready bead, walk its self-and-ancestor chain and check membership in HUMAN_BEADS or GATE_BEADS. Exclude if any match. Log one line per skipped bead:
   `[run-queue] SKIP <bead-id> human-paused at <flagged-ancestor-id> (reason: label|gate)`

## Out of Scope

- Resolution logic — that is 7bk.13's job
- Changes to bd CLI itself (no new bd flags)
- Worktree/state cleanup for mid-flight paused work (7bk.13 resume scenario)
- `bd human <id>` folklore cleanup — tracked as gcg (separate chore)
- Blocking via dep-edges other than parent chain

## Detection Probe — Canonical Shape

```
fn is_human_paused(id):
  # 'id' = the bead being routed/worked; walks self-and-ancestors via parent field only
  while id:
    if bd label list id contains 'human': return PAUSED(id)
    if bd gate list --json filtered by (bead=id AND type=human AND status=non-terminal) is non-empty: return PAUSED(id)
    id = bd show id --json | .parent  # empty string when root reached
  return CLEAR
```

Gate status "non-terminal" and field names MUST be verified against live schema before coding (see Req 1 Step 0).

## Park Semantics (implement-bead)

When the gate fires mid-molecule:
- Molecule status stays `in_progress` (NOT reverted to open)
- Parent-chain claims are NOT reversed
- Current in-flight worker (if any) finishes its active operation; next dispatch is blocked
- Orchestrator appends: `bd update <bead-id> --append-notes "Paused at <step-id> due to human flag on <flagged-id> at <ISO-timestamp>"`
- Resume is owned by 7bk.13 (/resolve-human-bead)

## Design Decisions

1. **Self-and-ancestor chain via parent field only** — dep-edges are not walked. blocker dep semantics are already enforced by `bd ready`. Walking the full dep graph would make the gate both unpredictable and expensive.

2. **Probe placement in implement-bead** — At pour time AND before each worker dispatch / after worker returns. Mid-worker interruption is out of scope (would require bd CLI hooks). Race acknowledged: a `human` label added while a worker is actively running will take effect at the next orchestrator boundary, not instantly.

3. **No override flag** — The ONLY documented resolution path is `/resolve-human-bead` (7bk.13) or its underlying primitives if 7bk.13 is unavailable. No `--force` introduced.

4. **Run-queue skip logged with structured format** — `[run-queue] SKIP <bead-id> human-paused at <flagged-ancestor-id> (reason: label|gate)`. Emitted to stdout. Greppable.

5. **Gate schema is discovered, not assumed** — `bd gate list --bead <id>` does not exist (flag absent). Implementer must verify schema via live DB or test fixture. Fallback: label-only detection + new bead for gate detection.

6. **Bundled delivery** — b416 depends on 7bk.13; refusal message names `/resolve-human-bead`. Skill must exist first. b416 and ffxh can implement in parallel after 7bk.13 lands.

7. **Run-queue perf** — Probe is precomputed per loop (one `bd list --label human` + one `bd gate list`) then membership-checked per bead. Not O(N*D) CLI calls.

## Files Affected

- `src/plugins/beads/.agents/skills/start-bead/SKILL.md` — gate check between Step 2 and Step 3
- `src/plugins/beads/.agents/skills/implement-bead/SKILL.md` — gate at pour and each worker dispatch
- `src/plugins/beads/.agents/skills/run-queue/SKILL.md` — pre-filter with structured log
- `src/plugins/beads/rules/beads.md` — document the pause gate invariant (plugin-scoped, not user-scoped)
close_reason: Superseded by 7bk.13 — NHEP (Human-Escalation Pattern, defined in arch doc §5.6 and src/plugins/beads/.claude/rules/beads.md) gates source beads via blocking deps on a separate human-labeled escalation bead. bd ready filters those out naturally. The pause-gate refusal logic this bead specified is no longer needed; the structural invariant 'source beads never carry human' is enforced by NHEP's escalation procedure rather than by a runtime check. Any residual defensive surface (start-bead surfacing 'this bead is blocked by a human-labeled escalation') is folded into 7bk.13's start-bead Route D.

title: Sweep stale agent/skill dispatches across the codebase to use new simplify skill and quality-reviewer agent
acceptance_criteria:
grep -rn 'code-simplifier' src/ README.md returns empty
grep -rn 'superpowers:code-reviewer' src/ README.md returns empty
11 files in scope each contain working references to simplify skill or quality-reviewer agent where stale names previously appeared (spot-check; intentional drops noted in bead comment)
agents-config-b7s closed (simplify skill exists at src/user/.agents/skills/simplify/SKILL.md per b7s locked-name constraint)
bd formula list succeeds without parse errors after formula TOML edits
bd preflight clean
notes:
# Sweep stale agent/skill dispatches across the codebase to use the new `simplify` skill and `quality-reviewer` agent

## Summary

Replace stale agent dispatches across **11 files** in this repo's source tree:
- All `code-simplifier` references (any form: agent name, agent dispatch, prose) → use the new `simplify` skill (created by agents-config-b7s; name LOCKED to `simplify` in b7s's description).
- All `superpowers:code-reviewer` references → `quality-reviewer` agent (post-merger, commit 827ef3e).

Foundation work for both agents-config-lu3.4 (which retires `ralf-it` by splitting it) AND agents-config-7bk.7 (which deletes the broken `code-simplifier` agent file). Depends on agents-config-b7s (the new `simplify` skill must exist before this bead's edits make sense). After this bead lands, the codebase is consistent: every dispatch site references the live `simplify` skill or `quality-reviewer` agent.

## Background / Motivation

Surfaced during Chunk 3 design (session 5385d9fc). Two stale-name classes break dispatches today:

1. **`superpowers:code-reviewer`** has been merged into `quality-reviewer` agent (commit 827ef3e). Old name still appears in `ralf-it/SKILL.md` and plugin formula TOMLs.
2. **`code-simplifier:code-simplifier`** is a broken agent (points at a phantom skill). Replacement target is the new portable `simplify` skill (b7s).

Why a skill, not the `/simplify` slash command:
- Slash commands can only be fired by the orchestrator session — subagents cannot.
- `/simplify` lives outside this repo (sidekick/plugin injection — see agents-config-7bk.7 NOTES). Not portable to non-sidekick installs or to Codex/Gemini.
- The new `simplify` skill (b7s) lives in `src/user/.agents/skills/simplify/` and ships via `install.sh` to all three tools. Same invocation pattern works in orchestrator, subagent, and foreign-agent contexts.

This bead does NOT create the `simplify` skill (b7s does that). It only renames dispatch sites to use it.

## Files in scope (11 files, 27 stale hits)

All paths relative to repo root. Confirmed via `grep -rn "code-simplifier\|superpowers:code-reviewer"` from repo root.

| # | File | Hits | Notes |
|---|------|------|-------|
| 1 | `src/user/.agents/skills/ralf-it/SKILL.md` | 7 (lines 51, 58, 175, 179, 242, 353, 354) | Lines 51 & 58 inside DOT graph node labels — see Implementation Notes. Line 353 is the only `superpowers:code-reviewer` hit. |
| 2 | `src/user/.claude/rules/completion-gate.md` | 2 (lines 9, 10) | This file is **append-merged** by install.sh, not replaced (see Design Notes). Edit source as usual; user may need to clean up `~/.claude/rules/completion-gate.md` manually after installer runs if duplicate entries appear. |
| 3 | `src/user/.agents/skills/test-review/SKILL.md` | 2 (lines 56, 58) | Both refer to `code-simplifier` agent in dispatch tables. |
| 4 | `src/user/.agents/skills/wait-for-pr-comments/SKILL.md` | 1 (line 360) | Inline gate sequence. |
| 5 | `src/user/.agents/skills/reply-and-resolve-pr-threads/SKILL.md` | 1 (line 15) | Negative-list prose — replace `code-simplifier` with `simplify` skill (see Implementation Notes for resolved ambiguity). |
| 6 | `src/user/.agents/agents/quality-reviewer.md` | 1 (line 134) | Workflow ordering prose. |
| 7 | `src/plugins/beads/.beads/AGENTS.md` | 1 (line 113) | Plugin-tree completion-gate description. |
| 8 | `src/plugins/beads/.beads/formulas/implement-feature.formula.toml` | 4 (lines 188, 215, 235, 237) | Live formula dispatch sites — drives bead workflows. |
| 9 | `src/plugins/beads/.beads/formulas/fix-bug.formula.toml` | 4 (lines 185, 202, 204, 206) | Live formula dispatch sites. |
| 10 | `src/plugins/beads/.beads/formulas/merge-and-cleanup.formula.toml` | 3 (lines 66, 73, 74) | Live formula dispatch sites. |
| 11 | `README.md` | 1 (line 53) | "Shipping agents" enumeration — `code-simplifier` is in the list of OUR agents. Once 7bk.7 deletes it, this enumeration must drop the entry. |

The implementer must re-grep each file before editing — line numbers may shift slightly with prior edits.

## Carved out of scope (with rationale)

- **`README.md` line 9 and `AGENTS.md` line 13** describe what the upstream `obra/superpowers` plugin provides ("Provides the skill/agent framework … code-reviewer, code-simplifier, …"). Different concern — upstream-plugin documentation accuracy. Tracked separately in **agents-config-vie**.
- **`docs/specs/2026-04-26-pr-review-skill-redesign.md`** lines 22, 106 — historical point-in-time spec record. Editing it would be revisionism. Leave alone.

## Implementation Notes (gotchas surfaced in fresh-eyes spec review)

- **DOT graph context (file 1, lines 51 & 58)**: Inside Graphviz `label="..."` strings with literal `\n` substrings (escaped, not actual newlines). Edit as a single-line string replacement — do not treat as multi-line edits. Preserve the `\n` literals.
- **"agent" noun gotcha (multiple locations)**: Sentences currently say "code-simplifier **agent**". Replacement is "the **`simplify` skill**" — drop the noun "agent" or the result reads as "simplify skill agent". Check surrounding prose for grammar.
- **Negative-reference prose (file 5, line 15) — RESOLVED**: The line currently reads `"No \`quality-reviewer\`, no \`code-simplifier\`, no \`verify-checklist\`, no \`git commit\`, no \`git push\`."` After this bead, replace `code-simplifier` with `the simplify skill`. The skill being documented (reply-and-resolve-pr-threads) doesn't run quality-gate steps, so it shouldn't run the new simplify skill either — preserving the line's intent.
- **Workflow ordering prose (file 6, line 134)**: "before code-simplifier and verify-checklist" — replace with "before the `simplify` skill and verify-checklist". Preserve sequence semantics.
- **Formula TOML dispatch lines (files 8, 9, 10)**: These are structured `Skill:` / `Agent:` directives with specific syntax. Examples to update:
  - `Agent: superpowers:code-reviewer` → `Agent: quality-reviewer`
  - `Skill: code-simplifier:code-simplifier (run as subagent)` → `Skill: simplify` (drop the `:code-simplifier` suffix and parenthetical, or restructure per current formula conventions — implementer should read the surrounding step block)
  - `Agent: code-simplifier:code-simplifier` → `Skill: simplify` (note the directive change from `Agent:` to `Skill:`)

## Requirements

### R1. Edit only repo source-of-truth files

Edit only files under `src/`, plus the in-scope `README.md`. Do NOT edit installed copies at `~/.claude/`, `~/.codex/`, `~/.gemini/`. Per `src/user/.claude/AGENTS.md`:
- `commands/`, `skills/`, `agents/` directories: collisions are FATAL install errors, so install.sh effectively replaces (or refuses).
- `rules/*.md` files: collisions APPEND with a `---` separator. The implementer should be aware that re-running `install.sh` after editing `completion-gate.md` may leave stale paragraphs in the user's `~/.claude/rules/completion-gate.md`. This is a known install.sh quirk, not a flaw in this bead's edits — the source-of-truth file is correct.

### R2. Apply the substitutions

For each in-scope file:
- All `code-simplifier` references → use the **`simplify` skill** (literal name `simplify` is locked in agents-config-b7s).
- All `superpowers:code-reviewer` references → `quality-reviewer` agent.
- For formula TOMLs (files 8, 9, 10), the directive often changes from `Agent:` to `Skill:` — read the surrounding step block to apply the right form.

### R3. b7s must land first

Verify `src/user/.agents/skills/simplify/SKILL.md` exists before applying R2. agents-config-b7s mandates the literal name `simplify` (locked in b7s's description). If b7s lands with a different name, that's a b7s bug — escalate, do not paper over by changing this bead.

### R4. Coordination with 7bk.7 — DONE

Already completed during this bead's brainstorm session: agents-config-7bk.7 notes have been updated to reflect the architecture flip (this bead owns the dispatch rewrite; 7bk.7 narrows to deleting the broken agent file). agents-config-7bk.7 now depends on agents-config-7bk.8. **No implementer action required for R4.**

### R5. Verify with grep — strict

After all edits, run from repo root:
```
grep -rn "code-simplifier" src/ README.md
grep -rn "superpowers:code-reviewer" src/ README.md
```
Both must return empty (excluding the carved-out `AGENTS.md` line 13 — but `AGENTS.md` is not in those grep paths anyway).

If any other file under `src/` or `README.md` contains a hit not listed in the in-scope table, address it (in this bead) — the sweep must be exhaustive. Do NOT extend to `docs/`, `AGENTS.md` (root), or `~/.claude/` install copies.

### R6. Spot-check positive presence

After edits, the implementer should briefly confirm each of the 27 stale-hit locations (from the staleness table) now contains a working reference, not a silent drop. No specific count assertion — prose-context variation makes counts brittle. Report any locations that were intentionally dropped vs replaced in a brief comment on this bead.

### R7. Build/typecheck/test/preflight

This is a docs/config repo per `AGENTS.md` ("No build system, tests, or linting"). Reduces to:
- `bd preflight` clean
- Manual sanity check that formula TOMLs parse (e.g., `bd formula list` succeeds without error after edits)

## Out of Scope

- Creating the `simplify` skill itself → tracked in agents-config-b7s
- Deleting the broken `code-simplifier` agent file → tracked in agents-config-7bk.7 (now depends on this bead)
- Splitting `ralf-it` into `ralf-review` + `ralf-implement` → tracked in agents-config-lu3.4 (depends on this bead)
- Auditing upstream-plugin descriptions in `README.md` line 9 / `AGENTS.md` line 13 → tracked in agents-config-vie
- Editing historical specs under `docs/specs/`
- Editing files outside repo source (`~/.claude/`, `~/.codex/`, `~/.gemini/` install copies)
- Renaming `quality-reviewer` agent itself or modifying its body — only updating dispatch references

## Acceptance Criteria

1. `grep -rn "code-simplifier" src/ README.md` returns empty.
2. `grep -rn "superpowers:code-reviewer" src/ README.md` returns empty.
3. The 11 files in scope each contain working references to either the `simplify` skill or `quality-reviewer` agent in the locations where stale names previously appeared (spot-check; report any intentional drops in a bead comment).
4. agents-config-b7s is closed (the `simplify` skill exists at `src/user/.agents/skills/simplify/SKILL.md` per b7s's locked-name constraint).
5. `bd formula list` succeeds without parse errors after formula TOML edits.
6. `bd preflight` clean.

## Design Notes

- **Source-of-truth principle**: install.sh stages from `src/` and copies to tool homes. Editing installed copies is always wrong. Per `src/user/.claude/AGENTS.md`, install.sh has different collision semantics by directory: `skills/`/`agents/`/`commands/` collisions are fatal (replace-or-refuse); `rules/*.md` collisions append. This bead's edits are correct in source either way; install-side cleanup may be needed for the `rules/` file.
- **Skill > slash command for dispatch consistency**: A skill works in every context (orchestrator, subagent, foreign agent). A slash command works only in the orchestrator session. Standardizing on the skill form means dispatch sites can be uniform without per-site "orchestrator-tier only" guards.
- **Why this bead, not absorbed into b7s or lu3.4**: b7s creates the skill; lu3.4 splits ralf-it. Sweeping stale dispatch names is a third, distinct concern that touches files neither needs to touch. Keeping it separate keeps each bead's scope clean.
- **Why the in-scope sweep is wider than originally listed**: Cycle 1 brainstorm scoped to 1 file (ralf-it). Cycle 2 fresh-eyes review surfaced 5 additional dispatch sites + 4 plugin formula TOMLs + 1 README enumeration = 11 total. The directive ("totally consistent state") justified the expansion.
- **Why README line 9 / AGENTS.md line 13 are carved out**: They describe the upstream `obra/superpowers` plugin's offering, not our dispatch sites. Filed as agents-config-vie.
- **Why docs/specs/ is carved out**: Historical snapshots. Editing them would be revisionism. The current state, captured by today's grep, is the truth.
- **Coordination with 7bk.7**: 7bk.7 originally planned to rewrite `completion-gate.md` itself. After the architecture flip (skill, not slash command), 7bk.8 is the consistent home for that rewrite. R4 documents that 7bk.7's notes have been updated and a `bd dep add 7bk.7 7bk.8` edge has been wired — preventing out-of-order execution.

## Open Questions

None remaining.
