notes:
# Spec: Remove content-destruction traps in bead formulas and skills

## Summary

Two sequential `bd update --notes=` calls in the `fix-bug` formula silently
destroy each other. Expand the scope of this bead to eliminate all content-
destruction traps across the project's beads formulas and related skills,
not just fix-bug.

## Background / motivation

Discovered while working on agents-config-smg (which was scoped to
implement-feature only).

`bd update --notes` REPLACES the bead's notes field. `bd update` has
supported `--append-notes` natively all along (appends with newline
separator). The fix-bug formula's two diagnose steps both use `--notes`,
so the second step silently destroys the first step's content.

Scott chose expanded scope (option B, session 11a30b53): audit all formulas
and skills for content-destruction traps and remove them.

### Audit results (live sites only — file-history matches ignored)

HARD TRAPS (mechanical destruction — sequential overwrites in a formula):
- `src/plugins/beads/.beads/formulas/fix-bug.formula.toml` line 54
  (reproduce step) and line 78 (root-cause step)

RISKY PATTERNS (single overwrite of pre-existing content; relies on agent
discipline to preserve context):
- `src/plugins/beads/.beads/formulas/brainstorm-bead.formula.toml` lines
  152-153 (write-spec step): `--acceptance=` and `--notes=` REPLACE any
  pre-existing content authored by create-bead or user investigation
- `src/plugins/beads/.agents/skills/create-bead/SKILL.md` line 99:
  `--notes=` on capture clobbers if create-bead is run twice

Good existing guards (keep as-is):
- `src/plugins/beads/.beads/formulas/implement-feature.formula.toml` line
  63: already warns "DO NOT call bd update --acceptance or --notes"

### Investigation options originally listed in the bead (superseded)

The original bead notes listed four fix approaches (a)-(d). All four are
workarounds for a semantic bd already supports natively via
`--append-notes`. Use the native flag wherever sequential appends are
needed.

## Requirements

### R1. fix-bug formula — replace both `--notes` calls with `--append-notes`

In `src/plugins/beads/.beads/formulas/fix-bug.formula.toml`:
- Line 54 (reproduce step):
  `bd update {{bead-id}} --notes "Repro: <steps>\nActual: ...\nExpected: ..."`
  →
  `bd update {{bead-id}} --append-notes "Repro: <steps>\nActual: ...\nExpected: ..."`
- Line 78 (root-cause step):
  `bd update {{bead-id}} --notes "Root cause: <explanation>"`
  →
  `bd update {{bead-id}} --append-notes "Root cause: <explanation>"`

### R2. brainstorm-bead formula — add "preserve prior context" guard to write-spec only

In `src/plugins/beads/.beads/formulas/brainstorm-bead.formula.toml`,
add a preamble to the `write-spec` step's `description = """..."""`
triple-quoted string, positioned IMMEDIATELY ABOVE the existing
`bd update {{bead-id}} --acceptance=...` line (currently line 152).

The guard text MUST include the exact sentinel string "REPLACES the entire
notes field" so it is greppable:

  Before writing: `--notes=` REPLACES the entire notes field, and
  `--acceptance=` REPLACES the entire acceptance-criteria field. If the
  bead currently contains prior investigation context (run `bd show <id>`
  once more if in doubt — prior context typically comes from `create-bead`
  or user-added notes before brainstorming), either (a) incorporate it
  into the spec you write below, or (b) move it to a comment via
  `bd comments add` before writing.

The `revise` step (currently lines 209-210) does NOT need this guard —
by the time revise runs, the write-spec step has already replaced any
pre-existing notes with the spec document, so there is no "prior
investigation context" left to protect. Revise legitimately overwrites
the freshly-written spec with the revised spec.

Do NOT change the overwrite mechanic of either step — the write-spec
step is the canonical spec author and replacement is intentional. Only
the guard preamble is added.

### R3. create-bead skill — switch capture to `--append-notes`, add rationale for --acceptance asymmetry

In `src/plugins/beads/.agents/skills/create-bead/SKILL.md` line 99:
- `bd update <id> --notes="<any additional context>"`
  →
- `bd update <id> --append-notes="<any additional context>"`

Line 98's `--acceptance=` stays as-is, but add a single inline comment
line immediately above or adjacent to line 98 explaining why the
asymmetry exists, e.g.:
- `# Note: bd has no --append-acceptance; a repeat create will replace AC intentionally.`

This makes the asymmetry self-documenting for future readers who
otherwise might "fix" it inconsistently.

## Out of scope

- Changing `brainstorm-bead` write-spec or revise to use `--append-notes`.
  The write-spec step IS the canonical spec author; replacement is
  intentional. Only the guard preamble is added (R2).
- Adding append-parity flags (`--append-acceptance`, `--append-design`,
  `--append-description`) to `bd` itself — that is upstream beads-plugin
  work, not ours. File as discovered work.
- Changing `implement-feature.formula.toml` — its line 63 is a warning,
  not a trap. Leave it alone.
- Migrating brainstorm-bead's spec from the `notes` field to the
  `description` field (to match `rules/beads.md` which says "specs may be
  written directly into the bead description"). This inconsistency is
  real but orthogonal to the trap fix. File as discovered work.
- Auditing file-history / paste-cache / transcripts — those are frozen
  snapshots, not live instructions.
- Automated test coverage for formula/skill content. Verification is
  manual + grep-based (see verification block in AC).

## Acceptance criteria

- fix-bug formula's reproduce + root-cause steps use `--append-notes` (not `--notes`). Grep check: `grep -cE 'bd update .* --notes "' src/plugins/beads/.beads/formulas/fix-bug.formula.toml` returns 0.
- brainstorm-bead formula's write-spec step carries the greppable guard sentinel. Grep check: `grep -c "REPLACES the entire notes field" src/plugins/beads/.beads/formulas/brainstorm-bead.formula.toml` returns >=1. The sentinel MUST appear inside the write-spec step's `description = """..."""` block, positioned above the `bd update {{bead-id}} --acceptance=...` line.
- brainstorm-bead's revise step is NOT modified (no guard added there; revise legitimately overwrites).
- create-bead SKILL.md uses `--append-notes` for notes capture on what was line 99 (or wherever it migrates). Grep check: `grep -cE 'bd update <id> --notes=' src/plugins/beads/.agents/skills/create-bead/SKILL.md` returns 0.
- create-bead SKILL.md contains a one-line inline comment near the `--acceptance=` invocation explaining the absence of `--append-acceptance`. Grep check: `grep -c "append-acceptance" src/plugins/beads/.agents/skills/create-bead/SKILL.md` returns >=1.
- Manual verification — reproducible command block:

  ```bash
  # 1. Create throwaway bead
  THROW=$(bd create --title="throwaway: hft repro verification" --type=bug --priority=4 --json | jq -r '.id')

  # 2. Simulate reproduce step
  bd update "$THROW" --append-notes "Repro: SENTINEL-REPRO"

  # 3. Simulate root-cause step
  bd update "$THROW" --append-notes "Root cause: SENTINEL-ROOT-CAUSE"

  # 4. Confirm BOTH sentinels survive
  HITS=$(bd show "$THROW" --json | jq -r '.[0].notes' | grep -cE "SENTINEL-REPRO|SENTINEL-ROOT-CAUSE")
  [ "$HITS" = "2" ] && echo "PASS" || echo "FAIL"

  # 5. Cleanup
  bd delete "$THROW" --reason "throwaway after hft verification"
  ```

  Expected: "PASS" printed.

- No other `bd update --notes=` / `--acceptance=` / `--design=` overwrite patterns remain live in `src/plugins/beads/.beads/formulas/` or `src/plugins/beads/.agents/skills/`. Grep check: `grep -rnE 'bd update [^|]*--(notes|acceptance|design)=' src/plugins/beads/.beads/formulas/ src/plugins/beads/.agents/skills/` returns only the brainstorm-bead write-spec/revise sites and create-bead SKILL.md's `--acceptance=` line (the intentional ones), PLUS a false-positive in `implement-feature.formula.toml:63` which is warning text ("DO NOT call bd update {{bead-id}} --acceptance or --notes"), not an invocation.
- Discovered work filed (see list below).
- Build passes. Typecheck passes. Tests pass. (N/A — this project has no build/typecheck/test harness for TOML/MD content; verification is manual + grep-based per above.)

## Design notes

- `bd update --append-notes` already exists and adds a newline separator —
  no shell gymnastics needed.
- `--acceptance`, `--description`, `--design` have NO `--append-*` parity
  today. Any fix for those would require upstream `bd` changes or shell
  read-modify-write wrappers. Out of scope.
- The brainstorm-bead `write-spec` guard is advisory (agent-discipline
  based), not mechanical. A mechanical fix would require restructuring
  the formula's spec-authoring step — out of scope.
- The brainstorm-bead `revise` step intentionally receives NO guard: by
  the time revise runs, write-spec has already replaced any pre-existing
  notes. The revise overwrite is by design and protects nothing beyond
  the freshly-written spec.
- `implement-feature.formula.toml:63` contains the strings `--acceptance`
  and `--notes` inside a WARNING sentence. Any future automated audit
  should match only `^\s*bd update` invocations, not the bare flag
  substrings, to avoid false-positives here. Non-blocking for this bead.

## Open questions

None remaining. Scope and approach confirmed by Scott in session 11a30b53
(2026-04-20).

## Implementation notes (CRITICAL — for the implementer)

⚠️ **META-BOOTSTRAP: DO NOT pour the `fix-bug` formula on this bead.**

This bead fixes a bug in the `fix-bug` formula itself. The broken behavior
is exactly the two `bd update --notes=` calls in reproduce + root-cause.
Pouring `fix-bug` on `agents-config-hft` would run those two steps against
this very bead and DESTROY the spec you are reading right now.

Use `start-bead` Route B (trivial / inline) with explicit subagent
dispatch for the completion gate (code-reviewer → code-simplifier →
verify-checklist) instead of pouring a formula. See the
`meta-bead-formula-bootstrapping` memory for precedent (originally
discovered on `agents-config-smg`).

`run-queue` should skip `agents-config-hft` and let a human drive
implementation via the inline route. If `run-queue` cannot be told to
skip, the operator should claim the bead manually and implement via
Route B before `run-queue` gets to it.

## Discovered work to file after implementation

1. Upstream `bd` should support `--append-acceptance`, `--append-design`,
   `--append-description` to give full append-parity with `--append-notes`.
   Non-blocking; nice-to-have.
2. `brainstorm-bead` formula writes the spec to the bead's `notes` field,
   but `rules/beads.md` says "specs may be written directly into the bead
   description". Resolve by either (a) migrating brainstorm-bead's
   write-spec step to use `--description` (via `--body-file`) and updating
   the finalize/downstream steps, or (b) updating `rules/beads.md` to
   reflect the actual `notes`-field convention.
Implementation progress (session 66272d4a, 2026-05-06):
- R2 applied: brainstorm-bead.formula.toml write-spec guard preamble added with sentinel 'REPLACES the entire notes field' (line 153, above --acceptance= at line 162)
- R3 applied: create-bead/SKILL.md line 101 switched to --append-notes; comment 'bd has no --append-acceptance' added at line 100
- AC grep checks: AC1=0, AC2=1, AC3 revise step untouched (lines 213-214), AC4=0, AC5=1, AC7 only intentional sites + create-bead --acceptance= remain
- Manual repro test (throwaway bead): PASS — both SENTINEL-REPRO and SENTINEL-ROOT-CAUSE survive sequential --append-notes
- Discovered work filed: agents-config-f298 (upstream --append-* parity), agents-config-2yyb (notes-vs-description inconsistency); both linked discovered-from agents-config-hft
- Branch: fix/agents-config-hft-notes-overwrite (worktree: .claude/worktrees/fix-hft-notes-overwrite)
- Quality gate in flight: quality-reviewer agent dispatched

title: implement-feature and fix-bug formulas contain redundant brainstorm step; should be gated on brainstormed label or replaced
acceptance_criteria: AC1: implement-feature.formula.toml has no [[steps]] block with id='brainstorm'. AC2: implement-feature.formula.toml has [[steps]] block with id='confirm' as first step, no needs field. AC3: worktree step needs=['confirm']. AC4: formula description (line 25) and Phase 1 header reflect 'confirm' (no 'brainstorm' in headers/comments). AC5: Step text forbids invoking superpowers:brainstorming. AC6: Step text describes happy-path: read bead, restate plan in 1-3 sentences, proceed. AC7: Step text describes autonomous-mode missing-label path: bd human {{bead-id}}, no prompt, leave step open / abort molecule. AC8: Step text describes interactive-mode missing-label path: warn, ask user (a) abort or (b) proceed; honor choice. AC9: Step text instructs agent to detect autonomous vs interactive mode from context. AC10: fix-bug.formula.toml has no semantic changes (incidental whitespace permitted only if introduced by tooling). AC11: implement-bead/SKILL.md label preflight unchanged AND no SKILL.md prose references removed brainstorm step. AC12: confirm step description includes exact abort-mechanism guidance (leave step open, print operator follow-up, do not bd mol burn from within step).
notes:
# Spec — replace redundant `brainstorm` step in `implement-feature.formula.toml` (rev 2)

## Summary
Replace the `brainstorm` step (lines 43-66) in `src/plugins/beads/.beads/formulas/implement-feature.formula.toml` with a new `confirm` step that validates the bead is brainstormed, restates the plan, and never invokes `superpowers:brainstorming`. The `confirm` step has explicit branches for autonomous-mode (run-queue) vs interactive-mode (start-bead / direct human pour). No changes to `fix-bug.formula.toml`.

## Background / motivation
The pipeline is `brainstorm-bead` → produces AC + labels (`brainstormed`, `implementation-ready`) → `run-queue` pours `implement-feature` → FIRST step of the poured molecule is ANOTHER `superpowers:brainstorming` invocation. This is redundant with brainstorm-bead's work and violates the `rules/beads.md` Skill Partnership rule ("the bead description IS the plan"). It can also overwrite AC that brainstorm-bead already captured.

Evidence:
- `src/plugins/beads/.beads/formulas/implement-feature.formula.toml` step `brainstorm` (line 44) invokes `superpowers:brainstorming` unconditionally.
- Runtime confirmation: `mol-uaq.children` included `"Brainstorm: requirements for Split ralf-it into ralf-review + ralf-implement…"` despite `lu3.4` carrying `brainstormed` + `implementation-ready` labels.

## Scope correction (from original bead)
Original bead alleged `fix-bug.formula.toml` had the same flaw. Assessment showed `fix-bug`'s first steps are `reproduce` + `root-cause` invoking `superpowers:systematic-debugging` / `superpowers:root-cause-tracing` — bug-specific diagnosis, NOT generic brainstorming. They are not redundant in the same way. `fix-bug.formula.toml` is **out of scope** for this bead. (A SEPARATE concern in fix-bug — the `--notes` overwrite between reproduce and root-cause steps — is captured in `agents-config-hft`.)

## Requirements

### R1. Step replacement
- Remove the `brainstorm` step from `implement-feature.formula.toml`.
- Add a `confirm` step as the first step (no `needs` field).
- Update the `worktree` step's `needs = ["brainstorm"]` to `needs = ["confirm"]`.
- Update the formula's header comment (lines 11-17 phase list) and the `description` field (line 25) to reflect `confirm` instead of `brainstorm`. Update the `# Phase 1: Intent` banner to match the step's new role (e.g. "Phase 1: Intent — confirm bead is brainstormed and restate plan").

### R2. `confirm` step contract — happy path
When `bd label list {{bead-id}}` shows BOTH `brainstormed` AND `implementation-ready`:
1. `bd show {{bead-id}}` — read description and AC.
2. Restate the implementation plan in 1-3 sentences.
3. Mark the step done. The next step (worktree) runs.
4. Never invoke `superpowers:brainstorming`.

### R3. `confirm` step contract — missing-label path (TWO mechanisms)
When EITHER label is missing, the response depends on execution mode:

- **Autonomous mode** (run-queue, or any context with no human at the keyboard):
  - Escalate the bead: `bd human {{bead-id}}` with a clear note explaining the missing labels and recommending `start-bead` / `brainstorm-bead`.
  - Do NOT prompt — there is no operator to answer.
  - Abort the molecule: leave the `confirm` step open / not closed, so the molecule does not advance.
  
- **Interactive mode** (start-bead routing, or a human running `bd mol pour` directly):
  - Print a clear stdout/chat warning describing the missing labels.
  - Ask the user via natural conversation:
    - (a) Abort and route the bead through `start-bead` / brainstorm-bead first, OR
    - (b) Proceed with implementation using whatever is in the bead description.
  - On (a): mark step still-open / abort molecule.
  - On (b): proceed to worktree.

Abort mechanism (both paths): leave the `confirm` step `open` (do not close it). Print a one-line follow-up: `Molecule paused at confirm. Run brainstorm-bead on this bead and re-attempt, or 'bd mol burn <mol-id>' to discard.` Do NOT call `bd mol burn` from within the step — leave the disposition to the operator (or to run-queue policy).

Mode detection: the agent driving the molecule infers mode from its own context (was it dispatched by `run-queue`? Is it in interactive conversation with the user?). The formula step text instructs the agent to make this judgment explicitly. No formula-level interactive primitive is required — the formula step is text guidance for the agent.

### R4. No state mutation before confirm proceeds
`confirm` MUST run before any state mutation in the molecule. Specifically, the parent-chain claim walk (currently in `worktree` step around lines 87-94) is downstream of `confirm`, so this is satisfied by step ordering — no additional code needed, but the AC verifies it.

### R5. Belt-and-suspenders
The `implement-bead` skill's existing preflight label-check (`SKILL.md` L19-46) is **kept unchanged**. It guards the `implement-bead` invocation path. The new formula `confirm` step guards a DIFFERENT, narrower leak path: a human (or script) running `bd mol pour implement-feature --var bead-id=<id>` DIRECTLY, bypassing `implement-bead`. Both gates retained because they cover different paths.

## Out of scope
- `fix-bug.formula.toml` — no changes.
- Making `brainstorm-bead` type-aware for bug beads (potential follow-up).
- The `--notes` overwrite concern in fix-bug — tracked separately as `agents-config-hft`.
- Any formula-level interactive prompt primitive — explicitly chose to use agent-driven mode detection instead.

## Acceptance Criteria

**Structural (file-level):**
- AC1: `implement-feature.formula.toml` no longer contains a `[[steps]]` block with `id = "brainstorm"`.
- AC2: `implement-feature.formula.toml` contains a `[[steps]]` block with `id = "confirm"` as the first `[[steps]]` block; this block has no `needs` field.
- AC3: The `[[steps]]` block with `id = "worktree"` in `implement-feature.formula.toml` has `needs = ["confirm"]`.
- AC4: The formula's `description` field (line 25) and Phase 1 header comment are updated to reflect `confirm` (no remaining mention of "brainstorm" as the first step in headers / comments).

**Behavioral (runtime contract — described in the `confirm` step text):**
- AC5: Step text explicitly forbids invoking `superpowers:brainstorming`.
- AC6: Step text describes the happy-path (both labels present): read bead, restate plan in 1-3 sentences, proceed.
- AC7: Step text describes autonomous-mode missing-label path: call `bd human {{bead-id}}`, do not prompt, leave step open / abort molecule.
- AC8: Step text describes interactive-mode missing-label path: print warning, ask user (a) abort or (b) proceed; honor the choice.
- AC9: Step text instructs the agent to detect autonomous vs interactive mode from its own context (run-queue dispatch ⇒ autonomous; user-present ⇒ interactive).

**Negative space:**
- AC10: `fix-bug.formula.toml` has no semantic changes (incidental whitespace permitted only if introduced by tooling).
- AC11: `src/plugins/beads/.agents/skills/implement-bead/SKILL.md` label preflight (lines 19-46 area) is functionally unchanged AND no SKILL.md prose elsewhere references the removed `brainstorm` step (cross-check during implementation).
- AC12: `confirm` step description includes the exact abort-mechanism guidance (leave step `open`; print operator follow-up; do not call `bd mol burn` from within the step).

## Design notes
- **Two-path missing-label handling** addresses C1 from the adversarial review: a single "interactively ask" mechanism would deadlock run-queue. Splitting by mode keeps autonomous flow safe (escalate via `bd human`) while preserving the interactive escape hatch the user requested.
- **Behavioral AC alongside structural AC** addresses C2: structural AC alone would let a future implementer pass the spec by inserting the right strings into the description without honoring the contract at runtime. Step text describing each branch is the closest the formula gets to a runtime check (the agent driving the step is the executor).
- **Belt-and-suspenders justified by leak path** addresses N3: the skill preflight covers `implement-bead` invocations. The formula confirm covers direct `bd mol pour` invocations (operator console use, scripts, imports). Two distinct entry points, two gates.
- **Docs drift covered by AC4** addresses N2.
- **Mode detection via agent judgment** is the simplest design — no new formula primitive. Risk: an agent misreading the mode. Mitigation: the step text gives explicit cues ("dispatched by run-queue ⇒ autonomous").
- **start-bead Route B / no conflict** (M4): start-bead routes a NOT-ready bead to brainstorm-bead BEFORE implement-feature is ever poured. The new confirm step lives INSIDE implement-feature, which runs only AFTER routing. No overlap.

## Open questions
- C1 (mechanism for missing-label response) — **resolved** by R3 / two-path handling.
- C2 (behavioral vs grep-bait AC) — **resolved** by adding behavioral AC5–AC9.
- n3 (abort mechanism) — **resolved** by R3 abort-mechanism paragraph and AC12.
- None remaining.

---

## Original bead context (preserved)
Context: the bead pipeline is brainstorm-bead → produces AC + labels (brainstormed, implementation-ready) → run-queue pours implement-feature → FIRST step of the poured molecule is ANOTHER brainstorm. The second brainstorm is redundant with brainstorm-bead's work and violates the beads.md Skill Partnership rule ('the bead description IS the plan').

Evidence:
- src/plugins/beads/.beads/formulas/implement-feature.formula.toml Phase 1 Intent step 'brainstorm' (lines 43-66 approx) unconditionally invokes superpowers:brainstorming.
- Confirmed at runtime: mol-uaq.children includes 'Brainstorm: requirements for Split ralf-it into ralf-review + ralf-implement…' despite lu3.4 carrying the brainstormed + implementation-ready labels.
- fix-bug.formula.toml was originally claimed to have the same flaw — reassessment showed this is false; fix-bug has bug-specific diagnosis steps, not redundant generic brainstorming. (Separate fix-bug concern about --notes overwriting tracked in agents-config-hft.)

Impact: double-brainstorming wastes cycles, can produce conflicting AC (the second brainstorm may overwrite the first), and undermines the whole brainstorm-bead → run-queue pipeline.
close_reason: Replaced redundant brainstorm step with confirm step in implement-feature.formula.toml. PR #16 merged (squash). Two-path handling (autonomous bd human + abort vs interactive warn+ask). Three Copilot mechanical fixes applied inline (string wrapping).

title: bd mol pour does not set parent on created molecule to target bead
notes:
Context: after run-queue poured implement-feature on lu3.4, the resulting molecule mol-uaq has parent=None, not lu3.4. Same issue on mol-gs8 (prior pour for Divergence 4 bead). No structural link exists from a molecule back to the bead it was poured for.

Repro:
  bd mol pour implement-feature --var bead-id=<bead-id> --var feature='<title>'
  bd show <new-mol-id> --json | python3 -c 'import sys,json; r=json.load(sys.stdin); r=r[0] if isinstance(r,list) else r; print(r.get("parent"))'
  # → None

Expected: new molecule's parent field points at the target bead, OR some other structural edge (dependency type, metadata field) encodes the 'molecule-for-bead' relationship so that 'find molecules for bead X' is answerable via bd list.

Impact: the 'does a molecule already exist for this bead?' check in start-bead Step 2 and run-queue orchestration is STRUCTURALLY impossible. The check always returns empty even when a live molecule exists. Combined with the text-output bug (filed separately), run-queue will re-pour molecules instead of resuming existing ones.

Possible fixes: (a) set parent = <bead-id> when pouring with --var bead-id=<id>; (b) add a 'molecule-for-bead' metadata/edge; (c) encode as a dependency.
close_reason: Worked around consumer-side in PR #15: stamp for-bead-<id> label on molecules at pour/wisp; probe with bd list --label ... --type molecule --json. Upstream beads bugs remain.

title: bd list text output ignores --parent and --type filters (JSON correct)
notes:
Context: discovered while run-queue checked for existing molecules on lu3.4.

Repro (run from this project):
  bd list --parent agents-config-lu3.4                       # returns lu3.4 ITSELF in text
  bd list --parent agents-config-lu3.4 --type mol            # returns lu3.4 (type=task) in text — both filters ignored
  bd list --parent agents-config-lu3.4 --json                # returns []
  bd list --parent agents-config-lu3.4 --type mol --json     # returns []

Actual: text output ignores --parent and --type filters; displays the referenced bead (or possibly the session's last-touched issue).
Expected: text output applies filters identically to JSON output.

Impact: any agent/skill/script relying on the text output is misled. In particular:
- start-bead Step 2 ('Check for existing molecule') uses 'bd list --parent <bead-id> --type mol 2>/dev/null' in text mode.
- run-queue uses the same pattern.
Both therefore ALWAYS find the bead itself in text and conclude 'a molecule exists' OR ignore that result — either way the check is unreliable.

Workaround until fixed: consumers must use --json and parse accordingly.
close_reason: Worked around consumer-side in PR #15: stamp for-bead-<id> label on molecules at pour/wisp; probe with bd list --label ... --type molecule --json. Upstream beads bugs remain.

title: Divergence 1: wisp formulas not materializing step beads (start-bead + formulas)
description:
# Root cause

`wisp.go:249-251` in the upstream `bd` binary forces root-only materialization unless the formula declares `pour = true`:

```go
if !rootOnly && subgraph != nil && !subgraph.Pour {
    rootOnly = true
}
```

The `brainstorm-bead.formula.toml` has `phase = "vapor"` but no `pour = true`. When wisped, only a root issue is created — no child step beads are materialized. `bd mol current` returns "0/0 steps", the agent has no step IDs to operate on, and the CLI's follow-up output (`bd close <root>.<step>`) misleadingly suggests steps exist.

The `pour` field is misleadingly named: it means "materialize children," not "use pour command." Formulas need `pour = true` for `bd mol wisp` to produce actionable step beads. `bd mol pour` materializes children regardless.

Secondary cause: `start-bead` Route C says "wisp the formula, then execute as MAIN AGENT" with no execution loop. Even with a materialized molecule, the agent has no contract for how to drive it.

# Edits required

## Formulas (src/plugins/beads/.beads/formulas/)

Add `pour = true` to all four formulas:

- `brainstorm-bead.formula.toml` (vapor — only ever wisped, bug fires)
- `merge-and-cleanup.formula.toml` (vapor — only ever wisped, bug fires)
- `implement-feature.formula.toml` (liquid — defensive)
- `fix-bug.formula.toml` (liquid — defensive)

Update `# Usage:` comments in `brainstorm-bead` and `merge-and-cleanup` to add next-step navigation:

    # bd mol current <wisp-id>   # shows steps with status

## Skill (src/plugins/beads/.agents/skills/start-bead/SKILL.md)

Replace Route C action block with an explicit execution loop:

    Action: wisp the brainstorm-bead formula:
      bd mol wisp create brainstorm-bead --var bead-id=<id>

    Then drive the molecule as the MAIN AGENT (brainstorming requires
    interactive conversation — do NOT dispatch a subagent):

      1. bd mol current <wisp-id>           # shows all steps with markers
      2. For the first [ready] step:
         - bd update <step-id> --claim       # lock as in_progress
         - bd show <step-id>                  # read step instructions
         - Execute the step (interactive for discuss)
         - bd close <step-id> --continue      # closes + auto-claims next
      3. Repeat until all steps are [done]

    Use step IDs from `bd mol current` output — do NOT synthesize
    `<root>.<step>` patterns. Step IDs are independent hash-based.

Add a failure-mode block under Route C:

    ### Failure mode: 0/0 steps after wisp creation

    If `bd mol current <wisp-id>` shows "0/0 steps complete" with no
    step list, the formula is missing `pour = true`.
      bd mol burn <wisp-id>
    Tell the user it is a formula bug. Do NOT fall back to invoking
    the brainstorming skill inline.

    For other mid-run abandonment (partial progress made), prefer
      bd mol squash <wisp-id> --summary "Aborted: <reason>"
    to preserve history for debugging.

Add two Red Flags rows:

    | "The molecule looks empty, I'll just do the work inline" | STOP. 0/0 = formula bug. Burn + report. |
    | "I'll make up step IDs — they look like <root>.<step>"  | No. Use IDs from bd mol current output. |

# Design decisions

- **Defensive `pour = true` on all four formulas** (not just vapor ones): zero downside, protects against future wisp misuse of liquid formulas.
- **`bd close --continue` in the loop**: cleanest auto-advance; saves one `bd mol current` query per step.
- **Burn vs squash recovery**: burn only for the 0/0 formula-bug case (nothing to preserve); squash for any partial-progress abandonment.
- **No new "execute-molecule" skill**: 5-line loop fits inline in Route C. `implement-bead` has a different pattern (dispatches subagents between steps) so cannot reuse the same verbatim loop.
- **Keep `bd mol wisp create`** as the command in Route C text: `create` is a valid subcommand (back-compat per `wisp.go:813`). Changing to bare `bd mol wisp` is churn with no behavior delta.

# Acceptance criteria

- All four formulas declare `pour = true`
- `brainstorm-bead` and `merge-and-cleanup` formula header comments include `bd mol current <wisp-id>` guidance
- `start-bead` Route C has explicit execution loop with `bd update --claim` / `bd show` / execute / `bd close --continue`
- `start-bead` has a failure-mode block for the 0/0-steps case with burn-vs-squash guidance
- `start-bead` Red Flags table includes the two new entries
- Manual verification: wisp `brainstorm-bead` against a test bead, confirm child step beads are materialized, confirm `bd mol current` surfaces step IDs
- Build passes. Typecheck passes. Tests pass.

# Notes

- Discovered during audit of discord-agent-scaffold session `64012e00`
- Related upstream `bd` CLI bug (misleading `bd close <root>.<step>` output at `wisp.go:303` when wisp is root-only) — tracked separately in the beads project issue tracker
- Expert consultation confirmed root cause via `wisp.go:249-251`, `pour.go:216`, and `mol_current.go:191-238` source evidence
close_reason: pour=true on all 4 formulas + start-bead Route C execution loop + failure mode + Red Flags

title: Harden bead-driven workflow boundaries (audit fixes + follow-on strategy)
description:
# Problem statement

AI agents working bead-tracked tasks silently bypass the formula/molecule protocol because of three compounding issues identified in the audit of `discord-agent-scaffold` session `64012e00`:

1. **Broken formula mechanism** — wisps were root-only by default; step beads never materialized; agents saw "0/0 steps" and abandoned the molecule.
2. **Skill-paradigm conflict** — beads (lifecycle) and superpowers (methodology) overlap on planning/execution. External superpowers skills pulled agents off the bead path via their auto-match triggers.
3. **Asymmetric authorization language** — rules emphasize "merge requires authorization" without parallel "PR creation is AUTOMATIC" language; agents over-generalize caution and stop mid-workflow.

Ralf-it also over-specifies outer-workflow concerns (worktree, delivery) that conflict with bead formulas, which emerged as a separate architectural question worth re-evaluating.

Strategy questions: after overlay rules ship, do residual risks warrant further closers? Is overlay-only sufficient or is a superpowers fork eventually needed?

# Context

- **Audit target**: discord-agent-scaffold session `64012e00-6f05-409f-95b2-243ae02e1f64`
- **Analysis session**: `c06c7b55` (April 2026)
- **Expert confirmation**: beads expert traced root causes to `wisp.go:249-251`, `pour.go:216`, `mol_current.go:191-238`
- **Upstream dep**: no changes needed in upstream `bd` binary for Phase 1; CLI output bug at `wisp.go:303` filed separately in the beads project issue tracker

# Initiative phases

## Phase 1 — Fix audit findings (P0/P1)

- **`lu3.1`** (P0) — Divergence 1: wisp formulas not materializing step beads. Add `pour = true` to four formulas; add execution loop to `start-bead` Route C; add failure-mode block for 0/0 state.
- **`lu3.2`** (P1) — Divergence 2: beads ↔ superpowers partnership contract. Rule section listing inner tools vs off-limits skills; formula step citations of methodology skills; red flags in start-bead and implement-bead. Scope also includes `implement-feature` delivery step citation (moved from lu3.3).
- **`lu3.3`** (P1) — Divergence 3: clarify delivery authorization boundaries. Action categorization (AUTOMATIC vs AUTHORIZATION-REQUIRED); "ready when you are" red flag; sharpen completion-gate HARD STOP; new beads overlay file for bead-aware delivery guidance.
- **`lu3.4`** (P1) — Re-evaluate ralf-it form (skill vs command vs agent). Design bead — needs brainstorming to decide form. Emerged from Divergence 3 analysis as a cleaner fix than patching ralf-it's over-specified workflow declarations in place.

## Phase 1.5 — Evaluate risk-closers (P3)

- **`717`** (P3) — After Phase 1 ships and is observed for drift rate, brainstorm and decide whether to implement: hook-level enforcement, user-direction guardrail, session-audit cadence, broader failure-mode block. Each option gets a `bd decision` record. Blocks Phase 2.

## Phase 2 — Fork superpowers if needed (P3)

- **`cx6`** (P3) — If overlay rules + risk-closers still leave drift, fork superpowers into a curated bead-aware plugin. Eliminates the three competing skills entirely; keeps the methodology keepers. Candidate names: `superbeadpowers`, `superbeads`, `beadpowers`. Blocked by `717` — cannot proceed until risk-closer evaluation completes.

# Scope

**In scope:**
- Formula TOML and skill text changes in `src/plugins/beads/`
- Core rule updates in `src/user/.claude/rules/`
- New beads overlay files in `src/plugins/beads/.claude/rules/` (appends via install mechanism)
- Ralf-it form re-evaluation and follow-on restructuring
- Strategic evaluation of risk-closers and fork option

**Out of scope:**
- Upstream `bd` CLI bugs (tracked in the beads project issue tracker)
- Non-bead workflow changes (superpowers usage outside bead context)
- Changes to the external superpowers plugin itself (unless Phase 2 fork is authorized)

# Acceptance criteria

The epic is complete when ALL of the following hold:

1. **Phase 1 closed**: `lu3.1`, `lu3.2`, `lu3.3`, `lu3.4` are all closed with their own acceptance criteria met.
2. **Observation**: the partnership contract has been observed in at least 2 subsequent session audits; drift rate documented.
3. **Phase 1.5 resolved**: `717` is closed — each risk-closer option has a `bd decision` (accepted/deferred/rejected with rationale). Accepted options are implemented as sub-beads.
4. **Phase 2 resolved**: `cx6` is either closed as "implemented" OR closed as "not needed — overlay + risk-closers sufficient" with rationale.
5. **End-to-end behavior**: an agent running `start-bead` on a fresh feature bead can execute the full `brainstorm → implement → deliver` pipeline without silently falling back to direct skill invocations or manual user handoffs.
6. **Quality gate**: Build passes. Typecheck passes. Tests pass.

# Notes

- Phase numbering mirrors the time horizon: Phase 1 is immediate, Phase 1.5 is post-observation, Phase 2 is conditional escalation.
- The epic description was rewritten in session `c06c7b55` to cover all three phases under a single umbrella (was previously scoped to audit fixes only).
close_reason: Phase 1 children all closed; Phase 1.5/2 tracked separately under 717 and cx6

title: Project-level config gate: should the agent wait for PR review feedback
description:
Make 'wait for PR review feedback' a project-level policy decision instead of an unconditional step.

## Problem
wait-for-pr-comments / prgroom currently assume an automated reviewer (Copilot) will show up and human merge approval is not required. On repos where Copilot is NOT enabled, or where a human review/merge approval is the real gate, the agent polls for reviewer feedback that will never arrive (or that it cannot act on autonomously), burning wall-clock and producing a false 'waiting' state.

## Desired outcome
A project-level configuration that states whether the agent should wait for PR review feedback, and for what kind of reviewer (automated vs human). The delivery flow (completion-gate -> finishing-a-development-branch -> wait-for-pr-comments) reads it and either polls, or skips the wait and hands off to the human, accordingly.

## Known prior art in-repo
- project-config.toml ALREADY declares [review-requirements] (copilot-required, human-approvers-required) with comment 'Read by: review-cycle' -- but grep src/ packages/ for these keys = 0 consumers. Declared-but-dead knob; review-cycle consumer does not exist.
- Related: 7bk.12 (brainstorm-time review-requirements knobs), abn9.8.19 (prgroom reviewer discovery), ukzs (overlap external waits).

## Scope (to refine in brainstorm)
- Decide the config surface (reuse [review-requirements]? new key?) and key naming (cf. 7bk.17 kebab/snake alignment)
- Decide who consumes it (wait-for-pr-comments skill, prgroom, delivery rules)
- Define skip-vs-poll vs human-handoff behavior and the terminal report
design: Design (final): docs/specs/2026-06-30-pr-review-merge-policy.md on branch worktree-wgclw14-review-merge-policy. TWO ORTHOGONAL AXES: (1) Review expectation [bot-review-expected + trusted bot-reviewers allowlist + timeout; human-approvers-required + timeout] drives the poll/resolve cycle; (2) Merge authorization [never | explicit(default) | rule-based(merge-rule)] decides who merges once eligible. merge-rule vocab: bot-quiescence, human-approvals, agent-ruling(design-reserved, deferred). Eligibility = no-blocker floor (no CHANGES_REQUESTED, no unresolved threads, CI green, fresh head, no prgroom blockers) computed live; positive review facts live in the rules. Zero-review auto-merge structurally impossible. Hardened over ~10 adversarial passes: distinct-current-approver counting, head-SHA freshness (no timestamp fallback, --match-head-commit), fail-closed on missing commit_id, exact bot-identity match, formal CI-green + bot clean-review predicates. Awaiting Scott's spec review before writing-plans.
notes:
--- 2026-07-01: adversarial loop round 2 (post-compaction) ---
Ran /codex:adversarial-review fresh; found 2 new highs despite prior 5-round
convergence (bot APPROVED bypassing triage; eligibility missing non-thread
feedback). Dispatched a fork to run up to 5 more fix+re-review rounds.

Result: cap reached at 5/5, never hit an early "clean" exit — every round
surfaced something, though severity narrowed from structural gaps to
precision-of-wording edge cases:
  c903ce3 - untriaged non-thread feedback gap in eligibility floor
  74da935 - stopped head-scoping negative blocker facts (CHANGES_REQUESTED
            must survive a push; freshness invariant only applies to positive facts)
  5cf4514 - CI required-set sourcing (statusCheckRollup omits not-yet-started
            checks); COMMENTED doesn't clear a prior CHANGES_REQUESTED;
            non-thread-feedback needs durable (not deleted-on-success) storage
  1c77c54 - durable triage record must not be SHA-keyed (contradicts 74da935);
            CI match must respect branch-protection source pin, not name alone
  b033ade - new eligibility floor row: block merge while an expected review is
            still in flight (closes explicit-merge-races-pending-review gap);
            self-reply exclusion by durably-recorded comment ID, never by
            author login (agent often shares the human operator's GitHub account)

Round 5's fixes (b033ade) were never re-verified by a 6th Codex pass (would
exceed the 5-round cap). Independently re-read the full 572-line spec after
the fork returned: both new pieces integrate cleanly with the existing
freshness-invariant / no-blocker-floor split and the Testing section was
updated to match. No fresh internal contradiction found on manual review.

Status: awaiting Scott's decision — stop and move to his substantive spec
review, or authorize one more adversarial pass beyond the stated cap.

Scott: stop the adversarial loop at 5/5, move to his substantive spec review.

--- 2026-07-01: spec approved, implementation plan written ---
Scott approved the spec (crit review, blanket approval + verbal proceed).
writing-plans executed: docs/plans/2026-07-01-pr-review-merge-policy.md
(commit 44acde2, 2553 lines, 22 TDD tasks).

Key ground-truth findings baked into the plan (from 3 research passes):
- delivery.md has NO source in src/ (archive only) — law amendment targets the
  5 real locations; orphaned installed copy filed as discovered work (Task 22)
- skill Python = stdlib+shebang only (no uv/PEP-723); resolver tests wrapped
  in *_test.sh for the [gates].test glob
- review_summary items have no script producer (orchestrator hand-builds from
  poll-copilot-review.sh reviews[]) — review_id lands in Phase-3 instruction
  + guard 3
- post-replies.sh discards the POST response — Task 16 captures created reply
  id → posted_reply_id (the floor's ID-based exclusion set)
- prgroom status envelope has no per-item dispositions — inventory union is
  the implemented durable triage source

Next: execution (subagent-per-task or inline TDD), Scott to choose.
Implementation plan executed via 5 phased workflows (subagent implement+adversarial-verify per task): docs/plans/2026-07-01-pr-review-merge-policy.md. All 22 tasks complete; full smoke suite green (find-glob *_test.sh, all pass); resolver sanity vs live project-config.toml: rule-based/bot-quiescence, exit 0. Dep edge wgclw.14->7bk.12 pre-existed as discovered-from (kept; plan wanted related-to — intent satisfied). Discovered work filed: xvmf8 (agent-ruling harness), ejcda (orphaned delivery.md), hvy47 (guard numbering), jdzcd (poll filter), oswzr/atlhk/9jkad (3 test-coverage gaps from phase verifiers). Completion-gate review workflow running; delivery next.

description: Correctness and data-safety bugs in the Python installer (packages/installer): prune correctness, data-loss-on-overwrite, merge dedupe, cross-tree relocation orphans, dead/guarded code paths.

title: Installer hardening & DX
description: Umbrella epic re-homing the Python installer (packages/installer) hardening and developer-experience work that was scattered as orphan top-level beads after the w1qls rewrite shipped and cut over (Epic H, closed). Children grouped into sub-epics: correctness & data-safety, parity & test hardening, DX accelerators. Lives under M1 (stabilize / finish in-flight / ship immediate accelerators). Backlog/re-eval P4 items (5qym, en7lb, fvtyt) are intentionally NOT under this epic.

title: docs: purge migration history and bead IDs from installer docs and AGENTS.md files
description: Strip all past/legacy content from docs/architecture/installer/installer-design.md, packages/installer/AGENTS.md, root AGENTS.md, and root README.md. These files should only describe current architecture and what agents need to know. Bead IDs, epic names (A-H), migration history, golden-master references, and future-work schedules do not belong in living docs. Any undone work items pulled from the docs should be in beads instead.

title: Golden master test_hooks_namespace_staged_with_exec_bit fails after ruff-postedit_test.sh added to .installignore
description: Commit 497b9bf added ruff-postedit_test.sh to .installignore but did not update the golden master parity test. The test picks the only non-executable file in src/user/.claude/hooks/ as its probe and then asserts it was installed — but .installignore now excludes it. Fix: update the test to filter .installignore'd basenames when selecting probe files, so the invariant only requires an installable non-executable hook.

title: Installer: rules namespace installs loose files to ~/.codex/rules/ and ~/.gemini/rules/ instead of inlining into AGENTS.md
description: CodexAdapter and GeminiAdapter accept all namespaces uniformly, so the rules namespace gets copied as individual files to ~/.codex/rules/ and ~/.gemini/rules/. Neither tool natively reads from a rules/ subdirectory (that is a Claude-only feature). The bash installer inlined rules into AGENTS.md via DYNAMIC-INCLUDE-ALL-RULES. The Python installer must either: (a) exclude the rules namespace for non-Claude tools and rely on template inlining into AGENTS.md, or (b) verify that DYNAMIC-INCLUDE-ALL-RULES in the codex/gemini AGENTS.md templates correctly pulls in all rules at template-flatten time and the loose files are redundant/harmless. Determine which path is correct and fix the divergence.
close_reason: Fixed in PR #197 (squash-merged). flatten_plan_templates now drops the rules-namespace items a plan's instruction file inlined via ALL-RULES (codex/gemini/opencode), restoring bash parity — no more loose ~/.codex/rules or ~/.gemini/rules. Claude keeps loose rules (no ALL-RULES marker). Consolidated OpenCodeAdapter's duplicate per-adapter drop into the shared layer; drop mirrors inliner condition (content is not None) so non-inlined dir items aren't silently lost. Passed quality-reviewer + codex adversarial (gpt-5.5) + simplify + Copilot (all clean/addressed). Follow-up integration suite: agents-config-24eo5.

title: prgroom: reframe outer PR-review cap as a retry cap (rename -> LIFECYCLE_PR_REVIEW_EXHAUSTED, retry-mechanize counter, default 5)
description:
## Why
Discovered during fca6.16 (the prgroom fix<->verify design). That design adds an INNER fix<->verify retry cap (LIFECYCLE_FIX_VERIFY_EXHAUSTED, default 2 retries / 3 opus spends). For human-intuition consistency the existing OUTER PR-review cap must be expressed as a sibling retry cap. The fca6.16 HLD artifacts depict the TARGET (retry-framed) state for BOTH caps with no excuses for current state; THIS bead carries the code + remaining prose to that target.

## What (target state)
1. Rename error code LIFECYCLE_HARD_CAP_EXCEEDED -> LIFECYCLE_PR_REVIEW_EXHAUSTED across errors.py (ErrorCode enum + _REGISTRY what/why/how), BlockingErrorCodes, run.py cap-guard, resolver.py, verb_error mapping, status surfacing, tests.
2. Rename the knob: --max-rounds -> --pr-review-retries; PRGROOM_MAX_ROUNDS -> PRGROOM_PR_REVIEW_RETRIES; .prgroom.toml max_rounds -> pr_review_retries. Precedence unchanged (flag>env>toml>default).
3. Re-mechanize the counter: replace the 1-indexed round (cap at max_rounds) with a 0-indexed fix-push RETRY count, PRESERVING the design-plan 3.4 CLI-vs-external push attribution + first-poll bootstrap semantics. Exhaustion when retries_used >= cap.
4. Default = 5 retries (matches the raised skill/bash PR-cycle cap). Off-by-one pinned: 5 retries = up to 6 review-eliciting pushes incl. the initial.
5. Retire hard-cap vocabulary in code + remaining prose in favor of PR-review retry budget.

## Sequencing
BLOCKS abn9.8.23 (verify-step wiring) so 8.23 builds on the renamed code + retry-framed cap, not legacy names. Does NOT touch the inner fix<->verify cap (8.23) or fix-agent arming (8.22).

## Acceptance
Code uses LIFECYCLE_PR_REVIEW_EXHAUSTED + pr_review_retries (default 5, 0-indexed retry counter); zero LIFECYCLE_HARD_CAP_EXCEEDED / max_rounds references remain; 3.4 attribution semantics preserved + tested; make ci-prgroom green.

title: Design the prgroom fix<->verify subsystem (fill the architecture gap)
description:
## Why this exists
prgroom's fix -> verify -> push loop has an architecture gap: docs/architecture/prgroom never designed the contract between the fix agent and the mechanical verifier. Beads 8.23 (consume recommended_gate) and 8.22 (arm the fix agent) are both slices of this undesigned subsystem and are BLOCKED until it is designed. Discovered while brainstorming 8.23.

## The problem
The fix agent edits code to address review clusters and can introduce NEW problems it should have caught. A mechanical verifier sits outside as a gate but expects a clean slate. Two halves are missing from the architecture:
1. The fix agent must be a full IMPLEMENTATION agent (run skills + subagents + its own completion gate + verify-checklist), not a muzzled editor. It is launched via `claude -p` (top-level, NOT a nested subagent), so it can orchestrate subagents safely -- the await-own-child footgun does NOT apply to it.
2. The fix<->verify CONTRACT: the fix agent emits its verify-checklist result as a structured artifact; the prgroom mechanical verifier confirms that artifact against reality before push.

## Decisions already made (this brainstorm -- ground them, do not relitigate)
- prgroom's verifier = MECHANICAL command gate (run the repo's tests/build/lint via the existing CommandRunner / proc.py), NOT an agent review. Agent review is what the FIX agent does itself; prgroom's gate is the independent deterministic confirmation. ('Code over Prose'; trust-but-verify.)
- Whole-branch, max-strength tier: recommended_gate is per-item but push is all-or-nothing (git push HEAD:branch) and the resolver already gates the whole PR on any FAILED item. So take the STRONGEST recommended_gate across clean fixed items (any 'full' => full, else 'lite') and run ONE whole-branch gate. No per-item push partitioning (no precedent; do not build it).
- Insertion point: a new `verify` VerbStep after `fix` (after its audits) and before `cap-guard`/`push`, mirroring the cap-guard pattern (lifecycle/run.py:412-429) -- a pre-push step that refuses the push by flipping phase.
- Fail path = AUTO-RE-FIX (operator decision): on gate fail, write the verification run output to a TEMP FILE, re-enter the fix stage with specific instructions pointing at it, re-verify, push only when green. Bounded by its OWN attempt budget (the hard cap counts pushes; a verify-fail never pushes, so the round cap cannot bound it). Proposed default budget 2, then HUMAN_GATED with the last verdict attached (confirm at design).
- Verdict persisted at BATCH level on PRGroomingState (not per-item: DispositionKind.FAILED drops the gate field, and verification is whole-branch). New structured error code surfaced in `status --json`.
- recommended_gate value contract: free str today (no enum/validation); to make it load-bearing, add an enum + validation/audit.
- Unconfigured verify command => HARD STOP early precondition: prgroom checks at startup and exits hard with helpful guidance (what to configure and where). NOT a silent skip. Auto-detection lives in the `--doctor` tool (separate bead).

## Ground-truth architecture map
A full code-level map of the lifecycle (run.py VerbStep/pipeline order), Disposition/state model, push seam, and verification primitives was produced this session via the `map-prgroom-gate` workflow (session 3f0791c2). Re-run that workflow if stale; the load-bearing facts are captured in the decisions above.

## Deliverables
- HLD in docs/architecture/prgroom/ for the fix<->verify subsystem: components, the fix-agent contract, sequence + state-machine deltas, the verify step, the re-fix loop.
- A dated design spec in docs/specs/ if the subsystem warrants it.
- Decompose into impl beads: feeds 8.23 (verify step + re-fix loop + recommended_gate consumption + hard-stop precondition) and 8.22 (arm the fix agent + capability/allow-list aggregation + verify-checklist contract emission).

## Out of scope
- Implementation (lands in 8.22 / 8.23 and any new children).
- prgroom --doctor (separate bead).
design:
# Fix<->Verify Subsystem — approved design (2026-06-20)

Canonical artifacts (this PR): docs/specs/2026-06-20-prgroom-fix-verify-subsystem.md + docs/architecture/prgroom/c4-l3-verify.md + deltas to c4-l3-lifecycle.md, state-machine.md, data-view.md, c4-l3-agent-dispatch.md, index.md. HLD depicts TARGET (retry-framed) state for BOTH caps.

## Pipeline
cluster -> fix -> VERIFY -> cap-guard -> push -> reply -> resolve -> rereview. verify inserted between fix(run.py:403) and cap-guard(404) in _build_pipeline. No-op when not has_queued. Refusal mechanism = set phase=HUMAN_GATED; the post-step terminal check (run.py:388-393) breaks the pipeline before cap-guard/push. Identical to cap-guard.

## Two retry caps (siblings)
- INNER fix<->verify: fix_verify_retries default 2 (max 3 opus spends/cycle). Exhaustion -> LIFECYCLE_FIX_VERIFY_EXHAUSTED.
- OUTER pr_review: pr_review_retries default 5 (initial + 5 fix-pushes = 6 pushes). Exhaustion -> LIFECYCLE_PR_REVIEW_EXHAUSTED (rename of LIFECYCLE_HARD_CAP_EXCEEDED). Outer reframe = SEPARATE follow-on abn9.8.25 (blocks 8.23); design shows target state only.
Both: LIFECYCLE_CAP tier, exit 0 graceful -> human-gated, in BlockingErrorCodes, re-armable (raise the knob -> entry-probe).

## Trust-but-verify
Fix agent (armed: full impl allow-list) runs its OWN completion gate and emits a REQUIRED verify_checklist artifact in FixOutput (the claim; missing/malformed on a FIXED batch -> CONTRACT_FIX_AUDIT_FAILED). prgroom verify step runs the configured tier command via proc.CommandRunner (whole-branch, AUTHORITATIVE). Tier = strongest Disposition.gate across clean FIXED items (any FULL => FULL else LITE).

## Convergence loop (in verify step)
Gate RED: write gate output to temp file -> dispatch whole-branch REPAIR fix (fix-repair prompt + verify_failure_path input; commit-attribution audit adapted - repair commits claimed by the verify-repair, not a review item) -> re-audit -> re-gate. Bounded by fix_verify_retries. GREEN -> fall through to cap-guard/push. Exhausted -> human-gated + LIFECYCLE_FIX_VERIFY_EXHAUSTED.

## Data / error / config deltas
- GateStrength(StrEnum){FULL,LITE}; Disposition.gate typed + validated (fix audit).
- VerifyVerdict batch field on PRGroomingState (additive, omit-when-None => schema_version stays 1): result, tier, retries_used, gate_output ref, decided_at. New status --json `verify` block.
- LIFECYCLE_FIX_VERIFY_EXHAUSTED (new). PRECONDITION_NO_VERIFY_CONFIG (user-error exit 2) hard-stop at run/fix entry when needed tier command unconfigured; auto-detect deferred to --doctor (separate bead).
- Config: [verify] table in .prgroom.toml (lite/full command(s) + fix_verify_retries default 2). Precedence flag>env>toml; NO built-in command default. Wire repo_config (today always None).

## Security residual risk
Armed fix agent = headless dontAsk + broad Bash on a branch carrying attacker-authored review text -> prompt-injection surface. Mitigated by worktree-trust + operator opt-in. Documented, not blocked.

title: Introduce shared .installignore exclusion manifest (replaces hardcoded DEAD_MARKERS; fixes rules/AGENTS.md content+file leak)
description:
Reframed from "rules-assembly parity divergence" to root cause + solution after investigation (2026-06-20).

ROOT CAUSE — bash and python disagree on which source files are EXCLUDED from the install fileset. Python has a hardcoded staging filter DEAD_MARKERS={AGENTS.md,CLAUDE.md,GEMINI.md} (staging.py:60,143) dropping namespace-level dev-doc markers; bash has NO equivalent. So a folder-doc marker like src/user/.agents/rules/AGENTS.md (added in the recent rules cleanup) leaks on the BASH side TWO ways, both downstream of staging:
  (1) CONTENT-LEAK — bash ALL-RULES handler `find "$staging/rules" -name '*.md' | sort` (install.sh:752) globs the marker and inlines its "# Rules"/Companion-readmes text into the assembled per-tool AGENTS.md/GEMINI.md. CAUGHT by golden-master test_bare_install_{codex,gemini,opencode} (currently RED on main).
  (2) FILE-LEAK — bash deploys the namespace AGENTS.md as a standalone file into the fileset (e.g. ~/.claude/rules/AGENTS.md). Python omits it (DEAD_MARKERS). The golden-master _diff.py:91-102 (_is_namespace_dead_marker) DELIBERATELY SKIPS dead markers on BOTH sides -> the oracle is BLIND to this file-leak BY DESIGN. UNTRACKED before this bead.
Python is CORRECT on both; bash leaks because it lacks the shared exclusion. The exclusion surface is already broader than the 3 markers: rules-readmes/ dirs (explicitly "not installed" per the leaked text), README.md / SESSION-PRIMER-README.md dev-docs, AGENTS.md markers in multiple namespace dirs.

SOLUTION (agreed 2026-06-20) — introduce a `.installignore` manifest (gitignore-FLAVORED, NOT gitignore-complete) consumed by BOTH installers at the STAGING / fileset-assembly choke point. One shared source of truth -> kills the bash<->python exclusion divergence class at the root; explicit, reviewable, testable; replaces the hardcoded DEAD_MARKERS frozenset. Applied at staging (upstream of BOTH deploy AND the ALL-RULES glob) it covers BOTH leak surfaces with one mechanism.

GUARDRAIL — do NOT reimplement full gitignore semantics in two languages (negation "!", "**", anchoring, per-dir cascade, precedence ordering); that recreates the exact divergence being killed. Use a SIMPLE documented subset: one pattern per line, "#" comments, exact basename or simple "*" glob, NO negation, NO "**". Provably identical in bash find/case and python fnmatch; itself parity-testable.

DESIGN DECISIONS to settle in brainstorm/spec:
- Scope rule: preserve DEAD_MARKERS' namespace-vs-root subtlety — drop rules/AGENTS.md but KEEP the tool-ROOT AGENTS.md/GEMINI.md instruction file. Patterns need namespace anchoring (e.g. "*/AGENTS.md" or per-namespace prefixes), never a bare "AGENTS.md".
- Location: single repo-root .installignore (patterns relative to source root) vs per-tree cascade — prefer single (cascade = more parser).
- Coverage: AUDIT whether rules-readmes/, README.md, *-readme.md actually leak today (may already be dropped by namespace-scoping) -> fold only the real leakers into the manifest.

ORACLE HARDENING (closes the blind-spot) — replace _diff.py's dead-marker SKIP with a POSITIVE assertion: "no path matching .installignore appears in either installed tree." Testable exclusion instead of a blind skip. Add a matcher-parity test (bash vs python agree on a fixture path set).

BASH TIMING — bash retires at 8.4 (install.sh -> uv wrapper). Its matcher is throwaway; the simple subset keeps the bash side a few cheap lines, while python's .installignore is the permanent post-cutover mechanism. Building both now buys green CI on interim installer PRs.

NEXT STEP — brainstorming/spec pass (subset grammar, scoping rule, location, oracle assertion) BEFORE implementation.
acceptance_criteria:
- .installignore manifest exists with a documented SIMPLE-subset grammar (one pattern/line, "#" comments, basename or simple "*" glob; no negation, no "**").
- BOTH bash install.sh and python install.py consume the manifest at the staging/fileset-assembly step; the hardcoded DEAD_MARKERS frozenset is removed (or sourced from the manifest).
- src/user/.agents/rules/AGENTS.md (and the other namespace dead-markers) are excluded from BOTH the assembled instruction file (content-leak) AND the deployed fileset (file-leak), on BOTH installers.
- The tool-ROOT AGENTS.md / GEMINI.md instruction files are STILL installed (namespace-vs-root scoping preserved).
- golden-master test_bare_install_{codex,gemini,opencode} GREEN.
- Oracle hardened: _diff.py asserts no .installignore-matching path appears in either installed tree (positive check), replacing the dead-marker SKIP.
- Matcher-parity test: bash and python agree on a fixture set of include/exclude paths.
- rules-readmes/ + README.md leak question audited; real leakers folded into the manifest, non-leakers documented as already-excluded.

title: [Impl] prgroom fix-agent dispatch — headless claude -p can't commit (add --permission-mode dontAsk + scoped --allowedTools)
description:
Discovered by 8.13's end-to-end test on PR #181. prgroom's `fix` verb dispatches the fix agent via `claude -p <prompt> --model <m>` (packages/prgroom/src/prgroom/agent/subprocess_runner.py `_invocation_for_claude`, ~line 216) with NO `--permission-mode`. In headless mode (no TTY), claude queues the Edit/Bash tool calls waiting for an interactive approval that never arrives, then exits having made no commit — so the fix agent CANNOT create the reachable commit a `fixed` disposition requires, and every fix lands `failed`.

Confirmed live on PR #181: all 5 inline-comment fixes were correctly identified and locally validated, but every one failed at commit. A POC (2026-06-20, full matrix in notes) settled the cause: the sole blocker is the missing permission flag — NOT an OS sandbox (Claude Code's OS sandbox is opt-in and was off; no `.git/index.lock` denial reproduced). Baseline (no flag) makes no commit; `dontAsk` + a scoped `--allowedTools` commits cleanly.

## Chosen approach (least-privilege — operator decision)
Dispatch the fix agent with `--permission-mode dontAsk` (fail-closed: never prompts; denies-and-continues on anything unlisted, so it never stalls waiting for an approval that can't arrive headless) plus a TIGHT `--allowedTools` allow-list — NOT a blanket `bypassPermissions`.
- Tight default floor: `--allowedTools "Read Edit Write Bash(git *)"` — enough to edit + commit, nothing destructive.
- Make the allow/deny lists CONFIGURABLE (start tight, allow override): aggregate project-config.toml + project `.claude/settings.json` (+ local?) + user `~/.claude/settings.json`, union'd, deny-wins. Full directive + gotchas in notes.

## In scope
- `subprocess_runner.py` `_invocation_for_claude`: append `--permission-mode dontAsk` + `--allowedTools "<scoped list>"` to the claude argv. Use the CLI tool-pattern grammar — space form, e.g. `Bash(git *)`, NOT the settings colon form `Bash(git:*)`.
- Allow-list scope: tight default; resolve the configurable-aggregation design (sources, precedence, ask-rule handling, non-configurable commit floor) per the notes directive.
- Tighten `fix.tmpl` so the agent does not attempt sub-agents / self-verify commands the tight allow-list denies (else it fail-closed-stalls).
- Tests: unit-test the argv (dontAsk + allowedTools present + correct grammar); a behavioral test that a fix item with a valid change lands `fixed` with a reachable commit SHA (not `failed`).
- `make ci-prgroom` green.

## Out of scope
- OS-sandbox behavior — Claude Code's OS sandbox is opt-in (off by default) and separate from prgroom; not prgroom's bug.
- The codex path (`codex exec --sandbox workspace-write`) already passes write capability; unchanged.

## Acceptance
- prgroom `fix` dispatches claude with `--permission-mode dontAsk` + a scoped `--allowedTools` allow-list (no blanket bypass).
- A headless fix agent can edit + `git commit`; a fix item with a valid change lands `fixed` with a reachable commit SHA.
- `make ci-prgroom` green.

## Relationship to 8.13
BLOCKS the 8.13 full-loop E2E proof. 8.13 (birth monitor-pr) is otherwise complete and its read-only contracts (poll / status --json / preconditions / dispositions) are validated live; 8.13 closes only after THIS fix lands and the full fix -> push -> reply -> resolve E2E is re-run clean on a real PR.
notes:
## POC results (2026-06-20 research session — no impl yet)

Settled the dispatch gap empirically on throwaway /tmp git repos replicating
subprocess_runner.py:216 (`claude -p <prompt> --model <m>`); model=haiku since
permission gating is model-independent:

- baseline (current, NO perm flag): NO commit. Inner agent printed "Awaiting
  permission approvals for Write and Bash commit operations... queued... once
  approved" then exited 0, HEAD unborn. THIS is the gap: default mode + no TTY =
  silent no-op.
- acceptEdits + `--allowedTools "Read Edit Write Bash(git *)"`: commit landed.
  (allow-list pre-approves the git bash; acceptEdits ALONE does NOT auto-approve
  `git commit`.)
- dontAsk + same allow-list: commit landed.

CORRECTIONS to prior premise:
1. NO `.git/index.lock` denial in ANY variant. The "OS sandbox denied index.lock"
   theory is DISCONFIRMED for this config: no `sandbox` key in settings (OS
   sandbox is opt-in via sandbox.enabled, OFF by default); probe showed $HOME
   writes ALLOWED (a workspace-confining Seatbelt sandbox would deny them). No
   nested-sandbox inheritance. The entire bug is the missing permission flag.
2. DECISION (Scott, this session): use `--permission-mode dontAsk`, NOT
   acceptEdits. dontAsk = headless-correct: never prompts, DENIES unlisted
   (fail-closed). acceptEdits PROMPTS on unlisted bash -> hangs headless (the
   baseline's "awaiting approval" no-op is exactly that failure mode for any
   non-edit/non-git command). Update in-scope + acceptance to dontAsk.
3. CLI allowedTools grammar is space-form `Bash(git *)` (per `claude --help`
   example + POC success), NOT settings-style `Bash(git:*)`.

NEW design tension surfaced by fix.tmpl (resolve at impl):
fix.tmpl line 2 tells the agent it "may choose your own skills and sub-agents"
and may self-verify. Under fail-closed dontAsk, any tool not allow-listed is
DENIED -- so Task/Agent (sub-agent spawn) or `make/pytest/ruff` (self-verify)
silently fail unless listed. Options:
  (A) TIGHT v1: allow-list = `Read Edit Write Bash(git *)` only. Agent edits +
      commits, does NOT self-verify or spawn sub-agents; rely on prgroom's own
      combined gate (recommended_gate). Also dodges the known
      "subagent-can't-await-own-child" idle bug. REQUIRES tightening fix.tmpl
      prose so the agent doesn't attempt sub-agents/verify and stall.
  (B) BROAD: add Bash(make *)/Bash(pytest *)/Bash(ruff *)/Task so the inner agent
      self-verifies + orchestrates. Wider privilege surface; re-introduces the
      nested-await footgun.
Recommend (A) for least-privilege + the vision. Confirm before impl.

POC harness kept at /tmp/prgroom-poc/run-matrix.sh.
## Allow-list design directive (Scott, 2026-06-20)

Decision: START TIGHT, but make the allow/deny lists CONFIGURABLE, not hardcoded.
Direction to explore at impl:
- Default (no config present): the tight floor `Read Edit Write Bash(git *)`
  (minimum to edit + commit; prgroom can't function below this).
- Make it overridable/extendable via project-config.toml.
- Consider DERIVING the lists DYNAMICALLY by AGGREGATING permission rules from,
  in precedence order:
    project-config.toml  +  <project>/.claude/settings.json  (+ settings.local.json?)
    +  ~/.claude/settings.json (user)
  Aggregate BOTH the allow-list AND the deny/block-list across all sources
  (union; deny overrides allow on conflict — standard permission semantics).
  Rationale: reuse the operator's existing trust decisions (DRY) instead of a
  second hardcoded list that drifts from their real Claude Code permissions.

Impl gotchas to resolve at build:
- GRAMMAR MISMATCH: settings.json uses colon form `Bash(git commit:*)`; CLI
  `--allowedTools` example uses space form `Bash(git *)` (POC-verified working).
  Confirm whether --allowedTools accepts colon form; else translate
  settings-form -> CLI-form during aggregation.
- `permissions.ask` entries: headless dontAsk cannot prompt -> safest is to treat
  `ask` as NOT-allowed (falls through to deny). Confirm desired behavior.
- Keep a NON-configurable minimum floor (`Bash(git *)` + `Write`) so a bad config
  can't disarm the fix agent's ability to commit at all.

Scope note: this grows 8.21 from "add a flag" to "configurable multi-source
permission aggregation." Could split into (1) static tight flag = quick
commit-capability win, and (2) config aggregation = its own design slice.
Parked at research per Scott; not split yet.
## PR #185 (open, CI green, Copilot clean)
Impl landed on branch worktree-prgroom-8.21-fix-perms (commits e3f7154 + 3948f0d).
- claude write role -> --permission-mode dontAsk + --allowedTools "Read Edit Write Bash(git *)" (gated is True, mirrors codex); default fix chain claude link write=True; cluster read-only; fix.tmpl tightened.
- make ci green (894 tests, 99.66% cov). msgpack 1.1.2->1.2.1 bumped in BOTH packages/prgroom/uv.lock AND packages/installer/uv.lock (GHSA-6v7p-g79w-8964) to clear repo-wide audit (operator-approved).
- Adversarial review (3 lenses): 2 LOW findings, both fixed (fix.tmpl network-claim accuracy; AgentSpec docstring write=claude+codex).
- Copilot: clean pass, 0 inline/thread comments.
AWAITING: operator merge authorization. Bead closes only after merge AND 8.13 live fix->push->reply->resolve E2E re-run on a real PR (this change unblocks it). Enhancement split: agents-config-abn9.8.22 (configurable multi-source perms).

title: Namespace-dir sync: bash rm-rf overwrites with NO backup (data loss); Python backs up — divergence
description: H.3 real-state smoke (real ~/.claude seeded into temp HOMEs, both installers run, tree-diffed) surfaced a real parity divergence the hermetic golden-master suite misses. ROOT CAUSE: bash sync_directory (install.sh ~1200-1260) replaces a CHANGED namespace item (skills/agents/rules/commands/hooks) via 'rm -rf $dest_item; cp -R' with NO backup() call — silent data loss of a user-modified skill/agent/hook. Python _install_dir/_install_file (core/sync.py) back up to <namespace>-backup/ BEFORE replacing. So Python is SAFER (preserves the user's drifted content); bash has a latent data-loss bug. Observed on real home: improve-codebase-architecture + writing-skills (content-drifted SKILL.md) and whats-next (dest-only __pycache__/*.pyc) all produced Python-only skills-backup/ dirs; hooks/ruff-postedit.py similarly. SUITE GAP: no golden-master scenario seeds a content-drifted (or dest-only-file-bearing) namespace DIR — only user-modified root files (different path), so the divergence is invisible. DECISION (Scott): this is 'Python fixes a latent bash bug' (design-doc carve-out) — keep Python's backup behavior, DOCUMENT it as an intentional Parity exception, teach the golden-master differ to accept Python-only <namespace>-backup/ dirs (like it accepts settings backups), and add a scenario seeding a drifted skills/<x>/ to pin the intended divergence. Alternative (match bash's no-backup rm-rf) re-introduces the data loss — reject. NOT a regression from the H.3 blocker fix (untouched code paths; dormant on valid settings).
close_reason: Obsolete — overtaken by PR #191 (commit 8b5b209, 'H.4 collapse install.sh to exec stub + retire golden master suite'), merged after this bead was filed (2026-06-19). The bead's premise is moot: (1) install.sh is now a 6-line exec stub with NO sync_directory/rm-rf/backup logic, so the bash-vs-Python data-loss divergence cannot exist (bash delegates to Python); (2) the golden-master parity suite/differ this bead asked to teach was deleted in #191 — there is no differ to teach and no scenario harness to extend; (3) Python's namespace-dir backup (the safe behavior the bead wanted preserved) is intact AND already tested by test_sync_plan.py::test_existing_dir_is_backed_up_then_cleanly_replaced (line 267). No parity exception to document (no bash counterpart remains). No residual work.

title: Gemini agent transform diverges: pyyaml block-seq vs bash inline tools:[...]
description: Discovered by the H.2 golden-master net (NOT in the G1-G10 audit). tools/gemini.py transform_agent_frontmatter round-trips frontmatter through yaml.safe_dump (default block style): emits tools: as a BLOCK sequence and reflows description block scalars / quoting / spacing. Bash transform_gemini_agent_frontmatter (install.sh:643) is surgical awk: strips skills/color/memory lines, rewrites 'tools: X, Y' -> inline 'tools: [X, Y]', leaves all else byte-for-byte. Agents carrying tools:/block-scalar frontmatter (pr-comment-fixer-team, quality-reviewer, tech-lead) diverge in content. Transform IS wired and strips keys correctly; divergence is serialization format only. Fix: port surgically (line-based, mirror the awk) for byte-identical output. Acceptance: gemini golden-master scenario reaches parity.

title: OpenCode must skip rules/ namespace (ALL-RULES inlines it)
description: H.1 audit. Python installs rules/ files for OpenCode that bash deliberately skips (bash inlines rules into the flat AGENTS.md via ALL-RULES; OpenCode gets no standalone rules/ dir). Fix: omit rules/ namespace for the OpenCode adapter. Acceptance: OpenCode golden-master scenario reaches parity (no orphan rules/ files on the Python side).

title: Implement named DYNAMIC-INCLUDE-RULES: a,b subset form
description: H.1 audit. The named-subset directive (DYNAMIC-INCLUDE-RULES: ruleA,ruleB) is unimplemented in core/templates.py; it passes through as literal text instead of inlining the named rules. Bash honors it. Fix: parse the comma-list and inline only the named rules in order. Acceptance: a scenario/template exercising the named form reaches parity.

title: Claude must be always-on in auto-detect (match bash ALL_TOOLS)
description: H.1 audit. tools/claude.py detects via (~/.claude/settings.json).is_file(); bash treats claude as always-installed ('claude always; others if ~/.<tool>/ exists'). Fresh machine with no settings.json -> Python auto-detect finds no tools -> exits; bash installs claude. Fix: claude unconditionally selected under auto-detect (explicit --tools still honored). Acceptance: fresh-HOME auto-detect golden-master scenario (no --tools) reaches parity; both install claude.
notes: Green-wiring note (from H.2 review): fixing this gap must also green test_autodetect_fresh_home. That requires deterministic tool-detection isolation — opencode/gemini/codex absent from PATH — WHILE keeping bash's required binaries reachable, notably jq (install.sh:189 is a hard precondition; settings union-merge is jq-driven). A bare PATH=/usr/bin:/bin starves the jq guard on stock macOS/CI. Compute the pinned PATH from shutil.which('jq')/('bash') parents + a minimal coreutils set, or use a symlink farm of support binaries. Today the test xfails deterministically via the fresh-HOME claude divergence with no PATH manipulation.

title: B install-wiring W4 — add the --plugins CLI flag
description: Register the missing --plugins CSV flag in cli._build_parser (advertised in AGENTS.md and install.sh but absent; both resolve_plugins call sites hardcode override_csv None). Feed it to resolve_plugins for install, --dump-stage, and --prune. Independent of the sync work.
acceptance_criteria: --plugins as a CSV scopes plugin overlay; absent means auto-detect (current behavior); applies to install, dump-stage, and prune.

title: B install-wiring W2 — interactive install consent UX (per-file diff plus confirm)
description: Build the install-path consent UX, equivalent to the bash confirm and diff which has no Python equivalent (consent.py only guards non-interactive starts). On a changed file: io.show_diff then io.confirm overwrite; decline keeps existing. Honor --yes (auto-accept, still backup first) and --dry-run (preview, no write). Reuse consent.require_consent as the no-TTY guard. Decision: full interactive parity with install.sh user-side from day one. Drives through ScriptedIO.
acceptance_criteria: interactive run shows diff and confirm per changed file; decline keeps file; --yes auto-accepts with backup; --dry-run writes nothing; non-interactive without --yes or --dry-run errors; transcript asserted.

title: B install-wiring W3 — wire main() default path to real install
description: Wire main() default branch (cli.py after Config build, before the prune branch): resolve_plugins then stage_and_transform(tools, repo_root, io, plugins) then install_pipeline honoring Config.auto_yes (currently built then discarded) and --dry-run. Slots ahead of prune. Makes install.py with no flags do a real install and ensures the Gemini frontmatter transform runs in real installs, not just dump and prune. Integrates W1, W2, W4.
acceptance_criteria: install.py no-flags installs to a tmp home through stage, transform, sync; Gemini frontmatter transform runs in the real install; Config.auto_yes drives --yes; integration test asserts files on disk.

title: B install-wiring W1 — plan-walking install sync (StagingPlan to dest_dir, backup, counters)
description: Grow core/sync.py (single-file, test-only today) into a plan-walking install sync that iterates StagingPlan.items plus dir_overrides and writes to adapter.dest_dir(home). Handle DIR-kind items (content None; create directory, model on dump._write_item), honor StagedItem.executable mode bit (0o755 vs 0o644; currently set and read nowhere), hash-compare skip, path-aware backup before overwrite via core/backup.back_up, tally Counters. Wrap as install_pipeline in core/run.py (analog to prune_pipeline). Keystone: W2 and W3 depend on it. TDD via ScriptedIO.
acceptance_criteria: install_pipeline walks a multi-item StagingPlan to a tmp home; DIR items create dirs; executable bit honored; identical files skipped via hash; changed files backed up before overwrite; Counters correct; 90 pct branch coverage.

title: Wire installer orchestrator into cli.main() — full build→transform→sync pipeline
description: D.4 introduced core/orchestrator.py::stage_and_transform (per active adapter: build_plan → post_staging_transforms), unit-tested but deliberately NOT called from cli.main(): the plan-walking sync that would consume the transformed plans does not exist yet (core/sync.py is still the single-file B.2 slice; plan-walking sync arrives with Epic E merge dispatch). When main() is wired to the full install pipeline, it MUST route every active tool through the transform pass (stage_and_transform), not just build_plan→sync — otherwise the Gemini frontmatter transform (and all future adapter transforms) silently never run in real installs. This is the explicit tracker for that wiring step; H.1 golden-master cannot pass without it.
notes: Scoped 2026-06-13. Decomposed into 4 children: hmxpp.1 (W1 plan-walking install sync, keystone), hmxpp.3 (W2 interactive consent UX), hmxpp.4 (W3 wire main), hmxpp.2 (W4 --plugins flag). Sequencing: W1 blocks W2 and W3; W2 and W4 block W3 (integration). Decision: full interactive diff+confirm parity with install.sh from day one (W2 not deferred). hmxpp blocks H.1 golden-master (w1qls.8.1). Grounded in 3-probe code audit: front half (stage_and_transform, merge registry, prune, backup, dump) built and wired; plan-walking sync and default-path install write are MISSING.

title: G.7 — --yes/-y: Config.auto_yes + sync auto-overwrite
description:
