Add Config.auto_yes: bool (from --yes/-y argv) and wire the auto-accept path in sync.py.

Scope:
- Add auto_yes: bool field to Config dataclass
- Wire --yes/-y in cli.py argparse
- sync.py: when auto_yes, hashes-differ branch skips show_diff + confirm and goes straight to path-aware backup + write (backup still runs — auto-yes is never a data-loss path)
- Non-interactive guard: stdin not a TTY + not dry_run + not auto_yes -> hard fail

G.4 already covers the prune side (--yes deletes all without prompting). This story covers the sync side and the Config field both paths share.
acceptance_criteria:
- Config.auto_yes: bool field exists and is populated from --yes/-y argv.
- sync.py: when auto_yes and hashes differ, writes unconditionally (path-aware backup still runs first).
- sync.py: --yes does NOT skip backup — every overwrite is still recoverable.
- Non-interactive stdin without --yes and without --dry-run: installer exits with a clear error.
- --yes + non-interactive stdin: guard does not trigger (this is the intended scripted-install path).
- Golden-master scenario for --yes passes (all overwrites auto-accepted, diff not shown).

title: Add missing edge-case tests for strip_template_suffix
description:
test_staging.py covers the happy paths but is missing edge cases discovered during C.1 review:

1. Embedded .template that is NOT the final suffix — e.g. Path('AGENTS.md.template.bak') should be returned unchanged (last suffix is .bak, not .template). Suggested test name: test_strip_template_suffix_against_embedded_match_is_unchanged.

2. Double .template suffix — e.g. Path('file.template.template') should strip only the outermost, yielding Path('file.template'). Confirms the 'only the final suffix is tested' invariant.

3. Any additional edge cases the implementer identifies during analysis (e.g. a plain no-suffix file like Path('Makefile') if not already implicit in existing coverage).

Each test should follow the existing Arrange/Act/Assert style in test_staging.py.

title: Path-traversal guard when wiring flatten_template into staging
description: When C.1 wires core/templates.py flatten_template into the staging/sync walk, guard the marker-supplied include path against traversal: mirror the sync.py check (reject absolute or '..'-containing relpaths with ValueError), or confine the resolved target with Path.resolve() + is_relative_to(base_dir). B.4 deliberately omits this because the transform is fed exclusively by the project's own trusted template tree, and the bash reference has no such guard — it is an improvement-over-parity to land at the integration seam where the trust model (and the bytes/str boundary) is decided. Surfaced by quality-reviewer during B.4 (w1qls.2.4).

title: F.5 — plugin-to-base-asset extension via YAML patches (Python)
description:
Port the apply_extensions() mechanism from phzj.4 into the Python installer. Plugins declare structured YAML patch files in scope-bearing extension directories (shared: .agents/extensions/, tool-specific: .<tool>/extensions/) that surgically patch base markdown assets (skills, agents, any .md) via target-file + target-section + precision + content. See the full requirements in agents-config-phzj.4 (deferred toward this bead).

Key scope:
- plugins/extensions.py: apply_extensions() called from install pipeline post-staging, once per enabled tool
- YAML schema validation (R2), section matching with fenced-code-awareness (R3), five precision verbs (R4-R5), frontmatter targeting (R5), multi-plugin ordering (R6), terminal failure modes (R7), dry-run support (R8)
- Extraction of merge-and-cleanup skill inline beads cheat block to src/plugins/beads/.agents/extensions/ (phzj.4 AC #8-9)
- Generalization smoke test against a non-SKILL.md base asset
- All AC from phzj.4 translated to Python context (no separate apply-extension.py helper — logic lives in the installer package)
notes: AC #8-9 (cheat-block extraction) re-scoped: merge-and-cleanup skill was never implemented (abn9.14 open) and src/plugins/beads/ was archived — no inline block exists. Mechanism proven by plan-level round-trip test instead; abn9.14 ships the cheat block as a plugin extension from birth.
close_reason: F.5 plugin-to-base-asset extensions shipped via PR #145 (squash 90cd677). md_patch engine + apply_extensions Phase 6.5, 372 tests / 99.77% branch cov. AC#8-9 rescoped to abn9.14 (recorded on both beads + design doc).

title: Promote-contract spec (Holding Place ↔ Orchestrator)
description:
## Scope

Specify the interaction contract between the Holding Place peer subsystem (`agents-config-wgclw.10`) and the PDLC Orchestrator (`agents-config-wgclw.2`). `CONTEXT.md > Holding Place` pins these as the **exactly two** orchestrator-side operations:

1. `promote(idea_id) → objective_id` — transitions an Idea into an Objective; called by the Orchestrator when an Idea reaches Agent-Worthy
2. `create_idea(provenance.decomposition_of=<container_id>)` — called by the Orchestrator during Decomposition to spawn a fresh Idea for a decomposed sub-unit

This bead is the **contract spec**, not the implementation. The contract is bidirectional: both sides bind against it; both sides have obligations.

## How this spec gets populated (Path II — tracer-first TDD)

Per the 2026-05-30 sequencing decision, this spec is populated **incrementally**, as a *derived artifact* of scenario implementations rather than as a precondition for them:

- **Happy-path portion** (operation signatures, idempotency for single-call case, fingerprint propagation, happy-path error taxonomy, reference Python implementation) — derived from `agents-config-wgclw.2.2.1` (Scenario 1 — TRACER BULLET P0). Captured back into this bead's description as the tracer's production code crystallizes
- **3-strike failure portion** — derived from `agents-config-wgclw.2.2.2` (Scenario 2 — 3-strike → AUTOPSY)
- **Decomposition `create_idea` portion** — derived from `agents-config-wgclw.2.2.3` (Scenario 3 — decomposition with mid-flight child kill)
- **Concurrent-edit / CAS-predicate portion** — derived from `agents-config-wgclw.2.2.4` (Scenario 4 — mid-tick human spec-edit)
- **Other failure modes** — as additional failure-mode-scenario beads land

This bead's acceptance closes when the contract is fully populated across all four sections. Until then, it stays open with markers indicating which sections are populated.

## In scope (full target shape; populated incrementally per above)

- Method signatures: request payload, response payload, content schemas
- Error taxonomy with structured error codes (idempotency-violation, not-found, schema-mismatch, storage-error, fingerprint-mismatch, transient-storage-unavailable)
- Idempotency contract: how the Holding Place handles a duplicate `promote(idea_id)` call (one of: idempotent-returns-existing, idempotent-creates-new, non-idempotent-errors — choose and justify)
- Fingerprint discipline: `originating_idea_id` propagation onto the Objective so the WorkTracker can correlate back to its origin
- Transaction boundaries: does `promote` atomically write to both Holding Place storage AND the WorkTracker, or is it two-phase with a marker?
- Failure modes: what does the orchestrator do on transient-storage-unavailable? On permanent error?
- Sequence diagram for the happy path
- Sequence diagrams for at least three failure paths
- Reference Python implementation (shipped as production code in `packages/holding-place/`)

## Happy-path Promote contract (derived from `agents-config-wgclw.2.2.1`)

The happy-path portion of the contract, derived from the Scenario-1 tracer
implementation. Failure-mode portions remain to be derived by the scenarios
listed under acceptance.

### Operation

`promote(idea_id) -> objective_id` — transitions a ready Idea into an
Objective and returns the new Objective's id.

### Boundary direction

The orchestrator does **not** import the Holding Place. The Holding Place
calls *outbound* into a port it owns — `ObjectiveCreator` — which the PDLC
WorkTracker satisfies structurally (dependency-inversion). The peer seam is
this port, not a shared import.

Port signature (owned by the Holding Place; the WorkTracker conforms):

    create_objective(
        *, parent_id: str | None, objective_type: str,
        title: str, body: str, originating_idea_id: str | None,
    ) -> objective_id: str

### Idempotency — idempotent-returns-existing

A duplicate `promote(idea_id)` on an already-promoted Idea returns the
**original** `objective_id` and creates **no** second Objective. Mechanism: on
a successful promote the Idea is stamped with `promoted_objective_id`;
subsequent calls short-circuit on that stamp. Chosen over
idempotent-creates-new (would mint duplicate work) and non-idempotent-errors
(would make crash-retry unsafe).

### Fingerprint propagation

`originating_idea_id` is passed to `create_objective` and carried onto the
resulting Objective record, so the work tracker / orchestrator can correlate
the Objective back to its originating Idea. Canonical provenance ownership is
the orchestrator sidecar (per the State-Ownership boundary); the reference
WorkTracker adapter retains the backreference so propagation is verifiable.

### Precondition & happy-path error taxonomy

- **Precondition**: the Idea must be `ready_for_promote` (the Holding-Place
  grooming/shaping bar). Violation -> `NotReadyForPromoteError`. The tracer
  seeds readiness directly, standing in for the Idea Curator's judgment.
- **idea-not-found**: an unknown `idea_id` raises `IdeaNotFoundError` from the
  storage layer.
- Deferred to failure-mode portions: storage-error, fingerprint-mismatch,
  transient-storage-unavailable, idempotency-violation under concurrency.

### Transaction boundary (happy path)

`promote` is two-step: (1) `create_objective` on the WorkTracker, then (2)
stamp the Idea `promoted_objective_id` in Holding-Place storage. Not atomic.
The idempotency guard makes a crash between the steps recoverable: a re-promote
returns the existing Objective if the stamp landed, or re-creates if it did not
(at-least-once on create, idempotent-read on the Idea side). Full two-phase /
transactional analysis is deferred to the failure-mode portions.

### Reference implementation

- Holding Place side: `packages/holding-place/` — `HoldingPlace.promote`, the
  `ObjectiveCreator` port, `FilesystemIdeaStorage` (YAML-per-Idea MVP backend).
- Work-tracker side: `packages/pdlc/` — `InMemoryWorkTracker.create_objective`
  (reference four-domain adapter).
- Exercised end-to-end by the Scenario-1 tracer integration test and
  unit-tested in `packages/holding-place/tests/`.

## Out of scope (in other epics)

- Holding Place storage implementation → parent epic (`agents-config-wgclw.10`) and its other children
- Orchestrator-side dispatch of `promote` → PDLC Orchestrator Core epic
- Decomposition Architect persona that emits `create_idea` calls → PDLC Design Phase epic

## Relationship to the tracer

`agents-config-wgclw.2.2.1` (tracer) was originally `blocks`ed by this bead. That dep was removed on 2026-05-30 when Path II was chosen — the tracer now PRODUCES this spec's content rather than waiting for it. A `related-to` edge preserves the bead-graph link for navigation. The two beads close together when all four contract sections are populated and the tracer's tests pass.

## Acceptance criteria

Closes when ALL four section markers are populated:

- [x] Happy-path section (derived from `agents-config-wgclw.2.2.1`) — populated above
- [ ] 3-strike failure section (derived from `agents-config-wgclw.2.2.2`)
- [ ] Decomposition `create_idea` section (derived from `agents-config-wgclw.2.2.3`)
- [ ] Concurrent-edit / CAS-predicate section (derived from `agents-config-wgclw.2.2.4`)

Plus:
- Sequence diagrams for happy path and at least three failure modes
- Idempotency contract is unambiguous and justified
- Reference Python implementation lives in `packages/holding-place/` and is callable by the test harnesses
- Build passes. Typecheck passes. Tests pass.

## Provenance

Filed during the PDLC tracer-scoping grill session (2026-05-25) after the `wgclw.4/.5/.6` sweep surfaced this as the single cross-epic contract the tracer literally exercises. Reframed to Path II tracer-first-TDD discipline on 2026-05-30 — this bead now derives content from scenario implementations rather than gating them. Other cross-epic seams (Spec format, Reviewer Toolbox dispatch, Finding payload, RCA inputs, Copilot adapter, HEP cross-milestone, WorkTracker ↔ bd-shim) are documented in `agents-config-wgclw.11` (far-horizon register) and may earn their own contract beads as they become near-horizon.

title: PDLC Holding Place — Idea pipeline peer subsystem
description:
## Scope

The Holding Place is the **peer subsystem** that owns the Idea pipeline (Capture → Grooming → Shape → Promote → Killed-with-Epitaph). It is NOT owned by the PDLC orchestrator. The orchestrator's calls into this service are exactly two, per `CONTEXT.md > Holding Place (working name: Icebox)`:

- `promote(idea_id) → objective_id` — transitions an Idea into an Objective tracked by the WorkTracker
- `create_idea(provenance.decomposition_of=<container_id>)` — emits an Idea back into Holding Place during Decomposition

Recorded in ADR-0001 (`docs/adr/0001-holding-place-is-a-peer-subsystem.md`).

## In scope

- Service interface: well-typed surface for the two orchestrator-facing operations plus the human-facing pipeline (Capture / Groom / Shape / Promote / Kill)
- Idea primitive: schema, provenance metadata, bucket position (Now / Next / Later / Library + Killed terminus), grooming-state metadata (last-groomed timestamp), epitaph for killed Ideas
- Capture protocol: how Ideas enter the system (human-typed, decomposition-emitted, brainstorm-emitted)
- Provenance Backreference: every Idea carries its origin trail (free text, decomposition_of, brainstorm-quarantined, etc.)
- Grooming ceremony UX: bucket promotion/demotion; the grooming nag (deterministic threshold)
- Cool Idea Quarantine UX hook (inside brainstorming)
- **Storage abstraction**: pluggable storage adapter such that MVP = filesystem (likely YAML-per-Idea under `<holding-place-root>/ideas/<idea-id>.yml`), post-MVP swappable to richer backends (Dolt, SQLite, Postgres, cloud KV) by config — not by code rewrite. Mirrors the WorkTracker adapter pattern.
- Idea Curator persona (transcribes only)

## Out of scope (in other epics)

- Promote-contract spec (interaction contract between Holding Place and Orchestrator) → separate bead under this epic
- Orchestrator-side handling of Promote and create_idea → PDLC Orchestrator Core epic
- Design Interrogator persona + Spec authoring workflow → PDLC Design Phase epic (Spec is a post-Promote Objective-side artifact)
- Decomposition Architect persona + Sizing Gate + Decomposition Plan → PDLC Design Phase epic (all post-Promote)
- Project-config schema → PDLC Orchestrator Core epic

## Why this is a peer subsystem (not orchestrator-owned)

The Idea pipeline and the Objective pipeline are **distinct primitives** with distinct lifecycles, storage, and human-interaction patterns. Mixing them would re-import the entanglement this architecture is explicitly trying to avoid. The orchestrator drives Objectives through a deterministic FSM; the Holding Place is a human-curated workspace for Ideas where judgment lives. Two operations bridge them.

## Success Criteria

- An Idea can be captured, groomed across buckets, shaped, and promoted to an Objective end-to-end against fixture inputs
- The two orchestrator-facing operations conform to the documented Promote contract (separate bead)
- Storage adapter passes fixture tests against the filesystem MVP backend
- Build passes. Typecheck passes. Tests pass.

## Provenance

Carved out of the PDLC Design Phase epic during the PDLC tracer-scoping grill session (2026-05-25). Holding Place was originally listed as in-scope for the Design Phase epic; this contradicted `CONTEXT.md`'s explicit "peer service, NOT owned by the Orchestrator" framing. Resolved as a peer subsystem with pluggable storage.

Children to be filed lazily as decomposition reaches them: full service spec (incorporates the prior g6ix3 service-spec bead reparented here), MVP filesystem storage adapter, Idea Curator persona spec, grooming ceremony UX, bucket / nag deterministic-threshold implementation, Cool Idea Quarantine integration.

title: Design shared bd-shim CLI (quarantine beads behind 'wt'-style abstraction)
description:
## Context

The prgroom CLI (Go, fca6 epic) and the PDLC orchestrator (Python, wgclw.2 epic and descendants) both wrap bd for their own purposes. Each tool's wrapper carries the same kinds of bd-quirk shims independently:

- jq path drift between bd versions
- parent-child auto-edge semantics
- task→epic dep-type restrictions
- bd label list / bd dep list output shape changes
- error translation, lock-contention retry

AGENTS.md (project-level) explicitly chartered this work under M0: 'Work vs beads — we'll be taking a step back from making that a first class citizen of this project's architecture, placing a work abstraction in front of it — end state is that beads is quarantined behind our own CLI'.

## What this bead is NOT

- It is NOT a unified `WorkTracker` interface across tools. prgroom's `Store` (a per-PR KV+lock blob store) and PDLC's `WorkTracker` (an Objective registry with CAS/fingerprints/Discovery markers) abstract genuinely different data and lifecycles. The naming collision is coincidental, not architectural.

- It is NOT a replacement for either tool's internal state-persistence interface. Both tools keep their own typed adapter layer; this CLI is what their bd-adapter sub-implementations shell out to.

## What it IS

A single CLI (language TBD — Python or Go; runtime-irrelevant since it's a CLI surface) that abstracts bd's command quirks. Both prgroom and PDLC orchestrator shell out to it whenever their bd-adapter is active. Schema/format changes in bd land in one place.

## Open design questions (to brainstorm before any code)

1. **Surface**: subcommand verbs (`wt objective list`, `wt dep add`, ...) or thin pass-through with normalization filters? Hybrid?
2. **Output contract**: machine-parseable JSON as primary; human-friendly format opt-in?
3. **Versioning**: how does a consumer pin a wt protocol version? `wt --protocol-version`?
4. **Naming**: `wt`, `bdsh`, `work`, something else?
5. **Sequencing vs in-flight work**: should this land before prgroom MVP ships, or after (with prgroom's bd adapter being deferred from v2 to post-shim)?
6. **PDLC integration shape**: PDLC's WorkTracker spec is still in flux (wgclw.2). When should it commit to consuming this shim vs continuing to bind bd directly?

## Why M0 child (not orphan)

M0's stated end-state explicitly includes 'beads is quarantined behind our own CLI'. This bead is exactly that work. Closing it satisfies one of M0's chartered outcomes.

## Provenance

Surfaced during fca6.12 (prgroom architecture diagrams) on 2026-05-25: when the L1 diagrams of both prgroom and the PDLC orchestrator showed a 'Work Tracker' external system, the question 'can we share this?' surfaced. After analysis, the answer was: not the WorkTracker interfaces themselves (different data shapes), but yes — the bd-quirk-shim CLI underneath. A pre-emptive rename of prgroom's `WorkTracker` to `prsession.Store` (landed in fca6.12) preserves the distinction at the interface level.

## Far-horizon register

Open design question #6 above is mirrored as architectural question Q2 in `agents-config-wgclw.11` (PDLC architecture far-horizon register), along with seam S8 (WorkTracker abstraction ↔ bd-shim CLI). The register is the cross-epic index; this bead remains the authoritative home for the shim design.

title: PDLC Orchestrator: Scenario 7 — Discovery-Sweep marker miss caught by full-reconcile
description: Integration test for Appendix A scenario 7: Discovery-Sweep marker is missed (e.g., during a crash window); full-reconcile catches the gap and corrects state. Validates the reconciliation guard's safety-net role. Exercises Integration Pipeline (agents-config-wgclw.5).

title: PDLC Orchestrator: Scenario 5 — Config-version divergence on in-flight Session
description: Integration test for Appendix A scenario 5: orchestrator config version changes while a Session is in flight. Validates version-pinning, divergence detection, and Session-completion semantics under config drift. Exercises execution pipeline (agents-config-wgclw.4).

title: PDLC Orchestrator: Scenario 6 — Tracker unreachable then recovered
description: Integration test for Appendix A scenario 6: WMS tracker (beads or successor abstraction) becomes unreachable mid-orchestration; orchestrator retries and recovers when service returns. Validates resilience layer of the tracker abstraction; depends on core infrastructure landing in agents-config-wgclw.2 itself (not a downstream sub-epic).

title: PDLC Orchestrator: Scenario 3 — Decomposition with mid-flight child kill
description: Integration test for Appendix A scenario 3: parent spawns two children via decomposition; one is killed mid-flight. Validates lifecycle handling, partial-failure cleanup, and parent-state semantics under uneven child completion. Exercises Stages 7-9 execution pipeline (agents-config-wgclw.4).

title: PDLC Orchestrator: Scenario 4 — Mid-tick human spec-edit
description: Integration test for Appendix A scenario 4: human edits Idea/spec while an orchestrator tick is in flight. Validates spec re-read semantics, state isolation, and divergence detection. Exercises Design Phase (agents-config-wgclw.3) and Execution Pipeline (agents-config-wgclw.4).

title: PDLC Orchestrator: Scenario 1 — TRACER BULLET P0 (happy-path Idea→MERGED)
description:
## Scope

Integration test for Appendix A scenario 1 from `agents-config-wgclw.2`: happy-path single Objective traversal from Idea-promote through the full PDLC pipeline to MERGED. **ARCHITECTURAL TRACER** — validates the end-to-end orchestrator skeleton before sibling sub-epics fan out.

Per Path II tracer-first TDD discipline (sequencing decision locked 2026-05-30 during this bead's scoping grill), this bead's scope includes **the minimum-viable production code** needed to make the test pass, PLUS **derived-contract capture** back into `agents-config-wgclw.10.1` (the Promote-contract spec) as the production code crystallizes.

## In scope

- **Integration test**: `packages/pdlc/tests/integration/test_scenario_1_tracer_bullet.py` (pytest, following `packages/installer/` precedent — Python 3.11+, uv, pyproject.toml + hatchling, pytest + pytest-xdist + pytest-cov, ruff + mypy)
- **Minimum-viable production code** at the seam layers:
  - `packages/holding-place/` — Holding Place service with: filesystem storage adapter (MVP), Idea primitive, `promote(idea_id) → objective_id` operation (real both sides)
  - `packages/pdlc/` — Orchestrator with: FSM engine (real), WorkTracker abstraction with bd adapter (real), JobSupervisor minimum-spawn-tracking (real-thin), TransitionLog persistence (real), tick loop (real)
- **Stubs for non-seam work**: every persona returns canned success; every gate returns canned pass; gate-evidence files are canned-pass YAML on disk; no real worker subprocess fork
- **Test harness**: pre-seeds an Idea with `ready_for_promote=true` (bypasses the Idea Curator's judgment); drives `orchestrator.tick()` in a bounded loop (`max_ticks=12`); asserts the TransitionLog
- **Derived-contract capture**: discovered Promote-contract shape (happy-path signatures, idempotency semantics, fingerprint propagation, happy-path error taxonomy) gets written back into `agents-config-wgclw.10.1`'s description as the code crystallizes

## Locked design decisions (from 2026-05-30 grill)

1. **Skeleton boundary** — real seams, stub interiors (Pragmatic Programmer tracer-bullet discipline)
2. **Happy-path sequence** — 10 states: `CANDIDATE_UOW → AGENT_WORTHY → DECOMPOSE → EXECUTABLE_READY → TEST_AUTHORING → IMPLEMENTING → REVIEWING → PR_VALIDATION → MERGING → MERGED`. Skipped by happy-path definition: `CONTAINER_DECOMPOSED`, `PR_HUMAN_HOLD`, `AUTOPSY`, `KILLED`, `PARKED`
3. **Mock contracts** — tracer binds against production interfaces; no new tracer-specific mocks
4. **TransitionLog assertion** — full-sequence strict-on-essence: exact 9-entry sequence; strict on `from_stage`, `to_stage`, `reason`, `actor`; flexible on `ts`, `config_hash`. Gate-bearing stages: `gate_evidence_ref` non-null + file exists + parseable YAML; no content assertions (would be brittle to the unresolved `worker-report-v1` seam)
5. **Language/layout** — Python 3.11+, uv, pytest, ruff, mypy; `packages/pdlc/` + `packages/holding-place/` as two packages (peer-subsystem seam enforced by import surface per ADR-0001)
6. **Tick driver** — direct Python loop in test harness; no real cron interpreter; no time injection

## Out of scope (in other beads)

- Failure-mode coverage (3-strike → AUTOPSY) → `agents-config-wgclw.2.2.2`
- Decomposition + mid-flight child kill → `agents-config-wgclw.2.2.3`
- Mid-tick human spec-edit → `agents-config-wgclw.2.2.4`
- Config-version divergence → `agents-config-wgclw.2.2.5`
- Tracker unreachable / discovery-sweep marker miss → `agents-config-wgclw.2.2.6/.7`
- PR_HUMAN_HOLD path → covered by `agents-config-wgclw.2.4` register
- Full Promote-contract spec (failure-mode sequence diagrams etc.) → derived by THIS bead for happy-path portion; remainder derived by failure-mode scenarios as they land
- Holding Place service spec beyond what the tracer exercises → `agents-config-g6ix3` and other future children of `agents-config-wgclw.10`

## Success Criteria

- `packages/pdlc/tests/integration/test_scenario_1_tracer_bullet.py` exists, runs, and passes against the minimum-viable production code in `packages/pdlc/` + `packages/holding-place/`
- All 9 expected transitions appear in the TransitionLog in correct order with correct essence-fields
- All gate-bearing stages have a non-null, existing, YAML-parseable `gate_evidence_ref`
- The final TransitionLog entry has `to_stage = MERGED`
- `agents-config-wgclw.10.1` description gets a happy-path-portion of the Promote contract (signatures, idempotency semantics, fingerprint propagation, happy-path error taxonomy) captured as a derived artifact
- Build passes. Typecheck passes. Tests pass.

## Provenance

Grilled to design-complete on 2026-05-30 via the `/grill-with-docs` skill. Six design decisions locked; structural prerequisites surfaced and resolved as bead surgery (Holding Place peer epic `agents-config-wgclw.10` carved out, Promote-contract spec home `agents-config-wgclw.10.1` filed, far-horizon register `agents-config-wgclw.11` filed, ADR-0001 written, scenario-coverage register `agents-config-wgclw.2.4` filed). Originally `blocks`ed by `agents-config-wgclw.10.1`; that dep was removed when Path II (tracer-first TDD) was chosen — the contract spec now derives from this bead's implementation rather than gating it. A `related-to` edge preserves the bead-graph link.
close_reason: Implemented + merged in PR #110 (squash 7925968). Scenario-1 tracer bullet green: 29 tests, mypy --strict + ruff clean, coverage pdlc 99.31% / holding-place 100%. Promote contract captured into wgclw.10.1.

title: PDLC Orchestrator: Scenario 2 — 3-strike Implementer → AUTOPSY
description: Integration test for Appendix A scenario 2: implementer fails 3 consecutive cycles, triggers circuit-breaker into AUTOPSY phase (dual-RCA: Specification + Architecture-Health). Requires Stage 11 autopsy machinery from agents-config-wgclw.6.

title: Fix wait-for-pr-comments post-replies.sh silently failing on specific reply bodies
description:
## Symptom

`post-replies.sh` (in the `wait-for-pr-comments` skill) emits `FAILED <comment_id> gh-rest-reply-failed` for some inventory items but POSTs the rest successfully. The script's per-item error reporting drops the comment_id from the failure line — observed output was just `FAILED  gh-rest-reply-failed` (empty comment_id), which makes diagnosis painful.

## Concrete observation (PR #105, round 2 of this invocation)

Inventory had 5 items, all `is_outdated: true` review_threads. Two FAILEDs and three POSTEDs. Re-running without `--skip-comment-ids` (idempotent-violation) created duplicate posts for the three previously-successful items AND failed again on the same problematic item.

After manual diagnosis:
- Comment 3297125890 (TodoWrite header thread) — script consistently failed
- A manual `gh api repos/.../pulls/105/comments/3297125890/replies -F body="..."` posted SUCCESSFULLY using the same comment_id and a body containing apostrophes
- The script's failure was NOT due to GitHub rejecting the reply — it was script-internal

## Likely root causes (one of, both, or other)

1. **Body content escaping**: `post-replies.sh` may be using a JSON-body or stdin pipeline that mangles content with apostrophes / single quotes / parentheses. The successful manual post used `gh api ... -F body="..."` (form-encoded). The script may be using `--input` / heredoc / printf which can choke on multi-line bodies with quoted phrases.

2. **Per-item failure swallowing**: Whatever the underlying gh-rest call's stderr was, the script collapsed it to `gh-rest-reply-failed` without emitting the actual API error. This makes the failure essentially silent.

3. **Empty comment_id in FAILED line**: The `FAILED  gh-rest-reply-failed` output has two spaces where the comment_id should be — the script is interpolating an empty variable.

## What to fix

1. Capture and surface the actual gh-rest error in the FAILED output (stderr from the gh call).
2. Audit the body-encoding pipeline — prefer `-F body="$BODY"` or `--field body=@<file>` over inline JSON construction.
3. Fix the comment_id interpolation so FAILED lines name the problematic comment.
4. Address the idempotency concern: the script docs say "NOT idempotent — caller MUST pass `--skip-comment-ids`" but `post-replies.sh` could itself probe the PR's existing replies and skip already-posted ones, OR write a per-run results file that subsequent runs read automatically. This would prevent the duplicate-post recovery mess.

## Provenance

Discovered during PR #105 round-2 invocation of wait-for-pr-comments / reply-and-resolve-pr-threads on agents-config (2026-05-25). The script lives at `/Users/scott/.claude/skills/reply-and-resolve-pr-threads/post-replies.sh` (deployed) — source likely under `src/user/.claude/skills/` or wherever the skill is authored.

## Acceptance

- FAILED output lines name the comment_id and surface the underlying gh error.
- Body content with apostrophes, parentheses, and multi-line text reliably posts.
- Idempotency story improved (auto-skip or self-recording) so accidental re-runs don't duplicate-post.
close_reason: PR #108 merged. Fixed post-replies.sh silent failures: per-kind cid dispatch, gh-stderr surfacing, body encoding, sidecar idempotency, review_summary support. Absorbed agents-config-lutqu.

comments.author: Scott Hamilton
comments.text: Absorbed agents-config-lutqu (closed as duplicate). Adds explicit scope: post-replies.sh stdout MUST match documented contract 'POSTED <comment_id>' for all 3 inventory kinds (review_thread → .reply_to_comment_id, issue_comment → .issue_comment_id, review_summary → .thread_id or equivalent). Add post-replies_test.sh assertion that POSTED line includes a non-empty token after the space.

title: Skill B: post-cap-exceeded fixup entry point (no standalone fatality)
description:
reply-and-resolve-pr-threads (Skill B) has no clean invocation path for the recurring scenario: 'Skill A trip the round cap, ESCALATEs were deferred, the user later addresses some/all of them on a single follow-up commit, and now wants Skill B to reply + resolve.'

The current behavior:
- Standalone invocation (no --from-inventory / no --resume) is a fatal startup error in v1 (SKILL.md Phase 0 rule 5).
- SKILL.md notes 'Standalone mode is deferred to a follow-up bead' but no bead appears to exist.
- The original Skill A inventory has been unlinked by Phase 9 by the time the cap-exceeded items are addressed, so --resume is also unavailable.
- The only working path is to hand-build a schema_version=1 JSON file by:
    1. ls'ing ~/.claude/state/pr-inventory/ to learn the naming convention
    2. Reading an existing inventory to crib the schema
    3. Querying GraphQL for the unresolved thread IDs + REST for comment IDs
    4. Filling in fix_outcome=committed + fix_commit_sha + fix_summary + fix_gate_variant per item
    5. Pre-rendering reply_body or trusting render-reply-bodies.sh
    6. Running validate-inventory.sh --phase 0
    7. Invoking Skill B --from-inventory <path>

This is ~100 lines of hand-built JSON and undocumented operator knowledge. Hit on PR #104 (this session) — worked, but should not be the canonical path.

PROPOSED OPTIONS (pick one during brainstorm):

(A) New helper script in Skill B: cap-fixup-inventory.sh
    Args: --pr <n> --commit <sha> --fix-summary <text-or-template> [--threads <id-list>]
    Behavior: query GraphQL for unresolved review threads, pair each with the fix-commit-sha and a per-thread or shared fix_summary, write the inventory to the canonical state path, validate, return the path. Caller then invokes Skill B with --from-inventory.

(B) New Skill B mode: --fixup <pr> --commit <sha> --fix-summaries <path-to-yaml-or-csv>
    Construct the inventory in-skill, then proceed through Phases 0-4 as normal. Removes the standalone fatality by re-routing it to a constrained mode.

(C) Document the manual hand-build path as a recovery procedure in SKILL.md's 'Concurrency recovery' table or a new 'Standalone recovery' section, with a worked example. Cheapest; preserves the deferred-standalone gate; just makes the manual path discoverable.

DESIGN QUESTIONS FOR BRAINSTORM:
- Does the cap-exceeded fixup pattern overlap with a 'Skill A re-run from current PR state' mode? If so, this might belong upstream in Skill A as a 'no-await, re-classify, re-reply' mode rather than a Skill B entry point.
- How do we know which threads the fix-commit addressed? (Operator declares per-thread; or fuzzy-match against diff hunks; or scope-by-default to all unresolved threads.)
- Does the helper file the inventory under the canonical state-path naming, or somewhere distinct so Skill A's Phase 1 concurrency check doesn't collide?

DEFINITION OF DONE:
- One of (A)/(B)/(C) selected during brainstorm.
- If (A) or (B): implementation + tests + SKILL.md updated to document the new entry point.
- If (C): SKILL.md updated with the recovery procedure; deferred-standalone gate explicitly cross-references.
- A second cap-exceeded PR is run through the new path end-to-end to validate.

DISCOVERED-FROM: session 2026-05-25 retrospective (PR #104 / fca6.2 round 4) — first observed standalone-invocation gap in real use; the SKILL.md 'deferred to follow-up bead' note is now this bead.
close_reason: Superseded by prgroom CLI design (fca6). The 100-line hand-built JSON inventory path goes away entirely — the cap-exceeded + escalation scenario maps to prgroom's human-gated phase + resolve-escalated verb. Residual UX question (whether --max-rounds raise + re-run is sufficient recovery UX, or whether resolve-escalated should support --extend-cap N to clear LIFECYCLE_HARD_CAP_EXCEEDED from BlockingErrorCodes in one step) filed as a child bead under fca6.

title: post-replies.sh prints 'POSTED ' with empty comment-IDs (jq path wrong)
description:
Helper script src/user/.agents/skills/reply-and-resolve-pr-threads/post-replies.sh emits 'POSTED ' (empty comment-id after the space) for every successfully-posted reply, contradicting its documented contract 'POSTED <comment_id>'.

ROOT CAUSE (confirmed by inspection on session 2026-05-25 / PR #104):

Line 82: cid="$(echo "$item" | jq -r '.comment_id // empty')"

Inventory items written by build-inventory-body.sh (Skill A) have no top-level .comment_id field. The actual ID fields per kind are:
  - kind=review_thread     → .reply_to_comment_id  (numeric REST id)
  - kind=review_summary    → no inline id (reply via gh pr comment)
  - kind=issue_comment     → .issue_comment_id

So cid is always empty for valid inventory items, and lines 112/135 print 'POSTED ' (whitespace + newline).

IMPACT:
- Operator-facing output looks like a no-op. False-negative panic on every Skill B run.
- Forces redundant manual verification via gh api queries (compounded by gh-pr-comments-pagination bug, see [[gh-pr-comments-pagination]] memory).
- Tempts the operator into duplicate-posting (observed on PR #104 thread 1, comment 3296919182 — got an extra reply because the empty cid output looked like the script failed).

FIX:
- Update line 82 to pick the right id per kind: 
    cid="$(echo "$item" | jq -r '.reply_to_comment_id // .issue_comment_id // .thread_id // empty')"
  (or kind-dispatch via case)
- Add post-replies_test.sh assertion: 'POSTED line includes a non-empty token after the space.'
- Re-run the live Skill B path on a fixture inventory in CI smoke if available.

DEFINITION OF DONE:
- post-replies.sh stdout matches the documented contract 'POSTED <comment_id>' for all 3 kinds.
- Test added that fails on the bug and passes after the fix.
- Skill installed to verify the deployed copy under ~/.claude/skills/ matches src/.

DISCOVERED-FROM: session 2026-05-25 retrospective (PR #104 / fca6.2 round 4).
close_reason: Duplicate of agents-config-7kgbk (same root cause: empty cid var from wrong jq path)

comments.author: Scott Hamilton
comments.text: Superseded by agents-config-7kgbk. Same root cause: line 82 jq path '.comment_id // empty' is wrong — the empty cid variable is the source of both 'POSTED ' (this bead) and 'FAILED  gh-rest-reply-failed' (7kgbk #3). Closing as duplicate; 7kgbk absorbs the POSTED-contract acceptance.

title: prgroom architecture diagrams (C4 L1-L3 + sequence + data)
description:
# Architecture diagrams for prgroom CLI

Filed during fca6.2 (Section 4 — Quiescence model) brainstorm closure on 2026-05-25. Operator (Scott) authorized P1 priority once the §4 spec artifact is complete.

## Deliverables

- **C4 Level 1 (System Context)** — prgroom CLI in its broader environment (GitHub, gh CLI, claude/codex agent CLIs, scheduler/cron, operator, bd tracker via WorkTracker interface)
- **C4 Level 2 (Container)** — prgroom internals at the container/process level: \`cmd/prgroom\` entry, \`internal/lifecycle\` (runLocked + verbs), \`internal/tracker\` (WorkTracker adapters), \`internal/agent\` (Contract A/B dispatch), \`internal/gh\` (GitHub API wrapper), \`internal/escalation\` (EscalationSink + human-review label)
- **C4 Level 3 (Component)** — drilldown of \`internal/lifecycle\`: the runLocked control flow with sub-component breakdown (poll → cluster → fix → push → rereview → reply → resolve → wait), showing call sites of escalate_if_needed and request_human_review_if_needed
- **Sequence diagrams** (4-6 key flows):
  - Happy path: PR push → review feedback → fix → push → quiesce
  - Bot silence path: Copilot doesn't engage → review_start_timeout auto-decline → quiesce
  - Hard-cap path: 3 rounds without quiescence → human-gated + auto-add human-review-required label
  - Resumability path: process crash mid-waitLocked → next invocation re-evaluates timeouts from stored UTC timestamps
- **Data diagrams** — entity-relationship for state schema (PRGroomingState, ReviewItem, Disposition, ReviewerState, QuiescenceState) and JSON contract objects (status output per §4.6, escalation events per Section 5)

## Location convention

All architecture artifacts under \`./docs/architecture/{component|feature}/\`:

- \`./docs/architecture/component/prgroom/c4-l1-system-context.{md,svg}\`
- \`./docs/architecture/component/prgroom/c4-l2-container.{md,svg}\`
- \`./docs/architecture/component/prgroom/c4-l3-lifecycle.{md,svg}\`
- \`./docs/architecture/feature/pr-grooming-lifecycle/sequence-*.{md,svg}\`
- \`./docs/architecture/component/prgroom/data-state-schema.{md,svg}\`

## Source-of-truth coupling

Diagrams are derived artifacts. Source of truth remains \`docs/plans/2026-05-12-prgroom-cli-design.md\` (Sections 1-8). Diagrams should cite the spec section they visualize and be regenerated when the spec changes.

## Tooling preferences (to confirm during brainstorm)

- C4: Structurizr DSL or PlantUML C4 macros (both render to SVG; both are textual sources friendly to git diffs)
- Sequence: Mermaid (rendered inline in markdown viewer) or PlantUML
- Data: Mermaid ER syntax or D2

## Acceptance criteria

C4 L1, L2, L3 diagrams produced for prgroom CLI. At least 4 sequence diagrams covering the named flows above. Data diagrams for state schema and key JSON contract objects. All artifacts under \`./docs/architecture/{component|feature}/\`. Tooling choice documented in a one-page README at \`./docs/architecture/README.md\`. Build/regeneration approach documented (manual or scripted).

## Provenance

Filed under fca6 epic. Surfaced via fca6.2 brainstorm; not blocked by other fca6 children. Recommended sequencing: after fca6.2 closes (Section 4 spec ratified), before or in parallel with fca6.10 [Impl] Section 3.
close_reason: prgroom HLD artifact set complete: all 8 diagrams drawn, 3 Copilot review rounds addressed (11 threads fixed/resolved), PR #109 open awaiting merge authorization.

title: PDLC Orchestrator: 7 integration-test scenarios (Round-1 acceptance corpus)
description: Implement the 7 integration test scenarios specified in agents-config-wgclw.2 design spec Appendix A: (1) TRACER BULLET P0 — happy-path single Objective Idea-promote -> MERGED, (2) 3-strike Implementer -> AUTOPSY, (3) Decomposition with two children one killed mid-flight, (4) mid-tick human spec-edit, (5) config-version divergence on in-flight Session, (6) tracker unreachable then recovered, (7) Discovery-Sweep marker miss caught by full-reconcile. Test code is impl-child territory; this bead is the umbrella that downstream impl-children will satisfy.
close_reason: Superseded by restructure: 7 acceptance-corpus children re-parented to their exercising sub-epics (wgclw.2 for tracer + tracker-recovery; .4 for execution scenarios; .5 for Discovery-Sweep; .6 for autopsy) so close-walk gates each epic on its own integration test. Appendix A scenarios from wgclw.2 remain canonical.

title: Holding Place service spec (PDLC Idea pipeline)
description: Specify the Holding Place service that owns the Idea-stage pipeline (Capture, Grooming, Bucket, Killed-without-Spec, Promote). Per agents-config-wgclw.2 Round-1 revision pass (CA-8 Option A): Idea is a distinct primitive from Objective; the Orchestrator does NOT drive Idea lifecycle. The Holding Place is a peer CLI-visible service. The orchestrator's only interaction is promote(idea_id) -> objective_id. This bead specs the peer service.

title: PDLC Orchestrator HLD multi-view architectural artifacts
description:
Produce a set of high-level design artifacts for the PDLC Orchestrator BEFORE any implementation child of wgclw.2 opens. Purpose: cement the big picture so decomposition and downstream impl beads ride on a shared mental model. This bead must close (or its outputs must be available) before any sibling implementation child is started.

Artifacts to produce (all in docs/specs/ or docs/primers/, Mermaid or ASCII where applicable):

1. **Block diagram** — top-level components: pdlc CLI, OrchestratorStateRepo, WorkTracker (and bd adapter), Session/JobSupervisor, project-config loader, persona dispatch. Show data flow + control flow.
2. **Sequence diagram(s)** — at minimum: (a) a single tick cycle (DISCOVER → RECONCILE → REAP → DISPATCH → PERSIST), (b) an Objective traversing CANDIDATE_UOW → MERGED happy-path with worker dispatches at each gate.
3. **State machine view** — the full PDLC lifecycle stage graph including retry edges, 3-strike → AUTOPSY routing, container-decomposition divergence, and terminal states (MERGED / KILLED / PARKED).
4. **Data-flow / persistence view** — what data lives in the tracker vs OrchestratorStateRepo (per L7 / Concept Area 4); how marker semantics and transition logs flow.
5. **Deployment view** — single-host MVP topology (CLI process, cron trigger, worker subprocesses, worktree filesystem, tracker store). Explicitly mark multi-host as post-MVP.

These artifacts serve human decomposition and onboarding; they are reference material, not load-bearing code.
acceptance_criteria:
All five HLD views exist as committed artifacts under docs/specs/ or docs/primers/ with clear filenames.
Each artifact is referenced from the wgclw.2 design spec OR from a single index file under docs/specs/.
The artifacts use named Lifecycle Stage Constants (IDEA_RAW, CANDIDATE_UOW, ..., MERGED, KILLED, PARKED) consistently with the design spec — no cryptic numeric stage IDs in production code or persistent diagrams.
Build passes. Typecheck passes. Tests pass. (Universal — applies to any code introduced; this bead is primarily docs.)
notes: Provenance: review-feedback CA-3 (S-09 from Scott crit). Filed during Phase 2 of wobbly-wiggling-pizza plan (Round 1 review preparation before /ultraplan invocation). This bead is intended to BLOCK all sibling implementation children of wgclw.2 — downstream impl-bead filers must add a 'blocks' dep on this bead when they are created. The Mermaid happy-path flowchart inserted into the design spec at Phase 2 is a sketch, not a substitute for this bead's deliverables.

title: PDLC Autopsy & Recovery (Stage 11)
description:
## Scope

Implements the PDLC's Autopsy state (stage 11) — the failure branch that diagnoses 3-Strike Circuit Breaker firings and routes the UoW back into the FSM via the closed Resolution Route taxonomy.

Implements stage 11 specified in `docs/specs/2026-05-19-pdlc-state-machine-design.md`.

## In scope

- 3-Strike entry handling: Execution Pipeline halt for the affected UoW; branch and worktree FROZEN (not destroyed) as forensic evidence; strike history, gate logs, and commit SHA chain preserved
- Specification RCA Agent: reads Spec + strike history + gate-failure + worktree state; emits structured machine-readable diagnosis (YAML/JSON) with named root-cause categories and citations
- Architecture Health RCA Agent: reads worktree at strike-3 + codebase context at fault sites + strike history; emits structured machine-readable diagnosis on architectural concerns
- Interactive interrogation capability: both RCA agents remain available (read-only) for human follow-up questions during autopsy resolution
- Autopsy Resolution Routes dispatcher: closed taxonomy (1 re-brainstorm, 2 re-decompose, 3 Killed, 4 Architectural-Debt-Park, 5 Tooling-Escalate)
- Branch destruction at autopsy-bead resolution (NOT at entry)

## Out of scope (in other epics)

- 3-Strike Circuit Breaker counter / detection logic -> PDLC Execution Pipeline epic (the counter fires Autopsy but lives in execution states)
- Worktree management primitives -> PDLC Orchestrator Core epic (Autopsy consumes them)

## Success Criteria

- 3-Strike from any of {Red Gate, Green Gate, Review fix-loop, Integration Stage A fix-loop} correctly routes to Autopsy.
- Branch is frozen-not-burned on entry; preserved through the autopsy.
- Both RCA agents emit structured machine-readable reports (YAML/JSON).
- Interactive interrogation works end-to-end.
- Each Resolution Route correctly dispatches the UoW back into the FSM.
- Branch destruction occurs only at autopsy-bead resolution.
- Build passes. Typecheck passes. Tests pass.

## Far-horizon register

Cross-cutting architectural questions that touch this epic but are not blockers are tracked in `agents-config-wgclw.11` (PDLC architecture far-horizon register). Items currently relevant: seam S5 (RCA input-data schema — aggregate contract over Spec, strike history, gate-failure, worktree state); persona-spec-bead deferrals for Specification RCA and Architecture Health RCA agents; project-config key gap (Resolution Route per-route config).

title: PDLC Integration Pipeline (Stage 10)
description:
## Scope

Implements the PDLC's Integration Pipeline (stage 10) — the three internal sub-stages A/B/C that carry a UoW from 'Review passed locally' to 'Merged on origin.'

Implements stage 10 specified in `docs/specs/2026-05-19-pdlc-state-machine-design.md`.

## In scope

- Stage A (PR Mechanical Validation): PR creation against the merge target; CI re-runs the Green Gate composite plus the project's mechanical Reviewer Toolboxes; external automated-reviewer adapter (Copilot-class subjected to Mechanical-vs-Advisory discipline)
- Stage B (Human Approval Hold, conditional): HEP-style human-tagged work item emission when `approval_required = true`; aging-nag (deterministic threshold) on human silence; no auto-escalation around silence
- Stage C (Merge + Cleanup): merge automation per project-config (squash/ff/rebase); parent-walk close semantics; worktree + branch cleanup; UoW closure
- Routing for infrastructure failures (merge conflict, branch protection, push rejection) to human-escalation via HEP, NOT to Autopsy

## Out of scope (in other epics)

- Orchestrator core -> PDLC Orchestrator Core epic
- Execution-pipeline states -> PDLC Execution Pipeline epic
- Autopsy on 3-strike from CI -> PDLC Autopsy epic

## Success Criteria

- A successful UoW traverses A -> (B) -> C end-to-end on a test fixture.
- `approval_required = true` correctly inserts B as a HEP-tagged work item.
- Aging-nag fires at the project-configured threshold and self-silences on human action.
- Merge automation handles squash, ff, and rebase per project-config.
- Post-merge parent-walk closes Containers when their closure conditions are met (all children closed + all Container-Level ATs pass + no Scaffold ATs persist).
- External LLM-reviewer adapter handles at least Copilot at MVP level.
- Build passes. Typecheck passes. Tests pass.

## Far-horizon register

Cross-cutting architectural questions that touch this epic but are not blockers are tracked in `agents-config-wgclw.11` (PDLC architecture far-horizon register). Items currently relevant: seams S4 (Finding payload schema — consumed here from `wgclw.4`, Advisory becomes an Idea via Holding Place `create_idea`), S6 (HEP cross-milestone seam — Stage B's consumer relationship to `abn9.28`'s `bd-hep-escalate.sh` helper), S7 (Copilot adapter wire format); project-config key gap (merge strategy enumeration); architectural question Q1 (does the Copilot adapter subsume prgroom's PR-grooming, or live alongside?).

title: PDLC Execution Pipeline (Stages 7-9)
description:
## Scope

Implements the PDLC's Execution Pipeline states (7-9) and their personas, plus the 3-Strike Circuit Breaker, the Finding class system, and the Reviewer Toolbox framework.

Implements stages 7-9 specified in `docs/specs/2026-05-19-pdlc-state-machine-design.md`.

## In scope

- Test-Authoring (stage 7): orchestrator-scaffolded test skeletons; Test-Author Agent fixture/mock fleshing-out; signature-only stub discipline with AST verifier
- Red Gate composite check (compile/import + run + fail)
- Implementation (stage 8): Implementer Agent with test-read-only authority; Green Gate composite (tests + typecheck + build + lint + coverage)
- Review (stage 9): parallel Reviewer Agents framework; configurable Reviewer Toolbox (Code Quality, Security, Performance, API Contract, Documentation at MVP)
- Finding classes: Mechanical (blocking), Advisory (non-blocking; captured as Ideas at merge), Proposed Rule (non-blocking on PR; gates future after human approval)
- 3-Strike Circuit Breaker: per-state-instance counters; routes to Autopsy on strike 3; resets on gate-pass

## Out of scope (in other epics)

- Orchestrator core -> PDLC Orchestrator Core epic
- Integration (PR/Merge) -> PDLC Integration Pipeline epic
- Autopsy / RCA -> PDLC Autopsy epic

## Success Criteria

- A Sized UoW traverses stages 7-9 end-to-end against a test fixture.
- Red Gate composite checks all three components individually diagnosable.
- Green Gate composite checks all five components individually diagnosable.
- Review's Mechanical-finding fix-loop converges (zero findings in a complete round) or 3-strikes to Autopsy.
- Reviewer Toolbox has at least one functional implementation per MVP domain (duplication detector, complexity metric, microbench harness, schema diff).
- Build passes. Typecheck passes. Tests pass.

## Far-horizon register

Cross-cutting architectural questions that touch this epic but are not blockers are tracked in `agents-config-wgclw.11` (PDLC architecture far-horizon register). Items currently relevant: seams S3 (Reviewer Toolbox dispatch protocol — produced here, consumed by `wgclw.2`), S4 (Finding payload schema — produced here, consumed by `wgclw.5`); persona-spec-bead deferrals for Test-Author, Implementer, Reviewer Agent, and 5 Reviewer Toolbox sub-personas; project-config key gaps (Reviewer Toolbox per-domain enable list, Finding discrimination rules).

title: PDLC Design Phase (Stages 1-5)
description:
## Scope

Implements the PDLC's design-phase states (post-Promote, CANDIDATE_UOW through DECOMPOSE) and their personas, plus the foundational artefacts those states produce and consume: the Spec file format and the Atomic-AT Linter.

Implements the post-Promote stages specified in `docs/specs/2026-05-19-pdlc-state-machine-design.md`.

## In scope

- Design Interrogator persona + Spec authoring workflow (drives the CANDIDATE_UOW → AGENT_WORTHY transition by interrogating the human until the Spec is ready)
- Decomposition Architect persona (Integration-Aware optimisation; drives DECOMPOSE → EXECUTABLE_READY or CONTAINER_DECOMPOSED)
- Sizing Gate composite mechanical score implementation
- Decomposition Plan authoring including Assembly Graph + Scaffold/Cleanup AT pair identification
- Spec file format (schema + serialisation)
- Atomic-AT Linter (deterministic; flags conjunctions, multi-verb assertions, multi-clause assertions)

## Out of scope (in other epics)

- Orchestrator process / state-machine engine → PDLC Orchestrator Core epic
- Project-config schema → PDLC Orchestrator Core epic
- Test-Author / Implementer / Reviewer / RCA Agents → Execution Pipeline / Autopsy epics
- **Holding Place + Idea pipeline (Capture / Grooming / Buckets / Cool Idea Quarantine / Idea Curator persona) → PDLC Holding Place peer epic (`agents-config-wgclw.10`)** — carved out 2026-05-25 to honour `CONTEXT.md > Holding Place`'s peer-service framing; see ADR-0001

## Success Criteria

- A Candidate UoW (produced by the Holding Place's Promote operation) advances through assessment to Agent-Worthy, then traverses Decomposition to either Agent-Ready (Sized) or Decomposed Container (Oversized) end-to-end against fixture inputs
- Atomic-AT Linter detects conjunctions, multi-verb assertions, and multi-clause assertions on a fixture corpus
- Sizing Gate is deterministic; identical input → identical verdict
- Decomposition Plan exit criteria are mechanically verified
- Build passes. Typecheck passes. Tests pass.

## Far-horizon register

Cross-cutting architectural questions that touch this epic but are not blockers are tracked in `agents-config-wgclw.11` (PDLC architecture far-horizon register). Items currently relevant: seam S1 (Spec file format read-side), the persona-spec-bead deferral pattern (Design Interrogator, Decomposition Architect), project-config key gap (Decomposition Plan exit-criteria thresholds).

title: PDLC Orchestrator Core & Foundations
description:
## Scope

The PDLC's bedrock. This epic produces the deterministic state-machine engine, the work-tracker abstraction that decouples execution agents from the WMS, and the project-config schema that hosts every PDLC parameter. Nothing in the other PDLC epics functions without these foundations.

Implements the foundational requirements specified in `docs/specs/2026-05-19-pdlc-state-machine-design.md`.

## In scope

- Orchestrator process: state-machine engine (Python or Go), agent dispatch, gate execution, strike-counter management, transition logging.
- Work-tracker abstraction: generic work-item interface; bd adapter passes fixture tests; future-proof for additional adapters (jira, github).
- Project-config schema: central configuration for all PDLC parameters (Reviewer Agent list, Sizing Gate weights/thresholds, coverage thresholds, `approval_required` defaults, aging-nag thresholds, etc.). Loader and validator implemented.

## Out of scope (in other epics)

- Spec format and Atomic-AT Linter -> PDLC Design Phase epic
- Individual persona implementations -> Design Phase / Execution Pipeline / Autopsy epics
- Integration / Autopsy state implementations -> their own epics
- **Holding Place + Idea pipeline -> PDLC Holding Place peer epic (`agents-config-wgclw.10`)**; orchestrator consumes via the Promote contract (`agents-config-wgclw.10.1`)

## Success Criteria

- Orchestrator process model defined and the state-machine engine prototyped to traverse a fixture FSM.
- Work-tracker abstraction with bd adapter passes a fixture test corpus.
- Project-config schema documented; loader passes fixture tests.
- Build passes. Typecheck passes. Tests pass.

## Far-horizon register

Cross-cutting architectural questions that touch this epic but are not blockers are tracked in `agents-config-wgclw.11` (PDLC architecture far-horizon register). Items currently relevant: seams S1 (Spec format read-side), S2 (`worker-report-v1` ↔ PDLC dispatch — flagged by M0 itself), S3 (Reviewer Toolbox dispatch protocol), S8 (WorkTracker ↔ bd-shim CLI); project-config key gaps (`review_max_rounds`, merge strategy, Reviewer Toolbox per-domain enable list, Finding discrimination rules, Resolution Route per-route config, Decomposition Plan exit-criteria); architectural question Q2 (when does WorkTracker commit to consuming `wgclw.9`'s bd-shim CLI).

title: Implementation metadata tracking: spec drift detection via implementation-notes.html
description:
Enhancement to the implementation workflow: every implementation MUST maintain a running implementation-notes.html file that serves as a living record of how the work diverges from, interprets, or refines the spec. This creates an auditable trail of decision-making and enables automated drift detection.

## What Gets Captured

The implementation-notes.html file SHALL record:

1. **Design Decisions**: When the spec is ambiguous and the implementation makes a choice (e.g., 'spec says validate input, but didn't specify max length — chose 256 chars based on storage constraints')

2. **Deviations**: Intentional departures from the spec with justification (e.g., 'spec calls for real-time sync, implemented eventual consistency due to latency constraints discovered during prototyping')

3. **Tradeoffs**: Alternatives considered and why each was rejected in favor of the chosen approach (e.g., 'considered both Redis and in-memory cache; chose in-memory for operational simplicity given the 10-minute TTL requirement')

4. **Open Questions**: Anything the implementer wants the spec owner to confirm, revise, or clarify (e.g., 'spec says "handle edge cases gracefully" — is silent fallback to default acceptable, or should we log + escalate?')

## Integration Points

- Implementation prompt: Add explicit instruction to maintain this file throughout work
- Completion gate: Accept implementation-notes.html as evidence of spec interpretation
- Automated drift detection: Parse notes to identify scope creep, unresolved ambiguities, and acceptance-criteria gaps
- PR review: Copilot/human review includes notes-to-spec validation

## Success Criteria

- Implementation prompt includes explicit requirement to maintain implementation-notes.html
- File format is parseable (HTML structure: sections for decisions/deviations/tradeoffs/questions, each with timestamp + rationale)
- Automated feedback system exists to detect:
  - Unresolved open questions (blocking criteria)
  - Deviations that contradict acceptance criteria (blocker)
  - Tradeoffs that introduce new dependencies (flag for review)
  - Design decisions resolving spec ambiguities (informational, no block)
- Feedback integrates into completion-gate workflow
- Documentation captures how to maintain and interpret the file

title: Brainstorm New Workflow (architecture 2.0) — PDLC State Machine Design
description:
# Revised Context Seed Doc: PDLC State Machine Design

## System Context & Persona Directive

You are my Lead Software Architect. We are designing a rigid, deterministic Product Development Life Cycle (PDLC) State Machine for an AI Agent Orchestrator. We are abandoning prompt-based "vibecoding" and building a Code-First execution pipeline.

## The Anti-Targets (Failure Modes We Must Prevent)

- **The AI Debate Club**: We will not design any state where two probabilistic models must reach a consensus to advance the pipeline.
- **Garbage Amplified**: We will not allow large, entangled, or ambiguous specifications to enter the execution phase.
- **Waterlogged Rocks**: We will not allow agents to inherit dirty git branches or failed code states after a catastrophic failure. Retries must be clean.
- **Bullet-Point Review Hell**: We will not design a system that requires the human to manually read hundreds of lines of generated test code or agentic apologies to figure out what went wrong.

## Immutable Architectural Laws

1. **The Orchestrator is Code**: The state machine will be built in Python/Go. Agents do not decide what task to do next; the Orchestrator tells them.
2. **Mechanical Gates**: No state transition can be authorized by an LLM "vibe check." Transitions from Brainstorming → Spec or Spec → Implementation MUST be gated by deterministic proofs (e.g., human approval, `npm test` compiling and failing, `npm test` passing).
3. **WMS Decoupling**: Execution agents have absolutely zero awareness of our Work Management System (Beads, Jira, GitHub). The Orchestrator manages all state persistence.
4. **The 3-Strike Circuit Breaker**: Agents get a maximum of 3 attempts to pass a mechanical gate. On strike 3, the pipeline halts, the branch is trashed, and the state routes to an "Autopsy" bead for human escalation and RCA.
5. **No Judgmental Acceptance Tests (ATs)**: "Agent-Ready" means the Unit of Work is backed by thin-sliced, machine-verifiable ATs.
acceptance_criteria:
## Design Acceptance Criteria (Definition of Done for this Session)

We are finished designing this PDLC only when we have mapped the exact, non-negotiable states a Unit of Work passes through from "Raw Idea" to "Merged," and for every single state we have explicitly defined:

1. **The State Name** and its purpose.
2. **The Agent Persona** operating inside it (or if it is purely Human/Orchestrator).
3. **The Mechanical Gate** (the deterministic trigger, script, or explicit human approval) required to exit this state and advance.
4. **The Failure Routing** (exactly where the state reverts if the mechanical gate rejects it).
close_reason: Design complete. Spec at docs/specs/2026-05-19-pdlc-state-machine-design.md; supporting glossary at CONTEXT.md. All four DoD fields (Name+Purpose, Persona, Mechanical Gate, Failure Routing) defined for stages 1-11. Implementation tracked in child epics under M0 (agents-config-wgclw).

title: H.5 — docs cleanup (AGENTS.md + README + golden-master retirement)
description: Update AGENTS.md to reflect the new install command and the golden-master retirement. Update README. Move tests/golden_master/ to tests/golden_master/_retired/ (or delete) once H.4 lands.
acceptance_criteria:
- AGENTS.md install instructions reference uv-managed Python installer.
- AGENTS.md golden-master note updated from transitional to retired.
- README installation section updated.
- Golden-master tests no longer run in CI by default.
notes: Golden master retirement pulled forward into H.4 PR (#191). H.5 scope is now docs-only: AGENTS.md, README, and docs/architecture/installer/ references to install.sh as the behavioural spec and prune-list as in-tree.

title: H.3 — parity confirmation (golden-master suite + real-home smoke + sign-off)
description: Parity confirmation gate. Run the full golden-master suite plus a real-home smoke test (install.py vs install.sh, --dry-run --verbose against the actual repo home) and confirm zero unexpected diffs. No fixed calendar wait: the gate clears when end-to-end parity scenarios pass with no blocker issues observed and the maintainer signs off. See docs/architecture/installer/installer-design.md (Epic H).
acceptance_criteria:
- Full golden-master suite passes (zero post-normalisation diff across all scenarios).
- Real-home smoke test produces zero unexpected diffs (install.py --dry-run --verbose vs install.sh --dry-run --verbose).
- No blocker-severity parity issues open.
- Maintainer sign-off recorded in this bead's notes field.
notes:
## H.3 parity confirmation — evidence (run 2026-06-19, worktree h3-parity-confirmation @ origin/main 4b639c4)

### Deterministic gate — GREEN
- Golden-master parity suite: 15/15 PASS (make golden-master-installer, 21s). Covers bare/codex/gemini/opencode, settings union-merge, user-modified backup, autodetect, re-install idempotency, prune, prune-only, plugin routes/overlay/collision.
- Full package CI (make ci-installer): ruff check + ruff format(105) + mypy --strict(42 files) + unit 557 pass @ 99.79% cov + pip-audit clean + install.py --help. All green.

### VERDICT: gate does NOT clear — 1 blocker + doc/coverage gaps (37-agent adversarial coverage audit)
Suite-green is partly FALSE-green: the suite stays green only because no scenario seeds the failing input.

BLOCKER — invalid-settings-json skip-vs-overwrite divergence:
  A pre-existing settings.json that is NOT valid JSON produces OPPOSITE on-disk outcomes.
  - bash install.sh:1299-1304: err 'contains invalid JSON. Fix it manually or remove it.' + tool_skipped++ + return -> bad file LEFT UNTOUCHED.
  - Python core/sync.py:48-51: merge_settings_bytes raises ValueError (json.loads fails) -> caught -> returns template -> _install_file backs up then CLOBBERS with template.
  - Differ WOULD catch it (live copies differ), so green only because no scenario seeds invalid JSON.
  - Undocumented in design-doc Parity exceptions; sync.py:44 docstring ('bash warns and replaces') is FACTUALLY WRONG (bash skips, never replaces) => porting defect, not a deliberate divergence.
  - Resolution (Scott's call): (A) make Python match bash (skip+error+preserve), or (B) keep backup+overwrite, fix docstring, record as documented exception. Either way: add a golden-master scenario seeding invalid settings.json (goes RED first).

META-CAVEAT — the H.3-specified real-home '--dry-run --verbose, zero unexpected diffs' smoke is a WEAK oracle:
  dry-run writes nothing -> a home-vs-home tree-diff of two dry-run runs is vacuously empty; the two installers' dry-run stdout is NOT line-comparable (bash 'Would merge'+diff -u vs Python 'would create <dest>'). Per design-doc:398 it is a human 'spot-check the preview diffs', NOT a mechanical zero-diff oracle. The genuinely load-bearing real-state check is a NON-dry-run install into a throwaway COPY of the real home, tree-diffed.

NON-BLOCKER gaps (real; latent/cosmetic/deferrable — recommend doc pass + follow-up beads):
  1. plugin-overlay+prune: Python prune.py beads/formulas scan hardcodes staged=set() (vs bash staging baseline); latent — no */formulas/* glob exists today.
  2. differ empty-dir blindness: bash eagerly mkdir ~/.beads/formulas, Python lazily -> 2 passing plugin scenarios false-green on a cosmetic empty dir (_diff.py indexes is_file only).
  3. differ .json semantic compare applies to ALL .json, not just settings.json (doc-vs-impl scope mismatch; no false-green today).
  4. process-contract divergences invisible to differ: unknown --tools/--plugins bash exit 1 vs Python 2; non-interactive-no-yes bash skip+continue vs Python ConsentRequiredError. Intentional/safer; doc gap only.
  5. multi-tool combined --tools=claude,codex,gemini,opencode never exercised (structurally isolated; most-used real invocation).
  6. carrier-merge byte-parity unexercised (DEAD in prod: src/plugins empty).
  7. settings deep-merge depth>1 + scalar-collision branches unexercised (simplest keep-existing).
  8. JSONC/TOML update+collision branches unexercised (unreachable from shipped config).
  9. prune-list literal tool/ns/base glob form unexercised (matcher proven by wildcard scenarios).

Sign-off path: fix BLOCKER (decision + red scenario + reconcile) -> document non-blockers 2/3/4 as explicit Parity exceptions -> run real-home smoke as human spot-check understanding its dry-run limits. Then green suite + smoke is trustworthy.
## Real-state smoke result (HOME-redirect, real ~/.claude copied into two temp HOMEs, both installers run, tree-diffed)

BLOCKER FIX CONFIRMED: real settings.json (VALID, 25 top-level keys) reached parity — settings.json NOT in the diff, no spurious backup. bash rc=0, python rc=0. Against valid input the new invalid-JSON guard is dormant, so this smoke == main behavior except where the fix's own scenario applies. PR #177 (the blocker fix) is gate-green + Copilot-clean (0 comments).

SMOKE ALSO SURFACED a real PRE-EXISTING divergence the hermetic suite misses (filed agents-config-23k33):
  Namespace-dir sync — bash sync_directory replaces a CHANGED skills/agents/hooks item via 'rm -rf + cp -R' with NO backup (latent data loss of user-modified content); Python _install_dir/_install_file back up to <namespace>-backup/ first. Python is SAFER; bash has the bug. Golden-master is green only because no scenario seeds a content-drifted (or __pycache__-bearing) namespace DIR. NOT caused by the H.3 blocker fix (untouched paths; dormant on valid settings).
  Diff observed (Python-only backups): skills-backup/{improve-codebase-architecture,whats-next,writing-skills}.backup-TS/*, hooks/ruff-postedit{.py,_test.sh}.backup-TS. Live content matched on both sides (so the only delta is the Python-only safety backups).

H.3 sign-off basis (honest): the real-home smoke does NOT show 'zero diffs' — it shows Python-only safety backups that all trace to one intentional, better-than-bash divergence. Sign-off should either (a) accept + document that divergence as a Parity exception (per 23k33) and acknowledge the hooks gap (w1qls.8.7), or (b) defer sign-off until 23k33's documentation+coverage lands. Maintainer's call.
## SIGN-OFF (maintainer: Scott, accept+document basis)

Gate cleared. Blocker fix merged to main as squash commit 512c38d (PR #177): Python matches bash on invalid settings.json (skip+preserve), with the differ byte-identical short-circuit it exposed. Full golden-master suite 16/16 + full installer CI gate green; Copilot review clean (0 comments).

Real-home smoke ran (HOME-redirect, non-destructive): the blocker fix holds against the real valid 25-key settings.json (parity). The smoke also surfaced one PRE-EXISTING, intentional 'Python is safer' divergence — bash sync_directory replaces changed namespace items with no backup (data loss) while Python backs up to <namespace>-backup/ first. ACCEPTED as a deliberate latent-bug fix and DOCUMENTED in scripts/AGENTS.md so future parity comparisons anticipate it. Formal design-doc Parity exception + differ acceptance + a drifted-namespace-dir golden-master scenario tracked as follow-up (the namespace-dir-sync divergence bead, discovered-from this).

Deferred non-blockers (all filed discovered-from this): golden-master coverage hardening, differ/design-doc parity-exception documentation gaps, Python prune beads/formulas staged=set() divergence, dead single-file sync() guard. Hooks-namespace gap maps to the existing open H-series hooks bead.

title: H.4 — install.sh collapse + scripts/prune-list retirement
description: Replace install.sh body with: exec uv run --project packages/installer python -m installer with passed args. Delete or symlink scripts/prune-list (its contents already live in packages/installer/installer.toml from G.2).
acceptance_criteria:
- install.sh is now <= 10 lines of wrapper.
- scripts/prune-list either deleted or replaced with a deprecation pointer.
- bash scripts/install.sh and python3 scripts/install.py produce identical install state in a fresh smoke test.

title: H.1 — golden-master harness + first 3 scenarios (bare, settings, user-modified)
description: Build tests/golden_master/_runner.py that runs install.sh into HOME_A and install.py into HOME_B and recursively diffs with timestamp normalisation. The runner is comparison-type-aware: .json files are compared SEMANTICALLY (parse + deep-equal), every other file byte-wise — so install.py's json_union need not reproduce jq's output bytes. First scenarios: bare install on clean home; install with pre-existing settings.json; install with user-modified skill (backup path).
acceptance_criteria:
- Harness runs both installers and emits a normalised diff.
- Normalisation is comparison-type-aware: .json files are compared SEMANTICALLY (parse both sides, deep-equal), never byte-wise, so JSON formatting artifacts (key order, whitespace, jq quirks) never register as a diff; all other files are compared byte-wise after timestamp normalisation.
- Bare install scenario passes (zero post-normalisation diff).
- Settings-merge scenario passes via semantic JSON comparison (install.py json_union is a semantic deep-merge; it need not reproduce jq output bytes).
- User-modified-skill scenario passes including backup placement check.

title: H.2 — remaining golden-master scenarios (prune, prune-only, plugin, single-tool, Gemini)
description: Author the rest of the parity scenarios: --prune --yes with orphans; --prune-only; plugin overlay with test-plugin; --tools=codex --plugins= (single-tool); Gemini frontmatter transform end-to-end.
acceptance_criteria:

notes:
Audit 2026-06-19 (scenario coverage vs tests/golden_master/): COVERED — plugin overlay (test_plugin_clean_overlay, test_plugin_overlay_collision_merges); single-tool codex (test_bare_install_codex); Gemini transform (test_bare_install_gemini). MISSING — (1) --prune --yes with orphans; (2) --prune-only. Zero 'prune' refs in golden_master. Python prune IS implemented (cli.py --prune/--prune-only, core/prune_flow.py, core/prune.py) and run_parity already accepts args/seed, so both are writable now, not blocked. Remaining H.2 work = author these two prune parity scenarios.
2026-06-19: PR #173 created. Two prune parity scenarios (--prune --yes with orphans, --prune-only) added to test_parity_prune.py. Copilot round 1: 4 FIX comments (dir seed, no-install gate, backup entry assertions). All fixed in commit a7af1df. Copilot round 2: APPROVED, 0 new comments. All threads replied and resolved. Gate: 572 tests, 99.79% coverage.

title: Epic H — Parity gate + cutover
description: Golden-master harness + scenarios; parity confirmation; install.sh collapse; prune-list retirement; docs cleanup. See docs/architecture/installer/installer-design.md (Epic H).

title: G.5 — --prune / --prune-only CLI integration
description: Wire prune flow into the orchestrator. --prune runs the install then prune; --prune-only skips install and only scans+prunes. Mutually exclusive.
acceptance_criteria:

- --prune-only skips install; only prunes.

title: G.6 — --dump-stage <path> flag
description: Materialise the in-memory StagingPlan to <path> as a real directory tree, print the path, exit 0. No destination writes. Mutually exclusive with --prune / --prune-only.
acceptance_criteria:
- Dump produces a tree at <path>/<tool>/... matching the in-memory plan.
- No writes to home destinations occur.

title: G.3 — core/prune.py orphan scan
description: Walk scoped namespaces under each dest dir; identify entries not in the StagingPlan AND matching a prune-list glob. Skip legacy *.backup-* files. Same orphan semantics as install.sh lines 1505-1543 but driven by in-memory plan.
acceptance_criteria:

title: G.4 — interactive prune flow via ScriptedIO

title: G.1 — path-aware backup placement in core/sync.py
description: Items in scoped namespaces (commands, skills, agents, rules, formulas) back up to sibling namespace-backup/ directories with timestamp. Other items get in-place .backup-<ts> suffix.
acceptance_criteria:

title: Epic G — Operations (backup, prune, debug)
description: Path-aware backup; installer.toml loader; orphan scan; interactive prune; --prune / --prune-only / --dump-stage flags. See docs/specs/2026-05-17-python-installer-rewrite.md (Epic G).

title: F.3 — carrier-merge logic (in-memory metadata)
description:
F.3 — carrier-merge file-carry via dir_overrides (in-memory metadata).

F.2 delivered the carrier metadata and detection: the `shared_carrier` flag on
StagedItem (set in build_plan Phase 2 for shared skills/agents DIRs), the overlay
`_place` carrier-merge interception (incoming plugin `.agents/` DIR + disjoint
file sets => merge, else fatal; flag cleared after merge; plugin-plugin fatal).

F.3 completes the merge: actually carry the plugin's disjoint files to the
destination. A DIR StagedItem has one source_path, so two source dirs at one dest
need a new channel.

Scope:
- Add `StagingPlan.dir_overrides: dict[Path, dict[Path, bytes]]` (keyed by DIR
  dest_relpath, then inner relpath, to file bytes).
- In overlay `_place`, on a successful carrier-merge, read the plugin DIR's
  disjoint files and record them into `dir_overrides[dest]` (keep the carrier item
  with the cleared flag).
- Strengthen the carrier-merge test to assert the plugin's file is REPRESENTED in
  the plan (via dir_overrides), not dropped.
- Plan-level only: emitting both dirs at sync is the future plan-walking DIR-sync
  story's job (today's sync.py is the single-file B.2 slice). F.3 proves plan-layer
  correctness; deployment proof is deferred.
- F.5 reuses this same dir_overrides channel for patched bytes — one primitive,
  two consumers. See the epic (w1qls.6) design field, Plugin Seam Integration Brief.

Branches off F.2's branch (feat/w1qls.6.2-plugin-overlay); rebase onto main when
F.2 merges.
acceptance_criteria:
- Shared-carrier dir + plugin overlay with disjoint files: merges file lists, no error.
- Shared-carrier dir + plugin overlay with overlapping files: fatal collision.
- Non-carrier dir + plugin overlay: fatal collision regardless of file sets.
- No on-disk sentinel created.
