title: Epic F — Plugins
description: PluginAdapter protocol + registry; Phase 6 overlay; carrier-merge via in-memory metadata; beads plugin. See docs/specs/2026-05-17-python-installer-rewrite.md (Epic F).
design:
# Plugin Seam Integration Brief (F.2 / F.3 / F.5)

Cross-story contract for the three siblings that converge on the post-staging
seam. F.4 (plugins/beads.py) is isolated and not governed here.

## The seam

`orchestrator.stage_and_transform()`, per-tool loop:
`build_plan` (Phases 1-5) -> plugin overlay (Phase 6) -> `post_staging_transforms`
(Gemini frontmatter) -> sync.

## Phase ladder (per tool)

1-5. base staging (`staging.build_plan`) — done
6.   plugin overlay — alphabetical plugin order, through the Epic-E merge registry  [F.2 done]
6c.  carrier-merge — INSIDE 6's DIR-collision path, not a separate phase           [F.2 detect / F.3 carry]
6.5. DYNAMIC-INCLUDE flatten (`templates.py`) — separate later story
6.9. `apply_extensions()` — YAML-patch base markdown                                [F.5]
6.95 Gemini frontmatter transform (`tools/gemini.py`) — done
7.   sync — done (single-file slice; plan-walking DIR-sync is a later story)

Ordering rationale: F.5 runs after overlay+carrier (phzj.4 R9), before the Gemini
transform (extension frontmatter patches are canonical; the tool transform adapts
them), and before DYNAMIC-INCLUDE flatten (a patched rule must flow into any
ALL-RULES flatten).

## Carrier-merge — split across F.2 (detection) and F.3 (file-carry)

### F.2 — delivered: metadata + detection
- `StagedItem.shared_carrier: bool = False`. Set `True` in `build_plan` Phase 2
  for `skills/` + `agents/` `kind==DIR` items only. (Not on `Provenance`.)
- Overlay `_place`: an incoming plugin DIR colliding with a `shared_carrier` DIR
  carrier-merges iff the incoming DIR is from the plugin's `.agents/` (shared)
  tree AND the on-disk file sets are disjoint (dotfiles excluded). Else fatal.
- On a successful merge the carrier item is kept with the flag cleared via
  `dataclasses.replace` (mirrors bash `rm -f sentinel`): a second plugin collision
  on the same dir is then true plugin-plugin = fatal.

### F.3 — to build: the actual file-carry via `dir_overrides`
F.2 validates and clears the flag but does NOT yet represent the plugin's added
files for sync — a DIR `StagedItem` has one `source_path`; two source dirs at one
dest cannot be expressed without a new channel. That channel is `dir_overrides`.

- Add `StagingPlan.dir_overrides: dict[Path, dict[Path, bytes]]` — keyed by the
  DIR item's `dest_relpath`, then inner relpath, to file bytes.
- On carrier-merge, the overlay reads the plugin DIR's disjoint files and records
  them into `dir_overrides[dest]` (keeping the carrier item + cleared flag).
- Strengthen the F.2 carrier test: assert the plugin's file is REPRESENTED in the
  plan (via `dir_overrides`), not silently dropped.
- Plan-level only: actual emission of both dirs is the future plan-walking
  DIR-sync story's job (today's `sync.py` is the single-file B.2 slice). F.3 proves
  correctness at the plan layer; deployment proof is deferred, not skipped.

## F.5 inside-DIR patching — reuses F.3's `dir_overrides`

Skills/agents stage as a single opaque `DIR` `StagedItem` (`content=None`; bytes
read from `source_path` at sync). `apply_extensions` cannot mutate that, so it
writes patched bytes into the SAME `dir_overrides` channel F.3 introduces:

- `apply_extensions` reads the source file bytes, applies the YAML patch (full
  phzj.4 semantics), writes the result into `dir_overrides`. It does NOT mutate
  `StagedItem.content` (stays `None` for dirs).
- One primitive, two consumers (carrier extra-files + patched bytes). Per-file
  expansion of skill/agent dirs (the cleaner end-state) is deferred debt,
  revisited post-parity.

## Execution order

F.2 done (PR #143). F.3 branches off F.2's branch (stacked; rebase onto main when
F.2 merges) and introduces `dir_overrides` + the carrier file-carry. F.4 isolated
(PR #141). F.5 after F.3's `dir_overrides` primitive lands.

title: E.3 — strategies/fatal.py
description: Fatal-collision strategy for (FileKind.NAMESPACED_MD, namespace in {commands, skills, agents}) and FileKind.DIR. Raises CollisionError naming both source paths.
acceptance_criteria:
- Collision raises CollisionError.
- Error message names both source paths.
- Caller surfaces the error to the user with non-zero exit.

title: E.4 — strategies/json_union.py
description: Deep union-merge for FileKind.SETTINGS_JSON. Dict+dict recurses; arrays union+sort+dedupe; scalar conflicts keep existing; type mismatch keeps existing. Matches the jq program at install.sh lines 431-454.
acceptance_criteria:
- Nested object precedence: existing wins on scalar conflict.
- Arrays in conflicting keys are unioned, deduped, and sorted.
- Keys present only in incoming are added.
- Type mismatch (dict vs scalar) keeps existing.

title: E.5 — strategies/last_wins_warn.py + last_wins_silent.py
description: FileKind.JSONC and TOML: last-wins with warning. FileKind.OTHER: last-wins silently.
acceptance_criteria:
- last_wins_warn emits a warning identifying both source paths.
- last_wins_silent emits no warning.
- Both return the incoming content.

title: E.1 — core/merge/base.py + core/merge/registry.py
description: MergeStrategy protocol (merge(existing, incoming) -> StagedItem). Registry maps (FileKind, namespace) -> strategy instance: NAMESPACED_MD items dispatch on their parent-dir namespace (rules -> append, commands/skills/agents -> fatal); non-namespaced kinds (SETTINGS_JSON/JSONC/TOML/OTHER/DIR) ignore the namespace component. Replaces install.sh's case-statement dispatch (classify_file at install.sh:486-505; collision matrix at 415-478). See docs/architecture/installer/installer-design.md (Epic E + Data model highlights).
acceptance_criteria:
- MergeStrategy protocol defined: merge(existing, incoming) -> StagedItem.
- Registry dispatches on (FileKind, namespace): NAMESPACED_MD keys on parent-dir namespace (rules vs commands/skills/agents dispatch differently); non-namespaced kinds (SETTINGS_JSON/JSONC/TOML/OTHER/DIR) ignore the namespace component.
- Unknown (FileKind, namespace) key raises with a clear message.

title: E.2 — strategies/append_rules.py
description: Append-merge strategy for (FileKind.NAMESPACED_MD, namespace="rules"). Concatenates incoming after existing, joined with the standard separator (\n---\n).
acceptance_criteria:
- Two rules with the same name merge by append with the separator between.
- Order is existing-then-incoming.
- Separator is exact; no leading or trailing blank-line artefacts.

title: Epic E — Collision matrix

title: C.2 — DYNAMIC-INCLUDE ALL-RULES

- Empty rules collection produces a warning and a blank expansion.

title: DEFERRED: DYNAMIC-INCLUDE named-RULES directive (no consumer)
description:
Deferred and orphaned from the installer-rewrite roadmap; not a prerequisite of any active story.

Scope if ever built: recognise the marker `<!-- DYNAMIC-INCLUDE-RULES: name1, name2 -->` and expand it to the named rule subset, in argument order, joined with the newline-three-dashes-newline separator; warn on any missing named rule. Behavioural reference: the named-RULES branch of `flatten_agents_md` in the bash installer.

Why deferred: no template consumes the marker, so the data model omits a NamedRulesInclude variant and the golden-master parity suite never exercises this path — parity holds with or without it. Building it now adds an unused variant and dead resolution code.

Reopen condition: a concrete template requires a named rule subset (not mere symmetry with the bash installer). On reopen, re-parent under the owning epic and add the consuming template in the same change.
acceptance_criteria:
- named-RULES directive expands to the listed rules in argument order.
- Unknown rule name produces a warning, not a hard fail.
- Empty list expands to blank.
close_reason: deferred — no consumer; reopen on concrete named-RULES requirement

title: Epic C — DYNAMIC-INCLUDE completion + shared content

comments.author: Scott Hamilton
comments.text: Installer filter rule discovered 2026-05-17 during OSS-namespace cleanup (cx6 work): in-repo AGENTS.md files exist at multiple folder depths inside src/user/.agents/skills/ (e.g. the new src/user/.agents/skills/AGENTS.md namespace-level file, plus any per-skill-folder AGENTS.md authored by contributors as project documentation). These files are valuable as project development context for agents working in THIS codebase, BUT they have no runtime meaning when staged to user space — Claude/Codex/Gemini/OpenCode hosts only inject the tree-root ~/.<tool>/AGENTS.md as system context, never per-skill or per-namespace AGENTS.md under skills/. The Python installer SHOULD filter in-repo AGENTS.md (and CLAUDE.md / GEMINI.md / etc.) files at any depth within shared-content namespace dirs (skills/, agents/, commands/, etc.) so they do not land in user-space staging output. Note: this filter does NOT apply to AGENTS.md.template files at the tool-root level — those ARE the user-space tree-root files. Reference: bd memory key 'per-skill-agents-md-is-dead-file' for the three-layer distinction (in-repo docs vs staged-to-user-space dead files vs in-SKILL.md HTML-comment provenance headers). Bash install.sh's current behavior on this is unverified — may already filter, may silently stage. Worth a verification spike + filter rule encoded explicitly in the staging adapter.

title: C.1 — Phase 1-5 equivalent: shared + tool-specific subdir staging
description: Stage shared content from src/user/.agents/ (agents, skills, rules) AND tool-specific content from src/user/.<tool>/ subdirs (commands, skills, agents, rules) into the active tool StagingPlan. Bash equivalents: Phases 1-5. Same staging mechanism (stage_content_from_dir-equivalent) applied to both source roots — scope widened during w1qls.2.1 brainstorm because Phase 4 was not enumerated as its own story and the mechanism is identical. Collisions deferred to Epic E; this story uses no-collision fixtures.
acceptance_criteria:
- Shared agents/, skills/, rules/ items from src/user/.agents/ arrive in the claude plan with correct namespace assignment.
- Tool-specific commands/, skills/, agents/, rules/ items from src/user/.claude/ arrive in the claude plan with correct namespace assignment.
- Tool-root *.md.template (Phase 1/3) and tool settings *.json/.jsonc/.toml.template (Phase 5) arrive at the plan root with .template stripped and correct FileKind.
- Items go to the correct dest namespace / dest_relpath.
- Tests use no-collision fixtures; collision behaviour is out of scope (a duplicate dest_relpath raises; merge dispatch lands in Epic E).
- Remove # pragma: no cover from ClaudeAdapter.scoped_namespaces and ClaudeAdapter.should_install_namespace; behavioural tests cover namespace iteration and the (always-True) gate on the claude adapter (per w1qls.2.1 forward pragma-removal tracking).
- Dead-file filter: AGENTS.md / CLAUDE.md / GEMINI.md appearing as TOP-LEVEL entries within a shared-content namespace dir (e.g. skills/AGENTS.md) are filtered out of the plan. Tool-root *.md.template files are NOT affected (staged via the template glob, not the namespace walk). Nested marker files inside staged skill/agent dirs are a sync-time concern; none exist today; out of scope here. (Verified: bash does not currently filter; improvement-over-parity.)
- Build passes. Typecheck passes. make ci-installer green (>=90% branch).
notes:
Scope: absorbed the in-repo AGENTS.md/CLAUDE.md/GEMINI.md dead-file filter into the ACs (previously captured only as a comment on epic w1qls.3). It lands naturally at the namespace-dir staging walk this story builds.
Refined filter AC after planning recon: only src/user/.agents/skills/AGENTS.md is reachable by namespace staging today (a top-level namespace entry). Root-level .agents/.claude marker files are never staged (template-glob only). No nested per-skill marker files exist -> layer-2 (sync-time recursive-copy exclusion) is YAGNI, noted not filed.

title: B.3 — .template suffix strip in staging
description: Files named *.template in source get the suffix stripped on the way into the StagingPlan.
acceptance_criteria:
- AGENTS.md.template arrives at the dest as AGENTS.md.
- Files without the suffix pass through untouched.
- Tests cover both cases.

title: B.4 — DYNAMIC-INCLUDE file form
description: Recognise the file-include marker in templates and substitute the referenced file content. Missing file emits a warning and leaves the marker line empty (matching bash behaviour).
acceptance_criteria:
- A template with one DYNAMIC-INCLUDE file marker resolves to the inlined content.
- Missing-file directive produces a warning, not a hard fail.
- Non-directive lines pass through unchanged.
- Trailing newlines preserved iff template had one.

title: B.1 — config + tools/base + tools/claude + tools/registry + CLI --tools=
description: Define the ToolAdapter Protocol in tools/base.py with six methods (name, source_dir, dest_dir, is_detected, scoped_namespaces, should_install_namespace, post_staging_transforms). Implement ClaudeAdapter probing ~/.claude/settings.json as the detection marker (deliberate divergence from install.sh 'always include claude' rule). Build tools/registry.py keyed by Tool enum with known_tools / get_adapter / parse_tool_name / UnknownToolError; parse_tool_name filters on registry membership (registry-is-truth). Define minimal frozen Config dataclass in config.py with home/tools fields only (other fields deferred to consuming stories per YAGNI). Implement resolve_tools honouring --tools= CSV (strip whitespace, dedupe first-occurrence-wins, registry validation) or auto-detect via adapter probes. Update cli.py to parse --tools=, build Config, exit 2 with structured stderr on invalid input. ClaudeAdapter methods without B.1 consumers carry # pragma: no cover with consumer-story references; downstream stories (w1qls.2.2, w1qls.3.1, w1qls.4.4) carry pragma-removal notes in their AC. Design doc: docs/specs/2026-05-23-w1qls.2.1-config-claude-adapter-design.md.
acceptance_criteria:
- ToolAdapter Protocol defined in tools/base.py with all six methods (name, source_dir, dest_dir, is_detected, scoped_namespaces, should_install_namespace, post_staging_transforms).
- ClaudeAdapter.is_detected returns True only when ~/.claude/settings.json exists as a file (deliberate divergence from install.sh; Python becomes uniform across adapters; documented for H.5 cleanup).
- tools/registry.py exposes a Tool-keyed registry plus known_tools(), get_adapter(), parse_tool_name(), and UnknownToolError; parse_tool_name filters on registry membership (registry-is-truth: --tools=opencode fails in B.1 with structured error).
- CLI parses --tools=claude and returns exit 0.
- CLI rejects --tools=opencode, --tools= (empty), and --tools=foo with exit code 2 and structured stderr (installer: Unknown tool ... / installer: --tools= requires at least one tool).
- CLI strips whitespace around CSV tokens and dedupes first-occurrence-wins.
- Config dataclass is frozen, slotted, carries only home and tools fields (repo_root and other fields deferred to consuming stories per YAGNI).
- ClaudeAdapter.source_dir, dest_dir, scoped_namespaces, should_install_namespace, post_staging_transforms ship with # pragma: no cover and a comment naming the consumer story (w1qls.2.2 / w1qls.3.1 / w1qls.4.4).
- Build passes. Typecheck passes (mypy --strict). Tests pass. Coverage >=90% on touched modules.
notes:
TEST PLAN (BDD/Gherkin) — load-bearing artifact, pasted before red phase per the rewrite spec's TDD discipline.

Design doc: docs/specs/2026-05-23-w1qls.2.1-config-claude-adapter-design.md

=== tests/unit/test_tools_registry.py — registry as truth ===

Feature: Registry is the source of truth for valid tools

Scenario: get_adapter on an unregistered Tool enum value raises KeyError
  Given the registry contains only Tool.CLAUDE
  When the caller invokes get_adapter(Tool.OPENCODE)
  Then a KeyError is raised
  # Pins: callers must validate via parse_tool_name first

Scenario: parse_tool_name accepts a registered name
  Given the registry contains Tool.CLAUDE
  When parse_tool_name("claude") is called
  Then it returns Tool.CLAUDE

Scenario: parse_tool_name rejects a Tool enum value whose adapter is not registered
  Given the registry contains only Tool.CLAUDE
  When parse_tool_name("opencode") is called
  Then UnknownToolError is raised
  And the error.name is "opencode"
  And the error.valid is ("claude",)
  # Pins: registry-is-truth — Tool enum existence alone is insufficient

Scenario: parse_tool_name rejects a string that is not a Tool enum value
  When parse_tool_name("foo") is called
  Then UnknownToolError is raised
  And the error.name is "foo"

Scenario: UnknownToolError exposes structured attributes
  When UnknownToolError("opencode", ("claude",)) is constructed
  Then the instance has .name == "opencode"
  And the instance has .valid == ("claude",)

=== tests/unit/test_config.py — resolve_tools end-to-end behavioural ===

Feature: resolve_tools translates CLI input into the resolved Tool tuple

Scenario: auto-detect includes claude when ~/.claude/settings.json exists
  Given a home directory with a file at .claude/settings.json
  When resolve_tools(home=that_home, override_csv=None) is called
  Then the result is (Tool.CLAUDE,)

Scenario: auto-detect excludes claude when ~/.claude/settings.json is absent
  Given an empty home directory
  When resolve_tools(home=that_home, override_csv=None) is called
  Then the result is ()
  # Pins: deliberate divergence from install.sh

Scenario: explicit --tools=claude is accepted
  When resolve_tools(home=any, override_csv="claude") is called
  Then the result is (Tool.CLAUDE,)

Scenario: explicit --tools=claude overrides an empty auto-detect
  Given an empty home directory
  When resolve_tools(home=that_home, override_csv="claude") is called
  Then the result is (Tool.CLAUDE,)
  # Pins: override is "I know what I am doing"

Scenario: empty --tools= value is rejected
  When resolve_tools(home=any, override_csv="") is called
  Then ValueError is raised

Scenario: whitespace around CSV tokens is stripped
  When resolve_tools(home=any, override_csv=" claude ") is called
  Then the result is (Tool.CLAUDE,)

Scenario: duplicate CSV tokens are deduped, first occurrence wins
  When resolve_tools(home=any, override_csv="claude,claude") is called
  Then the result is (Tool.CLAUDE,)

Scenario: an unregistered Tool enum value in CSV is rejected
  When resolve_tools(home=any, override_csv="opencode") is called
  Then UnknownToolError is raised

Scenario: a garbage CSV token is rejected
  When resolve_tools(home=any, override_csv="foo") is called
  Then UnknownToolError is raised

=== tests/unit/test_cli_smoke.py — argv to exit code (extends existing) ===

Feature: CLI translates argv to an exit code and structured stderr

Scenario: --help exits zero (existing, unchanged)
Scenario: no args returns zero against a hermetic home with settings.json (existing, AMENDED for home= injection)
Scenario: --tools=claude returns zero
Scenario: --tools=opencode returns 2 and writes Unknown tool to stderr
Scenario: --tools= (empty) returns 2 and writes usage error to stderr
Scenario: --tools=foo returns 2
Scenario: no args against an empty home returns zero with no tools (empty tools tuple is valid in B.1)

=== Implementation notes ===

- Pytest function names mirror the scenario, e.g. test_resolve_tools_autodetect_excludes_claude_when_settings_json_absent.
- Each pytest function carries the matching Given/When/Then as a docstring.
- The writing-unit-tests skill (and its Tautology Filter section) MUST be invoked before any test code lands.

title: B.2 — minimal core/sync.py (single-file copy, hash-skip, dry-run)

title: M0 — Discipline-layer rearchitecture: scripts own determinism, skills own judgment
description:
## Why M0 exists

The current discipline layer (skills, formulas, plugins) has accumulated significant prose that encodes *deterministic* logic — molecule existence probes, HEP escalation procedures, jq-quirk workarounds, container-detection branches, slug generation, label state machines, lifecycle walks. This makes SKILL.md files long, expensive to load, hard to test, and inconsistent across skills. When bd's interface shifts (jq paths, dual-shape contracts), the prose drifts silently.

M0 reverses the polarity: **scripts own deterministic decisions; skill prose owns judgment, conversation, behavioral guardrails, and user-facing UX.** The seam between them is a versioned directive contract — the script emits a deterministic directive, the skill interprets and acts. Every directive is fixture-tested.

## North-star qualities (target architecture)

1. Every deterministic decision currently in skill prose is a pure-function Python module with a fixture-tested contract
2. Every irreducibly-interactive moment (user conversation, judgment under fuzzy criteria, behavioral boundary) is named and lives in skill prose
3. The skill→script seam is a versioned directive schema with round-trip tests
4. SKILL.md files shrink to: frontmatter, directive schema reference, judgment criteria, interactive loops, recovery prose, surface messages, red flags
5. bd CLI quirks (jq paths, dual-shape contracts, label state machines) are encapsulated in Python helpers, not re-explained in every skill
6. The HEP escalation procedure lives in ONE Python entry point, not duplicated across multiple skills
7. Each migrated subsystem is TDD-able via a fixture corpus (bead states in → directives out)

## Scope (initial candidate set; subject to triage during M0 phase 2)

- start-bead skill (the exemplar)
- HEP escalation primitives (currently duplicated across start-bead, others)
- Lifecycle walks (claim/close — partially scripted today)
- Molecule existence/state probes (currently inlined in multiple skills)
- bd CLI quirks (jq paths, dual-shape contracts) — extract to a Python bd_helpers module
- implement-bead orchestrator (candidate)
- merge-and-cleanup formula touchpoints (candidate)

## Out of scope

- Skills that are PURE judgment/conversation (brainstorming, simplify, code-review)
- Persona/voice files
- Tool installation/distribution mechanics (those stay in install.sh)
- **run-queue skill and its replacement** (`agents-config-7bk.11`, `agents-config-acmh.9`) — the run-queue skill is on a separate replacement trajectory whose upstream spec work has not landed. M0 does NOT absorb these; they will be pulled in or rendered obsolete on their own schedule, post-M0 if applicable.

## Success criteria (M0 closes when)

- Thesis document committed and stable (in this bead's description or a referenced spec)
- Bead triage complete: every in-scope open bead is classified as (a) survives-as-is, (b) re-specced under M0, (c) historical reference, or (d) obsolete
- Each migrated subsystem has: Python module, directive schema, fixture-tested contract, shrunken SKILL.md
- start-bead migrated end-to-end as the proving exemplar
- HEP and lifecycle walks consolidated to single Python entry points
- Documentation updated: AGENTS.md project section reflects the new architecture; affected rules updated

## Sequencing

M0 blocks M1. Several of M1's currently-in-progress beads will likely be re-scoped, absorbed, or marked historical during the M0 phase 2 triage. M2/M3/M4 sequencing is unchanged.

## Related work — informational reference only (NOT pulled into M0)

These beads overlap M0's thesis but are NOT being reparented. They are listed here so that phase-2 triage has a working inventory and so contributors understand the surrounding terrain. Each will be classified during triage (survives-as-is, re-specced under M0, historical reference, or obsolete) at the point M0 reaches its respective surface.

### Sibling precedent (same architectural move, different domain)

- `agents-config-fca6` — *prgroom — PR-grooming CLI (replaces `wait-for-pr-comments` + `reply-and-resolve-pr-threads`)*. Applies the same "kill the skill, ship a CLI" thesis to PR grooming. M0 cites prgroom as an existing reference implementation; its design decisions inform M0's directive schema and TDD discipline. Children include `fca6.2`–`fca6.6`, `fca6.9`, `fca6.10`. Implementation umbrella: `agents-config-abn9.8` (with starter `abn9.8.1`).

### HEP / lifecycle helper extractions (open; pure thesis applied)

Phase-2 triage will likely absorb or re-spec these into M0's Python-first architecture.

- `agents-config-abn9.28` — Extract `bd-hep-escalate.sh` helper consolidating HEP escalation pattern
- `agents-config-abn9.29` — Extract `bd-detect-y-container.sh` helper for Y_container title-match discriminator
- `agents-config-abn9.30` — Extract `bd-classify-bead.sh` helper for standalone bead classification
- `agents-config-abn9.31` — Build `bd-static-check.sh` CI guard for HEP, claim-walk, and silent-failure invariants
- `agents-config-fl80` — Extract repeated formula shell blocks into helper scripts
- `agents-config-2gzy` — Refactor skill helper scripts to use named parameters and return help on misuse

### In-flight HEP / worker-fleet work (active; M0's thesis informs direction)

Pulling would disrupt active work; M0's thesis nonetheless informs these beads' shape.

- `agents-config-7bk.13` — resolve-human-bead skill + HEP rollout (in_progress, vision-85-5-10)
- `agents-config-7bk.19` — Redesign worker agent layer: split bead-implementor, define `worker-report-v1`, strip orchestrator concerns (in_progress). Notably defines a `worker-report-v1` contract whose relationship to M0's directive schema needs clarification in M0 phase 1.
- `agents-config-7bk.20` — Human-flag lifecycle: HEP escalation, safe resume, audit-trail closure (in_progress, vision-85-5-10)
- `agents-config-y9mm` + `agents-config-y9mm.6` — Active-child filter via `hep-pause` label (Round-4 hardening). Close to landing; let it ship, then have M0 absorb the resulting primitives.

### Cross-cutting downstream consumers (depend on M0 primitives)

- `agents-config-owqa` — Add brainstorm-readiness gate (verify-brainstorm skill). Lives under M2 (`qn0g`) by design, but its deterministic-check primitives are M0-shaped. M0 success criteria implicitly delivers what `owqa` consumes.

## Why this matters

The 85/5/10 ratio requires agents to be reliable at deterministic tasks AND good at the judgment seams. The current architecture asks the LLM to be reliable at deterministic tasks via prose — which is expensive (tokens), brittle (drift), and hard to verify (no fixture tests). M0 corrects the architectural mismatch before M1 ships further accelerators that would inherit it.

title: Audit and migrate legacy bead state (orphan legacy-Y, mis-stamped human, residual Y_container labels)
description:
One-time data hygiene pass to migrate the current bead store from pre-PR-#76 architectural patterns to the canonical post-helpers shape. Required before re-brainstorming agents-config-pqvc.

## Why this exists

User accepted that backwards-compat is a data hygiene issue, not a code-around-edge-cases issue. PR #76 was abandoned in favor of a helper-first rebuild. Before the new shape ships, we need to audit and remediate state created under the broken paths.

Three known hazards from the cross-cut audit:

1. Orphan legacy-Y beads (M3): when brainstorm-bead Step 1 detects a legacy single-Y shape (lines 341-354, 410-414), it strips produced-bead-* from source X and creates a fresh Y_container/Y_impl pair — but leaves the legacy Y intact with brainstormed + implementation-ready + formula-* + session-marker labels. These can be picked up by 'bd ready --label implementation-ready' and started in parallel with the new Y_impl. Real data-corruption-shaped risk.

2. Bare-stamped human beads (C2, C3): brainstorm-bead, merge-and-cleanup, implement-feature, fix-bug all bare-stamp 'human' label on source beads in violation of the single-bead human invariant. resolve-human-bead Probe 3 (Scenario C: HEP escalation) misroutes these into Probe 5 (orphan human task) because no incoming dep edge from a non-closed source exists. User-facing impact: bare-stamped sources today get prompted as 'Treat as a standalone task. Prompt for intent.' instead of getting the HEP escalation flow.

3. Mis-stamped produced-bead-* labels on epic-typed Y_containers (C1 backwards-compat): an earlier iteration may have double-stamped produced-bead-* defensively on the Y_container itself, not just on source X. If found, the residual would coincidentally satisfy the now-deprecated PARENT_HAS_PRODUCED check.

## Audit queries to run

### A1 — Orphan legacy-Y inventory
bd list --label brainstormed --status open --label implementation-ready --json \
  | jq -r '.[] | .id + ' + ' + .title' > /tmp/audit-a1-candidates.txt
# For each candidate, check if the source X (via produced-from-* label on candidate) carries a produced-bead-<different-id> that points elsewhere
# Any positive match is an orphan legacy-Y

### A2 — Bare-stamped human source inventory
bd list --label human --status open,in_progress --json \
  | jq -r '.[] | .id + ' + ' + .title' > /tmp/audit-a2-candidates.txt
# For each human-labeled bead, check if it has an incoming bd dep edge from a non-closed bead
# Beads with NO incoming dep edge AND that are the SOURCE of work (not an escalation bead) are bare-stamped sources
# Distinguish from legitimate g17x merge-or-handoff sites by presence of merge-ready label

### A3 — Mis-stamped produced-bead-* on epic Y_containers
bd list --label produced-bead-* --type epic --json \
  | jq -r '.[] | .id + ' + ' + .title' > /tmp/audit-a3-candidates.txt
# Any epic-typed bead with a produced-bead-* label is a Y_container that should NOT carry it
# Verify by checking that the label value points to a Y_impl child of this epic

## Remediation actions

A1 orphan legacy-Y: stamp 'superseded-by-<new-Y-impl>' label, remove implementation-ready, close with reason 'Superseded by <new>; legacy single-Y shape replaced under 2-level rebuild'. If no new Y_impl exists yet (e.g., partial migration), leave open but with implementation-ready stripped + 'paused-pending-rebuild' label so it stays out of ready queues.

A2 bare-stamped human source: for each, manually inspect to determine intent (was this a bd label remove away from a real escalation? was the source supposed to be paused?). Create a proper HEP escalation bead via the new bd-hep-escalate.sh helper, link via bd dep, then strip 'human' from the source bead. Annotate the new escalation with 'migrated-from-bare-stamp-source=<source-id>'.

A3 mis-stamped produced-bead-* on Y_container: simply bd label remove the offending label from the Y_container. Verify post-fix that source X still carries its own produced-bead-<Y_container-id> label.

## Acceptance Criteria

- Run A1, A2, A3 queries; persist full inventory output as bd comments on this bead
- For each finding, remediate per the action above; record in comments
- Total counts reported: orphan-legacy-Y migrated, bare-stamped human resolved, mis-stamped produced-bead-* stripped
- Final verification queries return empty (or only explicitly justified exceptions)
- Close this bead with full audit report
- Blocks: bd-hep-escalate.sh helper bead (needed for A2 remediation); detect-y-container.sh helper (helpful for A1 verification)
- Sequence: file this bead now (open, dep-blocked on the helper beads); run audit queries early to KNOW the scope before the helpers ship

title: Build bd-static-check.sh CI guard for HEP, claim-walk, and silent-failure invariants
description:
Build a static analysis tool that scans formulas, skills, and shell scripts for known anti-patterns whose repeated reinvention drove the cross-cut audit findings on PR #76.

## Why this exists

Round-by-round Copilot review of PR #76 + cross-cut audit revealed that the same architectural rules are violated in many places because there is no automated check. Specifically:

- Single-bead human invariant (beads.md HEP block): violated in 8+ inline sites by bare bd label add <source> human and bd label add <step-bead> human, outside the documented merge-or-handoff g17x exception
- Container-detection-required-before-bd-dep-add: violated in 7+ sites where bd dep add <source> <esc> runs without first detecting container shape (epic-wall hard-error risk for epic sources)
- bd update <source> --status open revert mandatory after HEP escalation: missing in multiple sites
- bd update X --status in_progress must go through bd-claim-walk.sh: violated in 4 inline ancestor-walk sites (start-bead Route B, docs-only preflight, bd-finalize State 0/1)
- bd close ... || true / bd close ... 2>/dev/null conflates 'already closed' (idempotent) with 'close failed' (real error)

The architectural answer is: make these invariants machine-enforceable via a CI gate.

## What this tool does

Single tool at src/plugins/beads/.beads/scripts/bd-static-check.sh that scans:
- src/plugins/beads/.beads/formulas/*.toml
- src/user/.agents/skills/**/SKILL.md
- src/plugins/beads/.agents/skills/**/SKILL.md
- src/plugins/beads/.beads/scripts/*.sh

For each known anti-pattern, emit a finding with file:line and rule name. Exit non-zero on any finding unless suppressed by an explicit allowlist comment.

Rules implemented:
- R1 SINGLE_HUMAN_INVARIANT: bare 'bd label add ...{{bead-id}}... human' or similar variable forms outside an allowlisted g17x site
- R2 CONTAINER_DETECTION_BEFORE_DEP: 'bd dep add <source> <esc>' lacking a preceding SRC_TYPE / IS_CONTAINER probe in the same code block
- R3 STATUS_OPEN_REVERT: a 'bd label add <id> human' on a NEWLY-CREATED esc bead must be followed by 'bd update <source> --status open' in the same code block
- R4 CLAIM_WALK_HELPER: 'bd update <id> --status in_progress' must be wrapped in a call to bd-claim-walk.sh OR explicitly justified via inline comment
- R5 CLOSE_SUPPRESSION: 'bd close ... || true' or 'bd close ... 2>/dev/null' is banned unless preceded by an explicit status probe

Allowlist mechanism:
- Inline comment '# bd-static-check: allow R<n> reason=<short>' suppresses one finding on the next non-comment line
- Allowlist file at src/plugins/beads/.beads/scripts/bd-static-check.allow.toml for known long-term exemptions (g17x merge-or-handoff sites)

## Acceptance Criteria

- Tool ships at src/plugins/beads/.beads/scripts/bd-static-check.sh
- Five rules implemented and tested with fixture files (positive + negative cases for each)
- Exit code: 0 if no findings, 1 if any unsuppressed finding, 2 if tool itself errors
- Output format: 'FINDING <rule-name> <file>:<line> <terse-message>'
- Allowlist mechanism supports both inline and file-based exemptions
- README documents each rule with a small example + the exemption procedure
- CI integration deferred to a follow-up bead (this bead ships the tool; wiring is separate)
- Tool runs in < 5s on the full repo
- Reuses bd-hep-escalate.sh, bd-detect-y-container.sh, bd-classify-bead.sh as references for what the 'correct' patterns look like (does not require those helpers to be ADOPTED yet — just identifies sites that need retrofit)

title: Extract bd-classify-bead.sh helper for standalone bead classification
description:
Extract a read-only shell helper that classifies any bead by structural shape, closing gaps in start-bead routing and parallel gaps in implement-bead + run-queue + whats-next.

## Why this exists

Cross-cut audit on PR #76 found that start-bead Step 2.7 uses a triple-check (epic-type + no implementation-ready + has-non-gate-child) to detect Y_container. The triple is not specific to Y_container — any project epic with active children matches identically. M6 from the cross-cut report.

Parallel gaps elsewhere:

- implement-bead epic/milestone fallback (line 137): currently safe (Y_containers have no formula-* label because --no-inherit-labels at creation), but the safety is coincidental, not designed
- run-queue: dispatches via bd ready --label implementation-ready. Rule C says Y_containers must NOT carry that label. If migration ever leaves a residual, run-queue picks up the Y_container and tries to implement-bead it (semantically wrong, bd accepts it silently)
- whats-next collect.py Rule B: filters containers from queues. Currently re-implements its own classification

All four consumers want the same question answered: 'given this bead, what kind is it?'

## What this helper does

Single helper at src/plugins/beads/.beads/scripts/bd-classify-bead.sh, read-only, returns one of:
  DECISION=leaf | y-container | project-epic | milestone | legacy-y

Probes used:
- bd show issue_type → milestone shortcut
- bd label list for produced-from-* (legacy-y if no [Impl] prefix on parent)
- bd list --parent <id> for children, filter [Impl]-prefixed-and-title-matching → y-container if found
- absence of all of the above + type=epic → project-epic
- everything else → leaf

Output: KEY=VALUE lines on stdout
  DECISION=<kind>
  REASON=<short text explaining the decision>
  Y_IMPL_ID=<id> (when DECISION=y-container)
  SOURCE_ID=<id> (when DECISION=y-container — the closed X that produced this container)

## Acceptance Criteria

- Helper ships at src/plugins/beads/.beads/scripts/bd-classify-bead.sh
- Five classifications covered: leaf, y-container, project-epic, milestone, legacy-y
- y-container detection: must have at least one [Impl]-prefixed child whose title sans prefix matches the candidate's title (avoids M6 misroute)
- legacy-y detection: bead is target of a closed source's produced-bead-* label AND title does not start with '[Impl] '
- Exit 0 on classification success; exit 1 on bd/jq error
- Retrofit start-bead Step 2.7 to call this helper (closes M6)
- Tests cover: (a) leaf feature, (b) Y_container with one Y_impl child, (c) Y_container with multiple [Impl] children, (d) project epic with non-[Impl] children, (e) milestone, (f) legacy single-Y bead, (g) ambiguous shapes (epic with mixed children)
- Follow-up beads file separately to retrofit implement-bead fallback + whats-next collect.py Rule B (out of scope for this PR)

title: Extract bd-detect-y-container.sh helper for Y_container title-match discriminator
description:
Extract a read-only shell helper that answers two questions about the 2-level Y_container/Y_impl shape, replacing 4 duplicated title-match sites — one of which is currently wrong.

## Why this exists

Cross-cut audit on PR #76 found the title-match discriminator (Y_container.title == Y_impl.title without '[Impl] ' prefix) is hand-coded in 4 sites:

- brainstorm-bead.formula.toml Step 1 orphan resume path (lines 380-407) — CORRECT, includes pending-split-<X> fallback for crash recovery
- merge-and-cleanup.formula.toml source-bead-gate (lines 175-187) — CORRECT, no pending-split fallback (legitimate: marker stripped by then)
- merge-and-cleanup.formula.toml cleanup step (lines 302-314) — CORRECT
- bd-finalize-create-impl-bead.sh State 2 reuse-guard (lines 269-283) — WRONG (uses PARENT_HAS_PRODUCED which is never set on Y_container; always falls through to State 0, duplicating Y_container/Y_impl pair on every State 2 resume)

C1 from the cross-cut report (CRITICAL severity) is a direct symptom of this duplication.

## What this helper does

Single helper at src/plugins/beads/.beads/scripts/bd-detect-y-container.sh, read-only, two modes:

Mode 1 (impl-driven): given a Y_impl candidate id, find and return the matching Y_container id
  bd-detect-y-container.sh --impl-id <id> [--allow-pending-split]

Mode 2 (container-driven): given a Y_container candidate id, verify it title-matches a specific Y_impl
  bd-detect-y-container.sh --container-id <id> --expected-impl-title <title>

Output: KEY=VALUE lines on stdout
  DECISION=is-container | is-impl | legacy | no-match
  CONTAINER_ID=<id> (when DECISION=is-container or is-impl)
  IMPL_ID=<id> (when DECISION=is-impl)
  PENDING_SPLIT=yes|no (when --allow-pending-split passed)

## Acceptance Criteria

- Helper ships at src/plugins/beads/.beads/scripts/bd-detect-y-container.sh
- Title-match logic: container.title == impl.title with '[Impl] ' prefix stripped
- --allow-pending-split flag accepts pending-split-<X> label on Y_container as secondary signal (covers brainstorm-bead Step 1 crash window)
- DECISION=legacy returned when source has produced-bead-* pointing to a non-[Impl]-prefixed Y bead (caller can decide whether to deprecate or escalate)
- DECISION=no-match returned when no candidate matches (caller falls through to State 0)
- Exit 0 on classification success (any DECISION value); exit 1 only on bd/jq error or malformed args
- Tests cover: (a) happy-path container+impl pair, (b) pending-split crash window, (c) legacy single-Y, (d) no match, (e) multiple impl children under same container (return both? or escalate? — to be decided in brainstorm)
- Retrofit all 4 known sites in this bead's PR (brainstorm-bead Step 1, merge-and-cleanup ×2, bd-finalize State 2) — this is the C1 critical fix
- Test files updated to assert helper invocation rather than inline grep

title: Extract bd-hep-escalate.sh helper consolidating HEP escalation pattern
description:
Extract a single shell helper that encapsulates the Human Escalation Pattern (HEP) defined in beads.md, replacing 12+ inline duplicated HEP shapes across formulas and skills.

## Why this exists

Cross-cut audit on PR #76 (agents-config-pqvc abandonment, see notes) revealed the HEP procedure is hand-rolled inline in at least 12 sites:

- brainstorm-bead.formula.toml — 3 sites (multiple produced-bead-* labels, multiple orphan Y candidates, unexpected children)
- merge-and-cleanup.formula.toml — 4 sites (source-bead-gate ×2, cleanup steps 4 and 5)
- implement-feature.formula.toml — 3 sites NOT covered by g17x exception (lines 84-87, 373-378, 673)
- fix-bug.formula.toml — 4 sites NOT covered by g17x exception (lines 85-86, 182-183, 417-418, 672)
- docs-only.formula.toml — 1 g17x-exempt site (merge-or-handoff hand-off)

Multiple sites drift independently:
- brainstorm-bead and merge-and-cleanup miss bd update <source> --status open revert
- implement-feature and fix-bug bare-stamp human directly on source + step bead, violating the single-bead human invariant
- Several do bd dep add on container-shaped feature sources without container detection (epic-wall hard-error risk)
- resolve-human-bead Probe 3 (Scenario C: HEP escalation) misroutes the bare-stamped cases into Probe 5 (orphan task) because no incoming dep edge exists

The correct shape exists already in bd-finalize-container-gate.sh hep_create_human (lines 125-186) but only handles the container branch.

## What this helper does

Single helper at src/plugins/beads/.beads/scripts/bd-hep-escalate.sh that takes:
- --source-bead-id <id> (required)
- --title <one-line> (required)
- --description <multi-line> (required)
- --scenario-hint <enum> (recommended: spec-amended | scope-expanded | tooling-credentials | architectural-rework | abandoned)
- --step-bead-id <id> (optional, audit context)
- --mol-id <id> (optional, audit context)
- --worktree <path> (optional, audit context)

Internally:
1. Detect SRC_TYPE via bd show; classify as container (epic, milestone, feature-with-active-children) vs non-container
2. Create human bead with proper shape: --parent for containers (no --no-inherit-labels needed since brand-new), sibling-with-blocks-dep for non-containers
3. Use dual-shape bd create --json parse (jq 'if type == array then .[0].id else .id end')
4. bd label add <new-id> human
5. bd update <new-id> --append-notes with audit block (source/step/mol/worktree/scenario)
6. Non-containers only: bd dep add <source> <new-id>
7. bd update <source> --status open (mandatory revert)
8. Exit 0 on success; exit 1 on any failure with stderr message and no partial state

## Acceptance Criteria

- Helper script ships at src/plugins/beads/.beads/scripts/bd-hep-escalate.sh
- Idempotent on retry: if previous escalation bead exists under same source with matching title, return existing id without creating a duplicate
- Container vs non-container shape decision matches the canonical HEP rule in beads.md
- --scenario-hint enum validated; reject unknown values
- Source bead always reverts to status=open after successful escalation (even on container branch)
- Step bead and source bead never receive the human label
- bats or shellcheck-driven tests cover: (a) leaf source, (b) epic source, (c) milestone source, (d) feature-with-active-children source, (e) feature-leaf source, (f) dual-shape bd create JSON variants
- Retrofit at least one formula site as proof-of-integration in this bead's PR (suggest brainstorm-bead Step 1 multiple-produced-bead path, which is small and isolated)
- Remaining retrofits filed as a follow-up bead (one per formula file) so this PR stays reviewable
- bd-static-check.sh (separate bead) catches future missed sites

title: [merge-gate] y9mm-a-active-child-filter-dis
description: Merge gate child for agents-config-y9mm.6. Closed only by merge-and-cleanup after merge + branch/worktree cleanup.

title: [Impl] [y9mm-A] Active-child filter: discriminate HEP children via hep-pause label
description:
## Summary

Introduce a hep-pause discriminator label to distinguish HEP escalation children from formula-gate [Human verify] children. Update both active-child-count filters to count HEP children (keeping a HEP-paused container classified as a container), and stamp hep-pause on all new HEP escalation children created under container sources.

## Background / Motivation

PR #72 (epic-hygiene) introduced the parent-child HEP shape for container sources. When an agent escalates on a container bead it creates a human-labeled child (HEB) under the container. Both collect.py (active_child_count) and bd-finalize-container-gate.sh exclude ALL human-labeled children from active-child counts. This exclusion was correct for its original purpose: [Human verify] children (brainstorm finalize step 5b, human-labeled, formula-gate artifacts) should not make a parent look like a container.

The new HEP shape made human-labeled children mean two different things:
  1. Formula-gate artifact ([Human verify]) — should be EXCLUDED from count
  2. Live HEP escalation (HEB) — should be COUNTED (container is paused; must not leak into queues)

Because both look identical to the filter, a container paused via HEP gets count=0 → classified as leaf → surfaces in planning/implementation queues while still awaiting human input. Cluster A of y9mm (R3.1, R3.7, R3.8 from PR #72 round-3 Copilot review).

## Requirements

1. New label hep-pause: Every HEP transaction that creates a human escalation bead as a CHILD of a container source MUST stamp hep-pause on the new HEB in this exact order:
   a. bd label add ESC_ID human
   b. bd label add ESC_ID hep-pause   ← immediately after human
   c. bd update ESC_ID --append-notes ...
   d. bd update SOURCE_ID --status open
   e. bd mol burn / cleanup
   This ordering ensures the HEB is consistently labeled before any subsequent operation can fail under set -euo pipefail.

2. collect.py filter narrowing (active_child_count population loop, ~L361):
   Change from: if 'merge-gate' in labels or 'human' in labels: continue
   to: if 'merge-gate' in labels or ('human' in labels and 'hep-pause' not in labels): continue
   NOTE: is_impl_candidate's separate 'human' not in labels guard (line 249) and
   is_brainstorm_candidate's 'human' not in labels guard (line 232) are intentionally NOT changed
   — see Design Notes.

3. bd-finalize-container-gate.sh filter narrowing (feature CHILD_COUNT probe, ~L91-92): apply same narrowed exclusion: exclude merge-gate OR (human AND NOT hep-pause).

4. Stamp sites — all HEP container branches must follow the stamp ordering in Req 1:
   - bd-finalize-container-gate.sh L141 (ambiguous-Y HEP) and L167 (reclassification HEP)
   - src/plugins/beads/.claude/rules/beads.md HEP section container branch
   - src/plugins/beads/.agents/skills/start-bead/SKILL.md Route C: length-0 and length-2+ container branches
   - src/plugins/beads/.agents/skills/implement-bead/SKILL.md §0 container branch
   NOTE: The SRC_ACTIVE_CHILDREN probe inside each of these code blocks (the feature container-detection probe that runs BEFORE creating the HEB) is intentionally NOT updated in this bead. It still uses 'merge-gate or human' exclusion. See Design Notes for rationale.

5. Doc-comment updates (descriptions of the filter logic):
   - collect.py L207-212: update filter description to (human AND NOT hep-pause)
   - bd-finalize-container-gate.sh L86-90: same update
   NOTE: SRC_ACTIVE_CHILDREN probe comments in beads.md, start-bead/SKILL.md, and implement-bead/SKILL.md are intentionally NOT updated — the probes themselves are unchanged, so their comments remain accurate.

6. Add hep-pause to label reference table in src/plugins/beads/.claude/rules/beads-labels.md:
   Set by: HEP procedure (container branch only)
   Meaning: Identifies a human-escalation bead (HEB) created as a CHILD of a container source. Unlike bare human-labeled children (formula-gate artifacts), hep-pause children are counted in active_child_count so the container stays classified as a container while awaiting human resolution.

7. Tests:
   - collect_test.sh T11: feature bead with one open child labeled human+hep-pause → is_container() = True (bead appears in planning-ready, not hidden). Uses the existing bd shim pattern.
   - collect_test.sh T12: feature bead with only a human+hep-pause child, run collect.py --mode implementation → feature does NOT appear in implementation section output. End-to-end via bd shim.
   - pr72_validate_c_test.sh (new fixture): feature source with exactly one open child labeled human+hep-pause, no produced-bead-* labels → bd-finalize-container-gate.sh returns 'handled' (not 'not-container'). Observable via the script's stdout decision token.
   - Existing PR #72 epic-hygiene tests remain green.

## Out of Scope

- y9mm-B (start-bead Route D + resolve-human-bead Probe 6): depend on this bead, ship after
- y9mm-C, y9mm-D, y9mm-E: separate beads
- SRC_ACTIVE_CHILDREN probes inside HEP skill procedures: these probes run BEFORE any HEB is created (pre-stamp), so no hep-pause child exists when they execute for the first escalation. Second-escalation safety is argued in Design Notes; narrowing those probes is tracked in y9mm-B scope.
- Backfill of existing legacy HEP-paused containers (zero open HEP escalations currently)
- Removal of merge-gate/human filter (pqvc scope; pqvc spec says KEEP)

## Design Notes

- hep-pause lives on the HEB child, not the source. It discriminates what kind of child the HEB is.
- is_impl_candidate L249 and is_brainstorm_candidate L232 'human' guards: intentionally unchanged. Source beads never carry 'human' (single-bead invariant). HEB children should never surface as brainstorm-ready or impl-ready regardless of hep-pause — those guards are vacuous architectural invariants, not active filters for the hep-pause case.
- SRC_ACTIVE_CHILDREN probes in skill bodies: intentionally unchanged. At first-escalation time, no HEB child exists yet, so the probe is correct regardless of narrowing. At second-escalation time (container already has a live HEB child), the probe would misclassify the container as a non-container — but this requires Route D to miss the existing HEP pause (a separate gap fixed by y9mm-B). For epic sources, this would fail loudly on the blocks epic-wall; for feature sources, y9mm-B closes the window. The narrowing would cause NO change at first-escalation time and would only help at second-escalation time — a scenario that y9mm-B prevents from being reached.
- Stamp ordering safety: placing hep-pause immediately after human (step b in Req 1, before notes-append and mol-burn) ensures that under set -euo pipefail, if any later step fails, the HEB already carries both labels and is in the correct state for recovery.
- Forward-only: no backfill. Zero existing HEP-paused containers; transitional leak window acceptable.
- pqvc compatibility: pqvc restructures finalize to Y_container (epic) + Y_impl (feature). After pqvc, merge-gate and [Human verify] children attach under Y_container (CONTAINER_ALWAYS). y9mm.1 filter change remains valid for non-Y feature-type container sources. pqvc spec says KEEP the collect.py filter. Do NOT block y9mm.1 on pqvc.

## Open Questions

None.
acceptance_criteria:
active_child_count in collect.py excludes (human AND NOT hep-pause) children, not all human-labeled. is_impl_candidate L249 and is_brainstorm_candidate L232 human guards unchanged (vacuous under single-bead invariant; source beads never carry human).
bd-finalize-container-gate.sh CHILD_COUNT probe applies same (human AND NOT hep-pause) exclusion.
All stamp sites follow human→hep-pause→notes-append→source-revert→mol-burn order. Sites: bd-finalize-container-gate.sh x2, beads.md, start-bead/SKILL.md Route C x2, implement-bead/SKILL.md §0.
collect_test.sh T11: feature with one open human+hep-pause child (feature itself carries NO implementation-ready) → is_container() = True (feature appears in planning-ready, not hidden or in impl section).
collect_test.sh T12: feature carrying implementation-ready with only a human+hep-pause child → collect.py --mode implementation → feature NOT in implementation output. (implementation-ready label on parent is required to make the negative assertion meaningful.)
pr72_validate_c_test.sh new fixture: feature source with only a human+hep-pause child, no produced-bead-* labels → gate returns 'handled'; AND bd label list <escalation-child> includes hep-pause (not just human). Both assertions required — 'handled' alone would pass against current unmodified code.
Existing PR #72 epic-hygiene tests remain green.
hep-pause in beads-labels.md: Set by = HEP procedure (container branch only); Meaning = counted in active_child_count so container stays classified while awaiting human resolution, unlike bare human (formula-gate) children.
SRC_ACTIVE_CHILDREN probe comments in beads.md, start-bead/SKILL.md, and implement-bead/SKILL.md: intentionally left matching the unchanged probes (which still use merge-gate or human). This is a known inconsistency with collect.py's narrowed filter; it is safe at first-escalation time and is tracked in y9mm-B scope.

title: Restructure brainstorm-bead finalize Y to: Y_container (epic|feature) → [Impl] + [merge-gate]
Reduce and rationalize agent instruction context
description: The agent instruction context loaded at session start has grown organically into a bloated, redundant, and in places contradictory state. This epic tracks the work to clean up the architecture: eliminate double-loading, extract anti-pattern bash scripts, rationalize the beads multi-source overlap, resolve behavioral conflicts, and establish conventions that prevent re-bloat as the rule set grows.

title: Resolve MEMORY.md conflict: bd prime incorrectly prohibits user's auto-memory system
description:
bd prime Core Rules contain an explicit instruction that conflicts with the user's established auto-memory system.

## The conflict

bd prime Core Rules inject on every SessionStart:
  'Memory: Use bd remember for persistent knowledge across sessions. Do NOT use MEMORY.md files — they fragment across accounts.'

The user's global AGENTS.md has a full auto-memory system at:
  ~/.claude/projects/.../memory/MEMORY.md

This system is actively used (10 entries, loaded in every session via claudeMd). Every session begins with a rule instructing the agent to avoid the memory infrastructure the user has deliberately built.

## Root cause

bd prime's memory guidance was authored for a context where bd remember is the ONLY memory mechanism. It predates (or ignores) the file-based auto-memory system in the user's global config. The 'do not use MEMORY.md' instruction is a bd-project-level preference, not a universal rule.

## Resolution options

A. Modify bd prime's memory rule to be additive rather than prohibitive:
   'Use bd remember for beads-related insights. Use the auto-memory system (memory/) for cross-project behavioral preferences and corrections.'

B. Remove the MEMORY.md prohibition from bd prime entirely; let the global AGENTS.md auto-memory documentation be authoritative.

C. Upstream change: configure bd prime to not emit the memory guidance when an auto-memory system is detected.

Option A or B are immediately actionable in this repo. Option C requires upstream beads plugin changes.

## Acceptance Criteria
- No instruction in bd prime contradicts use of the file-based auto-memory system
- The boundary between bd remember (beads insights) and auto-memory (behavioral corrections, cross-project preferences) is clearly defined
- Agents do not receive conflicting memory guidance at session start

title: Rationalize beads context: eliminate redundancy and fix conflicts across four sources
description:
Beads-related instructions currently arrive from four separate sources on every Claude session start, with significant redundancy, inconsistency, and at least one outright behavioral conflict.

## The four sources (~463 lines combined)

1. rules/beads.md (191 lines) — type reference, rules, HEP, session separation
2. rules/beads-labels.md (36 lines) — label reference, molecule linkage
3. Project AGENTS.md beads integration block (45 lines) — quick ref, core rules, session completion
4. bd prime SessionStart injection (191 lines) — 26 memories, session protocol, core rules, full CLI reference

## Specific redundancies

- 'Use bd for ALL tracking / no TodoWrite / no markdown TODOs' — stated in beads.md, project AGENTS.md block, AND bd prime Core Rules (three copies)
- Session separation guidance appears in beads.md AND bd prime
- bd prime Essential Commands overlaps with what beads.md covers at a different abstraction level

## Specific conflicts

**Session close protocol mismatch**: bd prime SESSION CLOSE PROTOCOL (git status, add, commit, push — 4 steps) omits 'bd dolt push', which project AGENTS.md treats as MANDATORY. An agent following bd prime's protocol loses Dolt sync silently.

**TaskCreate prohibition conflict**: bd prime Core Rules say 'Do NOT use TodoWrite, TaskCreate'. The harness system-reminder fires EVERY turn suggesting TaskCreate for progress tracking. These directly contradict each other every single turn.

## Proposed resolution

- Establish a single authoritative source for each rule, eliminate the copies
- 'Use bd for tracking' belongs only in beads.md (the rules file); remove from project AGENTS.md block and bd prime Core Rules
- Session protocol: bd prime and project AGENTS.md must agree on the canonical steps including bd dolt push
- TaskCreate conflict: scope bd prime's prohibition to 'use bd for bead/issue tracking' rather than blanket-prohibiting all task management primitives; or remove from bd prime and let beads.md be authoritative
- Review beads.md type reference vs bd prime Essential Commands for further overlap consolidation

## Acceptance Criteria
- No rule stated in more than one source without a clear cross-reference
- Session close protocol is identical in all sources that state it (including bd dolt push)
- TaskCreate prohibition does not conflict with the harness system-reminder
- Total beads-related context lines measurably reduced

title: [merge-gate] restructure-brainstorm-bead-fi
description: Merge gate child for agents-config-pqvc.

title: [Impl] Restructure brainstorm-bead finalize Y to: Y_container (epic|feature) → [Impl] + [merge-gate]
description:
## Summary

Restructure brainstorm-bead finalize to produce a 2-level Y_container/Y_impl shape instead of a single Y bead. Y_container (type=epic) is the structural container; Y_impl (formula-derived type, [Impl] prefix) is the executable leaf that carries impl-ready labels and is picked by run-queue. Also fixes an existing merge-and-cleanup jq bug that leaves merge-gate children open after cleanup.

## Background / Motivation

Currently finalize step 4 creates a single Y bead and step 5b attaches merge-gate + [Human verify] children directly under Y. This makes Y simultaneously a Rule B container (feature with active children) and the executable impl leaf. collect.py excludes merge-gate/human children from container detection to paper over this. PR #72 (eos5) established the Rule B/C foundation; this is the fast-follow to resolve the structural ambiguity at source.

## Requirements

1. finalize creates Y_container (type=epic, priority=X.priority) as structural container first, marks it in_progress immediately, then creates Y_impl (formula-derived type) as child of Y_container with [Impl] prefix in title.
2. Y_impl carries: brainstormed, implementation-ready, implementation-readied-session-*, formula-*, ralf:* (if any), produced-from-X dep, copied X labels. Y_impl type = formula-derived (implement-feature -> feature, fix-bug -> bug, docs-only -> task).
3. Y_container carries NONE of: brainstormed, implementation-ready, implementation-readied-session-*, formula-*, ralf:*, produced-from-X.
4. merge-gate child (and [Human verify] children) attach under Y_container as siblings of Y_impl. Y_ID in step 5b = Y_container_id.
5. Step 5a: pre-existing merge-gate and human-labeled children of X migrate to Y_container.
6. Step 6 dep migration: ALL dep types migrate from X to Y_impl. Y_container carries no migrated deps.
7. Step 7: X gets produced-bead-<Y_container_id> label (canonical forward pointer).
8. Step 8 close-walk: Y_container.parent = X.parent; walk halts because Y_container is open.
9. bd-finalize-create-impl-bead.sh refactored: outputs two IDs (Y_container_id on line 1, Y_impl_id on line 2 or as KEY=VALUE). Formula step 4 extracts both: Y_CONTAINER_ID and Y_IMPL_ID. Step 5a/5b use Y_CONTAINER_ID for --parent. Step 6 uses Y_IMPL_ID as dep migration target. Step 7 stamps produced-bead-<Y_CONTAINER_ID>.
10. merge-and-cleanup pour contract: implement-bead pours with bead-id=Y_impl (unchanged from current behavior). At the TOP of the formula execution block (before source-bead-gate), the formula resolves: CONTAINER_ID=; [ -z CONTAINER_ID ] && CONTAINER_ID=BEAD_ID (legacy fallback). ALL child lookups in source-bead-gate and cleanup steps use CONTAINER_ID, not bead-id directly.
11. Fix merge-and-cleanup line 279 jq bug: '.labels | index("merge-gate")' -> 'index("merge-gate")' (bd label list --json returns flat array, not object).
12. start-bead updates: (a) routing table gains new row: open epic container with impl-ready children -> container-routing-to-Y_impl; (b) Step 3 forward-target conditional: when preflight resolves to a container (epic, no impl-ready label), apply container-routing algorithm before Routes A/B/C/D.
13. collect.py merge-gate/human child-filter: KEEP (may produce features from brainstorms in future).
14. I3 discovered-work during Y_impl implementation: in-scope discoveries use --parent Y_container.

## Script Interface (bd-finalize-create-impl-bead.sh)

Input: --source-bead-id, --type (Y_impl type), --priority, --title (Y_impl title, [Impl] prefix), --labels, --spec-file, --ac-file, --parent (X.parent, passed as Y_container's parent).

Output (stdout, two lines):
  Y_CONTAINER_ID=<id>
  Y_IMPL_ID=<id>

Formula extracts via: eval  or parse by key.

## Idempotency State Table

Detection priority (checked in order):

| State | Detection | Resume action |
|-------|-----------|---------------|
| 3: all done | produced-bead-<id> on X AND Y_container has Y_impl child | return Y_CONTAINER_ID + Y_IMPL_ID; skip to step 8 consistency check |
| 2: both created, X not stamped | produced-from-X found on Y_impl via bd label search; no produced-bead-* on X | stamp X with produced-bead-<Y_container_id>; continue from step 5 |
| 1: Y_container only | pending-split-<X_id> label found on a bead (Y_container); no Y_impl with produced-from-X | create Y_impl under that Y_container; remove pending-split-<X_id>; continue from step 5 |
| 0: neither | none of the above | stamp Y_container candidate with pending-split-<X_id> immediately; create Y_container; create Y_impl; remove pending-split-<X_id> |

State 1 detection: Y_container gets a temporary label 'pending-split-<X_id>' immediately after its bd create call, before Y_impl is created. Removed immediately after Y_impl is confirmed created.

Escalate HEP if: multiple produced-bead-* labels on X, or multiple beads with pending-split-<X_id>.

## start-bead Container-Routing Algorithm

When closed-bead preflight resolves to Y_container (open, epic type, no impl-ready):
1. Probe impl-ready children: bd list --parent Y_container --label implementation-ready --json | jq '[.[] | select(.status != "closed")]'
2. Exactly one result: treat that Y_impl as routed target for ALL subsequent route decisions (A/B/C/D). Route A session gate evaluates against Y_impl labels.
3. Zero results: Y_container has no impl bead yet; run Route C on Y_container directly.
4. Multiple results: escalate HEP.

start-bead SKILL.md routing table addition:
  open | is-container | has impl-ready child(ren) | -> container-routing (probe and route to Y_impl)

## merge-and-cleanup Child-Lookup Contract

bead-id pour variable = Y_impl (implement-bead pours it; unchanged).

Resolution block at formula TOP (before source-bead-gate step):
  CONTAINER_ID=
  [ -z "$CONTAINER_ID" ] && CONTAINER_ID="$BEAD_ID"   # legacy single-Y fallback

source-bead-gate steps 1, 2, 3: replace 'bd list --parent bead-id' with 'bd list --parent $CONTAINER_ID'.
cleanup steps 4, 5, 6: same replacement.
BEAD_ID (Y_impl) still used for: bd close bead-id (closing Y_impl itself is NOT done — Y_impl closes via I2 close-walk from CONTAINER_ID close).

Note: merge-and-cleanup closes CONTAINER_ID (Y_container) and runs close-walk from CONTAINER_ID. Y_impl closes as part of that walk when all its siblings are also closed.

## Y_container I1 Claim-Walk

finalize marks Y_container in_progress immediately after creation (before creating Y_impl). When implement-bead's I1 claim-walk runs against Y_impl, it walks: Y_impl -> Y_container (already in_progress, no-op) -> X.parent. No gap.

## Legacy Compatibility

Legacy single-Y beads (pre-f19p) have Y.parent = empty (X's parent or null). CONTAINER_ID resolution falls back to BEAD_ID (Y_impl = legacy Y). All source-bead-gate and cleanup logic then operates on legacy Y as before.

## Out of Scope

- Multiple Y_containers from one brainstorm run (future bead)
- closed-bead-preflight multi-label handling (future bead)
- Removing collect.py merge-gate/human filter

## Design Decisions

- Y_container type = epic; all deps migrate to Y_impl (avoids epic wall entirely; Y_container is dep-free structural container)
- Script outputs two IDs; formula tracks Y_CONTAINER_ID and Y_IMPL_ID explicitly through steps 4-8
- pending-split-<X_id> temporary label enables State 1 crash-recovery without an unreliable orphan scan
- bead-id for merge-and-cleanup = Y_impl (run-queue/implement-bead unchanged); CONTAINER_ID resolved internally by formula
- merge-gate jq bug fix and legacy CONTAINER_ID fallback both in scope (same touch area)

## Open Questions

None.

## Spec Clarifications (cycle-3 findings)

### State 2 resume path (Step 1 ORPHAN_COUNT==1)

When the formula's Step 1 idempotency probe finds ORPHAN_COUNT==1 via produced-from-X label on Y_impl, the resume path must derive CONTAINER_ID before proceeding to steps 5+:

  Y_IMPL_ID=<extracted orphan id>
  CONTAINER_ID=$(bd show "$Y_IMPL_ID" --json | jq -r '.[0].parent // empty')
  [ -z "$CONTAINER_ID" ] && CONTAINER_ID="$Y_IMPL_ID"   # legacy single-Y fallback

Then: Y_CONTAINER_ID=$CONTAINER_ID. Step 5a uses Y_CONTAINER_ID for --parent (migrating X's children to Y_container, not Y_impl).

### merge-and-cleanup CONTAINER_ID resolution (corrected pseudocode)

  CONTAINER_ID=$(bd show "$BEAD_ID" --json | jq -r '.[0].parent // empty')
  [ -z "$CONTAINER_ID" ] && CONTAINER_ID="$BEAD_ID"

(The prior pseudocode block was garbled by shell expansion during spec authoring. This is the correct form.)

### closed-bead-preflight.sh: no change required

closed-bead-preflight.sh reads produced-bead-* on X and returns decision=forward target=Y_container_id. It does not need to know Y_container is an epic. Step 3 container-routing in start-bead handles the rest. No file changes needed.

### pending-split-<X_id> label placement (State 0 and State 1)

State 0 sequence:
  1. bd create --type epic ... -> Y_container_id
  2. bd label add Y_container_id 'pending-split-<X_id>'  (crash recovery marker)
  3. bd create ... --parent Y_container_id ... -> Y_impl_id
  4. bd label remove Y_container_id 'pending-split-<X_id>'

The label is stamped on Y_container AFTER its bd create, not before. State 1 detection: probe for any bead with pending-split-<X_id> label -> that bead is Y_container.

### Bead title note

The bead title says 'epic|feature' — this reflects the open question at creation time. The spec is authoritative: Y_container type = epic.
acceptance_criteria:
brainstorm-bead finalize produces Y_container (type=epic, priority=X.priority) + Y_impl (formula-derived type) 2-level shape on happy path.
Y_impl carries full impl-ready label set (brainstormed, implementation-ready, implementation-readied-session-*, formula-*, ralf:* if any, produced-from-X dep).
Y_container carries none of: brainstormed, implementation-ready, implementation-readied-session-*, formula-*, ralf:*, produced-from-X.

Human verify children (step 5b) attach under Y_container.

All dep edges (blocks, blocked-by, tracks, etc.) migrate from X to Y_impl. Y_container has no migrated deps.

start-bead: Y_container with zero impl-ready children routes to Route C (brainstorm); multiple impl-ready children escalates HEP.

notes:
PR: https://github.com/scotthamilton77/agents-config/pull/76
## 2026-05-15 — Decision: Abandon PR #76; rebuild on helper foundation

### Decision

PR #76 (https://github.com/scotthamilton77/agents-config/pull/76) is being walked away from rather than rescued. The current implementation approach (inline shell in formulas + hand-rolled HEP/discriminator/claim-walk logic in each site) is structurally unsound. The bead is reset to status=open, readiness labels stripped (implementation-ready, brainstormed, implementation-readied-session-88c04f10), and dep-blocked on a new set of foundational helper beads filed under M1. The PR will be closed without merging; the branch feat/agents-config-pqvc-impl-restructure-brainstorm-bea is preserved for forensic reference. The pqvc molecule (mol-udsx9) has its remaining open step closed.

### Rationale

A holistic non-shell review (opus[1m], effort max) of the WIP files in PR #76 followed by a cross-cutting impact analysis exposed that the bugs we were chasing in this PR are not local to brainstorm-bead.formula.toml / merge-and-cleanup.formula.toml / bd-finalize-create-impl-bead.sh — they are symptoms of architectural pattern duplication across at least 4 formulas + 4 skills + 5 scripts.

Specifically:

1. HEP (Human Escalation Pattern) is hand-rolled inline in 12+ sites across brainstorm-bead, merge-and-cleanup, implement-feature, fix-bug, and docs-only formulas. Multiple of these violate the canonical rule in beads.md:
   - bare-stamping 'human' label directly on source/step beads (single-bead human invariant violation; misroutes resolve-human-bead's Probe 3 into Probe 5)
   - missing container detection before bd dep add <source> <esc> (epic-wall hard-error risk on epic sources)
   - missing bd update <source> --status open revert after escalation

2. Y_container title-match discriminator is duplicated in 4 sites; one (bd-finalize State 2 PARENT_HAS_PRODUCED) is wrong — guard never fires, falls through to State 0, creates duplicate Y_container/Y_impl pairs on every State 2 resume.

3. claim-walk (I1 invariant) is hand-rolled in 4 inline sites instead of calling the existing bd-claim-walk.sh helper. Ancestor walks are missing entirely on Y_container creation.

4. Silent failure (bd close ... || true, bd update ... || true) conflates idempotent replay with real errors, creating false-positive cascade failures downstream.

5. Legacy single-Y migration paths in brainstorm-bead silently orphan the old Y while creating a fresh Y_container/Y_impl pair, leaving the orphan with implementation-ready intact and pickable by bd ready (data-corruption-shaped risk).

Round-by-round Copilot review of PR #76 cycled through three iterations exposing successively deeper structural rot — including a regression introduced in round 2 (PARENT_HAS_PRODUCED on Y_container's parent, which is never set there because produced-bead-* is stamped on source X). Each rescue cycle exposed more cross-cutting impact. The PR's stated scope (2-level Y_container/Y_impl shape) is sound; the implementation sits on rotted foundations.

User accepted that backwards-compat is a data-hygiene issue, not a code-around-edge-cases issue. With migration permitted, the helper-first rebuild becomes cheap.

### Recommendations

Five new beads filed under M1 (agents-config-abn9), all P1:

- agents-config-abn9.28 — Extract bd-hep-escalate.sh helper consolidating HEP escalation pattern (12+ inline sites collapse to one helper)
- agents-config-abn9.29 — Extract bd-detect-y-container.sh helper for Y_container title-match discriminator (4 sites collapse; C1 critical fix lands here)
- agents-config-abn9.30 — Extract bd-classify-bead.sh helper for standalone bead classification (closes M6 start-bead Step 2.7 gap)
- agents-config-abn9.31 — Build bd-static-check.sh CI guard for HEP, claim-walk, and silent-failure invariants (machine-enforceable rules)
- agents-config-abn9.32 — Audit and migrate legacy bead state (orphan legacy-Y, mis-stamped human sources, residual produced-bead-* on epic Y_containers); blocks on .28 and .29 for remediation tooling

pqvc dep-blocks on all 5. Sequence:
1. Helpers (28, 29, 30, 31) ship — independent, can parallelize
2. Data hygiene (32) runs after .28/.29 ship — uses the helpers for A2/A1 remediation
3. pqvc re-brainstormed on top of the helpers — formulas and SKILL.md become dramatically simpler because they invoke helpers instead of duplicating logic; the 2-level Y_container/Y_impl design itself is preserved as the goal

Python rewrite of the entire bead skill/formula layer was considered but explicitly DEFERRED. The shell-helpers path delivers ~80% of the architectural benefit at ~20% of the cost, keeps the existing dep footprint (bash + jq + bd, no Python interpreter required on agent hosts), and creates well-tested specs that translate trivially to Python later if the helpers themselves still feel painful. Re-evaluate Python after step 3 above.

### Forensic state preserved

- PR #76: closed without merging (see GitHub for explanation comment)
- Branch feat/agents-config-pqvc-impl-restructure-brainstorm-bea: preserved on remote and local; contains both pushed round-2 commits and 4 unpushed round-3 commits (136432e, e2900b8, e324089, 76022e0)
- Molecule mol-udsx9 (implement-feature): all steps closed; structure preserved as forensic record
- Worktree at .claude/worktrees/feat/agents-config-pqvc-impl-restructure-brainstorm-bea/: preserved
- This bead reset to status=open, blocked on the 5 new beads, with readiness labels stripped

### When ready to re-engage

After abn9.28-32 close, this bead can be re-brainstormed (via brainstorm-bead formula). The cross-cut audit report (see PR #76 conversation transcript) is the canonical design input alongside the original spec.
