title: Fix DYNAMIC-INCLUDE-RULES: decouple rule list from growing rule file set
description:
The DYNAMIC-INCLUDE-RULES mechanism in AGENTS.md.template contains a hardcoded list of rule file names to inline into assembled instruction files. This creates a coupling problem that gets worse as the rule set grows — and misses the underlying structural issue that the current layout doesn't distinguish general rules from agent-specific ones.

## Current state

AGENTS.md.template contains:
  <!-- DYNAMIC-INCLUDE-RULES: delegation,delivery,git-commits,subagents,worktrees -->

This list is manually maintained and already stale:
- completion-gate.md and codex-routing.md exist but are NOT in the list
- beads plugin adds beads.md and beads-labels.md but these are never added to the list
- Any new rule file requires a manual update to this marker
- All rules currently live under src/user/.claude/rules/ regardless of whether they are general or Claude-specific

## Target architecture: convention over include lists

Replace the hardcoded list with a directory convention:

- src/user/.agents/rules/    ← tool-agnostic rules (shared across ALL agents)
- src/user/.claude/rules/    ← Claude-specific rules only
- src/user/.codex/rules/     ← Codex-specific rules only (etc.)

Rules that apply universally (laws, constraints, decision-matrix concretizations like delegation and delivery) move to src/user/.agents/rules/. Rules that are meaningful only for a specific tool (e.g., worktrees placement convention for Claude, codex-routing) stay in the tool-specific folder.

## How install.sh handles each tool under the new convention

**Claude** (native rules loading):
- Stage src/user/.agents/rules/* + src/user/.claude/rules/* → staging/.claude/rules/
- Sync staging → ~/.claude/rules/ as before
- No DYNAMIC-INCLUDE-RULES marker in Claude's AGENTS.md.template
- Claude's native rules/ loading handles everything; no inlining needed

**Other agents (Codex, Gemini, OpenCode)** (flat-file, need inlining):
- DYNAMIC-INCLUDE-RULES marker replaced with DYNAMIC-INCLUDE-ALL-RULES (or equivalent)
- flatten_agents_md globs src/user/.agents/rules/*.md + src/user/.{agent}/rules/*.md in alphabetical order
- All general + agent-specific rules inlined automatically — no list to maintain
- New rule files in either folder are picked up on the next install with no marker change

## Impact of current state

- Context bloat for Claude (double-load of inlined + natively loaded rules)
- Silent omission of newer rules from non-Claude assembled files (incomplete context for those agents)
- Maintenance burden: every new rule file requires remembering to update the marker

## Migration

- Audit src/user/.claude/rules/ and classify each file as general vs. Claude-specific
- Move general rules to src/user/.agents/rules/ (create the directory)
- Update install.sh to combine both rule sources for Claude staging, and for flat-file tools
- Remove DYNAMIC-INCLUDE-RULES from Claude's AGENTS.md.template
- Update non-Claude templates to use the new auto-include marker

## Acceptance Criteria
- src/user/.agents/rules/ exists and contains all tool-agnostic rules
- src/user/.claude/rules/ contains only Claude-specific rules
- Claude's AGENTS.md.template has no DYNAMIC-INCLUDE-RULES marker
- install.sh combines agent-rules + tool-rules for Claude staging (both to rules/)
- install.sh inlines agent-rules + tool-rules for flat-file tools without a hardcoded list
- Non-Claude templates inline all rules files automatically on install without marker updates
- All rules currently installed remain installed and unchanged in content
acceptance_criteria:
src/user/.agents/rules/ exists and contains: delegation.md, delivery.md, completion-gate.md, subagents.md, worktrees.md
src/user/.claude/rules/ contains only Claude-specific rules; tool-agnostic rules are absent
git-commits.md and codex-routing.md do not exist anywhere under src/
src/user/.claude/rules/claude-sandbox.md and src/user/.claude/rules/claude-to-codex-routing.md exist with content identical to the renamed originals (except delegation.md:11 citation updated)
Claude AGENTS.md.template contains no DYNAMIC-INCLUDE-RULES marker
Codex AGENTS.md.template contains DYNAMIC-INCLUDE-ALL-RULES marker
Gemini GEMINI.md.template contains DYNAMIC-INCLUDE-ALL-RULES marker
OpenCode AGENTS.md.template contains DYNAMIC-INCLUDE-ALL-RULES marker (not the old hardcoded list)
install.sh Phase 2 stages src/user/.agents/rules/* to staging/<tool>/rules/ for all tools
install.sh flatten_agents_md uses find (not ls glob) to collect rules; emits warn if rules dir empty or missing
install.sh Phase 6 plugin_agents_dir loop includes 'rules' alongside 'skills' and 'agents'
After install, ~/.claude/rules/ contains all rules from all three sources (general + Claude-specific + plugin) with no content regression
After install, assembled Codex, Gemini, and OpenCode instruction files contain all general rules inlined (verifiable: delegation/delivery/completion-gate content present in assembled file)
delegation.md:11 references claude-to-codex-routing.md (not codex-routing.md)
wait-for-pr-comments/SKILL.md:657 references claude-sandbox.md (not git-commits.md)
src/user/.claude/README.md and AGENTS.md contain no references to git-commits.md or codex-routing.md
src/user/.opencode/OPENCODE-EXTENSIONS.md.template reflects new filenames and inclusion set
scripts/smoke/verify-artifacts.sh:123 regex catches DYNAMIC-INCLUDE-ALL-RULES as an unprocessed marker
scripts/prune-list contains entries for claude/rules/git-commits.md and claude/rules/codex-routing.md
Build passes. Tests pass (install.sh --dry-run against a temp HOME).
notes:
## Summary

Replace the hardcoded DYNAMIC-INCLUDE-RULES list with a directory convention. General rules move to src/user/.agents/rules/; Claude-specific rules stay in src/user/.claude/rules/ with unambiguous claude-* names. Flat-file tools (Codex, Gemini, OpenCode) get a new DYNAMIC-INCLUDE-ALL-RULES marker that auto-globs all staged rule files — no list to maintain, no silent omissions. Claude removes its marker entirely and relies on its native rules/ directory loading.

## Background

The DYNAMIC-INCLUDE-RULES marker in AGENTS.md templates requires a hardcoded comma-separated list of rule filenames. Problems:
- Already stale: completion-gate.md and codex-routing.md exist but are missing from the list
- Plugin-added rules (beads.md, beads-labels.md) are never in the list and never reach non-Claude tools
- Codex and Gemini have NO rules marker at all — they receive zero rules context
- Claude receives rules twice: once inlined via DYNAMIC-INCLUDE-RULES into AGENTS.md, and again via native rules/ loading at session start
- Two Claude-specific rules (git-commits.md, codex-routing.md) have misleadingly general names

## Requirements

### Directory convention
- src/user/.agents/rules/ — tool-agnostic rules (apply to all tools)
- src/user/.claude/rules/ — Claude-specific rules only
- Other per-tool rules/ directories follow the same pattern when they exist

### Rule classification (based on content analysis)
- GENERAL → src/user/.agents/rules/: delegation, delivery, completion-gate, subagents, worktrees
- CLAUDE-SPECIFIC → src/user/.claude/rules/: claude-sandbox (was git-commits), claude-to-codex-routing (was codex-routing)

### Renaming
- git-commits.md → claude-sandbox.md (content is about Claude Code sandbox constraints, not git broadly)
- codex-routing.md → claude-to-codex-routing.md (clarifies direction: from Claude, to Codex, via Claude plugin)
- Rule content is unchanged in both renamed files

### Template changes
- Claude (AGENTS.md.template): remove DYNAMIC-INCLUDE-RULES marker entirely; native rules/ loading is sufficient and already active
- OpenCode (AGENTS.md.template): replace DYNAMIC-INCLUDE-RULES list with DYNAMIC-INCLUDE-ALL-RULES (no args)
- Codex (AGENTS.md.template): add DYNAMIC-INCLUDE-ALL-RULES marker (currently has no rules marker)
- Gemini (GEMINI.md.template): add DYNAMIC-INCLUDE-ALL-RULES marker (currently has no rules marker)

### install.sh changes
1. Phase 2: add staging of src/user/.agents/rules/* → staging/<tool>/rules/ for all tools (currently Phase 2 only stages skills and agents, not rules)
2. flatten_agents_md: add 4th argument staging_tool_dir; add handler for DYNAMIC-INCLUDE-ALL-RULES that globs staging_tool_dir/rules/*.md alphabetically and inlines all files with --- separators; no hardcoded list
3. Call site of flatten_agents_md: pass staging dir as 4th argument
4. Phase 6 plugin overlay: extend plugin_agents_dir loop to include 'rules' subdir alongside existing 'skills' and 'agents' (enables src/plugins/<name>/.agents/rules/ support for future and current plugin use)

### Staging resolution
DYNAMIC-INCLUDE-ALL-RULES resolves AFTER Phase 6 (all plugin overlays applied). At Phase 6.5 flatten time, staging/<tool>/rules/ already contains: general rules (from Phase 2) + tool-specific rules (from Phase 3/4, append-merged for collisions) + plugin rules (from Phase 6). Glob at flatten time = single authoritative source, no list.

## Out of Scope
- Changing rule file content (only renames for claude-sandbox/claude-to-codex-routing)
- Refactoring rule files to remove Claude-specific skill names from general rules (acceptable bleed per content-unchanged requirement)
- Adding new rules or changing rule semantics
- Changing INSTRUCTIONS.md.template or the DYNAMIC-INCLUDE (non-rules) mechanism

## Design Notes
- worktrees.md classified GENERAL even though path references .claude/worktrees/ — pooling all tools' worktrees into one location is a feature, not a conflict
- Alphabetical ordering for DYNAMIC-INCLUDE-ALL-RULES is deterministic and sufficient; rules are mutually independent (cross-references are bidirectional and survive any ordering)
- Plugin .agents/rules/ support added in Phase 6 loop: one-line diff (add 'rules' to for subdir in skills agents); zero current consumers in beads plugin but symmetric with plugin .agents/skills and .agents/agents
- install.sh --prune will clean up old-name files (git-commits.md, codex-routing.md) from ~/.claude/rules/ on next pruned install

title: [y9mm-E] Doc consistency sweep + Cluster F autofix verification
description: Cluster E (R5.6, R5.7, R5.8) + Cluster F verification from agents-config-y9mm. Cluster E — three one-line doc-only fixes that drifted as surrounding code evolved: (a) whats-next/SKILL.md L138 empty-state message says 'attention' but all-mode aggregates planning+brainstorm+implementation; (b) whats-next/skill_test.sh L52 T3 comment still references 'default' mode/token but test+skill now use 'all'; (c) whats-next/collect.py L148 container-detection docstring says 'any feature with non-closed children is a container' but implementation intentionally excludes hep-pause + merge-gate children. Cluster F — re-verify Copilot autofix commits fe4be52 and 2eab31c cover bd-finalize-container-gate.sh header summary and brainstorm-bead.formula.toml Step 0 doc block; if not fully covered, patch the gap. Must come after A-D so docstrings reflect final implementation state.

title: [y9mm-D] HEP transaction: strip readiness labels from paused container source
description: Cluster D from agents-config-y9mm (R5.1-R5.5). HEP transaction creates hep-pause child bead and reverts source to open, but leaves pre-existing readiness labels intact (implementation-ready, brainstormed, implementation-readied-session-*). Rule C invariant: containers MUST NOT carry readiness labels. Compounding: run-queue polls bd ready --label implementation-ready directly, bypassing whats-next structural filter — so a container paused via parent-child HEP with leftover readiness labels CAN be auto-claimed by run-queue. Fix: HEP transaction container branch must strip implementation-ready, implementation-readied-session-*, and brainstormed from source before reverting to open. Depends on y9mm-A (hep-pause label identifies which branch of HEP transaction is container path). Multi-surface: bd-finalize-container-gate.sh L125 area; implement-bead/SKILL.md section 0 container branch; beads.md HEP section container branch; start-bead/SKILL.md Route C HEP sites (x2); brainstorm-bead.formula.toml Step 0 doc block.
acceptance_criteria: HEP transaction container branch strips implementation-ready, all implementation-readied-session-* labels, and brainstormed from source before reverting source to open status. All five surfaces updated consistently: bd-finalize-container-gate.sh, implement-bead/SKILL.md, beads.md HEP section, start-bead/SKILL.md Route C (both sites), brainstorm-bead.formula.toml Step 0. Test: paused container source with readiness labels does not appear in bd ready --label implementation-ready output. Existing tests green.

title: [y9mm-C] implement-bead: container gate before pour + gate before formula-label honor
description: Cluster C from agents-config-y9mm (R3.11, R3.12). Two gaps: (1) implement-bead formula-selection fallback for feature sources pours implement-feature without active-child probe — subsumes already-closed agents-config-rxpe. (2) implement-bead formula-label-wins-first logic bypasses container gate when container carries accidental formula-* label. Fix: (1) add active-child probe before pour in direct-dispatch fallback; container features route to HEP (parent-child shape). (2) run container gate BEFORE formula-label lookup — a container with a formula-* label cannot bypass the gate. Depends on y9mm-A (active-child exclusion predicate must include hep-pause). Surfaces: implement-bead/SKILL.md formula-selection fallback (~L98-112); label-wins-first block.
acceptance_criteria: Feature source direct-dispatch path probes active-child count (excluding hep-pause + merge-gate children) before selecting implement-feature. Container features route to HEP parent-child shape. Container gate runs before formula-label lookup in label-wins-first block. Test: container with implementation-ready label does not get poured via formula-label bypass path. agents-config-rxpe acceptance criteria satisfied. Existing tests green.

title: [y9mm-B] start-bead Route D + resolve-human-bead Probe 6: detect parent-child HEP escalations
description: Cluster B from agents-config-y9mm (R3.2, R3.3, R3.4, R3.5, R3.6). Route D detects HEP pauses via human label on source OR human-labeled blocks dep. Container HEP shape has neither — so Route D misses the pending escalation; retrying start-bead creates duplicate escalations or proceeds with normal routing. resolve-human-bead Probe 6 (source-bead pivot) similarly misses human children. Fix: extend Route D to child-probe source for open hep-pause-labeled children. Extend Probe 6 identically. Depends on y9mm-A (hep-pause label must exist before detection can probe for it). Surfaces: start-bead/SKILL.md Route D; resolve-human-bead/SKILL.md Probe 6.
acceptance_criteria: start-bead Route D detects HEP pause when source has open hep-pause-labeled child (in addition to existing source-human-label and human-blocking-dep detection). resolve-human-bead Probe 6 discovers parent-child HEP escalations via hep-pause child probe. Test: Route D and Probe 6 correctly identify paused container source with HEP child. Existing tests green.

title: [y9mm-A] Active-child filter: discriminate HEP children via hep-pause label
description: Cluster A from agents-config-y9mm (R3.1, R3.7, R3.8). active_child_count (collect.py) and feature child-count probe (bd-finalize-container-gate.sh) exclude ALL human-labeled children. HEP creates human-labeled children for container sources — so a container paused via HEP gets count=0, classified as leaf, leaks into planning/implementation queues while still awaiting human input. Strategy (decided): stamp new hep-pause label on HEP children inside the HEP transaction container branch. Update both active-child filters to exclude hep-pause-labeled children (not all human-labeled children). Surfaces: collect.py active_child_count; bd-finalize-container-gate.sh child-count probe; HEP transaction container branch (label stamp). This bead sets the discriminator vocabulary that y9mm-B and y9mm-D consume — must merge before those beads.
acceptance_criteria:
active_child_count in collect.py excludes (human AND NOT hep-pause) children, not all human-labeled. is_impl_candidate L249 and is_brainstorm_candidate L232 human guards unchanged (vacuous under single-bead invariant; source beads never carry human).
bd-finalize-container-gate.sh CHILD_COUNT probe applies same (human AND NOT hep-pause) exclusion.
All stamp sites follow human→hep-pause→notes-append→source-revert→mol-burn order. Sites: bd-finalize-container-gate.sh x2, beads.md, start-bead/SKILL.md Route C x2, implement-bead/SKILL.md §0.
collect_test.sh T11: feature with one open human+hep-pause child (feature itself carries NO implementation-ready) → is_container() = True (feature appears in planning-ready, not hidden or in impl section).
collect_test.sh T12: feature carrying implementation-ready with only a human+hep-pause child → collect.py --mode implementation → feature NOT in implementation output. (implementation-ready label on parent is required to make the negative assertion meaningful.)
pr72_validate_c_test.sh new fixture: feature source with only a human+hep-pause child, no produced-bead-* labels → gate returns 'handled'; AND bd label list <escalation-child> includes hep-pause (not just human). Both assertions required — 'handled' alone would pass against current unmodified code.
Existing PR #72 epic-hygiene tests remain green.
hep-pause in beads-labels.md: Set by = HEP procedure (container branch only); Meaning = counted in active_child_count so container stays classified while awaiting human resolution, unlike bare human (formula-gate) children.
SRC_ACTIVE_CHILDREN probe comments in beads.md, start-bead/SKILL.md, and implement-bead/SKILL.md: intentionally left matching the unchanged probes (which still use merge-gate or human). This is a known inconsistency with collect.py's narrowed filter; it is safe at first-escalation time and is tracked in y9mm-B scope.
notes:
## Summary

Introduce a hep-pause discriminator label to distinguish HEP escalation children from formula-gate [Human verify] children. Update both active-child-count filters to count HEP children (keeping a HEP-paused container classified as a container), and stamp hep-pause on all new HEP escalation children created under container sources.

## Background / Motivation

PR #72 (epic-hygiene) introduced the parent-child HEP shape for container sources. When an agent escalates on a container bead it creates a human-labeled child (HEB) under the container. Both collect.py (active_child_count) and bd-finalize-container-gate.sh exclude ALL human-labeled children from active-child counts. This exclusion was correct for its original purpose: [Human verify] children (brainstorm finalize step 5b, human-labeled, formula-gate artifacts) should not make a parent look like a container.

The new HEP shape made human-labeled children mean two different things:
  1. Formula-gate artifact ([Human verify]) — should be EXCLUDED from count
  2. Live HEP escalation (HEB) — should be COUNTED (container is paused; must not leak into queues)

Because both look identical to the filter, a container paused via HEP gets count=0 → classified as leaf → surfaces in planning/implementation queues while still awaiting human input. Cluster A of y9mm (R3.1, R3.7, R3.8 from PR #72 round-3 Copilot review).

## Requirements

1. New label hep-pause: Every HEP transaction that creates a human escalation bead as a CHILD of a container source MUST stamp hep-pause on the new HEB in this exact order:
   a. bd label add ESC_ID human
   b. bd label add ESC_ID hep-pause   ← immediately after human
   c. bd update ESC_ID --append-notes ...
   d. bd update SOURCE_ID --status open
   e. bd mol burn / cleanup
   This ordering ensures the HEB is consistently labeled before any subsequent operation can fail under set -euo pipefail.

2. collect.py filter narrowing (active_child_count population loop, ~L361):
   Change from: if 'merge-gate' in labels or 'human' in labels: continue
   to: if 'merge-gate' in labels or ('human' in labels and 'hep-pause' not in labels): continue
   NOTE: is_impl_candidate's separate 'human' not in labels guard (line 249) and
   is_brainstorm_candidate's 'human' not in labels guard (line 232) are intentionally NOT changed
   — see Design Notes.

3. bd-finalize-container-gate.sh filter narrowing (feature CHILD_COUNT probe, ~L91-92): apply same narrowed exclusion: exclude merge-gate OR (human AND NOT hep-pause).

4. Stamp sites — all HEP container branches must follow the stamp ordering in Req 1:
   - bd-finalize-container-gate.sh L141 (ambiguous-Y HEP) and L167 (reclassification HEP)
   - src/plugins/beads/.claude/rules/beads.md HEP section container branch
   - src/plugins/beads/.agents/skills/start-bead/SKILL.md Route C: length-0 and length-2+ container branches
   - src/plugins/beads/.agents/skills/implement-bead/SKILL.md §0 container branch
   NOTE: The SRC_ACTIVE_CHILDREN probe inside each of these code blocks (the feature container-detection probe that runs BEFORE creating the HEB) is intentionally NOT updated in this bead. It still uses 'merge-gate or human' exclusion. See Design Notes for rationale.

5. Doc-comment updates (descriptions of the filter logic):
   - collect.py L207-212: update filter description to (human AND NOT hep-pause)
   - bd-finalize-container-gate.sh L86-90: same update
   NOTE: SRC_ACTIVE_CHILDREN probe comments in beads.md, start-bead/SKILL.md, and implement-bead/SKILL.md are intentionally NOT updated — the probes themselves are unchanged, so their comments remain accurate.

6. Add hep-pause to label reference table in src/plugins/beads/.claude/rules/beads-labels.md:
   Set by: HEP procedure (container branch only)
   Meaning: Identifies a human-escalation bead (HEB) created as a CHILD of a container source. Unlike bare human-labeled children (formula-gate artifacts), hep-pause children are counted in active_child_count so the container stays classified as a container while awaiting human resolution.

7. Tests:
   - collect_test.sh T11: feature bead with one open child labeled human+hep-pause → is_container() = True (bead appears in planning-ready, not hidden). Uses the existing bd shim pattern.
   - collect_test.sh T12: feature bead with only a human+hep-pause child, run collect.py --mode implementation → feature does NOT appear in implementation section output. End-to-end via bd shim.
   - pr72_validate_c_test.sh (new fixture): feature source with exactly one open child labeled human+hep-pause, no produced-bead-* labels → bd-finalize-container-gate.sh returns 'handled' (not 'not-container'). Observable via the script's stdout decision token.
   - Existing PR #72 epic-hygiene tests remain green.

## Out of Scope

- y9mm-B (start-bead Route D + resolve-human-bead Probe 6): depend on this bead, ship after
- y9mm-C, y9mm-D, y9mm-E: separate beads
- SRC_ACTIVE_CHILDREN probes inside HEP skill procedures: these probes run BEFORE any HEB is created (pre-stamp), so no hep-pause child exists when they execute for the first escalation. Second-escalation safety is argued in Design Notes; narrowing those probes is tracked in y9mm-B scope.
- Backfill of existing legacy HEP-paused containers (zero open HEP escalations currently)
- Removal of merge-gate/human filter (pqvc scope; pqvc spec says KEEP)

## Design Notes

- hep-pause lives on the HEB child, not the source. It discriminates what kind of child the HEB is.
- is_impl_candidate L249 and is_brainstorm_candidate L232 'human' guards: intentionally unchanged. Source beads never carry 'human' (single-bead invariant). HEB children should never surface as brainstorm-ready or impl-ready regardless of hep-pause — those guards are vacuous architectural invariants, not active filters for the hep-pause case.
- SRC_ACTIVE_CHILDREN probes in skill bodies: intentionally unchanged. At first-escalation time, no HEB child exists yet, so the probe is correct regardless of narrowing. At second-escalation time (container already has a live HEB child), the probe would misclassify the container as a non-container — but this requires Route D to miss the existing HEP pause (a separate gap fixed by y9mm-B). For epic sources, this would fail loudly on the blocks epic-wall; for feature sources, y9mm-B closes the window. The narrowing would cause NO change at first-escalation time and would only help at second-escalation time — a scenario that y9mm-B prevents from being reached.
- Stamp ordering safety: placing hep-pause immediately after human (step b in Req 1, before notes-append and mol-burn) ensures that under set -euo pipefail, if any later step fails, the HEB already carries both labels and is in the correct state for recovery.
- Forward-only: no backfill. Zero existing HEP-paused containers; transitional leak window acceptable.
- pqvc compatibility: pqvc restructures finalize to Y_container (epic) + Y_impl (feature). After pqvc, merge-gate and [Human verify] children attach under Y_container (CONTAINER_ALWAYS). y9mm.1 filter change remains valid for non-Y feature-type container sources. pqvc spec says KEEP the collect.py filter. Do NOT block y9mm.1 on pqvc.

## Open Questions

None.

comments.author: Scott Hamilton
comments.text: Brainstormed; produced agents-config-y9mm.6 on 2026-05-15

title: Round-4 hardening for PR #72 epic-hygiene: parent-child HEP discoverability + active-child filter + container gates at all entry points
description:
Filed per wait-for-pr-comments hard-cap rule for PR #72 round 3 (12 items) + round 5 (8 items) = 20 FIX-class items deferred as ESCALATE: 'exceeded re-review round cap'.

Background: PR #72 introduced the parent-child HEP shape for container sources (Q1 owner decision) and a narrowed feature-container definition (Q2 owner decision, narrow in-PR scope). Round-3 Copilot review identified follow-on work that the parent-child shape implies but is not yet wired up. Round-5 Copilot review added a third architectural axis: the HEP transaction does not strip pre-existing readiness labels from the paused container source.

CLUSTER A — Parent-child HEP child wrongly excluded from active-child filter (R3.1, R3.7, R3.8).
The active_child_count in collect.py and the feature child-count probe in bd-finalize-container-gate.sh both exclude children labeled 'human'. But the new HEP creates 'human'-labeled children for container sources. A container whose only open child is its own HEP escalation gets active_child_count=0 → treated as leaf → can leak into planning/implementation queues while still waiting on human input. Discriminator needed: differentiate HEP children from [Human verify] children. Options: add new label hep-pause on HEP children; match by title prefix; or invert the filter to include 'merge-gate' + '[verify]' title pattern only.

CLUSTER B — Parent-child HEP not discoverable from source by resolution tooling (R3.2, R3.3, R3.4, R3.5, R3.6).
start-bead Route D detects HEP pauses via human label on source OR human-labeled blocks dep. The new container HEP shape has neither — source has no human label, source has no blocks dep. So Route D and resolve-human-bead miss the pending escalation; retrying start-bead on a paused container creates duplicate escalations or proceeds with normal routing. Need to extend detection: child probe on source for open human-labeled children. resolve-human-bead Probe 6 (source-bead pivot) similarly needs to consider human children.

CLUSTER C — Container check not at all entry points (R3.11, R3.12).
implement-bead's formula-selection fallback for feature sources still pours implement-feature without active-child probe (R3.11 — dup of agents-config-rxpe; rolled up here). implement-bead's formula-label-wins-first logic bypasses the type-based container gate when a container carries an accidental formula-* label (R3.12 — formula gate must run BEFORE formula label honor).

CLUSTER D — HEP transaction does not strip pre-existing readiness labels (R5.1, R5.2, R5.3, R5.4, R5.5).
NEW in round-5. Comment IDs 3241926056 / 3241926092 / 3241926112 / 3241926138 / 3241926164. When a container source has accidentally accumulated readiness labels (implementation-ready, brainstormed, implementation-readied-session-*), the parent-child HEP transaction creates the child human bead and reverts the source to 'open' but leaves the readiness labels intact. The Rule C invariant says containers MUST NOT carry readiness labels, but enforcement is missing at the HEP boundary. Compounding factor: 'run-queue' bypasses the whats-next structural filter — it polls 'bd ready --label implementation-ready' directly — so a container paused via parent-child HEP with leftover readiness labels CAN be auto-claimed by run-queue. Fix: HEP transaction (helper script + skill docs + rule docs + formula doc) must strip implementation-ready, implementation-readied-session-*, and brainstormed from the container source before reverting to 'open'. Surfaces: bd-finalize-container-gate.sh L125 area; implement-bead/SKILL.md §0 container branch; beads.md HEP section container branch; start-bead/SKILL.md both Route C HEP sites (L160 first, L253 second); brainstorm-bead.formula.toml Step 0 doc block.

CLUSTER E — Doc consistency drift (R5.6, R5.7, R5.8).
NEW in round-5. Comment IDs 3241925816 / 3241925900 / 3241926016. Three doc/comment items that drifted as the surrounding code evolved: (a) whats-next/SKILL.md L138 'all' empty-state message says 'attention' but all-mode aggregates planning + brainstorm + implementation; (b) whats-next/skill_test.sh L52 T3 comment still references 'default' mode/token but the test and skill now use 'all'; (c) whats-next/collect.py L148 container-detection summary in module docstring says any feature with non-closed children is a container, but the implementation intentionally excludes merge-gate / human children. Doc-only edits; one-line each.

CLUSTER F (R3.9, R3.10) — bd-finalize-container-gate.sh header summary and brainstorm-bead.formula.toml Step 0 doc block. Status: PARTIALLY RESOLVED by Copilot Autofix commits fe4be52 and 2eab31c. Re-verify on the y9mm worktree whether the autofix coverage is complete; if so, mark this cluster done.

Round-1+round-2 leftovers resolved in PR #72: see commits eeae9ef / cd6ed95 / 4721efd.
Round-5 file-path citation fix resolved in PR #72: see commit fd121de.

Skipped (NOT in this bead): R5.9 / R5.10 PR description meta complaints (comment IDs 3241925941 / 3241925989). These are PR-description meta (not code); owner-editable via GitHub PR description.

This bead bundles ALL 20 round-3+round-5 ESCALATEs so they can be addressed coherently in a follow-up. Suggested split-by-thesis when implementing: Cluster A (filter) + Cluster D (label stripping) + Cluster B (discoverability) + Cluster C (entry-point gates) + Cluster E (doc consistency) as five separate PRs per the future-discipline-only direction.
acceptance_criteria:
active_child_count (collect.py) and feature child-count probe (bd-finalize-container-gate.sh) distinguish HEP children from [Human verify] children; only [Human verify] / merge-gate children are excluded from container detection.
start-bead Route D detects HEP pauses via open human-labeled child of source (in addition to existing source-human-label / human-blocking-dep detection).
resolve-human-bead Probe 6 (source-bead pivot) discovers parent-child HEP escalations.
implement-bead direct-dispatch path applies container gate before pour for feature sources (also resolves agents-config-rxpe; that bead can be closed as duplicate of this work).
implement-bead container gate runs BEFORE formula-label-wins-first lookup (a container with formula-* label cannot bypass the gate).
bd-finalize-container-gate.sh header summary and brainstorm-bead.formula.toml Step 0 prose accurately describe the parent-child HEP shape.
Tests: new fixture asserting a container with only an HEP child stays classified as container; new fixture asserting Route D / resolve-human-bead discover parent-child escalations; existing PR #72 tests remain green.

title: Restructure brainstorm-bead finalize Y to: Y_container (epic|feature) → [Impl] + [merge-gate]
description:
From PR #72 (agents-config-eos5) Copilot R5/R7/R10 architectural call (owner decision: fast-follow).

Current brainstorm-bead finalize step 4 creates Y as type=feature with [Impl] prefix; step 5b then attaches merge-gate / [Human verify] children directly under Y. This conflates the executable impl bead with a structural container — Y is the leaf you run, but it has formula children, which contradicts Rule B (feature with active children = container).

Restructure (owner direction from PR #72 Q2): Y creation should always produce:
- Y_container (type=epic or feature) — the structural container
  - Y_impl (type=task or feature, with [Impl] prefix) — carries implementation-ready / brainstormed / formula-* / session marker labels; what run-queue picks
  - [merge-gate] Y_merge (label: merge-gate) — gate child  - [verify] children (label: human) — verification follow-ups, if any

Y_container is naturally container by Rule B; gets filtered out of impl queues; gets close-walk treatment when all children close. Y_impl is the actual executable bead.

Downstream impacts:
- brainstorm-bead.formula.toml step 4 (currently creates single Y) — rewrite to create two beads
- step 5b (creates merge-gate / verify children under Y) — must attach under Y_impl or under Y_container depending on shape
- bd-finalize-create-impl-bead.sh — likely needs sibling helper that creates the 2-level shape
- whats-next/collect.py — confirm impl-candidate filter handles Y_impl correctly (should work without changes once Y_container has no impl-ready label)
- implement-bead SKILL.md — confirm direct-dispatch path still routes to Y_impl
- merge-and-cleanup formula — verify it closes the right bead and walks correctly
- Tests across the board — update fixtures to the new shape

Scope is significant (~3-5 days). File as P1 fast-follow per owner direction.

Blocking: agents-config-eos5 must merge first (gives the Rule B/C foundation this builds on).
acceptance_criteria:
brainstorm-bead finalize produces Y_container (type=epic, priority=X.priority) + Y_impl (formula-derived type) 2-level shape on happy path.
Y_impl carries full impl-ready label set (brainstormed, implementation-ready, implementation-readied-session-*, formula-*, ralf:* if any, produced-from-X dep).
Y_container carries none of: brainstormed, implementation-ready, implementation-readied-session-*, formula-*, ralf:*, produced-from-X.
merge-gate child attaches under Y_container as sibling of Y_impl.
Human verify children (step 5b) attach under Y_container.
X produced-bead-* label points to Y_container id. Verified: bd label list X --json | jq 'map(select(startswith("produced-bead-")))' shows Y_container id.
All dep edges (blocks, blocked-by, tracks, etc.) migrate from X to Y_impl. Y_container has no migrated deps.
start-bead: closed X preflight forwards to Y_container; routes to Y_impl (not Route C brainstorm). Verified: start-bead on closed X ends at Route A on Y_impl.
start-bead: Y_container with zero impl-ready children routes to Route C (brainstorm); multiple impl-ready children escalates HEP.
merge-and-cleanup jq bug fixed: merge-gate child detected and closed after cleanup. Verified: bd show merge-gate-id shows status=closed after merge-and-cleanup runs.
merge-and-cleanup resolves CONTAINER_ID from Y_impl parent; falls back to Y_impl id for legacy single-Y beads.
merge-and-cleanup closes Y_container and runs close-walk when all children closed.
whats-next impl-candidate filter picks Y_impl; Y_container not surfaced as impl candidate. Verified: collect.py is_container(Y_container)=True, is_impl_candidate(Y_container)=False.
Y_impl (feature type) with no children is NOT classified as container by collect.py. Verified: is_container(Y_impl)=False when Y_impl has no active non-gate non-human children.
run-queue picks Y_impl via bd ready --label implementation-ready.
Idempotency: all 4 resume states handled without duplicate bead creation.
All existing brainstorm-bead tests + new 2-level shape tests green.
Existing whats-next, implement-bead, merge-and-cleanup tests updated for new shape.
notes:
## Summary

Restructure brainstorm-bead finalize to produce a 2-level Y_container/Y_impl shape instead of a single Y bead. Y_container (type=epic) is the structural container; Y_impl (formula-derived type, [Impl] prefix) is the executable leaf that carries impl-ready labels and is picked by run-queue. Also fixes an existing merge-and-cleanup jq bug that leaves merge-gate children open after cleanup.

## Background / Motivation

Currently finalize step 4 creates a single Y bead and step 5b attaches merge-gate + [Human verify] children directly under Y. This makes Y simultaneously a Rule B container (feature with active children) and the executable impl leaf. collect.py excludes merge-gate/human children from container detection to paper over this. PR #72 (eos5) established the Rule B/C foundation; this is the fast-follow to resolve the structural ambiguity at source.

## Requirements

1. finalize creates Y_container (type=epic, priority=X.priority) as structural container first, marks it in_progress immediately, then creates Y_impl (formula-derived type) as child of Y_container with [Impl] prefix in title.
2. Y_impl carries: brainstormed, implementation-ready, implementation-readied-session-*, formula-*, ralf:* (if any), produced-from-X dep, copied X labels. Y_impl type = formula-derived (implement-feature -> feature, fix-bug -> bug, docs-only -> task).
3. Y_container carries NONE of: brainstormed, implementation-ready, implementation-readied-session-*, formula-*, ralf:*, produced-from-X.
4. merge-gate child (and [Human verify] children) attach under Y_container as siblings of Y_impl. Y_ID in step 5b = Y_container_id.
5. Step 5a: pre-existing merge-gate and human-labeled children of X migrate to Y_container.
6. Step 6 dep migration: ALL dep types migrate from X to Y_impl. Y_container carries no migrated deps.
7. Step 7: X gets produced-bead-<Y_container_id> label (canonical forward pointer).
8. Step 8 close-walk: Y_container.parent = X.parent; walk halts because Y_container is open.
9. bd-finalize-create-impl-bead.sh refactored: outputs two IDs (Y_container_id on line 1, Y_impl_id on line 2 or as KEY=VALUE). Formula step 4 extracts both: Y_CONTAINER_ID and Y_IMPL_ID. Step 5a/5b use Y_CONTAINER_ID for --parent. Step 6 uses Y_IMPL_ID as dep migration target. Step 7 stamps produced-bead-<Y_CONTAINER_ID>.
10. merge-and-cleanup pour contract: implement-bead pours with bead-id=Y_impl (unchanged from current behavior). At the TOP of the formula execution block (before source-bead-gate), the formula resolves: CONTAINER_ID=; [ -z CONTAINER_ID ] && CONTAINER_ID=BEAD_ID (legacy fallback). ALL child lookups in source-bead-gate and cleanup steps use CONTAINER_ID, not bead-id directly.
11. Fix merge-and-cleanup line 279 jq bug: '.labels | index("merge-gate")' -> 'index("merge-gate")' (bd label list --json returns flat array, not object).
12. start-bead updates: (a) routing table gains new row: open epic container with impl-ready children -> container-routing-to-Y_impl; (b) Step 3 forward-target conditional: when preflight resolves to a container (epic, no impl-ready label), apply container-routing algorithm before Routes A/B/C/D.
13. collect.py merge-gate/human child-filter: KEEP (may produce features from brainstorms in future).
14. I3 discovered-work during Y_impl implementation: in-scope discoveries use --parent Y_container.

## Script Interface (bd-finalize-create-impl-bead.sh)

Input: --source-bead-id, --type (Y_impl type), --priority, --title (Y_impl title, [Impl] prefix), --labels, --spec-file, --ac-file, --parent (X.parent, passed as Y_container's parent).

Output (stdout, two lines):
  Y_CONTAINER_ID=<id>
  Y_IMPL_ID=<id>

Formula extracts via: eval  or parse by key.

## Idempotency State Table

Detection priority (checked in order):

| State | Detection | Resume action |
|-------|-----------|---------------|
| 3: all done | produced-bead-<id> on X AND Y_container has Y_impl child | return Y_CONTAINER_ID + Y_IMPL_ID; skip to step 8 consistency check |
| 2: both created, X not stamped | produced-from-X found on Y_impl via bd label search; no produced-bead-* on X | stamp X with produced-bead-<Y_container_id>; continue from step 5 |
| 1: Y_container only | pending-split-<X_id> label found on a bead (Y_container); no Y_impl with produced-from-X | create Y_impl under that Y_container; remove pending-split-<X_id>; continue from step 5 |
| 0: neither | none of the above | stamp Y_container candidate with pending-split-<X_id> immediately; create Y_container; create Y_impl; remove pending-split-<X_id> |

State 1 detection: Y_container gets a temporary label 'pending-split-<X_id>' immediately after its bd create call, before Y_impl is created. Removed immediately after Y_impl is confirmed created.

Escalate HEP if: multiple produced-bead-* labels on X, or multiple beads with pending-split-<X_id>.

## start-bead Container-Routing Algorithm

When closed-bead preflight resolves to Y_container (open, epic type, no impl-ready):
1. Probe impl-ready children: bd list --parent Y_container --label implementation-ready --json | jq '[.[] | select(.status != "closed")]'
2. Exactly one result: treat that Y_impl as routed target for ALL subsequent route decisions (A/B/C/D). Route A session gate evaluates against Y_impl labels.
3. Zero results: Y_container has no impl bead yet; run Route C on Y_container directly.
4. Multiple results: escalate HEP.

start-bead SKILL.md routing table addition:
  open | is-container | has impl-ready child(ren) | -> container-routing (probe and route to Y_impl)

## merge-and-cleanup Child-Lookup Contract

bead-id pour variable = Y_impl (implement-bead pours it; unchanged).

Resolution block at formula TOP (before source-bead-gate step):
  CONTAINER_ID=
  [ -z "$CONTAINER_ID" ] && CONTAINER_ID="$BEAD_ID"   # legacy single-Y fallback

source-bead-gate steps 1, 2, 3: replace 'bd list --parent bead-id' with 'bd list --parent $CONTAINER_ID'.
cleanup steps 4, 5, 6: same replacement.
BEAD_ID (Y_impl) still used for: bd close bead-id (closing Y_impl itself is NOT done — Y_impl closes via I2 close-walk from CONTAINER_ID close).

Note: merge-and-cleanup closes CONTAINER_ID (Y_container) and runs close-walk from CONTAINER_ID. Y_impl closes as part of that walk when all its siblings are also closed.

## Y_container I1 Claim-Walk

finalize marks Y_container in_progress immediately after creation (before creating Y_impl). When implement-bead's I1 claim-walk runs against Y_impl, it walks: Y_impl -> Y_container (already in_progress, no-op) -> X.parent. No gap.

## Legacy Compatibility

Legacy single-Y beads (pre-f19p) have Y.parent = empty (X's parent or null). CONTAINER_ID resolution falls back to BEAD_ID (Y_impl = legacy Y). All source-bead-gate and cleanup logic then operates on legacy Y as before.

## Out of Scope

- Multiple Y_containers from one brainstorm run (future bead)
- closed-bead-preflight multi-label handling (future bead)
- Removing collect.py merge-gate/human filter

## Design Decisions

- Y_container type = epic; all deps migrate to Y_impl (avoids epic wall entirely; Y_container is dep-free structural container)
- Script outputs two IDs; formula tracks Y_CONTAINER_ID and Y_IMPL_ID explicitly through steps 4-8
- pending-split-<X_id> temporary label enables State 1 crash-recovery without an unreliable orphan scan
- bead-id for merge-and-cleanup = Y_impl (run-queue/implement-bead unchanged); CONTAINER_ID resolved internally by formula
- merge-gate jq bug fix and legacy CONTAINER_ID fallback both in scope (same touch area)

## Open Questions

None.

## Spec Clarifications (cycle-3 findings)

### State 2 resume path (Step 1 ORPHAN_COUNT==1)

When the formula's Step 1 idempotency probe finds ORPHAN_COUNT==1 via produced-from-X label on Y_impl, the resume path must derive CONTAINER_ID before proceeding to steps 5+:

  Y_IMPL_ID=<extracted orphan id>
  CONTAINER_ID=$(bd show "$Y_IMPL_ID" --json | jq -r '.[0].parent // empty')
  [ -z "$CONTAINER_ID" ] && CONTAINER_ID="$Y_IMPL_ID"   # legacy single-Y fallback

Then: Y_CONTAINER_ID=$CONTAINER_ID. Step 5a uses Y_CONTAINER_ID for --parent (migrating X's children to Y_container, not Y_impl).

### merge-and-cleanup CONTAINER_ID resolution (corrected pseudocode)

  CONTAINER_ID=$(bd show "$BEAD_ID" --json | jq -r '.[0].parent // empty')
  [ -z "$CONTAINER_ID" ] && CONTAINER_ID="$BEAD_ID"

(The prior pseudocode block was garbled by shell expansion during spec authoring. This is the correct form.)

### closed-bead-preflight.sh: no change required

closed-bead-preflight.sh reads produced-bead-* on X and returns decision=forward target=Y_container_id. It does not need to know Y_container is an epic. Step 3 container-routing in start-bead handles the rest. No file changes needed.

### pending-split-<X_id> label placement (State 0 and State 1)

State 0 sequence:
  1. bd create --type epic ... -> Y_container_id
  2. bd label add Y_container_id 'pending-split-<X_id>'  (crash recovery marker)
  3. bd create ... --parent Y_container_id ... -> Y_impl_id
  4. bd label remove Y_container_id 'pending-split-<X_id>'

The label is stamped on Y_container AFTER its bd create, not before. State 1 detection: probe for any bead with pending-split-<X_id> label -> that bead is Y_container.

### Bead title note

The bead title says 'epic|feature' — this reflects the open question at creation time. The spec is authoritative: Y_container type = epic.

title: spec-modularization skill: decompose large specs into smaller loosely-coupled sections
description:
Sibling to ralf-spec (agents-config-abn9.11). When a spec target exceeds a size threshold (initial guess: 200 lines), the spec is a candidate for decomposition into smaller, modular, loosely-coupled sections that can be brainstormed, written, and reviewed independently.

Scope hints (to be brainstormed):
- Heuristic for 'too large to review well' (line count, section count, cross-section coupling, semantic complexity)
- Decomposition pattern: identify natural seams, propose 2-N child specs, generate parent-as-overview + child specs with cross-refs
- Integration with brainstorm-bead formula: maybe a new step 'modularize-if-needed' before write-spec
- Integration with ralf-spec: when ralf-spec preflights detect oversize target, in interactive mode warn the user and suggest invoking this skill; in autonomous mode either auto-invoke or log + proceed depending on policy
- The skill itself authors the child specs (or proposes their boundaries for human approval)

Source: surfaced by Scott during abn9.11 brainstorming. Warning-only handling is in abn9.11's preflight requirement; this bead adds the actual modularization capability that the warning suggests.
notes: Folded in hecr0 (closed as dup 2026-06-27): also cover splitting large *PDLC* design specs into mechanically-stitchable modules so concept areas can be revised in isolation.

title: prgroom — foundation brainstorm closure (Sections 1, 2, 5, 8-skeleton)
description:
# prgroom — foundation brainstorm closure

Memorializes the brainstorming work performed in a prior session for the prgroom CLI foundation (Sections 1, 2, 5, and 8-skeleton of the design document). This bead represents the brainstorm-closure artifact; the implementation work is tracked by the [Impl] bead under the prgroom-IMPL epic.

## Source of truth

- Design document: `docs/plans/2026-05-12-prgroom-cli-design.md`
- Review: 4 rounds of crit, 41 comments addressed
- Source bead: agents-config-fca6 (epic, brainstorming umbrella)

## Sections covered by this closure (drafted/locked)

- Section 1 — Architecture overview
- Section 2 — WorkTracker interface + state schema
- Section 5 — Agent dispatch internals (Cluster + Fix contracts; EscalationSink; config; templates; logging)
- Section 8 — PR memory management (skeleton; sub-design carved out at agents-config-fca6.5, hooks in Contract B)
- Cross-cutting: precondition gating (3-tier model)

## Locked decisions (MVP)

- Language: Go (single static binary, github.com/cli/go-gh/v2, sub-100ms cold start)
- CLI framework: cobra
- Repo placement: same agents-config repo, new cmd/prgroom/ + internal/ tree, Go module rooted at repo root
- Agent boundary: CLI shells out to agent-CLIs synchronously; fresh agent context per shell-out
- Command shape: subcommand verbs (poll, cluster, fix, push, rereview, reply, resolve, resolve-escalated, wait, status, run, sweep)
- MVP scope: PR-grooming only (excludes create-PR, merge, cleanup, bead-lifecycle helpers)
- Migration path: incremental — Phase 1 absorbs wait-for-pr-comments; Phase 2 absorbs reply-and-resolve-pr-threads
- State persistence: WorkTracker interface; MVP default file adapter; memory adapter for tests; bd adapter deferred to v2
- Agent contracts: Contract A = cluster (local-first via ollama+Gemma 4 → claude haiku → codex-mini), Contract B = fix (sonnet[1m] orchestrator that decides disposition AND implements)
- Escalation routing: EscalationSink interface (stderr default; bd + file adapters available)
- Per-contract config: primary + fallback chains in TOML; token-usage logged to JSONL
- Test discipline: interfaces-first; fit-tests per module; coverage gate 80% line / 70% branch on changed code

## Not in this closure

Sections 3, 4, 6, 7, and full Section 8 remain still-sketched. Each is a separate brainstorm-candidate sub-design bead under fca6:
- fca6.1 — Section 3 (Lifecycle state machine)
- fca6.2 — Section 4 (Quiescence model)
- fca6.3 — Section 6 (Migration plan)
- fca6.4 — Section 7 (Build, distribution, test discipline)
- fca6.5 — Section 8 (PR memory management, full)

## Acceptance

This closure bead is closed once its [Impl] bead exists under prgroom-IMPL with the correct formula labels (`produced-from-agents-config-<this-id>`, `implementation-ready`, `implementation-readied-session-<sid>`).
acceptance_criteria: Closure bead created and closed; [Impl] bead exists with produced-from-* label linking back; brainstorm-formula contract restored.

Spawned [Impl] bead agents-config-abn9.8.1 under the prgroom-IMPL epic, with produced-from-agents-config-fca6.8 + implementation-ready + implementation-readied-session-a82c6b20. title: prgroom — PR-grooming CLI (replace wait-for-pr-comments + reply-and-resolve-pr-threads)
description:
# prgroom — PR-grooming CLI

Replace the `wait-for-pr-comments` and `reply-and-resolve-pr-threads` skills with a Python CLI (`prgroom`) that takes over phase orchestration, reducing agentic-token cost by an order of magnitude.

## Source of truth

Design document: `docs/plans/2026-05-12-prgroom-cli-design.md` (reviewed via 4 rounds of crit; 41 comments addressed).

## Locked decisions (MVP)

- **Language:** Python 3.11+ — a `uv`-managed package that shells out to `gh`; reuses the repo's existing Python toolchain (`ruff`, `mypy --strict`, `pytest` + coverage, `pip-audit`) and mirrors the sibling `pdlc` package's `Protocol`/`StrEnum` state-model shape (~100–300ms console-script cold start)
- **CLI framework:** `typer` (type-hint-driven subcommands; pairs with `mypy --strict`)
- **Repo placement:** same `agents-config` repo, new `packages/prgroom/` package — a fourth sibling to `installer`, `pdlc`, and `holding-place`; `uv`-managed, standard `src/prgroom/` layout
- **Agent boundary:** CLI shells out to agent-CLIs (`claude -p` / `codex exec`) synchronously; fresh agent context per shell-out
- **Command shape:** subcommand verbs (poll, cluster, fix, push, rereview, reply, resolve, resolve-escalated, wait, status, run, sweep)
- **MVP scope:** PR-grooming only (excludes create-PR, merge, cleanup, bead-lifecycle helpers)
- **Migration path:** incremental — Phase 1 absorbs `wait-for-pr-comments`; Phase 2 absorbs `reply-and-resolve-pr-threads`
- **State persistence:** `WorkTracker` interface; MVP default `file` adapter (`$XDG_STATE_HOME/prgroom/<owner>-<repo>-<n>.json`); `memory` adapter for tests; `bd` adapter deferred to v2
- **Agent contracts (Section 5):** Contract A = `cluster` (cheap grouping; local-first via ollama+Gemma 4 → claude haiku → codex-mini), Contract B = `fix` (opus[1m] orchestrator that decides disposition AND implements)
- **Escalation routing:** `EscalationSink` interface (stderr default; bd + file adapters available)
- **Per-contract config:** primary + fallback chains in TOML; token-usage logged to JSONL for baseline (no analysis yet)
- **Test discipline:** interfaces-first; fit-tests per module; coverage gate 80% line / 70% branch on changed code

## Sections drafted vs sketched

**Drafted (in design doc):**
- Section 1 — Architecture overview
- Section 2 — `WorkTracker` interface + state schema
- Section 5 — Agent dispatch internals (Cluster + Fix contracts; EscalationSink; config; templates; logging)
- Section 8 — PR memory management (skeleton; sub-design carved out, hooks in Contract B)
- Cross-cutting: precondition gating (3-tier model)

**Still sketched (need brainstorm before implementation):**
- Section 3 — Lifecycle state machine (phase transitions, run verb's loop, error-code registry, failure handling)
- Section 4 — Quiescence model (probability function, thresholds, wait verb semantics, human-review signal, hard cap, auto-merge eligibility check)
- Section 6 — Migration plan (Phase 1 + Phase 2 detail, rollback strategy, coexistence)
- Section 7 — Build, distribution, test discipline (build pipeline, install path, versioning; test pyramid partially fleshed)

Each still-sketched section is a child sub-design bead.

## Supersedes

- `agents-config-d73c` — original *Optimize wait-for-pr-comments and reply-and-resolve-pr-threads skills* task

## Downstream / affected

- `agents-config-gmxo` (Redesign merge-gate bead) may depend on this MVP to define its boundary cleanly; user noted this dep direction in brainstorm
- `agents-config-td39` (Risk-tiered auto-merge policy) is further downstream and depends on gmxo

## Out of scope (deferred to v2+)

- Create-PR, merge, worktree cleanup (stay in `finishing-a-development-branch` / `merge-and-cleanup`)
- bd adapter for `WorkTracker`
- Parallel fix subagents
- `detect-pr-push.sh` PostToolUse hook rework
- Brainstorm/implement-bead formula changes
- Executable-bead primitive
- gmxo's structural changes
- Auto-detection of in-flight PR migration (none; in-flight PRs finish on the legacy skill)
- Cross-PR memory layer
- Token-usage analysis/aggregation (we log; we don't analyze yet)
acceptance_criteria: Sub-design beads for Sections 3, 4, 6, 7, 8 all closed. Toolchain assessment chore closed. CLI foundation (scaffold + tracker + preconditions + Cluster + Fix contracts) implemented and tested. wait-for-pr-comments skill thinned to a CLI invocation. reply-and-resolve-pr-threads skill thinned to a CLI invocation. d73c closed as superseded. Build passes. Tests pass. Coverage gate (80% line / 70% branch) holds on changed code.
close_reason: prgroom design epic complete: full C4 HLD set + sequence/state/data/ER views authored and folded with §8 PR-memory; design plan ratified and reconciled (§3.2). Implementation tracked separately under the abn9.8 tree.

title: Memory/context compaction governance for overnight runs
description: Vision-Gap-3 from the 2026-05-09 roadmap. Beads persist work; formulas persist process. title: [Impl] Audit skills, agents, commands, rules for best practices & coherence
description:
## Spec

Canonical spec: docs/specs/2026-05-09-acmh-comprehensive-audit-design.md (commit d9fc28d)

## Summary

Comprehensive multi-dimensional audit of all agent-facing content (skills, agents,
commands, rules, formulas, instruction templates, helper scripts/code) for
quality, coherence, signal-to-noise, right-tool-for-the-job placement, and
alignment with the project's 85/5/10 vision. Produces a tiered remediation
plan: Tier 1 mechanical fixes applied inline in this PR; Tier 2 design-required
work deferred to per-category follow-up beads; Tier 3 bd-sequence concerns
flagged and routed to existing/new follow-up beads.

## Architecture (3 phases)

- Phase 1 — Parallel category audits: 7 Opus 1M xhigh effort subagents
  (one per category: skills, agents, commands, rules, formulas, instruction
  templates, helper scripts/code) → docs/audits/phase1/<category>.md
- Phase 2 — Cross-cutting adversarial review: 6 Codex GPT-5.5 subagents,
  one per cross-cutting use case → docs/audits/phase2/<use-case-slug>.md
- Phase 3 — Aggregation: 1 Sonnet 1M subagent reconciles by category, by
  file, decision log, REMEDIATION_PLAN.md. USER REVIEW GATE before Tier 1
  application.

## Key Design Decisions

- Vision/Mission injected at dispatch from live AGENTS.md (no static drift).
- Every finding carries a mandatory Vision-advancement field explaining how
  the recommendation advances the 85/5/10 ratio (regression of cited issue
  is implicit).
- Right-tool-for-the-job inventory: skill ↔ rule ↔ command ↔ agent ↔ shell
  script/helper code.
- Pre-research primer set committed to docs/primers/ (FORMULAS, SKILLS,
  AGENTS, COMMANDS, RULES) so subagents share a knowledge baseline.
- xacz absorbed; bf6 and 2gzy linked but separate.
- wmjy chosen direction (script-extraction) covers dep-migration; remaining
  Tier 3 (children-check, label-copy-filter) goes to a new follow-up bead.

## Out of Scope

- Implementation of Tier 2 design changes (deferred to per-category beads).
- Long-bead spec externalization (agents-config-bf6).
- Helper script extraction itself (agents-config-2gzy / wmjy receive
  findings as input only).
- Refactoring or restructuring the install pipeline.

## Acceptance Criteria

See bead acceptance field. Full numbered list in spec §12.

## Execution Sequence

12 steps; spec §13. USER REVIEW GATE between Phase 3 aggregation and Tier 1
application is mandatory.

## Resource Budget (estimate)

- Phase 1: 7 × Opus 1M xhigh — heavy
- Phase 2: 6 × Codex GPT-5.5 — moderate
- Phase 3: 1 × Sonnet 1M — moderate
- Tier 1 inline fixes + completion gate + delivery — standard

See spec §15 for tradeoffs.

## Open Questions

None blocking. Optional refinements may surface during Phase 1 dispatch
prompt finalization (e.g. exact category slugs); these are in-flight
implementation details, not architectural.
acceptance_criteria:
1. All 7 Phase 1 category audit files exist at docs/audits/phase1/<category>.md
2. All 6 Phase 2 use-case adversarial files exist at docs/audits/phase2/<use-case-slug>.md
3. Phase 3 outputs complete: by-category/<category>.md per category, by-file/<file-slug>.md per source file with ≥1 finding, decisions.md, REMEDIATION_PLAN.md
4. Every finding has all schema fields populated, including non-empty Vision-advancement
5. User has reviewed REMEDIATION_PLAN.md and approved the Tier 1 fix list
6. All approved Tier 1 fixes applied to source files
7. scripts/install.sh --dry-run passes after Tier 1 fixes
8. One follow-up bead filed per category with ≥1 Tier 2 finding
9. Tier 3 bd-sequences follow-up bead filed (children-check + label-copy-filter)
10. Comments posted on agents-config-2gzy (script-interface findings) and agents-config-wmjy (dep-migration extraction confirmation)
11. AGENTS.md updated if audit surfaces findings against it
12. Completion gate passed: quality-reviewer → simplify → verify-checklist (all pass with evidence)
13. PR created via finishing-a-development-branch skill; summary links to REMEDIATION_PLAN.md
notes: Step 6 USER REVIEW GATE reached at 2026-05-10T07:39:25Z. REMEDIATION_PLAN.md ready at docs/audits/phase3/REMEDIATION_PLAN.md (worktree HEAD f623236). AUDIT_INPUT_SHA=af9c1bfc342bf7578ad491cc63dc95b07618c851. Authorize Tier 1 by adding 'APPROVED af9c1bfc342bf7578ad491cc63dc95b07618c851' as the first non-blank line of REMEDIATION_PLAN.md, OR posting a bd comment matching that pattern. Verifiable check #4 (tier-C share <= 30%) FAILED at 46.1% — flagged for user judgment, not auto-demoted.
close_reason: Audit complete and shipped via PR #61 (merge commit 9ed3a0b). 32 Tier-1 fixes applied across 7 categories. 12 Tier-2 follow-up beads filed (agents-config-acmh.1 through acmh.12) with discovered-from links. Adjacency notifications posted on agents-config-2gzy and agents-config-wmjy. All 4 Copilot review comments addressed and resolved.
