title: Reconcile persona 'Doubt yourself, ask when unsure' vs orchestration 'Decide, don't defer'
description:
## Problem

The persona file (`AGENT-PERSONA.md.template`) instructs agents: *"Doubt yourself until you've checked facts. Ask when unsure."* The orchestration rules (`INSTRUCTIONS.md.template` > `<orchestration>`) instruct: *"Decide, don't defer."*

Root cause: the persona stanza conflates two distinct concepts — (a) epistemic humility (verify before acting, compatible with orchestration) and (b) escalation default (ask vs. decide, conflicts with orchestration). Only the last line conflicts.

Secondary finding: the same over-broad escalation default appears in `agents/tech-lead.md` (duplicates a matrix) and `skills/ralf-implement/implementer-prompt.md:34` ("If ANYTHING is unclear, ask").

## Solution

### Edit 1a — Insert `<decision-matrix>` block into `INSTRUCTIONS.md.template`

Use the **exact tag name `<decision-matrix>`** (do not rename or alias). Place between `</constraints>` and `<workflow>` using this diff (the blank line on line 27 becomes the insertion anchor):

```diff
 </constraints>
 
+<decision-matrix>
+<!-- Canonical escalation hierarchy. Reference by concept, not by file path —
+     this content is flattened into all assembled instruction files at install
+     time via DYNAMIC-INCLUDE. Do not add per-tool copies. -->
+
+When facing a question or unknown, classify the situation, then act:
+
+| Situation                                                              | Action   | Quadrant                       |
+|------------------------------------------------------------------------|----------|--------------------------------|
+| Verifiable fact                                                        | Verify   | verify-facts                   |
+| In-scope decision; OR architectural but clear best option exists       | Decide   | decide-in-scope                |
+| Architectural w/ genuinely balanced trade-offs, or option-space needs human context | Escalate | escalate-architectural |
+| Conflicting directions L0–L3 precedence cannot resolve                 | Escalate | escalate-conflicting           |
+
+- **verify-facts** — the answer exists in the codebase, docs, git history, or
+  prior conversation. Read/grep/test. Do NOT ask.
+- **decide-in-scope** — implementation detail, naming, local refactor,
+  reasonable trade-off within stated requirements, OR an architectural question
+  where you can identify a clearly better option by standard engineering
+  judgment. Choose it. Do NOT ask for validation.
+- **escalate-architectural** — architectural or requirements implications where
+  a clearly better option CANNOT be identified: trade-offs are genuinely
+  balanced with no engineering-judgment winner, or the options themselves
+  depend on human context unavailable to the agent. If you can rank options,
+  you are in decide-in-scope — do NOT escalate for approval.
+- **escalate-conflicting** — instructions, constraints, or stakeholders pull
+  in incompatible directions that L0–L3 law precedence cannot resolve. Escalate,
+  surfacing the conflict with your read of each side.
+
+**Risk-asymmetric defaults are NOT escalations.** Specific rules that say
+"when in doubt, do the safe thing" (merge authorization, `git restore`
+coordination, merge-guard's `pass 0`) override the matrix on their narrow
+domain and are not affected by this change.
+</decision-matrix>
+
 <workflow>
```

**Cross-tool propagation is automatic.** The matrix reaches `.codex/AGENTS.md.template`, `.gemini/GEMINI.md.template`, and `.opencode/AGENTS.md.template` via their `<!-- DYNAMIC-INCLUDE: src/user/.agents/INSTRUCTIONS.md.template -->` markers (verified: `.opencode/AGENTS.md.template` line 5 carries this marker). Do NOT add per-tool edits.

### Edit 1b — Collapse `<orchestration>` "Decide, don't defer" bullet in `INSTRUCTIONS.md.template`

The exact current single-line content to replace (verify verbatim match before applying):

```diff
-- **Decide, don't defer**: Use your best judgment on implementation details and non-architectural questions. Only escalate to the user when: (a) the answer has significant architectural or requirements implications not already specified, or (b) there are conflicting directions that require human resolution. Decide everything else yourself. Do not ask questions you can answer from the codebase, existing instructions, or standard engineering judgment.
+- **Decide, don't defer**: apply the canonical decision matrix.
+  Quadrants `verify-facts` and `decide-in-scope` resolve without the user.
```

**Other `<orchestration>` bullets** (Subagents, Self-improvement, Verify before done, Tracer bullets, Isolate work, Elegance check) are untouched — they encode other behaviors, not escalation defaults.

### Edit 2 — `AGENT-PERSONA.md.template` surgical line replacement

```diff
 **Self-awareness**:

 - You assume too much
 - Doubt yourself until you've checked facts
-- Ask when unsure
+- Apply the canonical decision matrix to every ask-vs-decide moment
```

**Intentional preservation note:** Lines "You assume too much" and "Doubt yourself until you've checked facts" are deliberately kept. They encode epistemic humility — a reminder to verify facts before acting (aligns with the `verify-facts` quadrant). They are NOT escalation directives and do not conflict with orchestration. Only "Ask when unsure" is a de-facto escalation default that conflicts; only it is removed.

### Edit 3 — `agents/tech-lead.md` replace escalation criteria section

Replace the entire "Decision Criteria for Human Escalation" section (lines 78–86, heading through last bullet). Current six bullets and disposition:

```
- Subagent explicitly requests human input             ← KEEP (orchestrator-specific)
- Multiple agents report conflicting recommendations   ← KEEP (orchestrator-specific)
- Critical architectural decisions (long-term)         ← DROP — covered by escalate-architectural
- Resource constraints or technical limitations        ← DROP — covered by decide-in-scope or escalate-architectural
- Ambiguous requirements needing clarification         ← DROP — covered by escalate-architectural
- Team appears stuck after multiple attempts           ← KEEP (orchestrator-specific)
```

Replacement text:

```markdown
### Decision Criteria for Human Escalation

Apply the canonical decision matrix. Role-specific additions for the
tech-lead orchestrator:

- A dispatched subagent explicitly requests human input
- Multiple subagents report conflicting recommendations
- Team appears stuck after multiple attempts (loop detection)

The architectural-decisions, resource-constraints, and
ambiguous-requirements bullets dropped here are already covered by the
`escalate-architectural` quadrant of the canonical decision matrix.
```

### Edit 4 — `skills/ralf-implement/implementer-prompt.md` line 34

```diff
-If ANYTHING is unclear, ask before proceeding. Don't guess.
+If unclear, apply the canonical decision matrix. Don't guess.
```

## Scope

**In scope (4 files, 5 edits):**
- `src/user/.agents/INSTRUCTIONS.md.template` — two edits: (1a) insert `<decision-matrix>` block, (1b) collapse "Decide, don't defer" bullet
- `src/user/.agents/AGENT-PERSONA.md.template` — replace line 18 (Edit 2)
- `src/user/.agents/agents/tech-lead.md` — replace lines 78–86 (Edit 3)
- `src/user/.agents/skills/ralf-implement/implementer-prompt.md` — replace line 34 (Edit 4)

**Out of scope:**
- FIX/SKIP/ESCALATE comment classifications in `wait-for-pr-comments` and `reply-and-resolve-pr-threads` — different concept (PR triage, not agent escalation)
- `bugfix/SKILL.md:117` "escalate to complementary skill" — skill-to-skill handoff, not user escalation
- Risk-asymmetric defaults already in place (merge auth, `git restore`, merge-guard) — explicitly preserved by matrix caveat
- Per-tool subtrees (`.codex`, `.gemini`, `.opencode`) — propagation is automatic via DYNAMIC-INCLUDE; no direct edits needed

**Reference style constraint:** All new references to the matrix in changed files must use conceptual phrasing ("canonical decision matrix" and/or quadrant names: `verify-facts`, `decide-in-scope`, `escalate-architectural`, `escalate-conflicting`). Do NOT use file-path citations. Pre-existing path references unrelated to escalation (e.g., `.claude/agents/*` in tech-lead.md line 41) may remain unchanged.

## Smoke test (manual)

After all edits, read the four changed files and answer each question, citing the quadrant name (or "role-specific addition" for Q3):

| # | Question | Expected answer |
|---|---|---|
| Q1 | "Should I ask the user before deciding a variable naming convention?" | No → `decide-in-scope` |
| Q2 | "Two equally valid retry strategies exist for this component — one adds complexity, the other a latency penalty. No clear winner." | Ask with options + recommendation → `escalate-architectural` (balanced trade-offs, no engineering-judgment winner) |
| Q3 | "I am tech-lead and a dispatched subagent says it needs human input." | Escalate → tech-lead role-specific addition (not a generic matrix quadrant) |
| Q4 | "A completion-gate rule and a persona instruction pull in incompatible directions." | Ask → `escalate-conflicting` |
| Q5 | "I know the retry behavior should be exponential backoff — it's standard for this class of service." | Decide → `decide-in-scope` (can identify clearly better option) |

A fresh agent must cite the correct quadrant name (or "role-specific") for each. "I'll just ask to be safe" on Q1, Q2, or Q5 is a failing answer.
acceptance_criteria:
1. <decision-matrix> block (exact tag name) inserted between </constraints> and <workflow> in INSTRUCTIONS.md.template with all four named quadrants and risk-asymmetric-defaults caveat.
2. AGENT-PERSONA.md.template line 18 replaced; 'Ask when unsure' removed; lines 16-17 preserved with intentional note documented.
3. INSTRUCTIONS.md.template <orchestration> 'Decide, don't defer' bullet collapsed to two-line matrix reference (single-line old string replaced verbatim).
4. agents/tech-lead.md Decision Criteria section: three orchestrator-specific bullets retained; three generic bullets dropped with rationale.
5. skills/ralf-implement/implementer-prompt.md line 34 replaced with matrix reference.
6. All new matrix references use conceptual phrasing (quadrant names or 'canonical decision matrix') — no file-path citations.
7. Cross-tool propagation requires no per-tool edits (verified: DYNAMIC-INCLUDE markers present in all four per-tool templates).
8. Smoke test passes: fresh agent cites correct quadrant for all five questions (Q1 decide-in-scope, Q2 escalate-architectural, Q3 tech-lead role-specific, Q4 escalate-conflicting, Q5 decide-in-scope).

comments.author: Scott Hamilton
comments.text: Brainstormed; produced agents-config-abn9.5 on 2026-05-10

title: Add brainstorm-readiness gate (verify-brainstorm skill) to enforce spec quality before implementation
description:
**Goal**: The missing twin of `verify-checklist`. The completion gate audits implementations; this gate audits *brainstorm artifacts* before they earn the `implementation-ready` label, supporting the project's 85/5/10 human-time vision (see AGENTS.md > Vision).

**Problem**: Today, the bounce-back from agent to human ("no, not ready — your spec is missing X") relies on the brainstorming skill being well-followed, with no mechanical checklist that the AI must satisfy before stamping `implementation-ready`. Since brainstorming is the largest time slice in the target ratio, this gate is the highest-leverage point in the entire system.

**Approach sketch**: New skill (e.g., `verify-brainstorm`) under src/user/.agents/skills/, paralleling the `verify-checklist` pattern. Wired into start-bead / brainstorm-bead routing so that `implementation-ready` is gated on the audit passing.

**Out of scope**: rewriting the brainstorming skill itself; building any UI; replacing the start-bead flow.
acceptance_criteria:
- New skill under src/user/.agents/skills/ (name TBD: verify-brainstorm or similar)
- Skill audits a bead spec for at minimum: testable acceptance criteria; identified risks; explicit out-of-scope; pinned data shapes/contracts; ambiguity tags
- Skill blocks (or refuses to stamp) the implementation-ready label until criteria met
- Skill documents what to do when criteria cannot be met (escalate vs defer)
- Wired into the existing brainstorm/start-bead flow
- AGENTS.md and README.md reference the gate as part of the 85/5/10 enabler set

title: Extract bd orchestration sequences into idempotent shell helpers
description:
The brainstorm-bead finalize step embeds complex bd CLI sequences directly in LLM-executed formula prose. This creates two classes of bugs: (1) the LLM can issue side-effectful calls (bd create, dep migration loops) multiple times via parallel tool batching, producing duplicate beads; (2) the same boilerplate sequences (claim walk, close walk) are copy-pasted across 3-5 formula files with no single source of truth.

Extract the deterministic bd orchestration sequences into focused shell helper scripts that (a) encode idempotency internally, (b) present a single named command to the LLM (one invocation = one side effect), and (c) live in .beads/scripts/ so install.sh deploys them with chmod +x.

## Scope — Tier 1 (fixes the duplicate-bead root cause)
- bd-finalize-create-impl-bead.sh: creates the implementation bead during brainstorm finalize; built-in intra-step orphan guard prevents parallel-call duplicates
- Update brainstorm-bead.formula.toml finalize Step 4 to call this script

## Scope — Tier 2 (DRY violation fix)
- bd-claim-walk.sh: I1 claim walk (bead + ancestor epics → in_progress); used in brainstorm-bead, implement-feature, fix-bug
- bd-close-walk.sh: I2 close walk (close bead + cascade-close empty ancestors); used in brainstorm-bead, merge-and-cleanup
- Update all formula files that contain the inline equivalent sequences

## Out of scope
- Tier 3 scripts (children-check, dep-migration, label-copy-filter) — tracked separately
- docs-only formula (no claim/close walk patterns present)
acceptance_criteria:
bd-finalize-create-impl-bead.sh: --help prints purpose + contract; idempotency guard prevents duplicate bead creation when called twice with same args; formula Step 4 updated to single script invocation.
bd-claim-walk.sh: --help prints purpose + contract; marks bead + all ancestor epics in_progress; brainstorm-bead Phase 0 and implement-feature and fix-bug preflight Step 7 updated to single script invocation.
bd-close-walk.sh: --help prints purpose + contract; closes target bead with --reason then cascades up closing empty ancestors; brainstorm-bead Step 8 and merge-and-cleanup Step 6 updated to single script invocation.
All scripts live in src/plugins/beads/.beads/scripts/ (flat; install.sh deploys via existing glob).
No existing formula behaviour is changed — only the mechanism (inline bash vs named script).
close_reason: Merged PR #54 — bd orchestration shell helpers (bd-claim-walk, bd-close-walk, bd-finalize-create-impl-bead)

title: merge-agents-config-7bk.19.9
description: Merge gate child for agents-config-7bk.19.9. Closed only by merge-and-cleanup after merge + branch/worktree cleanup.

title: [Impl] Cleanup: delete bead-implementor.md, update arch spec catalog, update verify-artifacts smoke
description:
## Summary

Tail-end housekeeping for epic 7bk.19. Deletes the retired `bead-implementor` agent, refreshes `docs/specs/bead-pipeline-architecture.md` (7 occurrences across 6 sections) to reflect the three replacement agents (`tdd-red-team`, `tdd-green-team`, `bug-diagnoser`), annotates `docs/specs/worker-report-v1.md` with a historical-reference notice (the only spec doc that actually references `bead-implementor`), hardens `scripts/smoke/verify-artifacts.sh` with three real wiring checks (live three-agent existence, an orchestrator-contract unit test that sources a new `scripts/lib/worker-report.sh` helper against good and malformed worker-report-v1 fixtures, and an env-gated sandbox tracer-bullet), and rewrites the parent epic description in-place to fix the stale `worker-reports/` path reference.

## Background / Motivation

7bk.19 split the monolithic `bead-implementor` into three role-focused agents and migrated the audit contract to `worker-report-v1` with canonical write path `.beads/worker-audit/`. 7bk.19.3 (orchestrator rewrite) is closed. 7bk.19.4 (ralf-* skill updates) is closed, having produced 7bk.19.7 (Update ralf-* skills and formulas to consume worker-report-v1 and dispatch new workers), which is currently open. This bead retires the dead agent, brings the architecture spec into full alignment, ensures `verify-artifacts.sh` actually exercises the new wiring (rejecting the previously-proposed fixture-presence relaxation — fixture files prove nothing about behavior), and corrects path-naming staleness in the parent epic description.

## Requirements

### 1. Delete the retired agent

Delete `src/plugins/beads/.agents/agents/bead-implementor.md`. No callers should remain; the final invariant grep (Requirement 6) confirms zero references.

### 2. Update `docs/specs/bead-pipeline-architecture.md`

There are exactly 7 occurrences of `bead-implementor` across 6 sections. Replace all of them — use the section-level completion check (Requirement 6) as the authoritative done signal; do not trust line numbers (they shift as 7bk.19.7 lands).

Sections to update, by heading:

- **§ red-tests** (lines approx 107–138): Replace `bead-implementor` with `tdd-red-team` in the stage dispatch prose and the "Subagents dispatched" line. Document the explicit stage argument flow: formula step → `implement-bead` skill (with `stage=red-tests`) → `tdd-red-team` agent.
- **§ green-loop** (lines approx 139–174): Replace `bead-implementor` with `tdd-green-team`. Document stage argument flow: formula step → `implement-bead` skill (with `stage=green-loop`) → `tdd-green-team` agent.
- **§ docs-only formula** (lines approx 328–337): Replace `bead-implementor` with whichever worker handles the `apply-edits` stage for docs-only changes; if ambiguous, use `tdd-green-team` (the general implementation worker) and add a note.
- **§ 6 Agent catalog** (table, lines approx 703–715): Replace the single `bead-implementor` row with three rows (skill lists from actual agent frontmatter):
  - `tdd-red-team` | model: `opus` | effort: `high` | tools: `[Read, Edit, Write, Grep, Glob, Bash]` | skills: `superpowers:test-driven-development`, `superpowers:writing-unit-tests`, `superpowers:testing-anti-patterns`, `superpowers:using-git-worktrees` | role: TDD test-writing in red-tests stage
  - `tdd-green-team` | model: `opus` | effort: `high` (per flat-high dispatch policy; frontmatter does not set effort explicitly) | tools: `[Read, Edit, Write, Grep, Glob, Bash]` | skills: `superpowers:test-driven-development`, `superpowers:verification-before-completion`, `superpowers:testing-anti-patterns`, `superpowers:using-git-worktrees` | role: Iterative implementation in green-loop; apply-edits in docs-only
  - `bug-diagnoser` | model: `opus` | effort: `high` | tools: `[Read, Edit, Write, Grep, Glob, Bash]` | skills: `superpowers:systematic-debugging`, `superpowers:root-cause-tracing`, `superpowers:using-git-worktrees` | role: Root-cause diagnosis and fix in diagnose stage (bug-class only)
- **§ 7 Skill catalog / RALF-IT row** (lines approx 716–726): Update the policy that formerly read "bead-implementor runs at effort:high for iter 1 only; effort:medium for iters 2–5" to the new flat-high policy: all three workers dispatch at `effort: high` for every iteration.
- **§ 11 Bead corpus → architecture map** (lines approx 804+): Update the cross-reference table row for `bead-implementor` agent definition to reference the three new agents and their defining beads (7bk.19.1 / 7bk.19.2).

Also add a brief note or subsection clarifying the per-dispatch / iteration-loop boundary: the orchestrator skill (`implement-bead`) is responsible for a single dispatch (one worker call); the formula step owns the RALF-IT iteration loop.

### 3. Annotate `docs/specs/worker-report-v1.md` as containing a historical reference

Only `docs/specs/worker-report-v1.md` contains a `bead-implementor` reference (line 48 in Background). The other three 7bk.19.1 spec docs (`tdd-red-report-v1.md`, `tdd-green-report-v1.md`, `bug-diagnoser-report-v1.md`) contain no such references and need no annotation.

Add a short note near the top of `docs/specs/worker-report-v1.md` (or directly above the Background section). Suggested text (final wording at implementer's discretion, but must convey both points):

> **Historical reference**: This document's Background section mentions the `bead-implementor` agent to explain what the new worker-report-v1 contract replaces. The `bead-implementor` agent was retired in `agents-config-7bk.19.5`. The mention is historical context only — the live worker agents are `tdd-red-team`, `tdd-green-team`, and `bug-diagnoser`.

This annotation is paired with the `--exclude` flag in the invariant grep (Requirement 6) — exclusion is documented, not silent.

### 4. Update `scripts/smoke/verify-artifacts.sh`

All three checks are required. The previously-proposed static-only / fixture-file-presence relaxation is REJECTED — fixture files prove nothing about wiring.

**a. Three-agent existence check.** Replace the single `bead-implementor` existence check with three checks — `tdd-red-team`, `tdd-green-team`, `bug-diagnoser` — at their canonical paths under `src/plugins/beads/.agents/agents/`.

**b. Orchestrator-contract unit test.** This requirement has two parts:

*Part 1 — extract a testable shell helper.* Create `scripts/lib/worker-report.sh` as a new file in this bead's scope. It must export two shell functions:

- `parse_worker_report <yaml_file>`: reads the YAML file and outputs the following to stdout as `key=value` lines:
  - `status` — parsed from the YAML body (required field per worker-report-v1.md §1.1; values: `complete`, `needs_human`, `failed`)
  - `agent` — derived from the YAML filename (e.g. `tdd-red-team.yaml` → `agent=tdd-red-team`; `tdd-green-team-iter2.yaml` → `agent=tdd-green-team-iter2`)
  - `step_bead_id` — derived from the parent directory name of the YAML file path (the path format is `<repo-root>/.beads/worker-audit/<step-bead-id>/<agent-name>[-iter<N>].yaml`)
  Returns non-zero when `status` is absent from the YAML body (the orchestrator-minimum check per worker-report-v1.md §1.2).

- `stamp_audit_label <step_bead_id> <yaml_file>`: derives `<agent-name>[-iter<N>]` from the YAML filename (strip `.yaml`), then calls `bd label add <step_bead_id> worker-audit-<agent-name>[-iter<N>]` per the canonical format in worker-report-v1.md §3. Examples: file `tdd-red-team.yaml` → label `worker-audit-tdd-red-team`; file `tdd-green-team-iter2.yaml` → label `worker-audit-tdd-green-team-iter2`. Returns non-zero on failure.

*Part 2 — shell function in `verify-artifacts.sh`.* A function that sources `scripts/lib/worker-report.sh` and exercises it against two YAML fixtures at `scripts/smoke/fixtures/worker-report-v1/`:

- `good.yaml` — minimum valid worker-report-v1 content:
  ```yaml
  status: complete
  evidence: {}
  escalations: []
  discovered_work: []
  commits: []
  ```
  Assert: `parse_worker_report` returns 0; output contains `status=complete`; `stamp_audit_label` succeeds against a synthetic ephemeral step-bead id (create label, verify it exists, remove it as cleanup).

- `malformed.yaml` — missing the `status` field specifically (the orchestrator-minimum whose absence triggers `status: failed` synthesis per worker-report-v1.md §1.2):
  ```yaml
  evidence: {}
  escalations: []
  ```
  Assert: `parse_worker_report` returns non-zero; a synthetic `status: failed` outcome is generated (the test constructs this path, it does not invoke the full orchestrator); `stamp_audit_label` is still called and succeeds (per the audit contract — the label is stamped even on failure).

The shell function must source `scripts/lib/worker-report.sh` — it must not reimplement the parse/stamp logic inline. The AC verifies the live helper code path, not a copy.

**c. Sandbox tracer-bullet** (per 7bk.19.2 risk). Gated by `command -v claude`:

- When the Claude CLI IS available:
  1. Create a temporary git worktree at `.claude/worktrees/tracer-<timestamp>/` using `git worktree add`. Set the cleanup trap AFTER this step succeeds (or guard cleanup commands with `|| true` to handle pre-creation failure safely).
  2. Spawn a `claude -p` subagent inside that worktree with a minimal prompt: `"Write a file at <absolute-path-to-repo-root>/.beads/worker-audit/tracer-<timestamp>/test-agent.yaml with content: 'tracer-bullet-ok\n'"`.
  3. Success signal: the file exists at the absolute canonical path after the subagent exits.
  4. Cleanup: `rm -rf .beads/worker-audit/tracer-<timestamp>/ && git worktree remove .claude/worktrees/tracer-<timestamp>/ --force` — runs on both success and failure.
  5. If the write fails, print: `FAIL: sandbox tracer-bullet — subagent could not write outside worktree at <path>. The .beads/worker-audit/ path convention from 7bk.19.1 is broken; do not ship the epic until this is resolved.` and exit non-zero.

- When the CLI is absent: print `SKIP: claude CLI not on PATH; sandbox tracer-bullet not exercised in this run.` and return success (no failure).

### 5. Rewrite the parent epic description in-place

Use `bd update agents-config-7bk.19 --description "..."` to correct the stale `worker-reports/<step-bead-id>/<agent-name>` path reference in the Q1 text. The canonical path is `.beads/worker-audit/<step-bead-id>/<agent-name>.yaml` per `worker-report-v1.md` §2.

Note: this bead's own description now consistently uses the canonical path and needs no further correction.

### 6. Final invariant grep (run from repo root)

```bash
grep -rn 'bead-implementor' \
  --include='*.md' --include='*.toml' --include='*.sh' \
  --exclude='worker-report-v1.md' \
  . \
  | grep -v '.claude/worktrees/' \
  | grep -v '.git/' \
  | grep -v '.beads/issues.jsonl'
```

MUST return zero results. The single `--exclude='worker-report-v1.md'` flag is paired with the in-doc historical-reference annotation from Requirement 3.

## Out of Scope

- Annotating or modifying `tdd-red-report-v1.md`, `tdd-green-report-v1.md`, or `bug-diagnoser-report-v1.md` — none contain `bead-implementor` references.
- Any changes to ralf-* skills or formulas — those belong to 7bk.19.7 (successor to closed 7bk.19.4).
- Any orchestrator behavior changes — those belong to 7bk.19.3, which is closed.
- Changes to worker agent definitions — those belong to 7bk.19.1 and 7bk.19.2.
- Adjusting the `worker-report-v1` YAML schema itself; `scripts/lib/worker-report.sh` must parse whatever schema 7bk.19.1 / 7bk.19.3 produced.
- Aligning `tdd-green-team` agent frontmatter to add `effort: high` — the dispatch policy already enforces flat-high; frontmatter alignment is a separate cosmetic fix (file a follow-up if desired).

## Acceptance Criteria

- `src/plugins/beads/.agents/agents/bead-implementor.md` no longer exists.
- `docs/specs/bead-pipeline-architecture.md` no longer contains the string `bead-implementor` (zero exceptions; the Requirement 6 grep is the authoritative check).
- `docs/specs/worker-report-v1.md` contains a near-top historical-reference annotation per Requirement 3.
- `scripts/lib/worker-report.sh` exists and exports `parse_worker_report` and `stamp_audit_label` shell functions per Requirement 4b Part 1.
- `scripts/smoke/fixtures/worker-report-v1/good.yaml` exists with the minimum valid content specified in Requirement 4b Part 2.
- `scripts/smoke/fixtures/worker-report-v1/malformed.yaml` exists missing the `status` field per Requirement 4b Part 2.
- `scripts/smoke/verify-artifacts.sh` checks the three new agents (`tdd-red-team`, `tdd-green-team`, `bug-diagnoser`) at their canonical paths.
- `scripts/smoke/verify-artifacts.sh` contains an orchestrator-contract unit test function that sources `scripts/lib/worker-report.sh` and exercises `parse_worker_report` + `stamp_audit_label` on both fixtures with the assertions specified in Requirement 4b Part 2.
- `scripts/smoke/verify-artifacts.sh` contains a sandbox tracer-bullet per Requirement 4c: gated by `command -v claude`; when present it confirms a `claude -p` subagent inside a worktree can write to `.beads/worker-audit/<test-id>/test-agent.yaml`; when absent it prints a clear SKIP and exits 0.
- `agents-config-7bk.19` epic description has been rewritten in-place and references `.beads/worker-audit/` (not `worker-reports/`).
- The repo-wide invariant grep in Requirement 6 (run from repo root) returns zero matches.
- `scripts/smoke/verify-artifacts.sh` passes when run explicitly from repo root.
- Build passes. Typecheck passes. Tests pass.

## Design Notes

- **Narrow the annotation and exclude to `worker-report-v1.md` only.** A fresh grep confirms that only `worker-report-v1.md` (line 48) contains a `bead-implementor` reference; the other three 7bk.19.1 spec docs are clean.
- **Drop line numbers; use section-anchored selectors.** `bead-pipeline-architecture.md` has 7 `bead-implementor` occurrences across 6 sections. Line numbers drift as 7bk.19.7 lands; the Requirement 6 grep is the authoritative completion check.
- **Extract `scripts/lib/worker-report.sh` as the testable artifact.** 7bk.19.3's deliverable is a SKILL.md (LLM-executed prose), not a shell-callable helper — nothing to `source` directly. Extracting parse + stamp into a shell library gives a real artifact the unit test can exercise, and is advisory input for any future wiring into the orchestrator.
- **`agent` and `step_bead_id` come from the file path, not the YAML body.** `worker-report-v1.md` encodes both in the path (`<step-bead-id>/<agent-name>[-iter<N>].yaml`); neither is a top-level YAML field. `parse_worker_report` derives them from the path; implementers should not grep the YAML for an `agent:` key.
- **Canonical audit label format: `worker-audit-<agent-name>[-iter<N>]`.** Per `worker-report-v1.md` §3. Examples: `worker-audit-tdd-red-team`, `worker-audit-tdd-green-team-iter2`. No `audited:` prefix, no `:status` suffix.
- **`good.yaml` status: `complete`; `malformed.yaml` missing `status`.** The orchestrator minimum per `worker-report-v1.md` §1.2 is `status` — its absence triggers synthesis of a `status: failed` report. The malformed fixture exercises that specific branch.
- **Agent catalog skill lists from actual frontmatter.** `tdd-red-team`: `test-driven-development`, `writing-unit-tests`, `testing-anti-patterns`, `using-git-worktrees`. `tdd-green-team`: `test-driven-development`, `verification-before-completion`, `testing-anti-patterns`, `using-git-worktrees`. `bug-diagnoser`: `systematic-debugging`, `root-cause-tracing`, `using-git-worktrees`. (All `superpowers:` prefixed.)
- **tdd-green-team frontmatter does not set `effort:`.** The catalog table documents the dispatch effort (high, per flat-high policy). Aligning the frontmatter is out of scope here.
- **Trap scope for tracer-bullet cleanup.** Set the trap AFTER `git worktree add` succeeds, or guard cleanup commands with `|| true`, to avoid a noisy false-FAIL if worktree creation is unavailable.
- **Env-gate the tracer-bullet.** `claude -p` requires Claude Code install + auth; hard-failing CI blocks contributors who don't run it locally.
- **`--exclude` pattern is filename-only.** `grep --exclude='worker-report-v1.md'` matches on filename alone — correct given exactly one such file exists in this repo.
- **Path-naming correction.** `.beads/worker-audit/` is canonical per `worker-report-v1.md` §2. The parent epic `7bk.19` still uses the stale `worker-reports/` path in its Q1 text; Requirement 5 fixes it in-place.

## Open Questions

None. All three items from the 2026-05-05 fresh-eyes review are resolved (see Design notes). ralf-review cycle 1 findings were addressed (narrowed annotation, section-anchored selectors, shell helper, tracer-bullet contract, sibling status). ralf-review cycle 2 findings were addressed (corrected audit label format, specified fixture content, corrected agent catalog skill lists from actual frontmatter, clarified `agent`/`step_bead_id` path-derivation, tightened trap scope note).

title: [Impl] Cleanup: delete bead-implementor.md, update arch spec catalog, update verify-artifacts smoke
description:
## Summary

Tail-end housekeeping for epic 7bk.19. Deletes the retired `bead-implementor` agent, refreshes `docs/specs/bead-pipeline-architecture.md` (7 occurrences across 6 sections) to reflect the three replacement agents (`tdd-red-team`, `tdd-green-team`, `bug-diagnoser`), annotates `docs/specs/worker-report-v1.md` with a historical-reference notice (the only spec doc that actually references `bead-implementor`), hardens `scripts/smoke/verify-artifacts.sh` with three real wiring checks (live three-agent existence, an orchestrator-contract unit test that sources a new `scripts/lib/worker-report.sh` helper against good and malformed worker-report-v1 fixtures, and an env-gated sandbox tracer-bullet), and rewrites the parent epic description in-place to fix the stale `worker-reports/` path reference.

## Background / Motivation

7bk.19 split the monolithic `bead-implementor` into three role-focused agents and migrated the audit contract to `worker-report-v1` with canonical write path `.beads/worker-audit/`. 7bk.19.3 (orchestrator rewrite) is closed. 7bk.19.4 (ralf-* skill updates) is closed, having produced 7bk.19.7 (Update ralf-* skills and formulas to consume worker-report-v1 and dispatch new workers), which is currently open. This bead retires the dead agent, brings the architecture spec into full alignment, ensures `verify-artifacts.sh` actually exercises the new wiring (rejecting the previously-proposed fixture-presence relaxation — fixture files prove nothing about behavior), and corrects path-naming staleness in the parent epic description.

## Requirements

### 1. Delete the retired agent

Delete `src/plugins/beads/.agents/agents/bead-implementor.md`. No callers should remain; the final invariant grep (Requirement 6) confirms zero references.

### 2. Update `docs/specs/bead-pipeline-architecture.md`

There are exactly 7 occurrences of `bead-implementor` across 6 sections. Replace all of them — use the section-level completion check (Requirement 6) as the authoritative done signal; do not trust line numbers (they shift as 7bk.19.7 lands).

Sections to update, by heading:

- **§ red-tests** (lines approx 107–138): Replace `bead-implementor` with `tdd-red-team` in the stage dispatch prose and the "Subagents dispatched" line. Document the explicit stage argument flow: formula step → `implement-bead` skill (with `stage=red-tests`) → `tdd-red-team` agent.
- **§ green-loop** (lines approx 139–174): Replace `bead-implementor` with `tdd-green-team`. Document stage argument flow: formula step → `implement-bead` skill (with `stage=green-loop`) → `tdd-green-team` agent.
- **§ docs-only formula** (lines approx 328–337): Replace `bead-implementor` with whichever worker handles the `apply-edits` stage for docs-only changes; if ambiguous, use `tdd-green-team` (the general implementation worker) and add a note.
- **§ 6 Agent catalog** (table, lines approx 703–715): Replace the single `bead-implementor` row with three rows (skill lists from actual agent frontmatter):
  - `tdd-red-team` | model: `opus` | effort: `high` | tools: `[Read, Edit, Write, Grep, Glob, Bash]` | skills: `superpowers:test-driven-development`, `superpowers:writing-unit-tests`, `superpowers:testing-anti-patterns`, `superpowers:using-git-worktrees` | role: TDD test-writing in red-tests stage
  - `tdd-green-team` | model: `opus` | effort: `high` (per flat-high dispatch policy; frontmatter does not set effort explicitly) | tools: `[Read, Edit, Write, Grep, Glob, Bash]` | skills: `superpowers:test-driven-development`, `superpowers:verification-before-completion`, `superpowers:testing-anti-patterns`, `superpowers:using-git-worktrees` | role: Iterative implementation in green-loop; apply-edits in docs-only
  - `bug-diagnoser` | model: `opus` | effort: `high` | tools: `[Read, Edit, Write, Grep, Glob, Bash]` | skills: `superpowers:systematic-debugging`, `superpowers:root-cause-tracing`, `superpowers:using-git-worktrees` | role: Root-cause diagnosis and fix in diagnose stage (bug-class only)
- **§ 7 Skill catalog / RALF-IT row** (lines approx 716–726): Update the policy that formerly read "bead-implementor runs at effort:high for iter 1 only; effort:medium for iters 2–5" to the new flat-high policy: all three workers dispatch at `effort: high` for every iteration.
- **§ 11 Bead corpus → architecture map** (lines approx 804+): Update the cross-reference table row for `bead-implementor` agent definition to reference the three new agents and their defining beads (7bk.19.1 / 7bk.19.2).

Also add a brief note or subsection clarifying the per-dispatch / iteration-loop boundary: the orchestrator skill (`implement-bead`) is responsible for a single dispatch (one worker call); the formula step owns the RALF-IT iteration loop.

### 3. Annotate `docs/specs/worker-report-v1.md` as containing a historical reference

Only `docs/specs/worker-report-v1.md` contains a `bead-implementor` reference (line 48 in Background). The other three 7bk.19.1 spec docs (`tdd-red-report-v1.md`, `tdd-green-report-v1.md`, `bug-diagnoser-report-v1.md`) contain no such references and need no annotation.

Add a short note near the top of `docs/specs/worker-report-v1.md` (or directly above the Background section). Suggested text (final wording at implementer's discretion, but must convey both points):

> **Historical reference**: This document's Background section mentions the `bead-implementor` agent to explain what the new worker-report-v1 contract replaces. The `bead-implementor` agent was retired in `agents-config-7bk.19.5`. The mention is historical context only — the live worker agents are `tdd-red-team`, `tdd-green-team`, and `bug-diagnoser`.

This annotation is paired with the `--exclude` flag in the invariant grep (Requirement 6) — exclusion is documented, not silent.

### 4. Update `scripts/smoke/verify-artifacts.sh`

All three checks are required. The previously-proposed static-only / fixture-file-presence relaxation is REJECTED — fixture files prove nothing about wiring.

**a. Three-agent existence check.** Replace the single `bead-implementor` existence check with three checks — `tdd-red-team`, `tdd-green-team`, `bug-diagnoser` — at their canonical paths under `src/plugins/beads/.agents/agents/`.

**b. Orchestrator-contract unit test.** This requirement has two parts:

*Part 1 — extract a testable shell helper.* Create `scripts/lib/worker-report.sh` as a new file in this bead's scope. It must export two shell functions:

- `parse_worker_report <yaml_file>`: reads the YAML file and outputs the following to stdout as `key=value` lines:
  - `status` — parsed from the YAML body (required field per worker-report-v1.md §1.1; values: `complete`, `needs_human`, `failed`)
  - `agent` — derived from the YAML filename (e.g. `tdd-red-team.yaml` → `agent=tdd-red-team`; `tdd-green-team-iter2.yaml` → `agent=tdd-green-team-iter2`)
  - `step_bead_id` — derived from the parent directory name of the YAML file path (the path format is `<repo-root>/.beads/worker-audit/<step-bead-id>/<agent-name>[-iter<N>].yaml`)
  Returns non-zero when `status` is absent from the YAML body (the orchestrator-minimum check per worker-report-v1.md §1.2).

- `stamp_audit_label <step_bead_id> <yaml_file>`: derives `<agent-name>[-iter<N>]` from the YAML filename (strip `.yaml`), then calls `bd label add <step_bead_id> worker-audit-<agent-name>[-iter<N>]` per the canonical format in worker-report-v1.md §3. Examples: file `tdd-red-team.yaml` → label `worker-audit-tdd-red-team`; file `tdd-green-team-iter2.yaml` → label `worker-audit-tdd-green-team-iter2`. Returns non-zero on failure.

*Part 2 — shell function in `verify-artifacts.sh`.* A function that sources `scripts/lib/worker-report.sh` and exercises it against two YAML fixtures at `scripts/smoke/fixtures/worker-report-v1/`:

- `good.yaml` — minimum valid worker-report-v1 content:
  ```yaml
  status: complete
  evidence: {}
  escalations: []
  discovered_work: []
  commits: []
  ```
  Assert: `parse_worker_report` returns 0; output contains `status=complete`; `stamp_audit_label` succeeds against a synthetic ephemeral step-bead id (create label, verify it exists, remove it as cleanup).

- `malformed.yaml` — missing the `status` field specifically (the orchestrator-minimum whose absence triggers `status: failed` synthesis per worker-report-v1.md §1.2):
  ```yaml
  evidence: {}
  escalations: []
  ```
  Assert: `parse_worker_report` returns non-zero; a synthetic `status: failed` outcome is generated (the test constructs this path, it does not invoke the full orchestrator); `stamp_audit_label` is still called and succeeds (per the audit contract — the label is stamped even on failure).

The shell function must source `scripts/lib/worker-report.sh` — it must not reimplement the parse/stamp logic inline. The AC verifies the live helper code path, not a copy.

**c. Sandbox tracer-bullet** (per 7bk.19.2 risk). Gated by `command -v claude`:

- When the Claude CLI IS available:
  1. Create a temporary git worktree at `.claude/worktrees/tracer-<timestamp>/` using `git worktree add`. Set the cleanup trap AFTER this step succeeds (or guard cleanup commands with `|| true` to handle pre-creation failure safely).
  2. Spawn a `claude -p` subagent inside that worktree with a minimal prompt: `"Write a file at <absolute-path-to-repo-root>/.beads/worker-audit/tracer-<timestamp>/test-agent.yaml with content: 'tracer-bullet-ok\n'"`.
  3. Success signal: the file exists at the absolute canonical path after the subagent exits.
  4. Cleanup: `rm -rf .beads/worker-audit/tracer-<timestamp>/ && git worktree remove .claude/worktrees/tracer-<timestamp>/ --force` — runs on both success and failure.
  5. If the write fails, print: `FAIL: sandbox tracer-bullet — subagent could not write outside worktree at <path>. The .beads/worker-audit/ path convention from 7bk.19.1 is broken; do not ship the epic until this is resolved.` and exit non-zero.

- When the CLI is absent: print `SKIP: claude CLI not on PATH; sandbox tracer-bullet not exercised in this run.` and return success (no failure).

### 5. Rewrite the parent epic description in-place

Use `bd update agents-config-7bk.19 --description "..."` to correct the stale `worker-reports/<step-bead-id>/<agent-name>` path reference in the Q1 text. The canonical path is `.beads/worker-audit/<step-bead-id>/<agent-name>.yaml` per `worker-report-v1.md` §2.

Note: this bead's own description now consistently uses the canonical path and needs no further correction.

### 6. Final invariant grep (run from repo root)

```bash
grep -rn 'bead-implementor' \
  --include='*.md' --include='*.toml' --include='*.sh' \
  --exclude='worker-report-v1.md' \
  . \
  | grep -v '.claude/worktrees/' \
  | grep -v '.git/' \
  | grep -v '.beads/issues.jsonl'
```

MUST return zero results. The single `--exclude='worker-report-v1.md'` flag is paired with the in-doc historical-reference annotation from Requirement 3.

## Out of Scope

- Annotating or modifying `tdd-red-report-v1.md`, `tdd-green-report-v1.md`, or `bug-diagnoser-report-v1.md` — none contain `bead-implementor` references.
- Any changes to ralf-* skills or formulas — those belong to 7bk.19.7 (successor to closed 7bk.19.4).
- Any orchestrator behavior changes — those belong to 7bk.19.3, which is closed.
- Changes to worker agent definitions — those belong to 7bk.19.1 and 7bk.19.2.
- Adjusting the `worker-report-v1` YAML schema itself; `scripts/lib/worker-report.sh` must parse whatever schema 7bk.19.1 / 7bk.19.3 produced.
- Aligning `tdd-green-team` agent frontmatter to add `effort: high` — the dispatch policy already enforces flat-high; frontmatter alignment is a separate cosmetic fix (file a follow-up if desired).

## Acceptance Criteria

- `src/plugins/beads/.agents/agents/bead-implementor.md` no longer exists.
- `docs/specs/bead-pipeline-architecture.md` no longer contains the string `bead-implementor` (zero exceptions; the Requirement 6 grep is the authoritative check).
- `docs/specs/worker-report-v1.md` contains a near-top historical-reference annotation per Requirement 3.
- `scripts/lib/worker-report.sh` exists and exports `parse_worker_report` and `stamp_audit_label` shell functions per Requirement 4b Part 1.
- `scripts/smoke/fixtures/worker-report-v1/good.yaml` exists with the minimum valid content specified in Requirement 4b Part 2.
- `scripts/smoke/fixtures/worker-report-v1/malformed.yaml` exists missing the `status` field per Requirement 4b Part 2.
- `scripts/smoke/verify-artifacts.sh` checks the three new agents (`tdd-red-team`, `tdd-green-team`, `bug-diagnoser`) at their canonical paths.
- `scripts/smoke/verify-artifacts.sh` contains an orchestrator-contract unit test function that sources `scripts/lib/worker-report.sh` and exercises `parse_worker_report` + `stamp_audit_label` on both fixtures with the assertions specified in Requirement 4b Part 2.
- `scripts/smoke/verify-artifacts.sh` contains a sandbox tracer-bullet per Requirement 4c: gated by `command -v claude`; when present it confirms a `claude -p` subagent inside a worktree can write to `.beads/worker-audit/<test-id>/test-agent.yaml`; when absent it prints a clear SKIP and exits 0.
- `agents-config-7bk.19` epic description has been rewritten in-place and references `.beads/worker-audit/` (not `worker-reports/`).
- The repo-wide invariant grep in Requirement 6 (run from repo root) returns zero matches.
- `scripts/smoke/verify-artifacts.sh` passes when run explicitly from repo root.
- Build passes. Typecheck passes. Tests pass.

## Design Notes

- **Narrow the annotation and exclude to `worker-report-v1.md` only.** A fresh grep confirms that only `worker-report-v1.md` (line 48) contains a `bead-implementor` reference; the other three 7bk.19.1 spec docs are clean.
- **Drop line numbers; use section-anchored selectors.** `bead-pipeline-architecture.md` has 7 `bead-implementor` occurrences across 6 sections. Line numbers drift as 7bk.19.7 lands; the Requirement 6 grep is the authoritative completion check.
- **Extract `scripts/lib/worker-report.sh` as the testable artifact.** 7bk.19.3's deliverable is a SKILL.md (LLM-executed prose), not a shell-callable helper — nothing to `source` directly. Extracting parse + stamp into a shell library gives a real artifact the unit test can exercise, and is advisory input for any future wiring into the orchestrator.
- **`agent` and `step_bead_id` come from the file path, not the YAML body.** `worker-report-v1.md` encodes both in the path (`<step-bead-id>/<agent-name>[-iter<N>].yaml`); neither is a top-level YAML field. `parse_worker_report` derives them from the path; implementers should not grep the YAML for an `agent:` key.
- **Canonical audit label format: `worker-audit-<agent-name>[-iter<N>]`.** Per `worker-report-v1.md` §3. Examples: `worker-audit-tdd-red-team`, `worker-audit-tdd-green-team-iter2`. No `audited:` prefix, no `:status` suffix.
- **`good.yaml` status: `complete`; `malformed.yaml` missing `status`.** The orchestrator minimum per `worker-report-v1.md` §1.2 is `status` — its absence triggers synthesis of a `status: failed` report. The malformed fixture exercises that specific branch.
- **Agent catalog skill lists from actual frontmatter.** `tdd-red-team`: `test-driven-development`, `writing-unit-tests`, `testing-anti-patterns`, `using-git-worktrees`. `tdd-green-team`: `test-driven-development`, `verification-before-completion`, `testing-anti-patterns`, `using-git-worktrees`. `bug-diagnoser`: `systematic-debugging`, `root-cause-tracing`, `using-git-worktrees`. (All `superpowers:` prefixed.)
- **tdd-green-team frontmatter does not set `effort:`.** The catalog table documents the dispatch effort (high, per flat-high policy). Aligning the frontmatter is out of scope here.
- **Trap scope for tracer-bullet cleanup.** Set the trap AFTER `git worktree add` succeeds, or guard cleanup commands with `|| true`, to avoid a noisy false-FAIL if worktree creation is unavailable.
- **Env-gate the tracer-bullet.** `claude -p` requires Claude Code install + auth; hard-failing CI blocks contributors who don't run it locally.
- **`--exclude` pattern is filename-only.** `grep --exclude='worker-report-v1.md'` matches on filename alone — correct given exactly one such file exists in this repo.
- **Path-naming correction.** `.beads/worker-audit/` is canonical per `worker-report-v1.md` §2. The parent epic `7bk.19` still uses the stale `worker-reports/` path in its Q1 text; Requirement 5 fixes it in-place.

## Open Questions

None. All three items from the 2026-05-05 fresh-eyes review are resolved (see Design notes). ralf-review cycle 1 findings were addressed (narrowed annotation, section-anchored selectors, shell helper, tracer-bullet contract, sibling status). ralf-review cycle 2 findings were addressed (corrected audit label format, specified fixture content, corrected agent catalog skill lists from actual frontmatter, clarified `agent`/`step_bead_id` path-derivation, tightened trap scope note).

title: [merge-gate] impl-update-ralf-skills-and-fo
description: Merge gate child for agents-config-7bk.19.7. Closed only by merge-and-cleanup after merge + branch/worktree cleanup.

title: Human-flag lifecycle: HEP escalation, safe resume, audit-trail closure
description:
Groups the full lifecycle of human escalation in the bead pipeline under the **Human-Escalation Pattern (HEP)**: pause via blocking dep on a `human`-labeled escalation bead (defined in arch doc §5.6 and `beads.md` rule), safely resume after intervention via the `resolve-human-bead` skill, and enforce audit-trail closure to prevent silent state-honesty violations.

## Children

- **7bk.13** — HEP rollout: `resolve-human-bead` skill + slash command, plus the cross-skill updates (start-bead Route D, implement-bead escalation procedure rewrite, run-queue cleanup) that adopt HEP across the pipeline.
- **ffxh** — Audit-trail closure: enforce `bd human respond/dismiss` (or `verified-by-human` + `bd close`) over bare `bd close` on `human`-labeled beads. Depends on 7bk.13.
- ~~**b416**~~ — *closed as superseded by 7bk.13 (HEP makes the pause-gate refusal logic structurally unnecessary; `bd ready` filters via blocking deps).*

## Execution order

1. HEP architecture-of-record alignment (arch doc §5.6 + `beads.md` rule) — completed as part of 7bk.13's pivot transaction.
2. 7bk.13 ships first (skill + cross-skill updates).
3. ffxh ships after (audit-trail closure check, depends on HEP being in place).

## Why this epic exists

The original architecture mis-modeled human escalation as a label on the source bead. That label was visible to `bd ready --label implementation-ready` and did not actually gate readiness, so other agents could grab paused work. HEP corrects this by modeling pause as a structural blocker (dep edge), with a separate escalation bead carrying the `human` visibility label. This epic groups the work needed to roll the new pattern through the codebase and to enforce its closure invariants.

title: Audit-trail-required closure for human-labeled beads
description:
Under the Human-Escalation Pattern (architecture doc §5.6 / `beads.md` HEP rule), `human`-labeled beads are HEP escalation beads, merge-gate hand-off escalations, or `[h]` follow-up beads. They MUST be closed via class-correct primitives:

- **`bd human respond <id> --response "..."`** — adds the response as a comment AND closes; correct for resumable scenarios A–D in HEP.
- **`bd human dismiss <id> --reason "..."`** — closes with reason "Dismissed"; correct for abandonment scenario E.
- **`bd label add <id> verified-by-human` + plain `bd close <id>`** — correct for `[h]` follow-up verification scenario F (the `[h]` follow-up's parent edge to the source bead drives the I2 close-walk gate that `merge-and-cleanup` enforces; see arch doc §4.3).
- **`/merge-and-cleanup <source-id>`** — correct for merge-gate hand-off scenario G; the merge-and-cleanup formula closes the hand-off escalation as a final action of its merge step.

A bare `bd close <id>` on a `human`-labeled bead skips all four audit mechanisms: no response comment, no dismissal reason, no verification label, no merge-cleanup transaction. The most-acute consequence is for `[h]` follow-ups: a bare close silently bypasses the merge-and-cleanup gate-step's `verified-by-human` check, allowing an unverified follow-up to satisfy the I2 close-walk and the source bead to merge un-verified — a state-honesty violation.

## Scope (work owned by THIS bead)

The agent-facing rule (beads.md HEP section) is already in place as part of the HEP pivot (arch doc §5.6 + `src/plugins/beads/.claude/rules/beads.md`); see those documents for the canonical primitive table. This bead's remaining work is the **formula-facing enforcement check** plus a smoke test.

1. **`merge-and-cleanup` formula gate-step check** — at the gate-step that already validates "all `[h]` follow-up children closed" per arch §4.3, extend the check to verify each closed `[h]` follow-up child carries the `verified-by-human` label. If any closed follow-up lacks the label, the gate-step refuses to clear and surfaces a structured error naming the offending follow-up bead. Implementation lives in the `merge-and-cleanup.formula.toml`'s gate-step body (or equivalent).

2. **Smoke test** — procedural, run by hand against a scratch fixture, documented in PR description:
   - Set up: in `/tmp/ffxh-smoke`, `bd init`; create source bead `S` and a hand-crafted `[h]` follow-up child `FU` (per the §4.3 shape, since 7bk.12's auto-finalize hasn't shipped yet — see same fixture protocol as 7bk.13's Smoke 2).
   - Negative path: `bd close $FU` (bare close, no `verified-by-human` label). Pour `merge-and-cleanup` (or invoke its gate-step directly). Verify: gate-step refuses to clear; structured error names `$FU` as the offending follow-up; source `$S` does not close.
   - Positive path: `bd label add $FU verified-by-human` then close. Re-run the gate-step. Verify: gate-step clears; source closes per I2 walk.

## Out of scope

- Changes to the `bd` CLI itself.
- The agent-facing closure-path rule — already documented in the HEP pivot's `beads.md` rule and arch doc §5.6.
- The HEP escalation procedure and the `human` label semantics — owned by 7bk.13.
- A general-purpose pre-`bd close` hook intercepting all `human`-labeled bead closures (heavier mechanism; out of scope for this narrow enforcement target).

## Acceptance Criteria

- `merge-and-cleanup` formula gate-step refuses to clear when any closed `[h]` follow-up child lacks the `verified-by-human` label; structured error message names the offending follow-up.
- Gate-step clears cleanly when every closed `[h]` follow-up child carries `verified-by-human`.
- Smoke test executed and documented in PR description (negative path: gate refuses; positive path: gate clears). Synthetic-fixture protocol is acceptable since 7bk.12 (which auto-creates `[h]` follow-ups) is not yet implemented.
- The formula-step check references arch doc §4.3 (verification semantics) and §5.6 (HEP class taxonomy) inline.

## Dependencies

- Depends on 7bk.13 (HEP must be in place first; the closure-path rule and class taxonomy this bead enforces are defined there).
- (parent epic) 7bk.20.
- (deferred-from) Originally a broader "audit-trail closure for all `human`-labeled beads" — narrowed post-HEP because the agent-facing rule landed in the pivot itself.
acceptance_criteria:
merge-and-cleanup formula gate-step refuses to clear when any closed [h] follow-up child lacks verified-by-human; structured error message names the offending follow-up.
Gate-step clears cleanly when every closed [h] follow-up child carries verified-by-human.
Smoke test executed and documented in PR description (negative path gate refuses; positive path gate clears); synthetic-fixture protocol acceptable since 7bk.12 not yet implemented.
Formula-step check references arch doc 4.3 (verification semantics) and 5.6 (HEP class taxonomy) inline.

title: implement-feature.formula.toml: TOML parse error on invalid escape \[ in double-quoted string
description:
bd formula show implement-feature fails with: parse error: toml: line 167: invalid escape in string '\['

The formula has regex patterns like ^\[(m|h)\]\s(.*)$ inside TOML triple-double-quoted (basic) strings. TOML basic strings process backslash escapes, so \[ is invalid. Fix: switch the affected description blocks to TOML literal strings (''') which do not process escapes.

Affected file: src/plugins/beads/.beads/formulas/implement-feature.formula.toml (and ~/.beads/formulas/implement-feature.formula.toml)

Discovered while trying to pour implement-feature for bead agents-config-avq.
notes: Fixed in PR #43: switched red-tests description from TOML basic string (") to literal string (''') to avoid invalid escape errors.

title: Agent-team workflow audit: quality, clarity, simplicity, human-out-of-loop
description:
Use an agent team to walk through every workflow step-by-step, questioning everything for quality, clarity, simplicity, reliability, correctness, and opportunities for streamlining. Audit tool and script usage/optimization.

PRIME DIRECTIVE (the non-negotiable architectural goal against which every analysis is measured):
> Push the human upstream and downstream of the 'loop'. Bring the human into the loop ONLY for necessary escalations.

This means:
- Human defines intent, constraints, and success criteria UPSTREAM (before the loop starts)
- Agents execute, adapt, and self-correct WITHIN the loop
- Human reviews outcomes, provides directional feedback, and decides on scope changes DOWNSTREAM (after the loop completes)
- Escalation to human is a FAILURE MODE to be minimized, not a feature

Scope:
- Walk through each workflow defined in the re-architecture epics
- Question every step: Is this necessary? Could it be simpler? Is the tool the right one? Is the script robust? Is the error handling correct? Is the logging sufficient? Is the data flow clear?
- Identify friction points where humans are currently required mid-loop and determine how to eliminate them
- Look for redundancy, over-engineering, unclear boundaries, or missing guardrails
- Evaluate whether each workflow step advances the prime directive or violates it

Deliverables:
- Structured audit report per workflow
- Specific findings with severity (blocking, critical, major, minor)
- Recommendations for simplification, automation, or elimination
- Proposed rewrites or architectural adjustments where needed
- A 'human-touch heatmap' showing where humans are currently touched and whether each touchpoint is justified by the prime directive
notes:
ADDITIONAL CONSTRAINT (2026-05-06):

This audit is ESPECIALLY true of the bead formulas. Formulas create work placeholders (step beads, labels, notes) that the agent(s) will later have to process.

Every time a formula instructs an agent to 'infer' or 'decide' something that could instead be computed deterministically (e.g., via shell script, jq, awk, a small tool), that is a design flaw. It wastes context window, introduces execution drift, and creates unnecessary human-touch points.

Audit principle: prefer deterministic scripts and tools over LLM inference for the same outcome. If a formula step says 'the agent should figure out X', ask: could a script figure out X instead? If yes, the formula is wrong.

Target areas:
- Label computation (e.g., encoding URLs, deriving linkage labels)
- Status transitions (e.g., parent cascade, closure conditions)
- Molecular step progression (e.g., 'current step' resolution)
- Report formatting (e.g., IMPLEMENTATION-REPORT, FOREIGN-EYES blocks)
- Preflight checks (e.g., coverage opt-out, test-runner detection)

The formula should be a deterministic machine. The LLM should only touch what requires actual judgment.

title: Audit-driven M1 cleanups across agents/, commands/, formulas/, rules/, scripts/, skills/, templates/
description:
Rescoped 2026-05-10 during Phase 5b — was 'Audit skills, agents, commands, rules for best practices & coherence' (broad audit produced by il69); now M1-cleanups-only after the Tier 2/Tier 3 child beads spanning research spikes (acmh.2, acmh.5), M3 HEP semantics (acmh.10), and M4 run-queue replacement (acmh.9) were redistributed.

Scope: per-directory cleanup work derived from the il69 audit findings. Children: acmh.1 (agents/), acmh.3 (commands/), acmh.4 (formulas/), acmh.6 (rules/), acmh.7 (scripts/), acmh.8 (skills/), acmh.11 (templates/), acmh.12 (Tier 3 bd-sequence helper-script extractions). Excludes: structural / cross-cutting changes (now under their respective destinations).
acceptance_criteria: All residual children closed: acmh.1, acmh.3, acmh.4, acmh.6, acmh.7, acmh.8, acmh.11, acmh.12. Each child's specific F-N findings addressed per its own AC.
notes:
## Spec

Canonical spec: docs/specs/2026-05-09-acmh-comprehensive-audit-design.md (commit d9fc28d)

## Summary

Comprehensive multi-dimensional audit of all agent-facing content (skills, agents,
commands, rules, formulas, instruction templates, helper scripts/code) for
quality, coherence, signal-to-noise, right-tool-for-the-job placement, and
alignment with the project's 85/5/10 vision. Produces a tiered remediation
plan: Tier 1 mechanical fixes applied inline in this PR; Tier 2 design-required
work deferred to per-category follow-up beads; Tier 3 bd-sequence concerns
flagged and routed to existing/new follow-up beads.

## Architecture (3 phases)

- Phase 1 — Parallel category audits: 7 Opus 1M xhigh effort subagents
  (one per category: skills, agents, commands, rules, formulas, instruction
  templates, helper scripts/code) → docs/audits/phase1/<category>.md
- Phase 2 — Cross-cutting adversarial review: 6 Codex GPT-5.5 subagents,
  one per cross-cutting use case → docs/audits/phase2/<use-case-slug>.md
- Phase 3 — Aggregation: 1 Sonnet 1M subagent reconciles by category, by
  file, decision log, REMEDIATION_PLAN.md. USER REVIEW GATE before Tier 1
  application.

## Key Design Decisions

- Vision/Mission injected at dispatch from live AGENTS.md (no static drift).
- Every finding carries a mandatory Vision-advancement field explaining how
  the recommendation advances the 85/5/10 ratio (regression of cited issue
  is implicit).
- Right-tool-for-the-job inventory: skill ↔ rule ↔ command ↔ agent ↔ shell
  script/helper code.
- Pre-research primer set committed to docs/primers/ (FORMULAS, SKILLS,
  AGENTS, COMMANDS, RULES) so subagents share a knowledge baseline.
- xacz absorbed; bf6 and 2gzy linked but separate.
- wmjy chosen direction (script-extraction) covers dep-migration; remaining
  Tier 3 (children-check, label-copy-filter) goes to a new follow-up bead.

## Out of Scope

- Implementation of Tier 2 design changes (deferred to per-category beads).
- Long-bead spec externalization (agents-config-bf6).
- Helper script extraction itself (agents-config-2gzy / wmjy receive
  findings as input only).
- Refactoring or restructuring the install pipeline.

## Acceptance Criteria

See bead acceptance field. Full numbered list in spec §12.

## Execution Sequence

12 steps; spec §13. USER REVIEW GATE between Phase 3 aggregation and Tier 1
application is mandatory.

## Resource Budget (estimate)

- Phase 1: 7 × Opus 1M xhigh — heavy
- Phase 2: 6 × Codex GPT-5.5 — moderate
- Phase 3: 1 × Sonnet 1M — moderate
- Tier 1 inline fixes + completion gate + delivery — standard

See spec §15 for tradeoffs.

## Open Questions

None blocking. Optional refinements may surface during Phase 1 dispatch
prompt finalization (e.g. exact category slugs); these are in-flight
implementation details, not architectural.
Rescoped during Phase 5b. Pre-rescope title/description/AC captured at /tmp/phase5b-preflight-state.txt. Children redistributed: acmh.2 → Research-spikes, acmh.5 → Research-spikes, acmh.9 → M4, acmh.10 → 7bk (under M3).

comments.author: Scott Hamilton
comments.text:
PRESERVED FROM PRIOR NOTES (before write-spec overwrite, 2026-05-09):

SCOPE ADDITIONS (2026-05-06):

1. Remove references to other beads outside of bead-plugin space.
   Example: src/plugins/beads/.agents/skills/implement-bead/SKILL.md references 7bk.19.3.
   Bead IDs and internal bead-tracker references should not leak into shared skill/agent/command content.

2. Isolate 'beads' as a concept to the .beads plugin namespace only.
   Check src/user/.agents/ (shared content) for any bead terminology, bd command references, or bead-tracker jargon.
   The shared skills/agents should be tool-agnostic and not assume beads context.
   Found: wait-for-pr-comments, reply-and-resolve-pr-threads, writing-unit-tests, simplify, verify-checklist skills and quality-reviewer agent all reference beads/bd outside plugin space.

3. Cross-reference hygiene: skills, agents, and commands should ONLY refer to other assets when there is a clear dependency (e.g. 'use blah skill').
   Audit for spurious references, assumed context, or name-dropping other assets without a concrete dependency chain.

RESOURCE: Agent Skills Best Practices guide (https://platform.claude.com/docs/en/agents-and-tools/agent-skills/best-practices) — useful reference when auditing skill/agent/command quality and right-tool-for-the-job principles (2026-05-07)
Resource: https://internals.laxmena.com/p/what-youre-actually-writing-when — research when starting this bead

These items remain canonical scope; they are now covered/expanded in the spec at docs/specs/2026-05-09-acmh-comprehensive-audit-design.md (commit d9fc28d).
