Latent today — no */formulas/* glob exists in installer.toml or the prune list, so nothing matches — but it detonates the moment a formula-retiring glob is added while the active plugin ships a same-pattern formula.

(Originally filed as a bash-parity divergence vs install.sh:1545,971-975. That bash logic is retired — scripts/install.sh is now a 6-line uv-exec stub — so the parity framing is moot; this stands on its own as a latent-correctness bug.)

FIX: thread the active plugin set through _run -> _run_prune -> prune_pipeline -> scan_orphans; derive the beads/formulas staged baseline from the active plugins routes() (mirroring sync_routes: source_dir.glob(route.glob), files only, by name). A still-shipped formula is staged and protected; an empty plugin set preserves strict mode. _run_prune takes plugins as a REQUIRED kwarg to forbid a silent drop. Added 3 behavioral tests incl. a --plugins=beads --prune-only end-to-end scenario that reproduces the bug (live formula deleted) pre-fix.
close_reason: Fixed via PR #198 (squash-merged to main). scan_orphans now derives the beads/formulas staged baseline from active plugin routes; a still-shipped formula is protected, strict mode preserved when beads inactive.

title: Differ + design-doc parity-exception gaps (sign-off honesty)
description: Parity audit (H.3) found the golden-master differ (_diff.py) is sound but under-documented; record/scope these so silences are deliberate: (1) empty-directory blindness — _index_tree indexes is_file only, so bash's eager 'mkdir -p ~/.beads/formulas' (install.sh:960-961) vs Python's lazy create leaves a bash-only empty dir that two PASSING plugin scenarios mask; compare dir sets OR document as an accepted exception. (2) .json semantic/order-insensitive compare applies to ALL .json, but design-doc Parity-exceptions scopes it to settings.json only — tighten to basename=='settings.json' + byte-compare other .json, OR widen the doc. (3) process-contract divergences invisible to differ (unknown --tools/--plugins bash exit 1 vs Python 2; non-interactive-no-yes bash skip+continue vs Python ConsentRequiredError) — add to design-doc Parity-exceptions list. NB: the byte-identical-invalid-json short-circuit was already fixed under H.3.
close_reason: Obsolete: golden-master differ suite retired in #191 (commit 8b5b209) and install.sh collapsed to an exec stub. All three documented parity-exception gaps (empty-dir blindness in _index_tree, .json compare scope, process-contract divergences invisible to differ) were premised on the differ and/or the bash implementation, both now gone. No sign-off honesty remains to record.

title: prgroom: routed-memory PR-body PATCH has a lost-update race (GET→PATCH whole body)
description: Codex adversarial review (PR #174, gpt-5.5) HIGH finding. prgroom lifecycle/reply.py _route_memory reads the PR body (GET), splices the Decisions block locally, then PATCHes the whole body back. The per-PR lock guards only local prgroom state, not GitHub-side: a human or bot editing the PR description between GET and PATCH has their edit clobbered (lost update). Introduced by the 8.12 §8.3 CONTEXTUAL-memory-to-PR-body write path. Options: (a) route thread-less memory to a dedicated marked issue comment instead of mutating the PR description; (b) optimistic concurrency via ETag/If-Match + re-read-and-re-splice retry on conflict. 0866fd4 already skips no-op PATCHes (if merged != body), shrinking but not closing the window. Deferred from PR #174 by Scott.

title: prgroom: seed state.reviewers in _poll (passive required-reviewer / Copilot discovery)
description:
GAP (not scheduled): the reviewer subsystem is built top-to-bottom — _push/_poll review_found→not_requested flip (§3.4), has_required_reviewers_to_refresh, _rereview remove/add dance, _observe_engagement, evaluate_reviewer_timeouts auto-decline (§4.1), G_REVIEWERS quiescence gate (§4.1) — but NOTHING ever inserts a ReviewerState. grep 'ReviewerState(' src/ = 0 hits (tests only). poll_pr's six REST reads do not include requested_reviewers; _observe_engagement and evaluate_reviewer_timeouts only mutate EXISTING entries. Parent spec §4.1 specs reviewer transitions but never the seed/front-door.

CONSEQUENCE today (state.reviewers always empty in prod): _g_reviewers = all([]) = vacuously True so quiescence never waits on Copilot; evaluate_reviewer_timeouts is a no-op; has_required_reviewers_to_refresh is False so _rereview is inert. The 'wait a set time for Copilot to jump into the PR' intent is NOT wired.

BUILD (passive/reactive): _poll seeds state.reviewers — observe attached required reviewers (GET pulls/{n}/requested_reviewers) and/or Copilot's first review/comment, create ReviewerState(kind=BOT for [bot] logins, required per requested-reviewer/codeowner signal, status seeded appropriately). Downstream machinery (timeout, flip, rereview dance, G_REVIEWERS) then activates as designed.

OUT OF SCOPE: proactive review-request config (separate future option); multi-reviewer/codeowner generalisation = agents-config-iohrs (PDLC orchestrator), keep distinct.

Discovered during abn9.8.12 §6 rereview-resumability analysis: the rereview guard and reviewer-gated quiescence are inert in prod until this seed lands.

title: [Impl] prgroom hard-cap — gate at cycle entry; never run a fix that won't push
description:
§3.5 hard-cap rework discovered during abn9.8.12.

Today cap_guard (run.py _cap_guard_step) runs AFTER _fix, BEFORE _push: when commits are queued and round>=max_rounds it flips human-gated and refuses the push. This (a) burns fix-agent tokens (opus xhigh) on a fix that is never pushed, and (b) strands the fix commits + withheld replies locally until a human raises --max-rounds — a waste + data-loss risk.

Desired: move the budget check to CYCLE ENTRY (before cluster/fix). When the budget is spent AND a new review round has unprocessed items, escalate to human-gated immediately — no fix/reply/resolve work. Any cycle that DOES start a fix always carries it through push+reply+resolve (never fix-then-refuse). Rule: never do work that doesn't get used.

Boundary (pin exactly here): with cap=N, fix-and-push rounds complete and push through round N; escalate at entry when round N+1's review still cites issues. Decide whether the initial review (round 1 per §3.4 bootstrap) counts toward N.

Deferred optimization (NOT here): peek-for-skippability — at the cap, a round whose items are all skippable/wont_fix today auto-declines (no commits -> no trip); the entry gate will instead escalate it. Accepted tradeoff for now.

Touch points: run.py (_build_pipeline/_cap_guard_step -> entry gate; _entry_probe cap re-arm; _resolve_end_of_cycle priority-1 cap), and doc-sync §3.5 / §3.2 matrix / §3.4 round semantics. Owner: run-aggregate (abn9.8.10) territory.
acceptance_criteria:
- Budget checked at cycle entry; when exhausted + new unprocessed items, escalate to human-gated WITHOUT dispatching _fix (no wasted tokens).
- Any cycle that runs _fix always reaches _push then _reply/_resolve; the fix-then-refuse-push path is gone (no queued-but-unpushed-then-gated commits).
- Entry escalation sets LIFECYCLE_HARD_CAP_EXCEEDED + human-gated, emits exactly one Sink event; entry-probe re-arm on raised --max-rounds still resumes correctly.
- Boundary verified by example: cap=3 -> rounds fixed+pushed through round 3; a round-4 review with issues escalates at entry (initial-review counting decision recorded).
- end-of-cycle resolver + §3.2 matrix + §3.5/§3.4 docs synced to the entry-gate model.
- fakes-not-mocks tests: entry-gate escalation, no-_fix-on-cap, boundary off-by-one, re-arm; mypy --strict.

title: Post-cutover: reassess Claude always-on + real auto-detect/detection_signal (decoupled from bash)
description:
Product intent that outlives Epic H: once the golden-master parity suite is validated/signed off (H.3) and the bash installer is abandoned at cutover, we WILL want genuine per-tool auto-detection — for ALL tools INCLUDING Claude — decoupled from scripts/install.sh behavior.

During Epic H the Python port deliberately mirrored bash: ClaudeAdapter.is_detected was hardcoded to return True (bash unconditional TOOLS=(claude) floor) so the parity test stays green. Parity is the binding constraint until cutover; do NOT change detection while the golden-master suite is the active contract — it would break parity and fail the golden-master test.

REASSESS post-cutover:
- ClaudeAdapter.is_detected: replace the hardcoded return True with a real marker (e.g. ~/.claude/ presence) if always-on is no longer the desired product behavior.
- The "no agent tools detected" diagnostic in cli.py (the `if not tools:` block) was removed because always-on Claude made it unreachable. With real per-tool detection, auto-detect can be empty again -> reinstate the diagnostic.
- detection_signal was removed from the ToolAdapter protocol (base.py) + all 4 adapters (its sole consumer was that diagnostic). If a real status/diagnostic returns, restore detection_signal — and do it CORRECTLY: the old opencode value (.config/opencode) was incomplete, omitting the which(opencode) PATH branch that is_detected actually checks.

BREADCRUMB (what was removed, to reassess):
- PR #168
- Commit 12abda7eef7ef6e5c9ee4727a24beddbc1806cd8 — git show 12abda7 shows the exact removals.
- Files: tools/claude.py (is_detected->True), cli.py (diagnostic block removed), tools/base.py + tools/codex.py + tools/gemini.py + tools/opencode.py (detection_signal removed).

GATE: blocked by H.3 parity confirmation (w1qls.8.3) + cutover. Discovered while implementing 8.9.

title: Staging collision should merge-dispatch, not hard-fail (Epic E owned)
description: H.1 audit. A staging-plan key collision currently raises a fatal error; bash routes overlapping content through merge logic. This is Epic E (merge registry) territory - filed under Epic H per directive but OWNED by merge-strategy work. Gates cutover ONLY if a real install path triggers a collision (verify on pickup; if none collide, clean post-cutover defer). Fix: dispatch collisions to the merge registry instead of fataling.
notes:
TRIAGE VERDICT (read-only, 2026-06-20): DEFER_POST_H4 per the bead own gate. VERIFY-FIRST: no real shipped install path triggers a base-staging key collision. Fatal raise = staging.py:197-200 (_add_item), guards ONLY base staging (build_plan Phases 1-5). Plugin overlay (Phase 6, overlay.py:_place 81-123) ALREADY merge-dispatches via registry.resolve().merge() -> bead ask already done for plugin path. Census of real src/: only ClaudeAdapter has non-empty scoped_namespaces (codex/gemini/opencode return () -> no Phase-4 stage); all Claude base-staging dest sets DISJOINT (comm -12: shared-vs-claude root/rules/skills/agents all empty overlap). --plugins=beads doesnt reach _add_item (overlays Phase 6) and beads isnt in live src/plugins/ (only AGENTS.md; beads under archive/). Only collision exercised today is overlay path (test_parity_plugins.py:55, green); base-staging raise has NO real trigger (test_staging_build_plan.py:91 injects synthetic dup). Hard-fail unreachable on every real path -> does NOT gate H.4. Real Epic-E merge-symmetry work (wire _add_item to default_registry like overlay.py) but zero cutover urgency. Blocked on H.4 (8.4).
IMPLEMENTED 2026-06-21 (PR #194, branch worktree-w1qls.8.14-staging-merge-dispatch). Base staging now routes dest_relpath collisions through default_registry() via new core/merge/place.py::place_resolved, shared by _add_item and overlay._place. Registry-only (no carrier-merge in base staging); real merges on (rules append / settings json-union / OTHER last-wins / DIR+commands+skills+agents fatal). make ci-installer green (590 tests, 99.81% cov). quality-reviewer + simplify clean. Copilot review clean (0 comments, 0 threads). AWAITING MERGE AUTHORIZATION.

title: Re-install idempotency parity: restore mode on hash-equal files; stop re-backing-up dirs
description: H.1 audit (folds the earlier DIR-idempotency discovery). Two re-install defects vs bash: (1) exec-bit not restored on hash-equal files - sync skips unchanged content and never re-applies mode, so a dest file with wrong mode stays wrong (bash always chmods). (2) Python _install_dir backs up + replaces directories on EVERY run; bash is idempotent. Fix: on hash-equal still reconcile mode; make DIR sync idempotent (no backup/replace when unchanged). Acceptance: idempotent re-install golden-master scenario (run installer twice into same HOME) reaches parity and produces no spurious backups on 2nd run.

title: Add --verbose/-v flag to Python CLI
description: H.1 audit. cli.py lacks --verbose/-v; bash supports it and emits per-file detail. All verbose output is silently suppressed in the Python port. Fix: add the flag and plumb verbose-level output through IOPort. Acceptance: --verbose produces bash-equivalent information detail.

title: ruff-postedit hook: stop auto-deleting imports per-edit (F401 strip causes F821 churn across multi-edit authoring)
description:
Retrospective finding (prgroom 8.16 session: ~10+ blocking F821 hook errors). src/user/.claude/hooks/ruff-postedit.py line ~93 runs 'ruff check --fix --force-exclude' unconditionally after every Edit. F401 (unused import) is auto-fixed by DELETION: when an import is added in one edit before its first use lands in a later edit, the hook strips the import, and the usage edit then fails F821 'Undefined name'. The inverse (usage-first) trips F821 until the import edit lands. Either way, any time import and usage are in separate file regions across two edits (the common case), the hook emits a blocking error — wasting a re-add/re-read cycle every time, in every repo, for every agent (global hook).

Recommended fix (decide during impl):
1. Minimal: add '--unfixable F401,F841' to the line-93 'ruff check --fix' so unused imports/vars are reported but never auto-deleted. The import survives until the usage edit clears it cleanly. Genuinely-unused imports are still caught at the CI gate ('ruff check' in make ci-*).
2. Stronger: also downgrade F401/F841 to non-blocking in the postedit (don't return 2 for those rules), since they are transient during incremental authoring and the gate is the real enforcer. Keep blocking on real problems (syntax, undefined names that AREN'T mid-authoring artifacts).

Update the hook test (src/user/.claude/hooks/ruff-postedit_test.sh) to cover the add-import-then-use-in-separate-edits sequence. Note: the hook is staged to ~/.claude/ only on install.sh (user-run), so the fix is source-only until the user installs. Persisted lesson: native memory feedback_ruff_postedit_autofix_strips_imports.
notes: Done (edb9fc6, committed direct to main, no PR per request). Fix: --fix step marks F401,F841 --unfixable; final blocking check --ignore's them; real errors still block; gate stays the enforcer. Bare --unfixable/--ignore EXTEND project config (verified ruff 0.15.13). Hook test PASS=16/0 (added regression cases 8-9, argv-contract + real-F821-still-blocks). Source-only — takes effect on next install.sh (user-deferred).

title: prgroom preflight gate design: gh-auth + repo-reachable + CWD repo-identity (startup-once + re-check-on-terminal)
description: Brainstorm (#1 + #5 from the 8.16 error audit). PRECONDITION_NO_AUTH + PRECONDITION_REPO_UNREACHABLE are registered but never raised - no proactive preflight. And the wrong-branch guard checks branch name but not that the worktree's origin points at the PR's owner/repo (the deferred 'current-repo context seam'). Decision steer (Scott): preflight probes gh-auth + repo-reachable + repo-identity ONCE at run start (fail-fast), and a mid-run 401/404 is treated as terminal->human-gated (auth/repo expired). Design where the gate lives, what it probes, and how it composes with the 8.10 run loop. Brainstorm before implementing.

title: prgroom W-line error hardening: terminal git-failure classification + write-verb GhNotFound mapping + runtime-terminal telemetry
description:
Surfaced auditing 8.16 against unauth/CWD/fail-fast concerns. Three clear-cut correctness fixes, folded into PR #159 (unmerged):
#2 git adapter: 'fatal: not a git repository' and push permission/auth-denied stderr currently fall to RUNTIME_GIT_TRANSIENT (retry-forever). Add a narrow _GIT_TERMINAL_MARKERS allowlist (not-a-repo, auth/permission) -> RUNTIME_GIT_TERMINAL, mirroring _PUSH_REJECTED_MARKERS; everything else stays transient.
#3 write verbs (push/rereview/resolve) don't catch GhNotFoundError (a plain Exception, not PrgroomError) -> a PR/repo 404 mid-run escapes the CLI's 'except PrgroomError' as a raw traceback. Map it to a terminal tagged error, mirroring poll._vanished_pr_terminal.
#4 handle_cli_error renders the rich what/why/how block ONLY for PreconditionError; runtime-terminal errors print bare 'error: <CODE>' and drop .detail. Render code + what/why/how + detail for non-precondition PrgroomError so fail-fast errors carry the richest telemetry, not the least.
notes: Implemented + folded into PR #159 (commit 0a7186a). #2 git terminal-marker allowlist, #3 write-verb GhNotFound->terminal via shared vanished_pr_terminal, #4 unified render() with credential-redacted detail. Quality-reviewer HIGH (stale registry guidance) + suggestions (redaction, precondition-detail test, import alignment, case-sensitivity comment) all addressed. make ci-prgroom green: 752 tests, 99.87%. Copilot re-review: no new comments; 0 unresolved threads. Review-clean, pending merge of #159.

title: Repoint dead 2026-05-17-python-installer-rewrite.md citations to installer-design.md
description: Living code cites a non-existent spec. packages/installer/src/installer/core/sync.py and packages/installer/tests/unit/test_sync.py reference docs/specs/2026-05-17-python-installer-rewrite.md, which does not exist. Repoint these to the real design doc docs/architecture/installer/installer-design.md (or a section-name reference per the no-file-path-citation convention). Secondary: audit four dated specs that name the same dead path (docs/specs/2026-05-17-w1qls.1.1-installer-scaffold-plan.md, -design.md; 2026-05-17-w1qls.1.2-model-design.md; 2026-05-19-w1qls.1.3-io-port-design.md) — as point-in-time artifacts they may keep historical references, so decide per file. Discovered during Epic G wave-2 (PRs #150/#151); flagged by the prune-pipeline implementer as a future-agent footgun.

title: prgroom: wire per-repo .prgroom.toml path resolution across all verbs
description: No verb resolves the per-repo .prgroom.toml path: poll/status call PrgroomConfig.load() and cluster/fix call load_chain(repo_config=None) — read_toml(None) returns {} (config.py:215), so the file is NEVER consulted and [agents.*] provider overrides + quiescence/round config from TOML are inert; only the shipped default chain + built-in defaults apply. Needs a config-path resolver (cwd vs git-root-walk vs PRGROOM_CONFIG env — pick one) threaded into every verb's PrgroomConfig.load + load_chain call, plus the deferred --cluster-model/--fix-model override flags. Systemic/pre-existing (predates 8.15). Surfaced by Copilot on PR #152.

title: prgroom: response-file persistence lifecycle (fix produces, reply consumes)
description: fix_pr persists Disposition.response_path from run_fix output, but assemble_snapshot creates response_outbox_dir under the verb's ephemeral TemporaryDirectory scratch — so a provider-set response_path is persisted into state then deleted when the temp dir is cleaned, leaving a dangling pointer for the (unbuilt) reply verb to read. Needs a persistence contract: where response files live so they survive fix->reply (the Store Protocol exposes no per-PR persistent dir today), and who cleans them up (after resolve?). Cross-bead design decision spanning fix (8.15) and reply (8.12). Latent until reply lands. Surfaced by Copilot on PR #152 (comment 3408263720).

title: MCP server abstraction for beads CLI access
description:
Investigate and conditionally implement an MCP server that mediates script/tool access to beads, replacing direct CLI calls with a more reliable, typed interface. 

This aligns with the goal to quarantine beads behind our own CLI abstraction and move determinism out of bash scripts and into code helpers. 

Execution plan: (1) Brainstorm tradeoffs vs direct CLI (speed, reliability, testing, context isolation). (2) If aligned, design the MCP contract and proof-of-concept. (3) Migrate one script to verify the pattern works.

title: prgroom gh adapter: add --paginate to GhCli.rest list reads (snapshot + poll whole-PR completeness)
description: GhCli.rest runs 'gh api' without --paginate, so list reads (pulls/{n}/comments, reviews, issue comments) return only the first page (~30). snapshot._fetch_review_threads is therefore page-1-complete, not whole-PR-complete (§8.1 promises every thread with full reply-chain); poll's _ingest_items shares the same cap. Add --paginate to the adapter's list GETs and update caller tests. Cross-cutting gh-layer change. Discovered during 8.15.

title: prgroom poll: populate Identity.thread_id (GraphQL node id) + reconcile snapshot thread key-space
description: poll's _to_item never sets Identity.thread_id (REST ingest), and snapshot._fetch_review_threads keys threads by the REST root-comment id — a different key-space from the GraphQL PRRT_* node id thread_id is defined as. Net effect: recurrence.reopened (§8.2) is always False in the integrated poll->cluster->fix path, AND agent/fix.py::_resolve_thread_ids builds an empty known_thread_ids set (silently degrading CONTEXTUAL memory target_hint routing, §8.6). Fix: poll ingests review-thread node ids via GraphQL (or a REST->node reconciliation) and the snapshot keys threads consistently. Discovered during the 8.15 3-lens review; the derivation logic itself is correct + unit-tested.

title: prgroom §2 schema: retain disposition history + first-seen-round for real §8.2 recurrence counters
description: §8.2 recurrence specifies attempt_count and first_seen_round, but the §2 state schema retains exactly one Disposition per ReviewItem and has no first-seen-round field, so snapshot.derive_recurrence reports honest floors (attempt_count=1, first_seen_round=state.round). A schema_version bump adding per-item disposition history + a first_seen_round field unlocks real values. Deliberately frozen by §8.2/§2 at MVP. Discovered during 8.15.

title: Python installer: stage the hooks/ namespace (parity with install.sh hooks/ support)
description:
The ruff-postedit PostToolUse hook work (see docs/specs/2026-06-13-ruff-postedit-hook-design.md) introduces a new `hooks/` namespace to the LEGACY `scripts/install.sh`: a staged dir under src/user/.claude/hooks/ deploying to ~/.<tool>/hooks/, chmod +x, prune-exempt. For feature parity, the Python installer rewrite (packages/installer) must stage it the same way, or the rewrite regresses against the bash installer the moment hooks/ lands on the bash side.

## In scope
- Add `hooks` to the staged-namespace set in packages/installer (mirror the skills/agents/rules/commands handling in core/staging.py). It stages files (scripts), not the skills/agents dir-granularity model.
- Preserve the source executable mode bit (core/model.py already carries `executable` 0o755) so deployed hook scripts land +x in ~/.<tool>/hooks/.
- Prune parity: hooks/ is prune-exempt in bash (install.sh:1453) — ensure core/prune.py never lists ~/.<tool>/hooks/ entries as orphans.
- Parity coverage: add a hooks/ scenario to the Epic H golden-master harness (H.1/H.2) so H.3 parity-confirmation exercises it.

## Done when
- packages/installer stages src/user/.claude/hooks/ -> ~/.claude/hooks/ at parity with install.sh, exec bit preserved.
- Unit tests + golden-master scenario cover hooks/ staging and prune-exemption.
- `make ci-installer` green.

## Depends on / coordinates with
- The bash-side hooks/ namespace addition (ruff-postedit hook design). This bead is the rewrite-side parity counterpart; sequence after (or alongside) the bash change so the golden master has a reference to match.

title: [Impl] prgroom — default re-review round cap to 6 (not legacy 3) in §3.5 cap re-arm
description:
When the re-review round hard cap moves from prose into prgroom CLI code/config, its DEFAULT must be 6 rounds, NOT the legacy 3.

## Why
The legacy cap lived as a hand-typed `round >= 3` in the wait-for-pr-comments skill (Phase 6 hard cap + the rationalizations table). PR #149 bumped that prose to `round >= 6` because the 3-round ceiling was forcing premature ESCALATE (rationale "exceeded re-review round cap") on PRs that legitimately needed more Copilot iterations to converge. The CLI port must NOT silently reintroduce 3 and regress this.

## Where this lands
- Design doc: docs/plans/2026-05-12-prgroom-cli-design.md §3.5 (cap re-arm).
- Implementing task: the run loop (the §3.5 entry-time external-transition probe + cap re-arm). Make 6 the default; ideally expose it as config rather than a literal so it stops being a magic number — which is the whole point of the code-over-prose port.
- On cutover (monitor-pr replacing Skill A), ensure the new supervisor inherits the 6 default.

## Done when
- prgroom's re-review round cap defaults to 6.
- The value is a named constant/config knob, not an inline literal duplicated across sites.
- Exceeding the cap still triggers the human-review/escalation hook (behavior parity with the prose contract).

title: Experiment: track-progress skill — live HTML roadmap/progress dashboard
description:
Experiment with a `track-progress` skill that creates and continuously updates an HTML page visualizing progress against a roadmap, including LIVE status for in-progress tasks/items (not just done/not-done).

HOW this is built is TO-BE-BRAINSTORMED. Do NOT implement before a brainstorm produces a chosen approach. The seed ideas below frame that brainstorm only — they are not a committed design.
acceptance_criteria: A brainstorm has selected an approach, and a prototype track-progress skill can render an HTML roadmap that reflects real work state including live in-progress status.
notes:
Seed ideas (pre-brainstorm — framing, not design):
- A companion `build-roadmap` skill that produces the .metadata describing the context/roadmap to be tracked (source of truth for what the dashboard renders).
- Instructions woven into the orchestration agent so progress is reliably recorded as work proceeds.
- A monitoring subagent that watches progress and updates the UI / HTML page.

Open questions for the brainstorm:
- Data source: beads? a metadata file? both?
- How is 'in-progress' status detected and how live should it be (static regen vs polling vs push)?
- Where does the HTML live / how is it hosted & refreshed?
- How does it tie into existing milestones and whats-next?
- Single roadmap vs per-session/per-fleet views?

title: [QA] prgroom real-binary integration smoke for the four agent invokers (claude/codex/opencode/ollama)
description: Deep review of PR #142 (bead abn9.8.4, MINOR-8) flagged that nothing in the suite or CI exercises a real CLI: argv shapes (--effort, codex exec stdout discipline, opencode model format) and stdout JSON discipline are all unverified OS-boundary assumptions. Add a manual/optional (non-CI-gating) smoke that dispatches a trivial contract through each real binary and asserts parseable contract output. Should run before 8.15 wires _cluster/_fix into the lifecycle.

title: [Impl] prgroom usage-line token parser — extract input/output tokens from claude/codex CLI output
description: Deep review of PR #142 (bead abn9.8.4, MAJOR-1) found usage.py captures per-invocation records but has no way to populate input_tokens/output_tokens — no parser exists for the usage/stats lines the claude and codex CLIs emit. Build per-invoker token extraction feeding UsageRecord (fields are int|None until then). Scope: claude + codex first; ollama/opencode if/when their CLIs expose counts.

title: [Impl] prgroom lifecycle write verbs — _push + _rereview + _resolve (live GitHub) [8.9c]
description: _push (upload staged commits; bump round; flip stale reviewers) + _rereview (re-request required bot reviewers) + _resolve (GraphQL resolveReviewThread for fixed/already_addressed threads). First live mutation of a PR. Write slice of the 8.9 re-cut. Depends on 8.9b. Milestone M3 (it changes a PR). See prgroom impl grinder plan §3.
notes:
Registry gap surfaced in 8.5 (gh/git adapters): §3.7 has RUNTIME_GIT_TRANSIENT + RUNTIME_PUSH_REJECTED but NO general git-terminal code. So the 8.5 git adapter maps any non-rejection, non-timeout git failure (e.g. git binary missing, repo corrupt — permanent) to RUNTIME_GIT_TRANSIENT (retryable), which a scheduler could retry unbounded. When implementing _push/git error handling here, decide whether to add a RUNTIME_GIT_TERMINAL code (registry change) for permanent git failures. Latent until a verb actually drives git.
PR #159 opened (worktree-prgroom-8.16-write-verbs). Implemented _push + _rereview + _resolve + RUNTIME_GIT_TERMINAL + GhClient.head_ref_name + GitClient.current_branch + wrong-branch guard (PRECONDITION_WRONG_BRANCH). make ci-prgroom green: 743 tests, 99.87% cov. Awaiting Copilot review; parked at merge gate pending human authorization.
Copilot review complete (3 rounds): r1 cap-warning >=/== fix (b977835), r2 two doc-accuracy nits (209340d), r3 no new comments. All threads replied + resolved; 0 unresolved. PR #159 MERGEABLE/CLEAN, 3 commits. Parked at merge gate awaiting human authorization.

title: [Impl] prgroom lifecycle stage verbs — _cluster + _fix (local staging) [8.9b]
description: _cluster (group unprocessed items into fix-bundles; cheap agent, no disposition) + _fix (per-cluster fix agent decides disposition + implements; snapshot assembly + recurrence). Stages commits on the local worktree branch — nothing pushed to GitHub. Stage slice of the 8.9 re-cut. Depends on 8.9a (poll/read) + agent audits (8.7). Milestone M2 (it decides what to do). See prgroom impl grinder plan §3.
notes:
PR: https://github.com/scotthamilton77/agents-config/pull/152 (base main; bead left open per stacked-PR-human-merges). Discovered-work: qmoz5 (poll thread_id), us8da (§2 schema history), j8pdq (gh --paginate).
Copilot: 3 rounds, cap 3. 7 FIX (committed+resolved): malformed-ts crash guard (083), decisions-block wording (099), fix-verb all-dispositioned no-op (112), malformed-ts test (120), 2x dispatcher-docstring TOML overpromise (708/715), per-cluster gh_id apply (R3-1/284563). 1 SKIP (replied, unresolved): response_path persistence (720 -> oav16). All round fixes gated green (702 tests, 99.86%). PR #152 @ 24c3986. Parked at merge gate.

title: [Impl] prgroom Phase-1 — birth monitor-pr + cutover runbook + prove prgroom end-to-end (additive)
description:
Additive Phase-1 slice (the destructive cutover — retire Skill A, relocate hook, repoint closure — moved to its own bead blocked by this one). Births the monitor-pr supervisor skill ADJACENT to the still-intact wait-for-pr-comments (NO deletions, NO relocation, NO reference repointing here), documents the cutover runbook adjacent to existing prgroom docs, and uses THIS bead's own PR as the live test vehicle to exercise prgroom across its use cases. Close/exit ONLY when prgroom is confirmed working end-to-end. Per design §6.2 (monitor-pr contract), §6.4-6.6 (runbook), §3.7 (exit codes), §4.6 (status --json).

## In scope
- Create src/user/.agents/skills/monitor-pr/SKILL.md (new, ~50 lines) — contract-aware supervisor driving `prgroom run`. Mode-select from trigger (chat=interactive, cron/loop/GHA=autonomous) -> passes --interactive / --autonomous. Decision table over the §3.7 exit-code registry + §4.6 status --json + terminal phases: exit 0 + phase in {quiesced,merged} -> report (interactive) / silent exit 0 (autonomous); phase=human-gated -> surface each item's rationale + hand over `prgroom resolve-escalated <owner/repo#n> <item-id> --as {fixed|skipped|deferred|wont_fix} [--rationale ...] [--commits ...]` + re-invoke (interactive) / route via EscalationSink + non-zero (autonomous); RUNTIME_TERMINAL_USER -> surface+stop / sink+non-zero; RUNTIME_TRANSIENT -> bounded re-invoke / scheduler retry; RUNTIME_CANCELLED -> report+stop. Reads status --json fields (phase, last_error, items_summary, merge_gates, auto_merge_eligible). Frontmatter test (model: sonnet[1m], effort: medium).
- Install monitor-pr adjacent to the existing skills. wait-for-pr-comments + its detect-pr-push.sh stay FULLY INTACT and hook-wired. monitor-pr is exercised MANUALLY in this bead; hook wiring deferred to the cutover bead.
- Document the cutover runbook (drain-before-cutover §6.4; readiness gate §6.5 >=3 PRs quiesced/merged clean; rollback §6.5 git revert -> install.sh WITHOUT --prune) adjacent to existing prgroom docs (docs/architecture/prgroom/).
- Validate prgroom end-to-end via THIS bead's own PR: drive `prgroom run` via monitor-pr across the use cases (clean quiesce, human-gated escalation + resolve-escalated recipe, transient retry, status --json envelope).

## Pin
install.sh prgroom ownership (8.6, done) is the install path. If prgroom is not globally installed, prove via `uv --project packages/prgroom run prgroom`.

## Out of scope (-> cutover bead, blocked by this one)
- Delete wait-for-pr-comments (SKILL.md + scripts + tests + references/).
- Relocate detect-pr-push.sh + settings.json.template hook repoint.
- Repoint the reference closure (delivery, completion-gate, merge-guard, pr-comment-fixer-team, skills/AGENTS.md).
- pr-comment-fixer-team / handling-feedback.md fate decision.

## Modules
src/user/.agents/skills/monitor-pr/SKILL.md, src/user/.agents/skills/monitor-pr/<frontmatter test>, docs/architecture/prgroom/<runbook>.
acceptance_criteria:
- monitor-pr/SKILL.md body ~50 lines; selects mode from trigger and passes --interactive/--autonomous; decision table covers exit 0 + terminal phase, human-gated (+ resolve-escalated recipe), RUNTIME_TERMINAL_USER, RUNTIME_TRANSIENT, RUNTIME_CANCELLED; reads status --json fields; frontmatter test passes.
- monitor-pr coexists with an INTACT wait-for-pr-comments; this bead deletes/relocates/repoints NOTHING (grep -r wait-for-pr-comments src/ is unchanged from before this bead).
- Cutover runbook (drain / readiness gate / rollback) documented adjacent to existing prgroom docs.
- prgroom proven end-to-end on THIS bead's own PR across the use cases (clean quiesce, human-gated + resolve-escalated, transient retry, status --json); bead closed ONLY after that end-to-end confidence.
notes: PR #181 merged (squash e9f5211). Additive deliverable shipped: monitor-pr skill + frontmatter test + cutover runbook. E2E read-only contracts validated LIVE on #181 (poll, status --json envelope, PRECONDITION_NO_STATE, dispositions). Full fix->push->reply->resolve loop BLOCKED: prgroom's headless 'claude -p' fix dispatch has no --permission-mode, so the fix agent reasons correctly but cannot commit (all 5 fixes landed 'failed' on commit). Filed as 8.21 (P1, scoped --allowedTools approach chosen). CLOSE 8.13 only after 8.21 lands + full E2E re-run clean on a real PR.

title: [Impl] prgroom Phase-2 retirement — delete reply-and-resolve-pr-threads, re-repoint rules, document standalone verbs
description:
Phase 2: retire reply-and-resolve-pr-threads now that reply/resolve/resolve-escalated shipped in Phase 1. Deliberately light — retirement + cleanup, GATED on the Phase-1 readiness bake (§6.5). Per docs/plans/2026-05-12-prgroom-cli-design.md §6.3/§6.7.

## In scope
- Delete reply-and-resolve-pr-threads/SKILL.md + the 6 Skill B scripts (render-reply-bodies, post-replies, resolve-threads, verify-head-sha, probe-fix-shas, build-final-report) + ALL matching *_test.sh in the dir (incl. any frontmatter test); install.sh --prune removes deployed copies.
- Rewrite the completion-gate.md / delivery.md clause "invokes reply-and-resolve-pr-threads" -> "prgroom handles reply + resolve within run" and clean every remaining grep hit across the source tree (skills/AGENTS.md, merge-guard/SKILL.md; wait-for-pr-comments references already gone in Phase 1).
- Document standalone verbs (prgroom reply <pr>, prgroom resolve <pr>) and crash recovery (--resume becomes "re-invoke prgroom" — idempotent prsession read).

## Critic-fix pin
settings.json.template is NOT in scope — grep shows zero reply-and-resolve-pr-threads references there (the only settings.json reference was wait-for-pr-comments, repointed in Phase 1). The external (non-dying-dir) reply-and-resolve closure is exactly {completion-gate.md, delivery.md, skills/AGENTS.md, merge-guard/SKILL.md}.

## Out of scope
- any prgroom code change (Phase 2 is retirement + docs only); the future 5-class repo-wide memory router.

## Modules
src/user/.agents/skills/reply-and-resolve-pr-threads/ (deleted), rules/completion-gate.md, rules/delivery.md, skills/AGENTS.md, merge-guard/SKILL.md, docs/.
acceptance_criteria:
- reply-and-resolve-pr-threads/SKILL.md and all 6 named Skill B scripts deleted with ALL matching *_test.sh (incl. any frontmatter test); install.sh --prune removes deployed copies.
- completion-gate.md/delivery.md clause rewritten to "prgroom handles reply + resolve within run"; the closure {completion-gate.md, delivery.md, skills/AGENTS.md, merge-guard/SKILL.md} is cleaned; `grep -rln 'reply-and-resolve-pr-threads' src/` returns no matches after the rewrite; settings.json.template is NOT edited (no reference exists there).
- standalone verbs documented (prgroom reply <pr>, prgroom resolve <pr>); old --resume crash-recovery documented as "re-invoke prgroom" (idempotent prsession read).
- Gated on the Phase-1 readiness gate having passed (>=3 PRs groomed clean); lands as a git-revertible Phase-2 phase commit.
- Phase-2 revert restores Skill B as the rollback anchor (Phase 1 left it intact).

title: [Impl] prgroom status verb + merge-gate contract — status --json envelope, §4.4/§4.6 human-review derivation
description:
The status verb and the stable merge-gate handoff contract (the interface a future merge-gate consumes). Split out from the run-loop bead because the §4.6 status --json envelope is a reviewable PR's worth of stable-contract surface on its own. Per docs/plans/2026-05-12-prgroom-cli-design.md §4.4/§4.6.

## In scope
- lifecycle/status.py — lock-free status read + `--locked` opt-in; the status --json envelope as the stable merge-gate handoff contract.
- lifecycle/human_review.py (§4.4/§4.6) — the human-review-required merge-constraint derivation (label-based, non-blocking, bot-filtered).
- status --json envelope: merge_gates{phase_is_quiesced, last_error_clear, no_blocker_items, human_review_satisfied}, human_review{required, satisfied_by, candidates_seen}, auto_merge_eligible.

## §4.6 contract pins (critic coverage-gap fixes)
- human_review.satisfied_by resolves in PRECEDENCE order: 'label' (if human-approved label present) -> 'approval:{login}' (first non-Bot APPROVED review) -> None. The 'approval:{login}' format is pinned; github-copilot[bot]-style approvals are excluded by the bot-filter.
- human_review.candidates_seen carries one row per approval candidate with its bot-filter outcome (the §4.6 operator-debuggability field: "why didn't approval X count?").
- human_review_satisfied is derived, NEVER persisted, and NEVER blocks quiescence (§4.4 merge-constraint, not a lifecycle gate).

## Out of scope
- request_human_review_if_needed (the label POST is in the run-loop bead's escalation.py); the future merge-gate verb itself (separate sub-design).

## Modules
lifecycle/status.py, lifecycle/human_review.py, cli.py (status wiring).
acceptance_criteria:
- status default does a lock-free read; --locked exits 75 under contention; a racing read sees old-or-new complete file, never partial.
- status --json emits the full §4.6 envelope: merge_gates{phase_is_quiesced, last_error_clear, no_blocker_items, human_review_satisfied}, human_review{required, satisfied_by, candidates_seen}, auto_merge_eligible.
- auto_merge_eligible == phase_is_quiesced AND last_error_clear AND no_blocker_items AND human_review_satisfied.
- human_review.satisfied_by resolves in precedence order 'label' -> 'approval:{login}' (first non-Bot APPROVED review) -> None; the 'approval:{login}' format is asserted and a github-copilot[bot]-style approval is excluded by the bot-filter (test pins the precedence AND the bot exclusion).
- human_review.candidates_seen is populated with one row per approval candidate + its bot-filter outcome; human_review_satisfied is derived, never persisted, and never blocks quiescence.
- enum-exhaustiveness over reviewer status / disposition where the derivation matches on them; fakes-not-mocks; mypy --strict; error-path coverage.
notes:
Lock-free status interaction (found in 8.3 review): FileStore.read now rewrites the file in place on a schema migration, so read is no longer side-effect-free once a migrator is registered. The §3.3 lock-free status reader must either migrate-in-memory without writing, or take the lock before a migrating read — do NOT ship an unlocked migrating reader. Latent today (MIGRATIONS empty). Code note left at prsession/file.py FileStore._migrate.
PR: https://github.com/scotthamilton77/agents-config/pull/140 (stacked on 8.9a). 3 commits, make ci-prgroom green 100% (467 tests). In-house 3-lens: envelope spec-correct (GO); fixed HIGH 404-enrichment-escape (mirror poll.py terminal mapping), test gap (no-gh-before-state), DRY (public BLOCKER_DISPOSITIONS), truth table. Ratified: human_review live gh fetch (data-view); PRECONDITION_NO_STATE exit-2. Awaiting Copilot + Scott merge.
8.11 settled: PR #140 (stacked on 8.9a), 5 commits, make ci-prgroom green 100% (470 tests). In-house 3-lens (envelope spec-correct GO; HIGH 404-enrichment-escape, test gap, DRY, truth table) + Copilot R1 (loginless approval edge + 2 plan-doc drifts) + R2 (lazy gh construction) + R3 (no-login docstring). 0 unresolved. Awaiting Scott merge. M1 CODE-COMPLETE.
Live M1 DoD evidence (run pre-merge, against real PR #138): `prgroom poll scotthamilton77/agents-config#138` -> exit 0, ingested 21 review items, phase advanced to fixes-pending. `prgroom status scotthamilton77/agents-config#138 --json` -> exit 0, rendered the full §4.6 merge-gate envelope, auto_merge_eligible:false (not quiesced). End-to-end poll->status capability demonstrated.
MERGED to main 4b64368 (PR #140 squashed, --admin). Stacked-merge: merged origin/main into branch + keep-ours conflict resolution; make ci-prgroom green 100% post-resolution. M1 integrated on main 4b64368 (470 tests).

title: [Impl] prgroom deterministic verbs — reply (+ CONTEXTUAL memory routing), resolve-escalated
description:
The two no-agent deterministic verb bodies that slot into the established run pipeline, plus the §8.3 CONTEXTUAL memory write-path. These ship in Phase 1 (NOT Phase 2) because a Phase-1 run that stopped before reply/resolve would regress today's full post-push flow. Per docs/plans/2026-05-12-prgroom-cli-design.md §3.2/§6.2/§8.3/§8.8/§5.

## In scope
- lifecycle/reply.py (§3 + §8.3) — _reply renders template+agent replies, posts via gh REST, marks replied=True per item, idempotent by (round, source-item) with NO persisted dedup flag; routes CONTEXTUAL memory — target_hint entries as thread replies; thread-less CONTEXTUAL decisions PATCHed into the sentinel-bounded ## Decisions block in the PR body (gh edit, not a git commit), block rewritten wholesale; non-CONTEXTUAL classes accepted-but-deferred (logged) as the forward-compat seam to the future repo-wide router.
- lifecycle/resolve_escalated.py + cli wiring (§3.2/§5) — resolve-escalated <pr> <item-id> --as <disposition> [--rationale] [--commits]: flip one escalated item to a terminal disposition, set decided_by='human:<git-user>', PRECONDITION_NO_ESCALATIONS when none; from human-gated advance to fixes-pending iff no escalated remain AND no failed AND last_error not in BlockingErrorCodes; does NOT clear cap/state-corrupt/failed gating; does NOT increment round; own per-verb lock.
- Fill the _reply body in the §3.3 pipeline established by the run-loop bead (the pipeline shell + the built _resolve already exist).

## Pin
The run pipeline shell and _resolve (thread-resolve) already exist (run-loop + lifecycle-verbs beads); THIS bead supplies the _reply body + resolve-escalated. The full-fidelity reply/resolve e2e lives here.

## Out of scope
- the §3.3 pipeline shell (run-loop bead); monitor-pr skill; the future 5-class repo-wide memory router (separate bead).

## Modules
lifecycle/reply.py, lifecycle/resolve_escalated.py, cli.py (resolve-escalated wiring).
acceptance_criteria:
- _reply posts replies for unreplied items and marks replied=True; no-op when all replied; idempotent by (round, source-item) with NO persisted dedup flag so a crash-and-re-run of the same round never double-posts (reply is NOT cross-PR-idempotent by design; the drain protocol relies on this).
- CONTEXTUAL entry with target_hint posts on the referenced thread (path-form uses file contents verbatim); thread-less CONTEXTUAL decisions merge into the ## Decisions block via gh PATCH (not a git commit, no phase change), keyed by (round, source-item), skip-existing-key AND never-delete-prior, block rewritten wholesale so a same-round re-run yields a BYTE-IDENTICAL block; a fit-test proves byte-identical re-run AND the skip-existing-key path for a different same-round item.
- non-CONTEXTUAL (UNIVERSAL/PROJECT/PLANNED/HISTORICAL) entries validate + log as deferred, route nothing in MVP, write nothing to user memories/AGENTS.md/docs, and preserve channel shape so the future repo-wide router extends without a contract change (no memory-route verb, no route-memories git commit).
- resolve-escalated flips exactly one item escalated->--as terminal, sets decided_by='human:<git-user>', PRECONDITION_NO_ESCALATIONS when no escalated item; advances human-gated->fixes-pending iff no escalated remain AND no failed AND last_error not in BlockingErrorCodes else stays human-gated; does NOT clear LIFECYCLE_HARD_CAP_EXCEEDED/STATE_CORRUPT/failed gating; does NOT increment round; acquires its own lock; re-runnable.
- reply/resolve-escalated precondition-gate correctly (PRECONDITION_NO_UNREPLIED/NO_ESCALATIONS); fakes-not-mocks with a fake gh; mypy --strict; error-path coverage; this bead carries the full-fidelity reply/resolve e2e.
notes:
From PR #142 deep review (OBS-10): prompt_loader.py in agent/ ships its own template loading because the shared reply-rendering engine this bead builds didn't exist yet (documented in prompt_loader.py header). When implementing the reply renderer here, unify: agent prompt templates should load through the same engine. See packages/prgroom/src/prgroom/agent/prompt_loader.py.
[session handoff 2026-06-19] Design spec: docs/plans/2026-06-16-prgroom-8.12-reply-resolve-escalated-design.md (revised; 2 Codex gpt-5.5 rounds). Branch prgroom-8.12-reply-resolve (main tree, ahead 2). OPEN before impl: mini-brainstorm rereview-resumability — reorder push->reply->resolve->rereview broke the cycle-relative _rereview_guard (push_uploaded_commits_this_cycle); transient reply/resolve failure after push skips rereview forever. Round-2 spec fixes pending: realpath path-containment, drop target_hint full-snapshot over-claim, add PRECONDITION_ITEM_NOT_ESCALATED/FIXED_NEEDS_COMMITS codes, c4-l3-lifecycle doc update. Cap rework split to abn9.8.18. Full handoff: HANDOFF-prgroom-8.12.md (scratch, gitignored).

title: [Impl] prgroom run loop — _run aggregator, _wait, run verb, escalation/human-review hooks, optional sweep
description:
The keystone _run pipeline + _wait + the run() public wrapper + the two cross-cutting flush hooks. Per docs/plans/2026-05-12-prgroom-cli-design.md §3.3/§3.5/§4.2/§4.7/§1.

## In scope
- lifecycle/run.py (§3.3) — _run verb-step pipeline (list[VerbStep], single shared handle_verb_error, NOT the transcribed pseudocode): bootstrap/read, entry-time external-transition probe + §3.5 cap re-arm, loop-top terminal-for-CLI flush, ordered _poll->_cluster->_fix->[pre-push cap-guard]->_push->[_rereview guarded]->_reply->_resolve, end-of-cycle resolution, success-clears-last_error, conditional _wait; run-aggregate per-internal write discipline (recoverable to last completed verb, no crash_recovery flag).
- run() public wrapper — acquire lock once, build Deps-injected lifecycle object, map propagated tier via exit_code_for_tier, release in finally; --interactive/--autonomous/--no-prework modes; idle stderr advisory in interactive at awaiting-review/idle.
- lifecycle/wait.py (§4.2) — lock-held blocking poll loop, interruptible sleep via threading.Event.wait(poll_interval), wake events (signal-cancel, poll-error, phase-moved, quiescence-trip), RUNTIME_CANCELLED_SIGINT(130)/SIGTERM(143) with signum capture, no mid-sleep lock release, no hard wait-timeout in MVP.
- lifecycle/escalation.py (§3.3/§4.7) — escalate_if_needed (one EscalationSink event per qualifying item + lifecycle-cap emit, flag-not-set-on-failure best-effort) and request_human_review_if_needed (gh human-review-required label POST gated by human_review_label_added + auto_request_human_review), fired at exactly the two _run exit sites.
- OPTIONAL: lifecycle/sweep.py cross-PR serial aggregator (per-PR locks, per-PR failure isolation, exit 0 unless sweep-level error) — include ONLY if it lands cheaply; if dropped, its acceptance is vacuously satisfied.

## Pipeline-slotting pin
_run wires the §3.3 pipeline against FOUNDATION SKELETONS of _reply (no-op until the deterministic-verbs bead) and the already-built _resolve (B7). The full-fidelity reply/resolve e2e lives in the deterministic-verbs bead, NOT here.

## Out of scope
- _reply / _resolve-escalated business logic (deterministic-verbs bead); status verb + merge-gate contract (status bead); monitor-pr skill; Skill A retirement.

## Modules
lifecycle/run.py, lifecycle/wait.py, lifecycle/escalation.py, lifecycle/sweep.py (optional), cli.py (run/wait wiring).
acceptance_criteria:
- run() acquires store.lock(pr) once, holds it through the whole cycle (no mid-cycle release), releases on any terminal-for-CLI exit or PROPAGATE via finally; concurrent run on same PR exits PRECONDITION_LOCK_HELD (75); the cycle is an ordered verb-step pipeline with handle_verb_error applied in exactly ONE place; _rereview is a guarded step (push_uploaded_commits_this_cycle AND has_required_reviewers_to_refresh).
- entry probe runs _poll once from QUIESCED/HUMAN_GATED; cap re-arm fires iff phase==HUMAN_GATED AND last_error==LIFECYCLE_HARD_CAP_EXCEEDED AND NOT(has_queued_fix_commits AND round>=max_rounds), clearing last_error + setting FIXES_PENDING + resetting lifecycle_escalation_filed/human_review_label_added; pre-push cap guard sets HUMAN_GATED+LIFECYCLE_HARD_CAP_EXCEEDED and refuses the push (commits NOT uploaded); each chained _-internal writes before returning, so killing the process mid-cycle leaves on-disk state at the last completed internal; no crash_recovery field exists.
- _run wires the pipeline against foundation no-op skeletons of _reply and the built _resolve; the e2e test drives the full loop with recorded gh responses WITH reply as a no-op skeleton (full-fidelity reply/resolve e2e is the deterministic-verbs bead's, not this one's).
- _wait assumes the lock is held, wraps interruptible Event.wait(poll_interval), checks the cancel token at loop-top AND inside the sleep, returns on phase-move/quiescence-trip(writes QUIESCED+quiesced_at)/signal; SIGINT->130 SIGTERM->143 neither sets last_error nor is RUNTIME_TRANSIENT; no hard wait-timeout in MVP.
- both flush hooks fire at exactly two sites (loop-top terminal check + before each PROPAGATE re-raise); escalate_if_needed emits one event per qualifying item then sets per-item escalation_filed (not set on emit failure); request_human_review_if_needed POSTs the label once per gate (short-circuits on auto_request false / should-request false / flag already set) and resets the flag on successful non-human-gated resolution; success cycle clears last_error + resets both dedup flags.
- exit_code_for_tier applied by run() to the propagated tier; sweep (if shipped) iterates open PRs serially with per-PR locks, isolates per-PR failure (logs ref+exit+first stderr line, continues), exits 0 unless a sweep-level enumeration error; sweep acceptance is vacuously satisfied if sweep is not shipped; fakes-not-mocks; mypy --strict.
notes:
Run-loop execution-context constraint (from M2 live demo, h8u6v): _fix dispatches a nested 'claude -p' agent that establishes its OWN sandbox making the worktree's git metadata read-only — independent of the parent Bash sandbox flag. When prgroom runs nested inside a Claude Code session the fix agent cannot 'git commit' (EPERM on .git/.../index.lock); the fix-contract correctly fails safe (disposition=failed, nothing fabricated, nothing pushed). The run loop / overnight scheduler MUST execute _fix where the nested agent can write git (bare scheduler/cron, or granted git-write). Production/non-nested runs unaffected.
PR #165 opened (worktree-prgroom-8.10-run-loop). Implemented _run (VerbStep pipeline + single handle_verb_error site), _wait, run/wait CLI verbs, escalation+human-review flush hooks, gh.add_label, reply no-op skeleton, shared has_queued_fix_commits. make ci-prgroom green: 819 tests, 99.80% cov, mypy --strict + ruff + pip-audit clean. In-house quality-reviewer applied (H1 honest has_queued read, H2 termination-contract doc, M4 sink-fail warn). sweep deferred (optional). Parked at merge gate awaiting human authorization; Copilot review pending.
Copilot review converged over 5 rounds, now APPROVED (✅ Ready to approve). Findings fixed: r1 entry-probe has_queued-before-guard (d474c85, real bug); r2 capped->pipeline_terminated rename (9c6e43a); r3 cap-rearm docstring alignment (4b0d1e5); r4 has_queued_fix_commits 404->tagged + _guarded_has_queued for out-of-pipeline reads (7571ef3, real GhNotFoundError-escape bug, 3 comments/1 root cause); r5 no new findings. All 6 threads replied + resolved; 0 unresolved. make ci-prgroom green: 822 tests, 99.80% cov. PR #165 MERGEABLE/CLEAN, 5 commits. Parked at merge gate awaiting human authorization.

title: [Impl] prgroom lifecycle core — phase model, predicates, quiescence, end-of-cycle resolver, verb-error policy, locking wrappers
description:
The pure, gh/git-free spine of the loop: phase model, predicates, quiescence, resolver, verb-error policy, locking wrappers. Per docs/plans/2026-05-12-prgroom-cli-design.md §2/§3.1-3.3/§4.1/§4.3.

## In scope
- lifecycle/__init__.py + state.py — PRPhase StrEnum (idle/awaiting-review/fixes-pending/quiesced/human-gated/merged), lifecycle fields, terminal-for-CLI {QUIESCED,HUMAN_GATED,MERGED} and graph-terminal {MERGED} predicates, zero-value bootstrap.
- lifecycle/predicates.py — has_queued_fix_commits, has_required_reviewers_to_refresh, push_uploaded_commits_this_cycle, new_lifecycle_gate_this_cycle, shared required-reviewer-flip predicate.
- lifecycle/quiescence.py (§4.1) — quiescence_predicate (G_REVIEWERS, G_CI, G_DISPOSITIONS, G_NO_BLOCKERS AND idle_threshold) as a pure clock-injected function; evaluate_reviewer_timeouts auto-decline (timeout-no-start/timeout-stalled, deadlines derived never stored); failing-gate identifiable for status.
- lifecycle/resolver.py (§3.2) — resolve_end_of_cycle_phase six-priority cascade (cap, FAILED, ESCALATED, commit-pushed, quiescence-trip at priority 5, else awaiting-review), first-match-wins, pure function, fixes-pending-only domain (per the §3.3 pseudocode; §3.1 "any non-terminal phase" is descriptive shorthand).
- lifecycle/verb_error.py — handle_verb_error -> VerbDisposition{CONTINUE,PROPAGATE} tier policy.
- Locking wrappers (§2) — each public verb acquires store.lock(ref) -> _-prefixed internal -> finally release; status is the unlocked-read carve-out; run acquires once; PRECONDITION_LOCK_HELD (exit 75) on contention; clock/randomness injection seams (no stdlib singletons in lifecycle/).

## Reconciliation pin
EXTENDS foundation's config.py with the §4.3 knobs and CONSUMES the foundation TOML loader (does not rebuild it); ships duration.py if foundation did not. reviewers dict is dict[str, ReviewerState] keyed by the gh-login STRING (the prose's capitalized "Identity" is loose wording; Identity is the ReviewItem sub-dataclass, not a reviewer key type).

## Out of scope
- The _-internal verb bodies (lifecycle-verbs bead); the _run aggregator (run-loop bead); real gh/git integration.

## Modules
lifecycle/__init__.py, prsession/state.py, lifecycle/predicates.py, lifecycle/quiescence.py, lifecycle/resolver.py, lifecycle/verb_error.py, config.py, duration.py.
acceptance_criteria:
- PRPhase has exactly the 6 members; canonical strings tested at a user-facing boundary (status output) not at the enum definition; terminal-for-CLI predicate true only for {QUIESCED,HUMAN_GATED,MERGED}; graph-terminal true only for {MERGED}; bootstrap sets schema_version=1/phase=IDLE/round=0/empty SHAs/reviewers={}/items=[]/last_error=None/lifecycle_escalation_filed=False.
- quiescence_predicate true iff G_REVIEWERS (every required reviewer status in {review_found,declined}) AND G_CI (ci_state in {success,absent} for last_pushed_head_sha) AND G_DISPOSITIONS AND G_NO_BLOCKERS (no escalated/failed) AND now-last_activity_at>=idle_threshold; pure, clock injected; evaluate_reviewer_timeouts sets declined+declined_reason for review_start/finish_timeout with deadlines derived per-eval; resumable across a crash gap.
- resolve_end_of_cycle_phase: P1 cap->HUMAN_GATED+LIFECYCLE_HARD_CAP_EXCEEDED; P2 any FAILED->HUMAN_GATED; P3 unresolved ESCALATED->HUMAN_GATED; P4 commit-pushed->AWAITING_REVIEW; P5 zero-push AND quiescence->QUIESCED (sets quiesced_at); P6 else AWAITING_REVIEW; first-match-wins verified by a table-driven test (e.g. cap+escalated -> cap wins); resolver domain is fixes-pending-only.
- handle_verb_error: VerbDisposition StrEnum named distinctly from §2 Disposition; RUNTIME_TRANSIENT->PROPAGATE+set last_error (no phase change); RUNTIME_TERMINAL_USER/STATE_*->PROPAGATE+HUMAN_GATED+lifecycle_escalation_filed=False; CONTRACT_AUDIT_FAILED->CONTINUE no last_error; RUNTIME_CANCELLED->PROPAGATE no mutation; unknown->PROPAGATE without store.write; a test enumerates every Tier member.
- each public verb acquires store.lock(ref) then delegates to its _-internal and releases in finally; status performs an unlocked read; a second invocation under a held lock exits 75 naming owner/repo#n and pid; the 5 §4.3 config knobs resolve CLI>env>TOML>default and the duration parser handles 30s/10m/1h30m.
- lifecycle/ contains no direct datetime.now(UTC) or RNG call (both injected); EXTENDS (not redefines) foundation config.py; mypy --strict; fakes-not-mocks; error-path coverage including lock-release-in-finally on raise.
notes:
PR: https://github.com/scotthamilton77/agents-config/pull/138 (stacked on 8.5/PR #137). 2 commits, make ci-prgroom green 100% (353 tests). In-house 3-lens review: spec GO; CRITICAL lock-contention (blocking stores -> non-blocking exit-75 + pid, lock_held_error relocated to errors.py) fixed; real contention tests added. SCOPE NOTE: realizing §2 concurrency posture expanded 8.8 into FileStore/InMemoryStore lock acquisition (flagged to Scott in PR body). Awaiting Copilot + Scott merge.
8.8 settled: PR #138 (stacked on 8.5), 4 commits, make ci-prgroom green 100% (358 tests). Reviews: in-house 3-lens (CRITICAL lock-contention: non-blocking exit-75 + pid realized, lock_held_error -> errors.py) + Copilot R1 (5: fd-leak, gate-predicate, config strictness, test hermeticity) + R2 (1: held firm — CONTRACT_AUDIT_FAILED must NOT clear last_error per §3.3; doc clarified) + R3 clean. 0 unresolved. SCOPE NOTE flagged to Scott: 8.8 expanded into store-adapter lock acquisition. Awaiting merge.
MERGED to main 8ed75ca (PR #138 squashed, --admin). Stacked-merge: merged origin/main into branch + keep-ours conflict resolution; make ci-prgroom green 100% post-resolution. M1 integrated on main 4b64368 (470 tests).

title: [Impl] prgroom lifecycle read verb — _poll + read path (over gh/git) [8.9a]
description: _poll verb: fetch PR review state via the gh adapter, ingest review items + reviewers, update PRGroomingState. Read-only — no GitHub writes. Read slice of the 8.9 read→stage→write re-cut. Depends on the gh/git protocol layer (8.5) and lifecycle core (8.8); does NOT depend on the agent-dispatch arm. Milestone M1 (read-only PR inspection + status). See prgroom impl grinder plan §3.
acceptance_criteria:
- _poll bootstrap (last_poll_sha=='') sets round=max(round,1) on non-empty HEAD, leaves round=0 on empty HEAD; attribution branch: new_head==last_pushed_head -> no round bump, reviewers untouched; else round++ + flip required review_found->not_requested; idempotent no-op on unchanged SHA; phase transitions per §3.2 poll row verified against InMemoryStore + recorded-gh fake; maps RUNTIME_GH_TRANSIENT/GH_TERMINAL/GRAPHQL_FAILED.
- _cluster sets cluster_id on every unclustered item, no-op when all clustered, no phase change, atomic write before return; _resolve resolves only {FIXED,ALREADY_ADDRESSED}+unresolved and marks resolved=True, no-op otherwise, GraphQL failure -> RUNTIME_GRAPHQL_FAILED.
- _fix idempotent (skips dispositioned items, reprocesses disposition is None); sets one of the 7 DispositionKind per item; snapshot assembled immediately before dispatch with recurrence computed at assembly never stored; audit failure flips item to FAILED + raises CONTRACT_AUDIT_FAILED; transient agent failure -> RUNTIME_AGENT_TIMEOUT/UNAVAILABLE.
- _push has_queued_fix_commits via gh headRefOid vs git rev-list <remote>..HEAD; round++ exactly once per successful push, no bump on zero-commit push, partial-write does not double-increment; post-push flips required+review_found reviewers to not_requested leaving {requested,in_progress,declined}; one-line stderr warning when push reaches MAX_ROUNDS; push rejection -> RUNTIME_PUSH_REJECTED (77), network -> RUNTIME_GIT_TRANSIENT (75); cap REFUSAL is NOT in _push.
- _rereview re-requests required reviewers in {not_requested,declined} via gh remove+re-add, no-op (exit 0) when none, idempotent, no phase change, gh failure -> RUNTIME_GH_TRANSIENT/GH_TERMINAL.
- Consumes B3's gh/git Protocols and B5's audits; integration tests use real git on fixture repos + recorded gh responses; each _-internal writes state atomically before returning (run-aggregate recoverability); mypy --strict.
notes:
PR: https://github.com/scotthamilton77/agents-config/pull/NN (stacked on 8.8). 8 commits, make ci-prgroom green 100% (417 tests). In-house 3-lens: spine spec-correct; fixed §4.1 engagement gaps (HIGH last_review_at resumability; REVIEW_FOUND from approval; reply-to-id parent; CI coverage; help; fixtures). Awaiting Copilot + Scott merge.
8.9a settled: PR #139 (stacked on 8.8), 11 commits, make ci-prgroom green 100% (429 tests). Reviews: in-house 3-lens (§4.1 engagement: last_review_at resumability HIGH, REVIEW_FOUND-from-approval, reply-to-id, CI coverage) + Copilot R1 (URL fragment parse; §4.1 DECLINED/REVIEW_FOUND reconcile; auto-decline/last_activity_at held-firm) + R2 (real quiescence-breaking idempotency bug) + R3 (404 blast-radius across all PR/repo reads; CI error->failure). 0 unresolved. Awaiting Scott merge.
MERGED to main ccc0c77 (PR #139 squashed, --admin). Stacked-merge: merged origin/main into branch + keep-ours conflict resolution; make ci-prgroom green 100% post-resolution. M1 integrated on main 4b64368 (470 tests).

title: [Impl] prgroom installer ownership — install.sh uv-tool-install + console-script on PATH (the §6.1 gate)
description:
scripts/install.sh ownership of the prgroom install lifecycle — the HARD PREREQUISITE §6.1 gates every skill-thinning on ("installable means this install.sh path"). Split out from the cutover bead so the gate lands early and can build in parallel with the verb work. Per docs/plans/2026-05-12-prgroom-cli-design.md §7.2.

## In scope
- scripts/install.sh owns the prgroom install: `uv tool install ./packages/prgroom` (idempotent, `--force` on upgrade, guarded by a uv-present check; graceful skip when uv absent), ensuring the `prgroom` console-script is on PATH.
- `install.sh --prune` removes prgroom via `uv tool uninstall` when the package leaves the source tree, consistent with how --prune removes other orphaned deploy outputs.

## Trap to avoid
install.py is an early partial port and is NOT authoritative for the prgroom install path — do NOT add prgroom-install logic to install.py. install.sh stays the source of truth until the port catches up.

## Relationship to foundation
Foundation (abn9.8.1) produces the installable artifact (the console-script entry point + ci-prgroom target). THIS bead wires install.sh to actually install/uninstall it. The two together satisfy §6.1's "installable first" gate that the Phase-1 cutover depends on.

## Out of scope
- monitor-pr skill, Skill A retirement, reference repointing (cutover bead).

## Modules
scripts/install.sh.
acceptance_criteria:
- After scripts/install.sh runs, the prgroom console-script resolves on PATH via `uv tool install ./packages/prgroom` (idempotent, --force on upgrade, uv-present-guarded, graceful skip when uv absent).
- `install.sh --prune` removes prgroom via `uv tool uninstall`.
- NO prgroom-install logic is added to install.py (install.py remains non-authoritative for this path).
- The install path is exercised by a smoke check (entry-verify / console-script resolves) so §6.1's "installable first" gate is mechanically demonstrable before any skill thinning.
notes: Implementation complete — PR #175 (feat/prgroom-installer-ownership). install.sh now owns the prgroom uv-tool-install/uninstall lifecycle (design §7.2). Copilot review converged (1 comment → fixed in 36f8865, thread resolved). make ci-installer green; PR MERGEABLE, CI SUCCESS. Awaiting merge authorization — leaving bead in_progress until merge.
close_reason: Merged via PR #175 (squash 6ecde46). install.sh owns the prgroom uv-tool-install/uninstall lifecycle (design §7.2) — the §6.1 'installable first' gate. Unblocks Phase-1 cutover (8.13).

title: [Impl] prgroom agent audits — cluster validator, fix commit/disposition audit, memory-channel + containment audit
description:
The contract-audit layer that enforces Cluster/Fix invariants and the §8.6 memory-channel rules. Per docs/plans/2026-05-12-prgroom-cli-design.md §5/§8.6.

## In scope
- agent/errors.py — CONTRACT_CLUSTER_MALFORMED/COVERAGE, CONTRACT_FIX_MALFORMED/ORPHAN_COMMIT/UNREACHABLE_SHA/AUDIT_FAILED codes, all mapped to the CONTRACT_AUDIT_FAILED tier (exit 65), surfacing via per-item disposition.kind=FAILED (NOT state.last_error).
- agent/cluster.py + cluster_audit.py — cluster input-JSON assembly, output validator (schema-conformant, every input item in exactly one cluster, unique ids, non-empty rationale), retry-once then per-item degenerate-cluster fallback.
- agent/fix.py + fix_audit.py — fix input-JSON assembly (contract_version, pr, cluster_id, item_gh_ids, items + per-item recurrence, pr_detail_path/branch_state_path/memory_dir/response_outbox_dir, NO root_cause_note), serial per-cluster dispatch, output parse, SHA-reachability + orphan-commit + disposition+evidence audit, git-stash isolation preserving contamination on failure.
- agent/memory_audit.py (§8.6) — memory[] channel validation (exactly one of content|path, 5-class classification, optional target_hint thread-routability), memory_dir containment audit (absolute/.. escape -> FAILED + EscalationSink, security-relevant HARD fail), declared-but-unwritten -> soft stderr warning.

## Pin
Memory-channel violations reuse the tier-level CONTRACT_AUDIT_FAILED (no distinct CONTRACT_MEMORY_* code is registered in §3.7 — do not invent one).

## Out of scope
- EscalationSink Protocol/adapters (foundation); the escalate_if_needed emitter (run-loop bead); memory write/route actuation (deterministic-verbs bead); snapshot assembly (lifecycle-verbs bead).

## Modules
agent/errors.py, agent/cluster.py, agent/cluster_audit.py, agent/fix.py, agent/fix_audit.py, agent/memory_audit.py.
acceptance_criteria:
- All six CONTRACT_* codes registered verbatim per §3.7, all mapping to CONTRACT_AUDIT_FAILED (exit 65); audit failures surface via per-item disposition.kind=FAILED with rationale set and do NOT set state.last_error (test asserts both).
- cluster validator: schema violation -> CONTRACT_CLUSTER_MALFORMED, coverage violation -> CONTRACT_CLUSTER_COVERAGE; on malformed/missing-item/timeout retry once then fall back to per-item degenerate clusters; parametrized tests cover each invariant + the fallback path.
- fix audit: fixed requires >=1 commit reachable pre-cluster..post-HEAD (CONTRACT_FIX_UNREACHABLE_SHA on miss); already_addressed requires SHAs predate baseline AND reachable; every commit in range claimed by exactly one item else CONTRACT_FIX_ORPHAN_COMMIT; skipped|deferred|wont_fix|escalated|failed each require non-empty rationale; orphan commits trigger git-stash isolation; violation re-classifies item to FAILED + signals EscalationSink emit; malformed output -> CONTRACT_FIX_MALFORMED; table-driven against fixture git histories.
- memory audit: each entry sets exactly one of content|path (both/neither rejected); classification in {UNIVERSAL,PROJECT,PLANNED,HISTORICAL,CONTEXTUAL} (unknown -> CONTRACT_AUDIT_FAILED for that item); CONTEXTUAL target_hint must reference a real snapshot thread node-id; a memory_writes path resolving outside memory_dir (absolute or ..) flips cluster items to FAILED + fires EscalationSink (never soft-failed); non-CONTEXTUAL accepted-but-deferred (logged); declared-but-unwritten -> soft stderr warning; parametrized tests cover exclusivity, each class, routability, path escape, declared-but-missing.
- fakes-not-mocks: a fake orchestrator returning canned JSON drives the fix tests; mypy --strict; error-path coverage on each audit guard.
notes:
DELIVERY IN PROGRESS (PR #147, base main, branch worktree-prgroom-8.7-agent-audits, head 352e71d). Implemented + 3-lens reviewed (caught 1 critical coverage hole + 2 majors, all fixed). wait-for-pr-comments: Copilot rounds 1-4 ALL fixed = 8 real findings total (r1: type-lenient from_dict + root-dir containment; r2: relative-path containment anchoring + §8.6 ErrorCode doc; r3: orphan-bypass via GHOST rows + cluster-flip rationale detail; r4: unwritten anchoring + orphan BLOCK severity). Gate green: 646 passed, 100% line+branch. Cap extended to 5 total rounds per human (Scott) on round 3. RESUME: re-review already triggered context is stale — trigger round-5 re-review on head 352e71d (request-rereview.sh + poll); if a 5th review arrives it's the cap round (escalate new FIX items, do not fix); if clean -> Phase 7 write inventory + Phase 8 reply-and-resolve-pr-threads across all 8 threads (several now outdated) -> park at merge gate (D3, human). NOT merged. Worktree intact for iteration.
DELIVER COMPLETE (parked at merge gate). PR #147 head=3806f75, base main, gate green (647 pass, 100% line+branch). Copilot: 5 rounds, 9 findings, ALL fixed+committed+replied+resolved (0 unresolved threads). Round-5 (cap round) finding 3408100705: §8.6 swept-item rationale prefix divergence — DECIDED to fix (one-line, zero-risk spec-alignment) rather than escalate; honored cap by NOT triggering round 6. NOT merged — D3 human gate. Worktree intact.

title: [Impl] prgroom agent dispatch — Cluster/Fix contracts, subprocess wrapper, provider-chain fallback, token usage
description:
The src/prgroom/agent dispatch logic. Foundation (abn9.8.1) owns the Cluster/Fix contract Protocols + data types; THIS bead ships the dispatch LOGIC against them. Per docs/plans/2026-05-12-prgroom-cli-design.md §5/§8.2.

## In scope
- CONSUME the Cluster/Fix contract Protocols + data types (ClusterContract, FixContract, Cluster, FixResult, Recurrence, contract_version) defined in the foundation bead (abn9.8.1) — this bead ships the implementation against that interface, not the interface itself.
- agent/subprocess_runner.py — subprocess lifecycle wrapper (JSON-input-by-path, per-contract time budget, cancel-token threading.Event kill, per-invoker shapes for claude/codex/opencode/ollama).
- agent/dispatcher.py — provider-chain fallback ladder consuming the foundation TOML loader (cluster default ollama+Gemma->claude haiku high->codex-mini; fix default opus[1m] xhigh->codex gpt-5.5 write=true); --cluster-model/--fix-model overrides; both-fail surfaced for failed-disposition + escalation.
- agent/usage.py — per-invocation token-usage JSONL emitter to $XDG_STATE_HOME/prgroom/usage.jsonl (baseline capture, no aggregation).
- agent/prompts/{cluster,fix}.tmpl + prompt_loader.py — contract prompt templates loaded once via the shared reply-rendering template engine, with PRGROOM_PROMPTS_DIR override.

## Resolves discrepancy #1
Foundation's "Cluster/Fix implementations out of scope" vs §6.7 "Cluster+Fix contracts as Phase-1 dependency": foundation ships the @runtime_checkable Protocols + contract data types (the interface); THIS bead ships the dispatch logic; the audits land in the agent-audits bead. No bead redefines the Protocols.

## Out of scope
- The Cluster/Fix contract Protocols + data types themselves (foundation, abn9.8.1); Cluster/Fix audit + validators (agent-audits bead); lifecycle wiring of _cluster/_fix; snapshot assembly / gh legwork (lifecycle).

## Modules
agent/subprocess_runner.py, agent/dispatcher.py, agent/usage.py, agent/prompts/, agent/prompt_loader.py.
acceptance_criteria:
- The dispatcher's concrete provider STRUCTURALLY satisfies the foundation ClusterContract/FixContract Protocols (mypy --strict; a fit-test asserts the fit); the foundation-defined Recurrence (frozen+slots) carries reopened/attempt_count/prior_disposition/prior_commits/first_seen_round; contract_version is serialized into both Cluster and Fix JSON I/O.
- subprocess wrapper writes contract JSON to a temp file passed by path, enforces a per-contract time budget (kills child + raises timeout-tagged error on overrun), and terminates the child when the cancel-token Event is set; a fake subprocess proves kill-on-timeout and kill-on-cancel without spawning real CLIs.
- dispatcher resolves the per-contract chain from the foundation loader; fallback triggers exactly on binary-absent-from-PATH, quota/auth/network exit, or per-contract timeout; --cluster-model/--fix-model override the TOML chain; primary+fallback both-fail surfaces a caller-mappable failure; table-driven tests cover primary-success / primary-absent->fallback / primary-timeout->fallback / all-fail.
- usage emitter appends one JSONL record {ts,pr,contract,provider,model,input_tokens,output_tokens,duration_ms,outcome} per invocation honoring XDG_STATE_HOME; absent usage line is a no-op not an error.
- cluster+fix prompt templates render a contract dataclass with no unresolved placeholders via the single shared engine; a matching filename in PRGROOM_PROMPTS_DIR wins over the shipped template; mypy --strict; fakes-not-mocks.
notes:
PR: https://github.com/scotthamilton77/agents-config/pull/142 (base main; M2 stack bottom). make ci-prgroom green @ 100% line/branch; passed 3-lens adversarial review (8 findings fixed: ollama-stdin blocker, all-providers-fail tier 77->75, fix-ladder ValueError fall-through, reap+SIGTERM-grace, +3 test-quality). Commit 7211b42. NOT merged (D3), NOT closed (D4).
Plan correction noted at deep review (OBS-11): the in-scope text says Recurrence is CONSUMEd from foundation (8.1), but foundation never shipped it — this bead defines it in agent/recurrence.py per design spec §8.2. Plan was wrong, implementation is right; placement as a contract-input shape in agent/ matches §8.2's derived-not-persisted rule.
DELIVERY STATE (2026-06-12): PR #142 head 3600452. 6 Copilot rounds (14 findings) + in-house 3-lens review (8) + fable deep holistic review (2 MAJOR / 6 MINOR / 3 OBS, all actionable items fixed: usage emit hook, cancel plumbing, ollama inline payload per Scott's option-a call, public config imports, per-provider timeout key, tail-keeping truncation, OSError fallthrough, lenient stdout JSON, strict runner fake, unknown-key warning) + round-7 TOCTOU fix. Gate green at head: 551 passed, 100% line+branch. All 15 review threads replied + resolved. Discovered work: agents-config-25rmt (token parser), agents-config-qptb4 (real-binary smoke). AWAITING HUMAN MERGE (D3).
MERGED: PR #142 squash-merged to main as 587afb2 (2026-06-13). Gate green at final head 3600452 (551 passed, 100% line+branch, mypy --strict, pip-audit clean). All 15 review threads replied + resolved. Closed on human-authorized merge.

title: [Impl] prgroom gh/git Protocol adapter layer — boundary infra with recorded-response fakes
description:
The gh/git boundary-adapter layer every lifecycle verb depends on. Split out from the verb bead because it is foundational boundary infra with independent test value (the §7.6 "mock only at the system boundary" seam). Per docs/plans/2026-05-12-prgroom-cli-design.md §7.6 + §3 verb I/O.

## In scope
- gh/ adapter behind a @runtime_checkable Protocol — gh REST + GraphQL via `gh` subprocess shell-out (NOT a vendored API client); structurally satisfies its Protocol (no inheritance).
- git/ adapter behind a @runtime_checkable Protocol — git via subprocess (rev-list, headRefOid comparison, push, stash).
- Recorded-response fakes for gh (responses/respx-style canned JSON fixtures under tests/fixtures/) and a real-git-on-fixture-repo path for git integration tests.
- Runtime error taxonomy surfaced to callers and mapped to §3.7 codes: RUNTIME_GH_TRANSIENT / RUNTIME_GH_TERMINAL / RUNTIME_GRAPHQL_FAILED (gh) and RUNTIME_GIT_TRANSIENT / RUNTIME_PUSH_REJECTED (git).

## Why its own bead
Everything in the lifecycle-verbs and run-loop beads imports these Protocols; the only mock point in the whole package is this subprocess/HTTP system boundary (§7.6 "no mocks of code we own"). Building it first de-risks the verb work and gives the recorded-response fakes standalone fit-tests.

## Out of scope
- Verb bodies that call these adapters (lifecycle-verbs bead); the agent subprocess runner (agent-dispatch bead, distinct subprocess concern).

## Modules
gh/ (package), git/ (package), tests/fixtures/ (recorded gh responses + fixture repos).
acceptance_criteria:
- gh and git adapters each sit behind a @runtime_checkable Protocol and STRUCTURALLY satisfy it (mypy --strict verifies the fit at type-check time; no subclassing).
- The ONLY mocked surface is the subprocess/HTTP system boundary: gh tests drive recorded JSON responses (responses/respx), git tests run real git against fixture repos under tests/fixtures/ — no mock of any code we own.
- gh adapter maps gh failures to RUNTIME_GH_TRANSIENT vs RUNTIME_GH_TERMINAL vs RUNTIME_GRAPHQL_FAILED; git adapter maps to RUNTIME_GIT_TRANSIENT vs RUNTIME_PUSH_REJECTED — the §3.7 codes the verb bead consumes (assert each mapping).
- Runtime deps stay thin (typer + stdlib; gh via subprocess, not a vendored client); a fit-test per adapter exercises the public surface against its fake.
- mypy --strict; fakes-not-mocks; error-path coverage on each failure-classification branch.
notes:
PR: https://github.com/scotthamilton77/agents-config/pull/137 (stacked on 8.3 / PR #136; awaiting Copilot). 6 commits, make ci-prgroom green 100%. In-house 3-lens+mutation review (GO_WITH_FIXES) — 7 boundary-hardening fixes applied (locale, timeout, missing-binary, parse, 2 test gaps).
8.5 settled: PR #137 (stacked on 8.3), 9 commits, make ci-prgroom green 100%. Reviews: in-house 3-lens+mutation (7 fixes) + Copilot R1 (timeout default, plan-doc) + R2 (LANGUAGE/gettext locale, rest() type honesty) + R3 (_CwdRunner blast-radius: LANGUAGE+timeout, docstring) — all fixed. 0 unresolved.
