Loop broken at R3. Awaiting Scott merge at M1 boundary.
MERGED to main 9b93703 (PR #137 squashed, --admin). Stacked-merge: merged origin/main into branch + keep-ours conflict resolution; make ci-prgroom green 100% post-resolution. M1 integrated on main 4b64368 (470 tests).

title: [Impl] prgroom store residuals + runtime error model — adapter registry, schema-migration, error registry
description:
Store-layer residuals and the runtime error-tier model that every verb and the resolver import. Per docs/plans/2026-05-12-prgroom-cli-design.md §2/§3.6/§3.7.

## In scope
- prsession/registry.py — `--store file|bd` / `PRGROOM_STORE` selector resolving to a concrete adapter constructor (default file; `bd` resolves to a clear deferred/not-in-MVP terminal error).
- prsession/migrations.py + state.py read-path branch — migration registry `dict[int, Callable[[bytes],bytes]]` (empty in MVP), in-place rewrite via `write_atomic` on a registered migrator, `STATE_SCHEMA_UNKNOWN` (exit 78) trip when `schema_version` mismatches with no migrator.
- errors.py + lifecycle/errors.py — Tier StrEnum EXTENSION (RUNTIME_TRANSIENT/TERMINAL_USER/CANCELLED, CONTRACT_AUDIT_FAILED, STATE_CORRUPT, STATE_SCHEMA_UNKNOWN, LIFECYCLE_CAP) layered onto the foundation precondition tiers.
- TaggedError(tier, code, signum?), exit_code_for_tier, the full §3.7 stable error-code registry strings (what/why/how), and the BlockingErrorCodes constant.
- Wire the registry-selected Store into the CLI `--store` flag and `PRGROOM_STORE` env.

## Reconciliation pin
- This bead EXTENDS the single foundation `Tier` enum — it does NOT re-declare `PRECONDITION_USER_ERROR/NO_WORK/LOCK_HELD`. One Tier enum, one errors module owner; foundation defines the base, this layers runtime/contract/state/lifecycle tiers on.

## Out of scope
- Verb business logic; real gh/git integration; the 3-tier precondition model itself (foundation, abn9.8.1).

## Modules
prsession/registry.py, prsession/migrations.py, prsession/state.py, errors.py, lifecycle/errors.py.
acceptance_criteria:
- registry resolves 'file' to FileStore; default (no flag, no env) is file; --store wins over PRGROOM_STORE; unknown name is a terminal user-error precondition (non-zero, no stack trace); 'bd' resolves to a clear deferred error.
- Reading a state file whose schema_version != SCHEMA_VERSION with NO registered migrator trips STATE_SCHEMA_UNKNOWN (exit 78); a registered synthetic migrator is applied, the file is rewritten via write_atomic, then read proceeds; a raising migrator surfaces terminal, not silent corruption.
- Every §3.7 code string is registered with what/why/how; exit_code_for_tier maps USER_ERROR=2, NO_WORK=0, LOCK_HELD=75, RUNTIME_TRANSIENT=75, RUNTIME_TERMINAL_USER=77, RUNTIME_CANCELLED=128+signum, CONTRACT_AUDIT_FAILED=65, STATE_CORRUPT|STATE_SCHEMA_UNKNOWN=78, LIFECYCLE_CAP=0, default=1.
- PRECONDITION_NO_WORK set is EXACTLY the 6 NO_* codes; PRECONDITION_NO_AUTH and PRECONDITION_NO_PR_DETECTED resolve to USER_ERROR(2) despite the NO_ substring (regression test asserts no prefix-matching).
- BlockingErrorCodes == {LIFECYCLE_HARD_CAP_EXCEEDED, STATE_CORRUPT, STATE_SCHEMA_UNKNOWN, RUNTIME_GH_TERMINAL, RUNTIME_PUSH_REJECTED}; no STATE_LOCK_STALE/NOTICE_LOCK_STALE_CLEANED code exists (test asserts registry omits them).
- This bead EXTENDS foundation's single Tier enum (no PRECONDITION_* re-declaration); a unit test enumerates every Tier member to recover the exhaustiveness Python lacks; mypy --strict passes; fakes-not-mocks per the fit-test convention.

title: Fold §8 PR-memory deltas into prgroom HLDs + final cross-section audit
description:
Layer the Section 8 (PR-memory) deltas onto the Python-converted prgroom HLDs (after fca6.14's Go→Python conversion lands), then run a final cross-section audit of every fleshed design section (§3 lifecycle, §4 quiescence, §5 dispatch, §6 migration, §7 build/test, §8 memory) against its HLD counterpart.

Source of truth = source spec §8 (PR memory management) + §5 (fix contract I/O).

--- fca6.5 PR-memory (Section 8) HLD deltas ---
Source of truth = source spec §8 (PR memory management) + §5 (fix contract I/O). NOTE: `recurrence` is COMPUTED at snapshot-assembly time and is NOT a persisted state field (derived from disposition history) — do NOT add it to the persisted-state ER in data-view.md; show it only in the contract/snapshot-input annotation.

Per-file (most -> least change):
- data-view.md — contract-data section: add the fix-output `memory` channel [{content|path, classification, target_hint}] and the snapshot-input `recurrence` object. Persisted-state ER UNCHANGED.
- c4-l3-agent-dispatch.md (stub) — FixContract output now includes the classified `memory` channel; add the Section-8 memory-routing note alongside the existing RCA forward-note.
- c4-l3-lifecycle.md — add (a) the complete-PR-snapshot assembly step before fix dispatch, (b) CONTEXTUAL->PR routing at reply time: thread reply + `## Decisions` PR-body PATCH via the gh adapter (NOT a git commit). No persisted dedup flag — the sentinel-bounded Decisions block is idempotent (keyed by (round, source-item)).
- sequences.md — Sequence 1 fix cycle: show snapshot-in, `memory[]` in fix output, and the Decisions-block PATCH after resolve. Sequences 2-4 unaffected (no fix work).
- c4-l2-container.md — fix-contract description: note PR snapshot as input and `memory` channel in output.
- c4-l3-prsession.md (stub) — note the snapshot-assembly read path; schema stays version 1 (no persisted memory/recurrence fields).
- state-machine.md — one-line note: post-resolve CONTEXTUAL->PR routing side-effect (parallel to the auto-label side-effect).
- NO CHANGE: index.md, c4-l1-context.md, c4-deployment.md.

Review follow-up (from fca6.14 quality review): also correct docs/architecture/prgroom/index.md:81 — its data-view synopsis advertises 'the §8 PR-memory schema skeleton', which only becomes accurate once this bead folds the §8 deltas into data-view.md. Make the synopsis match reality when §8 lands.

title: Convert prgroom HLDs from Go to Python (ratified-design graduation)
description:
Convert the prgroom architecture HLDs (docs/architecture/prgroom/) from Go to Python so they match the ratified design doc (docs/plans/2026-05-12-prgroom-cli-design.md). The HLDs were authored before the design doc's Go→Python conversion (#129) and still describe the abandoned Go architecture throughout — module paths, type signatures, function names, technology labels, code fences. The §8 PR-memory deltas and the final cross-section audit are tracked in a sibling bead, blocked on this one.

Conversion key (locked from the ratified design):
- Module paths: cmd/prgroom → src/prgroom/cli.py; internal/{lifecycle,agent,prsession,gh} → src/prgroom/{…}; NEW src/prgroom/git/ (worktree-aware git ops); internal/escalation folded into src/prgroom/lifecycle (the §1 layout box and §7 Protocol list give escalation no dedicated module; escalate_if_needed is a lifecycle hook).
- Function names: runLocked→run()+_run; pollLocked→_poll; clusterLocked→_cluster; fixLocked→_fix; pushLocked→_push; rereviewLocked→_rereview; replyLocked→_reply; resolveLocked→_resolve; waitLocked→_wait; quiescencePredicate→quiescence_predicate.
- Store methods: Read/Write/Lock/List/Delete → read/write/lock/list/delete.
- Idioms: "Go function"/"Go interface" labels → "Python function"/"Python Protocol"; go-gh / github.com/cli/go-gh/v2 → gh subprocess (subprocess.run); map[string]ReviewerState → dict[str, ReviewerState]; ```go → ```python; "single static Go binary" → "uv-installed Python console-script".
- Fix-contract model = opus[1m]/xhigh per §5 — HLDs already correct; preserve.

Files: all ~10 under docs/architecture/prgroom/ (c4-l1-context, c4-l2-container, c4-l3-lifecycle, c4-l3-agent-dispatch, c4-l3-prsession, data-view, sequences, state-machine, c4-deployment, index).
acceptance_criteria: Every file under docs/architecture/prgroom/ reflects the ratified Python design — src/prgroom/* module paths, run()/_run/_poll/… function names, Python Protocol/dict idioms, opus[1m] fix model, gh-subprocess (no go-gh), uv console-script (no static Go binary). Zero residual Go-isms (grep clean for Go binary / cobra / go-gh / internal/ / cmd/prgroom / //go:build / ```go fences). index.md cross-refs intact; mermaid diagrams still parse. §8 PR-memory deltas + final cross-section audit tracked in the sibling bead (PR 2).
notes: Delivered via PR #132 (squash commit e57e77f), merged 2026-06-07. All 10 HLDs converted Go->Python; zero residual Go-isms. PR-2 scope (Section 8 PR-memory deltas + final cross-section audit) tracked separately in fca6.15.

title: Simplify json_union to a semantic deep-merge; drop jq-byte-parity machinery
Capture resumable agent session ids in audit logs (enable session-resume + retrospection harvest)
description:
Cross-cutting capability (every agent activity, not just prgroom). Record a resumable session handle for each agent dispatch so a COMPLETED agent can be re-opened later with new instructions.

Use cases (deferred feature):
- Resume-with-instructions: 're-open codex session_id 12345 with: what could have prevented X?'
- Agent-to-agent communication when a later agent needs a prior agent's working context
- Async feedback-loop harvesting / retrospection: 'retrospect on your session; give three changes that would have reduced iterations / time-to-completion' — feeds the self-improvement loop (vision commitment: substitute adversarial cross-model review; persist context)

NEAR-TERM SEAM (do this when prgroom §5 dispatch/audit record is implemented, so no later contract rework):
- Record {runtime, session_id} per agent dispatch in the dispatch/audit log (and wherever the append-only audit JSONL is written).
- Each runtime exposes a resumable handle: claude -p returns session_id in --output-format json and supports --resume; codex/opencode have analogous session ids. Confirm exact per-runtime mechanism as part of this bead, NOT now.

DEFERRED (the actual feature): the resume verb/skill, the retrospection-harvest prompt templates, and any agent-to-agent routing. Overnight-autonomy / feedback-loop flavored (M4-adjacent), but the SEAM lands with prgroom (M3). Kept standalone because it spans both.
acceptance_criteria: Design settles: where session handles are captured per runtime; audit-record schema field; resume mechanism per runtime (claude/codex/opencode); the retrospection-harvest contract. Near-term seam (reserve {runtime, session_id} in the prgroom dispatch/audit record) documented so §5 implementation honors it.

title: Five-class memory routing taxonomy — replace 2-tier memory-routing.md
description:
Replace the current 2-tier memory-routing.md rule (repo-specific vs general) in src/user/.agents/rules/ with a 5-class, PURPOSE-based taxonomy. The agent that produces a durable fact classifies it; routing sends it to the right home.

Five classes and homes:
- UNIVERSAL  — any agent, any project, anytime needs it -> ~/.claude/memories/ (native/personal memory)
- PROJECT    — permanent to this codebase (convention/gotcha/rule) -> project AGENTS.md (root or folder-scoped)
- PLANNED    — knowledge needed for upcoming work -> attach to/create actual work via the WORK ADAPTER (augment or create work beads; NOT a raw bd memory, NOT a side store)
- HISTORICAL — a real, surprising trade-off worth recording -> ADR in docs/adr/
- CONTEXTUAL — only relevant to work in flight, for self/subagents/foreign agents picking up the work -> stored with the work in the most portable place (path-scoped AGENTS.md, the PR description, and/or a PR comment/thread reply)

A memory may carry overlapping classifications -> route to all applicable homes.

Relationship to prgroom (fca6.5): fca6.5 implements the PR-specific CONTEXTUAL slice — the PR (description/labels/threads) IS the portable contextual memory; the prgroom fix agent READS it, and its forward-looking notes are routed back to the PR BY prgroom (the agent never writes the PR directly). This bead generalizes routing to the other four classes across ALL agent work, not just PR grooming.

Forward-compat seam (least-YAGNI now): the fix contract exposes a SINGLE agent-declared memory channel. prgroom MVP routes only CONTEXTUAL->PR. This bead extends the router to UNIVERSAL/PROJECT/PLANNED/HISTORICAL targets.

Scope notes:
- No bd-as-memory store. PLANNED routes through the work abstraction/adapter, consistent with the project's quarantine-beads-behind-our-own-CLI direction.
- Decide skill-vs-rule ownership: a routing rule (replaces memory-routing.md) plus optional helper; the producing agent classifies.
acceptance_criteria: memory-routing.md replaced with the 5-class taxonomy; each class's home + write mechanism specified; overlap handling defined; PLANNED uses the work-adapter not raw bd; documented relationship to fca6.5's CONTEXTUAL/PR slice.

title: Surface merge-engine warnings + unknown-key errors through IOPort when wiring merge into staging
description:
Discovered during Epic E (collision matrix). The merge strategies are pure (merge(existing, incoming) -> StagedItem) and have no IOPort, so two surfacing concerns fall to the CONSUMER that wires the registry into staging (likely F.2 plugin overlay / the staging-collision path):

1. LastWinsWarnStrategy (JSONC/TOML) emits its collision notice via stdlib warnings.warn(UserWarning) — the only channel available given the protocol shape. The rest of the engine routes user-facing warnings through IOPort.warn() (io_port.py), and bash emits the JSONC/TOML collision via its 'warn' function (install.sh:459,463) to the normal output stream. The staging consumer MUST capture these warnings (e.g. warnings.catch_warnings) and re-emit via IOPort.warn(), or the notice may never reach the user / be silenced by a -W setting. Add an integration test asserting a JSONC/TOML collision produces a visible IOPort.warn transcript entry.

2. MergeRegistry.resolve() raises UnknownMergeKeyError (ValueError) for an un-wired (FileKind, namespace) — e.g. a NAMESPACED_MD file in an unexpected dir. Bash falls through resolve_collision's '*)' arm to 'err unknown file_type; exit 1' (install.sh:473-475). Both halt, but the consumer should catch UnknownMergeKeyError and surface it as an actionable fatal install error, not an uncaught traceback.

Source: quality-reviewer findings on the Epic E PR (approve_with_nits, findings 2 & 4).
close_reason: Delivered in PR #199 (squash debf678): merge observability + symlink parity + installignore test-hardening. make ci-installer green (607 passed, 99.77% cov); Copilot review clean.

title: merge-guard eligibility script counts reply comments as unseen → false-positive block
description:
check-merge-eligibility.sh computes total_comments via raw `length` of the /pulls/N/comments array (line ~163), which includes reply comments (in_reply_to_id set) and the PR author's/agent's own comments. After replying to N review threads, total inflates by N; unless --comments-seen is manually bumped to match, the script exits 2 (unseen_comments) as a false positive.

Observed on PR #125: 11 top-level review comments (all triaged) + 11 agent-posted thread replies = 22 total → script reported 11 unseen and blocked. Manual ground-truth check (top-level comments not in triaged set) showed 0 genuinely unseen.

Fix: filter the jq for total_comments to top-level reviewer comments — at minimum exclude `.in_reply_to_id != null`; ideally also exclude the PR author's own login (.user.login). 

Source: src/user/.agents/skills/merge-guard/check-merge-eligibility.sh. Regression test: src/user/.agents/skills/merge-guard/check-merge-eligibility_test.sh — add a case with reply comments present.
notes:
PR #207 opened (branch fix/nnqwg-merge-guard-reply-comments). Fix: filter total_comments jq to in_reply_to_id==null. Regression test added via gh stub. Awaiting review.
Copilot review complete (2 rounds). R1: 2 FIX comments (pagination undercount + stub query-string fragility) — both fixed in 327483f, replied + resolved. R2: clean, no new comments. 0 unresolved threads. PR #207 review-clean, awaiting merge authorization.

title: Revisit prgroom design + architecture docs; produce full implementation work breakdown
description:
Holistic implementation-planning pass over the prgroom design + architecture documentation, producing the full dependency-ordered work breakdown.

Once the design sub-designs are fleshed (lifecycle §3, quiescence §4, agent-dispatch §5, migration §6, build/test §7, PR-memory §8) and any `docs/architecture/` artifacts exist, read the design doc (`docs/plans/2026-05-12-prgroom-cli-design.md`) end-to-end and create the implementation `[Impl]` child beads under this epic (`agents-config-abn9.8`).

Must include:
- The **Phase 1** and **Phase 2** migration impl beads whose boundaries are defined in Section 6.7 (Phase 1: core verbs + `reply`/`resolve`/`resolve-escalated` + `run` loop + create `monitor-pr` + delete `wait-for-pr-comments`; Phase 2: retire `reply-and-resolve-pr-threads`).
- Impl beads for every other fleshed design section that lacks one.
- Dependency edges in build order (foundation → verbs → skill thinning → retirement), respecting the `abn9.8.1` foundation prerequisite and the §7 install path.

This bead is the deliberate "later" moment for filing the migration impl beads — they are NOT filed at sub-design closure (they would only sit blocked-on-blocked). File them here, when the design is complete and foundation is ready to start.
acceptance_criteria: A complete, dependency-ordered set of [Impl] beads exists under agents-config-abn9.8 covering every fleshed design section. Includes the Phase 1 + Phase 2 migration impl beads (Section 6.7 boundaries). No fleshed design section lacks a corresponding impl bead.
notes:
Architecture-HLD graduation — including the fca6.5 PR-memory deltas — is owned by agents-config-fca6.14 (capstone child of the design epic fca6), NOT this bead. This bead = the [Impl] work-breakdown only; it reads docs/plans/2026-05-12-prgroom-cli-design.md as input.
Breakdown delivered: 12 [Impl] beads abn9.8.3–abn9.8.14 filed under epic abn9.8, dependency-ordered (foundation → store/error-model + agent-dispatch + gh/git adapters + installer → audits + lifecycle-core → verbs → run-loop + status → deterministic verbs → Phase-1 cutover → Phase-2 retirement). Foundation-seam items (write_atomic, JSON ser, console-script-on-PATH, 80/70 coverage override, clock/RNG Protocols, InMemoryStore under tests/, EscalationSink.emit naming, single Tier enum, config loader, Cluster/Fix contract Protocols+types) folded into abn9.8.1 in place. Decisions: 12-bead reviewability-optimal granularity (vs §6.7's 2-bead spine); rollback unit = squash-merge Phase-1 series onto one revertible commit; EscalationSink.emit(); drop cluster memory_path from MVP. Source: design doc 2026-05-12-prgroom-cli-design.md §1–8 + HLD set; produced via 7-section parallel read + synthesis + adversarial critic (needs-revision fixes applied: split status/merge-gate, split gh/git adapters, split installer, fix reply/resolve pipeline-slotting, drop phantom settings.json edit in Phase 2).

title: Remove stale bash-parity references from installer code comments and docs
description:
During the C.1 implementation, inline comments were added to aid porting from bash: '# port of the bash classify_file', 'scripts/install.sh:486-505', '# Stages, in bash order:' etc. Once the Python rewrite is complete and install.sh is retired, these references become dead weight — misleading to future readers who won't have the bash context.

Scope:
- Audit packages/installer/src/ for all '# port of the bash', 'install.sh:NNN', 'bash order', and similar provenance comments.
- Remove comments that only make sense relative to the bash implementation.
- Retain any that document a non-obvious invariant (e.g. ordering requirements) but rephrase them to stand alone without the bash reference.
- Also audit docs/ for any spec/plan prose that cites bash line ranges as the authoritative spec, updating to reference the Python implementation or the architecture doc instead.

Do NOT run this until install.sh is retired (Epic H).
notes: TRIAGE VERDICT (read-only, 2026-06-20): DEFER_POST_H4 — bead hard gate (Do NOT run until install.sh retired) is UNMET. install.sh is still a real 1870-line impl (no exec-uv-run wrapper) AND still the LIVE golden-master parity oracle (golden_master/_runner.py:24,145,150 spawns it; Makefile:22-23). Scrubbing the port-of-the-bash / install.sh:NNN provenance comments now destroys the map every parity test + installer-design.md:388 rely on. CENSUS: src/ = 17 files, ~68 install.sh:NNN cites / 106 bash-provenance lines (staging.py:39/43/207, overlay.py:5-6, prune.py:3, prune_flow.py:3, sync.py:229-230, templates.py:66/149/209/225, model.py:30/108/165, backup.py:4/25/28, installer_toml.py:4, +cli/config/consent/json_union/run/claude/gemini/opencode). SCOPE REFINEMENTS: (1) bead scopes src/ only but 24 test files carry same cites -> MOST are the parity oracle and must SURVIVE post-H.4; only unit-test docstrings go stale -> keep-the-oracle carve-out, not blanket scrub. (2) docs/specs + docs/plans cites are DATED point-in-time -> leave frozen; only evergreen installer-design.md needs rephrasing, retaining refs that describe the retirement plan (installer-design.md:11,17,210,317,320). Blocked on H.4 (8.4).

title: Rename test_build_plan_collects_all_phases to reflect partial-phase intent
description:
The current name 'test_build_plan_collects_all_phases' implies the test asserts completeness across all phases, but the test was deliberately written without a plan.items count assertion (to avoid brittleness when new phases are added). The name contradicts the intent.

Suggested rename: something like 'test_build_plan_includes_shared_and_tool_phases' or 'test_build_plan_produces_items_from_multiple_phases' — conveys that phases are exercised without implying exhaustive phase enumeration.

Pick the name that best reflects what the test actually pins: that build_plan draws from both shared and tool-specific sources, not that it covers every phase exactly.

title: Adopt the 'read outside the producing run?' razor as a prose-vs-code/fact triage convention
description:
## What

Adopt a one-line triage rule for the Code-over-Prose / "scripts own determinism, skills own judgment" decision:

  "Is this artifact read by a human or agent OUTSIDE the producing run (a downstream agent, one of the four tool CLIs, future-Scott as a fresh reader, an onboarding reader)? If yes -> it stays prose. If no -> a code helper / executable assertion should own it."

## Why

The redesign already holds the instinct ("anything code can do better than agents moves out of prose into code"), but lacks a crisp, testable boundary for WHICH prose to keep. This razor supplies it, and for this repo it correctly predicts: skills, rules, agent personas, CONTEXT.md, HLDs STAY prose (they are the installed product, read downstream), while installer logic and skill-trigger correctness correctly live as code (pytest, run_eval.py). A re-bloat-prevention convention, it is.

## Scope

- Add the razor as guidance in the Code-over-Prose / instruction-rationalisation conventions (cite by concept, DYNAMIC-INCLUDE-safe -- no internal file paths).
- Note its applicability is general (skills, helpers, specs), not only the agent instruction context.

## Provenance

Captured 2026-06-01 from a critical evaluation of two external SDD/"facts-over-specs" articles (Wasowski). The "read by a human outside the team?" razor was the single most transferable tool in the articles. Bound here to the one applicable case (downstream/onboarding readers) -- the articles' other "keep prose" domains (external auditor, B2B partner) do not exist for a solo-architect config repo.

## Relation

Sharpens the M0 Code-over-Prose polarity decision (related-to M0 milestone); filed under instruction-context rationalisation (the active epic chartered to establish anti-re-bloat conventions).

title: Property/invariant AT tier: universally-quantified facts beyond example-based ATs
description:
## What

Add a property / invariant tier to the Atomic-AT model: machine-verifiable facts expressed as universally-quantified predicates (property-based testing) and pre/post/invariant contracts (Design-by-Contract), alongside today's example-based ATs.

## Why

The fact-layer (Atomic AT, Red Gate, Test-Author) is currently example-test-shaped: each AT pins one input/output case a human imagined. Example tests cannot catch the cases nobody imagined. The M4 overnight-autonomy goal removes the human who would otherwise notice the gap — so the fact-layer needs facts that generalise. A property ("for all sorted lists, binary_search is correct") is a strictly stronger, still-deterministic machine verdict. Published precedent: Hughes/Quviq found 25 bugs (incl. race conditions invisible to example tests) from ~450 LOC of property tests over 60k LOC of production Erlang.

## Deliverables (decomposes into)

- Extend the Atomic-AT format/schema to express a property/invariant AT
- Update the Atomic-AT Linter to validate the new tier
- Red Gate accepts a failing property as a valid red state
- Test-Author Agent can author property/invariant ATs
- Select + integrate a PBT library (e.g. Hypothesis for the Python toolchain)
- Update the writing-unit-tests skill with a property/invariant AT tier + when-to-use guidance

## Prerequisites (why blocked)

Cannot begin until the Atomic-AT model + Linter (PDLC Design Phase) and the Red Gate (PDLC Execution Pipeline) exist — this tier extends both. Hard-blocked on those epics.

## Provenance

Captured 2026-06-01 from a critical evaluation of two external SDD/BDD/"facts-over-specs" articles (Wasowski, Apr/May 2026) against the project's AT model. The articles were ~85-90% corroboration of existing architecture; this property/invariant tier was the one genuinely net-new, high-leverage idea — the project has zero property-based-testing footprint today (verified).

## Out of scope

Not a prose-spec or BDD/Cucumber framework adoption — the project deliberately rejects the .feature/step-def ceremony (no PM/BA audience). This is purely a stronger class of executable fact.

title: Add 'spec/diagram/code agreement' authoring constraint to shared instructions
description: Add a constraint to the shared INSTRUCTIONS.md.template <constraints> block capturing the spec/diagram/code three-way agreement discipline: design specs + architecture diagrams (HLD) are the DESIRED-state authority; code is CURRENT state evolving to conform. They need not match on detail (code outpaces spec — expected), but on overlapping elements they must agree. When authoring a derived artifact (diagram/HLD) for a system under active development, draw from BOTH spec and code; never invent detail the code already defines; adopt code detail where it's merely more granular; and where spec/HLD and code CONTRADICT on a shared element, STOP, reject the task, and escalate (an escalate-conflicting case per the canonical decision matrix). The constraint references escalate-conflicting by concept (no file-path citations — the template flattens via DYNAMIC-INCLUDE). Generalized — no embedded session/PR history.
acceptance_criteria: New bullet present in the <constraints> block of src/user/.agents/INSTRUCTIONS.md.template; references escalate-conflicting by concept; cites no project-internal file paths; voice/length matches existing constraints; no session/PR history embedded; new-constraint self-sweep run against the PR's own changed files before push.
close_reason: Delivered via PR #118 (squash-merged as c137442) — 'Spec, diagram and code agreement' constraint added to shared INSTRUCTIONS.md.template.

title: Installer HLD artifact set (docs/architecture/installer/)
description: Produce the high-level design (HLD) artifact set for the Python installer rewrite, mirroring the prgroom HLD set (fca6.12). The installer-design.md companion spec already lives in docs/architecture/installer/; this bead adds the visual/structural artifacts the spec lacks: C4 L2 (Container), C4 L3 (Component / internal package structure), sequence diagrams (install execution flow + collision merge + sync decision + prune), a data-view (StagingPlan / StagedItem / Provenance / MergeStrategy / FileKind / Orphan / Config), and an index.md orientation file. L1 (too thin — single CLI in an obvious ecosystem) and Deployment (trivially local) are intentionally skipped. Diagrams are Mermaid, derived from installer-design.md, amended in place as the design evolves.
acceptance_criteria: index.md present and lists the artifact set; c4-l2-container.md, c4-l3-engine.md, sequences.md, data-view.md present and render as valid Mermaid; each file carries the standard header (Up/Source bead/Source spec) + glossary + 'What this does NOT show' + cross-references; installer-design.md references the new index.md; all internal relative links resolve.

title: Design decision: resolve-escalated cap-clearance UX — --max-rounds re-run vs --extend-cap flag
description:
## Context

When prgroom hits the hard cap AND has escalated items, the operator faces a two-step recovery:
1. Run `resolve-escalated` to flip item dispositions (escalated → terminal)
2. Raise `--max-rounds` AND re-run to clear `LIFECYCLE_HARD_CAP_EXCEEDED` from `BlockingErrorCodes`

Step 2 is required because `LIFECYCLE_HARD_CAP_EXCEEDED` is explicitly in `BlockingErrorCodes` (§3.2 resolve-escalated cell), so `resolve-escalated` alone does NOT move phase out of `human-gated` — even when all escalated items are cleared.

The design doc explicitly accepts this: 'Does NOT clear LIFECYCLE_HARD_CAP_EXCEEDED (requires --max-rounds raise + re-run).' That is a deliberate decision, but it is the same operator friction that motivated agents-config-3e95c — just with a different UX tax.

## Design question

Is `--max-rounds raise + re-run` sufficient recovery UX for the cap+escalation scenario?

OR should `resolve-escalated` support an `--extend-cap N` flag (or equivalent) that:
- Removes `LIFECYCLE_HARD_CAP_EXCEEDED` from `state.LastError` (clearing the BlockingErrorCode gate)
- Increments `MaxRounds` by N (or sets it to Round + N) in persisted state
- Allows phase to exit `human-gated` in a single operator command rather than two

## Options

**(A) Accept current design** — `--max-rounds raise + re-run` is the documented path; it is explicit and auditable. Document it clearly in the operator recovery guide (§3.6/§3.7). No code change.

**(B) `resolve-escalated --extend-cap [N]`** — adds cap-clearance to the verb when the operator explicitly opts in. Clears `LIFECYCLE_HARD_CAP_EXCEEDED` from `state.LastError` and bumps persisted `MaxRounds`. Allows single-command recovery: `prgroom resolve-escalated <pr> <item-id> --as skipped --extend-cap 1`.

**(C) New `reset-cap` verb** — dedicated operator escape hatch for cap-clearance, separate from escalation resolution. Cleaner separation of concerns; more discoverable; slightly more ceremony.

## Constraints

- Option (B) or (C) must not silently increase the cap on non-operator invocations — must be explicit opt-in
- Must not conflict with the BlockingErrorCodes contract (which exists to prevent silent runaway loops)
- Decision should be made before fca6.10 (lifecycle state machine impl) goes to code, as it affects the resolve-escalated implementation

## Definition of Done

- One of A/B/C selected with rationale recorded here
- If B or C: §3.2 resolve-escalated matrix cell + §3.7 error registry updated in the design doc; fca6.10 implementation scope updated accordingly
- If A: operator recovery guide section drafted or cross-referenced in §3.6/§3.7
close_reason: Decision A' delivered + merged in PR #115 (squash 824f1fa). Cap re-arm: a raised --max-rounds clears LIFECYCLE_HARD_CAP_EXCEEDED via the §3.3 run entry probe, fixing the human-gated recovery deadlock. Chosen over B (couples cap-extension into resolve-escalated) and C (redundant reset-cap verb). Implementation carried by fca6.10 (scope + 3 fit-tests recorded there). Docs: design doc §3.1/3.2/3.3/3.5/3.6/3.7 + prgroom architecture set.

comments.author: Scott Hamilton
comments.text:
DECISION: Option A′ — fix the run-from-human-gated recovery so a raised --max-rounds actually re-arms the hard cap. Chosen over B (resolve-escalated --extend-cap) and C (reset-cap verb).

Finding that reframed the call: the bead's framing (1-command vs 2-command ergonomics) was moot — the documented recovery ("raise --max-rounds and re-run") did not work at all. §3.3's run-from-human-gated only re-entered the cycle when poll observed an EXTERNAL push/merge; a bare cap-raise is invisible to poll, so LIFECYCLE_HARD_CAP_EXCEEDED never cleared and the phase stayed human-gated. Deadlock — hitting even the zero-escalation (pure fix-churn) case, where Option B doesn't even apply (resolve-escalated returns PRECONDITION_NO_ESCALATIONS).

A′ mechanism: the §3.3 entry probe re-evaluates the cap. When phase==human-gated AND LastError==LIFECYCLE_HARD_CAP_EXCEEDED AND the cap no longer trips (NOT(has_queued_fix_commits AND Round>=MaxRounds)), clear LastError, set fixes-pending, re-enter the loop so the refused fix commits push under the raised cap. The predicate is the exact inverse of the §3.5 pre-push cap-trip, so it self-gates on the explicit --max-rounds raise — the cap is never silently lifted. Escalated/failed gates are NOT bypassed: the re-entered cycle re-asserts human-gated via the §3.2 priority cascade.

Why A′ over B/C: minimal edit (~8 lines pseudocode, no new flag/verb), keeps cap-authorization separate from escalation-resolution (the --max-rounds flag is the opt-in), and is uniform across cap-only and cap+escalation cases. B couples cap-extension into the disposition verb and only covers cap+escalation; C is redundant surface over `run --max-rounds`.

Docs updated (PR pending): design doc §3.1 / §3.2 / §3.3 / §3.5; architecture state-machine.md (new re-entry edge + reverse-edge table row + human_gated note + Terminal-for-CLI glossary), sequences.md (hard-cap recovery Note), c4-l3-lifecycle.md (entry-probe comment), c4-l1-context.md (second human feedback path), index.md (resurrection-edge blurb). fca6.10 implementation scope extended via its own comment.

title: Standardize merge-guard/check-merge-eligibility.sh to --flag convention
description: check-merge-eligibility.sh still uses positional args: <owner/repo> <pr-number> <comments-seen>. Should be converted to --owner/--repo/--pr/--comments-seen flags for consistency with the rest of the PR-review helper ecosystem (done for all wait-for-pr-comments and reply-and-resolve-pr-threads helpers in PR #111 / bead agents-config-hwjei). Discovered during hwjei cleanup sweep.
close_reason: Folded into PR #111 (hwjei). Converted check-merge-eligibility.sh from positional <owner/repo> <pr-number> <comments-seen> to --owner --repo --pr --comments-seen flags, matching sibling wait-for-pr-comments helpers. title: Audit agents-config file-state / dot-folder sprawl + .gitignore footprint
description:
## Why
The project and the tools it configures write runtime state into a growing set of dot-folder locations (e.g. ~/.claude/state/pr-inventory/, $XDG_STATE_HOME/prgroom/, .beads/, .claude/, graphify-out/, .sidekick/), each requiring .gitignore entries and making it harder to keep consuming codebases clean. Scott (PR #109 review of the prgroom HLD) wants a holistic audit of ALL file-state management across agents-config and the assets it deploys.

## What
Inventory every location agents-config + its deployed skills/agents/hooks read or write runtime state. For each: purpose, lifecycle, whether it must be versioned vs ignored, and whether it pollutes consuming repos. Recommend a consolidation strategy (single state root? XDG compliance? per-tool conventions) and the .gitignore implications. M0 house-cleaning scope. Related: agents-config-wgclw.9 (shared bd-shim CLI) touches bead state specifically; this is the broader file-state picture.
acceptance_criteria: Audit doc enumerating all state locations with purpose/lifecycle/versioning disposition, plus a recommended consolidation strategy + .gitignore plan.

title: Design RCA / issue-analysis pass alongside the prgroom cluster contract
description:
## Why
prgroom's Contract A (cluster) only groups review items into fix-bundles; it does not assess the true scope, impact, or nature of each reported issue before fix dispatch. Scott (PR #109 review of the prgroom HLD) wants an RCA / issue-analysis pass that accompanies clustering to evaluate root cause and severity, feeding richer context into Contract B and potentially gating which clusters warrant a fix attempt.

## What
Brainstorm + design an RCA/issue-analysis step for prgroom. Candidate shapes: (a) extend Contract A output schema with per-cluster RCA metadata; (b) insert a dedicated analysis contract between cluster and fix. Must define: trigger point, contract I/O schema, provider chain (local-first?), how output feeds Contract B, and gating semantics. Post-MVP enhancement (beyond wait-for-pr-comments parity). Forward-context placeholder already noted in the prgroom L3 agent-dispatch HLD (Planned section).

## Cross-loop failure modes (from fca6.5 PR-memory brainstorm)
The fca6.5 discussion separated three distinct "prior fix was inadequate" cases by WHO can detect them:

1. Same thread reopened ("you said fixed, reviewer says still broken") — DETERMINISTIC. prgroom owns the disposition history + thread state, so it stamps a recurrence signal onto each item before fix dispatch: { reopened, attempt_count, prior_disposition, prior_commits, first_seen_round }. prgroom detects; it does not interpret. This recurrence flag is produced by fca6.5 and is THIS pass's primary new input.
2. Fix too narrow ("you patched one call site; the pattern recurs elsewhere") — JUDGMENT, proactive. This is RCA's core job: assess an item's intrinsic scope on first encounter, before fixing.
3. Fix caused new problems ("your commit broke Y") — JUDGMENT, reactive, causal. Not deterministically detectable; belongs to the fix agent / RCA, never to prgroom's FSM.

## Relationship to fca6.5 (MVP boundary)
fca6.5 (PR memory) produces the deterministic recurrence flag and feeds prior dispositions + full thread reply-chains to the fix agent. fca6.5 does NOT interpret. MVP decision (fca6.5 = Option A): the fix agent self-interprets the recurrence flag (widen sweep / rethink / escalate); RCA remains an INDEPENDENT, OPTIONAL enrichment and is NOT a hard dependency or load-bearing in the MVP fix loop. RCA consumes the recurrence flag as input when present.

## Open question for this brainstorm
Should RCA ALWAYS run after round 1 — i.e., on every reopened/repeat item (or every post-round-1 fix) — BEFORE the fix agent is invoked? This is the Option-A -> Option-B upgrade: forced RCA routing for reopened items (stronger guarantee; RCA becomes load-bearing) vs. RCA-as-optional-enrichment (the MVP shape). Decide the trigger policy: never-forced, forced-on-reopened-only, or forced-on-all-post-round-1.
acceptance_criteria: Design/brainstorm output settling: trigger point (incl. whether RCA is forced post-round-1 before fix dispatch — never / reopened-only / all-post-round-1), contract schema, provider chain, Contract B handoff, gating semantics, and how the pass consumes fca6.5's deterministic recurrence flag. prgroom L3 agent-dispatch diagram updated to draw the chosen shape.

title: PDLC Orchestrator: scenario coverage matrix (incl. Scenario 8 — PR_HUMAN_HOLD)
description:
## What this bead is

A **register** for PDLC orchestrator scenario coverage gaps surfaced during the `wgclw.2.2.1` tracer-scoping grill session (2026-05-30). It captures:

1. A specific named gap (Scenario 8 — `PR_HUMAN_HOLD` path)
2. The broader principle: every FSM branch × every gate × every failure mode is a candidate scenario; today's `wgclw.2.2.*` set is incomplete

This bead is **a placeholder for required planning, testing, and implementation work**. It is intentionally NOT a full enumeration today; the enumeration is deferred to the `wgclw.4/.5/.6` implementation phases when each epic's machinery is concrete enough to design the corresponding scenarios.

## Known coverage gap — Scenario 8

`PR_HUMAN_HOLD` is exercised by NO current scenario under `wgclw.2.2.*`. It is a conditional state inserted into the Integration Pipeline when an Objective's `approval_required = true`. Properties to exercise:

- HEP-style human-tagged work item emission on entry
- Aging-nag firing at the project-configured threshold on human silence
- Self-silencing on human action (approve / reject / annotate)
- Correct routing back into `MERGING` on approval
- No auto-escalation around human silence (no AUTOPSY, no failure cascade)
- TransitionLog entries showing `PR_VALIDATION → PR_HUMAN_HOLD → MERGING` correctly

Owning epic: `agents-config-wgclw.5` (PDLC Integration Pipeline — Stage B is where `PR_HUMAN_HOLD` lives).

## Broader principle — coverage matrix

The PDLC orchestrator's FSM has more permutations than the seven scenarios under `wgclw.2.2.*` can possibly cover. Other known-uncovered surfaces include:

- `CONTAINER_DECOMPOSED` full happy-path traversal (Scenario 3 exercises decomposition + mid-flight child kill, but not the all-children-succeed completion)
- `KILLED` terminal — Idea or Objective killed-with-epitaph at any pre-merge stage
- `PARKED` terminal — Objective parked at any stage with operator command
- Resurrection from a terminal state (terminal-to-non-terminal via explicit operator command)
- Tooling-strike accumulation without 3-strike trip (Layer 2 bounded retry — partial accumulation, then gate-pass clears counter)
- Pre-tick toolchain assertion failure (missing-toolchain code path)
- CAS-predicate conflict resolution (mid-tick concurrent edit causes retry)
- Discovery-Sweep marker advance under partial reconcile

Each of the above is a candidate scenario; enumeration is **deferred** to the implementation phase of the owning epic. This bead is the index that prevents the obligation from being forgotten.

## How to use this bead

- When `wgclw.4/.5/.6` implementation children open and start to grill their scenarios, consult this bead first
- Each new scenario filed gets a sibling under the appropriate `wgclw.4/.5/.6` epic (matching the existing `wgclw.2.2.*` parenting pattern)
- When a gap is closed by a new scenario, update this bead to remove the entry (or mark closed)

## Provenance

Created during the `wgclw.2.2.1` tracer-scoping grill session (2026-05-30) after Scott observed that the assertion-shape decision (Question 5 / Option A: full-sequence strict-on-essence) would only validate the happy-path branches, and that `PR_HUMAN_HOLD` plus other permutations would slip if not captured here.

Related: `agents-config-wgclw.11` (PDLC architecture far-horizon register) — that bead tracks cross-epic seams and architectural questions; this bead tracks scenario-coverage gaps. Sibling registers with different purposes.

title: post-replies.sh: defense-in-depth sentinel cid for empty per-kind dispatch
description:
Quality-reviewer finding H1 deferred from the post-replies.sh bugfix bead. The per-kind case dispatch now returns the canonical cid for each documented kind, but a malformed inventory item (no kind, no id fields) can still produce an empty cid, which then prints as 'POSTED  ' / 'FAILED  <reason>' with a double space — the same shape as the original silent-failure bug. Defense-in-depth: render a '<unknown-cid>' sentinel when cid is empty at any of the POSTED/FAILED/SKIPPED/FILTERED emit sites, so downstream parsers and operator-eyeballs never see a missing token.

Acceptance:
- All four output line types (POSTED / FAILED / SKIPPED / FILTERED) carry a non-empty cid token even when the inventory item is malformed (no kind, all id fields null/missing).
- Test fixture added: an inventory item with no kind and no id fields, asserting the output line includes the sentinel.

Out of scope: changing the per-kind dispatch itself (that already covers the 3 documented kinds correctly).
notes: Retired in triage 2026-06-27: subsumed by prgroom (abn9.8 implementation umbrella). prgroom replaces the artifact this bug lives in (typed FixInput/FixOutput contracts, ItemKind.REVIEW_SUMMARY, 6-priority phase resolver) so the bug does not carry forward. Reopen if prgroom slips and the old skill path keeps biting.

title: Decide pocock/diagnose verdict — lift into bugfix, drop, or close with rationale
description:
## Context

oss-snapshots/pocock/AGENTS.md row for `diagnose/` has been carrying a "(pending)" amalgamation verdict ("amalgamate → bugfix") since cx6.7.5. The sibling bead for `bugfix` did the superpowers/systematic-debugging lift but did NOT lift pocock/diagnose patterns. Diagnose-specific content remains upstream-only and unlifted.

## Diagnose-specific patterns still unlifted

- Loop-construction toolkit for Thread 2 of bugfix
- "Iterate on the loop itself" rules
- Ranked-hypothesis cadence in Synthesis Gate
- `scripts/hitl-loop.template.sh` (copy as `bugfix/scripts/`)

## Decision required

Three options:
1. **Lift** — pull the four patterns into bugfix with provenance header update + registry row update.
2. **Drop** — decide bugfix is already complete and pocock/diagnose adds no net value; close the pocock/AGENTS.md row with a rationale.
3. **Close with rationale** — same as drop, but record why for future re-sync decisions.

## Acceptance

- Verdict recorded with rationale in pocock/AGENTS.md (replace the "deferred — unlifted" row).
- If lift: bugfix SKILL.md provenance header updated; registry row in src/user/.agents/skills/AGENTS.md updated; lifted content reviewed.
- If drop/close: pocock/AGENTS.md row updated to reflect the verdict; no further action.

## Discovered from

cx6.7.7 close-out audit (2026-05-25) — pocock/AGENTS.md `diagnose` row carries a stale "(pending)" verdict with no tracking bead.

title: graphify PreToolUse hook fires on targeted greps where GRAPH_REPORT is overkill
description:
The PreToolUse hook in .claude/settings.json (line 31) injects 'graphify: Knowledge graph exists. Read graphify-out/GRAPH_REPORT.md for god nodes and community structure before searching raw files.' as additionalContext whenever a Bash command matches a search-tool keyword.

CURRENT TRIGGER (verified by inspection on 2026-05-25):
  case "$CMD" in
    *grep*|*rg\ *|*ripgrep*|*find\ *|*fd\ *|*ack\ *|*ag\ *)
      [ -f graphify-out/graph.json ] && echo '{...graphify reminder...}'
  esac

OBSERVATION: The trigger is correctly scoped to search tools. It does NOT fire on every Bash call (corrected: my session retrospective initially claimed it did — wrong). The friction is narrower: the reminder fires uniformly on BOTH genuinely-exploratory searches AND targeted single-file lookups, even though the recommendation 'read GRAPH_REPORT.md for god nodes' only helps the former.

WHEN IT'S NOISE (this session's pattern, PR #104 fca6.2):
- 'grep -n "ctx.Signum" docs/plans/2026-05-12-prgroom-cli-design.md' — explicit file, known token, line-count check. Reading GRAPH_REPORT.md would be pure ceremony.
- 'grep -n "wrapRuntimeCancelled\|runtimeCancelledError" <file>.md' — impact-analysis cascade check on ONE known file. The graph has nothing to add.
- 'grep -n "requestHumanReviewIfNeeded" <file>.md' — same shape.
- 'grep -rn "graphify" .claude/' — meta-search for the hook itself. The graph wouldn't know.

WHEN IT'S USEFUL (preserved by any fix):
- 'rg "concept" .' or 'grep -r "x" src/' — repo-wide exploration where INFERRED edges in the graph beat raw text matches.
- 'find . -name <pattern>' — open-ended file discovery.
- 'fd <name>' — same.

PROPOSED INVESTIGATION:
- Audit the trigger heuristic against a sample of recent sessions' Bash commands (this session, plus a few others if hook-event logs are retained).
- Pick one of three refinement options:
  (A) Tighten the case-glob to fire only on RECURSIVE / repo-wide invocations: 'grep -r', 'grep -R', 'rg <pattern>' without an explicit file, 'find . -path' without immediate -name single-match, etc. Targeted-grep skips the reminder.
  (B) Keep the broad case-glob but make the reminder content less prescriptive — change 'Read GRAPH_REPORT.md ... before searching' to 'For repo-wide concept searches, consider graphify query/explain/path commands' so it's an offer rather than a directive.
  (C) Add a second condition that only fires the reminder when the command lacks an explicit path target (i.e., is searching cwd or repo root by default).

OPEN QUESTIONS:
- Is the hook config staged from src/user/.claude/settings.json.template or hand-maintained in .claude/settings.json directly? (Check src/user/.claude/settings.json.template to see if the hook is installer-managed; if so, fix lives upstream.)
- Are there other graphify hooks (e.g., post-edit 'graphify update .' suggestions) that share the same friction?

DEFINITION OF DONE:
- Trigger heuristic tuned per chosen option (A/B/C).
- Hook tested against a representative command list (mix of targeted/exploratory).
- If installer-managed: change lives in src/user/.claude/settings.json.template and installs cleanly.

DISCOVERED-FROM: session 2026-05-25 retrospective (PR #104 / fca6.2 round 4).
notes:
ADDITIONAL OBSERVATION (2026-05-25, same session): The case-glob *grep*|*rg\ *|... is a SUBSTRING match against the ENTIRE Bash command string — not just the leading verb. So the hook fires on any command whose arguments contain those tokens: commit messages quoting 'grep', bead descriptions citing past grep commands, sed expressions, etc. Verified live: 'bd create --description=...quoting grep examples...' fired the hook. This widens the noise surface considerably; tightening the trigger to verb-only (anchored at start-of-command or word-boundary on the first token) would close that gap before any of the (A)/(B)/(C) refinements above.
REVIEWED 2026-06-01 (startup-context reassessment, post-PR#119): Scott chose to keep the hook AND graphify-out/ git-tracking as-is. Accepted noise; deferred, not won't-fix. No config change made. The substring-match false-fire (NOTES above) was surfaced to Scott at decision time. Reopen-for-action only if the nag becomes a real drag.

title: Tooling-failure handling: pre-tick toolchain assertion + bounded tooling_strike_count
description:
Implement the two-layer defence against non-transient tooling failures specified in:
- docs/specs/2026-05-23-pdlc-orchestrator-core-design.md § Tooling-failure handling
- docs/architecture/pdlc-orchestrator/state-machine.md § Tooling-failure handling: pre-tick assertion + bounded retry
- docs/architecture/pdlc-orchestrator/sequences.md (pre-tick toolchain probe in the tick cycle diagram)

Scope:
1. Layer 1 — Pre-tick toolchain assertion:
   - Extend project-config schema with [[toolchain.required]] entries: binary, min_version, version_cmd
   - Add a tick-start probe phase between config_hash compute and DISCOVER
   - On miss: mark all dispatchable Objectives needs_reconcile=true with code missing-toolchain:<binary>; suppress DISPATCH for this tick only; REAP/PERSIST still run
   - Log a toolchain-probe event to TransitionLog every tick
   - Surface missing-toolchain conditions on 'pdlc health'

2. Layer 2 — Bounded tooling_strike_count:
   - Add tooling_strike_count column(s) to ObjectiveLifecycleState keyed by (objective_id, lifecycle_stage, error_signature)
   - error_signature = stable hash of (triage class, binary/artifact name, exit-class bucket)
   - Reap increments the counter on each tooling or reviewer-artifact failure recurrence
   - At project-config.tooling_max_strikes (default 3): set needs_reconcile=true with code tooling-persistent:<error_signature>; halt dispatch on this Objective; surface on pdlc health

Acceptance criteria:
- Project-config schema covers toolchain.required and tooling_max_strikes
- Tick algorithm includes the pre-tick probe in the documented phase order
- ObjectiveLifecycleState carries the strike counter
- A test exercises the loop: missing-binary failure across 3 ticks results in needs_reconcile=true rather than continued dispatch
- pdlc health surfaces both missing-toolchain:<X> and tooling-persistent:<sig> conditions

Out of scope (separate post-MVP bead): ToolingFixer remediation persona.
notes: Discovered during wgclw.2.1 HLD review when the design's tooling-route (Autopsy route v) was found to re-dispatch the same stage without any retry bound — an infinite-loop trap on non-transient failures like a missing linter binary. HLD and core design spec amended to document the two-layer defence; this bead is the activation work.

title: Sub-design: Tick-based run model — rework §3.3 + §4 waitLocked semantics — prgroom CLI
description:
# Tick-based run model rework — REJECTED 2026-05-30

## Epitaph

Premise of "orchestrator alignment" was wrong. PDLC Orchestrator (wgclw.2) treats workers as long-lived processes spawned per-objective with success/fail/timeout terminal, not as per-tick callees. Tick-model rework is a category error.

## Context (preserved)

Filed 2026-05-25 during fca6.2 brainstorm. Proposed prgroom abandon §3.3's long-running `run --autonomous` model in favor of a scheduler-driven tick model (one cycle per tick). Cited motivations: orchestrator alignment, waitLocked simplification, operator visibility, operating-budget concurrency, resumability.

## Why rejected

- **Orchestrator alignment (primary justification, now dead):** PDLC Orchestrator's tick is for SCHEDULING + SUPERVISION (DISCOVER / RECONCILE / REAP / DISPATCH / PERSIST). The Workers it DISPATCHES (Test-Author, Implementer, Reviewer, RCA, prgroom) are long-lived processes that run to terminal under JobSupervisor lease/heartbeat/deadline supervision. Making prgroom itself tick-based would be a category error that pushes per-PR re-dispatch cadence UP into the orchestrator, which has no business knowing about PR-grooming-specific iteration logic.
- **Resumability:** already handled by §2 transactional write model.
- **waitLocked simplification:** real benefit but code-complexity, not architecture; addressable in-place within blocking model if implementation finds it painful. File a narrower successor at that time.
- **Operator visibility:** identical in both models (both write state to disk).
- **Cross-PR concurrency:** prgroom is IO-bound; concurrent long-lived processes are cheap. Tick model trades concurrency cost for serialized cross-PR latency — worse, not better.

## Resurrection

Legal but explicit. Cite new evidence that PDLC workers should be tick-driven rather than spawn-to-terminal — that would invalidate the architectural premise of the rejection.

## Related

- **fca6.10** ([Impl] Lifecycle SM, Section 3): no longer at risk of spec churn from this proposed rework; safe to start.
- **CLI configurability** for iteration caps / timeouts / poll intervals (per Scott's 2026-05-30 clarification): surface as flag-surface concerns in implementation. §3.5's existing flag set (`--max-rounds`, `PRGROOM_MAX_ROUNDS` env, `.prgroom.toml` override) covers max-rounds; file a successor bead only if implementation surfaces a specific gap (e.g., poll interval or quiescence threshold not yet externalized).

title: Lift 'Find Working Examples / Compare Against References' pattern into bugfix Thread 3
description:
Discovered during cx6.7.12 quality-reviewer pass. The upstream oss-snapshots/superpowers/systematic-debugging/SKILL.md Phase 2 contains a 'Find Working Examples' / 'Compare Against References' / 'Identify Differences' pattern that bugfix's Thread 3 (Data Flow Trace) does not currently address. For pattern-deviation bugs (this call site does X but seven other call sites do Y), reading source from entry-to-failure in isolation misses the diff signal.

Suggested integration: inline sentence inside Thread 3 — 'If similar working code exists in the codebase, read it and list every difference, however small — do not assume that cannot matter.' Avoid promoting to a 'fourth thread' since that inflates the 'Three Threads' identity.

Out of scope for cx6.7.12 because its AC specifically enumerated four patterns (Iron Law, 3-strike rule, multi-component evidence-gathering, 4-phase structure) and this is none of them. Sibling test: not on cx6.7.12's original plan → orphan + discovered-from edge, NOT a child.

Source: oss-snapshots/superpowers/systematic-debugging/SKILL.md (upstream commit f2cbfbefebbfef77321e4c9abc9e949826bea9d7, v5.1.0). Must complete BEFORE the superpowers plugin is removed (cx6.7.17).
acceptance_criteria: Inline sentence added to Thread 3 in src/user/.agents/skills/bugfix/SKILL.md covering 'find working example; list every difference; do not assume that cannot matter.' title: Promote PDLC orchestrator core design from docs/specs/ (dated) to docs/architecture/pdlc-orchestrator/design.md (evergreen)
description:
## Context

Discovered during wgclw.2.1 (HLD artifact creation). The orchestrator-core design lives at docs/specs/2026-05-23-pdlc-orchestrator-core-design.md — a date-prefixed file in the proposals folder. But the design IS the authoritative architecture for the orchestrator: it will be amended in place as implementation reveals new constraints, not superseded by a fresh dated spec.

The dated location implies frozen-at-creation. That mismatch will only worsen as wgclw.2 implementation children land.

## Three-tier model worth considering

| Folder | Tier | Lifetime |
|---|---|---|
| docs/specs/ | Dated **proposals** | Frozen-at-creation; what was decided when |
| docs/architecture/<subsystem>/ | Evergreen **canonical design** + HLD artifacts | Living; amended in place |
| docs/primers/ | Concept explainers for the discipline layer | Living |

## Proposed work

- Promote docs/specs/2026-05-23-pdlc-orchestrator-core-design.md → docs/architecture/pdlc-orchestrator/design.md (evergreen; no date prefix)
- Leave a one-line stub in docs/specs/ pointing to the new location (or retire the dated copy entirely)
- Update every cross-reference in docs/architecture/pdlc-orchestrator/ HLD artifacts to point to the new evergreen location
- Update AGENTS.md docs/architecture/ description to mention 'canonical design docs', not just diagrams
- Decide whether to apply the same treatment to docs/specs/2026-05-19-pdlc-state-machine-design.md (the FSM design)

## Scope considerations

- Sets precedent for wgclw.3 / wgclw.4 / wgclw.5 / wgclw.6 — each future epic will also have an authoritative architecture doc; same question will arise
- Affects every cross-reference in the wgclw.2.1 HLD artifact set
- Not blocking for wgclw.2.1 itself — that bead can complete with cross-references to the current dated location

## Acceptance criteria

- Decision recorded on whether to do the promotion (and same for FSM design)
- If yes: docs/specs/2026-05-23-pdlc-orchestrator-core-design.md moved or stubbed
- If yes: AGENTS.md docs/architecture/ description updated
- If yes: HLD artifact cross-references updated
- If no: rationale recorded as a project decision via bd decision

## Provenance

Discovered during wgclw.2.1 review with Scott (2026-05-24). The HLD artifacts highlighted the awkward straddle between docs/specs/ (dated) and docs/architecture/ (evergreen) for the same architecture.

title: Reconcile pr-comment-fixer-team report schema with wait-for-pr-comments audit contract
description:
Schema drift discovered during cx6.7.11.

The agent file 'src/user/.agents/agents/pr-comment-fixer-team.md' (recently un-archived) emits a report with fields:
  - classification: FIX | SKIP | ESCALATE
  - action: COMMITTED_FIX | ALREADY_ADDRESSED | NO_ACTION
  - commit_sha
  - already_addressed_by_sha
  - escalation_reason

But the orchestrator's 'wait-for-pr-comments/SKILL.md' Subagent report schema (lines ~463-472) expects:
  - fix_outcome: committed | already_addressed | failed | deferred | escalated | abandoned
  - fix_commit_sha
  - fix_gate_variant
  - verification_evidence.{test_command,output}

The audit script 'audit-subagent-report.sh' is documented as parsing the orchestrator's schema. With the current agent file in place, every report will fail audit (exit 2 schema violation), which re-classifies every FIX item to ESCALATE.

Pick one schema as canonical (likely the orchestrator's — it has more outcomes including 'failed/deferred/escalated/abandoned' and the gate-variant field) and conform the agent file to it. Update the agent's 'Report format' YAML example to match. Verify audit-subagent-report.sh against the conformed agent output.
close_reason: Conformed pr-comment-fixer-team report to the JSON fix_outcome audit schema; verified against audit-subagent-report.sh; merged via PR #208 (3 Copilot rounds, all resolved). Stopgap on the prgroom-doomed wait-for-pr-comments path.

title: [bug] post-replies.sh: review_summary kind falls through to review_thread path, fails on null reply_to_comment_id
description: post-replies.sh has no explicit case for kind=review_summary. The script's else branch defaults to the review_thread path, which requires reply_to_comment_id. Since review_summary items always have null reply_to_comment_id, the script exits with FAILED reply_to_comment_id_missing. Fix: add a review_summary branch that uses 'gh pr comment <pr> --body ...' (the correct endpoint per the skill spec). Discovered during cx6.7.9 PR #93 review where Copilot posted a review_summary.
notes: Retired in triage 2026-06-27: subsumed by prgroom (abn9.8 implementation umbrella). prgroom replaces the artifact this bug lives in (typed FixInput/FixOutput contracts, ItemKind.REVIEW_SUMMARY, 6-priority phase resolver) so the bug does not carry forward. Reopen if prgroom slips and the old skill path keeps biting.

title: Remove superpowers plugin from project config after cx6.7.6 follow-ups land
description:
Remove the `superpowers` plugin from the project's plugin configuration. This is the close-out bead for the cx6.7.6 verdict pass and its discovered amalgamation/defer work.

Prerequisites (this bead is blocked by all of these):
- cx6.7.6 (the 5 promotes land)
- The 4 amalgamation beads (receiving-code-review → wait-for-pr-comments, systematic-debugging → bugfix, verification-before-completion → verify-checklist, using-superpowers "1% rule" → project-owned primer)
- The 2 defer revisit beads (dispatching-parallel-agents, subagent-driven-development → Orchestrator seed) — these resolve to either "promote" or "do nothing", and either resolution lets removal proceed

Removal steps:
1. Identify the plugin manifest entry (likely in a `.claude/settings.json` plugin section or a project-level plugin config)
2. Remove the `superpowers` entry
3. Grep the entire `src/` tree for `superpowers:` namespaced references — update any that remain to point at in-tree non-namespaced skill names (e.g. `superpowers:test-driven-development` → `test-driven-development`)
4. Update the 5 user-scoped rule files (`delegation.md`, `completion-gate.md`, `delivery.md`, `subagents.md`, `worktrees.md`) to drop `superpowers:` prefixes
5. Run `install.sh --dry-run` and confirm none of the 14 `superpowers:*` skills appear in the staged skill list anymore
6. Start a fresh session and confirm:
   - The "1% rule" content still loads at session start via the project-owned primer (verifies bead #4 took)
   - All 5 promoted skills are discoverable under their non-namespaced names
   - No rule references resolve to missing skills
7. Optionally: snapshot the final `oss-snapshots/superpowers/AGENTS.md` verdict table state and add a closing note

Acceptance: superpowers plugin removed; no `superpowers:*` references remain anywhere in `src/`; install.sh runs clean; fresh-session smoke test passes.

Discovered during cx6.7.6 verdict pass — the verdict pass implied this work but did not own its scope.
acceptance_criteria: superpowers plugin entry removed from plugin config. Grep of src/ shows no remaining superpowers: namespaced references in rules or skill bodies. install.sh --dry-run completes clean with no superpowers:* skills staged. Fresh-session smoke test confirms 1% rule loads via project-owned primer, all 5 promoted skills discoverable under non-namespaced names, no broken rule references.
close_reason: Verified statically: src/plugins/ has no superpowers/ entry, grep -rn 'superpowers:' src/ is empty, the 5 user-scoped rules carry no superpowers refs, SESSION-PRIMER.md.template preserves the 1% rule via cx6.7.14, and the 5 promoted skills (test-driven-development, verify-checklist, finishing-a-development-branch, using-git-worktrees, writing-plans) are present non-namespaced. Remaining 'superpowers' matches are upstream provenance headers (required by amalgamation schema) and the brainstorming skill's .superpowers/brainstorm/ runtime state dir — neither is a plugin namespace reference. Force-closed ahead of cx6.7.15/.16 verdict resolution at user direction; those two defer-revisit beads remain open and do not block the removal that has already taken effect.

title: Amalgamate using-superpowers 1% rule into project-owned session primer
description:
Lift the "1% rule" and red-flag rationalization table from `oss-snapshots/superpowers/using-superpowers/SKILL.md` into a project-owned session primer BEFORE the superpowers plugin is removed.

Content to lift:
- **1% rule**: "Even a 1% chance a skill might apply means invoke it" — the core meta-rule
- **Red-flag rationalization table**: thought → reality pairs that catch common dodges ("this is just a simple question" → "Questions are tasks. Check for skills." etc.)
- **Skill priority ordering**: process skills (brainstorming, debugging) before implementation skills
- **"Invoke BEFORE response, even clarifying questions"** discipline

Currently the superpowers plugin injects this content at session start via its SessionStart hook (visible in this very session). When the plugin is removed, this hook disappears and the meta-rule disappears with it. The lift target is a project-owned location that stages at session start across all four tools (Claude Code, Codex, Gemini, OpenCode).

Host decision (subtask of this bead):
- Option A: New file `src/user/.agents/rules/skill-invocation.md` — clean separation, ships into each tool's rules/ dir
- Option B: Fold into `src/user/.agents/INSTRUCTIONS.md.template` — surfaces in the assembled top-level instruction file
- Option C: New file `src/user/.agents/SESSION-PRIMER.md.template` — explicit session-primer template that the installer wires in

Pick the host that gets read at session start across all four target runtimes. Verify with install.sh dry-run.

Add a provenance HTML-comment pointer in the host file citing `oss-snapshots/superpowers/using-superpowers/` at the pinned commit.

## Implementation note (added 2026-05-24, discovered while inspecting plugin)

The upstream mechanism is a **SessionStart hook**, not static markdown. Reference: `~/.claude/plugins/cache/claude-plugins-official/superpowers/5.1.0/hooks/hooks.json` defines:

```json
{
  "hooks": {
    "SessionStart": [{
      "matcher": "startup|clear|compact",
      "hooks": [{"type": "command", "command": "${CLAUDE_PLUGIN_ROOT}/hooks/run-hook.cmd session-start", "async": false}]
    }]
  }
}
```

The hook script (`hooks/session-start`) reads `skills/using-superpowers/SKILL.md`, wraps it in `<EXTREMELY_IMPORTANT>You have superpowers. ...</EXTREMELY_IMPORTANT>` envelope, and emits it via:
- Claude Code: `hookSpecificOutput.additionalContext` (nested)
- Cursor: `additional_context` (top-level, snake_case)
- Copilot CLI / SDK standard: `additionalContext` (top-level)

The matcher `startup|clear|compact` means it re-fires on `/clear` AND after context compaction — not just initial startup.

**Implication for host options A/B/C above:** all three are passive markdown that gets read ONCE via CLAUDE.md/AGENTS.md references at session start. They do NOT re-inject on `/clear` or `/compact`. To match the upstream re-injection behavior, this bead must ship BOTH a content file AND a hook configuration. Recommended shape:

1. **Content file**: Option C (`src/user/.agents/SESSION-PRIMER.md.template`) — purpose-built name, easy to reference from hook scripts
2. **Hook config**: SessionStart entry in `src/user/.claude/settings.json.template` (matcher: `startup|clear|compact`)
3. **Hook script**: small shell script under `src/user/.claude/hooks/` that reads the primer content file and emits the right JSON shape per platform (mirror the upstream `session-start` script's platform detection: `CLAUDE_PLUGIN_ROOT` vs `CURSOR_PLUGIN_ROOT` vs default)
4. **Cross-tool**: Codex, Gemini, and OpenCode hook support varies — verify each tool's hook contract before adding hook entries to their settings templates. Where hooks aren't supported, the markdown-only path (read via AGENTS.md reference) is the degraded baseline; document that explicitly.

Without the hook, post-`/clear` and post-compaction sessions will operate without the meta-rule discipline — a real regression vs. current behavior.

Discovered during cx6.7.6 verdict pass. Verdict for the upstream skill is "amalgamate"; this bead is the lift.
acceptance_criteria: 1% rule + red-flag table + skill priority ordering + invoke-BEFORE-response discipline present in a project-owned location. Host decision documented (option A/B/C). SessionStart hook configuration shipped in settings.json.template (matcher: startup|clear|compact) with a hook script that mirrors the upstream platform detection (Claude Code / Cursor / Copilot CLI JSON shapes). Cross-tool hook support documented per target (Codex, Gemini, OpenCode); markdown-only fallback documented for tools without hook support. install.sh dry-run confirms staging. Provenance HTML-comment pointer added citing oss-snapshots/superpowers/using-superpowers/ at f2cbfbe.
notes: PR #105 open: https://github.com/scotthamilton77/agents-config/pull/105. Copilot review complete (1 FIX: bead ID in provenance header — fixed in 952464d). Thread replied and resolved. Awaiting merge.

title: Amalgamate pocock/diagnose into bugfix + revisit Red/Green Mechanical Gate
description:
# Amalgamate pocock/diagnose into bugfix + revisit Red/Green workflow

## Background

From cx6.7.5 verdict pass: `oss-snapshots/pocock/diagnose/` (audit verdict: Compare-and-improve) has several distinctive deltas worth pulling into the native `bugfix` skill. The act of doing this amalgam exposes a tension with the M0 Red/Green Mechanical Gate framing that warrants a separate, deliberate revisit — hence filing under wgclw, not under cx6.7.

## What to amalgamate into `bugfix`

1. **Loop-construction toolkit for Thread 2 (Reproduce).** Currently Thread 2 is sparse ("Write a minimal failing test... If you cannot reproduce: say so."). pocock/diagnose lists 10 ways to construct a feedback loop, in rough order of preference:
   - Failing test at whatever seam reaches the bug
   - Curl / HTTP script against a running dev server
   - CLI invocation diffing stdout against a known-good snapshot
   - Headless browser script (Playwright / Puppeteer)
   - Replay a captured trace (HAR, payload, event log)
   - Throwaway harness (minimal subset, mocked deps)
   - Property / fuzz loop (1000 random inputs)
   - Bisection harness (`git bisect run`)
   - Differential loop (old vs new, two configs diffed)
   - HITL bash script (last resort, structured human-in-the-loop)

   Expand Thread 2 in `bugfix/SKILL.md` with this toolkit.

2. **"Iterate on the loop itself" rules.** Once you have *a* loop, treat it as a product: can it be faster (cache setup, narrow scope), sharper (assert on the specific symptom), more deterministic (pin time, seed RNG, isolate filesystem)? A 30-second flaky loop is barely better than no loop; a 2-second deterministic loop is a debugging superpower.

3. **Ranked-hypothesis cadence in the Synthesis Gate.** pocock/diagnose insists on 3-5 falsifiable ranked hypotheses *before* testing any of them — single-hypothesis generation anchors on the first plausible idea. Show the ranked list to the user before testing (cheap checkpoint, big time saver). Add as a sub-step in `bugfix`'s Synthesis Gate.

4. **`scripts/hitl-loop.template.sh` companion.** Copy from `oss-snapshots/pocock/diagnose/scripts/` into `src/user/.agents/skills/bugfix/scripts/`. It is the structured HITL harness from Phase 1's last-resort path.

## Do NOT replace `bugfix`'s 3-thread structure

Layer pocock/diagnose's loop-building rigor and hypothesis-ranking discipline *into* the existing 3-thread breadth-first structure. The two skills' philosophies are complementary:
- `bugfix` = breadth-first (parallel evidence from git/test/trace, synthesize)
- `diagnose` = depth-first on the feedback loop (build the right loop first)

The amalgam keeps bugfix's breadth-first opening but deepens the Reproduce thread and the Synthesis gate with diagnose's discipline.

## **CRITICAL — Red/Green workflow revisit (M0 implication)**

The M0 Mechanical Pipeline Gate currently reads:

> **The Red/Green Mechanical Gate:** The transition from Test Writing to Implementation is gated by a deterministic runner (`npm test`, `pytest`). Tests must compile against stubs and actively *fail* before implementation begins. No LLM "vibe checks" allowed for this gate.

This assumes you can **write** the test before implementation. For straightforward feature/bug work where the seam exists, that holds. For hard bugs — non-deterministic failures, perf regressions, bugs that appear only at integration boundaries, bugs whose minimum repro takes meaningful design — pocock/diagnose's wisdom is that **finding/building the right reproducer is often the hard part**, not writing implementation. Sometimes the failing test IS the loop (unit-test seam available); sometimes the loop is a curl + diff harness, a replay rig, a bisection script, or a property fuzzer.

The Red/Green gate as currently framed risks one of two failure modes for hard bugs:
1. Agents skip the gate ("can't write a failing test, so I'll just dive in") — defeats the deterministic-evidence-before-implementation discipline.
2. Agents write the wrong test (a unit test at the wrong seam, or one that passes-then-fails depending on timing) — defeats it differently, by greenlighting based on a misleading signal.

### Proposed revisit options

- **Option A — Expand the gate to a 3-phase definition:**
  1. Build feedback loop (any of the 10 toolkit techniques, not just unit tests)
  2. Confirm loop reproduces the symptom (Red)
  3. Implementation drives it to green
- **Option B — Generalise the gate's prose:** "deterministic mechanical signal that distinguishes bug-present from bug-absent" rather than "test must fail". A failing test IS a mechanical signal; so is a curl-diff, a bisect, a property-fuzz failure rate. The gate's contract is the *signal*, not the *test*.
- **Option C — Two-track gate:** keep Red/Green as-is for feature work and AFK execution where the seam is given; add a separate "Diagnostic Gate" with the build-the-loop-first discipline for the bugfix path.

### Sequence

1. Do the bugfix amalgam first (this bead) — let the toolkit and ranked-hypothesis discipline land in code.
2. Use the amalgam as the concrete artifact to argue for the gate revisit (or to discover that the current gate already covers it under a charitable reading).
3. Update M0 prose accordingly. Decide between Options A / B / C.

## Acceptance criteria

- `src/user/.agents/skills/bugfix/SKILL.md` includes the 10-technique loop-construction toolkit (expanded Thread 2), the "iterate on the loop" rules, and the ranked-hypothesis cadence in Synthesis.
- `src/user/.agents/skills/bugfix/scripts/hitl-loop.template.sh` exists (copied from pocock).
- Provenance HTML-comment header added to `bugfix/SKILL.md` crediting `oss-snapshots/pocock/diagnose/`.
- `src/user/.agents/skills/AGENTS.md` provenance registry has a row for `bugfix` ← `oss-snapshots/pocock/diagnose/`.
- `oss-snapshots/pocock/AGENTS.md` verdict for `diagnose/` updated from "amalgamate (pending)" to "amalgamated".
- M0 Red/Green Gate revisit produces either (a) updated prose in the project root `AGENTS.md` / milestone description / a new doc that addresses the loop-first reality, or (b) an explicit recorded decision that the current framing is sufficient under a charitable reading, with rationale.
acceptance_criteria: bugfix/SKILL.md includes 10-technique loop-construction toolkit (Thread 2), iterate-on-the-loop rules, and ranked-hypothesis cadence in Synthesis. bugfix/scripts/hitl-loop.template.sh exists. Provenance header + registry row added crediting oss-snapshots/pocock/diagnose/. oss-snapshots/pocock/AGENTS.md diagnose verdict updated to 'amalgamated'. M0 Red/Green Mechanical Gate prose either revisited (updated in project AGENTS.md / milestone description / new doc) to address feedback-loop-first reality, or explicit recorded decision that current framing suffices under a charitable reading.

title: Amalgamate pocock/tdd into writing-unit-tests
description:
# Amalgamate pocock/tdd into writing-unit-tests

## Background

From cx6.7.5 verdict pass: `oss-snapshots/pocock/tdd/` (audit verdict: Compare-and-improve) has two distinctive deltas worth pulling into the native `writing-unit-tests` skill.

## What to amalgamate

1. **Anti-pattern: horizontal slicing.** pocock/tdd explicitly prohibits "write all tests, then all implementation" — that produces tests of *imagined* behavior, not *actual* behavior; tests become insensitive to real changes. The correct approach is vertical tracer-bullet slices: one test → one impl → repeat. Include the ASCII diagram pocock uses:

   ```
   WRONG (horizontal):
     RED:   test1, test2, test3, test4, test5
     GREEN: impl1, impl2, impl3, impl4, impl5

   RIGHT (vertical):
     RED→GREEN: test1→impl1
     RED→GREEN: test2→impl2
     ...
   ```

2. **Proactive interface-design-for-testability pre-phase.** Current `writing-unit-tests` has a *reactive* "Refactoring for Testability" section ("when code is untestable, decompose it"). pocock/tdd's contribution is *proactive*: before writing the first test, identify deep modules (small interface, deep implementation, rarely changes), design interfaces for testability, list the behaviors to test (not implementation steps), get user approval on the plan. Add this as a sub-section under the existing "Refactoring for Testability" header (or as a new "Planning" section that precedes it).

## What NOT to do

- Do NOT modify `superpowers:test-driven-development` — it is plugin-sourced (from `obra/superpowers`), not ours to modify. Native amalgams land in `writing-unit-tests` (which we own).
- Do NOT pull in the full pocock/tdd companion files (`deep-modules.md`, `interface-design.md`, `mocking.md`, `refactoring.md`, `tests.md`). The two deltas above capture the load-bearing additions; the companions can stay in the snapshot and be referenced by concept if needed.

## Acceptance criteria

- `src/user/.agents/skills/writing-unit-tests/SKILL.md` includes the anti-horizontal-slicing rule + diagram and the proactive interface-design pre-phase.
- Provenance HTML-comment header added to `writing-unit-tests/SKILL.md` crediting `oss-snapshots/pocock/tdd/` (commit `e74f0061`).
- `src/user/.agents/skills/AGENTS.md` provenance registry has a row for `writing-unit-tests` ← `oss-snapshots/pocock/tdd/`.
- `oss-snapshots/pocock/AGENTS.md` verdict for `tdd/` updated from "amalgamate (pending)" to "amalgamated".
acceptance_criteria: writing-unit-tests/SKILL.md contains anti-horizontal-slicing rule + diagram and the proactive interface-design pre-phase.
