title: Investigate claude -p haiku skill-trigger eval mechanism (0% recall blind spot)
description:
During cx6.7.8 empirical dogfood pass on optimize-my-skill (--deep run, 2026-05-23), the Phase 4a description loop produced 0/3 trigger rate on every should_trigger:true query across all 5 description variants and 225 total haiku eval calls. The skill demonstrably triggers correctly in real Claude Code sessions, so this is most likely a systematic blind spot in the claude -p haiku eval mechanism (scripts/run_eval.py, scripts/improve_description.py), NOT a description defect.

Goal: Determine whether the eval mechanism's haiku subprocess invocation models real Claude Code skill selection accurately. If not, characterize the gap and decide between: (a) fix the mechanism, (b) document the limitation and accept reduced precision/recall signal for some skill classes, (c) replace the mechanism with a different probe (e.g. sonnet-class evaluator, or direct API call with full skill manifest).

Findings to capture:
- Per-skill-class recall pattern: does the 0% recall reproduce for other skills in the catalog, or only optimize-my-skill?
- Mechanism diff: how does run_eval.py construct the prompt vs how Claude Code surfaces skills at runtime?
- Cost/value: at $1.85-$2.35 per dogfood pass with no actionable description signal, the description loop's ROI is currently negative.

Acceptance criteria: A spike report (committed to bead notes or a docs/spikes/ file) covering the three findings above plus a recommendation on (a)/(b)/(c). No code changes required to close this spike — that's downstream work.

title: Revisit pocock setup/to-issues/to-prd/triage under M0 work-tracker abstraction
description:
# Revisit pocock setup/to-issues/to-prd/triage under M0 work-tracker abstraction

## Background

During cx6.7.5 (per-skill verdict pass for `oss-snapshots/pocock/`), four pocock skills were taken out of scope because they are tightly coupled to a project-wide issue-tracker + triage-label + domain-doc convention scaffolded by `setup-matt-pocock-skills`:

- `setup-matt-pocock-skills` — scaffolds `docs/agents/{issue-tracker,triage-labels,domain}.md` and an `## Agent skills` block in `AGENTS.md`/`CLAUDE.md`
- `to-issues` — converts a plan/spec/PRD into vertical-slice issues, publishes via the issue-tracker binding
- `to-prd` — synthesises conversation into a PRD, publishes via the issue-tracker binding
- `triage` — moves issues through a 5-role state machine (`needs-triage`/`needs-info`/`ready-for-agent`/`ready-for-human`/`wontfix`)

These four belong inside M0 (WMS-decoupling work) rather than the cx6.7 amalgamation pass.

## Coupling analysis (from cx6.7.5)

| Skill | Issue tracker | Triage labels | Domain glossary | Native overlap |
|---|---|---|---|---|
| `to-issues` | hard (publishes issues) | hard (applies `ready-for-agent`) | soft (vocab) | brainstorm-bead formula |
| `to-prd` | hard (publishes PRD) | hard (applies `ready-for-agent`) | soft (vocab) | brainstorm-bead formula |
| `triage` | hard (full CRUD) | hard (state machine) | soft | none |
| `setup-matt-pocock-skills` | n/a (scaffolds) | n/a (scaffolds) | n/a (scaffolds) | none |

## Architectural questions

**Q1. Convention adoption vs rewrite.** For the hard-coupled skills, do we:
(a) Adopt the pocock convention — add an `issue-tracker-beads.md` preset to `setup-matt-pocock-skills`, ship the setup skill as a prereq for consumer projects — making `docs/agents/issue-tracker.md` the abstraction layer. Swapping backends becomes a docs edit, not a code change in N skills.
(b) Rewrite the dependents to target `bd` directly. No convention layer; tighter coupling but no setup-skill prereq.

The AGENTS.md "Current state" section says: *"we'll be taking a step back from making [beads] a first class citizen of this project's architecture, placing a 'work' abstraction in front of it - end state is that beads is quarantined behind our own CLI."* That is exactly the abstraction pocock's convention provides. Recommendation: lean toward (a), once the `work` CLI is the actual backend.

**Q2. Triage state model — pocock's 5 roles vs. beads-native.** Beads' state model is `open`/`in_progress`/`closed`/`blocked` plus arbitrary labels. Pocock's 5-role state machine doesn't map 1:1. Options:
(a) Layer pocock's 5 roles on top via `bd label add` (triage:needs-info, triage:ready-for-agent, etc.).
(b) Reinterpret roles onto beads' existing statuses + a smaller new label set (map `needs-triage` → `open` without a triage label, label only the off-default states).
(c) Defer `triage` until the `work` CLI is the abstraction surface.

Recommendation: (b). The roles are useful — `ready-for-agent` matches M4 overnight-autonomy vision — but forcing all five orthogonal to beads' state field is duplicate state.

**Q3. Sequencing.** Likely sequence:
1. Wait for `work` CLI to stabilise (or stub it sufficiently to target).
2. Add `issue-tracker-work.md` (or `issue-tracker-bd.md` if work CLI is not ready) preset to `setup-matt-pocock-skills` — pocock skill becomes the convention's authoring tool.
3. Decide triage label vocabulary (Q2).
4. Promote setup + to-issues + to-prd + triage to `src/user/.agents/skills/<skill>/` with provenance + adapted companion files.
5. Compare brainstorm-bead formula output against `to-prd` output — decide whether to fold one into the other or keep both as alternate paths.

## Snapshot status

All four skills are already in `oss-snapshots/pocock/` (refreshed 2026-05-23 against upstream `e74f0061bb67222181640effa98c675bdb2fdaa7`). No re-snapshot needed; this bead is just the design + promotion work.
acceptance_criteria: Q1 (convention adoption vs rewrite), Q2 (triage state model), Q3 (sequencing) answered with rationale recorded in this bead's notes or a linked spec. If verdict is 'promote', the four skills exist under src/user/.agents/skills/<skill>/ with provenance headers, adapted setup-skill presets, and updated provenance registry. If verdict is 'defer further', a follow-up bead exists with the next gate condition (e.g. 'revisit after work CLI ships').

title: AGENTS.md template: note CONTEXT.md at project root as soft convention
description: Pocock's domain.md convention (and the improve-codebase-architecture skill promoted in cx6.7.5) establishes CONTEXT.md at the project root as the canonical location for the project's domain glossary. The amalgamated skills landing from cx6.7.5 (diagnose, tdd, zoom-out, improve-codebase-architecture) all reference 'the project's domain glossary' to varying degrees. agents-config's own AGENTS.md does not currently call this out as something deployed agents should look for in consumer projects. Update the relevant AGENTS.md template under src/user/ to add a soft-convention note: agents should check for a CONTEXT.md at the project root (and CONTEXT-MAP.md for multi-context repos) and use the vocabulary it defines when one exists. The note should be advisory, not mandatory — CONTEXT.md absence is not a blocker.

Note frames CONTEXT.md as a soft convention (read if present, do not require). Multi-context CONTEXT-MAP.md variant is mentioned. Note references the convention by concept (no file-path citations per project rule).

title: Upstream skill-creator defects in amalgamated scripts (port-kill, sort-typeerror, empty-train-set)
description: Copilot review on PR #85 (cx6.7.3) flagged three real defects in scripts amalgamated verbatim from anthropics/skill-creator (commit f458cee31a7577a47ba0c9a101976fa599385174). Drift policy is accept-periodic-resync so we should NOT patch locally without explicit divergence decision. The defects: (1) scripts/eval-viewer/generate_review.py _kill_port() SIGTERMs any process on requested port via lsof — kills unrelated user services; lsof unavailable on some platforms. Should retry port+1 a bounded number of times or bind to ephemeral. (2) scripts/eval-viewer/generate_review.py find_runs() sorts by (eval_id, id); eval_id from JSON may be string, mixing strings/numbers/inf raises TypeError. (3) scripts/run_loop.py split_eval_set() can produce empty train_set when eval set is small because max(1, ...) always pulls at least 1 from each class into test; loop then immediately exits as all_passed with no improvement iterations. Decisions to make: (a) file upstream PRs to anthropics/skills repo, (b) maintain local divergence in our scripts/ folder + update provenance header to note divergence, (c) accept-as-known-limitation + document in SKILL.md. (a) is the right long-term answer; (c) is the cheapest interim.
acceptance_criteria: Decision recorded (upstream PRs filed OR local divergence applied OR known-limitation documented). Provenance header in optimize-my-skill/SKILL.md updated to reflect chosen path. Build passes. Typecheck passes. Tests pass.
notes:
Source: Copilot review on PR #85 (cx6.7.3), comments 3292244886 / 3292244893 / 3292244901. SKIP-rationale on PR will reference this bead.
=== Round 2 Copilot review on PR #85 surfaced two additional concerns ===
- generate_review.py _kill_port() additionally raises PermissionError when trying to SIGTERM a PID owned by another user, crashing the viewer BEFORE the ephemeral-port fallback. Safer alternatives: catch PermissionError + continue, OR make killing opt-in via flag/env, OR remove the kill step entirely and bind to ephemeral on bind failure. (Comment 3292268416)
- aggregate_benchmark.py aggregate_results computes 'delta' using the first two configuration keys in 'results' (insertion order), which can silently flip baseline/primary if configs are discovered in an unexpected order, or become meaningless if there are >2 configs. Fix shape: select baseline/primary explicitly (e.g. prefer with_skill vs without_skill name), or require exactly two configs and error otherwise, or sort configs deterministically before computing delta. (Comment 3292268460)
All three (port-kill, sort-typeerror, empty-train-set) + these two (PermissionError, aggregate_results baseline flip) belong to the same decision class: upstream defect → file PR vs maintain local divergence vs document known-limitation.
close_reason: Merged via PR #87: 5 known skill-creator divergences documented in amalgam provenance header

title: Empirical dogfood pass on optimize-my-skill (full --deep run)
description: First real-customer use of the newly-wired --deep flow shipped in cx6.7.3. Hand-curate a meaningful evals.json for optimize-my-skill (~15 entries, mix of clear should-trigger, clear should-not-trigger, and near-miss adversarial queries that probe known overlap with writing-skills + skill-creator history). Run /optimize-my-skill optimize-my-skill --deep with default iterations and a ~5-prompt output-review battery. Apply any description and body improvements the empirical results justify; commit evals.json as the regression baseline. Capture model spend in bead notes for future cost calibration.
acceptance_criteria: evals.json committed under src/user/.agents/skills/optimize-my-skill/. Description and/or body changes (if any) committed with rationale tying back to the empirical results. Model spend captured in bead notes. Workspace artifacts NOT committed (.gitignored). Build passes. Typecheck passes. Tests pass.
notes:
Source: cx6.7.3 Task 15 step 1. Sibling of cx6.7.3 (child of cx6.7), not a child of cx6.7.3, to avoid close-walk trap.
Phase 4 model spend (cx6.7.8 dogfood run, 2026-05-23):

Phase 4a (description loop, haiku):
- Model: claude-haiku-4-5-20251001
- Iterations run: 5 (max_iterations reached, no convergence)
- Eval calls per iteration: 45 (15 queries × 3 runs/query)
- Total haiku eval invocations: 225
- Wall time per iteration: ~55s eval + ~45s improvement; total ~275s eval-only
- Description-improvement calls (sonnet-class): 4 (iterations 1-4)
- Approximate token totals (estimated):
  - Haiku eval calls: ~150K tokens (~$0.05 at haiku rates)
  - Sonnet improvement calls: ~30K tokens (~$0.30 at sonnet rates)

Phase 4b (output review, sonnet via general-purpose subagents):
- 3 run subagents: total_tokens 52493 + 52058 + 120558 = 225,109
- 3 grader subagents: total_tokens 58404 + 59754 + 69309 = 187,467
- Combined Phase 4b: 412,576 tokens (~$1.50-$2.00 at sonnet rates)

Estimated total session spend: ~$1.85 - $2.35 (rough; precise cost requires invoice data)
Wall time end-to-end (Phase 4a + 4b): ~12-15 minutes including parallel subagent overhead

Phase 4a finding (CRITICAL for follow-up): 0% recall on every should_trigger:true query across all 5 description variants and all 225 haiku eval runs. Best score (original description) was 2/5 test, entirely from correctly NOT triggering on false entries. Since this skill demonstrably triggers correctly in real Claude Code sessions, the eval mechanism (claude -p haiku) appears to have a systematic blind spot for this skill class. Discovered-work bead to follow.
M2 reconciliation: bead description said '~5-prompt output-review battery'; SKILL.md Phase 4b spec says 'First 3 entries'. Dogfood ran 3 prompts (per spec) and produced sufficient signal to justify both shipped body edits. Decision: bead's '~5' was approximate guidance; SKILL.md spec retained at 3. No drift to fix.
close_reason: Empirical dogfood pass complete. evals.json shipped, two body improvements applied (Boundaries section + multi-skill priority-summary), three rounds of Copilot review addressed, PR #90 merged at 046eef6. Spike agents-config-6wun7 filed for haiku eval mechanism investigation.

title: quick_validate.py amalgamated but unwired in optimize-my-skill — wire or delete
description: During cx6.7.3 Task 14 pre-flight, discovered quick_validate.py (amalgamated from skill-creator in cx6.7.3 Task 1, lives at scripts/quick_validate.py in optimize-my-skill) is NOT referenced anywhere in SKILL.md. grep -c 'quick_validate' SKILL.md returns 0. The script ships with the skill (install.sh stages it into ~/.claude/skills/optimize-my-skill/scripts/) but nothing invokes it. This is the helper-extraction-shipped-but-unwired-anti-pattern (see memory): three review tools can sign off on each commit while the latent contract gap persists. Decision needed: (a) wire it into Phase 2 'Assess Against Quality Criteria' as a deterministic frontmatter pre-check, OR (b) delete it from the amalgam since it's dead weight. quick_validate.py is also the sole pyyaml consumer; deletion drops the only third-party Python dep entirely.
acceptance_criteria: quick_validate.py is either invoked from SKILL.md OR deleted from the amalgam (and provenance header updated to reflect exclusion). No dead scripts ship in the optimize-my-skill deployable. Build passes. Typecheck passes. Tests pass.
notes: Source: cx6.7.3 Task 14 pre-flight investigation. Pattern matches the abn9.4 / PR #64 finding documented in memory.
close_reason: Merged via PR #87: amalgam provenance header documents dormant quick_validate.py

title: AskUserQuestion 4-cap routing guideline + fix optimize-agents-md 5-questions case
description:
Two motivations bundled:

1) Codify a routing rule in shared skills documentation: when to use AskUserQuestion (<=4 short mutually-exclusive options, mobile tap-to-select wins, hard turn-ending gate needed) vs prose letter-prompts with explicit wait-gate (>4 options, options need rich per-option explanation, >4 questions in one turn, or portability beyond Claude Code matters). Document the trade-off: AskUserQuestion is structurally turn-ending; letter-prompts are a soft prose convention requiring explicit STOP instruction.

2) Apply that rule to optimize-agents-md SKILL.md (lines 46-54), which currently says 'Use AskUserQuestion to present all 5 at once' for 5 open-ended Scope Discovery questions. AskUserQuestion's questions array enforces questions.maxItems=4, so 5 questions in one call also fails input validation. Switch to numbered prose questions with explicit wait-gate, OR split into two calls (4+1).

Surfaced during cx6.7.3 Task 11 quality review when looking at the analogous defect in optimize-my-skill Phase 5 (resolved inline as cx6.7.3.2).
acceptance_criteria: Routing guideline documented (likely as rule in src/user/.agents/rules/ or as a section in a shared skill). optimize-agents-md SKILL.md updated to comply. Build passes. Typecheck passes. Tests pass.
notes: Source: cx6.7.3 quality review (Task 11). Cousin defect to cx6.7.3.2 — same root cause (AskUserQuestion cap), different axis (questions vs options).
close_reason: Rule user-prompts.md added (4-cap + letter/numbered fallbacks + wait-gate); optimize-agents-md SKILL.md Phase 1 fixed; Copilot review addressed; PR #89 open

title: Phase 5 Iterate proposal exceeds AskUserQuestion 4-option limit
description: Phase 5 'Iterate' step 5 specified a 5-option AskUserQuestion (Accept all / Adopt description only / Adopt body edits only / Re-run --deep from Phase 4a / Reject all). The AskUserQuestion tool schema enforces options.maxItems=4, so this would have failed InputValidationError at runtime. RESOLVED inline within cx6.7.3 by switching to a prose letter-prompt (A-E) with explicit wait-gate prose ('end your turn, do not infer, wait for letter reply'). Trade-off: loses the structural turn-ending gate that AskUserQuestion provides; compensated by explicit prose instruction. Sibling concern (5 questions in one call in optimize-agents-md) tracked separately.
acceptance_criteria: SKILL.md Phase 5 step 5 uses letter-prompt, not AskUserQuestion. Letter prompt presents 5 distinct options. Wait-gate prose explicit. Build passes. Typecheck passes. Tests pass.
notes: Source: cx6.7.3 Task 11 quality review. Implementer preserved verbatim plan content per spec discipline; defect is in the plan body, not the implementation.
close_reason: Resolved inline in cx6.7.3 commit be11aef: switched Phase 5 step 5 from 5-option AskUserQuestion to prose letter-prompt (A-E) with explicit wait-gate.

title: Document --deep runtime contract (claude CLI on PATH, no SDK)
description: Original premise was wrong: I assumed --deep needed anthropic Python SDK + pyyaml installed. Investigation found: (1) the deep-path scripts (run_loop.py, generate_review.py) use Python stdlib only — no third-party packages; (2) model calls go via 'claude -p' subprocess, inheriting Claude Code session auth — no ANTHROPIC_API_KEY needed; (3) pyyaml is only used by quick_validate.py which is amalgamated-but-unwired (separate bead). Resolution: short 'Runtime contract' subsection added in SKILL.md between Phase 4 cost gate and Phase 4a documenting the claude-CLI-on-PATH requirement and the no-extra-deps reality.
acceptance_criteria: SKILL.md documents the 'claude on PATH + stdlib only' runtime contract for --deep. Build passes. Typecheck passes. Tests pass.
notes: Source: cx6.7.3 Task 5 finding (pyyaml install via --break-system-packages by Task 5 implementer). Originally planned to fold into Task 10 SKILL.md rewrite but kept strict-verbatim to honor plan; this bead carries the deferred concern.
close_reason: Resolved by cx6.7.3 commit 8377f86: 'Runtime contract' subsection added to SKILL.md documenting the claude-CLI-on-PATH requirement and the no-extra-deps reality. Original anthropic-SDK premise was incorrect.

title: Test-suite hygiene: stale tests reference quarantined paths + don't propagate failure exit codes
description:
Two pre-existing test-suite issues surfaced during cx6.7.2 completion-gate verification:

1. **Stale path references.** Three test scripts probe for files that no longer exist on main (likely moved during the archive/ quarantine phase):
   - `src/user/.agents/skills/bugfix/bd_json_extractor_dual_shape_test.sh` — preconditions FAIL on:
     - `src/plugins/beads/` directory present
     - `brainstorm-bead.formula.toml` present
     - `create-bead SKILL.md` present
   - `src/user/.agents/skills/ralf-implement/_test.sh` — AC11 and AC12 assertions FAIL on:
     - `src/plugins/beads/.beads/formulas/implement-feature.formula.toml`
     - `src/plugins/beads/.beads/formulas/fix-bug.formula.toml`

2. **Exit-code non-propagation.** Multiple test scripts emit `FAIL:` lines but exit 0, so the project's `test` gate (`project-config.toml [gates] test = ...`) reports overall success despite the failures. The gate's `|| exit 1` only sees the script-level exit code.

Either the tests need updating to match the post-quarantine codebase state, or the affected scripts need to be archived alongside the files they reference. Decision is human-judgment-bound.

Confirmed not caused by cx6.7.2 — same FAILs occur on main with no changes applied.
acceptance_criteria: Stale path references either updated to match current codebase state or moved to archive/ alongside the files they reference. Test scripts that emit FAIL: lines also exit non-zero so the project test gate can detect failures via the existing `|| exit 1` chain in project-config.toml.
notes:
Resolution (2026-05-17, commit 7475a28 on worktree-94wzh-test-hygiene):

Fix #1 (stale path references): both scripts moved to archive/ mirroring the live source-tree hierarchy:
  - src/user/.agents/skills/bugfix/bd_json_extractor_dual_shape_test.sh -> archive/src/user/.agents/skills/bugfix/
  - src/user/.agents/skills/ralf-implement/_test.sh -> archive/src/user/.agents/skills/ralf-implement/

Fix #2 (exit-code non-propagation): vacuously satisfied post-#1. Empirical gate run with the 17 surviving test scripts: zero FAIL: emissions, exit 0. The two archived scripts were the entire population the bead's symptom described — their preconditions/AC11/AC12 assertions were the FAIL: lines being swallowed. Survivors follow either fail()-immediate-exit or FAIL=0 accumulator -> exit $FAIL patterns; both propagate correctly.

Did NOT add a defensive meta-test codifying the FAIL-implies-nonzero-exit invariant; user opted for minimal-edits close. If regression surfaces, file a new bead with the defensive-lint scope.
close_reason: Fix #1: both stale tests archived (commit 7475a28). Fix #2: vacuously satisfied — empirical gate run on 17 surviving scripts shows 0 FAIL: emissions, exit 0. Archived scripts were the entire offending population.

title: CI hardening — pin runner version, SHA-pin setup-uv action, restrict workflow permissions
description:
Discovered during the A.1 (w1qls.1.1) completion-gate quality-reviewer pass. Three CI workflow hardening items deferred from A.1 because they are project-wide security/reproducibility decisions, not A.1 scope:

1. `runs-on: ubuntu-latest` -> pin to `ubuntu-24.04` (or current LTS) to prevent surprise breakage on runner-image rotation.

2. `astral-sh/setup-uv@v3` (floating major tag) -> SHA-pin (`astral-sh/setup-uv@<commit-sha>`) for supply-chain hardening. The action is third-party (Astral, not GitHub-first-party), so a tag-repoint at Astral could silently change behavior on the next CI run.

3. No explicit `permissions:` stanza. For a CI workflow that only checks out and runs `make ci` (no push, no comment), `permissions: contents: read` at the job level is the correct minimal scope. Hardens against future steps being added without revisiting token scope.
acceptance_criteria:
## Acceptance Criteria
- `.github/workflows/ci.yml` uses a pinned Ubuntu runner version (e.g. `ubuntu-24.04`) rather than `ubuntu-latest`.
- `astral-sh/setup-uv` step uses a commit-SHA pin rather than a floating major tag.
- Workflow declares an explicit minimal `permissions:` block (`contents: read` is sufficient for current usage).
- `make ci` and the GitHub Actions CI workflow both still exit 0.
- Decision noted in the relevant spec or design doc so future packages inherit the policy.

title: Final cross-namespace optimize-my-skill pass + provenance registry close-out
description: After cx6.7.B/C/D/E/F land, run the modified optimize-my-skill (from cx6.7.C, in --deep mode where it adds value) across every newly-deployable skill under src/user/.agents/skills/. Reconcile any optimize-my-skill findings. Verify amalgamation pointers in oss-snapshots/<repo>/AGENTS.md files resolve to real native targets. Sanity-check src/user/.agents/skills/AGENTS.md provenance registry against actual deployed skills (no orphan registry rows, no deployed skills missing from the registry). Update top-level navigation (project AGENTS.md, etc.) if needed. Close cx6.7; close-walk will transitively close cx6.
acceptance_criteria: optimize-my-skill runs clean across the deployable set with evidence in bead notes. Provenance registry matches actual src/user/.agents/skills/ contents (no orphans either direction). All amalgamation pointers verified resolvable. Top-level docs updated if needed. cx6.7 ready to close (cx6 transitively closes via close-walk).
notes:
## Audit evidence (2026-05-25, session 7260e699)

### Registry vs deployed skills (src/user/.agents/skills/AGENTS.md)

- 15 OSS-derived skills in registry (16 rows; writing-skills has 2 source rows).
- 27 deployed skill folders under `src/user/.agents/skills/` + 1 under `src/user/.claude/skills/` (handoff).
- 12 in-house non-OSS skills correctly absent from registry: merge-guard, optimize-agents-md, optimize-my-agent, ralf-implement, ralf-review, reply-and-resolve-pr-threads*, self-improving-agent, simplify, test-review, wait-for-pr-comments*, whats-next, where-does-this-fit. (* = in-house host with selective OSS amalgam; see below.)
- Reconciliation result: 0 orphan registry rows. 0 OSS-derived skills missing from registry, after the 2 amalgam-host rows added in this pass.

### Registry corrections applied

- Added `wait-for-pr-comments` row citing `oss-snapshots/superpowers/receiving-code-review/` (pushback discipline lifted into `references/handling-feedback.md`).
- Added `reply-and-resolve-pr-threads` row citing the same upstream (host references the sibling `references/handling-feedback.md`; no independent reference file).

### Amalgamation pointers in oss-snapshots/*/AGENTS.md

All landing-target pointers verified resolvable to a real native skill or recorded outcome:

- anthropics/AGENTS.md: skill-creator → writing-skills + optimize-my-skill (both have provenance HTML headers).
- pocock/AGENTS.md: caveman, grill-with-docs, handoff (Claude-only), improve-codebase-architecture, prototype, tdd → writing-unit-tests, zoom-out — all resolve.
- superpowers/AGENTS.md: brainstorming, finishing-a-development-branch, receiving-code-review → wait-for-pr-comments + reply-and-resolve-pr-threads, systematic-debugging → bugfix, test-driven-development, using-git-worktrees, using-superpowers → SESSION-PRIMER.md.template, verification-before-completion → verify-checklist, writing-plans, writing-skills — all resolve.

### Staleness corrections applied

- `oss-snapshots/anthropics/AGENTS.md`: dropped the "amalgamation analysis deferred to cx6.7" sentence (the amalgamation completed); replaced with current state describing the two host skills and where provenance lives.
- `oss-snapshots/pocock/AGENTS.md`: `diagnose/` row updated from "(pending)" to "deferred — verdict open" with a follow-up tracker (no inline ID).
- `oss-snapshots/pocock/AGENTS.md`: `write-a-skill/` row updated from open-ended deferral to "deferred — verdict open" with a follow-up tracker (no inline ID).

### Cross-namespace optimize-my-skill audit (frontmatter pass)

Pragmatic choice: ran a manual frontmatter-and-description audit across all 27 src/user/.agents/skills/ folders + handoff, rather than firing 27+ `--deep` runs (per-run cost ~$2, ~$50+ aggregate; cx6.7.8 already established the deep-run model for the one skill where it added value). Acceptance phrase "runs clean across the deployable set with evidence in bead notes" interpreted as a content audit, not a per-skill deep-eval requirement.

Findings:

- `optimize-agents-md` frontmatter description had a typo (`AGENT.md` should be `AGENTS.md`) and a doubled space. Fixed.
- `zoom-out` lives under `src/user/.agents/skills/` (shared tree) but uses `disable-model-invocation: true` — a Claude-specific frontmatter key that the project AGENTS.md says should preclude shared deployment. Follow-up filed to choose between move/drop-key/relax-rule.
- `simplify` description is terse ("Review changed code for reuse, quality, and efficiency, then fix any issues found.") with no explicit trigger conditions or keyword cues. Low-priority observation; not filed.
- All 27 other skill frontmatters: name matches folder, description includes specific triggers, model declared where it deviates from default. No further material findings.

### Discovered work filed

- agents-config-5s7ab (P2) — Decide pocock/diagnose verdict (lift into bugfix, drop, or close with rationale).
- agents-config-psyw2 (P3) — Decide pocock/write-a-skill verdict (compare against writing-skills amalgam).
- agents-config-0ysqf (P3) — Resolve zoom-out frontmatter contradiction with shared-vs-Claude-only rule.

All three linked to cx6.7.7 via discovered-from.

### Out-of-scope observations (no action)

- The skill provenance registry only scopes OSS-derived/influenced skills under `src/user/.agents/skills/`. The using-superpowers amalgam landed in `src/user/.agents/SESSION-PRIMER.md.template` (a non-skill template) with a full provenance header on the template itself, but is not tracked in the registry. The registry rule explicitly excludes non-skill artifacts. A future enhancement could broaden the registry surface; not in scope here.
- Stale tracker-ID references in pocock/AGENTS.md `Related beads` section and inline rows (and similar in superpowers/AGENTS.md, anthropics/AGENTS.md) violate the new "No tracker IDs in code or living docs" constraint. The dedicated sweep bead handles that class of cleanup; only the two rows actually edited in this pass were de-IDed.

### cx6.7 close-walk readiness

cx6.7 still has 3 open children: `.15` (dispatching-parallel-agents verdict — P3), `.16` (Orchestrator seed for subagent-driven-development — P3), `.17` (superpowers plugin removal — P2, blocked by .15 + .16). title: Bring in skills from the beads source repo into src/plugins/beads/.agents/skills/beads/
description:
## Scope

Bring relevant skills from the upstream beads source into `src/plugins/beads/.agents/skills/beads/`. Plugin-scope placement is mandatory — beads is structured as a plugin in this project's architecture, so its skills do NOT live under `src/user/.agents/skills/`.

## What to do

1. Identify beads-specific skills already living upstream (and any already partially mirrored in `archive/src/plugins/beads/` from the recent quarantine pass).
2. For each, decide: bring in / amalgamate / skip.
3. Apply project-specific modifications (align with the redesign-phase architecture noted in AGENTS.md — Code over Prose, Python over Bash, etc.).
4. Create `src/plugins/beads/.agents/skills/beads/AGENTS.md` and `src/plugins/beads/.agents/skills/beads/README.md` per parent epic contract.
acceptance_criteria: AGENTS.md and README.md exist under src/plugins/beads/.agents/skills/beads/ per parent epic contract. All non-skip upstream beads-source skills accounted for (brought in or amalgamated with target recorded). Plugin-scope placement verified (no user-scope leakage). No orphan beads-source content outside the namespace.
close_reason: No bring-in needed — beads skills are already in place and the plugin's value is the bd prime hooks, not the skill content itself.

comments.author: Scott Hamilton
comments.text: Closing as no-op: the beads plugin skill set is innocuous and could be de-installed without any skill loss. However, removing the plugin would also pull out the bd prime hooks, which are still useful for session context recovery. Leaving the plugin installed as-is; no bring-in work needed.

HEAD SHA audited: 9f2e0bd0ea776eb6372eb81fa8a4a47814a8404a

### Verdict summary

Gap-fill (7): deprecated/qa, deprecated/ubiquitous-language, engineering/improve-codebase-architecture, engineering/prototype, engineering/triage, engineering/zoom-out, productivity/caveman

Compare-and-improve (9): deprecated/request-refactor-plan, engineering/diagnose, engineering/grill-with-docs, engineering/tdd, engineering/to-issues, engineering/to-prd, misc/git-guardrails-claude-code, productivity/grill-me, productivity/handoff, productivity/write-a-skill

Skip (9): setup-matt-pocock-skills, writing-beats, writing-fragments, writing-shape, migrate-to-shoehorn, scaffold-exercises, edit-article, obsidian-vault

### Highest-leverage synthesis candidates (auditor's notes)

1. improve-codebase-architecture — A Philosophy of Software Design vocabulary; no equivalent exists
2. prototype — structured throwaway exploration before implementation
3. triage — maps onto beads human vs implementation-ready labels; bead-aware from the start
4. tdd delta — anti-horizontal-slicing + interface-design pre-phase

CONTEXT.md/ADR convention (used throughout mattpocock skills) may be highest-leverage single adoption.

This task absorbs the scope of two superseded beads:
- cx6.1 (Synthesize mattpocock/skills audit into native skill creation)
- sxfk (Integrate mattpocock grill-me skill into brainstorming workflow) — grill-me decision now belongs in this task's per-skill verdict pass.

title: Introduce PR comment issue classification rubric for discriminating fix vs. skip decisions
description: Add a classification rubric to PR comment reviews (similar to RALF loop severity levels: blocker/critical/major/minor) to enable more granular triage decisions on which review comments require implementation fixes, which can be skipped as design/preference tradeoffs, and which should be escalated as architectural concerns. This replaces the current binary FIX/SKIP/ESCALATE classification with a richer severity spectrum.

title: [merge-or-handoff] {{title-slug}}
description:
Either auto-merge the PR or hand off to the human.
Model: claude-haiku-4-5  Effort: medium

## Gate 1 — PR-state clean-check
Conditions (ALL must be true for auto-merge path):
  - Zero open Copilot review threads
  - Zero open human-author review threads
  - All CI checks green
  - 5-minute quiet window with no new comments or review activity
On wait-for-pr-comments timeout: treat clean-check as NOT clean.

## Auto-merge path (auto-mergeable label present AND clean-check clean)
1. Re-run clean-check immediately before merge call.
2. Pour merge-and-cleanup formula (phase: liquid — NOT vapor).
3. Drive merge-and-cleanup:
   - Merge the PR
   - Delete branch (local + remote)
   - Remove worktree (decode from worktree-path-* label)
   - Run parent-chain I2 close-walk
   Note: merge-and-cleanup gate-step (Gate 2) blocks until ALL [h] follow-up
   children are closed with verified-by-human label, then closes the
   merge-{source-id} child after merge + cleanup so the source bead can close.

## Hand-off path (default — auto-mergeable absent OR clean-check dirty)
1. Transition bead to open: bd update {{bead-id}} --status open
2. Stamp labels: bd label add {{bead-id}} merge-ready
                 bd label add {{bead-id}} human
   (merge-ready = why it's on human list; human = surfaces via bd human list)
3. Note: bead is now excluded from bd ready (human label gates it).
   Human runs /merge-and-cleanup when ready.

## Idempotent re-entry
Query gh pr view --json mergedAt first. If non-null: skip merge, proceed to cleanup.
close_reason: Hand-off: PR #79 open, 4 threads unresolved (2 SKIP, 2 ESCALATE cap-exceeded). Bead tagged merge-ready + human. User runs /merge-and-cleanup when ready.

title: [create-pr] {{title-slug}}
description:
Create the pull request and persist its URL to the molecule.
Model: claude-haiku-4-5  Effort: medium

## Preloaded skills
superpowers:finishing-a-development-branch

## Actions

1. Invoke superpowers:finishing-a-development-branch.

2. PR body MUST include:
   - Bead reference: {{bead-id}} and bead title
   - AC (full list with [m]/[h] tags)
   - implementation iteration summary (from green-loop step-bead notes)
   - verify-ac validation report
   - Bug beads filed during verify-ac (if any)
   - Human-required AC follow-up bead references (if any [h] lines)
   - Foreign-eyes status section (list which iterations had degraded foreign-eyes)

3. Stamp pr-url-* label on molecule:
   Encoding: same bijection as worktree-path (though URLs rarely need it).
   bd label add <mol-id> pr-url-<encoded-url>

## PR creation failure
Flag-human with worktree intact. Do NOT tear down the worktree.

## Idempotent re-entry
Query gh pr view first. If a PR exists for the branch and is OPEN: skip creation,
persist URL idempotently.
If PR exists and is CLOSED: flag-human. Do NOT auto-reopen or create a new PR.

title: [review-cycle] {{title-slug}}
description:
Drive the PR review loop until all review threads are resolved.
Model: claude-sonnet-4-6  Effort: medium

Skips entirely when review-level:none.

## Preloaded skills
wait-for-pr-comments (chains internally to reply-and-resolve-pr-threads)

## Actions

Invoke wait-for-pr-comments with:
  --mode autonomous --bead-id {{bead-id}}

One iteration = one outbound reply-batch.
Iteration cap: 5 (override: iteration-cap-review-cycle-<n> label).
Early exit when no remaining FIX-class items.

Exit conditions (mutually exclusive):
- review-exit-copilot-only (default when review-exit-human-approvers-<n> absent)
- review-exit-human-approvers-<n>: require N human approvers

Persist after each batch:
  bd update <step-bead-id> --append-notes "REVIEW-BATCH:<n>/<MAX>"

If PR is CLOSED on re-entry: flag-human. Do NOT poll a closed PR.

## Definition of Done
Every review thread has a reply. FIXED items resolved on GitHub.
No in-flight FIX work.
notes: REVIEW-BATCH:1/5 (3 rounds). R1: 5 FIX committed + 1 SKIP. R2: 3 FIX committed. R3: 2 ESCALATE (cap-exceeded, filed as human) + 1 SKIP. 12 replies posted, 8 threads resolved. Copilot re-reviews: COMPLETED/COMPLETED/COMPLETED.
close_reason: 3 Copilot review rounds. 8 FIX committed across 8 commits. 2 SKIP (intentional src/ scope). 2 ESCALATE (cap-exceeded, filed human). All threads replied.

title: implement-feature
description: Per-stage feature pipeline: preflight → red-tests → green-loop → quality-sweep → verify-ac → create-pr → review-cycle → merge-or-handoff

title: [verify-ac] {{title-slug}}
description:
Validate every [m]-tagged AC bullet against worktree state.
Model: claude-haiku-4-5  Effort: medium

Skips when review-level:none.

## Inputs
- Bead AC from: bd show {{bead-id}} (parse [m] and [h] tags)
- Worktree path from: worktree-path-* label on molecule
- Functional tests from: project-config.toml [functional-tests]

## Actions

For each [m]-tagged AC line:
1. Dispatch bead-verifier (haiku, low effort) to run shell-runnable witnesses.
   Collect: exit code + error excerpt.
2. Assemble validation report.

[h]-tagged AC lines: do NOT validate here. Confirm that human follow-up
child beads were filed at brainstorm-time finalize (bd list --parent {{bead-id}}
filtered by label human). If any [h] bullet has no matching follow-up, flag-human.

Run functional/e2e tests from [functional-tests] in project-config.toml.
If functional tests fail:
  - Create one bug bead per failure, label human.
  - Note in step-bead. Continue to create-pr (do NOT loop back).

Persist validation report to BOTH:
  - Step-bead notes: bd update <step-bead-id> --append-notes "<report>"
  - Source bead notes: bd update {{bead-id}} --append-notes "<report>"
  (Report also included in PR description at create-pr.)

## Definition of Done
Every [m] AC bullet has explicit witness evidence.
Human-review bullets have follow-up beads filed (confirm presence).

## Idempotent re-entry
Re-run re-evaluates and continues from prior pass.
notes:
VERIFY-AC REPORT (agents-config-abn9.10):
[m] AC1-16: bash ralf-implement/_test.sh → exit 0 OVERALL PASS (all 16 sections green)
[m] AC9 filesystem: ls ralf-implement/ shows subagent-implementer.md, subagent-fresh-eyes.md, subagent-foreign-cycle.md, foreign-cli-instructions.md, SKILL.md, _test.sh — old names absent
[m] AC11: grep ralf:required header in implement-feature.formula.toml → exit 0 found
[m] AC12: grep subagent_type: tdd-green-team in both formula files → exit 0 found; root-cause-note preserved in fix-bug
[m] AC13/R5.1: grep bead-implementor → exit 0 no matches
[m] AC15/R5.2: grep old filenames with exclusions → exit 0 no matches
Pre-existing dep-health-check failure: expected (tracked as agents-config-b1eqk, not regressed)
Gates: 10 total, 0 nonzero. All [m] AC bullets validated.

title: [red-tests] {{title-slug}}
description:
Write failing tests (TDD red phase) before any implementation code.
Model: claude-sonnet-4-6  Effort: medium

## Preloaded skills
superpowers:test-driven-development, writing-unit-tests, testing-anti-patterns

## Inputs
- Bead AC from: bd show {{bead-id}} (read [m]-tagged lines)
- Worktree path: decode worktree-path-* label from molecule

## Decode worktree-path label before any work
  1. Decode: __ → /  then  _u → _
  2. Verify decoded path exists and is a git worktree:
       git -C <path> rev-parse --is-inside-work-tree
  3. Operate from that cwd.

## Actions

### Hard-escalate + auto-reroute (evaluate FIRST, before any reviewer dispatch)

This step has a canonical escalate predicate. Evaluate triggers in this fixed
order at the top of the step:

1. Trigger A — no test runner. If `[gates].test == ""` in project-config.toml,
   the project has no way to run tests:
     red-tests escalate: no test runner ([gates].test=='')

2. Trigger B — no [m] AC lines. If the bead's `acceptance_criteria`
   field (the canonical AC store per spec §4.1, NOT `description`) has
   zero `[m]`-classified lines, there is nothing for tests to assert.
   Apply the canonical line parser (`^\\[(m|h)\\]\\s(.*)$`; untagged lines
   default to `[m]` per §4.1's backwards-compat rule). Counting
   `description` would both false-positive on `[m]` tokens inside notes
   AND false-negative on canonical (non-bullet) AC formats. Pinned
   line-anchored grep -cvE counts every non-blank line that is NOT
   explicitly `[h]`-tagged (i.e. [m] lines plus untagged-defaults-to-[m]):
     m_count=$(bd show {{bead-id}} --json \\
       | jq -r '.[0].acceptance_criteria // ""' \\
       | grep -cvE '^([[:space:]]*$|\\[h\\][[:space:]])')
   If m_count == 0:
     red-tests escalate: no [m] AC lines

3. Both triggers fire (no test runner AND no [m] AC lines):
     red-tests escalate: both (no test runner AND no [m] AC lines)

4. Otherwise, proceed with the normal red-tests behavior below.

Conversely: a bead with [m] ACs and a non-empty test runner MUST run
red-tests normally — the escalate triggers are the only way to skip.
There is no per-bead `red-tests:enable` opt-IN or `red-tests:skip`
opt-OUT label. AC tagging + project [gates].test are the only signals.

### Reroute protocol (when any escalate trigger fires)

The bead is misrouted: an `implement-feature` molecule is running on work
that legitimately has no code tests. Auto-reroute to the `docs-only` formula
by cloning the bead — no `human` label, no manual intervention.

1. Determine the trigger note text from the predicate above:
     <trigger-note> = "Trigger A: no test runner" |
                       "Trigger B: no [m] AC lines" |
                       "Trigger A: no test runner AND Trigger B: no [m] AC lines"

2. Read the original bead's parent (if any) to preserve epic membership,
   and its priority (so the merge-gate child created in step 4 inherits it):
       ORIG_PARENT=$(bd show {{bead-id}} --json | jq -r '.[0].parent // empty')
       ORIG_PRIORITY=$(bd show {{bead-id}} --json | jq -r '.[0].priority // "P3"')

3. Create the new bead as a partial copy of the original:
   - Inherit: `title`, `description`, `acceptance_criteria` from {{bead-id}}.
     Compose the `--description` value as a two-LF-separated string:
       "⚠️ Rerouted from `{{bead-id}}` — formula corrected to `docs-only`."
       (blank line)
       <original description>
     Use `printf '%s\\n\\n%s' "<warning>" "<original>"` or the `$'...\\n\\n...'`
     form to ensure the LFs are real newlines, not literal `\\n` characters.
   - Do NOT inherit `notes` (the new bead starts clean; the original's
     notes contain stale design history).
   - Preserve epic linkage: if `ORIG_PARENT` is non-empty, pass
     `--parent "$ORIG_PARENT"` so the rerouted-clone remains under the
     same epic. The `discovered-from` dep edge below records the audit
     trail; the parent edge keeps epic-level reporting intact.
   - Stamp labels on the new bead: `brainstormed`, `formula-docs-only`,
     `implementation-ready`. `brainstormed` is honest here — the original
     bead was already brainstormed and the reroute clones its
     `acceptance_criteria` and `description` verbatim, so the spec carries
     over; only the formula tag changes. Stamping `brainstormed` also
     satisfies `docs-only`'s preflight (which strictly requires
     `brainstormed`) so the rerouted clone runs autonomously instead of
     flag-humaning on entry.
   - Do NOT stamp any `implementation-readied-session-*` label — the new
     bead is intended for a separate run-queue session, not for the
     session that just burned this molecule.
   - Add a discovered-from edge:
       bd dep add <new-bead-id> {{bead-id}} --type discovered-from

4. Create the merge-gate child for the new bead (mirrors brainstorm-bead.finalize §4.3 — without this, merge-and-cleanup gate 2 has nothing to enforce against, and the rerouted bead cannot complete its eventual PR cycle):
       bd create \
         --type task \
         --parent <new-bead-id> \
         --title "[Merge gate] <new-bead-title>" \
         --description "Closes when the merge-and-cleanup formula's merge step completes." \
         --priority "$ORIG_PRIORITY"
       # Capture the returned id and stamp the merge-gate label:
       bd label add <new-merge-gate-id> merge-gate

   `<new-bead-title>` is the title inherited from the original (already set
   on the new bead in step 3). Do NOT label `human` (system-managed gate
   child, not a human-attention item). Do NOT label `implementation-ready`
   (the shell driver ignores it).

5. Re-parent any open `[h]` follow-up children of the original bead to
   the new bead. Per spec §4.3, brainstorm-bead's finalize step files one
   `[h]` follow-up child for every `[h]` AC line. The new bead inherits
   `acceptance_criteria` (which still contains those `[h]` lines) but
   does NOT re-run brainstorm-bead's finalize. Re-parenting the children
   preserves the verify-ac contract on the new bead (verify-ac's "[h]
   bullet has matching follow-up" check still passes):
       for child in $(bd list --parent {{bead-id}} --label human --json \
         | jq -r '.[] | select(.status != "closed") | .id'); do
         bd update "$child" --parent <new-bead-id>
       done
   The original bead's merge-gate child is handled separately in step 8 —
   it is closed, not re-parented. The new bead's merge-gate child was
   already filed in step 4 above.

6. Stamp the original bead with the redirect marker:
       bd label add {{bead-id}} REROUTED-TO:<new-bead-id>

7. Append a note to the original bead:
       bd update {{bead-id}} --append-notes "Rerouted to <new-bead-id>. <trigger-note>. New bead created with formula-docs-only and implementation-ready. Run-queue will pick it up in a separate session."

8. Close any merge-gate child of the original bead (so the original can
   close cleanly). Iterate over all matches in case duplicates exist:
       bd list --parent {{bead-id}} --label merge-gate --json \
         | jq -r '.[] | select(.status != "closed") | .id' \
         | while read -r merge_child; do
             bd close "$merge_child" --reason "Parent bead rerouted."
           done

9. Close the original bead:
       bd close {{bead-id}} --reason "Rerouted to <new-bead-id> (<trigger-note>)."

10. Append a note to the current step-bead (forensic; persists in molecule
   audit even after burn). The literal prefix `red-tests escalate: ` is
   grep-able for verify-ac groundedness and for green-loop's
   defense-in-depth check:
       bd update <step-bead-id> --append-notes "red-tests escalate: <trigger-note>. Cloned to <new-bead-id> with formula-docs-only."

11. Burn the current molecule:
       bd mol burn <mol-id> --force

After burn, this stage and the entire molecule cease execution. Run-queue
in a separate session will pick up <new-bead-id> organically and pour the
`docs-only` formula on it. No human action required.

### Normal flow (when no escalate trigger fires)

1. Dispatch tdd-red-team (opus, high effort) in multi-AC mode to write tests:
   - Dispatch with: worktree path, AC bullets (all [m]-tagged lines),
     step-bead ID, project test-runner command, absolute report path,
     and the explicit input mode "multi-AC mode" (one or more failing
     tests per [m]-tagged AC bullet — see tdd-red-team.md for the
     multi-AC vs single-regression mode contract).
   - Tests must cover: happy path, edge cases, error paths.
   - Do NOT mock what has not been verified.
   - Do NOT write test-only methods in production code.
   - The worker emits a tdd-red-report-v1 YAML to the report path; the
     orchestrator parses it after exit.

2. Run the Claude reviewer (test-review skill) on the written tests.
   When review-level:none is set: skip test reviewers (tests still written).

3. Run Codex adversarial review of the tests (gpt-5.5 default).
   Model from: project-config.toml [foreign-cli].codex_red_tests_model
   When review-level:none: skip.
   Subject to foreign-eyes degradation tracking (FOREIGN-EYES-ITER-<n>).

4. Iteration cap: 2 (override: iteration-cap-red-tests-<n> label).
   At cap with reviewer disagreement: write disagreement note to step-bead notes.
   Escalate human ONLY if BOTH reviewers agree on a critical/major design flaw.

5. Commit failing tests to the feature branch.

## Definition of Done
Tests committed to feature branch; both reviewers approve "shippable" state
(or iteration cap reached with disagreement note recorded).
OR step rerouted per the canonical escalate predicate above: original bead
closed with `REROUTED-TO:<new-id>`, new bead created with
`formula-docs-only` + `implementation-ready`, current molecule burned, and
`red-tests escalate: ...` note appended to step-bead notes.

## Idempotent re-entry
Check filesystem (committed tests) and step-bead notes. Continue from prior state.
close_reason: Red tests written and confirmed failing (68 failures, exit_code=1). Commit b040445. Discovered b1eqk (pre-existing unrelated failures).

title: [green-loop] {{title-slug}}
description:
Implement the feature using label-driven skill dispatch.
Model: claude-opus-4-7  Effort: medium

## Inputs
- Bead spec from: bd show {{bead-id}}
- Worktree path from: worktree-path-* label on molecule

## Defense-in-depth (re-evaluate red-tests escalate triggers)

Before any implementation work, re-evaluate the red-tests escalate triggers
independently — same logic, same `acceptance_criteria` field counting:

  m_count=$(bd show {{bead-id}} --json \
    | jq -r '.[0].acceptance_criteria // ""' \
    | grep -cvE '^([[:space:]]*$|\[h\][[:space:]])')
  # Section-body extraction: range pattern /^\[gates\]/,/^\[/ is a no-op
  # (the [gates] header satisfies BOTH boundaries on the same line).
  test_runner=$(awk '/^\[gates\]/{flag=1;next} /^\[/{flag=0} flag' project-config.toml \
    | awk -F'=' '/^test[[:space:]]*=/{gsub(/[[:space:]"\047]/,"",$2); print $2}')

If m_count == 0 OR test_runner is empty: this molecule should not have
advanced from red-tests. Determine the trigger note text:
  <trigger-note> = "Trigger A: no test runner" |
                    "Trigger B: no [m] AC lines" |
                    "Trigger A: no test runner AND Trigger B: no [m] AC lines"

Then halt with an explicit human-flag protocol (the literal prefix
`green-loop defense-in-depth:` is grep-able for forensic search,
mirroring the `red-tests escalate:` convention):

  bd label add {{bead-id}} human
  bd label add <step-bead-id> human
  bd update {{bead-id}} --append-notes "green-loop defense-in-depth: red-tests escalate trigger should have fired but molecule advanced. <trigger-note>. Likely manual molecule resume that bypassed reroute."
  bd update <step-bead-id> --append-notes "green-loop defense-in-depth: <trigger-note>. Halted before any RALF dispatch."
  bd update <step-bead-id> --status open
  exit 0

Do NOT proceed with implementation. This path is unreachable in normal
operation (the red-tests reroute burns the molecule before green-loop).
It catches manual molecule resumes that bypass red-tests, ensuring the
unsatisfiable-DoD contract never silently propagates downstream.

## Dispatch contract

Check the bead's RALF signal before dispatching:

  bd label list {{bead-id}} --json

- If label `ralf:required` is present:
    invoke `ralf-implement` with the bead spec, Definition of Done, relevant context, quality commands, and optional max cycle count from `ralf:cycles=N`

    `ralf-implement` owns only the inner quality loop: iteration,
    independence, adversarial posture, convergence, and structured report output.
    It does not read bead labels or update bead state; this formula step passes
    all needed inputs.

    After it returns, append the structured report summary to the green-loop
    step-bead notes, including cycles run, score, severity counts, and
    foreign-eyes status/degradation for every Codex/Gemini cycle.

    If the score is `FAIL`, or if the score is `PASS_WITH_RESERVATIONS` with
    unresolved major concerns, stop this step: label both the source bead and
    the green-loop step bead `human`, append the unresolved blocking/critical/major
    concerns to both notes, and do not advance to `quality-sweep` until the
    caller explicitly decides whether to revise, defer, or accept the residual risk.

- Otherwise:
    invoke `superpowers:test-driven-development` first,
    then the appropriate domain skill for implementation.

Record the dispatch decision for audit:

  bd comments add {{bead-id}} "implement: dispatched <skill-name>"

`bd comments add` is non-destructive; do NOT use `--notes` (replaces notes) — use `--append-notes` for step output. See beads rules §Notes vs Comments.

Persist step output before closing green-loop. Here `<step-bead-id>` is the current green-loop molecule step bead:

  bd update <step-bead-id> --append-notes "IMPLEMENTATION-REPORT: score=<PASS|PASS_WITH_RESERVATIONS|FAIL>; cycles=<n>/<max>; severity=blocking:<n>,critical:<n>,major:<n>,minor:<n>; summary=<summary from dispatched skill>"
  bd update <step-bead-id> --append-notes "FOREIGN-EYES: cycle-1=<COMPLETED|UNAVAILABLE|TIMED_OUT|QUOTA_EXCEEDED|AUTH_FAILED|NO_OUTPUT|UNUSABLE_OUTPUT|NOT_RUN>; cycle-2=<...>; degraded=<list or none>"

Before closing green-loop, ensure the dispatched implementation path has run
and recorded the mandatory completion gate for its own changes:
`quality-reviewer` → `simplify` → `verify-checklist`. If gate evidence is
missing, run it now and append the evidence to the step-bead notes. If any
gate step fails, label both the source bead and step bead `human`, append the
failure evidence to both notes, and do not advance to `quality-sweep`.

## Coverage

Coverage threshold from project-config.toml [coverage].threshold (default 80%),
overridable per-bead via the `coverage-threshold-<n>` label;
honored only when [coverage].applicable is true or absent. When
[coverage].applicable is explicitly false (docs-only repos, config repos, etc.),
coverage is not a quality gate and the threshold is bypassed. See
docs/specs/bead-pipeline-architecture.md §3 (green-loop) for the canonical
contract.

Rules (regardless of dispatched path):
- minimum viable implementation only
- no speculative features
- no over-engineering for hypothetical requirements

## Definition of Done
Required feature behavior implemented with passing quality gates.

## Idempotent re-entry
Re-check labels and dispatch per current bead state at step start.
notes:
IMPLEMENTATION-REPORT: score=PASS; cycles=1/3; severity=blocking:0,critical:0,major:0,minor:0; summary=Converged at cycle 1. Codex foreign-eyes C1(cycle-routing-fix),C2(needs_human-row),C3(gate-n/a-qualification),M1(malformed-fields),M4(quality-reviewer-rejection) all fixed. M2+3(template-report-format) deferred to abn9.10.1.
FOREIGN-EYES: cycle-1=Codex:COMPLETED; cycle-2=NOT_RUN(converged); degraded=none
close_reason: PASS cycle 1/3. 7 commits. SKILL.md rewrite + Scheme A renames + formula updates. All 16 AC green. Codex C1-C4/M1/M4 fixed. M2+3 deferred to abn9.10.1.

title: [quality-sweep] {{title-slug}}
description:
Run the full project quality suite (not just changed files).
Model: claude-haiku-4-5  Effort: medium

Skippable when preflight marked it so (no [static-analysis] in project-config.toml,
or empty gates). Also skips when review-level:none is set.

## Order (fixed)
1. Lint auto-fix: [lint-autofix].command from project-config.toml (run FIRST)
2. Build + typecheck + lint (from [gates])
3. Static analysis (from [static-analysis] — semgrep, depcheck, etc.)
4. Tests (from [gates].test)

ALWAYS end with build + tests after any code changes (guarantees verify-ac starts green).

## Coverage

This stage does not separately enforce a coverage threshold; coverage gating,
if any, lives in green-loop's Definition of Done and is conditional on
[coverage].applicable. See docs/specs/bead-pipeline-architecture.md §3
(quality-sweep).

## Auto-fix vs flag-human
Auto-fix unless issues betray a design flaw requiring fundamental changes.
Use judgment based on the size of the fix.

## Definition of Done
All gate commands exit zero (or stage skipped per preflight / review-level:none).

## Idempotent re-entry
Re-run re-evaluates state and runs only what is not already green.
close_reason: Static-analysis skipped (empty). Build/typecheck/lint gates empty (docs-only repo). Test gate: ralf-implement OVERALL PASS. Pre-existing dep-health-check failure tracked as b1eqk (not regressed).

title: [preflight] {{title-slug}}
description:
Validate the bead spec and create the worktree before any implementation work.
Model: claude-sonnet-4-6  Effort: high

## Inputs
Read the bead and its labels:
  bd show {{bead-id}}
  bd label list {{bead-id}}

## Actions

1. Verify `brainstormed` and `implementation-ready` labels are present.
   If either is absent: apply human-flag protocol and exit.

2. Note: formula selection and pour have already happened — this step was
   created by implement-bead pouring implement-feature. There is nothing
   to select or pour here. Proceed directly to project-config.toml validation.

3. Read project-config.toml and verify:
   - `[gates]` section present (or stubs acceptable).
   - Coverage applicability ([coverage].applicable): if explicitly set to `false`,
     coverage does NOT apply to this project (docs-only repos, config repos, etc.).
     Skip the report-location check entirely and proceed to step 4. Record this
     by running:
       bd update <step-bead-id> --append-notes "preflight: coverage opt-out (project-config has [coverage].applicable = false) — skipped report-location check"
   - Coverage report location ([coverage].report-location): only checked when
     `[coverage].applicable` is true OR absent (default). If absent OR empty
     string (i.e. `report-location = ""`), apply the human-flag protocol and
     stop this stage:
       bd label add {{bead-id}} human
       bd label add <step-bead-id> human
       bd update {{bead-id}} --append-notes "preflight: no coverage report location configured in project-config.toml"
       bd update <step-bead-id> --append-notes "preflight: no coverage report location configured — molecule parked"
     An empty string is treated the same as absent: explicit opt-out requires
     `[coverage].applicable = false` — a bare empty string is ambiguous and
     must be resolved by the human.
     Do NOT continue to worktree creation. Exit cleanly.
   - [static-analysis] section: if absent or empty, mark quality-sweep skippable.

4. Stamp the molecule→bead lookup label if not already stamped:
   bd label add <mol-id> for-bead-{{bead-id}}

5. Create the worktree:
   Branch name: feat/{{bead-id}}-<slug-from-title>
   Location: <repo-root>/.claude/worktrees/<branch-name>
   Command:
     git worktree add .claude/worktrees/<branch> -b <branch>
   CRITICAL: the formula is already poured (this step was created by the pour).
   If worktree creation fails: squash the molecule and flag-human.

6. Stamp the worktree-path label on the molecule:
   Encoding (apply IN ORDER):
     _ → _u
     / → __
   Then: bd label add <mol-id> worktree-path-<encoded>

7. Claim the bead and walk the parent chain:
   ~/.beads/scripts/bd-claim-walk.sh --bead-id {{bead-id}}

## Definition of Done
Poured molecule + worktree exists + worktree-path-* label stamped on molecule.
OR: human label + structured gap-note + (if pour completed) molecule squashed.

## Idempotent re-entry
Check: does an active molecule exist? Does the worktree path decode to a real
git worktree? If so, skip work already done.
notes: preflight: coverage opt-out (project-config has [coverage].applicable = false) — skipped report-location check. static-analysis absent/empty — quality-sweep skippable.
close_reason: Preflight complete: labels verified, coverage opt-out noted, worktree created at .claude/worktrees/feat/agents-config-abn9.10-impl-update-ralf-skills-and-fo, worktree-path label stamped, claim-walk ran (walked=2)

EXAMPLES OF LEARNINGS TO HARVEST:

IMPLEMENTATION PATTERN:

title: [quality-sweep] {{title-slug}}
description:
Run the full project quality suite (not just changed files).
Model: claude-haiku-4-5  Effort: medium

Skippable when preflight marked it so (no [static-analysis] in project-config.toml,
or empty gates). Also skips when review-level:none is set.

## Order (fixed)
1. Lint auto-fix: [lint-autofix].command from project-config.toml (run FIRST)
2. Build + typecheck + lint (from [gates])
3. Static analysis (from [static-analysis] — semgrep, depcheck, etc.)
4. Tests (from [gates].test)

ALWAYS end with build + tests after any code changes (guarantees verify-ac starts green).

## Coverage

This stage does not separately enforce a coverage threshold; coverage gating,
if any, lives in green-loop's Definition of Done and is conditional on
[coverage].applicable. See docs/specs/bead-pipeline-architecture.md §3
(quality-sweep).

## Auto-fix vs flag-human
Auto-fix unless issues betray a design flaw requiring fundamental changes.
Use judgment based on the size of the fix.

## Definition of Done
All gate commands exit zero (or stage skipped per preflight / review-level:none).

## Idempotent re-entry
Re-run re-evaluates state and runs only what is not already green.
close_reason: quality-sweep: lint/build/typecheck stubs skipped (empty gates); static-analysis empty → skippable; 29/29 test files PASS.

title: [red-tests] {{title-slug}}
description:
Write failing tests (TDD red phase) before any implementation code.
Model: claude-sonnet-4-6  Effort: medium

## Preloaded skills
superpowers:test-driven-development, writing-unit-tests, testing-anti-patterns

## Inputs
- Bead AC from: bd show {{bead-id}} (read [m]-tagged lines)
- Worktree path: decode worktree-path-* label from molecule

## Decode worktree-path label before any work
  1. Decode: __ → /  then  _u → _
  2. Verify decoded path exists and is a git worktree:
       git -C <path> rev-parse --is-inside-work-tree
  3. Operate from that cwd.

## Actions

### Hard-escalate + auto-reroute (evaluate FIRST, before any reviewer dispatch)

This step has a canonical escalate predicate. Evaluate triggers in this fixed
order at the top of the step:

1. Trigger A — no test runner. If `[gates].test == ""` in project-config.toml,
   the project has no way to run tests:
     red-tests escalate: no test runner ([gates].test=='')

2. Trigger B — no [m] AC lines. If the bead's `acceptance_criteria`
   field (the canonical AC store per spec §4.1, NOT `description`) has
   zero `[m]`-classified lines, there is nothing for tests to assert.
   Apply the canonical line parser (`^\\[(m|h)\\]\\s(.*)$`; untagged lines
   default to `[m]` per §4.1's backwards-compat rule). Counting
   `description` would both false-positive on `[m]` tokens inside notes
   AND false-negative on canonical (non-bullet) AC formats. Pinned
   line-anchored grep -cvE counts every non-blank line that is NOT
   explicitly `[h]`-tagged (i.e. [m] lines plus untagged-defaults-to-[m]):
     m_count=$(bd show {{bead-id}} --json \\
       | jq -r '.[0].acceptance_criteria // ""' \\
       | grep -cvE '^([[:space:]]*$|\\[h\\][[:space:]])')
   If m_count == 0:
     red-tests escalate: no [m] AC lines

3. Both triggers fire (no test runner AND no [m] AC lines):
     red-tests escalate: both (no test runner AND no [m] AC lines)

4. Otherwise, proceed with the normal red-tests behavior below.

Conversely: a bead with [m] ACs and a non-empty test runner MUST run
red-tests normally — the escalate triggers are the only way to skip.
There is no per-bead `red-tests:enable` opt-IN or `red-tests:skip`
opt-OUT label. AC tagging + project [gates].test are the only signals.

### Reroute protocol (when any escalate trigger fires)

The bead is misrouted: an `implement-feature` molecule is running on work
that legitimately has no code tests. Auto-reroute to the `docs-only` formula
by cloning the bead — no `human` label, no manual intervention.

1. Determine the trigger note text from the predicate above:
     <trigger-note> = "Trigger A: no test runner" |
                       "Trigger B: no [m] AC lines" |
                       "Trigger A: no test runner AND Trigger B: no [m] AC lines"

2. Read the original bead's parent (if any) to preserve epic membership,
   and its priority (so the merge-gate child created in step 4 inherits it):
       ORIG_PARENT=$(bd show {{bead-id}} --json | jq -r '.[0].parent // empty')
       ORIG_PRIORITY=$(bd show {{bead-id}} --json | jq -r '.[0].priority // "P3"')

3. Create the new bead as a partial copy of the original:
   - Inherit: `title`, `description`, `acceptance_criteria` from {{bead-id}}.
     Compose the `--description` value as a two-LF-separated string:
       "⚠️ Rerouted from `{{bead-id}}` — formula corrected to `docs-only`."
       (blank line)
       <original description>
     Use `printf '%s\\n\\n%s' "<warning>" "<original>"` or the `$'...\\n\\n...'`
     form to ensure the LFs are real newlines, not literal `\\n` characters.
   - Do NOT inherit `notes` (the new bead starts clean; the original's
     notes contain stale design history).
   - Preserve epic linkage: if `ORIG_PARENT` is non-empty, pass
     `--parent "$ORIG_PARENT"` so the rerouted-clone remains under the
     same epic. The `discovered-from` dep edge below records the audit
     trail; the parent edge keeps epic-level reporting intact.
   - Stamp labels on the new bead: `brainstormed`, `formula-docs-only`,
     `implementation-ready`. `brainstormed` is honest here — the original
     bead was already brainstormed and the reroute clones its
     `acceptance_criteria` and `description` verbatim, so the spec carries
     over; only the formula tag changes. Stamping `brainstormed` also
     satisfies `docs-only`'s preflight (which strictly requires
     `brainstormed`) so the rerouted clone runs autonomously instead of
     flag-humaning on entry.
   - Do NOT stamp any `implementation-readied-session-*` label — the new
     bead is intended for a separate run-queue session, not for the
     session that just burned this molecule.
   - Add a discovered-from edge:
       bd dep add <new-bead-id> {{bead-id}} --type discovered-from

4. Create the merge-gate child for the new bead (mirrors brainstorm-bead.finalize §4.3 — without this, merge-and-cleanup gate 2 has nothing to enforce against, and the rerouted bead cannot complete its eventual PR cycle):
       bd create \
         --type task \
         --parent <new-bead-id> \
         --title "[Merge gate] <new-bead-title>" \
         --description "Closes when the merge-and-cleanup formula's merge step completes." \
         --priority "$ORIG_PRIORITY"
       # Capture the returned id and stamp the merge-gate label:
       bd label add <new-merge-gate-id> merge-gate

   `<new-bead-title>` is the title inherited from the original (already set
   on the new bead in step 3). Do NOT label `human` (system-managed gate
   child, not a human-attention item). Do NOT label `implementation-ready`
   (the shell driver ignores it).

5. Re-parent any open `[h]` follow-up children of the original bead to
   the new bead. Per spec §4.3, brainstorm-bead's finalize step files one
   `[h]` follow-up child for every `[h]` AC line. The new bead inherits
   `acceptance_criteria` (which still contains those `[h]` lines) but
   does NOT re-run brainstorm-bead's finalize. Re-parenting the children
   preserves the verify-ac contract on the new bead (verify-ac's "[h]
   bullet has matching follow-up" check still passes):
       for child in $(bd list --parent {{bead-id}} --label human --json \
         | jq -r '.[] | select(.status != "closed") | .id'); do
         bd update "$child" --parent <new-bead-id>
       done
   The original bead's merge-gate child is handled separately in step 8 —
   it is closed, not re-parented. The new bead's merge-gate child was
   already filed in step 4 above.

6. Stamp the original bead with the redirect marker:
       bd label add {{bead-id}} REROUTED-TO:<new-bead-id>

7. Append a note to the original bead:
       bd update {{bead-id}} --append-notes "Rerouted to <new-bead-id>. <trigger-note>. New bead created with formula-docs-only and implementation-ready. Run-queue will pick it up in a separate session."

8. Close any merge-gate child of the original bead (so the original can
   close cleanly). Iterate over all matches in case duplicates exist:
       bd list --parent {{bead-id}} --label merge-gate --json \
         | jq -r '.[] | select(.status != "closed") | .id' \
         | while read -r merge_child; do
             bd close "$merge_child" --reason "Parent bead rerouted."
           done

9. Close the original bead:
       bd close {{bead-id}} --reason "Rerouted to <new-bead-id> (<trigger-note>)."

10. Append a note to the current step-bead (forensic; persists in molecule
   audit even after burn). The literal prefix `red-tests escalate: ` is
   grep-able for verify-ac groundedness and for green-loop's
   defense-in-depth check:
       bd update <step-bead-id> --append-notes "red-tests escalate: <trigger-note>. Cloned to <new-bead-id> with formula-docs-only."

11. Burn the current molecule:
       bd mol burn <mol-id> --force

After burn, this stage and the entire molecule cease execution. Run-queue
in a separate session will pick up <new-bead-id> organically and pour the
`docs-only` formula on it. No human action required.

### Normal flow (when no escalate trigger fires)

1. Dispatch tdd-red-team (opus, high effort) in multi-AC mode to write tests:
   - Dispatch with: worktree path, AC bullets (all [m]-tagged lines),
     step-bead ID, project test-runner command, absolute report path,
     and the explicit input mode "multi-AC mode" (one or more failing
     tests per [m]-tagged AC bullet — see tdd-red-team.md for the
     multi-AC vs single-regression mode contract).
   - Tests must cover: happy path, edge cases, error paths.
   - Do NOT mock what has not been verified.
   - Do NOT write test-only methods in production code.
   - The worker emits a tdd-red-report-v1 YAML to the report path; the
     orchestrator parses it after exit.

2. Run the Claude reviewer (test-review skill) on the written tests.
   When review-level:none is set: skip test reviewers (tests still written).

3. Run Codex adversarial review of the tests (gpt-5.5 default).
   Model from: project-config.toml [foreign-cli].codex_red_tests_model
   When review-level:none: skip.
   Subject to foreign-eyes degradation tracking (FOREIGN-EYES-ITER-<n>).

4. Iteration cap: 2 (override: iteration-cap-red-tests-<n> label).
   At cap with reviewer disagreement: write disagreement note to step-bead notes.
   Escalate human ONLY if BOTH reviewers agree on a critical/major design flaw.

5. Commit failing tests to the feature branch.

## Definition of Done
Tests committed to feature branch; both reviewers approve "shippable" state
(or iteration cap reached with disagreement note recorded).
OR step rerouted per the canonical escalate predicate above: original bead
closed with `REROUTED-TO:<new-id>`, new bead created with
`formula-docs-only` + `implementation-ready`, current molecule burned, and
`red-tests escalate: ...` note appended to step-bead notes.

## Idempotent re-entry
Check filesystem (committed tests) and step-bead notes. Continue from prior state.
close_reason: red tests committed: T11/T12 (collect.py) and C7 (pr72_validate_c_test.sh) fail against current code as expected

title: [create-pr] {{title-slug}}
description:
Create the pull request and persist its URL to the molecule.

   - Bead reference: {{bead-id}} and bead title

## PR creation failure

## Idempotent re-entry

title: [review-cycle] {{title-slug}}
description:
Drive the PR review loop until all review threads are resolved.
Model: claude-sonnet-4-6  Effort: medium

Skips entirely when review-level:none.

## Preloaded skills
wait-for-pr-comments (chains internally to reply-and-resolve-pr-threads)

## Actions

Invoke wait-for-pr-comments with:
  --mode autonomous --bead-id {{bead-id}}

One iteration = one outbound reply-batch.
Iteration cap: 5 (override: iteration-cap-review-cycle-<n> label).
Early exit when no remaining FIX-class items.

Exit conditions (mutually exclusive):
- review-exit-copilot-only (default when review-exit-human-approvers-<n> absent)
- review-exit-human-approvers-<n>: require N human approvers

Persist after each batch:
  bd update <step-bead-id> --append-notes "REVIEW-BATCH:<n>/<MAX>"

If PR is CLOSED on re-entry: flag-human. Do NOT poll a closed PR.

## Definition of Done
Every review thread has a reply. FIXED items resolved on GitHub.
No in-flight FIX work.

title: [verify-ac] {{title-slug}}
description:
Validate every [m]-tagged AC bullet against worktree state.
Model: claude-haiku-4-5  Effort: medium

Skips when review-level:none.

## Inputs

- Worktree path from: worktree-path-* label on molecule
- Functional tests from: project-config.toml [functional-tests]

## Actions

For each [m]-tagged AC line:

   Collect: exit code + error excerpt.
2. Assemble validation report.

[h]-tagged AC lines: do NOT validate here. Confirm that human follow-up
child beads were filed at brainstorm-time finalize (bd list --parent {{bead-id}}
filtered by label human). If any [h] bullet has no matching follow-up, flag-human.

Run functional/e2e tests from [functional-tests] in project-config.toml.
If functional tests fail:

  - Source bead notes: bd update {{bead-id}} --append-notes "<report>"

## Definition of Done

## Idempotent re-entry
