title: Sub-design: Quiescence model (Section 4) — prgroom CLI
description:
Flesh out Section 4 (Quiescence model) of the prgroom design doc at `docs/plans/2026-05-12-prgroom-cli-design.md`.

## Status (2026-05-25)

Design phase complete. Section 4 spec is ratified, covering:

- Hard gates + idle timer quiescence predicate (G_REVIEWERS, G_CI, G_DISPOSITIONS, G_NO_BLOCKERS + Idle)
- Per-reviewer auto-decline timeouts (`review_start_timeout=3m`, `review_finish_timeout=15m`)
- `waitLocked` internals with 5 wake-event paths + resumability via absolute UTC timestamps
- Human-review merge constraint (NOT a lifecycle blocker) with `human-approved` label OR human PR approval (Bot-filtered)
- Auto-request `human-review-required` label on lifecycle gating (cap-trip, escalated, failed)
- `prgroom status --json` auto-merge eligibility contract (handoff surface for future `gmxo`/`td39`)
- Configuration surface (5 knobs at `quiescence.*`) per §3.5 precedence

**Implementation plan:** `docs/plans/2026-05-25-prgroom-quiescence-impl.md` (16 tasks, TDD discipline).

**Implementation bead:** to be filed separately under fca6 epic when ready; do NOT auto-claim this bead.

## Coverage originally required (per brainstorm scope)

- Probability function inputs: time-since-last-update, required-reviewer statuses (every `Required=true` reviewer must be in `review_found` or `declined` for quiescence), open-thread count, CI state, items-with-no-Disposition count
- Threshold parameters and how they're set (flags? config file? per-repo `.prgroom.toml`? heuristics bead?)
- `wait` verb semantics: how long to sleep, what events break the wait
- `human-review-required` upstream signal: how marked (brainstorm? hand-flagged label?), how the CLI reads it, override semantics
- Hard cap parallel to current `wait-for-pr-comments` round-3 cap
- Auto-merge eligibility check signals (handed off to future merge-gate / `td39`; outside MVP)

Note: "probability function" reframed during brainstorm as **hard gates + idle timer** for operator debuggability — see §4 of the design doc.

## Related beads

- `agents-config-fca6.11` (quarantined): Tick-based run model — rework §3.3 + §4 waitLocked semantics
- `agents-config-fca6.12` (P1): Architecture diagrams (C4 levels 1-3, sequence, data)
acceptance_criteria: Section 4 of docs/plans/2026-05-12-prgroom-cli-design.md fleshed; probability function specified concretely enough to be implementation-ready; thresholds and config decisions made.
close_reason: Design phase complete: Section 4 (Quiescence model) ratified in 2026-05-12-prgroom-cli-design.md and 16-task TDD plan written to docs/plans/2026-05-25-prgroom-quiescence-impl.md. Merged via PR #104 (commit e9cff44). Implementation will be a separate future bead under the fca6 epic.

title: Sub-design: Lifecycle state machine (Section 3) — prgroom CLI
description:
Flesh out Section 3 (Lifecycle state machine) of the prgroom design doc at `docs/plans/2026-05-12-prgroom-cli-design.md`.

Coverage required:
- Exact phase transitions: which verb sets which `Phase`; valid transitions; terminal phases
- Phase-to-verb mapping for the `run` aggregate verb (when does it invoke cluster vs fix vs rereview vs wait)
- Round-counter semantics; hard-cap behavior
- Failure handling: when a verb fails (push fails, GraphQL error, gh API error), what's the next state
- Full precondition error-code registry (referenced from Section 1's cross-cutting precondition gating)

The lifecycle ASCII diagram in Section 2 is the starting point — flesh it into a complete state machine.
acceptance_criteria: Section 3 of docs/plans/2026-05-12-prgroom-cli-design.md drafted at the same detail level as Sections 1, 2, 5, 8 (phase state graph, phase × verb matrix, run algorithm pseudocode, round-counter rules, hard-cap behavior, failure tier model, error-code registry). All 11 per-PR lifecycle verbs (poll, cluster, fix, push, reply, resolve, rereview, wait, resolve-escalated, status, run) covered in the transition matrix; the optional cross-PR sweep verb is documented separately. Error-code registry enumerates PRECONDITION_*, RUNTIME_*, CONTRACT_*, STATE_*, LIFECYCLE_* categories with what/why/how strings per Section 1's structured-stderr contract. The run pseudocode uses *Locked internal lifecycle functions to avoid nested lock acquisition and releases the PR lock when the phase reaches terminal-for-CLI states (quiesced/human-gated/merged). Build passes. Tests pass.
notes:
# Section 3 spec summary

Full spec lives in the design doc: docs/plans/2026-05-12-prgroom-cli-design.md, Section 3.

## Coverage delivered

- 3.1 Phase state graph (expanded from Section 2's sketch; every edge labeled with verb+condition trigger)
- 3.2 Phase × verb transition matrix (10 verbs × 6 phases; precondition-fail / no-op / state-change pinned per cell)
- 3.3 `run` aggregate verb algorithm (pseudocode for the long-running blocking loop; interactive variant)
- 3.4 Round-counter semantics (increment only on push that uploaded ≥1 commit; external-push attribution)
- 3.5 Hard-cap behavior (default MaxRounds=3, --max-rounds flag, PRGROOM_MAX_ROUNDS env, .prgroom.toml override)
- 3.6 Failure tier model (extends Section 1's 3-tier precondition gating into runtime: TRANSIENT / TERMINAL_USER / CONTRACT_AUDIT / STATE / LIFECYCLE_CAP; sysexits.h exit codes)
- 3.7 Error-code registry (PRECONDITION_*, RUNTIME_*, CONTRACT_*, STATE_*, LIFECYCLE_*; each with what/why/how)

## Key design decisions during brainstorming

1. `run --autonomous` is **long-running blocking** (locks PR for full lifecycle until terminal-for-CLI phase). Chosen over one-shot-per-cron-tick (Option B) and hybrid (Option C). Rationale: matches Section 1's wording; simpler caller-side semantics; partial-state interleaving is already prevented by per-verb atomic Write so the long lock-hold does not introduce new failure modes.
2. **Round increments on visible-to-reviewer push only** — zero-commit fix cycles count toward quiescence (§4) but not the hard cap. Manual external pushes observed by poll also increment Round to keep the counter honest under mixed-mode operation.
3. **End-of-cycle phase resolution is a priority-ordered cascade** (hard-cap > HumanReviewRequired > unresolved escalations > commits-pushed > quiescence > stay-awaiting-review) rather than an unordered set of rules, so the transition logic is deterministic.
4. **Failure tiers extend the Section 1 precondition pattern** rather than introduce a parallel taxonomy. TRANSIENT → exit non-zero, no phase change, scheduler retries. TERMINAL_USER → human-gated + Sink escalation. CONTRACT_AUDIT → per-item failed disposition, may promote phase.
5. **Error codes use `<CATEGORY>_<SPECIFIC>` structure** with stable identifiers; adding new codes is non-breaking, renaming is breaking.

## Terminal phases

- Graph-terminal: `merged` only
- Terminal-for-CLI (autonomous re-entry requires external signal): `quiesced`, `human-gated`, `merged`
- Re-entry paths: `resolve-escalated` (operator), `poll` observing external push or merge

## Open hand-offs to other sections

- §3.5 hard-cap interaction with §4 quiescence threshold trip — both can fire in the same end-of-cycle resolution; the cascade order in §3.2 disambiguates.
- §3.6 `LIFECYCLE_HUMAN_REVIEW_REQUIRED` is set by an upstream signal whose mechanism is §4's responsibility.
- §3.3 `run` calls `wait` between cycles; `wait`'s timeout + break conditions are §4's responsibility.


## Cycle 1 revisions (applied 2026-05-12)

Cycle 1 was a parallel Codex experiment (V1 fresh-eyes-prompt as-is; V2 same + confidence-loop appendix), both at model gpt-5.5. Reports captured in .beads/worker-audit/agents-config-wisp-do2t/codex-v1.md and codex-v2.md. Consolidated 13 findings (2 BLOCKING / 4 CRITICAL / 6 MAJOR / 1 MINOR after dedupe). All addressed in the doc:

- **Lock-model fix (Findings 1, 2).** Section 2 transactional model rewritten to introduce public locking wrappers + lock-assuming `*Locked` internal lifecycle functions. `run` is the only verb that acquires the lock once and calls multiple `*Locked` internals in sequence; on terminal-for-CLI phase it returns and releases the lock so resolve-escalated can acquire.
- **Diagram fix (Finding 4).** Section 3.1 ASCII diagram redrawn — '≥1 commit pushed' edge now loops back to awaiting-review, not merged. Terminal/non-terminal edge labels disambiguated.
- **rereview integration (Finding 3).** Section 3.3 run algorithm now calls rereviewLocked after a successful push when required bot reviewers need fresh review.
- **Self-heal disambiguation (Finding 5).** Section 3.2 matrix preamble clarifies cells show terminal --no-prework behavior; default self-heal auto-runs missing deterministic prework.
- **Failure tiers wired into pseudocode (Finding 6).** Section 3.3 run pseudocode now has handle_verb_error() that maps each tier to action (continue, propagate, transition to human-gated, set LastError, file Sink event).
- **CONTRACT_FIX_AUDIT_FAILED resolution (Finding 5/V2).** Section 3.2 end-of-cycle priority 3 now explicitly routes any unresolved Disposition.Kind == failed (from contract OR runtime cause OR agent-reported) to human-gated.
- **HumanReviewRequired routing (Finding 12).** Section 2 + Section 3.2 priority 2 reconciled: HumanReviewRequired=true always routes to human-gated, never quiesced.
- **Round off-by-one fix (Finding 7).** Hard-cap check moved pre-push in run pseudocode (§3.3, §3.5). With MaxRounds=3 the visible push history is exactly initial (Round=1) → fix-push #1 (Round=2) → fix-push #2 (Round=3) → cap blocks fix-push #3.
- **CLI-vs-external SHA attribution (Finding 8).** Section 2 schema gains LastPushedHeadSHA field; Section 3.4 specifies the attribution rule (CLI push sets LastPushedHeadSHA in same write as Round++; external push leaves it untouched and pollLocked increments Round).
- **Commit-queue detection (Finding 9).** Section 3.4 specifies that queued commits are derived from git rev-list @{upstream}..HEAD (git is source of truth, no separate state field); crash-after-fix-before-push recovers idempotently via re-invocation.
- **EscalationSink always exists (Finding 11).** Section 3.2 priority 4 rewritten: any unresolved escalated disposition routes to human-gated and files one Sink event per cycle (deduped). The 'no Sink resolution path' branch and 'autonomous-with-no-autodefer' language are deleted as undefined.
- **AC verb count (Finding 10).** AC updated to '11 per-PR lifecycle verbs' with sweep called out as the cross-PR aggregator outside the matrix.
- **STATE_LOCK_STALE clarification (Finding 13).** §3.7 now distinguishes operational notice (NOTICE_LOCK_STALE_CLEANED, advisory only, exit 0, no phase change) from failure-tier STATE_CORRUPT (genuine state corruption, human-gated, exit 78).


## Cycle 2 revisions (applied 2026-05-12)

Cycle 2 was a Claude general-purpose fresh-eyes pass (.beads/worker-audit/agents-config-wisp-do2t/claude-cycle2.md). Found 0 BLOCKING / 6 CRITICAL / 13 MAJOR / 9 MINOR. Cap extended to 4 cycles by operator. Cycle 2 revisions applied; addressing all 6 criticals + 10 of 13 majors + 5 of 9 minors:

- **C1 — Merge edges (§3.1).** Added textual note: every non-terminal phase transitions to merged when poll observes PR-closed; diagram edges omitted for clarity but enumerated in §3.2 matrix.
- **C2 — wait/fixes-pending (§3.2, §3.7).** New code PRECONDITION_WAIT_NOT_APPLICABLE (exit 2 EX_USAGE) replaces the misleading PRECONDITION_NO_WORK label in the matrix cell.
- **C3 — failed disposition gating (§3.2, §3.6).** Unified to 'any item with Disposition.Kind == failed → human-gated, regardless of underlying cause'. §3.6 CONTRACT_AUDIT_FAILED row updated to match.
- **C4 — cap predicate (§3.6, §3.7).** LIFECYCLE_CAP and LIFECYCLE_HARD_CAP_EXCEEDED entries now use the pre-push guard predicate (has_queued_fix_commits(state) AND Round >= MaxRounds), aligned with §3.3/§3.5.
- **C5 — external push + cap silent steal (§3.5).** Rewrote 'External pushes and the cap' bullet to explicitly accept the behavior with rationale; pushLocked emits a stderr warning when approaching MaxRounds.
- **C6 — Round init (§3.4).** Pinned zero-value Round=0; first successful pollLocked observing any HEAD (detected by LastPollSHA == '') sets Round=1 unconditionally; attribution rules apply only after Round>=1.
- **M7/M21/M23 — Escalation functions and dedup (§3.3).** Inline definitions for escalate() and escalate_if_needed(); per-item dedup via Disposition.EscalationFiled flag (already in §2 schema); handle_verb_error and resolver share the same flag.
- **M8 — Lock-hold prose (§3.3).** Tightened: lock held continuously through each cycle's full verb sequence; defer release fires only on runLocked return.
- **M9 — NO_UNREPLIED vs NO_UNRESOLVED (§3.2, §3.7).** Split codes; matrix cells now correctly label success-no-op behavior.
- **M10 — resolve-escalated clearable conditions (§3.2).** Enumerated which gating conditions resolve-escalated CAN clear (escalated items only) and which it CANNOT (hard-cap, HumanReviewRequired, STATE_CORRUPT, failed items).
- **M11 — fix matrix cell (§3.2).** Reworded: fix sets Disposition but does NOT change phase; resolver handles all phase consequences.
- **M12 — Priority-7 rationale (§3.2).** Added one-paragraph explanation of the all-skipped/wont_fix/deferred path back to awaiting-review.
- **M13 — Retry policy (§3.6).** Clarified: 3 total attempts (initial + 2 retries) with 1s/4s backoff; Retry-After header honored when present.
- **M14 — ErrNotFound handler (§3.3).** Pseudocode now bootstraps zero-value state when tracker.Read returns ErrNotFound on first invocation.
- **M15 — cluster/awaiting-review (§3.2).** Removed dead-code conditional; awaiting-review has no items by definition.
- **M16 — Force-push attribution (§3.4).** Documented best-effort attribution; force-push/rebase edge cases accepted with rationale.
- **M17 — LastError clearing (§3.5).** Pinned: cleared automatically on successful cycle completion; no clear-error verb needed.
- **M18 — flock vs fcntl (§3.7).** Committed to flock(2) per §2; deleted STATE_LOCK_STALE and NOTICE_LOCK_STALE_CLEANED entries (kernel auto-releases flock on process death, so the stale-lock code path cannot occur).
- **M19 — PRECONDITION_LOCK_HELD exit code (§3.7).** Added explicit registry entry with exit 75 (RUNTIME_TRANSIENT-equivalent for scheduler retry-friendliness).
- **M20 — git rev-list upstream ref (§3.4).** Switched to gh pr view --json headRefOid as authoritative remote tip; avoids @{upstream} tracking-ref requirement.
- **M22 — ReviewerState.Status post-push (§3.4, §3.2).** pushLocked flips Required reviewers with Status=review_found to not_requested after a successful upload; rereview cell updated to include not_requested in the target set.
- **m3 — releases lock cell prose.** Dropped from matrix cells; lock release is implicit on Run return.
- **m4 — PRECONDITION_NO_AUTH precondition vs runtime.** Reconciled: startup check = PRECONDITION_NO_AUTH; mid-flight token expiry = RUNTIME_GH_TERMINAL.
- **m5 — LIFECYCLE_TERMINAL_NO_ACTION.** Deleted; never emitted per §3.2 matrix.
- **m7 — Config precedence (§3.5).** Stated explicitly: flag > env > TOML > built-in.

Deferred to follow-up (minor matrix readability, non-blocking AC checklist refinement).


## Cycle 3 revisions (applied 2026-05-12)

Cycle 3 was a Claude general-purpose fresh-eyes pass (.beads/worker-audit/agents-config-wisp-do2t/claude-cycle3.md). Found 0 BLOCKING / 5 CRITICAL / 14 MAJOR / 6 MINOR. Trend: criticals 6→5; shifting from cross-section contradictions to pseudocode-implementation details. Cycle 3 revisions:

- **C1 — handle_verb_error exit-code wiring.** Added explicit tier → exit-code mapping (exitCodeForTier function in §3.3). Public Run wrapper applies it to translate tier-tagged errors into sysexits codes (2/65/75/77/78). handle_verb_error returns Disposition enum (Continue/Propagate) instead of raw error.
- **C2 — idle/interactive silent no-op.** Pseudocode + waitLocked contract now emits stderr advisory ('prgroom: nothing to do — PR has no commits yet (phase=idle)') on idle/interactive return.
- **C3 — cap-trip + escalate_if_needed double-emit.** Restructured: ALL Sink emission flows through the single loop-top escalate_if_needed call. Cap-trip and end-of-cycle resolver only WRITE state, setting LifecycleEscalationFiled=false to invite one emit. Added LifecycleEscalationFiled bool to §2 schema.
- **C4 — state shadowing + re-reads.** Each *Locked internal now returns (*PRGroomingState, error). runLocked threads in-memory state through; no disk re-reads. Section 2 lock guarantees single-owner.
- **C5 — waitLocked contract.** Pinned contract surface in §3.3 (signature, behavior, error tiers, cancellation, lock semantics); §4 owns quiescence/timeout details but §3.3 specifies enough surface to be implementation-ready.
- **M7 — direct-invocation vs run-driven matrix.** Added preamble: §3.2 matrix describes public verb behavior on direct invocation; §3.3 describes run-driven flow (bypasses per-verb self-heal).
- **M8 — has_required_reviewers_to_refresh.** Added explicit predicate definitions in §3.4 (has_queued_fix_commits, has_required_reviewers_to_refresh, push_uploaded_commits_this_cycle, new_lifecycle_gate_this_cycle).
- **M9 — PRECONDITION_LOCK_HELD §3.6 footnote.** Added note explaining the precondition-named-but-transient-exit classification rationale (lock contention is short-lived; scheduler retry-on-cadence is the right recovery).
- **M10 — poll × quiesced external-push.** Updated poll row's quiesced and human-gated cells to handle external-push transitions (→ awaiting-review or fixes-pending respectively, with Round++).
- **M11 — *Locked idempotency contract.** Added explicit subsection in §3.3 formalizing the idempotency requirement per internal (cluster/fix/reply/resolve/push), required for safe re-entry per §3.2 priority 7.
- **M12 — First-poll on already-active PR.** Added §3.5 paragraph: cap counts only rounds observed by prgroom; historical out-of-band rounds don't consume budget.
- **M14 — Tier column in registry.** Added per-code tier assignment in §3.7 RUNTIME_* table; added tier-assignment-rule preamble for other tables. All codes now map to a tier.
- **M15 — RUNTIME_PUSH_REJECTED tier.** Pinned to RUNTIME_TERMINAL_USER (exit 77, human-gated). Retry without manual intervention is futile; scheduler shouldn't burn cycles.
- **M16 — Auto-bootstrap policy.** Pseudocode comment: 'Auto-bootstrap is performed regardless of --no-prework; absence of state is a discovery condition, not a precondition failure.'
- **M17 — Partial-write self-correction.** Added paragraph in §3.4: Round increments exactly once via the external-push attribution path on the next pollLocked when state-write fails mid-push.
- **M18 — Per-API-call retry budget.** §3.6 retry policy now explicit: per logical API call, not per verb. A single pollLocked may issue several gh calls; each gets its own 3-attempt budget independently.
- **M19 — STATE_SCHEMA_UNKNOWN exit code.** Now pinned to STATE_CORRUPT tier (exit 78 EX_CONFIG) via the registry tier assignment rules.
- **M21 — bd Sink notes idempotency.** Documented: bd-adapter MUST use label-only emit or content-hash dedup on notes; --append-notes is not idempotent.

Remaining (deferred to follow-up if Cycle 4 surfaces or budget exhausted): minor matrix readability (poll row density), .prgroom.toml cross-reference to §7, zero-commit draft PR semantics, PRECONDITION_NO_AUTH ↔ RUNTIME_GH_TERMINAL bidirectional cross-reference, mid-cycle resolve-escalated operator UX note.


## Cycle 4 outcome (final cycle per Scott's extended 4-cap)

Cycle 4 was a Claude general-purpose fresh-eyes pass (.beads/worker-audit/agents-config-wisp-do2t/claude-cycle4.md). Found 0 BLOCKING / 4 CRITICAL / 15 MAJOR / 6 MINOR. Cycle cap exhausted with criticals still present.

### Trend
- BLOCKING: 2 → 0 → 0 → 0 (zeroed and held)
- CRITICAL: 4 → 6 → 5 → 4 (slow grind; not full convergence)
- MAJOR: 6 → 13 → 14 → 15 (roughly flat as spec gains detail)
- MINOR: 1 → 9 → 6 → 6

### Cycle 4 unresolved criticals (per Scott's directive: stop and report)

1. **Duplicate handle_verb_error blocks in §3.3** — Cycle 3 revision left an orphan legacy block. Easy fix.
2. **escalate_if_needed single-point invariant broken** — handle_verb_error's Propagate paths for terminal tiers return BEFORE loop-top emit fires, so lifecycle Sink events for auth-expiry/push-rejection/state-corrupt never emit. Fixable.
3. **Round init vs external-push count rules contradict** — Two §3.5 paragraphs disagree about whether the very first observed pushes are 'external' or 'first-observation.' Fixable by picking a single rule.
4. **LastError clearing rule not algorithmically implemented** — Spec asserts clearing on success but pseudocode never clears. Fixable.

All 4 are surgically fixable (no architectural change required). Three were introduced by my Cycle 3 revision pass (incomplete execution). One is a documentation/code-implementation gap in my earlier wording.

### Status

- Spec is structurally complete against all bead AC.
- Cycle 4 self-scored PASS_WITH_RESERVATIONS but per ralf-review rubric is FAIL (4 criticals).
- Cycle budget exhausted per operator's extended 4-cap.
- Per operator instruction ('if still there are critical/blocking items, stop and report'): **STOPPING**. Implementation-readiness gate is NOT auto-stamped; awaiting operator decision on path forward (accept FAIL with documented criticals + finalize anyway, OR another revision-only pass without further review, OR pause for now).

## Cycle 6 outcome (final under operator-extended cap)

**Score:** PASS_WITH_RESERVATIONS (per reviewer self-score); rubric-strict score: borderline (2 criticals — both surgical and now patched)

**Trend across all 6 cycles:**
| Severity | C1 | C2 | C3 | C4 | C5 | C6 |
|---|---|---|---|---|---|---|
| BLOCKING | 2 | 0 | 0 | 0 | 0 | 0 |
| CRITICAL | 4 | 6 | 5 | 4 | 3 | 2 |
| MAJOR    | 6 | 13 | 14 | 15 | 11 | 5 |
| MINOR    | 1 | 9 | 6 | 6 | 5 | 8 |

Cycle 5 → Cycle 6: criticals 3 → 2, majors 11 → 5. Clear convergence.

**Cycle 6 surgical fixes applied (per operator authorization):**
- C1 — RUNTIME_REPO_DELETED removed from BlockingErrorCodes (covered by RUNTIME_GH_TERMINAL)
- C2 — pushLocked bootstrap rule rewritten with explicit mutual exclusivity vs attribution branch; pollLocked bootstrap idempotent via max(state.Round, 1)
- M1 — pollLocked external-push attribution branch now mirrors pushLocked reviewer-state flip
- M4 — runLocked bootstrap struct literal now initializes SchemaVersion=1, PR=pr, Items=[], all schema fields explicit

**Follow-up bead filed:** agents-config-fca6.9 (P2, parent=fca6, discovered-from fca6.1) — captures remaining 3 majors (M2, M3, M5) + 8 minors (m1-m8). None architectural; all surgical doc/spec refinements that improve implementer clarity but do not block Go transliteration.

Cycle reports: .beads/worker-audit/agents-config-wisp-do2t/claude-cycle{1..6}.md

Operator authorized finalize → ralf-spec-review step closing; molecule continues to finalize step.

title: [Impl] Tier 2: agents/ cleanups (F4 F6 F7 F8 F9 F11)
description:
## Spec

### Summary

Complete the migration from the legacy `bead-implementor` mega-agent to a typed-report worker family (the existing trio plus two new specialists), and apply five smaller agent-quality fixes from the il69 audit (F6/F7/F8/F9, with F4 closing as superseded). Result: `bead-implementor` is deleted; every former caller dispatches a purpose-built worker that emits a typed `worker-report-v1` YAML; the architecture spec, smoke verifier, and all dispatch surfaces reflect the new family.

### Background / motivation

The agents-config-il69 Phase 3 audit identified six Tier 2 findings against `agents/`:

| ID | Severity | File | Issue |
|----|----------|------|-------|
| F4 | Med | `bead-implementor.md` | model tier (sonnet/medium) — deferred until F11 resolved |
| F6 | Low | `quality-reviewer.md` | `memory: project` with no eviction policy |
| F7 | High | `tech-lead.md` | missing "Do NOT dispatch when:" negative triggers |
| F8 | Med | `tech-lead.md` | hardcoded `.claude/agents/*` (Claude-only) in shared content |
| F9 | Med | `tech-lead.md` | code-free orchestrator but no `disallowedTools` enforcement |
| F11 | High | `bead-implementor.md` | bead-implementor vs trio: two living specs disagree |

Pre-brainstorm investigation (2026-05-12) established the F11 ground truth:

- bead-implementor dispatched by **4 active surfaces**: `implement-feature.formula.toml` (red-tests), `fix-bug.formula.toml` (red-tests + diagnose), `docs-only.formula.toml` (apply-edits), `wait-for-pr-comments` SKILL (per-comment fixer).
- The dedicated trio (`tdd-red-team`, `tdd-green-team`, `bug-diagnoser`) dispatched by **exactly one surface**: `implement-bead`'s stage-map skill.
- agents-config-7bk.19.2 (closed, merged commit eac41ca) deliberately introduced the trio with the stated purpose: *"Replace the single bead-aware mega-agent (`bead-implementor`) with three focused, bead-agnostic worker agents."*
- Migration stalled: trio agents exist + dispatch skill points to them, but formulas, architecture spec, smoke verifier, and `wait-for-pr-comments` still name bead-implementor.

Design improvements the trio captures (lost if we keep bead-implementor):

- Pure task functions — no `bd` commands in agent bodies; orchestrator owns bead-state lifecycle
- Typed `worker-report-v1.yaml` output — machine-parsable evidence at every stage transition
- Bead-agnostic — usable in non-bead contexts in principle
- model: opus, effort: high — matches reasoning load of authoring meaningful tests / root-cause traversal

### Requirements

#### R1 — Migrate the formula dispatch surfaces (F11)

R1.1 — `src/plugins/beads/.beads/formulas/implement-feature.formula.toml` red-tests normal flow: replace "Dispatch bead-implementor (sonnet, medium effort) to write tests" with dispatch of `tdd-red-team` in **multi-AC mode**, passing AC bullets + worktree path + step-bead ID. Reports written to `<repo-root>/.beads/worker-audit/<step-bead-id>/tdd-red-team.yaml`.

R1.2 — `src/plugins/beads/.beads/formulas/fix-bug.formula.toml` red-tests normal flow: replace bead-implementor dispatch with `tdd-red-team` in **single-regression mode**, passing `root_cause_note` + `reproduction_steps` from the upstream `bug-diagnoser` report. Reports written to `<repo-root>/.beads/worker-audit/<step-bead-id>/tdd-red-team.yaml`.

R1.3 — `src/plugins/beads/.beads/formulas/fix-bug.formula.toml` diagnose stage: replace bead-implementor dispatch with `bug-diagnoser`. Reports written to `<repo-root>/.beads/worker-audit/<step-bead-id>/bug-diagnoser.yaml`.

R1.4 — `src/plugins/beads/.beads/formulas/docs-only.formula.toml` apply-edits stage: replace bead-implementor dispatch with `docs-edits-team`. The apply-edits contract is a single-pass docs/spec/prose change — it doesn't fit the TDD red/green/diagnose taxonomy, so a dedicated specialist is cleaner than overloading tdd-green-team with a stage flag.

R1.5 — `docs/specs/bead-pipeline-architecture.md`: remove ALL references to bead-implementor (§6 agent table, §3 red-tests prose, §3.5 diagnose prose, docs-only apply-edits subsection, RALF-IT note ~line 723, scope-item-7 cross-reference ~line 812, and any other occurrence). Add rows for `tdd-red-team`, `tdd-green-team`, `bug-diagnoser`, `docs-edits-team`, `pr-comment-fixer-team` to the §6 agent table; **each new row must include a markdown link to its per-agent report spec doc** (e.g., docs-edits-team → `docs/specs/docs-edits-report-v1.md`). Update all referencing prose sections to name the appropriate worker per stage.

R1.6 — Delete `src/plugins/beads/.agents/agents/bead-implementor.md`. No deprecated stub; no transition window.

R1.6.x — Update `scripts/smoke/verify-artifacts.sh`: remove the `bead-implementor agent exists` assertion block (currently ~lines 76-79). Replace with assertions for the five new worker agents: `tdd-red-team.md`, `tdd-green-team.md`, `bug-diagnoser.md`, `docs-edits-team.md`, `pr-comment-fixer-team.md` — all under `src/plugins/beads/.agents/agents/`.

R1.6.y — Update `tests/verify-implement-bead.sh`: remove any stale bead-implementor references in comments or assertion text. The AC17 comment block ("# AC 17 — No bead-implementor in either file") should be updated to reflect the current assertion semantics (asserting legacy-agent absence is still valid; update the comment text to remove the bead-implementor name and describe the assertion generically).

#### R2 — Introduce two new specialists

R2.1 — Author `src/plugins/beads/.agents/agents/docs-edits-team.md`:
- Frontmatter: `model: opus`, `effort: high`, `color: cyan`, `tools: Read, Edit, Write, Grep, Glob, Bash`
- Body: pure task function; zero `bd` subcommand invocations; cites `docs/specs/docs-edits-report-v1.md` by repo-root-relative path
- Contract: receives worktree path + bead description + acceptance_criteria + step-bead ID + **explicit absolute report path**; applies the documented changes; commits with a descriptive message referencing the source bead; writes `docs-edits-report-v1.yaml` to the absolute path the caller provides
- Hard rules in body: no new code requiring a test harness; no speculative refactoring; no test scaffolding (if tests needed, flag-human per existing protocol)

R2.2 — Author `src/plugins/beads/.agents/agents/pr-comment-fixer-team.md`:
- Frontmatter: `model: opus`, `effort: high`, `color: orange`, `tools: Read, Edit, Write, Grep, Glob, Bash`
- Body: pure task function; zero `bd` subcommand invocations; cites `docs/specs/pr-comment-fix-report-v1.md` by repo-root-relative path
- Contract: receives a single PR comment object (id, thread_id, body, code-location) + repo path + **explicit absolute report path**; either commits a fix, recognizes the concern as already-addressed, or escalates — writes `pr-comment-fix-report-v1.yaml` to the absolute path the caller provides
- **Contract-purity rule:** the agent contains NO logic that detects or branches on whether beads infrastructure is present. The caller is always responsible for providing the absolute report path; the agent writes there unconditionally.

R2.3 — Author `docs/specs/docs-edits-report-v1.md`:
- Opens with a relative link to `docs/specs/worker-report-v1.md` as the shared base contract
- Describes only the extension delta (added fields):
  - `files_changed: [string]` (paths)
  - `commit_sha: string`
  - `summary: string` (1-line)
  - `skipped_items: [{path, reason}]` (when worker chose not to touch a section)
- Add `docs-edits-team` to the `### 1.3 Per-agent extensions` table in `docs/specs/worker-report-v1.md` (columns: Agent | Schema | Adds).
- Also update the Quick Reference table near the top of `worker-report-v1.md` (the one listing per-agent report extension filenames) to include `docs-edits-report-v1.md`.

R2.4 — Author `docs/specs/pr-comment-fix-report-v1.md`:
- Opens with a relative link to `docs/specs/worker-report-v1.md` as the shared base contract
- Describes only the extension delta (added fields):
  - `comment_id: string`
  - `comment_thread_id: string`
  - `classification: enum { FIX, SKIP, ESCALATE }`
  - `action: enum { COMMITTED_FIX, ALREADY_ADDRESSED, NO_ACTION }`
  - `fix_summary: string` (when action == COMMITTED_FIX)
  - `commit_sha: string` (when action == COMMITTED_FIX)
  - `already_addressed_by_sha: string` (when action == ALREADY_ADDRESSED)
  - `escalation_reason: string` (when classification == ESCALATE)
- Add `pr-comment-fixer-team` to the `### 1.3 Per-agent extensions` table in `docs/specs/worker-report-v1.md`.
- Also update the Quick Reference table near the top of `worker-report-v1.md` to include `pr-comment-fix-report-v1.md`.

R2.5 — Update `src/user/.agents/skills/wait-for-pr-comments/SKILL.md`:
- Line ~207 dispatch: change `subagent_type: "bead-implementor"` → `subagent_type: "pr-comment-fixer-team"`
- Adjust dispatch task-spec to match R2.4 input contract
- The skill always computes and passes an explicit absolute report path to the agent — `<repo-root>/.beads/worker-audit/<step-bead-id>/pr-comment-fixer-team.yaml` in a beads workflow, or `<cwd>/.pr-comment-fixer-<comment-id>.yaml` otherwise. The agent never infers context — it writes to the caller-provided path, always.

#### R3 — tech-lead.md edits (F7, F8, F9)

R3.1 (F7) — Add a new H2 section `## Do NOT Dispatch When` to the **body** (not the frontmatter `description:` YAML field) of `src/user/.agents/agents/tech-lead.md`, covering at minimum:
- Work already routed via start-bead, implement-bead, or run-queue
- Tasks already decomposed by the caller (sub-task list handed to tech-lead)
- Single-worker tasks (no multi-component orchestration warranted)

Include at least one negative example (e.g., "User asks to fix a typo in CLAUDE.md → do NOT dispatch tech-lead; do the edit inline").

R3.2 (F8) — In the body, all three existing occurrences where `.claude/agents/*` is used as a primary source of callable agents must be replaced. Currently:
- ~line 43: "Scan `.claude/agents/*` to understand available team members"
- ~line 55: "Scan `.claude/agents/*` to inventory available expertise"
- ~line 121: "You must work only with available agents in `.claude/agents/*`"

All three must be rewritten to follow audit decision D19: "use the caller-provided roster of callable agents and tool limits; if the caller does not provide one, inspect the current tool's agent registry or documented agent directory as a fallback." `.claude/agents/*` may be cited at most once in the body, and only in a clause that explicitly labels it as "the Claude Code-specific fallback example."

R3.3 (F9) — Add `disallowedTools: Write, Edit` to the frontmatter of `src/user/.agents/agents/tech-lead.md`.

#### R4 — quality-reviewer.md Memory Protocol (F6)

R4.1 — Keep `memory: project` (per audit decision D20 — PARTIAL synthesis).

R4.2 — Add a `## Memory Protocol` section to `src/user/.agents/agents/quality-reviewer.md` body:

- **Categories persisted** (exhaustive):
  1. Recurring vulnerability patterns (observed across ≥ 2 reviews)
  2. Project-specific anti-patterns (conventions violated more than once)
  3. Prior false-positive corrections (findings the reviewer raised that human ratified as not-an-issue)

- **Horizon**: 30 review cycles per category. After 30 cycles without reference, entry is eligible for eviction.

- **Eviction rule**: LRU within each category; on overflow, drop least-recently-referenced first.

- **Hard cap**: 50 entries total across all categories.

- **Write trigger**: memory appended only when a finding's outcome receives **explicit human ratification** (PR merge, explicit user response). No auto-persist of every observation.

#### R5 — F4 resolves as superseded

bead-implementor.md is deleted under R1.6; its model/effort tuning is moot. No standalone code change needed. Recorded via architectural decision bead agents-config-yijq.

### Out of scope

- Migration of the `wait-for-pr-comments` skill itself from `src/user/.agents/skills/` to `src/plugins/beads/.agents/skills/` — tension noted in DW1 below; not in acmh.1 scope
- ralf-implement / ralf-review skill internals
- green-loop's label-driven skill dispatch (it dispatches *skills*, not agents)
- The `merge-and-cleanup` formula (no bead-implementor usage)
- New tech-lead capability beyond F7/F8/F9 edits
- Schema extensions to `worker-report-v1` beyond the two new per-agent extensions
- Re-evaluation of opus/high settings on the trio post-migration

### Design notes (key decisions made during brainstorming)

1. **Complete the migration vs hybrid/rollback/defer** — chosen: complete. Rationale: honors 7bk.19.2's stated intent; eliminates the two-living-specs surface; the trio's typed-report contract gives the pipeline a stronger audit trail than bead-implementor's note-appending model.

2. **All six findings in one bead** — chosen over split. F4 is moot once F11 lands; F6/F7/F8/F9 are independent small edits; coordinated PR cycle is acceptable.

3. **New `pr-comment-fixer-team` specialist vs reuse trio vs keep bead-implementor** — chosen: new specialist. Rationale: PR-comment fixing has its own task shape (classified comment → fix or already-addressed or escalate) that doesn't fit the TDD taxonomy.

4. **`docs-edits-team` specialist for docs-only apply-edits** — chosen on the same principle as #3: apply-edits is a single-pass non-TDD stage; overloading tdd-green-team with a "stage=apply-edits, skip test_results" flag would muddy that agent's contract. A dedicated specialist with its own report is symmetric with the pr-comment-fixer-team decision and keeps each worker's contract tight.

5. **bead-implementor deleted, not stubbed** — no transition window; worker agents have no external API.

6. **Concrete report-path contract (C2 resolution)** — orchestrator always computes and passes an explicit absolute report path; agents write to that path unconditionally. No in-agent branching on beads vs non-beads context. This is already the trio pattern; R2.5 makes it explicit for the new agents.

7. **`verify-artifacts.sh` must be updated (C1 resolution)** — the smoke verifier asserts agent file existence. Adding R1.6.x ensures the verifier reflects the new worker family; failing to do so would make the smoke gate contradict the repo state.

8. **F6 horizon numbers calibration** — 30 cycles ≈ a medium-sized multi-PR epic where the same quality-reviewer accumulates cross-PR pattern knowledge; 50-entry hard cap fits in under 2KB of context (100 chars avg × 50 entries). Both are v1 starting points; re-tuning is a separate optimization bead.

9. **wait-for-pr-comments cross-tool tension** — accepted as-is for this bead; the skill lives in shared content but dispatches a beads-plugin agent. This tension is tracked as DW1 for a follow-up bead.

### Discovered work (file as follow-up beads, not in acmh.1 scope)

DW1 — `wait-for-pr-comments` skill lives in `src/user/.agents/skills/` (shared) but post-migration dispatches `pr-comment-fixer-team` from `src/plugins/beads/.agents/agents/` (beads-plugin only). Resolutions: (a) move skill into beads plugin; (b) move agent into shared content; (c) design graceful degradation for non-beads contexts. R2.5 implements the minimal fix (absolute-path contract); DW1 is the long-term resolution.

DW2 — Post-migration, re-evaluate whether opus/high is still right for the trio's actual workload. Analogous to how F4 was originally framed for bead-implementor.

### Open questions

None blocking after ralf-spec-review cycle 2 revisions (see resolution summary below).

---

## Pre-brainstorm analysis (assess step, 2026-05-12) — preserved for audit trail

### F11 usage findings: bead-implementor IS still active

**Active dispatchers of bead-implementor (4 callers):**
1. `src/plugins/beads/.beads/formulas/implement-feature.formula.toml:293` — red-tests stage normal flow
2. `src/plugins/beads/.beads/formulas/fix-bug.formula.toml:327` — red-tests stage normal flow
3. `src/plugins/beads/.beads/formulas/docs-only.formula.toml:131` — apply-edits stage (sole worker)
4. `src/user/.agents/skills/wait-for-pr-comments/SKILL.md:207` — per-comment fixer

**Where the dedicated trio is dispatched (zero formula callers):** Only in `src/plugins/beads/.agents/skills/implement-bead/SKILL.md` (stage-map: red-tests→tdd-red-team, green-loop→tdd-green-team, diagnose→bug-diagnoser).

**The actual conflict:** The formula TOML prose and the implement-bead skill's dispatch map disagree. This bead lands the alignment.

**Git history (decisive):** agents-config-7bk.19.2 (commit eac41ca) stated intent: "Replace the single bead-aware mega-agent (`bead-implementor`) with three focused, bead-agnostic worker agents." Migration stalled; acmh.1 is the resumption.

### ralf-spec-review cycle 1 findings addressed

**C1** — `scripts/smoke/verify-artifacts.sh` bead-implementor assertion: addressed via R1.6.x.
**C2** — R2.5 fallback contract TBD: resolved by concrete absolute-path contract (design note #6); R2.5 and R2.2 updated.
**M1** — New report specs must reference worker-report-v1 and update rollup table: addressed in R2.3/R2.4.
**M2** — R3.1 body-vs-frontmatter ambiguity: resolved — H2 section in body per R3.1 text.
**M3** — R3.2 AC too semantic: resolved — split into two mechanical grep-checkable sub-ACs.
**M4** — F6 numbers lack calibration: addressed in design note #8.
**M5** — docs-edits-team "ratification hedge" removed: committed to the decision; design note #4 updated.
**m1** — Agent colors: docs-edits-team cyan, pr-comment-fixer-team orange, per R2.1/R2.2.
**m2** — Mode arguments in formula dispatch: added to requirements R1.1/R1.2 and AC.
**m3** — R1.5 scope too narrow for bead-pipeline-architecture.md: R1.5 now requires removal of ALL references in that file.
**m4** — "historical-state-only sections" vague: cross-cutting AC now enumerates explicit exemptions (docs/audits/, docs/plans/ only).

### ralf-spec-review cycle 2 findings addressed (applied post-cycle-cap)

**F1 (blocking)** — `tests/verify-implement-bead.sh` has stale bead-implementor comment: addressed via R1.6.y; cross-cutting AC scope extended to include tests/.
**F2 (critical)** — worker-report-v1.md rollup AC was imprecise: updated R2.3/R2.4 to name the exact `### 1.3 Per-agent extensions` table AND the Quick Reference table; rollup ACs updated to match.
**F3 (major)** — R2.5 behavioral AC not grep-verifiable: added AC asserting `worker-audit` or `.pr-comment-fixer-` appears in updated SKILL.md (confirms path-construction logic is present).
**F4 (major)** — tech-lead.md three `.claude/agents/*` occurrences; AC missed line 121: R3.2 now enumerates all three lines; R3.2b AC updated to catch all three (zero-match or one-example-labeled-only match).
**F5 (major)** — arch spec §6 table lacks report-spec links: R1.5 requirement updated; AC updated to check new rows include markdown links.
**F6 (minor)** — R4.2(e) write-trigger AC not grep-checkable: AC updated to require phrase 'human ratification' or 'explicit human ratification'.
**F7 (minor)** — tdd-red-team mode semantics unlinked: R1.1/R1.2 AC updated to include cross-reference to tdd-red-team.md.

title: Decision: complete bead-implementor → trio migration; add two new specialists; delete bead-implementor
description: Discovered while spec-writing for agents-config-acmh.1 (F11 resolution). Records the architectural decision so future readers can trace the why.
notes: Resolves F11 from the il69 audit. Pre-brainstorm investigation established that bead-implementor was dispatched by 4 surfaces while the trio was dispatched by only 1 (the implement-bead skill's stage-map). The trio was deliberately introduced by agents-config-7bk.19.2 (commit eac41ca) as a REPLACEMENT for bead-implementor; the migration stalled. Decision: finish the migration. Formulas (implement-feature, fix-bug, docs-only) and wait-for-pr-comments will dispatch typed-report workers; bead-implementor.md is deleted. Two new specialists are introduced because docs-only apply-edits and PR-comment fixing don't fit the TDD red/green/diagnose taxonomy: docs-edits-team (for docs-only apply-edits) and pr-comment-fixer-team (for wait-for-pr-comments per-comment fix). Each emits its own typed report extending worker-report-v1. F4 closes as superseded — bead-implementor's model tier is moot once the agent is deleted. F6/F7/F8/F9 are addressed as the audit recommended with concrete schemas chosen in the spec (Memory Protocol categories/horizon/eviction; tech-lead D19 caller-provided-roster phrasing; tech-lead disallowedTools enforcement). Implemented in agents-config-acmh.1.
close_reason: Decision recorded; resolution captured in agents-config-acmh.1 spec (R1-R5). Closing per --implemented semantics.

notes:
## Audit source

Report committed at: docs/beads/4vn5.2-mattpocock-skills-audit.md (branch docs/4vn5.2-audit-mattpocock-skills)
Upstream repo: https://github.com/mattpocock/skills
HEAD SHA audited: 9f2e0bd0ea776eb6372eb81fa8a4a47814a8404a
Audited: 2026-05-12 (28 skills found)

## Verdict summary

Gap-fill (7): deprecated/qa, deprecated/ubiquitous-language, engineering/improve-codebase-architecture, engineering/prototype, engineering/triage, engineering/zoom-out, productivity/caveman

Compare-and-improve (9): deprecated/request-refactor-plan, engineering/diagnose, engineering/grill-with-docs, engineering/tdd, engineering/to-issues, engineering/to-prd, misc/git-guardrails-claude-code, productivity/grill-me, productivity/handoff, productivity/write-a-skill

Spark (3): deprecated/design-an-interface, in-progress/review, misc/setup-pre-commit

Skip (9): setup-matt-pocock-skills, writing-beats, writing-fragments, writing-shape, migrate-to-shoehorn, scaffold-exercises, edit-article, obsidian-vault

## Highest-leverage synthesis candidates

1. improve-codebase-architecture: A Philosophy of Software Design vocabulary; no equivalent exists
2. prototype: structured throwaway exploration before implementation
3. triage: maps onto beads human vs implementation-ready labels; bead-aware from the start
4. tdd delta: anti-horizontal-slicing + interface-design pre-phase
5. handoff: reference-artifacts-by-path rule + skill suggestions for next session

## Ecosystem note

CONTEXT.md/ADR convention (used throughout mattpocock skills) may be highest-leverage single adoption. Evaluate as part of synthesis work.

## Next step

Brainstorm synthesis plan: which Gap-fill skills to build first, which Compare-and-improve deltas to incorporate into which existing skills, whether to adopt the CONTEXT.md/ADR convention as a foundation.

title: Review-cycle: address PR review feedback
description:
Drive the PR review loop until all review threads are resolved.
Model: claude-sonnet-4-6  Effort: medium

Skips entirely when review-level:none.

## Preloaded skills
wait-for-pr-comments (chains internally to reply-and-resolve-pr-threads)

## Actions

Invoke wait-for-pr-comments with:
  --mode autonomous --bead-id {{bead-id}}

One iteration = one outbound reply-batch.
Iteration cap: 5 (override: iteration-cap-review-cycle-<n> label).
Early exit when no remaining FIX-class items.

Exit conditions (mutually exclusive):
- review-exit-copilot-only (default when review-exit-human-approvers-<n> absent)
- review-exit-human-approvers-<n>: require N human approvers

Persist after each batch:
  bd update <step-bead-id> --append-notes "REVIEW-BATCH:<n>/<MAX>"

If PR is CLOSED on re-entry: flag-human. Do NOT poll a closed PR.

## Definition of Done
Every review thread has a reply. FIXED items resolved on GitHub.
No in-flight FIX work.
close_reason: Superseded by strategic redirect: audit outcome captured in cx6.1

title: Verify-AC: [m] grep-witnesses if any; warn-and-pass if none
description:
Validate every [m]-tagged AC line if any exist; warn-and-pass otherwise.
Model: claude-haiku-4-5  Effort: medium

Skips when review-level:none.

## Inputs
- Bead AC from: bd show {{bead-id}} (parse [m] and [h] tags from
  acceptance_criteria field per §4.1's canonical line parser)
- Worktree path from: worktree-path-* label on molecule

## Actions

1. Count [m]-tagged AC lines from the acceptance_criteria field:
     m_count=$(bd show {{bead-id}} --json \
       | jq -r '.[0].acceptance_criteria // ""' \
       | grep -cvE '^([[:space:]]*$|\[h\][[:space:]])')

2. If m_count == 0:
   - Log a warning to step-bead notes:
       bd update <step-bead-id> --append-notes "verify-ac: no [m] AC lines — skipping mechanical witness verification (warn-and-pass)"
   - Pass the step. Do NOT block.
   - Encourages greppable landmarks (e.g. grep for a new heading in the
     spec file) without requiring them.

3. If m_count > 0:
   For each [m]-tagged AC line, dispatch bead-verifier (haiku, low effort)
   to run a shell-runnable witness (typically `grep` for an inserted
   landmark, `test -f` for a new file, etc.). Collect: exit code +
   excerpt. Assemble validation report.

4. [h]-tagged AC lines: do NOT validate here. Confirm that human follow-up
   child beads were filed at brainstorm-time finalize
   (bd list --parent {{bead-id}} filtered by label human). If any [h]
   bullet has no matching follow-up, flag-human.

5. Persist validation report to BOTH step-bead notes AND source bead notes
   (so it appears in the eventual PR description at create-pr).

## Definition of Done
Every [m] AC line has explicit witness evidence (m_count > 0 path)
OR the warn-and-pass note is recorded (m_count == 0 path).
Human-review bullets have follow-up beads filed (confirm presence).

## Idempotent re-entry
Re-run re-evaluates and continues from prior pass.
close_reason: Superseded by strategic redirect: audit outcome captured in cx6.1; molecule abandoned at apply-edits completion

title: Merge-or-handoff: auto-merge or hand off to human
description:
Either auto-merge the PR or hand off to the human.
Model: claude-haiku-4-5  Effort: medium

## Gate 1 — PR-state clean-check
Conditions (ALL must be true for auto-merge path):
  - Zero open Copilot review threads
  - Zero open human-author review threads
  - All CI checks green
  - 5-minute quiet window with no new comments or review activity
On wait-for-pr-comments timeout: treat clean-check as NOT clean.

## Auto-merge path (auto-mergeable label present AND clean-check clean)
1. Re-run clean-check immediately before merge call.
2. Pour merge-and-cleanup formula (phase: liquid — NOT vapor).
3. Drive merge-and-cleanup:
   - Merge the PR
   - Delete branch (local + remote)
   - Remove worktree (decode from worktree-path-* label)
   - Run parent-chain I2 close-walk
   Note: merge-and-cleanup gate-step (Gate 2) blocks until ALL [h] follow-up
   children are closed with verified-by-human label, then closes the
   merge-{source-id} child after merge + cleanup so the source bead can close.

## Hand-off path (default — auto-mergeable absent OR clean-check dirty)
1. Transition bead to open: bd update {{bead-id}} --status open
2. Stamp labels: bd label add {{bead-id}} merge-ready
                 bd label add {{bead-id}} human
3. Note: bead is now excluded from bd ready (human label gates it).
   Human runs /merge-and-cleanup when ready.

## Idempotent re-entry
Query gh pr view --json mergedAt first. If non-null: skip merge, proceed to cleanup.
close_reason: Superseded by strategic redirect: audit outcome captured in cx6.1

title: Review: quality-reviewer + simplify on the diff
description:
Run the standard in-house review pair on the committed diff.
Model: claude-opus-4-7  Effort: medium

Skips entirely when review-level:none.

## Actions

1. Invoke `quality-reviewer` against the diff produced by apply-edits.
   Address findings inline (per the receiving-code-review skill):
   - Critical / major: fix and re-commit before proceeding.
   - Minor: fix or record explicit deferral with rationale.

2. Invoke the `simplify` skill on the same diff. Address findings the
   same way.

3. Persist the review summary:
     bd update <step-bead-id> --append-notes "REVIEW: quality-reviewer=<n_findings>; simplify=<n_findings>; deferred=<list or none>"

## Definition of Done
All critical/major findings resolved. Deferrals recorded. Diff is committed
and ready for verify-ac.

## Idempotent re-entry
Re-running re-evaluates the current diff; prior fixes already committed
are picked up via filesystem state.
close_reason: Superseded by strategic redirect: audit outcome captured in cx6.1; molecule abandoned at apply-edits completion

title: Create-PR: push branch and open pull request
description:
Create the pull request and persist its URL to the molecule.
Model: claude-haiku-4-5  Effort: medium

## Preloaded skills
superpowers:finishing-a-development-branch

## Actions

1. Invoke superpowers:finishing-a-development-branch.

2. PR body MUST include:
   - Bead reference: {{bead-id}} and bead title
   - AC (full list with [m]/[h] tags)
   - apply-edits summary (from step-bead notes)
   - review summary (quality-reviewer + simplify findings + resolutions)
   - verify-ac validation report (or warn-and-pass note)
   - Human-required AC follow-up bead references (if any [h] lines)

3. Stamp pr-url-* label on molecule:
   bd label add <mol-id> pr-url-<encoded-url>

## PR creation failure
Flag-human with worktree intact. Do NOT tear down the worktree.

## Idempotent re-entry
Query gh pr view first. If a PR exists for the branch and is OPEN: skip
creation, persist URL idempotently.
If PR exists and is CLOSED: flag-human. Do NOT auto-reopen or create a new PR.
