title: Apply-edits: single bead-implementor pass to write/edit the docs
description:
Apply the docs/spec/prose changes via a single bead-implementor pass.
There is no red-tests phase and no RALF-IT iteration on this formula.
Model: claude-sonnet-4-6  Effort: medium

## Inputs
- Bead spec from: bd show {{bead-id}} (description + acceptance_criteria)
- Worktree path: decode worktree-path-* label from molecule

## Decode worktree-path label before any work
  1. Decode: __ → /  then  _u → _
  2. Verify decoded path exists and is a git worktree:
       git -C "<path>" rev-parse --is-inside-work-tree
  3. Operate from that cwd. Quote the decoded path in any subsequent
     command that consumes it — paths with spaces or shell-special
     characters will otherwise misparse.

## Actions

1. Dispatch bead-implementor (sonnet, medium effort) with:
   - Worktree path
   - Full bead description and acceptance_criteria
   - Step-bead ID
   - Instruction: apply the documented changes; commit with a single
     descriptive message referencing {{bead-id}}.
   - Rules:
     * No new code that requires a test harness.
     * No speculative refactoring of unrelated content.
     * Do NOT add tests scaffolding here — if tests are needed, the bead
       was misrouted (file a follow-up bead and flag-human).

2. After the worker exits, persist a one-line summary to step-bead notes:
     bd update <step-bead-id> --append-notes "apply-edits: <summary>; files=<n>; sha=<head>"

## Definition of Done
Edits committed to the feature branch. Step-bead notes record the summary.

## Idempotent re-entry
Check filesystem (committed edits) and step-bead notes. If a commit already
exists for this step, skip and proceed to review.
notes: apply-edits: committed mattpocock audit report (28 skills); files=1; sha=62bb05c; strategic redirect — synthesis bead agents-config-cx6.1 filed instead of 19 individual adoption beads
close_reason: Audit report committed to branch; strategic direction confirmed by Scott; outcome captured in cx6.1

title: Preflight: spec validation, worktree creation (no coverage check)
description:
Validate the bead spec and create the worktree before any edits.
Model: claude-sonnet-4-6  Effort: high

## Inputs
Read the bead and its labels:
  bd show {{bead-id}}
  bd label list {{bead-id}}

## Actions

1. Verify `brainstormed` and `implementation-ready` labels are present.
   If either is absent: apply human-flag protocol and exit.

2. Note: formula selection and pour have already happened — this step was
   created by implement-bead pouring docs-only. There is nothing to select
   or pour here.

3. Coverage and quality-gate checks are intentionally skipped: docs-only
   is the routing target for work that has no test runner / no [m] ACs.
   Do NOT inspect [coverage] or [gates] in project-config.toml here.

4. Stamp the molecule→bead lookup label if not already stamped:
   bd label add <mol-id> for-bead-{{bead-id}}

5. Create the worktree:
   Branch name: docs/{{bead-id}}-<slug-from-title>
   Location: <repo-root>/.claude/worktrees/<branch-name>
   Command:
     git worktree add .claude/worktrees/<branch> -b <branch>
   CRITICAL: the formula is already poured (this step was created by the pour).
   If worktree creation fails: squash the molecule and flag-human.

6. Stamp the worktree-path label on the molecule:
   Encoding (apply IN ORDER):
     _ → _u
     / → __
   Then: bd label add <mol-id> worktree-path-<encoded>

7. Claim the bead and walk the parent chain:
   bd update {{bead-id}} --status in_progress
   PARENT=$(bd show {{bead-id}} --json | jq -r '.[0].parent // empty')
   while [ -n "$PARENT" ]; do
     bd update "$PARENT" --status in_progress
     PARENT=$(bd show "$PARENT" --json | jq -r '.[0].parent // empty')
   done

## Definition of Done
Poured molecule + worktree exists + worktree-path-* label stamped on molecule.
OR: human label + structured gap-note + (if pour completed) molecule squashed.

## Idempotent re-entry
Check: does an active molecule exist? Does the worktree path decode to a real
git worktree? If so, skip work already done.
close_reason: Preflight complete: labels verified, worktree created at .claude/worktrees/docs/4vn5.2-audit-mattpocock-skills, worktree-path stamped on molecule

title: merge-agents-config-4vn5.2
description: Merge gate child for agents-config-4vn5.2.

title: [Impl] Audit mattpocock/skills repo for applicable skills
description:
## Summary

Audit the mattpocock/skills GitHub repo by reading every skill in full, assigning a per-skill verdict, presenting a human-readable report to Scott for approval, then filing adoption beads for approved non-skip skills. l0v0 closes when rollup is appended and post-approval beads are filed.

## Background / Motivation

Original notes: Examine all skills in https://github.com/mattpocock/skills for utility and fit with agents-config project. Identify candidates for integration, reuse, or reference.

Matt Pocock (TypeScript educator / Total TypeScript) maintains a skills repo for AI coding assistants. Given agents-config's loose-bar charter (anything that sparks an idea), a thorough audit against our existing skills tree may surface gap-fills, compare-and-improve opportunities, or novel approaches worth tracking.

## Requirements

1. Clone (or gh-fetch) mattpocock/skills to a temp dir.
2. Enumerate every skill (every directory with a SKILL.md).
3. Per skill: read full SKILL.md (frontmatter + body); assign verdict: Gap-fill | Compare-and-improve | Spark | Skip.
4. Produce report table: skill name | verdict | rationale (1-3 sentences) for every skill.
5. Present report to Scott for review/adjustment BEFORE filing beads.
6. After Scott approves: file one capture-only adoption bead per non-skip skill as 4vn5 child.
   - Title: 'Adopt mattpocock skill: <name>' (Gap-fill, Spark)
   - Title: 'Evaluate mattpocock skill: <name>' (Compare-and-improve)
   - Notes: rationale + source link; no AC, no labels (start-bead will route to Route C)
7. Append rollup table to l0v0 notes via bd update --append-notes.
8. Close l0v0 with summary; run close-walk.

## Out of Scope

- Writing specs or AC for any adoption bead (Route C handles that later)
- Installing or integrating any mattpocock skill (adoption beads own that)
- Auditing other external skill repos
- Implementation of any kind in this audit

## Design Notes

- Bar = loose (Scott's explicit choice; accepts a follow-up triage pass for any noise)
- Adoption beads live under 4vn5 (siblings of l0v0), NOT under l0v0 — they are independent follow-on work
- Adoption beads are capture-only: no brainstormed/implementation-ready labels
- Audit depth = thorough (full SKILL.md read per skill) — loose bar means sparks hide in body content
- Report gate: present report BEFORE filing beads so Scott can adjust verdicts or nix skills
- Close-walk I2 will leave 4vn5 open correctly (adoption beads will be open children)

## Design Notes — Adversarial Review Addendum

**Verdict rubric (M1):**
- Gap-fill: addresses a capability we currently lack in our skills tree
- Compare-and-improve: overlaps an existing skill and may improve it
- Spark: non-overlapping idea or technique worth tracking, even if adoption is uncertain
- Skip: irrelevant domain, tool-specific lock-in, or already covered with no meaningful delta

**Report-gate outcome tree (M2):**
- Scott approves fully → file all non-skip beads; close l0v0
- Scott edits verdicts → rollup reflects post-adjustment verdicts, not auditor originals
- Scott approves subset → file approved subset only; note skipped-by-Scott entries in rollup
- Scott rejects entire report → l0v0 closes with 'retired: no applicable skills found' (or per Scott)
- Scott defers / no response → l0v0 stays in_progress; append a 'pending Scott review' note

**Enumeration definition (M3):**
Enumeration = exact match SKILL.md (case-sensitive) at any depth; non-matching artifacts ignored. If zero SKILL.md files found, close l0v0 with a 'no skills found' rollup.

**Adoption bead title for Spark verdict (m3):**
Use 'Capture mattpocock spark: <name>' for Spark verdicts — not 'Adopt' — to avoid pre-committing to adoption at capture time. Gap-fill → 'Adopt ...'; Compare-and-improve → 'Evaluate ...'; Spark → 'Capture ...'.

**Audit target provenance (m2):**
Record HEAD SHA of mattpocock/skills in the closing l0v0 summary so the rollup remains verifiable if the upstream repo reorganizes.

**Approved-non-skip contract (m4):**
If Scott downgrades a verdict to Skip during the report-gate review, NO adoption bead is filed for that skill. The rollup reflects Scott's final adjusted verdicts exclusively.
acceptance_criteria:
Every skill in mattpocock/skills is enumerated with an explicit verdict (Gap-fill | Compare-and-improve | Spark | Skip).
Report table presented to Scott and approved before any adoption bead is filed.
Each approved non-skip skill has a capture-only adoption bead filed as a child of epic 4vn5.
Rollup table (skill | verdict | bead-id or skip-reason) appended to l0v0 notes via --append-notes.
l0v0 closed with a one-line summary after rollup is complete.
close_reason: Audit complete: 28 mattpocock skills evaluated, report committed (SHA 9f2e0bd), rollup appended to l0v0, synthesis strategy captured in cx6 epic and cx6.1 child bead

title: merge-agents-config-abn9.7
description: Merge gate child for agents-config-abn9.7. Closed only by merge-and-cleanup after merge + branch/worktree cleanup.

title: [Impl] Improve step-bead naming conventions across all formulas
description:
## Summary

Establish a uniform, human-readable naming convention for all beads created by formula pour/wisp operations and by helper scripts within the `brainstorm-bead` formula. Every new formula step bead and `brainstorm-bead` finalize child bead will carry a title of the form `[<step-or-gate>] <title-slug>`, where `<title-slug>` is a kebab-cased derivative of the source bead or feature title.

## Background / motivation

Formula step beads currently use static, generic titles like "Preflight: spec validation, formula selection, worktree creation". When multiple molecules run the same formula in parallel, `bd mol current` and `bd list` produce indistinguishable walls of identical titles. The auto-generated numeric suffixes (`.1`, `.9`) added by `bd mol pour/wisp` are opaque and convey no semantic information. This makes it impossible to tell at a glance which molecule step belongs to which source bead, forcing users to click through every bead.

## Requirements

### R1. Uniform `title-slug` variable across all formulas

Add `[vars.title-slug]` to every `.formula.toml`:
- `brainstorm-bead`
- `implement-feature`
- `fix-bug`
- `docs-only`
- `merge-and-cleanup`

The variable is `required = true` and described as: "Kebab-cased slug derived from the source bead/feature title (≤30 chars). Used to make step-bead titles human-readable and source-identifiable."

### R2. Formula step titles use `[<step-id>] {{title-slug}}`

Update every `[[steps]] title` in all five formulas to the format:
```
[<step-id>] {{title-slug}}
```

where `<step-id>` is the step's `id` field (e.g., `preflight`, `red-tests`, `claim`, `verify`).

Examples:
- `[preflight] {{title-slug}}`
- `[red-tests] {{title-slug}}`
- `[claim] {{title-slug}}`
- `[merge] {{title-slug}}`

### R3. `start-bead` skill generates and passes `title-slug`

When the `start-bead` skill creates a `brainstorm-bead` wisp:
```bash
bd mol wisp create brainstorm-bead --var bead-id=<bead-id> --var title-slug=<slug>
```

The slug is generated from the source bead's title using the canonical algorithm (R6).

### R4. `implement-bead` skill generates and passes `title-slug`

When the `implement-bead` skill pours any formula (`implement-feature`, `fix-bug`, `docs-only`, `merge-and-cleanup`):
```bash
bd mol pour <formula> --var bead-id=<source-bead-id> --var title-slug=<slug> [other vars]
```

The slug is generated from the source bead's title using the canonical algorithm (R6).

### R5. Script-generated child beads in `brainstorm-bead` finalize follow the convention

Update the shell script in `brainstorm-bead.formula.toml`'s finalize step so that child beads created via `bd create` use:
- Merge-gate child (under Y): `[merge-gate] <title-slug>`
- Human-verify children (under Y): `[verify] <title-slug> - <ac-line>` (AC line retained for uniqueness)
- Escalation children (under Y): `[escalate] <title-slug>`

Pre-Y escalation beads (steps 1–2, created as orphan task beads linked to the source bead X via `blocked-by` dependency during idempotency probes) are **excluded** from this convention; they retain their diagnostic titles.

**Idempotency probe synchronization:** The finalize script uses exact-title probes to prevent duplicate child creation:
- Step 5a probes for `child_title = "merge-{{bead-id}}"` to identify legacy merge-gate children.
- Step 5b probes for `title == "[Human verify] $ac_line"` to avoid duplicate human-verify children.

These probes MUST be updated to match the new title patterns:
- Step 5a: probe for `child_title = "[merge-gate] $TITLE_SLUG"` (or a dual probe matching both old and new patterns during a transition window).
- Step 5b: probe for `title == "[verify] $TITLE_SLUG - $ac_line"`.

**Shell variable binding:** At the top of the finalize script, set:
```bash
TITLE_SLUG="{{title-slug}}"
```
`bd mol pour/wisp` substitutes `{{title-slug}}` with the literal slug value at molecule creation time, so the script sees `TITLE_SLUG="context-aware-session-renaming"` and can reference `$TITLE_SLUG` consistently in all `bd create` title strings and `jq --arg title` probes.

**Transition window for old-format children:** Step 5a's rename and step 5b's duplicate-avoidance probes MUST match BOTH old and new title patterns during a bounded transition window (until all beads created before this convention's deployment are closed, or 30 days from deployment, whichever is shorter). After the window, probes may match only the new patterns.

### R6. Canonical slug generation algorithm

The title-slug is generated by:
1. Lowercasing the source bead/feature title
2. Replacing spaces with hyphens
3. Stripping all characters except `a-z`, `0-9`, and hyphens
4. Collapsing consecutive hyphens to a single hyphen
5. Truncating to a maximum of 30 characters
6. Stripping leading and trailing hyphens
7. **Fallback:** If the result is empty (e.g., the title contained only non-ASCII characters or symbols), fall back to the first 30 characters of the bead-id.

Example: "Context-aware session renaming" → `context-aware-session-renaming`

### R7. No migration of existing beads

Existing open step beads and child beads are left as-is. The convention applies only to new `bd mol pour/wisp` operations and new `bd create` calls within formula scripts.

## Out of scope

- Renaming or migrating existing open molecule steps or child beads.
- Changing `bd` CLI behavior (e.g., auto-generating slugs at the tool level).
- Applying the convention to beads created outside of formulas (e.g., manual `bd create` by users).
- Modifying the numeric suffix behavior of `bd mol pour/wisp` itself — we work within the existing suffix mechanism by making the prefix human-readable.
- Script-generated beads created by formulas other than `brainstorm-bead` (e.g., `discovered_work` filings in `implement-feature` or `fix-bug`).

## Design notes

- **Why step-id and not step name?** The `id` field is stable, short, and already used for step referencing within formulas. Using it in the title provides immediate visual correlation with `bd mol current` output.
- **Why kebab-case slug instead of full title?** Titles can be 100+ characters. Truncating to 30 chars keeps `bd list` and `bd mol current` output readable while still conveying the source identity.
- **Why not include the bead ID?** The original proposal included `{source_bead_id}`, but the user explicitly chose a shorter slug-only format for readability. The `for-bead-<bead-id>` molecule linkage label already provides the machine-readable association.
- **Formula vars strategy:** We add `title-slug` as a new uniform variable alongside existing context-specific vars (`feature`, `bug`, `topic`, `pr`). Existing vars remain for other uses within step descriptions.
- **merge-and-cleanup:** This formula currently only has `pr` and `bead-id` vars. It gains `title-slug` so its steps (e.g., `[merge] {{title-slug}}`) are also source-identifiable.

## Acceptance criteria

1. [m] All five formula `.toml` files declare `[vars.title-slug]` with `required = true` and a descriptive `description`.
2. [m] Every `[[steps]] title` in all five formulas follows the exact format `[<step-id>] {{title-slug}}` (bracketed step id, single space, `{{title-slug}}`).
3. [m] The `start-bead` SKILL.md documents the slug generation algorithm (R6) and the actual `bd mol wisp create brainstorm-bead` code block in Route C includes `--var title-slug=<slug>`.
4. [m] The `implement-bead` SKILL.md documents the slug generation algorithm (R6) and every `bd mol pour` code block for the four formulas includes `--var title-slug=<slug>`.
5. [m] The `brainstorm-bead.formula.toml` finalize-step shell script creates child beads with titles matching `[merge-gate] <slug>`, `[verify] <slug> - <ac-line>`, and `[escalate] <slug>` (pre-Y escalation beads excluded).
6. [m] The finalize-step idempotency probes (step 5a merge-gate probe, step 5b human-verify probe) are updated to match the new title patterns.
7. [m] No existing open step beads or child beads are renamed.
8. [m] Grep verification: `grep -c 'title-slug' src/plugins/beads/.beads/formulas/*.formula.toml` returns ≥ 5.
9. [m] Grep verification: `grep -cE '^\[vars\.title-slug\]' src/plugins/beads/.beads/formulas/*.formula.toml` returns ≥ 5.
10. [m] Grep verification: `grep -cE 'title = "\[[a-z-]+\] \{\{title-slug\}\}"' src/plugins/beads/.beads/formulas/*.formula.toml` returns a count equal to the total number of `[[steps]]` across all five formulas.
11. [m] Grep verification: `grep -c 'title-slug' src/plugins/beads/.agents/skills/start-bead/SKILL.md` returns ≥ 1; same for `implement-bead/SKILL.md`.

## Open questions

1. **Reroute-created merge-gate children (M2 from RALF cycle 2):** `implement-feature` and `fix-bug` contain red-tests→docs-only reroute paths that may create merge-gate child beads. The title format for these reroute beads should be verified during implementation to ensure consistency with the `[merge-gate] <title-slug>` convention.
2. **Preflight branch naming unification (m2 from RALF cycle 2):** The `implement-feature`/`fix-bug`/`docs-only` preflight steps compute `<slug-from-title>` for git branch names (e.g., `feat/{{bead-id}}-<slug-from-title>`). Unifying this with `{{title-slug}}` is a future refinement not in this bead's scope.
acceptance_criteria:
[m] All five formula .toml files declare [vars.title-slug] with required=true and descriptive description.
[m] Every [[steps]] title in all five formulas follows the exact format [<step-id>] {{title-slug}}.
[m] The start-bead SKILL.md documents the slug generation algorithm (R6) and the actual bd mol wisp create brainstorm-bead code block in Route C includes --var title-slug=<slug>.
[m] The implement-bead SKILL.md documents the slug generation algorithm (R6) and every bd mol pour code block for the four formulas includes --var title-slug=<slug>.
[m] brainstorm-bead.formula.toml finalize-step shell script creates child beads with titles [merge-gate] <slug>, [verify] <slug> - <ac-line>, [escalate] <slug> (pre-Y escalation beads excluded).
[m] Finalize-step idempotency probes (step 5a merge-gate probe, step 5b human-verify probe) are updated to match new title patterns, with dual old/new pattern matching during a bounded transition window.
[m] No existing open step beads or child beads are renamed.
[m] Grep: grep -c 'title-slug' src/plugins/beads/.beads/formulas/*.formula.toml returns >= 5.
[m] Grep: grep -cE '^\[vars\.title-slug\]' src/plugins/beads/.beads/formulas/*.formula.toml returns >= 5.
[m] Grep: grep -cE 'title = "\[[a-z-]+\] \{\{title-slug\}\}"' src/plugins/beads/.beads/formulas/*.formula.toml returns count equal to total [[steps]] across all five formulas.
[m] Grep: grep -c 'title-slug' src/plugins/beads/.agents/skills/start-bead/SKILL.md returns >= 1; same for implement-bead/SKILL.md.
notes: verify-ac: AC8/9/10/11/5 all PASS. 37/37 step titles correct. No [h] ACs.
close_reason: feat(formulas): title-slug variable shipped via PR #65; all step-beads complete, all review threads resolved

title: wait-for-pr-comments: support fork-based PRs (head repo ≠ base repo)
description:
detect-pr-context.sh in wait-for-pr-comments uses gh pr view --json headRepository,headRepositoryOwner and threads those values through every downstream gh api call. For PRs opened from a fork, the PR number, review threads, and comment endpoints live on the BASE repository — not the contributor's head fork. Every gh call (fetch comments, post replies, resolve threads, request re-review) will 404 or target the wrong repo on fork PRs.

Current assumption holds for this project's branch-based workflow; only manifests for public-OSS / fork-contributor workflows.

Surfaced by Codex adversarial review of PR #64 (abn9.4), 2026-05-11. Flagged as 'high' severity but theoretical for the in-repo workflow.
acceptance_criteria: detect-pr-context.sh resolves and propagates BASE repository owner/name (baseRepository/baseRepositoryOwner) for all comment/review API paths; head_sha continues to come from the head ref. All downstream helpers (post-replies.sh, resolve-threads.sh, count-unresolved-threads.sh, request-rereview.sh, verify-head-sha.sh) use base owner/repo. Smoke test covers a fixture where head repo differs from base repo. Inventory schema documents the distinction explicitly. Build passes. Typecheck passes. Tests pass.

title: Split model tier: orchestrator at sonnet[1m]/medium, fix subagents at opus[1m]/high via explicit dispatch
notes: PR-review skills (wait-for-pr-comments, reply-and-resolve-pr-threads) currently run wholly at opus[1m]/high. Inspection shows ~70% of phase work is mechanical (jq/GraphQL/git/regex). The legitimate model judgment is comment classification (Skill A Phase 3) and code-fix authoring (per-comment subagents, Phase 4). Decision: drop orchestrators to sonnet[1m]/medium and explicitly dispatch fix subagents at opus[1m]/high in the Agent tool call. Subagent dispatch must NOT inherit the parent's model — silent inheritance regressions are the failure mode this guards against. Captured as an explicit Red Flag in Skill A and as AC #4 of agents-config-abn9.3.

title: Split model tier: orchestrator at sonnet[1m]/medium, fix subagents at opus[1m]/high via explicit dispatch
notes: Superseded by 2026-05-12-prgroom-cli-design.md section 5 (ratified). Premise collapsed: the architecture moved phase-orchestration out of an agent and into deterministic Python (prgroom), so there is no sonnet-tier orchestrator agent to tier-split. The cheap-vs-expensive boundary became cluster-contract (haiku/local-first) vs fix-contract (opus[1m]/xhigh). The fix orchestrator sets its OWN subagent model and effort -- prgroom does not explicitly dispatch them -- which inverts xv73's explicit-dispatch premise. Fix tier is TOML-configurable via [agents.fix] and --fix-model, so any cost-driven sonnet fallback is a config choice, not an architecture decision. Closed per Scott on 2026-06-07.

title: Codify periodic content audit as repeatable formula + skill (audit-codebase)
description:
Periodic content audits of the agents-config repo (exemplar: il69, PR #61) are fully ad hoc: no standard category checklist, no tier rubric, no remediation plan format, no gate convention. Each run re-derives all of these from scratch.

Build two artifacts to make the audit pipeline repeatable:

## Artifact 1: audit-codebase formula (pipeline backbone)
Location: src/plugins/beads/.beads/formulas/audit-codebase.formula.toml

Phases (in order, matching il69 exemplar):
1. audit — parallel subagent dispatch, one per content category; each produces a findings list
2. triage — aggregate findings; classify each by tier + vision tag; generate REMEDIATION_PLAN.md
3. gate — formula gate step; blocks until human annotates plan (opt-out gate model)
4. apply-t1 — single bead-implementor pass applying all Tier-1 (mechanical) fixes
5. file-t2-beads — create child beads for every Tier-2+ finding; link discovered-from to audit bead
6. delivery — standard create-pr + review-cycle steps

## Artifact 2: audit-codebase skill (methodology guide)
Location: src/user/.agents/skills/audit-codebase/SKILL.md

Must contain:
- Content category checklist (7 categories from il69: agents/, skills/, commands/, rules/, formulas/, scripts/, templates/)
- Tier classification rubric (see Notes)
- Vision rubric (see Notes)
- REMEDIATION_PLAN.md format/template
- Opt-out gate annotation convention (see Notes)
- T2+ bead-filing pattern (discovered-from dep, parent=audit-epic, strip inherited labels)
notes:
## Design decisions from il69 exemplar run (2026-05-10, PR #61)

### Tier classification rubric
- T1 = Mechanical / no judgment required — string fixes, dead-code removal, missing exit-code docs, portability fixes. Can batch into a single commit per category. Budget: respect vision C-share cap (≤30% of T1 items may be vision-C cleanup).
- T2 = Judgment call — behavioral changes, naming decisions, structural refactors, semantic contradictions. File as individual beads; do NOT apply in the T1 pass.
- T3 = Extraction / major refactor — helper-script extractions, skill splits, formula rewiring. File as beads; may need their own epics.

### Vision rubric (from REMEDIATION_PLAN.md gate)
- Vision A: directly advances 85/5/10 ratio (e.g., removes human-in-loop friction, adds spec-readiness gate)
- Vision B: indirectly supports the vision (e.g., clarity improvement enabling faster agent comprehension)
- Vision C: repo hygiene / cleanup (e.g., dead HTML comments, stale references) — cap at 30% of T1 items to prevent scope creep masquerading as vision work

### Opt-out gate convention (from il69 session — user-approved alternative to column-0 markers)
Default: every finding is APPROVED unless annotated otherwise.
Annotations applied inline in REMEDIATION_PLAN.md by human reviewer:
- [APPROVED] — explicit approval (optional; default is already approved)
- [DROP] — do not implement; finding is invalid or out of scope
- [DEMOTE] — move finding to lower tier (e.g., T1 → T2 or T2 → T3)
- [NOTE: ...] — contextual instruction for the implementor (e.g., 'keep existing naming paradigm')
- [DISCUSS: ...] — escalate; requires human decision before implementing
Gate releases when human posts 'approved' in chat OR when all findings carry inline annotation.

### REMEDIATION_PLAN.md format
Columns: | Finding ID | Category | Description | Tier | Vision | Status | Decision/Notes |
Finding IDs: category-prefix + sequential number (e.g., A1, SK3, CM2, R5, F4, SC2, T1)
Status values: APPROVED / DROP / DEMOTE / DISCUSS
Decision record: decisions.md file alongside the plan; each row D1..Dn capturing rationale

### T2+ bead-filing pattern
- Parent: the audit epic (NOT the audit bead itself — sibling pattern)
- dep type: discovered-from pointing to the audit bead
- Strip inherited labels post-create (bd create --parent copies parent lifecycle labels; audit and strip immediately)
- Group related T2 findings into single beads where they share a coherent fix story
- T3 extractions may warrant their own sub-epics

### Known pitfalls from il69
1. Main-branch merge mid-pipeline: if origin/main had fixes since AUDIT_INPUT_SHA, merge main into audit branch BEFORE applying T1 to avoid conflicting edits. Record superseded findings as D-series decisions.
2. TOML false positives: Python 3.9 toml package rejects triple-quoted strings; production bd formula show parser accepts them. Do not treat Python parse failure as a regression.
3. label inheritance: bd create --parent copies ALL parent labels including lifecycle markers. Always audit and strip after filing T2 beads.
4. Grep pattern precision: column-aligned TOML fields use two-space alignment (name  = ) not one-space (name = ). Single-space grep misses ~17 occurrences per run.

### Exemplar reference
- Audit bead: agents-config-il69 (closed, PR #61, squash commit 9ed3a0b)
- AUDIT_INPUT_SHA: af9c1bfc342bf7578ad491cc63dc95b07618c851
- Findings: 100 P1 + 48 P2; 31 T1 applied; 12 T2 beads filed (acmh.1–acmh.12)
- Audit artifacts committed at: docs/audits/phase3/REMEDIATION_PLAN.md, docs/audits/phase3/decisions.md

title: Spike: feedback loops at points of greatest realization + processing/improvement mechanism
description: Research spike per Scott's round-2 ask. Beyond agents-config-4htl (spec post-mortem), the roadmap currently has no built-in feedback hooks at strategic 'points of greatest realization' — moments in the bead workflow where lessons surface and could be captured. Examples: PR review (Copilot finds an anti-pattern → did our tests catch this?), HEP escalation (worker hit a decision point → was the spec under-specified?), overnight-run completion (what unexpected outcomes emerged?), bead closure (did the AC accurately predict success criteria?). Spike investigates: (1) what 'points of greatest realization' map to in the actual bead workflow; (2) what mechanisms could capture lessons at those points (hooks, audit beads, memory writes); (3) how to process and act on captures (likely overlaps with Vision-Gap-3 memory governance). Outcome: produce concrete adoption beads per identified hook, OR close as deferred if cost/benefit doesn't pencil.
acceptance_criteria: Spike outcome documented: (a) list of identified feedback points; (b) per-point capture mechanism proposal; (c) processing/improvement mechanism proposal; (d) per-point verdict — file concrete adoption bead, or close as deferred. Spike itself closes when all verdicts are applied.

title: Define PR classification policy for RALF-only vs human-required review
description: Vision-Gap-2 from the 2026-05-09 roadmap. RALF substitutes cross-model review for human review (commitment 3), but no open bead defines the decision rule for which PR classes get RALF-only vs human-required review. This is the prerequisite for agents-config-td39 (risk-tiered auto-merge): without a classification policy, the tiering has nothing to tier against. Output: a documented PR-classification policy keyed off blast radius, file types touched, and security surface — specific enough that a script can read a PR diff and emit a tier verdict.
acceptance_criteria: A docs artifact (in this repo) defines PR classification rules with concrete examples per tier. A script (or specification) exists that maps a PR diff to a tier verdict (RALF-only / RALF + human / human-required). agents-config-td39 references this policy as its blocker dep.

title: Post-MVP capabilities
description: Bucket for capabilities deferred until after M1-M4 MVP ships. Includes td39 (risk-tiered auto-merge — needs M3-MVP dogfooding to inform tier classification), ukzs (wall-clock pipelining of external waits), z7a (RALF foreign-eyes hard-fail), 7bk.16 (A/B testing per-stage allocations), syod (bd native gates research), and the Vision-Gap-2 PR-classification-policy bead. Reference: docs/plans/2026-05-09-roadmap.md Section 6.
acceptance_criteria: Each child bead has a verifiable AC of its own. Bucket closes when all children close.
notes:

All post-MVP capability children are either closed, promoted to a milestone, or explicitly deferred. The bucket exists only as long as any child remains open and unscheduled.

comments.text: Project-level deferral: all run-queue work is on hold until this redesign begins. Agents working in unrelated areas should NOT spend cycles on run-queue patching, hardening, or limitations triage. Incidental findings worth recording (e.g. "run-queue would mis-handle scenario X") should be appended here as a comment, then dropped. See AGENTS.md "Deferred Topics" section for the full protocol. Recent example: the round-5 review on PR #72 surfaced that run-queue bypasses the whats-next Rule B structural filter (polls `bd ready --label implementation-ready` directly), so leftover readiness labels on container sources CAN cause silent re-claim — this is a known limitation that will be addressed by this redesign rather than patched in run-queue today.

notes:
# Spec: Cross-cutting formulas-as-skills review (acmh.5)

## Summary
Survey of all 5 bead formulas for skill-extraction candidates produces ONE new skill — `merge-and-cleanup` — designed as a beads-agnostic, ad-hoc-invokable skill backed by three CLI helpers. All other audit-flagged "extraction" candidates resolve to either existing skill invocations, beads-specific orchestration that stays inline in formulas, or pure-mechanics CLI helpers (tracked by acmh.12). One discovered-from follow-up bead filed: agents-config-phzj.3 (install.sh skill-extension mechanism).

## Findings (spike outcome)

**Q (bead)**: For each formula, what should be translated into one or more skills?

**A**: One new skill (`merge-and-cleanup`), three CLI helpers (gh-merge mechanics, beads close-walk wrapper, PR-readiness checker), four invocation tiers. All other formulas already invoke the right skills today.

### Per-formula verdict

| Formula | Skill-extraction candidates evaluated | Verdict |
|---|---|---|
| `brainstorm-bead` | finalize formula-selection + handoff logic — *evaluated, rejected* | **Skip** — beads-specific orchestration, stays inline |
| `implement-feature` | adds terminal merge-step that invokes merge-and-cleanup skill (Tier C) | **No new skills** — invokes existing skill at end |
| `fix-bug` | adds terminal merge-step that invokes merge-and-cleanup skill (Tier C) | **No new skills** — invokes existing skill at end |
| `docs-only` | none beyond existing | **No new skills** |
| **`merge-and-cleanup`** | THE skill (formula collapses to Tier-D wisp wrapping it) | **DESIGN** below |

## Background / Motivation
Tier-2 audit finding il69 surfaced that several formula steps mix mechanical bash with agent judgment. The user named `merge-and-cleanup` as the canonical candidate for skill-extraction ("often wished as ad-hoc invokable skill"). acmh.5 was split out of acmh.4 (formulas/ cleanups) so the merge-and-cleanup-specific findings (F8, F9, F10, F15) could be resolved by skill-extraction rather than formula prose edits.

Strategic intent (per discuss step):
- Agent should be minimally involved in merge-and-cleanup beyond orchestration + troubleshooting.
- Mechanics live in deterministic CLI helpers; skill is a thin agent-facing wrapper.
- Skill is beads-unaware at runtime (consumers without beads must work end-to-end). Beads-specific extension carried in an inline cheat block delimited by HTML comments, to be extracted to a plugin overlay file once agents-config-phzj.3 ships.

## Requirements

### R2 — merge-and-cleanup skill design

**Location**: `src/user/.agents/skills/merge-and-cleanup/SKILL.md` (shared, beads-agnostic at runtime).

**Skill body (prose contract)**:
1. **Detect invocation context**: open PR number (required) and bead/molecule context observed from prior conversation/active formula (optional — informs cheat block activation).
2. **Invoke PR-readiness check** via `gh-pr-readiness-check.sh --pr <N> --json` (B3 below). Helper returns structured findings categorized as:
   - `bot_review_pending` — a bot reviewer (Copilot, Codex, etc.) submitted a review on a stale commit but not on the current HEAD.
   - `unresolved_threads` — GraphQL `pullRequestThread.isResolved == false` AND last comment author ≠ PR author (genuine unresponded actionable feedback). Each entry may carry an advisory `escalation_hint: true` annotation if its last comment matches the configured escalation-phrase regex (advisory only — does NOT gate readiness).
   - `pending_review_request` — `reviewRequests` non-empty (CODEOWNERS / required reviewer outstanding).
   - `review_decision_block` — `reviewDecision` ∈ {REVIEW_REQUIRED, CHANGES_REQUESTED}.
3. **Triage readiness findings**: any non-empty signal → present findings to user, ask for explicit override-or-wait. NO findings → proceed. Override response (verbatim user text + timestamp) is captured in the audit trail per D9. **Note on `bot_review_pending` UX**: this signal will fire on most PRs whose bots reviewed an early commit and then the PR received further pushes (GitHub exposes no native "review complete vs. in-flight" marker — see Feasibility Annex). Expect override-with-explanation to be a routine flow for this signal specifically; the skill MUST present `bot_review_pending` findings with a one-line context blurb explaining the staleness heuristic so the user can make an informed override decision.
4. **Verify explicit merge authorization** — two paths. **Conveyance is prose-based, not a literal CLI flag**: SKILL.md is read by an agent, not invoked as a binary, so "passes through" means the captured phrase lives in conversation context (Tier A/B) or in the prior molecule step's notes (Tier C/D) that the agent reading the skill prose can directly observe.
   - **Pre-captured auth (Tier C/D and any caller that captured "ship it"-class phrase before invoking the skill)**: prior formula/wisp step captures the phrase in its OWN user prompt and writes it to that step's notes (e.g., `bd update <step-bead-id> --append-notes "auth-phrase: <verbatim>"`). The skill body instructs the executing agent to read those notes and validate the phrase against R8's **authoritative phrase set** using R8's comparator. Match → proceed. Mismatch → HALT and re-prompt the user via path B below; on a valid re-prompt, the prior step's notes are updated via `bd update --append-notes "auth-phrase-corrected: <new-verbatim>"` so the audit trail records the actual phrase used. (Note: prior-step responsibility for capture-and-validate per R6 should prevent this re-prompt path in practice — see D5.)
   - **No pre-captured auth (Tier A/B direct invocation)**: skill prompts the user for explicit authorization in the current conversation turn, validates against the authoritative phrase set using R8's comparator.
   In both paths, NO valid authorization → HALT. Trust-but-verify is non-negotiable.
5. **Invoke `gh-pr-merge-and-cleanup.sh --pr <N> [--worktree <path>] [--branch <name>] [--mode squash|merge|rebase]`** (B1) to perform PR merge + branch delete + worktree teardown. Skill reads B1's JSON output to determine outcome (see B1 exit-code contract in R3); B1 exit code 0 always means "script ran" — outcome lives in JSON.
6. **Inline beads-cheat block** (extract-later): "if the current work is tracked by a bead or molecule step bead, also run `bd-merge-cleanup.sh --bead-id <id>` (B2) to perform the beads close-walk and merge-gate-child detection." Block delimited by `<!-- BEGIN beads-cheat — extract via agents-config-phzj.3 -->` / `<!-- END beads-cheat -->`. Cheat-block invariants in R4. **Conditional execution**: Tier-A invocations skip this block; Tier-B/C/D activate it.
7. **Report outcome**: PR URL, merge SHA, branch/worktree status, beads context status (if cheat block activated), any override decisions captured in step 3.

**Four invocation tiers** all hit the same skill — they differ only by whether bead/molecule context is observable to the agent. **All four tiers run the same PR-readiness check in step 2** — Tier A is not exempt.
- **Tier A (untracked PR)**: open PR with no bead/molecule context; skill runs end-to-end without invoking the cheat block; skill prompts directly for authorization (R2 step 4 second path).
- **Tier B (bead-tracked, no wisp)**: agent observes the bead context from conversation; cheat block activates; auth flow follows whichever path applies (typically Tier-A second path because there's no formula step capturing the phrase).
- **Tier C (mid-formula fast-path; DEFAULT for new work)**: implement-feature / fix-bug formula adds a terminal merge step. That step captures the user's authorization phrase in its OWN prompt, then invokes the skill with `--authorization "<phrase>"`. Skill validates without re-prompting (R2 step 4 first path). Bead context = the active molecule's source bead.
- **Tier D (merge-and-cleanup wisp; retroactive / cross-formula path)**: the existing `merge-and-cleanup` formula refactored to a two-step thin wisp (R6). Survives for cases where Tier C wasn't used (work merged via separate `bd mol pour merge-and-cleanup`).

### R3 — Three CLI helpers (ship together with the skill in the Y bead)

All three helpers share an **exit-code contract**: exit 0 means "script ran to a defined outcome" (success OR documented partial-state OR documented refusal); the JSON output carries the actual outcome via dedicated fields. Non-zero exit is reserved for **usage errors** (bad args, missing required dependency, gh not authenticated) — never for partial-success states. This keeps `set -e` callers (the skill) from short-circuiting before they read the JSON.

**B1: `gh-pr-merge-and-cleanup.sh`** — location: `src/user/.agents/scripts/`, deployed to `~/.agents/scripts/` by install.sh (see R10 — install.sh enhancement in Y scope).
- Args: `--pr <N>` (required), `--worktree <path>` (optional), `--branch <name>` (optional), `--mode squash|merge|rebase` (optional; default `squash`).
- Behavior: `gh pr merge --<mode> --delete-branch <N>`; if `--worktree` given, attempt `git worktree remove --force` after merge succeeds.
- Output: JSON `{ "merge": "ok|failed", "merge_sha": "<sha>|null", "branch": "deleted|preserved|n/a", "worktree": "removed|failed|n/a", "error": "<msg>|null" }`.
- Beads-unaware.

**B2: `bd-merge-cleanup.sh`** — location: `src/plugins/beads/.beads/scripts/`, deployed to `~/.beads/scripts/` via existing plugin scripts mechanism.
- Args: `--bead-id <id>` (required), `--reason <text>` (optional; default `"Merged via merge-and-cleanup skill"`).
- Behavior — explicit phase list (preserves existing formula's 3-phase ordering). **B2 is purely deterministic — it does NOT execute HEP**; on data-anomaly outcomes, it emits a structured outcome and exits 0, letting the skill (which has conversational context, molecule-ID, and worktree path) decide whether to escalate via HEP.
  1. **Detect merge-gate child** of `<bead-id>` via the F10 logic. If exactly one → proceed to phase 2. If zero → emit `{ "outcome": "no_merge_gate_child", ... }` and exit 0. If two or more → emit `{ "outcome": "multiple_merge_gate_children", "merge_gate_candidates": ["<id1>", "<id2>", ...] }` and exit 0. **Skill is responsible for invoking HEP on these data-anomaly outcomes** (the skill has the molecule context HEP requires).
  2. **Close merge-gate child** with `bd close <merge-gate-id> --reason "<reason> (merge-gate)"`.
  3. **Close source bead** with `bd close <bead-id> --reason "<reason>"`.
  4. **Invoke** `~/.beads/scripts/bd-close-walk.sh --bead-id <bead-id>` to walk ancestor epics (closes any whose remaining children are all closed). **WRAPS, does not reimplement** `bd-close-walk.sh`.
- Output: JSON `{ "outcome": "ok|no_merge_gate_child|multiple_merge_gate_children|partial", "merge_gate_child": "<id>|null", "merge_gate_candidates": ["<id>", ...], "closed": ["<id>", ...], "walked": <N>, "error": "<msg>|null" }`.
- Beads-specific.

**B3: `gh-pr-readiness-check.sh`** — location: `src/user/.agents/scripts/`, deployed to `~/.agents/scripts/` (see R10).
- Args: `--pr <N>` (required), `--json` (optional; default true), `--bot-authors <comma-list>` (optional; default `"copilot-pull-request-reviewer,chatgpt-codex-connector"`), `--escalation-phrases <regex-pattern>` (optional override of the default escalation-phrase pattern for `escalation_hint` annotation; value MUST be a single POSIX-extended regex — commas are NOT separators; see Feasibility Annex for default).
- Behavior: queries gh CLI / GraphQL for the four readiness signals enumerated in R2 step 2 (see Feasibility Annex below for exact probes). `escalation_hint` annotations are computed on `unresolved_threads` entries via regex match; they are NOT a standalone gating signal.
- Output: JSON `{ "ready": true|false, "signals": { "bot_review_pending": [...], "unresolved_threads": [{ "id": "...", "path": "...", "line": ..., "last_comment_author": "...", "escalation_hint": true|false }, ...], "pending_review_request": [...], "review_decision_block": "<state>|null" } }`. `ready=true` iff all four signal arrays/values are empty/null. (`escalation_hint` is advisory only.)
- Beads-unaware.

### R4 — Inline beads-cheat block invariants
The skill's beads section is bracketed by HTML comments naming agents-config-phzj.3 as the bead that will eventually extract it. Convention:
- **Single contiguous region** between the BEGIN/END markers.
- **Self-contained** — readable as standalone prose without referring to surrounding sections.
- **Deletable without breaking surrounding prose** — the base skill flow MUST remain coherent if the cheat block is removed in entirety. Tier-A users effectively see this scenario at runtime.
- **No other content between BEGIN/END** — no nested conditionals, no other plugin's extension content. One cheat block per plugin.
- **Extraction direction-of-travel** (not a hard guarantee): the cheat block is structured to make extraction by phzj.3 mechanically trivial. If phzj.3's eventual design requires shape changes (e.g., per-section append markers, YAML overlay, templating shim), the adjustments will be captured at phzj.3's spec time as a discovered-from edge back to acmh.5. This spec does not bind a not-yet-brainstormed sibling to a verbatim-move commitment.

### R5 — Follow-up bead production (finalize stamps `produced-bead-<Y>`)
Exactly ONE Y bead, covering: the skill, three CLI helpers, install.sh enhancement (R10), Tier-C terminal merge steps in `implement-feature.formula.toml` + `fix-bug.formula.toml`, Tier-D wisp refactor of `merge-and-cleanup.formula.toml`, and delivery.md auth-phrase reference rewrite (R8) — all as one cohesive impl unit. Parent: agents-config-abn9 (M1). Discovered-from: acmh.5. The brainstorm-bead finalize step files it and stamps the produced-from edge.

Note on scope: Y is wider than initially scoped due to user direction (keep Tier C in scope; expand to update install.sh). If Y's implementer surfaces blast-radius concerns, they may split portions into a child bead. Decision deferred to Y bead.

### R6 — Tier-D wisp shape (current merge-and-cleanup formula refactor)
The existing `merge-and-cleanup.formula.toml` is refactored to a two-step wisp:
- **Step 1 — `preflight`**: validates `pr` and `bead-id` vars; performs claim-walk on the source bead (marks in_progress + ancestors); **captures the user's authorization phrase with R8 validation in-step** — i.e., this step prompts, validates against R8's phrase set using R8's comparator, and re-prompts until valid before committing the validated phrase to the step's notes (`bd update <step-bead-id> --append-notes "auth-phrase: <verbatim>"`). Step 2 can then trust the notes.
- **Step 2 — `invoke-skill`**: invokes the `merge-and-cleanup` skill with observable bead context. Skill prose (R2 step 4 first path) reads the validated auth phrase from step 1's notes. Skill executes R2's flow (steps 1–7), with step 6 (cheat block) activating because bead context is present.

Vars retained: `pr`, `bead-id`. Vars dropped: `title-slug` (only needed for step-bead naming, irrelevant in a 2-step wisp).

**Backward compatibility**: existing molecule instances created from the current 7-step formula retain their vars (including `title-slug`) in their persistent state — no migration required. Only new pours/wisps after Y lands use the slimmer schema.

The existing formula's steps `verify-completion-gate-evidence`, `check-pr-comments`, `authorize-merge`, `merge`, `cleanup`, `audit` collapse into the skill body (R2). F8 (file header purpose statement) still applies to the slimmer file header in acmh.4's scope; F9 disappears (no separate authorize-merge step survives); F10 is implemented by B2; F15 disappears (delegate-to-wait-for-pr-comments wording superseded by lighter PR-readiness check living in B3).

### R7 — Hook-convention idea bound to AC #6 (and phzj.3)
Future extensibility (out of Y scope, deferred to phzj.3 brainstorm): pre-/post-merge hook script directory convention under `merge-and-cleanup/scripts/extensions/`. Main `gh-pr-merge-and-cleanup.sh` would auto-discover and invoke matching hooks at corresponding hook points, with a contract for status/error reporting, ordering, and stdout isolation.

This requirement is satisfied by acceptance criterion #6 — a comment with full hook-convention exploration notes posted on phzj.3 during this spike's discuss step.

### R8 — Auth-phrase source of truth, comparator, and reference mechanism
The skill is the **authoritative source** for the merge-authorization phrase set. The set is enumerated explicitly in SKILL.md R2 step 4 prose.

**Initial phrase set**: `"ship it"`, `"go ahead and merge"`, `"merge it"`, `"merge that"`, `"approved, merge"`. Y bead's implementer adjusts only with explicit user signoff.

**Comparator (deterministic)**: case-insensitive substring match against the **normalized user response**, where normalization is: lowercase, trim leading/trailing whitespace, collapse internal whitespace runs to single spaces. A response matches if at least one phrase in R8's set is found as a contained substring of the normalized response. Examples:
- `"yeah, go ahead and merge it now"` → matches (`"go ahead and merge"` is a substring).
- `"ship it!"` → matches after normalization (trailing `!` is not part of the phrase but the substring still matches).
- `"yeah, go"` → no match (no enumerated phrase is contained).
- `"GO AHEAD AND MERGE"` → matches (case-insensitive normalization).

**Reference mechanism (interim, picks the cheap-and-clear option)**: Y bead edits `src/user/.claude/rules/delivery.md` to remove its current enumerated phrase list and replace with a prose pointer naming the `merge-and-cleanup` skill (R2 step 4) as the authoritative source. **Wording flexibility**: Y's implementer may adjust the pointer wording for tone; the contract is (a) no enumerated phrases remain in delivery.md, (b) the pointer names the merge-and-cleanup skill as authoritative, (c) human readers can follow the pointer to find the actual phrase set.

**Future-state (out of Y scope, captured as discovered-from from Y)**: factor the phrase set into a shared file consumed by both surfaces (e.g., `~/.agents/auth-phrases.txt`) so the reference is mechanical rather than prose-only. Y's implementer files a discovered-from bead capturing this idea.

### R9 — acmh.4 ripple (concrete owner: this spike's finalize step)
On finalize, do BOTH:
1. **Post a comment** on agents-config-acmh.4 summarizing the ripple.
2. **Edit acmh.4's description** to:
   - Strike F9 from in-scope (no separate authorize-merge step survives in the Tier-D wisp).
   - Strike F10 from in-scope (absorbed by B2 in Y).
   - Strike F15 from in-scope (superseded by the skill's lighter PR-readiness check; not absorbed).
   - Keep F8 in-scope (slimmer file header still needs the purpose-statement rewrite).

acmh.4 unblocks once Y lands.

### R10 — install.sh enhancement (in Y scope per user direction on C1)
Y bead expands `scripts/install.sh` to deploy shared agent scripts:
- New install path: `src/user/.agents/scripts/*` → `~/.agents/scripts/` (mirror existing `src/plugins/<plugin>/.beads/scripts/` → `~/.beads/scripts/` pattern at install.sh lines 1018–1097).
- Helpers B1 (`gh-pr-merge-and-cleanup.sh`) and B3 (`gh-pr-readiness-check.sh`) ship via this path so they're available regardless of which plugins are enabled (preserves Tier-A "no beads required" intent).
- B2 (`bd-merge-cleanup.sh`) continues to ship via the existing beads-plugin scripts mechanism (only deployed when beads plugin enabled — correct, because B2 is beads-specific).
- **Prune behavior**: shared scripts MIRROR the existing beads-scripts no-prune behavior (`--prune` does NOT remove scripts that are no longer in source). This is intentional and consistent — scripts are rare, hand-managed, and a user removing one from source should not silently lose their installed copy. Y bead documents this in install.sh prose alongside the new install path.

This bundling is by user direction (acmh.5 discuss): preferable to opening yet another install-mechanism sibling bead and hard-blocking Y on it.

## Out of Scope (revised)
- **Implementation** of the skill, CLIs, formula edits, install.sh enhancement, delivery.md rewrite (Y bead's job — but ALL of these are now in Y's scope).
- **install.sh skill-extension injection mechanism for SKILL.md** (agents-config-phzj.3 — itself needs future brainstorm before impl). Distinct from R10's scripts-deploy path which IS in Y.
- **Other formula-level audit findings** (acmh.4's F8 prose edit; acmh.12's CLI helper extractions — except B2 which Y absorbs because it implements F10).
- **Hook-convention design** for pre-/post-merge scripts (deferred to phzj.3 brainstorm).
- **brainstorm-bead, docs-only** structural restructuring (no new skills surfaced; existing skill invocations already cover them).
- **Shared auth-phrases file mechanism** (R8 future-state — Y files a discovered-from bead).

NOTE: implement-feature and fix-bug **ARE in scope** per Tier C — Y bead adds a terminal merge step to both. This replaces any earlier "Out of Scope §4" wording about not restructuring those formulas.

## Acceptance Criteria (inlined in spec; bead acceptance field carries the same list)

1. Per-formula verdict produced for all 5 formulas. implement-feature and fix-bug confirmed as Tier-C consumers, not new-skill sources.
2. merge-and-cleanup skill design specified: beads-agnostic at runtime SKILL.md body, FOUR invocation tiers (all running PR-readiness check), pre-captured-auth path for Tier-C/D + direct-prompt path for Tier-A/B, code-backed PR-readiness check with override-or-wait UX on findings, inline beads-cheat block with R4 invariants delimited by HTML comments naming phzj.3.
3. Three supporting CLI helpers specified with args + behavior + JSON output + exit-code contract (exit 0 always means "script ran"; outcome via JSON; non-zero only for usage errors).
4. Y bead scope includes install.sh enhancement (R10): deploy `src/user/.agents/scripts/` to `~/.agents/scripts/`, mirror existing plugin scripts pattern.
5. Y bead scope includes delivery.md edit (R8) replacing enumerated phrase list with a prose pointer to the skill as authoritative source. Initial phrase set enumerated in this spec.
6. agents-config-phzj.3 description/comments updated with the future hook-convention idea.
7. acmh.4 ripple on finalize is concrete: comment posted AND acmh.4 description edited to strike F9/F10/F15 from scope and keep F8 as residual; acmh.4 unblocked once Y lands.
8. ONE Y bead filed at finalize covering ALL of the above as one cohesive impl unit; parent agents-config-abn9 (M1); discovered-from acmh.5; produced-from edge stamped on acmh.5.

## Design Notes / Decisions Made
- **D1 (skill-vs-formula intent)**: skill is the canonical implementation; beads workflow USES the skill, not vice versa.
- **D2 (beads-agnostic at runtime, not at file content)**: skill is shared (`src/user/.agents/skills/`), not plugin-scoped. Consumers without beads run it end-to-end with no beads-side calls executed. The base SKILL.md *does* carry a clearly-bracketed beads-extension block in its file content (until phzj.3 ships), but Tier-A users observe and execute the file as if the block were absent.
- **D3 (one Y bead, blast-radius known)**: skill + three CLIs + install.sh enhancement + two formula step-additions + one formula refactor + delivery.md edit ships as one Y. Y implementer may split if they hit limits.
- **D4 (cheat block now, mechanism later)**: install.sh has no plugin → SKILL.md append mechanism today. Inline beads section in base SKILL.md is pragmatic interim; phzj.3 captures the formal-mechanism work. R4 invariants govern the cheat block's shape; R4's last bullet softens "MUST move verbatim" to "structured for trivial extraction; phzj.3 may adjust at its spec time."
- **D5 (auth gate non-negotiable, single auth event per merge)**: skill ALWAYS validates against its authoritative phrase set (R8). Tier C/D pre-capture the phrase and pass it through so the user authorizes ONCE per merge — not once at the formula step AND once at the skill. Tier A/B direct invocations prompt the user.
- **D6 (formula survives as Tier D wisp)**: `merge-and-cleanup.formula.toml` refactored to two-step wisp (R6). NOT deleted — preserves the molecule audit trail.
- **D7 (PR-readiness vs. wait-for-pr-comments — composable, not overlapping)**:
  - `wait-for-pr-comments` OWNS the **classify-fix-push-reply-resolve** cycle. Mutates: writes fixes, pushes commits, posts replies, resolves threads.
  - `merge-and-cleanup`'s readiness check OWNS the **merge-time go/no-go signal**, NOT the resolution of any comment. Read-only against the PR surface; emits structured findings.
  - Composable workflow: agent runs wait-for-pr-comments first (if any feedback exists); then merge-and-cleanup's readiness check confirms residual state is mergeable. Readiness check **assumes** wait-for-pr-comments has already run if it was needed.
- **D8 (Tier C vs Tier D canonicality)**: Tier C is the DEFAULT path for new work tracked via implement-feature/fix-bug. Tier D survives for retroactive merge (work whose original formula didn't include a terminal merge step) and for ad-hoc / cross-formula bead-tracked merges. Both paths exist intentionally.
- **D9 (audit trail medium and format)**: For Tier B/C/D, the skill appends a structured audit block to the source bead's notes via `bd update <bead-id> --append-notes` at three points: after readiness-check completes, after any override decision, and after the final merge outcome. **Format**: each append is a marker line `[merge-and-cleanup audit @<ISO8601-timestamp>]` followed by `key: value` lines, e.g.:
  ```
  [merge-and-cleanup audit @2026-05-14T17:23:00Z]
  phase: readiness-check
  pr: 123
  ready: false
  signals: bot_review_pending=[copilot:14f3ccd3] unresolved_threads=[] pending_review_request=[] review_decision_block=null
  ```
  Subsequent appends (override, merge-outcome) follow the same pattern. For Tier B (bead-tracked, no wisp) the skill reads `<bead-id>` from observable bead context in the conversation and writes directly to that bead's notes — no intermediary mechanism. For Tier A (no bead), the conversation transcript IS the audit trail — no extra persistence required.
- **D10 (exit-code contract, R3 paragraph)**: see R3's leading paragraph. Always exit 0 unless the script literally couldn't run; outcome flows through JSON. Keeps `set -e` callers safe and JSON-first.

## Feasibility Annex — gh probes for PR-readiness check

Validated against real example: agents-config PR #67 (HEP rollout) — 30 reviews from Copilot, Codex, and the PR owner; 30 inline review comments; multiple bot review submissions across the PR's lifetime.

GitHub's REST/GraphQL API exposes the following signals; B3 composes these into the structured output specified in R3.

| Readiness signal | Primary probe | Notes |
|---|---|---|
| `bot_review_pending` (bot reviewed older commit, not HEAD) | `gh pr view <N> --json reviews,headRefOid` then jq filter for reviews whose `commit.oid` ≠ `headRefOid` AND author ∈ known bot list (`copilot-pull-request-reviewer`, `chatgpt-codex-connector`). | Bot list is configurable in B3 via `--bot-authors` arg (default carries the two above). Robust against PR #67's observed pattern: each bot reviewed the commit-of-the-moment at the time of review, not a single sustained "running" review. |
| `unresolved_threads` (actionable comments with no author resolution) | `gh api graphql -f query='{repository(owner:OWN,name:REP){pullRequest(number:N){reviewThreads(first:100){nodes{isResolved,path,line,comments(first:50){nodes{author{login},body,createdAt}}}}}}}'` then filter `isResolved == false` AND last-comment author ≠ PR author. Per-entry, compute `escalation_hint` as advisory annotation by regex-matching last comment body. | GraphQL `isResolved` is the canonical signal. REST `/pulls/N/comments` does not expose thread-resolution state; only GraphQL does. |
| `pending_review_request` (CODEOWNERS or required reviewer unmet) | `gh pr view <N> --json reviewRequests` — non-empty list means at least one requested reviewer has not submitted. | reviewRequests gets cleared when a review is submitted; persistent value = genuine pending. |
| `review_decision_block` (overall PR review-state) | `gh pr view <N> --json reviewDecision` — value ∈ {APPROVED, REVIEW_REQUIRED, CHANGES_REQUESTED}. | Treat REVIEW_REQUIRED and CHANGES_REQUESTED as hard-stop signals. APPROVED clears the signal. |
| `escalation_hint` (advisory, NOT a separate signal) | Same GraphQL as `unresolved_threads`. Each unresolved-thread entry carries `escalation_hint: true` iff its last comment matches the default phrase regex `/(escalat(ed|ion)|deferred (to|until)|blocked on human|needs (design|product) input)/i`. | Demoted from a standalone gating signal to an advisory annotation. Default regex is intentionally narrower than the prior "deferred" catch-all to reduce false positives like "I deferred the typo fix." Configurable via B3's `--escalation-phrases` arg. |

**Key API observations from PR #67:**
- GitHub does **NOT** expose a native "review in progress" state for bots. Bots indicate "reviewing now" by eventually submitting a review with `state == COMMENTED`. The "is the bot currently looking?" question is undecidable from the API alone. Our `bot_review_pending` heuristic (reviewed an older commit but not HEAD) is the closest proxy.
- Both Copilot and Codex use `state == COMMENTED` (never APPROVED / CHANGES_REQUESTED). `reviewDecision` is unaffected by bot reviews unless the bot is in CODEOWNERS.
- PR #67 carried 3 separate Copilot review submissions and 3 separate Codex review submissions across the PR's lifetime. Cross-commit multiplicity confirmed; per-(author, commit) multiplicity not specifically validated by this annex — Y bead's implementer should sanity-check on a small sample whether bots ever submit two reviews on the same commit and adjust B3's dedupe rule (default: latest review per (author, commit) wins) if needed.
- Top-level PR comments (`/issues/N/comments`) were empty in PR #67; all bot output came through the review surface (`/pulls/N/reviews`). B3 focuses on the review surface.

## Open Questions
None blocking. Y-bead first design tasks:
1. **Instrumentation**: should the skill emit a lightweight log line (or PR comment, or bead-note tag) so we can observe adoption rate, false-positive readiness signals, and override patterns? Y bead's implementer decides; the D9 audit blocks already give per-invocation evidence — instrumentation question is whether to aggregate.
2. **`bot_review_pending` future enhancement**: M4 in cycle 2 review surfaced that the stale-bot signal will fire chronically. Initial implementation accepts this and documents the override UX (per R2 step 3). Y bead's implementer may add a time-window cap (e.g., "bot reviews older than 14 days OR more than 3 commits ago are treated as completed-and-stale") as a follow-up improvement; not blocking initial Y completion.

## Related beads
- Parent: agents-config-4vn5 (EPIC: Research spikes — retire-or-rescope)
- Blocks: agents-config-acmh.4 (formulas-tier2 — F8 residual scope; F9/F10/F15 stripped by R9 ripple edit)
- Discovered-from: agents-config-il69 (audit)
- Discovered: agents-config-phzj.3 (install.sh skill-extension mechanism for SKILL.md — needs own brainstorm before impl; distinct from R10's scripts-deploy path which IS in Y)
- Sibling Tier-2 split: agents-config-acmh.12 (pure-mechanics helper-script extractions for formula content NOT in acmh.5's skill scope — F1/F4/F5/F14)
- Produces: `<Y-bead-id>` (filed at finalize; "Impl: merge-and-cleanup skill + three CLI helpers + install.sh enhancement + Tier C/D formula refactors + delivery.md auth-phrase reference rewrite")

title: Tier 2: commands/ cleanups (F1 F2 F4 F5 F6 F7)
description: Tier 2 follow-up from agents-config-il69 audit (Phase 3). See docs/audits/phase3/by-category/commands.md and REMEDIATION_PLAN.md. Theme: commands exceed lean-body target by 2-3×, embedding methodology that should delegate to peer skills; scope confusion between agent-persona files vs AGENTS.md configuration. Findings: F1 optimize-my-skill 233 lines (lean-delegation reshape); F2 optimize-my-agent 107 lines (extract methodology to optimize-my-agent skill); F4 $ARGUMENTS default-path ambiguity for user-level installs; F5 optimize-my-agent rubric divergence from AGENTS_PRIMER schema; F6 refresh-agents-md hardcodes dispatching-parallel-agents w/o fallback; F7 refresh-agents-md re-implements optimize-agents-md skill principles inline.
acceptance_criteria:
src/user/.agents/skills/optimize-my-skill/SKILL.md exists; ≤500 lines; frontmatter: name=optimize-my-skill, model=sonnet, allowed-tools: Read, Write, Edit, Glob, Grep, AskUserQuestion (comma-separated); SKILLS_PRIMER-compliant description ≥3 observable triggers, negative trigger contains 'Do NOT use for agent persona files or AGENTS.md configuration files', no XML angle brackets, name excludes 'claude'/'anthropic'; contains Phase 1 discovery, Phase 2 quality criteria (frontmatter checks, folder structure, body content, anti-patterns), Phase 3 propose improvements, Phase 4 suggest testing, Phase 5 confirm/apply, 'What Makes Skills Effective' reference (file structure, frontmatter fields, body structure, key principles); each Phase section ≥10 lines; each 'What Makes Skills Effective' sub-section ≥5 lines; no occurrences of '5,000 words'
src/user/.agents/skills/optimize-my-agent/SKILL.md exists; ≤500 lines; frontmatter: name=optimize-my-agent, model=sonnet, allowed-tools: Read, Write, Edit, Glob, Grep, AskUserQuestion (comma-separated); SKILLS_PRIMER-compliant description ≥3 observable triggers, negative trigger contains 'Do NOT use for SKILL.md files or AGENTS.md configuration files', no XML angle brackets; contains all 5 optimization phases, quality rubric with exactly 6 items matching AGENTS_PRIMER titles verbatim with exact casing (Over-broad role; No examples in description; Wrong model tier; Body mixes role with task; Bead references in shared agents; `skills` lists unused skills), 7-item failure checklist, phase 4 recommendation structure (Missing/Weak Area / Current / Recommended Addition / Why template); each section ≥10 lines; scoped to agent persona files
optimize-my-skill.md command ≤80 lines; invokes optimize-my-skill skill; no occurrences of '5,000 words'; empty ARGUMENTS: probe ~/.claude/skills/ depth-1 SKILL.md subdirs → probe .claude/skills/ same predicate → emit 'No skills found. Pass a path: /optimize-my-skill <path>' if both fail; non-existent directory satisfies fails-predicate
optimize-my-agent.md command ≤80 lines; invokes optimize-my-agent skill; scope: agent persona files only
refresh-agents-md.md Step 1: a-priori probe for dispatching-parallel-agents (matches ~/.claude/skills/dispatching-parallel-agents/SKILL.md or ~/.claude/plugins/*/skills/dispatching-parallel-agents/SKILL.md); if matches run simultaneously; if not run sequentially — Git Analyzer, File Inventory, Directory Discovery
refresh-agents-md.md Step 3b: no inline principle enumeration; optimize-agents-md skill referenced by name; skip-scope-questions instruction present; focus_areas and parent-file context forwarded (semantic check)
refresh-agents-md.md Step 3d: no inline validation checklist; optimize-agents-md skill Validation Checklist section referenced by name (semantic check)
scripts/install.sh --dry-run exits 0
PR description references F1/F2/F4/F5/F6/F7 and links to docs/audits/phase3/by-category/commands.md and docs/audits/phase3/REMEDIATION_PLAN.md
notes:
## Summary

Six Tier-2 audit findings (F1/F2/F4/F5/F6/F7) from the Phase 3 commands/ audit. Two major commands embed full methodology inline instead of delegating to skills; a third hardcodes a plugin dependency and duplicates skill content inline. Fix: create two new skills, reshape all three commands to lean-delegation shape, add availability fallback and correct default path.

## Background / Motivation

Phase 3 audit (SHA af9c1bfc) found commands/ violating the lean-body target (<=80 lines) and the lean-delegation pattern. optimize-my-skill.md (233 lines) and optimize-my-agent.md (107 lines) carry full methodologies inline. refresh-agents-md.md hardcodes a plugin skill with no fallback and duplicates optimize-agents-md principles in two steps. Each creates a secondary maintenance surface that drifts from the canonical skill over time.

## Requirements

F1: Create src/user/.agents/skills/optimize-my-skill/SKILL.md — extract Phase 2-5 methodology and the 'What Makes Skills Effective' reference section. Required sections: Phase 1 discovery, Phase 2 quality criteria (frontmatter checks, folder structure, body content, anti-patterns), Phase 3 propose improvements, Phase 4 suggest testing, Phase 5 confirm/apply, 'What Makes Skills Effective' reference (file structure, frontmatter fields, body structure, key principles). Each Phase section >=10 lines of substantive content; 'What Makes Skills Effective' sub-sections each >=5 lines of substantive content. During extraction, normalize any '5,000 words' threshold to '500 lines' — absorbs Tier-1 F3.
Frontmatter: name=optimize-my-skill, model=sonnet, allowed-tools: Read, Write, Edit, Glob, Grep, AskUserQuestion (comma-separated, per optimize-agents-md convention). Description: SKILLS_PRIMER-compliant third-person, >=3 observable triggers, explicit negative trigger: 'Do NOT use for agent persona files or AGENTS.md configuration files'. No XML angle brackets in values; name does not contain 'claude' or 'anthropic'.

F2: Create src/user/.agents/skills/optimize-my-agent/SKILL.md — extract 5-phase optimization workflow, quality rubric, and failure checklist. Required sections: all 5 optimization phases, quality rubric with exactly the 6 AGENTS_PRIMER persona-file quality issues (titles verbatim with exact AGENTS_PRIMER casing and punctuation: 'Over-broad role'; 'No examples in description'; 'Wrong model tier'; 'Body mixes role with task'; 'Bead references in shared agents'; '`skills` lists unused skills'), 7-item failure checklist, phase 4 recommendation structure (the Missing/Weak Area / Current / Recommended Addition / Why template from the source command). Each section >=10 lines of substantive content. Scope: agent persona files (agents/*.md with frontmatter name/description/model/color/tools).
Frontmatter: name=optimize-my-agent, model=sonnet, allowed-tools: Read, Write, Edit, Glob, Grep, AskUserQuestion (comma-separated). Description: SKILLS_PRIMER-compliant third-person, >=3 observable triggers, explicit negative trigger: 'Do NOT use for SKILL.md files or AGENTS.md configuration files'. No XML angle brackets; name does not contain 'claude' or 'anthropic'.

F4: optimize-my-skill.md empty-ARGUMENTS default path. 'Has skills' predicate: directory exists AND contains at least one immediate subdirectory (depth 1) containing a SKILL.md file (case-sensitive exact; regular file or symlink to regular file). Fallback order: (1) ~/.claude/skills/ — if fails predicate, (2) .claude/skills/ — if fails predicate, (3) emit 'No skills found. Pass a path: /optimize-my-skill <path>'. Non-existent directory satisfies 'fails predicate'.

F5 (resolved via F2): Replace optimize-my-agent rubric with AGENTS_PRIMER persona-file quality issues verbatim. Delivered by F2 skill AC.

F6: refresh-agents-md.md Step 1 — add a-priori availability check: check whether the dispatching-parallel-agents skill directory exists by probing these paths in order (match any): ~/.claude/skills/dispatching-parallel-agents/SKILL.md, ~/.claude/plugins/*/skills/dispatching-parallel-agents/SKILL.md. If probe matches: run three discovery subagents simultaneously. If not: run sequentially — (1) Git Analyzer, (2) File Inventory, (3) Directory Discovery. This applies regardless of whether the obra/superpowers plugin is installed — it is NOT a cross-tool concern; the command file is Claude-specific.

F7: refresh-agents-md.md Steps 3b and 3d — remove inline principle enumeration.
- Step 3b: replace instruction block with a direct invocation reference to the optimize-agents-md skill, with explicit instruction to skip scope questions and infer context from the codebase. Must also forward focus_areas value and parent-file context gathered in Step 3a. Semantic requirement: no inline principle enumeration; skill referenced by name; scope-questions skip stated; context-handoff present.
- Step 3d: replace inline validation checklist with reference to the optimize-agents-md skill's Validation Checklist section. Semantic requirement: no inline checklist; section referenced by name.

After F1+F2 skills are created, reshape commands:
- optimize-my-skill.md: parse ARGUMENTS (target: directory or specific skill path), invoke optimize-my-skill skill, report results. Retain command-shape content only. No occurrences of '5,000 words'.
- optimize-my-agent.md: parse ARGUMENTS (target: agent persona file path), invoke optimize-my-agent skill, report results. Scope: agent persona files only.

## Out of Scope

- F3 (5,000-words threshold fix) — absorbed by F1 at extraction time; any open Tier-1 F3 bead can be closed as already-resolved after this PR lands
- F8 (optimize-my-agent.md heading mismatch) — Tier 1; the lean-delegation rewrite replaces the heading body anyway; F8 bead can be closed as already-resolved after this PR lands
- Refactoring optimize-agents-md SKILL.md — F7 references its existing Validation Checklist section as-is
- Migration of installed ~/.claude/commands/ versions — install.sh handles on next run
- Any commands not named in F1/F2/F4/F5/F6/F7

## Design Notes

- Single PR: all six findings are Tier 2, same audit, same category, thematically unified. Expected diff: ~600+ lines added (two new skills), ~400 lines removed (three commands reshaped).
- F5 scope decision: user confirmed optimize-my-agent targets agent persona files (not AGENTS.md config). New skill rubric uses AGENTS_PRIMER quality issues table titles verbatim including casing and backticks.
- F4 path preference: command installs user-wide (~/.claude/commands/) so ~/.claude/skills/ is canonical; .claude/skills/ is project-level fallback. User-level skills take precedence even when project-level skills exist; pass explicit path to target project skills.
- F4 predicate: case-sensitive 'SKILL.md'; regular file or symlink; depth-1 only.
- F6 probe: check ~/.claude/skills/dispatching-parallel-agents/SKILL.md and ~/.claude/plugins/*/skills/dispatching-parallel-agents/SKILL.md. Not a cross-tool concern — command is Claude-specific; probe accounts for varying superpowers install locations.
- F6 fallback: sequential order: Git Analyzer, File Inventory, Directory Discovery.
- F7 peer dependency: 'skip the 5 scope questions' relies on optimize-agents-md retaining its numbered five-question structure. Future edits to that skill must update refresh-agents-md.md in lockstep.
- F7 context handoff: Step 3b invocation must forward focus_areas and parent-file context; these context inputs are gathered by Step 3a and must not be silently dropped.
- F3 + F8 absorbed: F1 extraction normalizes '5,000 words' => '500 lines'; F2/F5 command rewrite replaces the heading. Both Tier-1 beads (if tracked) can be closed after this PR.
- allowed-tools: comma-separated format used throughout (matching optimize-agents-md peer); SKILLS_PRIMER documents space-separated but this codebase normalizes on comma form.

## Open Questions (post-ralf-review cycle cap, for implementer judgment)

- F2 phase 4 recommendation structure: 'phase 4 recommendation structure' means the Missing/Weak Area / Current / Recommended Addition / Why template from source command lines 36-46. AC references this template; implementer should preserve all four fields.
- F1 'What Makes Skills Effective' sub-sections: each sub-section should preserve the substantive content from the source command; headers-only is not sufficient.
- F4 edge case: empty-but-existing SKILL.md still satisfies the predicate (file presence, not content validity).
- F7 Step 3a/3b boundary: Step 3a (Gather Context) survives unchanged; only the principle-enumeration block in 3b is replaced; surrounding scaffolding that gathers context or applies focus_areas remains.
