title: Verify-AC: mechanical acceptance criteria validation
description:
Validate every [m]-tagged AC bullet against worktree state.
Model: claude-haiku-4-5  Effort: medium

Skips when review-level:none.

## Inputs
- Bead AC from: bd show {{bead-id}} (parse [m] and [h] tags)
- Worktree path from: worktree-path-* label on molecule
- Functional tests from: project-config.toml [functional-tests]

## Actions

For each [m]-tagged AC line:
1. Dispatch bead-verifier (haiku, low effort) to run shell-runnable witnesses.
   Collect: exit code + error excerpt.
2. Assemble validation report.

[h]-tagged AC lines: do NOT validate here. Confirm that human follow-up
child beads were filed at brainstorm-time finalize (bd list --parent {{bead-id}}
filtered by label human). If any [h] bullet has no matching follow-up, flag-human.

Run functional/e2e tests from [functional-tests] in project-config.toml.
If functional tests fail:
  - Create one bug bead per failure, label human.
  - Note in step-bead. Continue to create-pr (do NOT loop back).

Persist validation report to BOTH:
  - Step-bead notes: bd update <step-bead-id> --append-notes "<report>"
  - Source bead notes: bd update {{bead-id}} --append-notes "<report>"
  (Report also included in PR description at create-pr.)

## Definition of Done
Every [m] AC bullet has explicit witness evidence.
Human-review bullets have follow-up beads filed (confirm presence).

## Idempotent re-entry
Re-run re-evaluates and continues from prior pass.

title: Red-tests: write failing tests covering all [m] AC bullets
description:
Write failing tests (TDD red phase) before any implementation code.
Model: claude-sonnet-4-6  Effort: medium

## Preloaded skills
superpowers:test-driven-development, superpowers:writing-unit-tests,
superpowers:testing-anti-patterns

## Inputs
- Bead AC from: bd show {{bead-id}} (read [m]-tagged lines)
- Worktree path: decode worktree-path-* label from molecule

## Decode worktree-path label before any work
  1. Decode: __ → /  then  _u → _
  2. Verify decoded path exists and is a git worktree:
       git -C <path> rev-parse --is-inside-work-tree
  3. Operate from that cwd.

## Actions

1. Dispatch bead-implementor (sonnet, medium effort) to write tests:
   - Dispatch with: worktree path, AC bullets (all [m]-tagged lines),
     step-bead ID, instruction to write failing tests and confirm they fail.
   - Tests must cover: happy path, edge cases, error paths.
   - Do NOT mock what has not been verified.
   - Do NOT write test-only methods in production code.

2. Run the Claude reviewer (test-review skill) on the written tests.
   When review-level:none is set: skip test reviewers (tests still written).

3. Run Codex adversarial review of the tests (gpt-5.5 default).
   Model from: project-config.toml [foreign-cli].codex_red_tests_model
   When review-level:none: skip.
   Subject to foreign-eyes degradation tracking (FOREIGN-EYES-ITER-<n>).

4. Iteration cap: 2 (override: iteration-cap-red-tests-<n> label).
   At cap with reviewer disagreement: write disagreement note to step-bead notes.
   Escalate human ONLY if BOTH reviewers agree on a critical/major design flaw.

5. Commit failing tests to the feature branch.

## Definition of Done
Tests committed to feature branch; both reviewers approve "shippable" state
(or iteration cap reached with disagreement note recorded).

## Idempotent re-entry
Check filesystem (committed tests) and step-bead notes. Continue from prior state.

title: Confirm bead is brainstormed and restate the implementation plan
description:
Verify that bead {{bead-id}} has been through brainstorm-bead and that
the spec exists in the bead. NEVER invoke superpowers:brainstorming —
the bead description IS the plan.

Read bead and labels:

  bd show {{bead-id}}
  bd label list {{bead-id}}

== HAPPY PATH (both `brainstormed` AND `implementation-ready` labels present) ==

1. Read the bead description and acceptance criteria.
2. Restate the implementation plan in 1-3 sentences to prove understanding
   (e.g. "I will modify <files> to <do X> so that <AC pass>").
3. Mark this step done. The next step (worktree) runs.

DO NOT call `bd update {{bead-id}} --acceptance` or `--notes` —
brainstorm-bead already wrote the spec; rewriting it would destroy it.

== MISSING-LABEL PATH (either label absent) ==

Aborting from either branch below means: leave this `confirm` step
OPEN (do not close it), do NOT call `bd mol burn` from within this
step, and print this one-line follow-up:

  Molecule paused at confirm. Run brainstorm-bead on this bead and re-attempt, or `bd mol burn <mol-id>` to discard.

Leave the disposition (re-attempt or burn) to the operator (or to
run-queue policy).

Detect execution mode from your own dispatch context:

- AUTONOMOUS mode (you were dispatched by run-queue, or any
  context where no human is at the keyboard):
  - Escalate the bead so a human can address it:
      bd label add {{bead-id}} human
      bd update {{bead-id}} --append-notes "missing brainstormed/implementation-ready labels; recommend running brainstorm-bead before implementation."
    (NOT `bd human <id>` — that's the help command, not a label applier.)
  - Do NOT prompt — there is no operator to answer.
  - Then abort (per the shared abort mechanism above).

- INTERACTIVE mode (start-bead routed you here, or a human ran
  `bd mol pour` directly and is in conversation with you):
  - Print a clear warning: "Bead {{bead-id}} is missing brainstormed/implementation-ready labels — it has not been through brainstorm-bead. Implementation may run on an incomplete spec."
  - Ask the user:
    (a) Abort and route the bead through start-bead/brainstorm-bead first.
    (b) Proceed with implementation using whatever is in the bead now.
  - On (a): abort (per the shared abort mechanism above).
  - On (b): proceed to worktree.
close_reason: Confirmed: brainstormed + implementation-ready labels present. Plan restated.

title: Housekeeping: close, remember, file discovered work
description:
Complete the lifecycle and preserve context for future sessions.

1. Close the bead:
     bd close {{bead-id}} --reason "Implemented and PR merged"

2. Walk the parent chain; close each ancestor epic whose remaining
   children are all closed (stops at the first ancestor that still
   has any non-closed children, or when there is no parent):

     PARENT=$(bd show {{bead-id}} --json | jq -r '.[0].parent // empty')
     while [ -n "$PARENT" ]; do
       NON_CLOSED=$(bd list --parent="$PARENT" --json | jq '[.[] | select(.status != "closed")] | length')
       [ "$NON_CLOSED" = "0" ] || break
       bd close "$PARENT" --reason "All children closed"
       PARENT=$(bd show "$PARENT" --json | jq -r '.[0].parent // empty')
     done

3. Record any non-obvious decisions or hard-won insights:
     bd remember "insight about this system or pattern"
     bd decision record --title="Why we chose X over Y"

4. File any work discovered during implementation. Prefer epic-sibling
   placement over orphan-with-dep:

     PARENT=$(bd show {{bead-id}} --json | jq -r '.[0].parent // empty')

     # Decide placement via the sibling test (see rules/beads.md I3):
     # would this work have been on the parent epic's original plan, if
     # we'd thought of it? Yes → sibling. No → orphan + discovered-from.
     IS_SIBLING_SUBTASK=false  # set to true only when the answer is yes

     if [ -n "$PARENT" ] && [ "$IS_SIBLING_SUBTASK" = true ]; then
       # Create INSIDE the parent epic as a sibling:
       bd create "Found: ..." -t "<type>" -p "<prio>" --parent "$PARENT"
     else
       # Otherwise create as an orphan and link with discovered-from:
       NEW=$(bd create "Found: ..." -t "<type>" -p "<prio>" --json | jq -r '.id')
       bd dep add "$NEW" {{bead-id}} --type discovered-from
     fi

   Sibling test (see rules/beads.md I3): would this discovered work
   have been on the parent epic's original plan, if we'd thought of
   it? Yes → sibling (--parent). No → orphan + discovered-from.

5. Burn this wisp (if running as vapor):
     bd mol burn <this-mol-id>
close_reason: Housekeeping: discovered work filed as 7bk.17 and 7bk.18, key memories saved.

title: Gemini agent loader rejects shared agent frontmatter (tools/skills/color/memory)
notes:
Repro: Install user-scope content via scripts/install.sh with Gemini detected, then start gemini.

Actual: Gemini fails to load three agents with validation errors:

  ~/.gemini/agents/bead-verifier.md
    - tools: Expected array, received string
    - Unrecognized keys: 'skills', 'color'

  ~/.gemini/agents/quality-reviewer.md
    - tools: Expected array, received string
    - Unrecognized keys: 'skills', 'memory', 'color'

  ~/.gemini/agents/tech-lead.md
    - Unrecognized key: 'color'

Expected: Agents installed to ~/.gemini/agents/ load cleanly under Gemini CLI's stricter schema validator.

Root cause (suspected): Shared agent files in src/user/.agents/agents/ use Claude-flavored frontmatter. The installer copies them verbatim to ~/.gemini/agents/. Gemini's loader requires:
  - tools as a YAML array (not a comma-separated string)
  - no unrecognized keys (skills, color, memory are Claude-specific)

Fix options to consider during brainstorming:
  1. Tool-specific frontmatter transform during install (strip/convert per target)
  2. Normalize shared agent frontmatter to a lowest-common-denominator schema
  3. Maintain per-tool agent variants with shared body via partials

Affected files (initial scan target): src/user/.agents/agents/{bead-verifier,quality-reviewer,tech-lead}.md and possibly other shared agents not yet exercised by Gemini.
close_reason: Implemented Gemini agent frontmatter transformation in install.sh to strip Claude-specific keys and convert tools to YAML arrays.

title: Single-context mode for implement-bead (alternative to per-stage claude -p)
description:
ONE agent runs the entire molecule in ONE session, no per-stage `claude -p` invocations. Proposes an alternate operational model that is mutually exclusive with 7bk.9's per-stage `claude -p` architecture.

Full design (current target): `docs/specs/bead-pipeline-architecture.md` §10 (Alternative architectures).

## Why this exists as a sibling exploration (not in epic 7bk)

The agent-fleet epic (7bk) commits to specialized agents dispatched per stage, which under current Claude Code constraints requires the per-stage `claude -p` model from 7bk.9. This bead asks the orthogonal question: should we instead run a single long-lived session for the whole molecule?

Trade-offs to evaluate during brainstorm:
- Bounded vs unbounded context: single-context bounds at one session; per-stage bounds at one stage
- Subagent-spawn constraint: single-context cannot dispatch subagents from within subagents (Claude Code limit). Per-stage works around this by exiting + respawning
- Performance: single-context avoids `claude -p` cold-start tax (~5-30s × 8 stages). Per-stage costs that tax but allows parallelism across beads
- Resilience: per-stage idempotent re-entry handles crashes; single-context loses everything on crash

## Open question for brainstorm

Is this:
- (a) A fallback profile for trivial beads where per-stage overhead exceeds benefit?
- (b) An A/B alternative for performance comparison?
- (c) An eventual replacement when subagent-spawning constraints relax in Claude Code?
- (d) An obsolete idea given 7bk.9 ships first?

The relationship to 7bk.9 must be made explicit before implementation.

## Dependencies

Soft dep on agents-config-7bk via `related-to` edge — bd CLI does not allow tasks to hard-block epics. The dependency is OPERATOR-RESPECTED BY CONVENTION: brainstorm of this bead should not start until 7bk's main per-stage architecture (7bk.9) has shipped, but `bd ready` does not exclude this bead automatically.

## Acceptance Criteria

- Brainstorm produces a decision among (a)/(b)/(c)/(d) above
- If implementing: spec details, formula variant, model + effort defaults
- Build passes. Typecheck passes. Tests pass.

title: RALF-IT: hard-fail on foreign-eyes degradation; record degradation status in PR
description:
Currently RALF-IT silently degrades when foreign-eyes (Codex, Gemini) are unavailable: "foreign-agent failure → degrade to pure fresh-eyes (iteration still counts)." Result: anti-bias property silently disappears; reviewers and operators have no visibility into degradation.

## Why

The architectural justification for foreign-eyes is anti-bias: a different model with different training catches Claude's blind spots. When foreign-eyes are unavailable for multiple iterations, RALF-IT becomes a self-review loop branded as adversarial. If shipped with this property invisibly disabled, the architecture's quality claim is unfounded for those beads.

Three failure modes to handle:
- Codex auth expired or quota exhausted
- Gemini quota exhausted or service unavailable
- Both unavailable concurrently (network incident, etc.)

## What changes

1. RALF-IT skill tracks foreign-eyes invocation outcomes per iteration (success / unavailable / errored).
2. After each iteration, persist degradation state to step-bead notes:
   `FOREIGN-EYES-ITER-<n>: codex=<status>, gemini=<status>`
3. At end of RALF loop:
   - If foreign-eyes were degraded in ≥2 of N iterations → flag-human; do NOT mark green-loop complete.
   - Stamp `foreign-eyes-degraded:<n>/<N>` label on the source bead.
4. PR description includes a foreign-eyes status section:
   - For each iteration: which foreign-eye ran successfully.
   - Aggregate degradation count.
   - If degradation occurred but did not trigger flag-human (only 1 of N), note this so reviewers know.

## Fallback options (out of scope; flag for future design)

- Cursor / direct ChatGPT API as a third foreign-eye option
- Local self-hosted model fallback

## Dependencies

None. Modifies the RALF-IT skill independently of the agent-fleet epic. NOT a child of the epic.

## Acceptance Criteria

- RALF-IT skill tracks per-iteration foreign-eye outcomes in step-bead notes
- Hard-fail trigger: ≥2 of N iterations degraded → flag-human + stamp foreign-eyes-degraded:<n>/<N> label
- PR description includes foreign-eyes status section (template added to superpowers:finishing-a-development-branch flow OR injected by RALF-IT)
- Smoke test: simulate Codex unavailable for iter 1 + Gemini unavailable for iter 2; verify flag-human fires and label is stamped
- Build passes. Typecheck passes. Tests pass.

title: Per-step Model: and Effort: directives in formula TOMLs
description:
Add support for per-step Model: and Effort: override directives in formula TOML files. These get applied at bd mol pour / bd mol wisp time (or read at orchestrator dispatch time, depending on bd's actual capabilities — see Open Question).

## Why

7bk.9's per-stage claude -p model needs a way to specify which model and effort drives each stage's orchestrator. Without this, every stage falls through to the same default — losing the "right-sized models" pillar of the agent-fleet epic.

The Tier 3 cost-rightsizing bead leans on this mechanism for at least 5 cost-rightsizing recommendations: per-stage opus vs sonnet, per-stage effort:high vs effort:medium, per-iteration vs final-pass model differentiation.

## Open Question (resolve at implementation)

Investigate whether bd formula TOML parser already supports Model: / Effort: directives in step bodies, OR whether bd needs a parser extension. If extension is needed, that becomes a bd-side dependency.

Likely resolution paths:
- (a) bd already supports them as free-form keys in step body, just need a convention.
- (b) bd needs a small parser change to recognize them as structured fields.
- (c) Apply at pour-time via post-processing: pour reads formula step body, extracts directives, stamps step-bead with model-<id> / effort-<level> labels.

## Behavior

- Formula step body specifies Model: <model-id> and Effort: <level> directives.
- bd mol pour / wisp processes them per the chosen mechanism above.
- Driver reads next-ready step-bead's labels (or notes, depending on mechanism) and spawns claude -p with the appropriate model and effort.
- No directive in formula step → agent's own frontmatter defaults apply.
- No source-bead-level override mechanism in initial scope. If a particular bead truly needs a non-formula model/effort, that is a manual edit / one-off concern — do not build vocabulary for it now.

## Out of Scope

- Per-source-bead override labels (no model-override-* / effort-override-* labels at this stage; formula-only)
- Model/effort proposals at brainstorm-time (classify-and-policy step does NOT include model/effort knobs)

## Dependencies

- agents-config-7bk.9 (the per-stage claude -p model that consumes these directives)

## Acceptance Criteria

- bd formula DSL supports per-step Model: and Effort: directives (or post-processing path documented)
- implement-feature.formula.toml + fix-bug.formula.toml updated with directives per Tier 3 recommendations (when Tier 3 lands)
- Smoke test: pour a formula with directives; verify step-beads carry the expected sizing; verify driver dispatches with the specified model/effort
- Build passes. Typecheck passes. Tests pass.

Run the tests and confirm they FAIL. Failure is correct here.

  go test ./...           # Go projects
  pnpm test               # Node/TypeScript projects
  pytest                  # Python projects

If a test passes before any implementation, either the test is wrong
or the feature already exists. Investigate before continuing.

title: implement-feature
description: TDD feature implementation: confirm → worktree → red/green/refactor → completion gate → PR → close

title: Implement install.sh: add --prune flag to remove orphaned user-space items not in template (green phase)
description:
Write the minimum code to make all failing tests pass.

Skills: superpowers:test-driven-development, then domain skill
  (e.g. typescript-developer, backend-developer, etc.)

Rules:
- Minimum viable implementation — write only what tests require
- No speculative features, no over-engineering
- No abstractions for hypothetical future requirements

Run tests after each meaningful change:
  go test ./...   (or equivalent)

This step is complete only when all tests are GREEN.

title: Completion gate: verify with evidence
description:
Run the full verification suite. Evidence is required — no claims without output.

Skill: superpowers:verification-before-completion

Required gates — show the actual command output for each:
  [ ] Tests pass        → run test suite, paste output
  [ ] Build succeeds    → run build, paste output
  [ ] Lint clean        → run linter, paste output
  [ ] Type check clean  → run type checker, paste output

Do NOT mark this step complete unless all four gates show clean output.
"I believe it passes" is not evidence. Run the commands.

HARD STOP: After this gate, do NOT commit to main and do NOT push
directly. The next step is create-pr. Go there next.

Tests/build/typecheck N/A per spec AC#23 (no infra in shell project).

title: Await PR review and triage feedback
description:
Invoke the wait-for-pr-comments skill.

Skill: superpowers:wait-for-pr-comments

- Wait for Copilot review to complete (or time out)
- Check BOTH top-level PR comments AND inline review comments
  (inline comments are easy to miss — always verify both)
- Auto-fix unambiguous feedback

Do NOT merge or clean up the worktree until review is complete

title: Create pull request
description:
Invoke the finishing-a-development-branch skill.

Skill: superpowers:finishing-a-development-branch

- Push the branch to origin
- Create a PR with a clear summary linked to {{bead-id}}
- PR title should be ≤70 characters
- PR body should include: what changed, why, and how to test it

Do NOT merge. Do NOT squash. Await review in the next step.

title: Housekeeping: close, remember, file discovered work
description:
Complete the lifecycle and preserve context for future sessions.

1. Close the bead:
     bd close {{bead-id}} --reason "Implemented and PR merged"

2. Walk the parent chain; close each ancestor epic whose remaining
   children are all closed (stops at the first ancestor that still
   has any non-closed children, or when there is no parent):

     PARENT=$(bd show {{bead-id}} --json | jq -r '.[0].parent // empty')
     while [ -n "$PARENT" ]; do
       NON_CLOSED=$(bd list --parent="$PARENT" --json | jq '[.[] | select(.status != "closed")] | length')
       [ "$NON_CLOSED" = "0" ] || break
       bd close "$PARENT" --reason "All children closed"
       PARENT=$(bd show "$PARENT" --json | jq -r '.[0].parent // empty')
     done

3. Record any non-obvious decisions or hard-won insights:
     bd remember "insight about this system or pattern"
     bd decision record --title="Why we chose X over Y"

4. File any work discovered during implementation. Prefer epic-sibling
   placement over orphan-with-dep:

     PARENT=$(bd show {{bead-id}} --json | jq -r '.[0].parent // empty')

     # Decide placement via the sibling test (see rules/beads.md I3):
     # would this work have been on the parent epic's original plan, if
     # we'd thought of it? Yes → sibling. No → orphan + discovered-from.
     IS_SIBLING_SUBTASK=false  # set to true only when the answer is yes

     if [ -n "$PARENT" ] && [ "$IS_SIBLING_SUBTASK" = true ]; then
       # Create INSIDE the parent epic as a sibling:
       bd create "Found: ..." -t "<type>" -p "<prio>" --parent "$PARENT"
     else
       # Otherwise create as an orphan and link with discovered-from:
       NEW=$(bd create "Found: ..." -t "<type>" -p "<prio>" --json | jq -r '.id')
       bd dep add "$NEW" {{bead-id}} --type discovered-from
     fi

   Sibling test (see rules/beads.md I3): would this discovered work
   have been on the parent epic's original plan, if we'd thought of
   it? Yes → sibling (--parent). No → orphan + discovered-from.

5. Burn this wisp (if running as vapor):
     bd mol burn <this-mol-id>

Agent: code-simplifier:code-simplifier

This step exists because the implementing agent always misses
at least one DRY violation or clarity issue. It is not optional.

Address ALL findings. Re-run tests after any changes made here.

title: Completion gate: code review

Dispatch code-reviewer subagent against ALL changed files.

Scope: every file changed since branch creation, reviewed against:

Address ALL findings before proceeding. If a finding is deferred,
document the rationale explicitly.

For high-stakes changes (security-sensitive, architecture shifts):
  /codex:adversarial-review --wait --model gpt-5.4

title: Set up isolated git worktree
description:
Invoke the using-git-worktrees skill to create an isolated branch.

Skill: superpowers:using-git-worktrees

- Branch from main (never from another feature branch)
- Verify you are IN the worktree directory before writing any code
- Confirm no leftover state from other work

Then claim the bead and walk the parent chain:

  bd update {{bead-id}} --status in_progress

  # Walk parent chain; mark each ancestor epic in_progress
  PARENT=$(bd show {{bead-id}} --json | jq -r '.[0].parent // empty')
  while [ -n "$PARENT" ]; do
    bd update "$PARENT" --status in_progress
    PARENT=$(bd show "$PARENT" --json | jq -r '.[0].parent // empty')
  done

If `implement-bead` already claimed the bead before pouring this
formula, the first `bd update` is a no-op — re-running it is safe.

title: install.sh: add --prune flag to remove orphaned user-space items not in template
acceptance_criteria:
1. install.sh --prune --dry-run prints orphan list and exits 0 with no deletes/backups
2. install.sh --prune-only --dry-run does the same WITHOUT running install pass (Phase 7 skipped); Phases 1-6 still build staging
3. install.sh --prune -y runs install, then backs up + deletes all orphans without prompting; exits 0
4. install.sh --prune-only -y skips install (Phase 7), backs up + deletes all orphans; exits 0
5. install.sh --prune (interactive) shows orphan list, then prompts [a]ll/[o]ne-by-one/[c]ancel; Enter=cancel, EOF=cancel, invalid input=re-prompt; cancel after install pass exits 0
6. install.sh --prune --prune-only exits non-zero with explicit 'mutually exclusive' error
7. install.sh --prune non-interactive no -y/--dry-run: warn 'prune phase requires confirmation, skipping' + skip prune; install pass result drives exit code
8. install.sh --prune-only non-interactive no -y/--dry-run: hard fail with warning, exit non-zero (user intent unfulfilled)
9. Orphan = top-level item DIRECTLY inside ~/.<tool>/{commands,skills,agents,rules}/ or ~/.beads/formulas/ that is not present in current run staging; nothing at ~/.<tool>/ top level is ever pruned (incl. unknown subdirs like hooks/)
10. Subdir orphan-detection scope is exactly: {commands, skills, agents, rules, formulas} — no other dirs scanned even if they exist in dest
11. Exclusion rule: skip any subdir matching *-backup at the namespace-parent level (e.g., ~/.<tool>/commands-backup/); exclusion is NOT recursive into deeper paths
12. Backup destination = ~/.<tool>/<namespace>-backup/<basename>.backup-<YYYYMMDD-HHMMSS> for both files and directories; mkdir -p lazily on first backup
13. backup() refactored to one function, path-aware: detects parent dir name; if in {commands,skills,agents,rules,formulas} → routes to <grandparent>/<namespace>-backup/; otherwise → in-place. Handles files (cp) and dirs (cp -R)
14. Existing call sites: sync_templates and sync_settings_file backup top-level files only → behavior UNCHANGED. stage_and_install_beads backs up files in formulas/ namespace → behavior CHANGES (in-place → ~/.beads/formulas-backup/). Documented in commit message.
15. Per-tool prune counters: new tool_pruned[$tool] array (incl. tool_pruned[beads]); verbose summary gains 'Pruned: N' line; quiet summary parts list gains 'N pruned' entry
16. With --plugins= excluding plugins, plugin-installed user-space items ARE identified as orphans under --prune (strict mode); existing line-260 warning text updated to mention --prune behavior
17. Existing sync_directory orphan-warning at line 775 is suppressed when --prune or --prune-only is active (no duplicate noise)
18. Prune respects --tools= filter and auto-detection identically to install pass; tools not in active TOOLS set are NOT scanned even if their dirs exist in ~/
19. ~/.beads/formulas/ is scanned for orphans whenever --prune/--prune-only runs, regardless of beads plugin auto-detection state; staging-comparison applies — if beads not in PLUGINS, formulas staging is empty → all dest formulas are orphans (consistent with strict mode)
20. ~/.<tool>/*.md, settings.json, settings.local.json, top-level *.toml are NEVER pruned
21. Existing install behavior unchanged when --prune and --prune-only are absent
22. Documentation (README.md, AGENTS.md, CONTRIBUTING.md) updated to describe new flags
23. Build passes. Typecheck passes. Tests pass. (No build/typecheck/tests exist for shell script; manual verification of AC scenarios is the bar.)
notes:
Add an optional switch to install.sh to identify and optionally remove commands, skills, and agents in user space that are not part of this project's template.

Behavior:
- In dry-run mode: show all such 'orphaned' targets that would be deleted
- In regular run: report all orphans first, then prompt whether to delete all at once, or confirm one-by-one
- With -y flag: delete all orphans without prompting

Scope: applies to all detected tools (Claude, Codex, Gemini) and their respective install locations (~/.claude/, ~/.codex/, ~/.gemini/).

This helps keep the user's installed config in sync with the template by pruning items that no longer exist in the source project.

title: wait-for-pr-comments: tune Phase 4 timing windows from real-world Copilot data
description:
Empirical timing data gathered from 13 PRs in scotthamilton77/claude-code-sidekick (PRs #160, #168, #173–#183) covering 18 copilot_work_started events. Repo has auto-Copilot-review + auto-rereview-on-push enabled at the org/repo level — every review_requested event is server-generated within seconds of a push.

KEY TIMINGS (push → review_requested → copilot_work_started):

| Phase                                  | push→request | request→work | push→work    |
|----------------------------------------|--------------|--------------|--------------|
| Initial review (1st on PR)             | 34–164s      | 22–39s       | 24–193s      |
| Re-review after prior COMMENTED review | 2–7s         | 22–37s       | 24–44s       |
| Long quiescent / draft resumption      | up to 10hrs  | ~30s         | up to 10hrs  |

Follow-up case (what Phase 4 cares about): tight cluster, 24–44s total push→work_started.

Iteration-depth observations:
- 2nd review on a PR: 4 samples, push→request = 2, 3, 3, 7s
- 3rd review on a PR: 1 sample (PR #183), push→request = 3s
- 4th review on a PR: 0 samples in 6-week dataset — auto-rereview stops after some N. Mechanism unknown (hypotheses: hard iteration cap, diff-similarity skip, rate limit). Out of scope.

PROPOSED FIXES TO wait-for-pr-comments:

1. Phase 4 (poll-copilot-rereview-start.sh) currently polls 3 × 10s = 30s window. Real upper bound for follow-up push→work is ~44s. The 30s window is on the edge. Extend to 60s minimum (6 × 10s polls).

2. Add an explicit floor sleep before the first poll. Polling 1s after push will always miss; lower bound is push+24s. Recommend a 20s pre-sleep.

3. Proactively detect repo-level auto-rereview is configured (gh api repos/{owner}/{repo}) and adjust report wording — users don't need to manually re-request. The unknown 'sometimes stops' condition is out of scope; the script should still correctly report 'no_rereview_started' rather than a stale-cache false 'review found'.

4. Combined with sidekick-aga3 (stale poll bug), holistic fix for poll-copilot-review.sh in --skip-request-check mode: filter reviews by submitted_at > last_commit_pushed_at (or > work_started_at if known). Polling loop continues up to the 10-minute budget until a fresh review lands or budget exhausts — never returns a cached review as 'fresh found'.

Acceptance: Build passes. Typecheck passes. Tests pass. PR #183 today (4th push, no auto-rereview) should produce 'no_rereview_started' in the correct 60s window, not a false 'review found' off stale cache.

title: Digest: brainstorm-bead
description: Brainstorm session for agents-config-7bk.2 expanded into a larger architectural redesign. Key findings: (1) implement-bead's 'every step dispatches a subagent' model is structurally broken because subagents cannot spawn subagents; (2) the run-queue skill creates unbounded context growth; (3) the simplify step was structurally impossible because /simplify is a slash command and subagents can't fire them. Outcome: pivot to per-stage claude -p orchestration model captured in agents-config-7bk.9, which absorbs and expands 7bk.2's original scope. Discussion thread in session 5385d9fc-40dd-499c-8e57-959cf1192160.

title: Expand brainstorm-bead scope: AC mechanical/human classification, brainstorm-time knobs (auto-mergeable, review-requirements), human-only follow-up beads
description:
Expand brainstorm-bead so the brainstorm phase captures policy decisions: which acceptance criteria are machine-verifiable vs require human judgment, whether the bead should auto-merge, and what depth of code review applies. Wire downstream formulas (merge-and-cleanup, implement-feature, fix-bug) to honor those decisions.

Full design: `docs/specs/bead-pipeline-architecture.md` §4 (Brainstorm-bead expansion), §4.1 (AC classification), §4.2 (Policy knob labels), §4.3 (Human follow-up beads + two-gate model + idempotent finalize).

## Scope

### R1 — AC classification

- AC lines are tagged `[m]` (mechanical) or `[h]` (human)
- Canonical line parser regex + fallback: see architecture doc §4.1
- bead-spec personas (per 7bk.3) draft AC tags during write-spec; user reviews and may override

### R2 — Policy knob labels

- `auto-mergeable` — presence label; when set, merge fires automatically once both gates clear (PR-state clean-check + source-bead closure gate-step). See architecture doc §4.3.
- `review-level:none|light|standard|deep` — gates downstream review depth per the skip matrix in architecture doc §4.2.

Reader contract for label vocabulary (collision rules, parameterized prefixes): see architecture doc §4.2.

### R3 — Human follow-up beads + merge-gate child

For each `[h]` AC line, the `finalize` step spawns a child bead per architecture doc §4.3:
- `--parent <source-bead-id>`, `--type task`, label `human`, NOT labeled `implementation-ready`
- Description carries the canonical close-without-verify warning text
- A human closing this bead WITHOUT applying the `verified-by-human` label does NOT satisfy the merge gate (per architecture doc §4.3 + R5 below).

Additionally, `finalize` files a single `merge-{source-id}` child (architecture doc §4.3):
- `--parent <source-bead-id>`, `--type task`, `--title "[Merge gate] <source-title>"`
- Description: "Closes when the merge-and-cleanup formula's merge step completes."
- Label `merge-gate` (so the gate-step can identify it)
- NOT labeled `human` (system-managed, not human-attention)
- NOT labeled `implementation-ready`

The merge-{source-id} child closes as the FINAL action of merge-and-cleanup's merge step. Its presence ensures the source bead's I2 close-walk does not fire prematurely on the auto-merge path.

### R4 — brainstorm-bead.formula.toml changes

- `write-spec` step: instruct agent to draft AC with `[m]/[h]` tags
- NEW `classify-and-policy` step inserted between `write-spec` and `ralf-spec-review`. Re-reads AC tags, proposes auto-mergeable + review-level, presents recommendations together for single user confirmation cycle, stamps agreed labels (with prior-label removal for mutually-exclusive sets like `review-level:*`). Per architecture doc §4.2 combination warnings, the step also WARNS and requires explicit user override when:
  - `review-level:none` AND `auto-mergeable` AND zero `[h]` AC (no verification gate of any kind)
  - `review-level:none` on a bead whose title or description mentions auth/token/password/permission/secret keywords
  - `auto-mergeable` on a bead with any `[h]` AC line (the auto-merge bypass cannot fire if `[h]` children block the gate; combination is logically inconsistent)
- `ralf-spec-review` scope widened: heuristically reviews classification + knob choices for plausibility
- `finalize` step is **idempotent** per architecture doc §4.3:
  - Lists current open follow-up children of source bead (filter by `human` label + parent)
  - For each `[h]` AC line: create matching child if absent; leave existing alone
  - For each existing follow-up whose AC line is no longer `[h]` in current spec: close with reason "AC re-classified to [m] or removed; follow-up no longer needed"
  - Creates `merge-{source-id}` child if absent
  - Re-running with unchanged AC is a no-op
  - Stamps `implementation-ready` label only after follow-ups are reconciled

### R5 — Consumer formula changes

- `merge-and-cleanup.formula.toml`:
  - MUST be `phase = "liquid"` (vapor cannot persist across the [h] verification window)
  - Gate-step blocks until source-bead closes AND every `[h]` follow-up child carries the `verified-by-human` label. A `closed` follow-up without `verified-by-human` does NOT satisfy the gate; gate-step flags-human with a note about the missing label.
  - PR-state clean-check (Copilot threads zero, human threads zero, CI green, 5-minute quiet window) — gate 1 of the two-gate model
  - merge step closes the `merge-{source-id}` child as its FINAL action (ensures I2 close-walk on source fires correctly)
- `implement-feature.formula.toml` — gate steps (code-review, simplify, verify, adversarial-codex) self-skip per the source bead's `review-level:*` label. Skip matrix: see architecture doc §4.2.
- `fix-bug.formula.toml` — same `review-level:*` self-skip pattern applied to its review/simplify/verify gate steps.

### R6 — Documentation updates

- `src/plugins/beads/.claude/rules/beads.md`: extend the Bead Lifecycle and Labels table with `auto-mergeable`, `review-level:*`, `verified-by-human`, and `merge-gate` rows; note that [h]-spawned human follow-ups are children of the source bead and participate in the I2 close walk; cross-reference architecture doc §4.1 for canonical AC parser
- `src/plugins/beads/.claude/rules/delivery.md`: note the auto-mergeable two-gate semantics + gate-step mechanism that blocks merge on open [h] follow-up children OR follow-ups missing `verified-by-human`

## Out of Scope

- Beads with ONLY [h] AC (spec-only, no implementation)
- AC tag aliases (`[Mechanical]`, `[H]`, etc.) — strict regex only
- Migration of existing in-flight beads — defaults handle them transparently
- Shared AC-parsing library — each consumer inlines the canonical pattern
- New formula primitive for conditional steps — pattern (c), step self-skips by reading source bead labels, is sufficient
- Recursive human follow-ups — they are leaf tasks

## Dependencies

- 7bk.3 — bead-spec personas (provides the agent that drafts AC tags). Velocity dep, not correctness; could land first if 7bk.3 stalls.

## Acceptance Criteria

- [m] `brainstorm-bead.formula.toml` contains new classify-and-policy step inserted between write-spec and ralf-spec-review with `Agent: bead-assessor` (or bead-specwriter) dispatch directive
- [m] `classify-and-policy` step warns on dangerous combinations (review-level:none + auto-mergeable + zero [h]; review-level:none on security-keyword beads; auto-mergeable + [h] AC) and requires explicit user override
- [m] `brainstorm-bead.formula.toml` write-spec step instructs agent to tag AC with `[m]/[h]`, defaults untagged lines to `[m]`, and documents the canonical regex parser
- [m] `brainstorm-bead.formula.toml` finalize step is idempotent: re-runs reconcile follow-up children to current AC (create new, close obsolete, leave matching alone)
- [m] `finalize` step spawns one child bead per `[h]` AC line (parent=source, label=human, type=task, NOT labeled `implementation-ready`)
- [m] `finalize` step files a `merge-{source-id}` child (parent=source, label=merge-gate, NOT labeled human, NOT labeled implementation-ready)
- [m] `merge-and-cleanup.formula.toml` is `phase = "liquid"` (persists across [h] verification window)
- [m] `merge-and-cleanup.formula.toml` gate-step blocks on source bead closure AND every [h] child carries `verified-by-human` label
- [m] `merge-and-cleanup.formula.toml` adds PR-state check-cleanliness step (zero open Copilot threads, zero open human threads, CI green, 5-minute quiet window)
- [m] `merge-and-cleanup.formula.toml` merge step closes merge-{source-id} child as its FINAL action
- [m] `implement-feature.formula.toml` gate steps self-skip per source bead `review-level:*` per architecture doc §4.2 skip matrix
- [m] `fix-bug.formula.toml` applies same `review-level:*` self-skip pattern
- [m] `src/plugins/beads/.claude/rules/beads.md` label table extended with `auto-mergeable`, `review-level:*`, `verified-by-human`, `merge-gate` rows
- [m] `src/plugins/beads/.claude/rules/delivery.md` describes auto-mergeable two-gate semantics + verified-by-human requirement
- [m] Build passes
- [h] Smoke test 1: brainstorm fresh bead with mixed `[m]/[h]` AC; confirm `[h]` lines spawn child beads with parent=source, label=human, no `implementation-ready` label; confirm merge-{source-id} child created
- [h] Smoke test 2: confirm `merge-and-cleanup` halts (gate-step blocks) when at least one `[h]` follow-up child is open OR closed without `verified-by-human`; closing all children with `verified-by-human` clears the gate
- [h] Smoke test 3: confirm `auto-mergeable` label bypasses explicit human auth gate ONLY when both gates clear; on timeout or dirty PR, human auth still required
- [h] Smoke test 4: confirm `review-level:*` labels (none, light, standard, deep) correctly route through implement-feature per the skip matrix
- [h] Smoke test 5: confirm finalize idempotency — re-run brainstorm-bead with [h] AC line re-classified to [m]; confirm corresponding follow-up child closes automatically
- [h] Smoke test 6: confirm classify-and-policy warns on dangerous combinations and requires user override
acceptance_criteria:
[m] Build passes
[m] brainstorm-bead.formula.toml contains new classify-and-policy step inserted between write-spec and ralf-spec-review with Agent: bead-spec dispatch directive
[m] brainstorm-bead.formula.toml write-spec step instructs agent to tag AC with [m]/[h], defaults untagged lines to [m], and documents the canonical regex parser
[m] brainstorm-bead.formula.toml classify-and-policy step proposes auto-mergeable and review-level:<value> with rationale, then prompts user to confirm or override
[m] brainstorm-bead.formula.toml finalize step spawns one child bead per [h] AC line with --parent <source>, label=human, type=task, NOT labeled implementation-ready, with description carrying the close-without-verify warning
[m] merge-and-cleanup.formula.toml adds a gate step that blocks on source bead closure (cleared via bd mol ready --gated when [h] follow-up children close)
[m] merge-and-cleanup.formula.toml adds a check-cleanliness step that evaluates the R2 clean definition (zero open Copilot threads, zero open human threads, CI green, 5-minute quiet window)
[m] merge-and-cleanup.formula.toml respects auto-mergeable label, skipping the human merge-auth gate when check-cleanliness returns clean; requires human auth on timeout or dirty; re-checks immediately before merge
[m] implement-feature.formula.toml gate steps (code-review, simplify, verify, adversarial-codex) self-skip based on source bead review-level:* label per the R5 skip matrix
[m] fix-bug.formula.toml applies the same review-level:* self-skip pattern to its review/simplify/verify gate steps
[m] src/plugins/beads/.claude/rules/beads.md label table extended with auto-mergeable and review-level:* rows; note added that [h] follow-ups are source-bead children participating in the I2 close walk; cross-reference to canonical AC parser
[m] src/plugins/beads/.claude/rules/delivery.md describes auto-mergeable short-circuit with operational clean definition and the gate-step blocking-on-open-children mechanism
[h] Smoke test 1: brainstorm a fresh bead with mixed [m]/[h] AC and confirm the [h] lines spawn child beads with parent=source, label=human, no implementation-ready label
[h] Smoke test 2: confirm merge-and-cleanup halts (gate step blocks) when at least one [h] follow-up child is open; closing all children clears the gate and bd mol ready --gated surfaces the molecule for resume
[h] Smoke test 3: confirm auto-mergeable label bypasses the explicit human auth gate ONLY when check-cleanliness returns clean; on timeout or dirty PR, human auth is still required
[h] Smoke test 4: confirm review-level:* labels (none, light, standard, deep) correctly route through implement-feature: each gate step self-skips per the R5 matrix

title: Production shell driver for autonomous bead processing (replaces run-queue skill)
description:
Production shell driver for autonomous bead processing — replaces the run-queue skill once functional parity is reached.

Depends on 7bk.9 (per-stage claude -p architecture).

## Why

The current run-queue skill polls bd for implementation-ready beads in a single Claude session. Two problems: (1) unbounded context growth across many beads in one session; (2) it dispatches implement-bead, which under the per-stage redesign (7bk.9) needs to be replaced by per-stage claude -p invocations.

A shell driver outside Claude Code can poll bd, spawn `claude -p` per ready stage, respect the molecule DAG, and recycle resources cleanly without unbounded context growth.

## Brainstorm-time questions to resolve

- **Concurrency model:** single driver instance vs. multiple? Per-bead vs. per-stage parallelism? Cap on concurrent claude -p processes?
- **Foreign-CLI rate limits:** if multiple green-loop stages run concurrently, Codex/Gemini rate limits apply. Per-driver concurrency limit on foreign-eyes calls?
- **Worktree contention:** all worktrees go to .claude/worktrees/. Git lock contention on shared .git/ at scale?
- **Driver lifecycle:** cron, daemon, on-demand invocation? Crash recovery semantics? Stuck-detection (stage open >N hours)?
- **bd database access:** concurrent updates to .beads/*.db from multiple driver instances. Locking? Single-writer enforced?
- **Mid-pipeline main-branch drift:** when bead A merges while bead B is mid green-loop, B's worktree is behind main. Rebase protocol?
- **`claude -p` start failure handling:** API down / quota / auth — exponential backoff per-bead-per-stage? When does the driver give up and flag-human?
- **Removal of run-queue skill:** at what point does this driver replace user-facing run-queue functionality? Coordinate with 7bk.9.

## Acceptance Criteria (placeholder — refine at brainstorm)

- Driver runs continuously, polls bd, spawns claude -p per ready stage per molecule
- Concurrency model documented + enforced
- Crash recovery semantics defined
- run-queue skill removed once driver achieves functional parity
- Build passes. Typecheck passes. Tests pass.

comments.author: Scott Hamilton
comments.text: Project-level deferral: all run-queue work is on hold until this redesign begins. Agents working in unrelated areas should NOT spend cycles on run-queue patching, hardening, or limitations triage. Incidental findings worth recording (e.g. "run-queue would mis-handle scenario X") should be appended here as a comment, then dropped. See AGENTS.md "Deferred Topics" section for the full protocol. Recent example: the round-5 review on PR #72 surfaced that run-queue bypasses the whats-next Rule B structural filter (polls `bd ready --label implementation-ready` directly), so leftover readiness labels on container sources CAN cause silent re-claim — this is a known limitation that will be addressed by this redesign rather than patched in run-queue today.

title: Single-context mode for implement-bead (one agent runs entire molecule in one session, no per-stage claude -p)
close_reason: Superseded by agents-config-76r; relocated outside epic per S1 disposition (2026-05-03). Original 7bk.10 had no description/notes content; full spec now lives in 76r.

title: Per-step claude -p orchestration: redesign bead pipeline (replace run-queue skill, rewrite implement-bead, define 7-stage feature workflow)
description:
Implement the per-stage `claude -p` execution model defined in `docs/specs/bead-pipeline-architecture.md` (canonical target architecture). Replaces the run-queue skill's single-session polling model and the prior implement-bead-as-molecule-orchestrator model.

## Scope (work owned by THIS bead)

1. Rewrite `implement-bead` skill — execute ONE stage of a molecule and exit; not "drive the entire molecule." Per-stage orchestrator behavior. Reads the appropriate stage step-bead, dispatches subagents/skills/slash commands per the stage's spec, persists state-out, exits.

2. Add `/implement-bead` slash command wrapper at `src/plugins/beads/.claude/commands/implement-bead.md` — thin wrapper taking `$ARGUMENTS` (bead-id) and invoking the implement-bead skill. Both invocation paths supported: interactive Claude sessions reach the skill via discovery; the shell driver's `claude -p` reaches it deterministically via the slash command.

3. Rewrite `start-bead` skill — Route A's hand-off destination changes to the shell driver (was: run-queue). Routing logic otherwise preserved. Route D (resolve-human-bead) is added by 7bk.13.

4. Add minimal test shell driver at `scripts/bead-driver-test.sh` — `bd ready --label implementation-ready --json` query loop + spawn `claude -p` per ready stage. Mark `run-queue` skill DEPRECATED but do NOT delete it; deletion happens in 7bk.11 (production driver) once it achieves functional parity.

5. Update `implement-feature.formula.toml` — restructure step list to match the architecture's 8 role-named stages (`preflight`, `red-tests`, `green-loop`, `quality-sweep`, `verify-ac`, `create-pr`, `review-cycle`, `merge-or-handoff`). Each step body becomes "orchestrator-tier instructions for one stage's claude -p" rather than wrapped-subagent instructions.

6. Update `fix-bug.formula.toml` — same 8-stage structure plus a `diagnose` stage at position 2 (between `preflight` and `red-tests`). The diagnose stage runs `superpowers:systematic-debugging` + `superpowers:root-cause-tracing` and produces a root-cause note before any tests are written.

7. Create `bead-implementor.md` agent at `src/plugins/beads/.agents/agents/`. Specs: see architecture doc §6 (Agent catalog).

8. Document worktree-path label lifecycle in `implement-bead/SKILL.md` — orchestrator owns worktree creation in `preflight` stage and stamps the `worktree-path-*` label per the encoding spec in architecture doc §5.3. Subsequent stages read and inject.

9. Document `project-config.toml` schema per architecture doc §5.1; provide an example file at the repo root.

10. End-to-end smoke test against scratch fixture project — `scripts/smoke/setup.sh` creates fresh `/tmp/bead-pipeline-smoke-<timestamp>/`, initializes with `bd init`, creates trivial source + test, populates `project-config.toml`. `scripts/bead-driver-test.sh` processes a seed bead through all stages. Smoke acceptance: all stages execute and exit cleanly, molecule completes, scratch project's PR materializes; the agents-config bd database remains untouched.

11. Slash-command discovery from worktree subdirectories verified by smoke test (Claude Code's `.claude/` walk-up resolves to the worktree's checked-in commands).

## Out of scope (covered elsewhere)

- Production driver (cron, daemon, concurrency): 7bk.11
- Single-context mode alternative: 76r
- Brainstorm-bead policy capture (AC classification, knobs): 7bk.12
- Per-step Model:/Effort: directives mechanism: 7bk.14
- Per-stage default model/effort table: 7bk.15
- A/B testing methodology: 7bk.16
- RALF-IT foreign-eyes hard-fail: z7a
- Decomposed bead-spec personas + brainstorm formula rewiring: 7bk.3
- Skill-folklore sweep (`bd human <id>`): gcg

## Acceptance Criteria

- All scope items 1-11 implemented per architecture doc
- End-to-end smoke test passes in isolated `/tmp/` scratch project
- Stage idempotent re-entry verified for green-loop (kill mid-iteration, re-spawn, resume from `RALF-IT-ITER` marker)
- Pinned UUIDv5 namespace `27ece4fd-4a06-49bf-a921-bf07ecb0dc10` used for session-id derivation
- Build passes. Typecheck passes. Tests pass.

## Dependencies

- ✓ 7bk.1 (Phase 1 — bead-verifier, quality-reviewer delivered)
- ✓ 1ck (closed: *-developer agents removed)
- ✓ 7bk.2 (closed: superseded by this bead)
- (peer) 7bk.7 — code-simplifier agent deletion
- (peer) 7bk.8 — sweep stale dispatches
- (peer) 7bk.12 — brainstorm-bead expansion
- (downstream) 76r — single-context mode alternative
- (downstream) 7bk.11 — production shell driver
acceptance_criteria: See bead description's Acceptance Criteria section (authoritative). Summary: implement-bead skill drives ONE stage per invocation; /implement-bead slash-command wrapper added; start-bead Route A hands off to shell driver; run-queue removed and replaced by scripts/bead-driver-test.sh; implement-feature.formula.toml and fix-bug.formula.toml use role-named step IDs (preflight/red-tests/green-loop/quality-sweep/verify-ac/create-pr/review-cycle/merge-or-handoff); bead-implementor agent exists; project-config.toml schema documented with example; worktree-path encoding is bijective with documented charset; per-stage claude -p contract pinned (UUIDv5 namespace 27ece4fd-4a06-49bf-a921-bf07ecb0dc10, cwd-per-stage); preflight selects formula via formula-<name> label with per-type defaults; brainstorm-time knob vocabulary defined and honored (role-named labels); merge-or-handoff hand-off uses status open + merge-ready + human (I1 exception documented); smoke test runs in isolated /tmp/ scratch project with bd init; green-loop idempotent re-entry verified. Build passes. Typecheck passes. Tests pass.
close_reason: Per-stage claude -p pipeline redesign complete. PR #28 open and reviewed. All 11 scope items implemented, 8 Copilot comments addressed.

title: Completion gate: code review
description:
Dispatch code-reviewer subagent against ALL changed files.

Agent: superpowers:code-reviewer
Scope: every file changed since branch creation, reviewed against:
  - The implementation plan restated in the confirm step (per the bead spec)
  - SOLID and DRY principles
  - Security by design (OWASP top 10, input validation, no injection paths)
  - Architectural consistency with the rest of the codebase
  - The standards in INSTRUCTIONS.md

Address ALL findings before proceeding. If a finding is deferred,
document the rationale explicitly.

For high-stakes changes (security-sensitive, architecture shifts):
  /codex:adversarial-review --wait --model gpt-5.4
close_reason: PASS — all 9 ACs satisfied, no blockers, no architectural drift, no collisions. Two nits deferred (inline+bulleted enumeration is belt-and-suspenders but defensible; Claude 'Agent' wording matches spec verbatim though current Claude Code tool is 'Task' — spec wording wins)

title: Confirm bead is brainstormed and restate the implementation plan
description:
Verify that bead {{bead-id}} has been through brainstorm-bead and that
the spec exists in the bead. NEVER invoke superpowers:brainstorming —
the bead description IS the plan.

Read bead and labels:

  bd show {{bead-id}}
  bd label list {{bead-id}}

== HAPPY PATH (both `brainstormed` AND `implementation-ready` labels present) ==

1. Read the bead description and acceptance criteria.
2. Restate the implementation plan in 1-3 sentences to prove understanding
   (e.g. "I will modify <files> to <do X> so that <AC pass>").
3. Mark this step done. The next step (worktree) runs.

DO NOT call `bd update {{bead-id}} --acceptance` or `--notes` —
brainstorm-bead already wrote the spec; rewriting it would destroy it.

== MISSING-LABEL PATH (either label absent) ==

Aborting from either branch below means: leave this `confirm` step
OPEN (do not close it), do NOT call `bd mol burn` from within this
step, and print this one-line follow-up:

  Molecule paused at confirm. Run brainstorm-bead on this bead and re-attempt, or `bd mol burn <mol-id>` to discard.

Leave the disposition (re-attempt or burn) to the operator (or to
run-queue policy).

Detect execution mode from your own dispatch context:

- AUTONOMOUS mode (you were dispatched by run-queue, or any
  context where no human is at the keyboard):
  - Escalate the bead so a human can address it:
      bd human {{bead-id}}
  - Note in escalation: "missing brainstormed/implementation-ready labels; recommend running brainstorm-bead before implementation."
  - Do NOT prompt — there is no operator to answer.
  - Then abort (per the shared abort mechanism above).

- INTERACTIVE mode (start-bead routed you here, or a human ran
  `bd mol pour` directly and is in conversation with you):
  - Print a clear warning: "Bead {{bead-id}} is missing brainstormed/implementation-ready labels — it has not been through brainstorm-bead. Implementation may run on an incomplete spec."
  - Ask the user:
    (a) Abort and route the bead through start-bead/brainstorm-bead first.
    (b) Proceed with implementation using whatever is in the bead now.
  - On (a): abort (per the shared abort mechanism above).
  - On (b): proceed to worktree.
close_reason: Bead confirmed brainstormed + implementation-ready; plan restated

title: Await PR review and triage feedback
description:
Invoke the wait-for-pr-comments skill.

Skill: superpowers:wait-for-pr-comments

- Wait for Copilot review to complete (or time out)
- Check BOTH top-level PR comments AND inline review comments
  (inline comments are easy to miss — always verify both)
- Auto-fix unambiguous feedback
- Report findings and triage results to the user

Do NOT merge or clean up the worktree until review is complete
or has explicitly timed out.
close_reason: Copilot review found (3 inline comments), all classified Mechanical, all auto-fixed in commit dc031cf, replies posted, threads resolved on GitHub. No re-review fired (Copilot idempotent on push).

title: Refactor for clarity (refactor phase)
description:
Clean up the implementation without changing observable behavior.
Tests must remain green throughout.

Skill: code-simplifier:code-simplifier (run as subagent)

Look for:
- Duplication (three similar lines is a smell; two is acceptable)
- Names that require a comment to understand
- Logic that could be expressed more simply
- Dead code

Re-run tests after every change. If tests break, you changed behavior.
Undo the change and try again.

This step is NOT about adding features or improving performance.
It is about making the code readable by the next person (who is you,
in six months, having forgotten everything).
close_reason: Tightened Operating Discipline preamble to remove duplication with Phase 2's parallel-vs-serial guidance; dropped redundant parenthetical and added cross-reference

title: Create pull request
description:
Invoke the finishing-a-development-branch skill.

Skill: superpowers:finishing-a-development-branch

- Push the branch to origin
- Create a PR with a clear summary linked to {{bead-id}}
- PR title should be ≤70 characters
- PR body should include: what changed, why, and how to test it

Do NOT merge. Do NOT squash. Await review in the next step.

title: Housekeeping: close, remember, file discovered work
description:
Complete the lifecycle and preserve context for future sessions.

1. Close the bead:
     bd close {{bead-id}} --reason "Implemented and PR merged"

2. Walk the parent chain; close each ancestor epic whose remaining
   children are all closed (stops at the first ancestor that still
   has any non-closed children, or when there is no parent):

     PARENT=$(bd show {{bead-id}} --json | jq -r '.[0].parent // empty')
     while [ -n "$PARENT" ]; do
       NON_CLOSED=$(bd list --parent="$PARENT" --json | jq '[.[] | select(.status != "closed")] | length')
       [ "$NON_CLOSED" = "0" ] || break
       bd close "$PARENT" --reason "All children closed"
       PARENT=$(bd show "$PARENT" --json | jq -r '.[0].parent // empty')
     done

3. Record any non-obvious decisions or hard-won insights:
     bd remember "insight about this system or pattern"
     bd decision record --title="Why we chose X over Y"

4. File any work discovered during implementation. Prefer epic-sibling
   placement over orphan-with-dep:

     PARENT=$(bd show {{bead-id}} --json | jq -r '.[0].parent // empty')

     # Decide placement via the sibling test (see rules/beads.md I3):
     # would this work have been on the parent epic's original plan, if
     # we'd thought of it? Yes → sibling. No → orphan + discovered-from.
     IS_SIBLING_SUBTASK=false  # set to true only when the answer is yes

     if [ -n "$PARENT" ] && [ "$IS_SIBLING_SUBTASK" = true ]; then
       # Create INSIDE the parent epic as a sibling:
       bd create "Found: ..." -t "<type>" -p "<prio>" --parent "$PARENT"
     else
       # Otherwise create as an orphan and link with discovered-from:
       NEW=$(bd create "Found: ..." -t "<type>" -p "<prio>" --json | jq -r '.id')
       bd dep add "$NEW" {{bead-id}} --type discovered-from
     fi

   Sibling test (see rules/beads.md I3): would this discovered work
   have been on the parent epic's original plan, if we'd thought of
   it? Yes → sibling (--parent). No → orphan + discovered-from.

5. Burn this wisp (if running as vapor):
     bd mol burn <this-mol-id>
close_reason: Bead b7s closed (no parent); two memories persisted (per-skill-agents-md-is-dead-file; b7s-simplify-drift-policy); no discovered work to file

title: Set up isolated git worktree
description:
Invoke the using-git-worktrees skill to create an isolated branch.

Skill: superpowers:using-git-worktrees

- Branch from main (never from another feature branch)
- Verify you are IN the worktree directory before writing any code
- Confirm no leftover state from other work

Then claim the bead and walk the parent chain:

  bd update {{bead-id}} --status in_progress

  # Walk parent chain; mark each ancestor epic in_progress
  PARENT=$(bd show {{bead-id}} --json | jq -r '.[0].parent // empty')
  while [ -n "$PARENT" ]; do
    bd update "$PARENT" --status in_progress
    PARENT=$(bd show "$PARENT" --json | jq -r '.[0].parent // empty')
  done

If `implement-bead` already claimed the bead before pouring this
formula, the first `bd update` is a no-op — re-running it is safe.
close_reason: Worktree at .claude/worktrees/feat-b7s-simplify-skill on branch feat/b7s-simplify-skill; extracted SKILL.md relocated into worktree

title: Write failing tests (red phase)
description:
Write tests FIRST. No implementation code at this step.

Skills: superpowers:test-driven-development, superpowers:writing-unit-tests, superpowers:testing-anti-patterns

Rules:
- Tests must be written against the acceptance criteria on the bead (set by brainstorm-bead, restated in the confirm step)
- Cover: happy path, edge cases, error paths
- Do NOT mock what you have not verified you understand
- Do NOT write test-only methods in production code

Run the tests and confirm they FAIL. Failure is correct here.

  go test ./...           # Go projects
  pnpm test               # Node/TypeScript projects
  pytest                  # Python projects

If a test passes before any implementation, either the test is wrong
or the feature already exists. Investigate before continuing.
close_reason: Docs-only repo — no test harness (no package.json/go.mod/requirements.txt). ACs #1-#7 are mechanically inspectable content checks; AC #9 (Build/Typecheck/Tests) honored vacuously per brainstorm spec. Verification handled by 'verify with evidence' step via grep/file-existence checks.

title: Completion gate: simplify
description:
Dispatch code-simplifier subagent against changed code.

Agent: code-simplifier:code-simplifier

This step exists because the implementing agent always misses
at least one DRY violation or clarity issue. It is not optional.

Address ALL findings. Re-run tests after any changes made here.
close_reason: None warranted — file was clean after refactor. Reuse/Quality/Efficiency reviews ran serially (3-way parallel dispatch on a 130-line markdown file is overkill). All AC-pinned content (frontmatter, HTML comment, capability-shaped Phase 2 wording, explicit category enumerations) verified intact via required greps.

title: Implement Create simplify skill (port from /simplify command body) (green phase)
description:
Write the minimum code to make all failing tests pass.

Skills: superpowers:test-driven-development, then domain skill
  (e.g. typescript-developer, backend-developer, etc.)

Rules:
- Minimum viable implementation — write only what tests require
- No speculative features, no over-engineering
- No abstractions for hypothetical future requirements

Run tests after each meaningful change:
  go test ./...   (or equivalent)

This step is complete only when all tests are GREEN.
close_reason: Refined src/user/.agents/skills/simplify/SKILL.md: added HTML comment header (source=/simplify slash command, drift=C periodic re-sync, last sync=2026-05-02); added Operating Discipline section (worktree scoping, apply-vs-flag with explicit category enumerations, no-abstractions-without-2-call-sites, boundary rules, memory persistence with no-memory fallback); replaced Claude-tool-named Phase 2 dispatch with capability-shaped wording (Claude Agent, Codex Task, Gemini equivalents); expanded Phase 3 output format (Simplifications applied / Flagged for review / None warranted + Memory updated or Recurring pattern observed). Phase 2 reviewer checklist preserved verbatim. Frontmatter name=simplify locked. Committed as e9d03ff.

title: implement-feature
description: TDD feature implementation: confirm → worktree → red/green/refactor → completion gate → PR → close
close_reason: All 11 steps complete; b7s implemented and PR #23 awaiting merge authorization

title: Completion gate: verify with evidence
description:
Run the full verification suite. Evidence is required — no claims without output.

Skill: superpowers:verification-before-completion

Required gates — show the actual command output for each:
  [ ] Tests pass        → run test suite, paste output
  [ ] Build succeeds    → run build, paste output
  [ ] Lint clean        → run linter, paste output
  [ ] Type check clean  → run type checker, paste output

Do NOT mark this step complete unless all four gates show clean output.
"I believe it passes" is not evidence. Run the commands.

HARD STOP: After this gate, do NOT commit to main and do NOT push
directly. The next step is create-pr. Go there next.
close_reason: PASS — all 9 ACs verified by mechanical evidence. AC1-6/8 grep-confirmed; AC7 install.sh dry-run for claude/codex/gemini/beads completed Done. with 5 new + 11 updated for claude (no fatal collisions); AC9 vacuous in docs-only repo. Single-file change confined to src/user/.agents/skills/simplify/SKILL.md.

title: Apply writing-skills RED-GREEN-REFACTOR to writing-unit-tests and testing-anti-patterns
description: Improve both project skills via writing-skills methodology: dispatch baseline subagents to capture verbatim rationalizations, edit skills to plug those specific holes, re-test for compliance. NO superpowers cross-refs in either skill — keep us decoupled from third-party plugins.

title: Delete code-simplifier agent; rewire completion-gate to invoke /simplify directly
notes:
## Update (agents-config-7bk.8 brainstorm session, 2026-05-02)

**Architecture flip — this bead's scope has narrowed.**

Original Path C ("Have completion-gate step 3 invoke /simplify directly via the orchestrator") is OBSOLETE. New plan:
- agents-config-b7s creates a portable `simplify` skill (ported from the /simplify command body)
- agents-config-7bk.8 rewrites `src/user/.claude/rules/completion-gate.md` step 3 (and ~10 other dispatch sites across the codebase) to invoke the new `simplify` skill — uniform invocation, no orchestrator-tier-only guard
- This bead (7bk.7) now ONLY deletes the broken agent file at `src/user/.agents/agents/code-simplifier.md`

**New dependency**: agents-config-7bk.7 now depends on agents-config-7bk.8 (the sweep must complete before the agent file deletion, otherwise stale dispatches pointing at a non-existent agent would proliferate).

**Original notes (preserved for context, but now partially obsolete):**

---

## Discovery (verbatim from session 5385d9fc)

1. /simplify is a slash command, NOT a skill. It lives outside this repo (installed via a plugin or sidekick injection — not present in src/). When invoked, its body is loaded into the session as a prompt.
2. The /simplify command is itself a parallel-subagent dispatcher. Its Phase 2 explicitly says: "Use the Agent tool to launch all three agents concurrently in a single message" — three reviewers (reuse, quality, efficiency), then aggregate, then apply fixes.
3. The code-simplifier agent at src/user/.agents/agents/code-simplifier.md is BROKEN. It instructs itself to "invoke the bundled /simplify skill … via the Skill tool with skill name simplify" — but no such skill exists. It points at a phantom dependency.
4. Subagents CANNOT invoke slash commands (those are user/orchestrator-initiated), so a simple "fix the reference" patch will NOT work.
5. completion-gate.md step 3 currently calls the code-simplifier agent.

## Decision (Path C) — SUPERSEDED, see Update above

Delete the code-simplifier agent layer entirely. Have completion-gate step 3 invoke /simplify directly via the orchestrator (the orchestrator CAN run slash commands).

## What must be preserved (currently encoded in the agent — needs a new home)

- Worktree scoping (operate in the supplied worktree path, not the main tree)
- Behavior-preserving discipline (apply-vs-flag classification, "no abstractions without ≥ 2 call sites")
- Project memory persistence (.claude/agent-memory/code-simplifier/ recurring-pattern capture)
- Boundary rules (no scope creep, no new dependencies, no test rewrites, no commits/pushes)

→ NEW HOME: agents-config-b7s ports these into the `simplify` skill body.

## Files involved (revised)

- src/user/.agents/agents/code-simplifier.md — DELETE (this bead)
- src/user/.claude/rules/completion-gate.md — handled by 7bk.8, not this bead
- /simplify command source — handled by b7s
- Any other rules/skills referencing code-simplifier agent — handled by 7bk.8 sweep

## Suggested execution steps (revised)

1. Verify 7bk.8 sweep is complete (`bd show 7bk.8` status=closed).
2. Confirm `grep -rn "code-simplifier" src/` returns empty (the sweep should have made it so).
3. Delete src/user/.agents/agents/code-simplifier.md.
4. Run completion gate on the change.

## Relationship to agents-config-7bk.2

Phase 2 (7bk.2) of the agent fleet epic is in flight. Original coordination still applies — 7bk.2's formula updates land independently.
close_reason: Superseded by agents-config-7bk.8 (Plan C bundled the broken-agent file deletion into the sweep PR #24)

acceptance_criteria: 10 agent files removed from src/user/.agents/agents/. No references to deleted agent names remain in scripts/install.sh, AGENTS.md, CLAUDE.md, or any template files. Repo still installs cleanly via scripts/install.sh --dry-run. Build passes. Typecheck passes. Tests pass.
notes:
Files to remove from src/user/.agents/agents/:
- api-developer.md

- javascript-developer.md

KEEP: quality-reviewer.md, tech-lead.md

Rationale: Independent reviews from Claude (Opus 4.7) and Codex agreed all 10 are buzzword checklists encoding only frontier-LLM-resident knowledge with no non-obvious or project-specific guidance. Codex: 'the content is the problem, not the format' — converting to skills would still add nothing.

Direct duplicates of existing skills:
