title: Phase 2: bead-implementor agent + formula rewiring
description:
Phase 2 of agents-config-7bk. Wires the new specialized agent fleet into the bead pipeline by introducing the bead-implementor agent, augmenting implement-bead with worktree lifetime ownership, and rewiring the implement-feature and fix-bug formulas to dispatch the right agents for review/implement/verify and to invoke /simplify directly (orchestrator-side) for the simplify gate.

## Summary

Add a single named "implementor" agent (bead-implementor) that subagents are dispatched as for write-tests, implement, and diagnose steps. Move worktree creation/teardown from per-step responsibility into the implement-bead orchestrator, persisting the worktree path on the molecule via a label so every dispatched subagent receives it. Update both implementation formulas to use current agent names (post Phase 1 + post *-developer cleanup), and convert the simplify step from subagent dispatch to orchestrator-direct /simplify invocation.

## Background

Originally specced 2026-04-26 alongside the dynamic-dove plan. The world has shifted since:
- *-developer agents were deleted (commit 92fdfda; bead 1ck closed); the dynamic-dove "preload domain skills by subject area" justification is gone.
- code-debugger.md was deleted in the same commit; superpowers:systematic-debugging + root-cause-tracing replace it.
- code-reviewer was merged into quality-reviewer (commit 827ef3e).
- bead-verifier was delivered in Phase 1 (7bk.1) but is not yet wired into any formula.
- Formulas still reference stale agent paths (superpowers:code-reviewer, code-simplifier:code-simplifier).
- DISCOVERY (session 5385d9fc): /simplify is a slash COMMAND (not a skill), and the code-simplifier agent is broken (points at a phantom skill). Subagents cannot invoke slash commands, so the simplify step must be orchestrator-direct, not subagent-dispatched. The actual deletion of the code-simplifier agent is tracked separately in agents-config-7bk.7 (peer cleanup bead, non-blocking).

This bead consolidates the surviving Phase 2 work: add bead-implementor as the implementing/debugging muscle, wire bead-verifier into the verify gate, fix stale agent references, centralize worktree lifecycle in the orchestrator, and convert the simplify step to orchestrator-direct invocation.

## Requirements

1. **CREATE `src/plugins/beads/.agents/agents/bead-implementor.md`** — minimal "implementor" agent.
   - Frontmatter: name=bead-implementor, model=sonnet, color=blue, tools=Read+Edit+Write+Grep+Glob+Bash, memory=project.
   - skills: [superpowers:test-driven-development, writing-unit-tests, testing-anti-patterns, superpowers:using-git-worktrees, superpowers:verification-before-completion, superpowers:systematic-debugging, superpowers:root-cause-tracing]
   - Body: cd into the worktree path passed by the orchestrator (via prompt — derived from molecule label, see #2), follow preloaded TDD methodology for write-tests/implement steps, follow preloaded debugging skills for diagnose steps. Never declare done without verification evidence. NO domain-skill preloading machinery — just the fixed skills list above.

2. **AUGMENT `src/plugins/beads/.agents/skills/implement-bead/SKILL.md`** — orchestrator owns the worktree for the bead's full lifetime.
   - On the FIRST dispatched step that needs a worktree (typically write-tests for features, diagnose for bugs), the orchestrator invokes superpowers:using-git-worktrees, captures the path, and stamps a label on the molecule of the form `worktree-path-<sanitized>` where slashes in the path are replaced by `__` (decode on read).
   - On every subsequent subagent dispatch, the orchestrator parses the label off the molecule and injects the decoded worktree path into the subagent's prompt.
   - Worktree teardown happens in the merge-and-cleanup formula (out of scope for this bead beyond noting the contract — merge-and-cleanup already exists).
   - Document the label format and parse/encode helpers in the SKILL.md so future agents can interpret the convention.

3. **UPDATE `src/plugins/beads/.beads/formulas/implement-feature.formula.toml`:**
   - Replace `Agent: superpowers:code-reviewer` → `Agent: quality-reviewer` (review step).
   - Convert simplify step from `Agent: code-simplifier:code-simplifier` to ORCHESTRATOR-DIRECT: the step body instructs the orchestrator to invoke the `/simplify` slash command directly (no Agent: line), since /simplify itself dispatches three parallel subagents internally. Document this in the step text so future readers understand why simplify is structured differently from review/verify.
   - Wire `Agent: bead-implementor` into write-tests and implement steps (replacing whatever generic dispatch is currently there).
   - Wire `Agent: bead-verifier` into the verify step.

4. **UPDATE `src/plugins/beads/.beads/formulas/fix-bug.formula.toml`:**
   - Same review and simplify changes as #3 (review = quality-reviewer; simplify = orchestrator-direct /simplify).
   - Wire `Agent: bead-implementor` into the diagnose, write-tests, and implement steps (bead-implementor's preloaded systematic-debugging + root-cause-tracing skills cover diagnose).
   - Wire `Agent: bead-verifier` into the verify step.

5. **DROP from original spec:** no longer modify code-debugger.md (file deleted in 92fdfda; debugging skills now live as preloaded skills on bead-implementor per #1).

6. **End-to-end smoke test:** push a trivial bead through run-queue and confirm the new dispatches fire correctly — bead-implementor runs in the persisted worktree, bead-verifier reports exit codes, quality-reviewer runs in the review step, and the simplify step's orchestrator-direct /simplify invocation completes (or is skipped cleanly if 7bk.7 hasn't landed yet — confirm graceful behavior either way). Worktree label stamps on the molecule and parses correctly across step boundaries.

## Out of Scope

- Domain-skill preloading on bead-implementor (1ck closed; *-developer skills not built).
- Deletion of src/user/.agents/agents/code-simplifier.md and rewriting of completion-gate.md step 3 — tracked in agents-config-7bk.7.
- Editing the /simplify command body (also 7bk.7 territory if that bead chooses to add a Phase 0 discipline section).
- Teardown logic in merge-and-cleanup beyond confirming the existing flow can read the label-stored path.
- Changes to brainstorm-bead.formula.toml (no agent dispatch in brainstorming).
- Updating tech-lead.md or any other surviving agent (Phase 1 territory or already done).
- Replacing the existing `for-bead-<bead-id>` molecule label convention (orthogonal; worktree-path label is additive).

## Design Notes

- **Why a single bead-implementor instead of multiple specialized "implementor" variants:** the original dynamic-dove rationale was domain-skill preloading. With *-developer skills not built, there's no useful axis on which to specialize. One named role keeps formula entries traceable and gives a clear seam if domain preloading is ever revived.
- **Why label storage for worktree path:** labels are lifecycle-neutral, queryable via existing bd CLI, and don't entangle the molecule's notes/description (which formulas may overwrite). Sanitization is a known cost; the alternative (mol notes) gets clobbered by step logic that uses --notes.
- **Why bead-implementor handles diagnose:** option (a) from the brainstorm Q3 — preloading systematic-debugging + root-cause-tracing on a single agent is cheaper than building a parallel bead-debugger and gives the formula one consistent dispatch target across feature/bug pipelines.
- **Why simplify is orchestrator-direct, not subagent-dispatched:** /simplify is a slash command (not a skill) and itself dispatches three parallel reviewer subagents internally. Subagents cannot invoke slash commands, so wrapping /simplify in an "Agent: code-simplifier" dispatch is structurally impossible. The orchestrator (the agent driving the molecule) IS the only layer that can run /simplify, so the formula step body instructs it directly.
- **Worktree label encoding:** `worktree-path-<path-with-slashes-replaced-by-double-underscore>`. Decode by reversing the replacement. Example: `/Users/scott/foo/bar` → `worktree-path-__Users__scott__foo__bar`. Skills/agents that read this MUST parse from the well-known prefix.
- **Relationship with 7bk.7:** 7bk.7 (delete code-simplifier agent + rewrite completion-gate step 3) is a peer cleanup, not a blocker. 7bk.2's formula updates avoid referencing the doomed agent regardless of when 7bk.7 lands. They are independent.

## Acceptance Criteria

- src/plugins/beads/.agents/agents/bead-implementor.md exists with frontmatter and body matching requirement #1.
- src/plugins/beads/.agents/skills/implement-bead/SKILL.md documents and implements the worktree-label lifecycle (create-on-first-need, stamp, parse-and-inject on subsequent dispatches).
- implement-feature.formula.toml and fix-bug.formula.toml reference quality-reviewer (not superpowers:code-reviewer), do NOT reference code-simplifier:code-simplifier (simplify step is orchestrator-direct /simplify), reference bead-implementor for write-tests/implement(/diagnose), and reference bead-verifier for verify.
- A trivial test bead processed through run-queue completes successfully with the new dispatches firing in the expected steps.
- Build passes. Typecheck passes. Tests pass.

## Dependencies

- ✓ agents-config-7bk.1 (Phase 1 — bead-verifier, quality-reviewer delivered)
- ✓ agents-config-1ck (closed: *-developer agents removed; dynamic-dove preload superseded)
- (peer, non-blocking) agents-config-7bk.7 — cleanup of the code-simplifier agent layer
acceptance_criteria: src/plugins/beads/.agents/agents/bead-implementor.md exists with required skills and tools. implement-bead SKILL.md documents and implements worktree-label lifecycle. implement-feature.formula.toml and fix-bug.formula.toml reference quality-reviewer (not superpowers:code-reviewer), do NOT reference code-simplifier subagent (simplify is orchestrator-direct /simplify), reference bead-implementor for write-tests/implement(/diagnose), and reference bead-verifier for verify. Trivial test bead processed through run-queue completes with new dispatches firing. Build passes. Typecheck passes. Tests pass.

title: Phase 1: Foundation — verifier agent, code-reviewer + code-simplifier upgrades
description:

   - Frontmatter: name, description, model: claude-opus-4-7[1m], effort: medium, tools: Read+Edit+Grep+Glob+Bash, memory: project, color: yellow

3. CREATE src/plugins/beads/.agents/agents/bead-verifier.md (NEW directory + file):

   - Frontmatter: name: bead-verifier, model: haiku, effort: low, tools: Read+Grep+Glob+Bash, skills: [superpowers:verification-before-completion], color: cyan

4. MODIFY src/user/.agents/skills/verify-checklist/SKILL.md (mirrors to installed copy):

ACCEPTANCE:

Three new/upgraded agents shipped: bead-verifier (haiku, beads plugin), code-simplifier (opus[1m], user-scoped), quality-reviewer (skills+memory upgrade). title: Specialized agent fleet (M3 worker fleet)
description:
Rescoped 2026-05-10 during Phase 5b beadification — was 'Specialized agent fleet for the bead pipeline' covering all per-stage subagent + cost-rightsizing + brainstorm-bead-rewiring + production-driver work; now M3-fleet-only after children spanning M1 hygiene, M2 brainstorm-readiness, M4 production-driver, post-MVP, and research-spike were redistributed.

Scope: redesign and ship the worker fleet that runs the bead pipeline end-to-end from feature-bead implementation through PR creation, including per-stage right-sized worker dispatch (worker-report-v1 schema, tdd-red-team / tdd-green-team / bug-diagnoser / bead-implementor-replacement) and cost-rightsizing controls (per-step Model:/Effort: directives in formula TOMLs; F-04 default table). Excludes: brainstorm-readiness work (now under brainstorm-readiness epic under M2), production shell driver work (now under M4), A/B testing and post-MVP multipliers (now under Post-MVP), and standalone hygiene/spike beads (now under M1 / Research-spikes).

title: Convert *-developer agents into preloadable skills
description: Convert the existing *-developer agents (typescript-developer, backend-developer, frontend-developer, javascript-developer, api-developer) into skills that bead-implementor can preload when a bead's domain calls for it. Currently these are agent files at ~/.claude/agents/ and src/user/.agents/agents/. Under the dynamic-dove design (plan file at ~/.claude/plans/i-think-you-need-dynamic-dove.md), bead-implementor is a single generic agent that preloads domain skills based on the bead's subject area. Outcome: each *-developer agent becomes a skill (SKILL.md with appropriate frontmatter) that can be referenced in the 'skills:' frontmatter list of bead-implementor or invoked by the orchestrator at dispatch time. Blocks Phase 2 of dynamic-dove rollout.
close_reason: Independently verified: *-developer agents removed (commit 92fdfda) — no longer needed as skills or agents. Dynamic-dove preload design superseded.

title: Fix broken YAML frontmatter in 12 src/user agent files
description: All 12 agent files in src/user/.agents/agents/ fail YAML frontmatter parsing. Multi-line 'description:' with embedded <example> XML and 'Examples:' sibling keys cause 'while scanning a simple key' errors when Claude Code loads agents from ~/.claude/agents/. Fix: convert description to block scalar (| or |-) and indent body so Examples live inside the description string, preserving project convention that examples belong in frontmatter. Affects: api-developer, backend-developer, code-debugger, code-documenter, code-refactor, code-reviewer, data-scientist, database-designer, frontend-developer, javascript-developer, tech-lead, typescript-developer.
acceptance_criteria: All 12 agent files parse as valid YAML. Build passes. Typecheck passes (n/a, doc repo). Tests pass (n/a). Installer copies files unchanged in shape.
close_reason: Merged via PR #17 (squash commit b7cf3f1). All 12 agent files now parse as valid YAML.

title: Externalize long bead specs to docs/beads/ files; bead description stays a short summary referencing the doc
description: Specs live in bead fields (description, notes, design, acceptance_criteria) — the bead IS the plan. If a write balks at field size, externalize to docs/beads/<short-id>-<<slug>.md with a relative-path reference. Commit: b866455
notes:
Context: while brainstorming lu3.4, the final spec grew to ~300 lines. Writing the full spec into the bead notes field via 'bd update --notes' requires shell-escape gymnastics and replaces the field wholesale (see bug guard in lu3.4 spec R6). Manually archived lu3.4 spec to docs/specs/2026-04-19-ralf-it-form-reevaluation-design.md to preserve it.

Proposal: adopt a pattern where long bead specs live at docs/beads/<bead-id>-<slug>.md (or docs/specs/<date>-<slug>.md), and the bead's description/notes is a short summary + reference to the doc file path. The bead remains self-descriptive; the full spec is versioned in git alongside code.

Benefits:
- shell-safe authoring (edit the file directly, no CLI string escaping)
- PR-reviewable spec changes via normal diff review
- grep-friendly
- no duplication between bd's notes field and docs/specs
- bead description stays scannable in 'bd show <id>'

Open questions (for start-bead/brainstorm-bead to resolve, not now):
- Threshold for 'long enough' to externalize (line count? number of sections?)
- Should brainstorm-bead.formula.toml's write-spec step be updated to write long specs to file + summary to bd notes?
- Should create-bead / start-bead skills know about this pattern?
- Directory choice: reuse docs/specs/ vs new docs/beads/ subdir?
- How does the bead reference the doc — relative path? URL to repo? Commit-pinned?
- Do we need a 'seal' mechanism to prevent bd notes from drifting out of sync with the external doc?
close_reason: Done — convention documented in src/plugins/beads/.claude/rules/beads.md. Commit b866455.

title: wait-for-pr-comments: poll-copilot-review.sh returns stale reviews on re-review cycles
description:
# Problem

After pushing fixes and re-requesting Copilot review, poll-copilot-review.sh (in the wait-for-pr-comments skill) short-circuits on attempt 1 and reports copilot_review_found — but returns the STALE prior review rather than the new one. The JSON it emits has a commit_id field pointing at the OLD commit, which the skill's Phase 3 triage then operates on.

# Impact

Auto-fix loop spins on the same comments it already addressed. Discovered on agents-config-uvt PR #12 during two re-review cycles; had to fall back to manual gh api polling with a submitted_at > last-commit-timestamp filter.

# Root cause

The script searches /pulls/N/reviews for ANY review authored by Copilot. It doesn't compare commit_id against the latest pushed commit, nor submitted_at against the re-request time.

# Fix direction

poll-copilot-review.sh should accept an optional --since-commit or --since-timestamp argument, and the Phase 4 re-review flow in wait-for-pr-comments/SKILL.md should pass the latest commit's timestamp so only newer reviews match.

# Discovered-from

agents-config-uvt

comments.author: Scott Hamilton
comments.text:
Second repro on scotthamilton77/claude-code-sidekick PR #183 (2026-05-02):

- 11:05Z: Copilot review #1 with 4 inline findings.
- After fixes pushed, copilot_work_started fired at 14:08:33Z.
- poll-copilot-review.sh --skip-request-check entered sub-phase C, immediately found review #1 (predates work_started), reported 'Copilot review found (attempt 1)' and exited 0.
- Skill emitted Variant 1 'All fixed, no Copilot re-review' final report.
- Actual second review landed at 14:14:38Z (6 minutes later) with 6 NEW inline findings — caught manually, not by the script.

Adds two UX dimensions worth fixing alongside the timestamp filter:

1. Risk multiplier: a false 'All clear' Variant 1 report is the exact moment a user authorizes merge ('merge it' / 'ship it'). The skill needs to express uncertainty when a re-review is in flight, not declare clear.

2. Pre-trigger hint: even before copilot_work_started fires, the existence of commits with author.date > last_review.submitted_at is a strong signal a re-review may be queued. The report should surface that timing relationship explicitly so users can wait if needed.

Originally filed as scotthamilton77/claude-code-sidekick:sidekick-aga3 (now deleted as duplicate of this bead).

title: Bring in & adapt named external skill sources via oss-snapshots/ + cx6.7 amalgam work
description:
# Strategy: Bring in & adapt named external skill sources (oss-snapshots/ + amalgams)

## What this is (current, post-2026-05-17 restructure)

Snapshot-and-adapt named external skill sources. The first phase — pulling unmodified snapshots into the repo — shipped under `oss-snapshots/` (commit 512cd4d, 2026-05-17). The second phase — per-skill deployable verdicts, amalgams, and a final cross-namespace optimization pass — runs under cx6.7's subtasks.

## Phase 1: Snapshots (complete)

| Source | Snapshot location | Cx6 child | Status |
|--------|-------------------|-----------|--------|
| mattpocock/skills | `oss-snapshots/pocock/` | cx6.3 | closed (snapshot phase) |
| obra/superpowers | `oss-snapshots/superpowers/` | cx6.4 | closed (snapshot phase) |
| beads plugin source | `src/plugins/beads/.agents/skills/beads/` | cx6.5 | closed (no-op; plugin already vendored) |
| anthropics/skills (skill-creator) | `oss-snapshots/anthropics/` | cx6.6 | closed (snapshot phase) |

## Phase 2: Deployable verdicts + amalgams (cx6.7 subtree)

- cx6.7.1 — reconcile drift between original cx6 framing and the oss-snapshots/ + cx6.7 restructure
- cx6.7.2 — author writing-skills amalgam at `src/user/.agents/skills/writing-skills/` (sourced from superpowers/writing-skills + skill-creator)
- cx6.7.3 — add empirical eval-loop integration (`--quick`/`--deep` modes) to optimize-my-skill, pulling from skill-creator's eval scripts
- cx6.7.4 — bring skill-creator over as standalone at `src/user/.agents/skills/skill-creator/`
- cx6.7.5 — per-skill verdict pass for `oss-snapshots/pocock/` (promote, amalgamate, or skip)
- cx6.7.6 — per-skill verdict pass for `oss-snapshots/superpowers/` (promote, amalgamate, or skip)
- cx6.7.7 — final cross-namespace optimize-my-skill pass + provenance registry close-out

## Provenance convention

Skills derived from any oss-snapshots/ source must carry an HTML-comment provenance header at the top of SKILL.md (citing snapshot path + upstream commit + last-sync date + drift policy). The project-wide registry lives at `src/user/.agents/skills/AGENTS.md`. Each oss-snapshots subdirectory carries its own AGENTS.md documenting source repo, commit, and per-skill inventory.

## Out of scope

- Maintaining forks of external repos with sync mechanisms
- Sources outside the named four
- Adopting external skills wholesale without per-skill examination

## Relationship to prior cx6 framing

This restructure (2026-05-17) supersedes both the original synthesis-assimilation framing AND the placement choice of "namespace folder under src/user/.agents/skills/<source>/". Snapshots live in `oss-snapshots/` to keep them isolated from depth-1 host discovery; deployable skills live flat at `src/user/.agents/skills/<skill>/` and carry provenance headers. Closed-superseded children: cx6.1, cx6.2, sxfk. Closed-deferred: 7bk.24.
acceptance_criteria: All four source-specific tasks closed (each with the target folder populated and AGENTS.md + README.md in place per the per-source contract above). Final review/optimize/document task closed. No external-source skill content sitting outside its namespaced folder.
notes:
DEPRIORITIZATION RATIONALE (added 2026-05-05)

Fork escalation is intentionally deferred at P4 because recent sessions have not shown noticeable divergence recurrence. Keep this as a contingency path only; start only if 717 evaluation/KPIs indicate partnership controls are insufficient.


REPRIORITIZATION (2026-05-17): Re-parented under M0 (wgclw, Discipline-layer rearchitecture) and bumped P4 → P2. The 2026-05-05 deprioritization rationale above is superseded: synthesis assimilation is now an active M0 deliverable, not a fork-escalation contingency. The Gap-fill + Compare-and-improve work in cx6.1, cx6.2, sxfk, and 7bk.24 maps directly onto M0's 'skills own judgment' pillar.

title: Tighten wait-for-pr-comments auto-fix scope + add hand-off to resolve-pr-comments
description: Add an explicit hand-off decision point in wait-for-pr-comments Phase 3. Tighten its auto-fix scope to zero-risk mechanical changes only (typos, obvious magic numbers, single-line additions). Anything requiring judgment, multi-file edits, refactors, or behavior changes must be handed off to resolve-pr-comments with a clear pointer. Update When-to-Use and Red Flags to reinforce the boundary. This is a targeted clarification, not a structural rewrite — the full architectural question (should PR review response be a formula/molecule?) is tracked in agents-config-bl3, which may supersede this.
close_reason: Merged in PR #10 (squash commit 2fd1524). wait-for-pr-comments now classifies via Mechanical/Non-trivial/Ambiguous buckets and hands off Non-trivial items to resolve-pr-comments.

title: Create resolve-pr-comments skill
description: Add a dedicated skill that addresses PR review comments using a structured per-comment subagent workflow. Triggers: user wants to resolve open PR review comments (Copilot, human, or both). Workflow: clarify ambiguity only when judgment cannot, dispatch one subagent per fix, use ralf-it for significant work, run completion gate (code-review + code-simplifier + verify-checklist), commit back to PR, reply to each comment with what was fixed/skipped + reason, GH-resolve conversations only for FIXED comments. Lives in src/user/.agents/skills/resolve-pr-comments/SKILL.md (shared content, copied to all tools).
close_reason: Merged in PR #9 (squash commit d91243a). Skill landed at src/user/.agents/skills/resolve-pr-comments/SKILL.md; .beads/issues.jsonl gitignored.

title: Refactor for clarity (refactor phase)
description:
Clean up the implementation without changing observable behavior.
Tests must remain green throughout.

Skill: code-simplifier:code-simplifier (run as subagent)

Look for:
- Duplication (three similar lines is a smell; two is acceptable)
- Names that require a comment to understand
- Logic that could be expressed more simply
- Dead code

Re-run tests after every change. If tests break, you changed behavior.
Undo the change and try again.

This step is NOT about adding features or improving performance.
It is about making the code readable by the next person (who is you,
in six months, having forgotten everything).
close_reason: Refactor phase: 3 prose-clarity fixes to root README — simplified dense prerequisites callout, de-bloated Tool-specific extensions bullet into sub-bullets, fixed alphabetical order in skills table. Subfolder READMEs, CONTRIBUTING, LICENSE, and AGENTS.md drift-fix reviewed — no changes needed.

title: implement-feature
description: TDD feature implementation: brainstorm → worktree → red/green/refactor → completion gate → PR → close

title: Brainstorm: requirements for Documentation pass for public repo readiness
description:
Invoke the brainstorming skill BEFORE any design, tests, or code.

Skill: superpowers:brainstorming

Questions to resolve:
- What problem does this actually solve?
- Who uses it and how?
- What does "done" look like? (acceptance criteria)
- What are the edge cases and error paths?
- Any architectural implications or constraints?
- Is there a simpler approach that solves the same problem?

Output: written acceptance criteria and a clear implementation plan.

Write criteria back to the bead so it persists across sessions:
  bd update {{bead-id}} --acceptance="<one line per criterion>"

If anything is ambiguous or contradictory, stop and ask the user
before proceeding to the next step.
close_reason: Brainstorming completed upstream via brainstorm-bead formula. Spec, requirements, and acceptance criteria live in parent bead agents-config-mxe (notes + acceptance fields), already ralf-reviewed.

title: Set up isolated git worktree
description:
Invoke the using-git-worktrees skill to create an isolated branch.

Skill: superpowers:using-git-worktrees

- Branch from main (never from another feature branch)
- Verify you are IN the worktree directory before writing any code
- Confirm no leftover state from other work

Then claim the bead:
  bd update {{bead-id}} --status in_progress

If the bead has a parent epic, mark that in_progress too:
  bd show {{bead-id}}   # check for parent
  bd update <parent-id> --status in_progress

title: Implement Documentation pass for public repo readiness (green phase)
description:
Write the minimum code to make all failing tests pass.

Skills: superpowers:test-driven-development, then domain skill
  (e.g. typescript-developer, backend-developer, etc.)

Rules:
- Minimum viable implementation — write only what tests require
- No speculative features, no over-engineering
- No abstractions for hypothetical future requirements

Run tests after each meaningful change:
  go test ./...   (or equivalent)

This step is complete only when all tests are GREEN.

title: Write failing tests (red phase)
description:
Write tests FIRST. No implementation code at this step.

Skills: superpowers:writing-unit-tests, superpowers:testing-anti-patterns

Rules:

- Do NOT mock what you have not verified you understand

Run the tests and confirm they FAIL. Failure is correct here.

  go test ./...           # Go projects
  pnpm test               # Node/TypeScript projects
  pytest                  # Python projects

If a test passes before any implementation, either the test is wrong
or the feature already exists. Investigate before continuing.

Scope: every file changed since branch creation, reviewed against:

title: Completion gate: verify with evidence

Run the full verification suite. Evidence is required — no claims without output.

Skill: superpowers:verification-before-completion

Required gates — show the actual command output for each:

Do NOT mark this step complete unless all four gates show clean output.

directly. The next step is create-pr. Go there next.

title: Housekeeping: close, remember, file discovered work
description:
Complete the lifecycle and preserve context for future sessions.

1. Close the bead:
     bd close {{bead-id}} --reason "Implemented and PR merged"

2. If all siblings in a parent epic are now closed, close the parent:
     bd show {{bead-id}}    # check for parent
     bd list --parent=<parent-id>  # check siblings

3. Record any non-obvious decisions or hard-won insights:
     bd remember "insight about this system or pattern"
     bd decision record --title="Why we chose X over Y"

4. File any work discovered during implementation:
     bd create "Found: ..." -t bug -p 1
     bd dep add <new-id> {{bead-id}} --type discovered-from

5. Burn this wisp (if running as vapor):
     bd mol burn <this-mol-id>

notes:
# Documentation pass for public repo readiness

## Summary
Prepare the agents-config repo for public consumption. Scope: polish existing top-level docs, correct known drift, and fill gaps a first-time visitor expects (LICENSE, CONTRIBUTING, per-subfolder READMEs). No GitHub-specific scaffolding, no prose-level rewrites.

## Background / motivation
Repo is going public. Current state: root README.md is detailed but has drift from the rules/ reorganization; root AGENTS.md has duplicated session-completion blocks; tool-specific subfolders under src/user/ have no or near-empty READMEs; no LICENSE file exists despite README declaring MIT; no CONTRIBUTING.md. A stranger landing on the repo needs accurate install guidance and a clear picture of what each folder contains.

Primary reader: fork-and-install users, not contributors. Light contributor notes only.

## Requirements
- Add a root LICENSE file matching the MIT license already declared in README.md. Copyright holder: "Scott Hamilton". Year: 2026.
- Add a short CONTRIBUTING.md acknowledging the repo is a personal config and asking contributors to open an issue first.
- Add a README.md to each tool-specific subfolder: src/user/.agents/, src/user/.claude/, src/user/.codex/, src/user/.gemini/. Each explains purpose, contents, install target, and intended audience.
- Correct drift in existing root README.md and root AGENTS.md: any stale references to file paths, section names, or structure that have since moved (notably content relocated from CLAUDE-EXTENSIONS.md to rules/).
- Verify README.md Skills table and Agents section against the actual contents of src/user/.agents/skills/ and src/user/.agents/agents/.
- Delete the 4-line stub at src/user/.claude/AGENTS.md in the same PR that adds src/user/.claude/README.md (README added first, stub removed after).

## Out of scope
- .github/ scaffolding (issue templates, PR templates, workflows)
- CODE_OF_CONDUCT.md
- Restructuring or rewriting the root README for style
- Changes to docs/plans/ or docs/specs/ content
- Changes to src/plugins/AGENTS.md (already in good shape) — but root README tree SHOULD mention src/plugins/
- Any non-doc changes (no code edits, no install.sh changes)
- Any edits inside the root AGENTS.md BEADS INTEGRATION v:1 marker block — auto-generated by the beads plugin, regenerated on bd runs. Only the non-generated Session Completion and Beads Quick Reference blocks that appear outside or alongside the markers should be deduplicated.

## Design notes

### Drift detection (README.md / AGENTS.md)

Concrete checks — after edits, these greps should return nothing (or only current-truth results):

- No instruction to "copy CLAUDE-EXTENSIONS.md.template" without also mentioning rules/.
- No claim that `<orchestration>`, `<delegation>`, `<completion-gate>`, `<delivery>`, or `<beads>` sections live in CLAUDE-EXTENSIONS.md. They live in INSTRUCTIONS.md (orchestration) and rules/ (the rest).
- No reference to a skill name that doesn't correspond to a directory in src/user/.agents/skills/.
- No reference to an agent name that doesn't correspond to a file in src/user/.agents/agents/.

### Skills/Agents verification

Bidirectional by directory name:
- Every directory in src/user/.agents/skills/ has a row in the README Skills table.
- Every row in the README Skills table corresponds to a real directory.
- Agents section either lists the shipping agent names or points to the directory; do NOT leave it as vague prose.

### Beads integration markers

Root AGENTS.md contains `<!-- BEGIN BEADS INTEGRATION v:1 ... -->` and `<!-- END BEADS INTEGRATION -->` markers around a plugin-managed block. This block is regenerated by the beads plugin on install and must not be edited by hand. Any deduplication work on AGENTS.md must target content OUTSIDE these markers.

### Subfolder README style

- Short (roughly 20-50 lines each). Short enough that a first-time visitor can scan.
- Link up to the root README for install flow; do not duplicate install instructions.
- Answer three questions per folder: what lives here, how does it get installed, who is it for.

### LICENSE

Canonical MIT license text. Format:
```
MIT License

Copyright (c) 2026 Scott Hamilton

Permission is hereby granted, free of charge, ...
```

### CONTRIBUTING.md

Example draft:
> This is currently a personal configuration repository. If you find it useful and want to contribute agents, skills, or commands, please open an issue to discuss before sending a PR. See AGENTS.md for file format conventions (agent frontmatter, skill SKILL.md layout, command markdown).

### Delivery

- Single branch, single PR.
- Docs-only. No code changes, no install.sh changes, no settings changes.
- Drift fixes are surgical — edit only stale claims, not prose style.

## Open questions
- None blocking. Minor copy-edit decisions during implementation are fine to decide inline.

title: merge-guard/eligibility test+robustness polish batch (completion-gate minors)
description: Residual minors from the wgclw.14 completion-gate review, batched: (1) check-merge-eligibility.sh legacy combined-status fetch is unpaginated (default page size 30) — an unpinned required context past 30 distinct legacy statuses is invisible; (2) terminal_ok disposition set recognizes SKIP and FIX/{committed,already_addressed} — check the spec disposition set for whether a deferred-with-rationale terminal state should also clear (verify against spec before changing); (3) check-merge-eligibility_test.sh fixture realism: mk_review uses user.type "Human", real GitHub enum is User/Bot/Organization; (4) grep-the-source assertions in check-merge-eligibility_test.sh (~lines 22-28) are implementation-text tautologies — replace with behavior tests or drop; (5) resolve_policy_test.py test_agents_config_policy_resolves hand-copies repo config instead of resolving the real project-config.toml — rename or point at the real file. All found by the 6-dimension gate review during wgclw.14 execution.

title: check-merge-eligibility.sh: multi-page fixture for unresolved-thread cursor pagination
description: fetch_threads_page cursor-pagination loop has zero coverage — the single-env-var gh stub cannot express hasNextPage=true continuation. Extend the stub (e.g. FIXTURE_THREADS_PAGE2 + cursor-keyed dispatch) and assert multi-page accumulation and cursor threading. Also noted as an accepted known gap in the plan self-review. Found by the Task 11 adversarial verifier during wgclw.14 execution.

title: check-merge-eligibility.sh: test wholly-missing commit_id key on approvers fact
description: Approvers fact handles a missing commit_id key via (.commit_id // "") — behaviorally covered for the mismatched/stale case (carol fixture) and for the bot fact (Task 8 tests), but no test drops the key entirely on an APPROVED review. Add a fixture with no commit_id key; assert the approval is not counted. Found by the Task 10 adversarial verifier during wgclw.14 execution.

title: check-merge-eligibility.sh: test exit-3 fail-closed paths (PR not open, missing head SHA)
description: The script fail-closes with exit 3 on a non-open PR and on a missing/null headRefOid, but no test exercises either path — the plan Task 7 test file omitted them. Add stub fixtures for closed/merged PR state and null head SHA; assert exit 3 and no eligible output. Found by the Task 7 adversarial verifier during wgclw.14 execution.

title: wait-for-pr-comments SKILL.md guard-numbering off-by-one vs validate-inventory.sh
description: A/SKILL.md narrative guard list (~819-852) numbers guards off-by-one vs the authoritative script labels (script guard 3 = narrative #4). Align the narrative list to script numbering.

title: poll-copilot-review.sh: consider bot-reviewers-driven filter
description: Polling still matches Copilot by substring (COPILOT_REVIEW_FILTER). Harmless (detection, not a trust boundary — merge enforcement uses the exact-identity allowlist), but aligning the poll filter to the policy bot-reviewers list would generalize polling to non-Copilot bots. Optional.

title: Installer: stream file hashing (hashlib.file_digest) across all hash sites
description: Copilot (PR #204) flagged dir_content_digest reading whole files into memory via read_bytes() before sha256. Valid but the same read-into-memory pattern is the installer-wide convention: sync._sha256, prune_hash.is_safe_to_prune file-orphan branch, and dir_content_digest. Convert all sites to bounded-memory streaming (hashlib.file_digest, stdlib 3.11+) in one consistent pass rather than diverging a single site. Semantics-identical (same digest). Low priority — owned dirs hold small files; matters only for user-dropped large files at the prune boundary.
close_reason: Shipped via PR #205 (squash-merged): streaming sha256_file helper across sync/prune_hash/dir_content_digest.

title: Reconcile installer.toml / Config doc-code drift in installer HLD
description:
Copilot (PR #200 review) flagged docs/architecture/installer/sequences.md:53 claiming config.py loads installer.toml ([tools] overrides), but config.py does NOT. Verified pervasive, PRE-EXISTING drift (on main; this PR did not introduce it):

- config.py Config dataclass carries only home/tools/auto_yes. No tool_overrides/dry_run/plugins/dump_stage (data-view.md:101 claims those fields), and no load_installer_toml() call.
- load_installer_toml / InstallerToml have ZERO production callers (grep: only the module's own definition + a docstring mention in installignore.py). Commit e43919b (remove [prune] config) removed the last consumer; tool_dest_overrides was always documented 'parsed but NOT yet consumed'.
- The 'config.py reads installer.toml' / Config-fields claim appears in ~20 places across all 5 HLD docs (sequences.md, data-view.md, installer-design.md, c4-l2-container.md, c4-l3-engine.md).

DECISION NEEDED (human/roadmap context): is installer.toml [tools]-override loading (and tool_dest_overrides) a planned-but-unbuilt feature, or abandoned cruft?
  (a) PLANNED -> annotate the HLD docs as intended-not-yet-wired; optionally a separate bead to build it.
  (b) ABANDONED -> delete installer.toml + core/installer_toml.py + scrub the ~20 doc refs (separate cleanup PR).
  (c) WIRE IT -> implement load_installer_toml in Config construction (feature work).

Out of scope for the receipt-prune PR (#200); orthogonal to pruning. Found by Copilot review round on PR #200.

title: Receipt prune: atomic validate-and-delete on the prune boundary (close residual TOCTOU)
description:
Residual TOCTOU surfaced by codex adversarial review (round 10) of PR #200. `_back_up_and_delete` re-validates the orphan (revalidate at prune_flow.py:241), then later unlinks/rmtrees it (prune_flow.py:263/268). The path could be swapped between those two points; the replacement would be backed up then removed.

NON-SEVERE / heavily mitigated, NOT data-loss:
- Backup precedes delete (prune_flow.py:251-252 -> back_up), so a raced-in replacement is copied to <namespace>-backup/ before removal: recoverable, not destroyed.
- The whole install->prune->write runs under the single-writer advisory flock (cli.py), so no other installer run can race; only a non-installer process/adversary writing into the install target inside a ~2-syscall window on a path the installer was about to touch anyway.
codex rated it [medium]; with backup-first + lock, real-world impact is closer to low.

Fix options (pick during implementation):
1. (codex-preferred) Atomically move the candidate to quarantine, validate the MOVED object against the recorded hash/type, then finalize -- validate and delete operate on the same FS object. Restructures _back_up_and_delete (backup routing, finalize/restore semantics, receipt bookkeeping).
2. (minimal) Snapshot lstat identity (st_dev/st_ino/st_mtime) before revalidate; re-check immediately before unlink/rmtree; skip-and-keep (do not count pruned, do not add to removed) on mismatch.

Acceptance: a regression test that swaps the orphan path after revalidate and before deletion proves the replacement is left intact (option 2) or quarantined-and-validated (option 1); make ci-installer green.
notes: PR #203 up and review-complete (3 Copilot rounds, all 8 threads resolved). Commits: 10c38cc (fix+test), b70e045 (recheck before backup — restores summary._is_changed invariant), d2d43ce (block only present replacement; vanish-mid-window test), 484b763 (comment/log precision). make ci-installer green: 691 passed, 99.42% cov. Option 1 (quarantine) and fkewj (dir drift) deferred. Awaiting merge authorization.

title: Validate discovered-but-excluded plugin roots against live code, not allowlist
description: prune_pipeline builds live_roots_by_owner only from ACTIVE plugins, so a discovered-but-excluded plugin (in scope via discovered_plugin_names, dropped via --plugins=) falls to validate_entry's retired/allowlist branch instead of its live route roots. Spec (design doc lines 118,130) says a currently-discovered plugin's root comes from live code. Behavior is SAFE today (fails closed; root is always in prior.roots if ever installed, so excluded plugins still prune correctly) — this is spec-alignment/defense-in-depth, not a live bug. Fix: thread discovered plugin ADAPTERS (not just names) into prune_pipeline and build live_roots_by_owner from the discovered set. Found in quality-review of the install-receipt-pruning feature.

title: Receipt prune: directory-level ownership-drift protection (recursive digest)
description: Follow-up to the install receipt (abn9.37.1.2). v1 ships hash-aware protection for FILE orphans: a file orphan whose on-disk bytes differ from the recorded sha256 is relinquished (kept, not deleted). DIRECTORY orphans have no single digest in v1, so they still go through backup-and-delete. This bead extends drift protection to directories: record a recursive digest (or per-file inner manifest) for dir entries and refuse auto-delete when the directory's contents have drifted from the recorded owned state. Scope narrowed from the original 'all sha256-aware prune' now that the file case is in v1.

title: Integration tests: rules inline/drop across all tools + plugin rules via stage_and_transform
description: Discovered during q1q6r (codex + quality adversarial review). The rules-inline/drop fix is unit-pinned at the flatten layer (test_templates_flatten_plan.py) and has one real build_plan->flatten integration test for OpenCode (test_staging_opencode.py). MISSING: full stage_and_transform end-to-end coverage proving (1) Claude keeps loose rules, (2) Codex/Gemini/OpenCode inline+drop, (3) a PLUGIN rule overlaid into a Codex/Gemini/OpenCode plan is inlined+dropped, (4) a plugin rule overlaid into Claude survives, (5) optional: --prune sees stale loose codex/gemini rules as orphans. The drop is provenance-agnostic so plugin rules are handled by the same code path, but no test pins the overlay->flatten->drop chain end-to-end. Not a correctness gap (logic verified by two adversarial reviews); additive confidence. Needs a hermetic repo+plugin fixture.

title: Installer: wrap rule content in XML-tagged blocks in assembled output
description: When rules are inlined into AGENTS.md (or equivalent) via DYNAMIC-INCLUDE-ALL-RULES, each rule's content should be wrapped in an XML-boxed segment (e.g. <rule name="completion-gate">...</rule>) rather than concatenated as raw markdown. Benefits: tools that parse structured context can identify rule boundaries; easier to audit which rules are active; consistent with how Claude's system prompt uses XML structuring for semantic grouping. Needs: design for tag naming convention, whether the rule filename or a frontmatter field is the tag name, and impact on existing rule-reading tooling.

title: Explore: ~/.agents/ as canonical base with symlinks from tool-specific dirs
description: Instead of copying files to ~/.claude/, ~/.codex/, ~/.gemini/, etc. independently, explore using ~/.agents/ as the single canonical location and symlinking from each tool's dir into it. Potential benefits: a single source of truth on disk, no drift between tool installations, simpler install/update model. Risks: tools that write back to their config dirs would follow symlinks; some tools may not tolerate symlinked config files. Needs feasibility audit per tool before committing to design.

title: Installer: --diff mode to show delta between installed files and staged content
description: Add a --diff flag that compares each target file against the staged content and prints a unified diff (or summary) without writing anything. Useful for users who have hand-modified installed files and want to see what an install run would change before committing to it. Complements --dry-run (which only shows create/update/skip counts) with actual content-level visibility.

title: Installer: skip backup when target file is byte-equivalent to source
description: The installer currently creates a backup of the target file before overwriting, even when the staged content is identical to what is already on disk. This creates spurious backup files and noise. Fix: compare the staged bytes against the target bytes before the backup/write step; skip both backup and write when they are identical. This is distinct from the 'up-to-date' skip that already exists — that skip happens earlier (before staging); this fix applies when a file reaches the write step but happens to be identical.

title: Test-hardening follow-ups for .installignore manifest
description:
Deferred low-value nits from quality-review + simplify of agents-config-37sep (none block merge):
- L2: .installignore content is hand-duplicated in 3 hermetic test fixtures (conftest ignore fixture, test_installignore_parity, test_cli_smoke _write_installignore). A shared helper reading the repo-root manifest would prevent drift on NEW exclusion classes (live-leaker regression already guarded by test_installignore_invariant).
- N1a: no test for present-but-unreadable manifest (chmod 000) on either installer — fail-fast is correct but unpinned (deferred due to chmod-test flakiness; gate as os.access/root-skip if added).
- N1b: --dump-stage against a manifest-less repo is only transitively covered for exit-2.
- N2: installer/core/installignore.py loader docstring names FileNotFoundError/PermissionError/OSError; CLI catches OSError base. Consider an unreadable-file test to lock the except-OSError contract.
Also consider documenting in packages/installer/AGENTS.md: subprocess-invoking tests must resolve the executable to a full path (shutil.which) to satisfy bandit S607 under the strict ruff gate.
notes: Convention to document in AGENTS.md (surfaced by PR #190 round-2 Copilot review): bash helpers under scripts/lib/ MUST be zsh-safe because install.sh re-execs into zsh (preferred) on shells lacking bash-4 assoc arrays. Specifically: associative-array STORE subscripts must be unquoted (arr[$key]=1, not arr["$key"]=1) — zsh embeds literal quotes in the key otherwise while lookups use [$1] unquoted, so matches silently miss. Fixed in 197a13a; cross-shell regression now in installignore_test.sh. CI missed it because the test env has bash 4+ (no re-exec).
close_reason: Delivered in PR #199 (squash debf678): merge observability + symlink parity + installignore test-hardening. make ci-installer green (607 passed, 99.77% cov); Copilot review clean.

title: Dead single-file sync() path — delete or guard against SETTINGS_JSON
description: quality-reviewer (H.3) LOW: the standalone sync() in core/sync.py (the B.2 minimal single-file path) has ZERO production callers (test-only) and does a pure sha-256 byte compare with NO SETTINGS_JSON branch — so it would byte-overwrite a settings.json rather than merge or apply the new invalid-JSON skip guard. Not a bug today (nothing routes settings through it), but a latent footgun: a future caller could reintroduce the clobber bug H.3 just fixed. Fix: delete sync() if superseded by sync_plan, or assert it must never receive FileKind.SETTINGS_JSON.
close_reason: Fixed in PR #196 (squash-merged). Guard sync() against FileKind.SETTINGS_JSON sources via classify_file, raising ValueError before any read/write so the merge-aware sync_plan path cannot be bypassed. Chose guard over delete: sync() is dead production code but the sole exerciser of back_up()'s namespace backup-routing (shared live-path logic) across 28 tests. Gate green; quality-reviewer (no blocking, 2 polish items applied) + simplify clean; Copilot clean.

title: Golden-master coverage hardening — deferred parity scenarios
description: Parity audit (H.3) found real but deferrable coverage gaps in packages/installer/tests/golden_master. Add scenarios when their paths go live: (1) multi-tool combined --tools=claude,codex,gemini,opencode in one run (per-tool overlay+flatten never jointly exercised; most-used real invocation); (2) carrier-merge byte-parity (shared-carrier skill/agent dir + disjoint plugin dir; DEAD today, src/plugins empty); (3) settings deep-merge depth>1 object overlap + scalar-collision keep-existing branch; (4) JSONC update-on-diff + JSONC/TOML staging last-wins-warn collision (unreachable from shipped config today); (5) prune-list literal tool/ns/base glob form (matcher proven by wildcard scenarios). All structurally protected or dead in production; non-blocker for cutover.
close_reason: Not parity coverage — a 'write tests when X ships' reminder for dead/future Python paths, mis-homed under the retired golden_master suite (empty dir; README: 'golden-master suite retired (H.4)'). None of its 5 scenarios run bash. Re-file per-feature when each path activates; provenance preserved via discovered-from w1qls.8.3.

title: prgroom: verb remote side-effects are non-atomic vs single persist → duplicate replies on retry
description: Codex adversarial review (PR #174, gpt-5.5) MEDIUM finding. prgroom verbs perform remote side effects (POST replies, GraphQL thread replies, PR-body PATCH) then return a deepcopy the run loop persists once on success. On partial failure (first POST ok, a later call fails) the verb raises; _execute_step persists the pre-verb state, so replied=False / pending_memory survive and the next run re-posts the already-sent reply → duplicate. This is the systemic verb-atomicity model (deepcopy → effects → single persist) shared by EVERY prgroom verb, not unique to reply_pr. Options: (a) crash-idempotent remote ops via stable hidden idempotency markers + pre-post existence check; (b) per-effect progress persistence before later remote calls. Add a partial-failure test (one reply ok, later call fails, rerun must not duplicate). Deferred from PR #174 by Scott.

title: CLI output parity: prune-exclusion warning + 'merged' counter
description: H.1 audit, cosmetic. (G7) Python emits no warning when prune excludes an item; bash does. (G8) Python summary lacks the 'merged' counter bash prints. Neither affects installed bytes - post-cutover-acceptable polish. Fix: add both to the Python output.

title: prgroom residual write-path edge-case coverage (PR-closed-mid-run, state-write failure, externally-resolved thread, detached-HEAD)
description:
The 'what else' edge cases from the 8.16 error audit that aren't already inside cwnh2 (fail-fast fixes) or s0u9c (preflight gate). Each needs a behavior decision THEN a test:

1. PR closed/merged (not deleted) between _poll and a write verb. GhNotFound (cwnh2 #3) covers DELETION (404), but a closed-but-present PR is not 404 - git push to a closed PR's branch silently succeeds and does nothing useful. Decide: re-check PR state in the write verbs (or rely on the run loop's per-cycle _poll) and add a test.

2. State-write failure mid-verb (disk full / EIO on store.write). store.write is in the CLI closure; an IOError is not a PrgroomError, so it would escape as a raw traceback (same class as the GhNotFound gap). Decide a tagged mapping (STATE_CORRUPT or a transient I/O code) and test it.

3. resolveReviewThread on a thread resolved/deleted externally. Currently RUNTIME_GRAPHQL_FAILED (transient-then-escalate); 'already resolved' is arguably idempotent success, not a failure. Decide (treat already-resolved as success vs transient) and test.

4. Detached-HEAD push: current_branch() returns the literal 'HEAD' which mismatches the PR branch -> PRECONDITION_WRONG_BRANCH. Same code path as wrong-branch but add an explicit detached-HEAD test (branch=='HEAD').

Cross-refs: cwnh2 (unauth telemetry, not-a-repo terminal, push-permission terminal, GhNotFound-in-write-verb), s0u9c (repo-identity, auth preflight). Together these three beads fully cover the 8.16 'edge cases worth a test' list.

title: prgroom fix: nested claude -p agent can't git commit under a parent Claude Code sandbox — run fix in an unsandboxed context
description: Surfaced by the M2 live demo (poll->cluster->fix against a real PR). The fix agent (a nested 'claude -p' subprocess dispatched by SubprocessAgentRunner) correctly read the snapshot, implemented the requested fix, wrote a regression test, and ran pytest (passed) — but every 'git commit' failed with 'Operation not permitted' creating '.git/worktrees/<wt>/index.lock'. The nested 'claude -p' establishes its OWN sandbox that makes the worktree's git metadata read-only, independent of the parent Bash tool's sandbox flag. prgroom's fix-contract correctly failed-safe (disposition=failed with a precise rationale; NO fabricated 'fixed'), and stayed read-only toward GitHub (nothing pushed). Net: when prgroom is invoked from within a Claude Code session, the fix agent cannot commit. A production/scheduler run (not nested in Claude Code) is unaffected. Action for the run loop (8.10) / overnight autonomy (M4): execute fix in a context where the nested agent can write git (bare scheduler/cron, or grant the agent git-write), and/or document the constraint in the prgroom runbook. Not a prgroom code defect; an execution-environment constraint. The demo otherwise PASSED: poll populated a live PRRT_* thread_id (qmoz5), cluster grouped items, the agent produced a correct+tested fix, dispositions applied, nothing pushed.
notes: Triage (2026-06-13): P2->P3. Not a prgroom defect and production-unaffected, so not P2 as a work item. The design-level risk is now carried in 8.10's notes (so the run-loop implementer designs for the writable-context requirement). Residual scope of THIS bead: (1) document the constraint in the prgroom runbook; (2) verify fix->commit end-to-end in a non-nested execution context (scheduler/cron) as part of 8.10 / M4 acceptance.

title: prgroom snapshot: hoist per-cluster PR-level reads (snapshot + thread-id map) to once-per-fix-cycle
description: fix_pr assembles a fresh snapshot per cluster; assemble_snapshot re-reads the PR resource + pulls/{n}/comments (REST) AND now the reviewThreads thread-id map (GraphQL) once per cluster. With N clusters the same PR-level reads run N times. Pre-existing per-cluster redundancy (deliberate, to minimise staleness per snapshot.py docstring); the qmoz5 GraphQL map inherits it. Optimization: hoist the PR-level reads (and the thread-id map) to once per fix cycle, passing the shared result into each cluster's assemble_snapshot, keeping only genuinely per-cluster work inside the loop. Cross-fetch note: hoisting the map to once-per-cycle would also close the poll/snapshot pagination-boundary divergence noted in qmoz5's review. Surfaced by the qmoz5 3-lens review (Lens B efficiency).
notes: Triage (2026-06-13): linked related-to j8pdq. Both edit snapshot.py's PR-level read path — j8pdq adds --paginate (correctness: whole-PR, not page-1); this bead hoists those reads + the thread-id map to once-per-cycle (efficiency: kill N-cluster redundancy). The pagination-boundary divergence this bead's desc cites is exactly j8pdq's domain. Distinct work but bundle into one pass to avoid touching that path twice.

title: Harden is_safe_relpath with resolve-and-contain (symlink defense-in-depth)
description: installer.core.paths.is_safe_relpath is a LEXICAL guard: it rejects absolute paths and any '..' component, but does not resolve symlinks. A symlink under a trusted base dir could still let base/relpath escape base at runtime. Copilot flagged this on PR #146 (round 1); the docstrings were clarified to state the lexical limitation, and the predicate was kept lexical by design (current threat model: trusted source tree). This bead tracks optional defense-in-depth: add a resolved-path containment check (e.g. (base/rel).resolve() relative_to base.resolve()) at the I/O boundary for callers that join under attacker-influenced trees. Scope: decide separate resolve-and-contain helper vs tighten is_safe_relpath; keep the pure lexical predicate for pre-I/O validation.

title: Consolidate path-traversal relpath guard into shared helper
description: The invariant `path.is_absolute() or '..' in path.parts` is triplicated: core/sync.py:44, core/templates.py:130, plugins/extensions.py:119 (F.5 added the third). Extract a single `assert_safe_relpath`/`is_safe_relpath` into a shared module (e.g. core/paths.py); each call site keeps its own contextual error wrap. Security-relevant guard — should have exactly one definition. Surfaced by F.5 simplify reuse review; deferred to avoid scope creep into untouched modules.

title: Carrier-merge bash-parity hardening: symlink handling (L1) + settings-glob superset (L2)
description:
Carrier-merge bash-parity hardening — two Low findings from the F.3 quality review (non-blocking; merged as-is).

L1 — `core/overlay.py` `_carry_files` follows symlinks: a symlinked file is read through to its target's bytes, and the walk descends symlinked directories. Bash `cp -R` (macOS) preserves symlinks as symlinks. The port materializes link targets where bash would copy the link. Not a live vulnerability (plugin trees are git-tracked; generated relpath keys are confined, no `..`/absolute escape) — but a parity divergence. Fix option: skip symlinks in the walk (`p for p in entry.rglob('*') if p.is_file() and not p.is_symlink()`), or document the dereference as intentional.

L2 — `core/overlay.py` plugin tool-settings staging calls `stage_settings`, which globs json + jsonc + toml template forms; bash Phase 6 plugin-settings injection (scripts/install.sh:829) globs only `*.json.template`. The Python is a harmless superset (no current plugin ships jsonc/toml settings, so behaviour is identical today). Fix: narrow to json-only for parity, or add a comment noting the intentional widening.

Both are parity nuances the golden-master parity suite (Epic H) will systematically catch; filed here so the cutover gate carries them explicitly. Low priority — polish, not correctness.
close_reason: Delivered in PR #199 (squash debf678): merge observability + symlink parity + installignore test-hardening. make ci-installer green (607 passed, 99.77% cov); Copilot review clean.

title: Fix AGENTS.md drift: 'Repository Structure' claims src/plugins/beads/ has content (it is empty)
description:
Doc drift in the repo-root AGENTS.md "Repository Structure" section.

It states: "src/plugins/ — Optional plugin content … beads/ — beads plugin: skills, Claude rules, formulas." But `src/plugins/beads/` does not exist — only `src/plugins/AGENTS.md` is present (`git ls-files src/plugins/` returns one file). The beads plugin's actual content (.beads/formulas/*.toml, .beads/scripts/*.sh, skills, rules) has not yet been authored; only the installer adapter (F.4) that would route it exists.

Fix: correct the "Repository Structure" entry to describe src/plugins/ as currently holding only AGENTS.md (plugin content forthcoming), so the map matches the territory. One-line doc edit; can be batched with other doc-sweep work.

Discovered during F.4 (BeadsPlugin adapter) — the adapter resolves paths relative to a not-yet-populated source tree.

title: json_union _union_arrays: dedupe within sort-key group, not just adjacent pairs
description: In packages/installer/src/installer/core/merge/strategies/json_union.py, _union_arrays dedupes by comparing each element to only its immediate predecessor (key != last_key or elem != last_elem). The sort orders by jq sort key alone, so distinct Python values that share a sort key are not guaranteed adjacent. For float()-overflow integers (all mapped to +/-inf), a repeated value separated by a distinct same-key value survives as a surplus duplicate. Example: [10**400, 10**401, 10**400] keeps all three; true jq unique (and this module's preserve-distinct-values intent) yields [10**400, 10**401]. Severity: surplus duplicate, NOT data loss; unreachable in the settings.json domain. Fix: group by sort key, dedupe within each group by value membership (~3 lines). Discovered during PR #130 round-5 review (merged squash e62c62b51acbc3b3d152da6030fa6a86f6affb85); filed low priority per YAGNI since the data-loss bug it descends from is already fixed.
acceptance_criteria: _union_arrays returns no surplus duplicate for an array containing 3+ float()-overflow integers with a non-adjacent repeat; a regression test pins [10**400, 10**401, 10**400] -> [10**400, 10**401].
close_reason: Already resolved by #164 (c19c5b7, 2026-06-15), filed 2026-06-07. That PR ('json_union semantic deep-merge, drop jq-byte-parity') deleted the sort-by-jq-key + adjacent dedupe (last_key) implementation this bead describes, replacing it with full-membership dedupe (elem not in deduped). The non-adjacency defect is structurally gone. Verified empirically: _union_arrays([10**400,10**401,10**400],[]) == [10**400,10**401] (the bead's exact acceptance case). No regression test added: against full-membership dedupe, the non-adjacent case tests Python's 'in' operator, not a coded decision (tautology). General union-dedupe behavior already pinned by 13 tests in test_json_union.py.

title: PDLC architecture far-horizon register
description:
## What this is

A **register** of architectural questions, cross-epic seams, missing personas, and project-config gaps that surfaced during the PDLC tracer-scoping sweep (2026-05-25) but are **not blockers for the PDLC tracer**. Each item is recorded here in coarse-grained form so it is not lost.

When any item becomes near-horizon (i.e. some specific implementation work is about to touch it), it is promoted into its own bead and removed from this register.

This bead is **informational, not action**. It does not block any other bead. It does not have a target completion. It is the index that lets future work find the relevant question quickly.

## Two-horizon framing

- **Near-horizon** (acted on during the 2026-05-25 surgery): Holding Place peer epic (`agents-config-wgclw.10`), Promote-contract bead, ADR-0001.
- **Far-horizon** (this register): everything below.

## Cross-epic seams without contract beads

| ID | Seam | Producer | Consumer | Note |
|---|---|---|---|---|
| S1 | Spec file format read-side | `wgclw.3` | `wgclw.2` | `wgclw.3` owns the schema; `wgclw.2` reads it to dispatch personas. Versioning, forward-compat, validation contract unspecified. |
| S2 | `worker-report-v1` ↔ PDLC dispatch | `agents-config-7bk.19` (M1) | `wgclw.2` | M0 milestone description already flags this as "needs clarification in M0 phase 1." Cross-milestone seam. |
| S3 | Reviewer Toolbox dispatch protocol | `wgclw.4` | `wgclw.2` | `wgclw.4` owns toolboxes; `wgclw.2` dispatches them. Protocol unspecified. |
| S4 | Finding payload schema | `wgclw.4` | `wgclw.5` | Mechanical/Advisory/Proposed Rule classification emitted in `.4`; consumed at merge in `.5` (Advisory → Idea via Holding Place `create_idea`). Schema unspecified. |
| S5 | RCA input-data schema | Orchestrator + `wgclw.3` + `wgclw.4` | `wgclw.6` | RCA agents read Spec + strike history + gate-failure + worktree state. Aggregate input contract unspecified. |
| S6 | HEP cross-milestone seam | `agents-config-abn9.28` (M1) | `wgclw.5` Stage B | Does Stage B consume the M1 `bd-hep-escalate.sh` helper or rebuild? |
| S7 | Copilot adapter wire format | external Copilot | `wgclw.5` Stage A | May be subsumable by prgroom's PR-grooming CLI (`fca6` epic). Open architectural question. |
| S8 | WorkTracker abstraction ↔ bd-shim CLI | `wgclw.2` | `wgclw.9` | `wgclw.9`'s own description carries the unresolved Open Design Question: "when should PDLC commit to consuming this shim vs continuing to bind bd directly?" |

## Personas without persona-spec beads

Each persona below is implicit in its parent epic's scope but has no dedicated spec bead. Pattern recognised (same shape as Holding Place's gap before resolution). Defer-by-default: file lazily as decomposition reaches each persona.

| Persona | Defined in | Pinned by |
|---|---|---|
| Idea Curator | `wgclw.10` (Holding Place) + `CONTEXT.md` | epic scope only |
| Design Interrogator | `wgclw.3` + `CONTEXT.md` | epic scope + glossary |
| Decomposition Architect | `wgclw.3` + `CONTEXT.md` | epic scope + glossary |
| Test-Author Agent | `wgclw.4` + `CONTEXT.md` | epic scope + glossary |
| Implementer Agent | `wgclw.4` + `CONTEXT.md` | epic scope + glossary |
| Reviewer Agent (configurable list) | `wgclw.4` + `CONTEXT.md` | epic scope + glossary |
| Reviewer Toolbox sub-personas (Code Quality, Security, Performance, API Contract, Documentation) | `wgclw.4` + `CONTEXT.md` | epic scope + glossary |
| Specification RCA Agent | `wgclw.6` + `CONTEXT.md` | epic scope + glossary |
| Architecture Health RCA Agent | `wgclw.6` + `CONTEXT.md` | epic scope + glossary |

Only existing persona-spec bead: `agents-config-n7q0p` (ToolingFixer, P3, post-MVP, deferred until after `wgclw.2.3` lands).

## Project-config keys not yet covered

`wgclw.2`'s listed schema scope: Reviewer Agent list, Sizing Gate weights/thresholds, coverage thresholds, `approval_required` defaults, aging-nag thresholds. The "etc." has these orphans:

| Key | Referenced in | Owning epic (per scope) |
|---|---|---|
| `review_max_rounds` | `state-machine.md` L97, L226 | unstated |
| Merge strategy (squash/ff/rebase) | `wgclw.5` scope | `wgclw.2` (per "per project-config" language) |
| Reviewer Toolbox per-domain enable list | `wgclw.4` scope | `wgclw.2` or `wgclw.4` (TBD) |
| Finding discrimination rules (Mechanical vs Advisory) | `wgclw.4` + `CONTEXT.md > Finding` | `wgclw.4` or `wgclw.2` (TBD) |
| Resolution Route per-route config | `wgclw.6` scope | unstated |
| Decomposition Plan exit-criteria thresholds | `wgclw.3` scope | unstated |

Already covered by `wgclw.2.3`: `tooling_max_strikes`, `toolchain.required`, `toolchain.auto_install_permitted`.

Layered-config future work: `agents-config-p2dq8` (P3, ".local + user-defaults overlay").

## Architectural questions

- **Q1** — Does `wgclw.5`'s Copilot adapter subsume prgroom's PR-grooming, or live alongside?
- **Q2** — When does `wgclw.2`'s WorkTracker commit to consuming `wgclw.9`'s bd-shim CLI? (Mirrors `wgclw.9`'s Open Design Question #6.)

## How to use this register

- **Reading**: search for the topic; the register points to the relevant epic, glossary entry, or spec section.
- **Promoting an item**: when an item becomes near-horizon, file a dedicated bead and remove the entry here (`bd update` the description, NOT a new comment).
- **Adding an item**: when a new architectural question surfaces during ongoing work, add it here rather than letting it slip into the parent epic's notes.

## Provenance

Created during the PDLC tracer-scoping grill session (2026-05-25). Sweep methodology: read the `## In scope` sections of `wgclw.3/.4/.5/.6` against `CONTEXT.md` and the orchestrator HLD artifacts under `docs/architecture/pdlc-orchestrator/`, classifying every gap into four categories — peer-subsystem scope claims, cross-epic seams without contract beads, personas without persona-spec beads, project-config keys without an owner.

title: Installer: pin cross-tree relocation prune behavior (regression test; bug subsumed by receipt prune)
description:
## Observed

After moving src/user/.agents/skills/zoom-out → src/user/.claude/skills/zoom-out (closeout of agents-config-0ysqf), the previously-staged copies at:

- ~/.codex/skills/zoom-out
- ~/.gemini/skills/zoom-out
- ~/.config/opencode/skills/zoom-out

are now stale: the source no longer fans out to those tools, but the prior install left a copy that none of those tools should be auto-invoking (the skill's frontmatter sets disable-model-invocation: true, which non-Claude tools silently ignore).

scripts/install.sh --dry-run --verbose --prune reports "No orphans detected" against all four tools, with Pruned: 0.

## Expected

After a relocation that narrows a shared skill to a single tool, the orphan scan should flag the now-stale destination copies in the tools that no longer receive the skill.

## Hypothesis

scan_orphans (install.sh ~L1523) compares dest namespaces against the merged source set. If the merge logic treats "any source tree contains this name" as a hit, a cross-tree relocation (shared → claude-only) leaves the dest copies in non-Claude tools matching against the Claude source — i.e. the orphan-detector cannot distinguish "this name appears in src/user/.claude/" from "this name is staged into ~/.codex/".

## Repro

In a checkout where src/user/.claude/skills/zoom-out exists and src/user/.agents/skills/zoom-out is absent (post-merge of the closeout-0ysqf branch), with prior installs to all four tools:

    scripts/install.sh --dry-run --verbose --prune --tools=claude,codex,gemini,opencode

Expected output to include orphan entries for ~/.codex/skills/zoom-out, ~/.gemini/skills/zoom-out, ~/.config/opencode/skills/zoom-out. Actual: "No orphans detected".

## Acceptance

- scan_orphans tracks per-tool effective source set (claude-only skills do NOT count as source presence for codex/gemini/opencode).
- Post-relocation dry-run with --prune lists the stale per-tool dest copies as orphans.
- Test fixture (or smoke probe) demonstrates the case.
notes:
RE-EVAL (2026-06-22): originally recorded against bash install.sh, which H.4 collapsed to an exec stub (logic now in packages/installer). Re-evaluate against the Python installer's prune/orphan path (core/sync.py prune + installer.toml prune-list). Related: 23k33, qd8ca, kdh1h. Confirm whether the cross-tree relocation orphan gap reproduces in the Python prune before doing work.
SPIKE VERDICT (2026-06-27): Bug DOES NOT reproduce in the Python receipt-based prune (PR #200 / 26353f5). Three-thread bugfix investigation converged:
- Data-flow: orphan detection is a per-owner tuple diff at receipt_diff.py:100 (if (e.owner,e.path) in desired_keys) — NO merged-source name check, which was the entire bash-era root cause. Bug is structurally impossible in the receipt model.
- Repro: prune_pipeline-level test of the zoom-out shared->Claude-only relocation PASSES; codex/gemini/opencode stale copies pruned, Claude kept.
- Archaeology: design doc 2026-06-25-install-receipt-pruning-design.md lines 23-24/189-190 names the zoom-out case; commit 26353f5 states it 'subsumes cr7bi'.
RESIDUAL: design line 210 case (c) MANDATED a cross-tree relocation regression test that was never written. Recommend converting this bug to: lift the spike's passing test into packages/installer/tests/unit/. Optional belt-and-suspenders: full cli.main end-to-end from a real source fixture (spike drove the prune layer with a hand-built receipt, not source-tree scanning).

title: Resolve zoom-out frontmatter contradiction with shared-vs-Claude-only rule
description:
## Decision (2026-05-25)

Resolution path **A** chosen: move `zoom-out` from `src/user/.agents/skills/` to `src/user/.claude/skills/`.

Rationale: `disable-model-invocation: true` in the frontmatter declares user-triggered-only intent. Other tools (Codex / Gemini / OpenCode) silently ignore the key and would auto-invoke the skill against project intent, so portability adds nothing here. Moving honours both the stated rule and the skill author intent.

## Context (original finding)

`src/user/.agents/skills/AGENTS.md` shared-skill contract states:

> Skills whose Claude-specific features (`!`-command syntax, `disable-model-invocation`, `allowed-tools`) preclude shared deployment live under `src/user/.claude/skills/` instead.

But `src/user/.agents/skills/zoom-out/SKILL.md` has `disable-model-invocation: true` and lives in the shared tree — direct contradiction.

## Plan steps

1. Move the folder: `git mv src/user/.agents/skills/zoom-out/ src/user/.claude/skills/zoom-out/`
2. Remove the `zoom-out` row from the "Skill provenance registry" table in `src/user/.agents/skills/AGENTS.md`.
3. Add a `zoom-out` row to the "Claude-only OSS-derived skills" table in the same file (`Location: src/user/.claude/skills/zoom-out/`; existing snapshot/upstream/last-sync/drift-policy values carry over).
4. Verify the skill provenance HTML-comment header in the moved SKILL.md still resolves to the same snapshot path (it should — the relative-path resolution does not change).
5. Run `scripts/install.sh --dry-run` and confirm `zoom-out` no longer stages into `~/.codex/skills/`, `~/.gemini/skills/`, or `~/.config/opencode/skills/`; only into `~/.claude/skills/`.

## Acceptance

- Folder moved; git history preserved via rename.
- Registry rows updated (one removed from standard table, one added to Claude-only table).
- `install.sh --dry-run` shows `zoom-out` staged into Claude only.
close_reason: Already complete via PR #107 (commit 91c4345, 'relocate zoom-out to Claude-only tree'). Verified on origin/main: (1) folder moved to src/user/.claude/skills/zoom-out/ — no copy remains under src/user/.agents/skills/ or any non-Claude tree; (2) removed from the standard 'Skill provenance registry' table; (3) added to the 'Claude-only OSS-derived skills' table (src/user/.agents/skills/AGENTS.md:81) with Location src/user/.claude/skills/zoom-out/; (4) provenance header 'Source: oss-snapshots/pocock/zoom-out/' resolves (snapshot dir present); (5) Claude-only staging guaranteed by architecture since the source now lives only in the .claude tree. NOTE: the stale dest copies in ~/.codex|.gemini|.config/opencode left by this relocation are the subject of held bead cr7bi (orphan-scan miss).

title: Decide pocock/write-a-skill verdict — compare against writing-skills amalgam
description:
## Decision (2026-05-25)

**Close as won't-fix.** Update `oss-snapshots/pocock/AGENTS.md` row for `write-a-skill/` to a final verdict so the row stops carrying an open-ended deferral.

Rationale: the in-tree `writing-skills` skill is already an amalgam of `obra/superpowers/writing-skills` + `anthropics/skill-creator` (the two most comprehensive upstream sources on skill authoring). The marginal value of comparing a third upstream against it does not justify the agent-time cost. Future pocock snapshot re-syncs will surface any large drift; a per-skill diff is the right trigger point, not a scheduled comparison.

## Context (original finding)

`oss-snapshots/pocock/AGENTS.md` row for `write-a-skill/` was deferred during cx6.7.5 with "Compare against existing `writing-skills` amalgam; if a delta is worth pulling in, file a follow-up." No follow-up was filed; the cx6.7.7 close-out audit surfaced this bead to track the comparison.

## Plan steps

1. Update the `write-a-skill/` row in `oss-snapshots/pocock/AGENTS.md` from "deferred — verdict open" to "**dropped — covered by `writing-skills` amalgam**" with a one-line rationale: "the in-tree `writing-skills` skill amalgamates `superpowers/writing-skills` + `anthropics/skill-creator`; pocock/write-a-skill is unlikely to contribute meaningful deltas. Revisit on future pocock re-sync if upstream changes substantially."
2. Close this bead with the same rationale.

## Acceptance

- pocock/AGENTS.md row updated to "dropped" with rationale.
- Bead closed.
- No skill or registry changes (the upstream snapshot folder stays as a reference baseline).

title: Bridge Claude-only tool names in writing-skills/references/persuasion-principles.md for cross-tool runtimes
description:
## Context

Discovered during cx6.7.14 (session primer lift). The session primer's "Platform Adaptation" pointer was dropped because upstream's references/copilot-tools.md and references/codex-tools.md don't exist in-tree. However, the in-tree skill `writing-skills/references/persuasion-principles.md` still references `TodoWrite` (a Claude-Code-specific tool name) at lines 45, 92, 93.

For agents running on Codex CLI, Gemini CLI, or OpenCode, the `TodoWrite` references will look like dangling tool names — the agents may infer the principle correctly from context (checklist tracking) but the bare tool name carries no semantic weight.

## What to do

Option A: Generalize the references to "task-tracking mechanism" (or similar) so they're tool-agnostic.

Option B: Add a small platform-adaptation table (Claude Code → TodoWrite, Codex → ?, Gemini → ?, OpenCode → ?) so each runtime can map the term.

Option C: Accept the leak — TodoWrite is well-known enough that cross-tool agents will understand the intent from context, and persuasion-principles is a reference doc rather than a hot-path skill.

Decide and apply.

## Why it's separate from cx6.7.14

cx6.7.14 scope was the primer lift. This is a separate cross-tool tool-name leak in an unrelated skill — sibling test: this would NOT have been in cx6.7.14's original plan.

## File

`src/user/.agents/skills/writing-skills/references/persuasion-principles.md` (lines 45, 92, 93)

title: ToolingFixer persona: autonomous tooling remediation (post-MVP)
description:
A persona dispatched on tooling-class failures (between Layer 1's detection and Layer 2's escalation as documented in docs/specs/2026-05-23-pdlc-orchestrator-core-design.md § Tooling-failure handling) that attempts a closed taxonomy of remediation operations:

- npm install / yarn install
- pip install -r requirements.txt
- make setup
- (other package-manager installs declared in project-config)

Permission gate: project-config.toolchain.auto_install_permitted controls whether this persona is enabled at all. When disabled (default), failures escalate via Layer 2 as today.

Acceptance criteria:
- ToolingFixer persona defined alongside Test-Author, Implementer, Reviewer
- Dispatched on first tooling-strike (before tooling_strike_count would otherwise increment)
- Bounded by its own strike count (so it can't loop forever either)
- Successful remediation re-dispatches the original stage with cleared strike counter
- Failed remediation falls through to Layer 2 escalation
- Permission gate honoured: when disabled, this persona never dispatches

Dependencies:
- Blocked on agents-config-wgclw.2.3 (Layer 1 + Layer 2 must exist first)
