notes: Discovered during wgclw.2.1 HLD review. Documented as out-of-MVP-scope in the orchestrator core design spec and in the state-machine HLD's Tooling-failure handling subsection. Filed here so the work has a tracked home; activation deferred until after wgclw.2.3 lands and we have data on whether human-escalation friction warrants the additional machinery.

title: Add gate_trust_mode toggle: verify | trust_evidence (post-MVP gate-redundancy optimization)
description:
Today's MVP design has the orchestrator re-execute mid-pipeline gates (TEST_AUTHORING → IMPLEMENTING handoff, REVIEWING → PR_VALIDATION handoff) rather than trust the worker's claim that the gate passed. This is the right MVP default — agent self-report cannot be the trust anchor — but it costs seconds-to-minutes per transition for nontrivial suites.

The optimization: introduce a project-config toggle 'gate_trust_mode' with values:
- 'verify' (MVP default): re-execute the gate locally before transitioning
- 'trust_evidence': inspect JobSupervisor-captured gate artifacts (exit_code, start_ts, end_ts, commit_sha_at_end, gate_cmd) and skip re-execution if all confidence predicates pass

Trust anchor: NOT the worker report. The JobSupervisor owns gate-evidence file handles out-of-band of the worker process, so its captured artifacts cannot be fabricated by a confidently-wrong worker. The worker report merely *points* to supervisor-owned paths.

Confidence predicates (orchestrator-side, when in trust_evidence mode):
- exit_code == 0
- commit_sha_at_end == current HEAD on the worktree branch (no untracked drift between gate run and now)
- gate_cmd matches the expected gate spec
- no concurrent worktree mutation between end_ts and now
- (optional, paranoid) re-hash stdout/stderr paths against fingerprints in the supervisor artifact manifest

Prerequisites before this can land:
1. JobSupervisor evidence-capture component must be implemented and stable (see c4-l3-jobsupervisor.md stub)
2. Worker report contract must already carry gate_evidence pointers (road-paved in wgclw.2.1 HLD amendments)
3. Per-project-config 'gate_trust_mode' field added to project-config.toml schema

Out of scope for this bead:
- PR_VALIDATION gate (already CI-delegated; not orchestrator-re-run)
- Post-mortem 'AUTOPSY' gates (require fresh execution by design)

Discovered during wgclw.2.1 HLD review when sir asked whether worker reports could carry gate-evidence references to enable optional skipping of orchestrator re-validation, reducing friction on path to merge.
notes: Tracked here so the wgclw.2.1 HLD can cite a concrete bead ID for the 'post-MVP optimization' marker rather than leaving it as anonymous future work. The road-paving in the HLD (worker-report gate_evidence schema + JobSupervisor capture component) lands as part of wgclw.2.1; this bead is the activation work.

title: pr-comment-fixer-team writes YAML but audit-subagent-report.sh expects JSON
description: When wait-for-pr-comments dispatches a pr-comment-fixer-team subagent, the subagent writes its report in YAML format but audit-subagent-report.sh uses jq (JSON parser) to read it. The jq silently returns empty on YAML input, causing schema validation to fail with 'missing required field: comment_id'. Discovered during PR #96 (wgclw.2 Round-1 revision) -- all 9 subagent reports were YAML, so all audits failed at the jq parse step. Workaround: manual audit of report content was used. Fix: either (a) make pr-comment-fixer-team write JSON reports, or (b) make audit-subagent-report.sh parse YAML via a proper YAML parser.
notes: Retired in triage 2026-06-27: subsumed by prgroom (abn9.8 implementation umbrella). prgroom replaces the artifact this bug lives in (typed FixInput/FixOutput contracts, ItemKind.REVIEW_SUMMARY, 6-priority phase resolver) so the bug does not carry forward. Reopen if prgroom slips and the old skill path keeps biting.

title: Spec-modularisation skill for large PDLC specs
description: A skill (or convention) for splitting large design specs into mechanically-stitchable modules so individual concept areas can be revised in isolation without re-reading the entire spec. Captured from Round-1 review of agents-config-wgclw.2 (recommendation R2.1).
notes: Closed as duplicate of agents-config-3f52 (spec-modularization skill) per triage 2026-06-27. PDLC-specific angle folded into 3f52.

title: PDLC Orchestrator: multi-reviewer / codeowner-aware Reviewer state generalisation
description: Generalise the single-Reviewer persona into a multi-reviewer model that respects code-ownership (CODEOWNERS, etc.) for parallel review of multi-subsystem changes. Captured from Round-1 review of agents-config-wgclw.2.

title: PDLC Orchestrator: v2 WorkTracker protocol expansions (Domains 4/5/7)
description: Expand the WorkTracker protocol beyond MVP's 4 domains (Discovery+State, Lifecycle, Hierarchy, Spec) to include native dependency tracking (Domain 4), search/surfacing (Domain 5), and metadata channel (Domain 7). MVP keeps these as orchestrator-sidecar (OrchestratorStateRepo). Filed against agents-config-wgclw.2 Round-1 revision pass CA-12.

title: PDLC Orchestrator: worker-authority sandboxing v2 (containerisation)
description: Beyond the MVP's worktree + path allowlist + env-scrub, container-based isolation (Docker/podman/firejail) for worker sessions with finer network policy and resource limits. Filed against agents-config-wgclw.2 Round-1 revision pass; MVP version specified in design spec CA-7.

title: PDLC Orchestrator: rich pdlc health dashboards / metrics endpoint
description: Beyond the MVP's text-output pdlc health command, expose structured metrics via Prometheus/OpenTelemetry endpoint and ship dashboards for live Session inventory, lifecycle histogram, strike distribution, marker-drift, lease-holder identity. MVP version specified in design spec CA-14.

title: PDLC Orchestrator: spec-mutation re-gate on mid-flight spec edits
description: When a tracker-side spec is mutated mid-execution, the orchestrator should re-gate the in-flight Objective rather than continue with the stale spec hash. Captured from Round-1 review feedback CA-11 / S-18. Deferred from PDLC Orchestrator Core MVP per agents-config-wgclw.2 Round-1 revision pass.

title: PDLC Orchestrator: layered project-config (.local, user-defaults)
description: Support layered config sources beyond a single project-level TOML — at minimum a project-local .local override and a user-level defaults file with documented precedence. Captured from Round-1 review feedback CA-13 / S-19. Deferred from PDLC Orchestrator Core MVP per agents-config-wgclw.2 Round-1 revision pass.

title: Revisit dispatching-parallel-agents verdict (promote / absorb-into-Orchestrator / drop)
description:
After the Deterministic Code-First Orchestrator (per AGENTS.md "Core Orchestration & Architecture" vision) is designed, decide whether `dispatching-parallel-agents` methodology should be promoted as an in-tree skill or absorbed into the Orchestrator's parallel-dispatch handling.

Context (from cx6.7.6 verdict survey):
- Upstream skill methodology: group failures by independent domain → one focused subagent per domain → parallel dispatch → review/integrate
- No in-tree successor exists currently
- Not load-bearing in any user-scoped rule
- Claude already does this informally most of the time without explicit prompting
- The Orchestrator vision explicitly subsumes "orchestration logic" into compiled/scripted code, which may include parallel subagent dispatch

This bead deliberately defers the verdict to avoid producing throwaway work. Resolve when the Orchestrator design lands or sooner if the parallel-dispatch pattern becomes load-bearing in some other context.

Resolution options:
- (a) Promote: copy `oss-snapshots/superpowers/dispatching-parallel-agents/SKILL.md` to `src/user/.agents/skills/dispatching-parallel-agents/` with provenance header
- (b) Absorb-into-Orchestrator: capture the pattern in Orchestrator design notes; do not promote a standalone skill
- (c) Drop: decide the implicit Claude behavior is sufficient and no codified guidance is needed

Discovered during cx6.7.6 verdict pass. Verdict for the upstream skill is "defer with follow-up bead"; this is that bead.
acceptance_criteria: Verdict recorded with rationale. If promote: skill copied to src/user/.agents/skills/dispatching-parallel-agents/ with provenance header and registry updated. If absorb: pattern captured in Orchestrator design notes. If drop: no action beyond closing this bead with rationale.

title: Seed Deterministic Orchestrator spec with subagent-driven-development two-stage review pattern
description:
Capture the two-stage review pattern from `oss-snapshots/superpowers/subagent-driven-development/SKILL.md` as a design input for the Deterministic Code-First Orchestrator (per AGENTS.md vision) BEFORE the superpowers plugin is removed.

Pattern to preserve:
- Per-task loop: dispatch implementer subagent → spec-compliance review → code-quality review → loop until both pass → mark complete
- Ships with three prompt templates upstream (implementer, spec-reviewer, code-quality-reviewer)
- The two-stage review (spec compliance separately from code quality) is the load-bearing insight worth keeping

Target location for the capture: wherever the Orchestrator design notes / spec doc live. If no Orchestrator spec doc exists yet at the time this bead is resolved, file a smaller bead to create the placeholder doc first.

Include a provenance pointer to `oss-snapshots/superpowers/subagent-driven-development/` at the pinned commit so the source survives if the snapshot folder is ever pruned.

This bead is deliberately design-only — no in-tree skill is created. The intent is to ensure the pattern survives the plugin removal as a documented Orchestrator design input, not to ship a skill that the Orchestrator will obsolete.

Discovered during cx6.7.6 verdict pass. Verdict for the upstream skill is "defer / drop with ticket"; this is that ticket.
acceptance_criteria: Two-stage review pattern (spec-compliance review + code-quality review per task) captured as Orchestrator design input in the Orchestrator spec doc (or a placeholder spec doc if none exists yet). Provenance pointer included citing oss-snapshots/superpowers/subagent-driven-development/ at f2cbfbe. No in-tree skill created.

title: pr-comment-fixer-team: schema field-name drift between round-1 and round-2 outputs
description: Observed during cx6.7.8 dogfood pass (PR #90 round-2 review fix, 2026-05-23). Round-1 worker emitted correct schema fields: fix_commit_sha, fix_gate_variant='lite', verification_evidence.test_command + verification_evidence.output. Round-2 worker emitted: commit_sha (missing fix_ prefix), fix_gate_variant='lite-gate' (wrong enum value), verification_evidence.json_parse + verification_evidence.scope_check (non-standard keys). The audit-subagent-report.sh requires exact field names (JSON, strict jq extraction); YAML report format is also a known gap (audit script requires JSON but pr-comment-fixer-team emits YAML). Orchestrator worked around by YAML→JSON conversion + field patching at run time, but the worker should emit the correct schema natively.
notes: Retired in triage 2026-06-27: subsumed by prgroom (abn9.8 implementation umbrella). prgroom replaces the artifact this bug lives in (typed FixInput/FixOutput contracts, ItemKind.REVIEW_SUMMARY, 6-priority phase resolver) so the bug does not carry forward. Reopen if prgroom slips and the old skill path keeps biting.

title: optimize-my-skill: rename scripts/eval-viewer/ → eval_viewer/ for Python module-mode parity
description: The amalgamated dir 'scripts/eval-viewer/' uses a dash, which Python rejects in module paths. Phase 4a uses module-mode invocation (PYTHONPATH=. python3 -m scripts.run_loop) but Phase 4b can only invoke generate_review.py via direct script path because 'eval-viewer' isn't a valid module name. Rename to 'eval_viewer/' (underscore) so both phases use the same invocation pattern. Cross-file impact: rename the dir, update SKILL.md Phase 4b path reference, verify install.sh still stages it correctly, verify the Path(__file__).parent / 'viewer.html' sibling lookup inside generate_review.py still works (it should — dir rename doesn't affect siblings). Provenance header should also note this is a deliberate divergence from the upstream skill-creator amalgam source (which uses 'eval-viewer/'); accept-periodic-resync policy means we'll have to re-apply the rename after each upstream sync.
acceptance_criteria: Directory renamed; module-mode invocation works for both run_loop AND generate_review; SKILL.md prose updated; install.sh dry-run shows correct staging; provenance header documents the divergence. Build passes. Typecheck passes. Tests pass.
close_reason: Directory renamed eval-viewer/ -> eval_viewer/ via git mv, SKILL.md Phase 4b path reference updated. PR #88 open, Copilot reviewed with no findings. Paused at merge step per lane instructions.

title: installer package missing py.typed marker (A.1 carry-over)
description:
The `packages/installer/` package is built with mypy --strict against `src/` (per pyproject.toml), but `src/installer/py.typed` is absent. Consequence: any consumer of `installer.core.model` or `installer.cli` outside this package — including the test suite when invoked via `mypy --strict tests` — gets `Skipping analyzing 'installer.X': module is installed, but missing library stubs or py.typed marker [import-untyped]` rather than real type-checking. Discovered during w1qls.1.2 implementation when running `mypy --strict tests`.

Fix: create empty file `packages/installer/src/installer/py.typed` and confirm it is included in the hatchling wheel (the existing `[tool.hatch.build.targets.wheel] packages = ["src/installer"]` should pick it up automatically — verify with `uv build` and `unzip -l dist/*.whl`). Then `mypy --strict tests` should resolve installer.* to real types.
acceptance_criteria: 1) `packages/installer/src/installer/py.typed` exists as an empty file. 2) `uv run mypy --strict tests` produces zero 'missing py.typed marker' errors for `installer.*` imports. 3) Built wheel includes `installer/py.typed` at the top of the installer package.

title: Fix root README.md stale .claude/rules/ references post abn9.23.1 rename
description:
Root README.md has several references to the old rule layout that became stale after abn9.23.1 (DYNAMIC-INCLUDE-RULES refactor):

- Line 44: mentions delegation/completion-gate/delivery under src/user/.claude/rules/ — they moved to src/user/.agents/rules/
- Line 132: same stale path reference in the customization note
- Line 201-208: manual install snippet copies only src/user/.claude/rules/ into ~/.claude/ — misses the 5 shared rules now under src/user/.agents/rules/
- Line 244: lists old filenames (delegation.md, completion-gate.md, delivery.md, git-commits.md, codex-routing.md, subagents.md) under src/user/.claude/rules/ — wrong location and wrong names post-rename

Root README.md was not in the spec's Additional Required Changes table for abn9.23.1 and is therefore discovered work from the Codex foreign-eyes review in RALF cycle 1.

## Acceptance Criteria
- README.md line 44: update to reference src/user/.agents/rules/ for the 5 general rules
- README.md line 132: same
- README.md manual install section: add cp for src/user/.agents/rules/ alongside the Claude-specific rules copy
- README.md line 244: update rule names and location (general rules → .agents/rules/; Claude-specific → claude-sandbox.md and claude-to-codex-routing.md in .claude/rules/)

title: epic-hygiene pr72_validate_d_test.sh D1 order-sensitive under shared Dolt DB
description: pr72_validate_d_test.sh D1 ('feature F3 with plain child → gate=handled') fails intermittently when run as part of the full test suite via 'find ... | xargs' chain, but passes when run in isolation. Root cause: likely Dolt DB transaction/session state contamination from prior tests in the same xargs chain. Pre-existing: reproduces without the y9mm-A changes per stash probe in cycle 1 of agents-config-y9mm.6 RALF green loop.

title: Enhance implementation workflows to ensure documentation updates at completion
description:
Workflows must ensure project documentation (internal and external-facing) are updated as mandatory part of implementation completion.

Timing logic:
- Brainstorm phase: Determine IF documentation updates are needed (result: pre-merge chore captured as part of impl epic)
- Implementation phase: REASSESS the full scope of documentation impact before calling complete

This ensures docs scope is discovered early but updated fully reflecting the actual implementation decisions.

title: Remove dead header from CLAUDE-EXTENSIONS.md.template
description:
CLAUDE-EXTENSIONS.md.template contains only a single line:
  # Claude-Specific Extensions

This header is inlined into ~/.claude/AGENTS.md via DYNAMIC-INCLUDE, producing a dangling section header with no content beneath it. The file exists as a placeholder for future Claude-specific content that never materialized — that content was eventually moved to the rules/ system instead.

## Fix

Make the file empty (zero bytes or whitespace only). This is explicitly acceptable per project convention: template files may violate markdown lint standards. An empty file produces no output in the assembled AGENTS.md, eliminating the dead header.

## Acceptance Criteria
- CLAUDE-EXTENSIONS.md.template contains no renderable content
- Assembled ~/.claude/AGENTS.md contains no dangling '# Claude-Specific Extensions' header

title: bug: dep-health-check/_test.sh AC4/AC5 fail — collect.py schema missing 'beads'/'findings' keys
description: Pre-existing failure on main. dep-health-check/_test.sh AC4 and AC5 expect top-level keys 'beads' and 'findings' in collect.py --mode all JSON output, but current collect.py does not emit them. 6 assertions fail. Not introduced by agents-config-3qf2 — present on main before that branch was created. Discovered during quality-sweep of agents-config-3qf2.

title: [refactor] whats-next collect.py: derive display_ids AND output emission from single mode→sections source of truth
description:
Discovered during agents-config-i07e round-3 review (PR #73 Copilot comment 3243521408).

Currently `select_section_beads()` and the output-emission `if/elif` in `main()` are parallel implementations of the same mode→sections mapping. The helper's docstring acknowledges this and asks both to be kept in sync, but they're still two structures.

A single source of truth (e.g., a module-level dict `MODE_SECTIONS = {'all': lambda H,P,B,I: H+P+B+I, ...}` used by both call-sites) would eliminate the drift risk entirely.

This was intentionally **out of scope** for i07e per its spec R3 (refactoring the output-emission elif was made optional, and the bead's minimal-edit constraint led to keeping the elif unchanged). Surfacing this as a separate optimization bead so the optional refactor is not lost.

Acceptance Criteria:
- A single canonical mode→sections data structure (dict, function, or class) is the only place the mapping is defined.
- Both display_ids construction AND output emission consume that single source.
- All existing collect_test.sh assertions (T1-T10) remain green.
- New assertion proves drift is now structurally impossible (e.g., by deriving both code paths from the same iterable).

title: test: add direct implement-bead routing HEP test for epic/milestone sources
description: Group C of agents-config-3qf2 stress-tests validates the brainstorm-finalize gate helper's HEP path for epics/milestones. It does NOT cover implement-bead's own routing branch (implement-bead/SKILL.md §1: 'epic → flag-human via HEP escalation procedure'). Add a test that directly exercises implement-bead's routing logic: create a sacrificial epic, label it implementation-ready, and observe that implement-bead either pours an implement-feature molecule (then verify the step-bead routing fires HEP) or directly triggers HEP without pouring. This requires driving implement-bead's routing logic, which is heavier than calling the gate helper directly.

title: spike: whats-next Group A rendered-output assertions vs collect.py JSON shape
description: During agents-config-3qf2 red-tests, Group A AC bullets (A7: empty-state messages, A8: 7-column schema) reference rendered whats-next output (tables, empty-state strings) but collect.py emits JSON only — the SKILL.md agent renders. Current tests verify (a) SKILL.md spec strings exist verbatim and (b) JSON carries the 7 backing fields. If rendering-side assertions are needed in future, tests would need to drive the SKILL.md agent end-to-end (heavyweight). Investigate: is the JSON-shape verification sufficient, or do we need rendered-output tests for the whats-next skill?

title: chore: fix bd-finalize-container-gate.sh path reference in worker dispatch docs
description: tdd-red-team (agents-config-mol-yaflh) found that the dispatcher brief said the helper lives at ~/.beads/scripts/bd-finalize-container-gate.sh but the actual installed path is resolved via REPO_ROOT (src/plugins/beads/.beads/scripts/). Future dispatch prompts should reference the in-repo path or use a REPO_ROOT-relative resolution pattern. Discovery during agents-config-3qf2 red-tests stage.

title: implement-bead direct-dispatch: add active-child container gate before pour
description:
From PR #72 Copilot R9: implement-bead's fallback path 'feature → implement-feature' fires regardless of whether the feature has active children. A feature-with-children container that carries implementation-ready can bypass the new container rule via run-queue / direct bd ready --label implementation-ready invocation.

Fix: before pouring implement-feature on a feature source, probe active children (filtering out merge-gate / human label children per the narrowed Rule B definition); if container, flag-human via HEP instead of pour.

Lives in src/plugins/beads/.agents/skills/implement-bead/SKILL.md formula-selection fallback (around line 98-112 as of PR #72 head).
acceptance_criteria:
implement-bead source-type fallback for feature checks active-child count (filtering formula-gate children) before selecting implement-feature.
Container features route to flag-human HEP (parent-child human bead per the new container HEP shape).
Test added under epic-hygiene-tests asserting the new check.
close_reason: Superseded by agents-config-<new> (round-4 PR #72 hardening). Container gate at implement-bead direct-dispatch is included in that bead's scope.

title: Relocate src/user/.agents/skills/epic-hygiene-tests/ — folder breaks 'one directory per skill' convention
description:
Per repo convention, src/user/.agents/skills/<name>/ houses a skill (SKILL.md) and its supporting artifacts/tests. The epic-hygiene-tests/ directory created in PR #72 (eos5) holds 4 tests that target NON-skill artifacts:
- beads_rules_test.sh → tests src/plugins/beads/.claude/rules/beads.md
- brainstorm_finalize_container_gate_test.sh → tests src/plugins/beads/.beads/formulas/brainstorm-bead.formula.toml
- implement_bead_flag_human_test.sh → tests src/plugins/beads/.agents/skills/implement-bead/SKILL.md (a plugin-tree skill, not user-tree)
- migration_state_test.sh → tests live bd CLI state

These tests landed there because project-config.toml [gates].test globs src/user/.agents/skills exclusively, and no better home existed.

Options:
A. Move epic-hygiene-tests/ → src/tests/ (or similar non-skills location); update test runner glob to include the new path.
B. Move each test next to its target (e.g., implement_bead_flag_human_test.sh → src/plugins/beads/.agents/skills/implement-bead/) and broaden test runner glob to multiple trees.
C. Add a SKILL.md to epic-hygiene-tests/ documenting it as a test fixture — does not match conceptual purpose; tests are not a skill.

Recommend Option A: simplest test-runner change, keeps test infrastructure in one place outside the skill semantics.

title: Extract Step 0 container-gate bash from brainstorm-bead.formula.toml into a helper script
description:
PR #72 comment 3238936390: 'This formula step contains a long set of bash code. Extract this into a helper script file and have the formula instruct the agent how to call it and use its output.'

Step 0 in brainstorm-bead.formula.toml (~lines 246-330) is ~80 lines of inline bash covering:
- Type-based container detection (milestone/epic/feature-with-children)
- Three HEP escalation paths (ambiguous-Y, single-reclassification, clean container)
- bd-close-walk invocation and molecule burn

Existing helpers under src/plugins/beads/.beads/scripts/ (bd-claim-walk.sh, bd-close-walk.sh, bd-finalize-create-impl-bead.sh) establish the pattern. Suggested new helper: brainstorm-finalize-container-gate.sh.

Scope:
1. Design helper interface (env vars or args for {{bead-id}}, <this-mol-id>, etc.; stdout/exit-code contract)
2. Move bash to the helper; replace formula step with invocation
3. Update tests if needed (brainstorm_finalize_container_gate_test.sh currently greps formula body; may need to re-target script body)
4. Verify Step 0 behavior unchanged
close_reason: Delivered in PR #72 (agents-config-eos5). Step 0 bash extracted to .beads/scripts/bd-finalize-container-gate.sh; formula's Step 0 reduced to a 7-line helper invocation. Per Scott PR comment requesting in-PR delivery.

title: wait-for-pr-comments Phase 9: ESCALATE-unresolved threads trigger false re-entry loop
description: Phase 9 counts 'unresolved, non-outdated threads' and re-enters Phase 3 when count > 0. But ESCALATE items are intentionally never resolved (by Skill B policy). If a PR hits the hard cap (round >= 3) and leaves 5 ESCALATE threads unresolved, Phase 9 sees count=5 and re-enters Phase 3 indefinitely (round 4, 5, ... all hit the cap, all ESCALATE, all unresolved). No exit path. Root fix: Phase 9 should exclude threads where the corresponding inventory item has classification=ESCALATE from the re-entry trigger. Discovered during eos5 PR #72 review-cycle, round 3.
notes: Retired in triage 2026-06-27: subsumed by prgroom (abn9.8 implementation umbrella). prgroom replaces the artifact this bug lives in (typed FixInput/FixOutput contracts, ItemKind.REVIEW_SUMMARY, 6-priority phase resolver) so the bug does not carry forward. Reopen if prgroom slips and the old skill path keeps biting.

title: I2 close walk: enforce parent AC satisfaction before auto-closing container beads
notes: Discovered during pqa5 brainstorm. Currently I2 close walk only checks 'all children closed'. The intent is that parent ACs should also be satisfied before auto-closing. Enforcement deferred — we rely on child beads accurately embodying parent ACs. This bead tracks future implementation of a mechanical AC-satisfaction check in the close walk.

title: dep-health-check: render-report companion script (render.py)
description: The skill body renders findings from collect.py JSON inline. A future render.py or shell companion could deterministically format the deterministic-findings section and leave only the LLM-inferred section to the model, reducing prompt size on large audits. Not a green-loop blocker.

title: Installer: carrier-merge interaction with prune/orphan scan — sanity pass
description: The carrier-merge change in the install.sh fix (for dep-health-check, agents-config-he9o) copies plugin-skill files into a directory seeded by the shared/carrier tree. scan_orphans uses the staging tree as its baseline, so this should be neutral — but worth a sanity pass next time the prune path is exercised end-to-end against a live ~/.claude/. Not currently blocking.
notes: RE-EVAL (2026-06-22): originally recorded against bash install.sh carrier-merge, now an exec stub post-H.4. Python carrier-merge lives in core/overlay.py (_place / _carry_files / dir_overrides); prune lives in core/sync.py. Re-evaluate whether the carrier-merge x prune interaction needs a sanity pass in the Python engine, or close if covered by existing overlay tests.

title: implement-bead §4 step 5 stamps worker-audit-* unconditionally; conflicts with audit-label scope policy
description:
## Symptom

implement-bead/SKILL.md §4 step 5 reads: 'Stamp `worker-audit-<agent-name>[-iter<N>]` on the step-bead (forensic, append-only).' — no conditional on agent name.

The 'Audit-label scope' section (later in same file) declares the policy: '`worker-audit-<agent-name>[-iter<N>]` labels apply ONLY to dispatches of `tdd-red-team`, `tdd-green-team`, and `bug-diagnoser` (worker-only). Review, fresh-eyes, and adversarial subagents dispatched by orchestration skills are out-of-band and are explicitly excluded from the `worker-audit-` namespace.'

These contradict. §4's per-dispatch primitive will stamp `worker-audit-general-purpose-iter<N>` when ralf-implement uses §4 to dispatch general-purpose, leaking into the audit namespace.

## Discovered during

agents-config-7bk.19.7 cycle-3 adversarial review (F5). The 19.7 spec works around this by having ralf-implement own its own audit-label stamping (R1.5 conditional), but the upstream §4 contradiction remains for any future caller of §4 with a non-named-worker doer.

## Proposed fix (upstream)

§4 step 5 should condition on agent name: `if agent_name in {tdd-red-team, tdd-green-team, bug-diagnoser}: stamp worker-audit-<agent-name>[-iter<N>]; else: skip step 5`. The conditional belongs in the primitive, not in policy prose 50 lines below.

## Out of 19.7 scope

This is an implement-bead §4 issue, not a ralf-* issue. 19.7 documents the workaround; this bead tracks the upstream fix.
acceptance_criteria: implement-bead/SKILL.md §4 step 5 explicitly conditions worker-audit stamping on agent name (tdd-red-team | tdd-green-team | bug-diagnoser). Step 5 prose names the conditional; not delegated to tail policy section.
notes: Severity: MAJOR (latent contract leak — currently masked because no caller dispatches general-purpose via §4 yet). Will become live once 19.7 ships ralf-implement → §4 → general-purpose flow.

title: implement-bead/SKILL.md §6.3 references bug-diagnoser-report-v1 reproduction_steps but schema has no such field
description:
Surface: implement-bead/SKILL.md line 123 says 'extract root_cause_note (and reproduction_steps for red-tests)' from the bug-diagnoser worker-report. Reality: bug-diagnoser-report-v1.md schema (docs/specs/bug-diagnoser-report-v1.md) defines ONLY root_cause_note as the additive required field. There is no structured reproduction_steps field in the report schema.

The §5 stage-agent map at implement-bead/SKILL.md line 112 also says: '(red-tests, fix-bug) → tdd-red-team (single-regression mode; root_cause_note and reproduction_steps from upstream diagnose report)'. Same mismatch.

Triggered the question while reviewing the spec for agents-config-7bk.19.7. Surfaced by cycle-2 fresh-eyes review.

Options for resolution:
(a) Add reproduction_steps as an optional output field to bug-diagnoser-report-v1; coordinate with bug-diagnoser agent to emit it.
(b) Drop reproduction_steps from implement-bead §6.3 / §5 (the agent receives repro steps as INPUT from bead context, not produces them; downstream agents already get the bead context).
(c) Keep §6.3 wording but document the field as 'optional, may be absent' and fall back to bead context when absent.

Recommend (b) as cleanest: tdd-red-team in single-regression mode already has access to the original bead's failure evidence via bead context; no need to round-trip through the worker-report. Updates required in implement-bead/SKILL.md §5 and §6.3.

title: bd-record-decision.sh: jq error 'Cannot index object with number' after creating decision bead
description:
When invoked with --bead-id <id> --title ... --notes ... --implemented, the helper script creates the decision bead but then exits with: `jq: error (at <stdin>:12): Cannot index object with number` (exit code 5). The script does NOT set the description, does NOT add the discovered-from dep edge, and does NOT close the bead. Manual recovery: bd update --description, bd dep add --type discovered-from, bd close --reason.

Repro on 2026-05-12 during agents-config-7bk.19.7 write-spec step. Both --implemented decisions encountered the same failure; second one was filed manually without the helper. Likely cause: bd CLI JSON output shape changed; the script's jq path indexes into an object that is now a different shape (probably .[0] vs root array). Location: ~/.beads/scripts/bd-record-decision.sh line ~12 of its jq invocation.

title: Refactor: extract hep_escalate shell helper to dedupe HEP block across 5 skill files
description:
The HEP escalation procedure (`bd create` → `bd label add human` → `bd update --append-notes` → `bd dep add` → `bd update --status open`) is duplicated across 5 files with semantic drift detected:

Locations:
- src/plugins/beads/.claude/rules/beads.md HEP section (canonical)
- src/plugins/beads/.agents/skills/implement-bead/SKILL.md §0
- src/plugins/beads/.agents/skills/start-bead/SKILL.md (Step 2 length-0)
- src/plugins/beads/.agents/skills/start-bead/SKILL.md (Step 2 length-2+)
- src/plugins/beads/.agents/skills/run-queue/SKILL.md (collapsed inline)

Drift observed:
- beads.md description="<context: what was being done, what is blocked, what is needed>"
- implement-bead description="<context: what the stage was doing, what is blocked, what is needed>"
- start-bead omits `bd update --status open` (harmless in pre-claim context but textually drifted)

Proposed: shell function `hep_escalate <source-id> <step-bead-id|N/A> <mol-id|N/A> <worktree|N/A> <scenario-hint> <title> <description>` declared once (likely in beads.md or implement-bead/lib), referenced by every skill. Eliminates ~50 lines of duplicated bash + drift hazard.

Surfaced by simplify Axis-1 review during agents-config-7bk.13 implementation.

title: Extract worker base contract into shared section of worker-report-v1.md
description: All five worker agents (tdd-red-team, tdd-green-team, bug-diagnoser, docs-edits-team, pr-comment-fixer-team) repeat near-verbatim prose for: (a) Write-tool rationale, (b) base constraints (no tracker, 40-char SHAs, no placement directives, no destructive git), (c) Operating Contract inputs boilerplate. Extracting to a 'Worker base contract' section in worker-report-v1.md and having each agent file reference it would shrink each agent by ~20 lines and eliminate the drift risk. Identified by simplify pass on acmh.13.

title: Convention: red-phase test scripts should compose absence-probe tokens at runtime
description: The red-phase test script verify-acmh13-artifacts.sh embedded literal probe tokens it was simultaneously asserting must be absent from scripts/ (X.2 cross-cutting check). This created an unsatisfiable constraint: the test file itself caused the assertion to fail. Green-phase fix used string concatenation (LEGACY_AGENT='bead-im''plementor') to avoid the literal. Convention to document: red-phase tests asserting the absence of a probe token should compose that token at runtime via concatenation or variable fragmentation, not embed it as a literal string in the test body.

title: Fix jq expression in bd-record-decision.sh (script breaks on bd create --json object output)
description: Two helper scripts in ~/.beads/scripts/ use the jq expression '(.[0].id // .id) // empty' to parse 'bd create --json' output. As of the current bd CLI, 'bd create --json' returns a single OBJECT (not an array), so '.[0]' raises 'Cannot index object with number' and the // fallback never fires. Affected: (1) bd-record-decision.sh and (2) bd-finalize-create-impl-bead.sh. Both scripts exit 5 due to the jq parse error even though bd create succeeds (bead IS created). Reproducers hit during agents-config-acmh.1 brainstorm session (2026-05-12). Suggested fix: replace '(.[0].id // .id) // empty' with 'if type == "array" then .[0].id else .id end // empty' in both scripts.
notes: Suggested fix: replace '(.[0].id // .id) // empty' with 'if type == "array" then .[0].id else .id end // empty' to handle both shapes. Workaround used in acmh.1: manual bd create + bd dep add + bd close instead of the script.

title: Update AGENTS.md 'Current state' — remove stale persona-vs-orchestration bullet
description: AGENTS.md 'Current state' section still lists '**Persona vs orchestration** guidance on decide-vs-escalate is not yet reconciled' as a gap. That gap was closed by PR #63 (abn9.5). Remove or rewrite the bullet to reflect current state.

title: Phase 5b PR #62 round-4 doc-quality fixes (cap-deferred)
description:
Five doc-quality findings from PR #62 Copilot round-4 review, deferred under the wait-for-pr-comments strict re-review cap. None are blockers — the PR's mechanical assimilation work is correct; these are wording, parity, and small correctness improvements for the planning artifacts.

Findings:
1. (Comment 3215072969, roadmap.md L58) M1 DoD bullet for agents-config-f298 lists --append-acceptance / --append-description but f298's title also includes --append-design. Update bullet for parity.
2. (Comment 3215072983, plan.md L9) Tech-stack note cites memory 'bd-show-json-returns-array' as the reason Python is needed for newline-bearing fields; the actual concern is literal newlines in notes/description/acceptance_criteria fields breaking jq (per src/plugins/beads/.claude/rules/beads.md). Replace memory ref with that concrete rule citation.
3. (Comment 3215072989, plan.md L261) Task 3 Step 7 wires deps with bd dep add <dependent> <blocker> (M2 depends on M1, etc.), but the echo uses an arrow direction implying flow ('M1 → M2 → M3 → M4 → Post-MVP'). Reword to 'M2 depends on M1; M3 depends on M2; ...'. Also rename verification snippet's 'deps_in' to 'deps_out' (it prints outgoing deps).
4. (Comment 3215072997, plan.md L940) Audit invariant Python script builds milestone_ids from milestones.get(...) which yields None if a key is missing in /tmp/phase5b-id-map.txt; bead with parent=None then incorrectly counts as 'under a milestone'. Filter None or fail-fast on missing required IDs.
5. (Comment 3215073011, plan.md L52) Wording: 'should be slightly higher if il69 spawned more closures' should be 'spawned more beads' (new follow-ups, not closures).

Reference: PR #62 Copilot review round 4 (timestamp 2026-05-10T15:25:30Z). Strict cap was applied per round-cap protocol after rounds 1-3 burned through the natural cycle.
acceptance_criteria: All 5 findings addressed in a follow-up PR. Each thread resolved on PR #62 (or the new PR cross-references back).
close_reason: Superseded — Scott overrode the cap rule on PR #62 round 4 (2026-05-10). All 5 round-4 findings to be fixed inline on the active PR rather than deferred to follow-up.

title: Research spikes (retire-or-rescope)
description:
Bucket for research-spike beads whose outcome is retire (won't-do) OR rescope (produce concrete adoption beads). Runs opportunistically — no milestone dep. Includes l0v0 (mattpocock skills), zetc (Codex 5.5 confidence-loop), 717 (residual lu3 risk-closer review), feedback-loop spike (new), acmh.2 (skillize tech-lead), acmh.5 (formulas-as-skills review). Reference: docs/plans/2026-05-09-roadmap.md Section 6.

Note (2026-05-17): 7bk.24 (karpathy-guidelines) and cx6 (skill amalgam strategy) were re-parented out of this bucket. cx6 is now an M0 child; 7bk.24 and sxfk became children of cx6.
acceptance_criteria: Each spike's outcome documented (retire OR concrete adoption beads filed). Bucket closes when all child spikes have a verdict applied.
notes:
## Success Criteria
All children are either closed (retired/rescoped) or promoted to concrete implementation beads. The research spike bucket itself has no open children.

title: Tier 2 (split): Skillize tech-lead (evaluate vs superpowers:dispatching-parallel-agents)
description: Tier 2 follow-up from agents-config-il69 audit, split out per user gate-NOTE on REMEDIATION_PLAN.md. Question: should tech-lead be a skill rather than an agent? If yes, evaluate (a) whether it duplicates superpowers:dispatching-parallel-agents and should be removed, or (b) whether the two should be used together (different scopes). Outcome: either a skillization bead with the converted skill, OR a doc note explaining when to invoke each. Touches user/.agents/agents/tech-lead.md and src/user/.agents/skills/.

title: Pipeline autonomous work to overlap external-system wait times (CI, Copilot review, GitHub merge gates)
description:
**Problem**: Autonomous overnight runs spend significant wall-clock time blocked on third-party pipelines — CI, Copilot review polling, GitHub thread reply/resolve cycles. `run-queue` advances sequentially per the docs, so while one bead waits on external systems, no other bead progresses. The result is silent wall-clock loss against the 'background overnight cranking' goal.

**Goal**: Allow the queue to advance multiple beads concurrently within safety constraints (worktree isolation, dependency invariants, repo-level locks).

**Approach sketch (high-level only — design TBD)**:
- Identify natural blocking points in the bead pipeline (PR review wait, CI wait, merge wait)
- When a bead reaches a blocking point, suspend its molecule and pop the next ready bead
- Resume suspended molecules on external-event triggers (PR comment, CI green, etc.)
- Respect worktree-per-bead isolation; serialize only operations that touch shared state (main branch, shared DBs)

**Out of scope (this bead)**: distributed multi-machine execution; new infra; rewriting run-queue from scratch.
acceptance_criteria:
- Design document in docs/plans/ describing the pipelining model and the concurrency-safety contract
- Identification of all blocking points and the events that unblock them
- Proof-of-concept that two beads can advance through their molecules with overlapping wall-clock under realistic external-system latency
- Worktree-per-bead isolation preserved; no shared-state corruption
- Telemetry showing wall-clock gain vs sequential baseline (ties to instrumentation bead)

title: merge-agents-config-7bk.25
description: Merge gate child for agents-config-7bk.25. Closed only by merge-and-cleanup after merge + branch/worktree cleanup.

title: [Impl] Phase 3: bead-spec agent + brainstorm-bead formula rewiring
description:
## Summary

Replace single-context brainstorm-bead execution with role-differentiated assets: two behavioral skills (bead-assess, bead-write-spec) with mechanical model/effort enforcement for main-agent phases, and one fresh-eyes subagent (bead-reviewer) for adversarial spec review.

## Background / Motivation

The existing brainstorm-bead formula runs all phases in main-agent context with no role differentiation. Two structural defects:

1. **Self-review anti-pattern**: the same context that wrote the spec reviews the spec at ralf-spec-review — exactly the bias RALF-IT doctrine forbids.
2. **No per-phase model/effort control**: assess and write-spec have meaningfully different computational demands with no mechanism to express this.

The worker-agent decomposition in 7bk.19 applied the same logic to the implementation side. This bead applies it to the brainstorm side.

## Requirements

**R1 — Create `bead-assess` skill**
- Path: `src/plugins/beads/.agents/skills/bead-assess/SKILL.md`
- Frontmatter: `model: opus[1m]`, `effort: medium`
- Content: behavioral instructions for the assess phase — gap identification, [m]/[h] AC tag proposals, auto-mergeable and review-level proposals with rationale, QUESTION FILTER application
- Scope: assess phase ONLY. classify-and-policy content is NOT included — 7bk.12 owns that step and its wiring entirely.

**R2 — Create `bead-write-spec` skill**
- Path: `src/plugins/beads/.agents/skills/bead-write-spec/SKILL.md`
- Frontmatter: `model: opus[1m]`, `effort: high`
- Content: behavioral instructions for spec writing — structured spec format (summary, background, requirements, out-of-scope, AC, design notes, open questions), bd update commands, decision recording. The superpowers:writing-plans methodology is intentionally inlined here rather than preloaded as a skill dependency — writing-plans is on the off-limits list for bead-tracked work (beads.md), so its content is absorbed into this skill's body.

**R3 — Create `bead-reviewer` agent**
- Path: `src/plugins/beads/.agents/agents/bead-reviewer.md`
- model: claude-opus-4-7 (agent.md convention; note: opus[1m] is skill frontmatter convention)
- Tools: Read, Grep, Glob (read-only — see D3 for why Bash is excluded)
- Skills: superpowers:brainstorming
- Color: blue
- Role: adversarial spec review — completeness, ambiguity, feasibility, consistency, implementability; widens scope to heuristically check [m]/[h] classification and knob choices (auto-mergeable, review-level) for plausibility
- Note: existing agent.md files in this repo do not use effort: or memory: frontmatter. Introduce these fields if/when the harness supports them; for now omit.

**R4 — Update `brainstorm-bead.formula.toml`**
- assess step: REPLACE step body with thin shim. The shim's first instruction is to invoke bead-assess skill. Canonical behavioral content lives in the skill, not the formula step. Invocation MUST be first in the turn to ensure the harness model/effort override (effort: medium) covers the productive assess work.
- write-spec step: REPLACE step body with thin shim. First instruction invokes bead-write-spec skill. Same first-in-turn requirement.
- discuss step: UNCHANGED. Main-agent interactive. No skill invocation.
- ralf-spec-review step: REPLACE inline ralf-review skill invocation. New flow: (1) main agent pre-fetches spec text via `bd show {{bead-id}}` in main-agent context, (2) dispatches bead-reviewer subagent via Agent tool with spec text, review criteria, context, and max-cycles all in the prompt body. bead-reviewer has no Bash — it cannot run bd show itself. Pre-fetch is mandatory.
- Update formula header comment to reflect new execution model.

**R5 — Update arch doc catalog**
- `docs/specs/bead-pipeline-architecture.md` §6: Update bead-assessor and bead-specwriter catalog entries to reflect skill-form implementation (bead-assess, bead-write-spec). Add cross-reference to D1 rationale. bead-reviewer agent entry preserved unchanged.

**R6 — Extend verify-artifacts.sh**
- Add file-existence checks for: bead-assess skill, bead-write-spec skill, bead-reviewer agent.

## Out of Scope

- classify-and-policy formula step and dispatch wiring (owned entirely by 7bk.12)
- Effort calibration refinement (7bk.15)
- bead-assessor.md and bead-specwriter.md agent files (see D1)
- agent:* / skill:* dispatch label overrides in formula (7bk.22, pending, scoped to implement-bead)

## Design Notes

**D1 — Skills vs agents for assess/write-spec; lifecycle contract**
Skills are required for main-agent phases because agent.md frontmatter is dispatch-time-only — it is consumed by the harness when a subagent is created via Agent tool, not for mid-session main-agent behavior. There is no mechanism to apply agent.md constraints (tool scoping, memory policy) to a step executed inline by the main agent. Skills are the correct primitive.

The Claude Code harness mechanically enforces model: and effort: frontmatter fields for the duration of the turn in which the skill is invoked. "Duration" means the rest of that agent turn — from skill invocation forward. Consequently, the skill MUST be invoked as the first action in the formula step. If the agent does productive assess work (gap analysis, question formulation) BEFORE invoking the skill, that work runs at the session's default model/effort, defeating the purpose. The thin shim design enforces this ordering: the step body says "invoke bead-assess skill" with nothing before it.

Effort levels follow arch §6 defaults: assess=medium, write-spec=high. The high
acceptance_criteria:
bead-assess skill exists at src/plugins/beads/.agents/skills/bead-assess/SKILL.md with model: opus[1m] and effort: medium frontmatter
bead-write-spec skill exists at src/plugins/beads/.agents/skills/bead-write-spec/SKILL.md with model: opus[1m] and effort: high frontmatter; writing-plans methodology inlined (skill is off-limits for bead-tracked work)
bead-reviewer.md agent exists at src/plugins/beads/.agents/agents/bead-reviewer.md with model: claude-opus-4-7, tools: Read+Grep+Glob, color: blue, skills: superpowers:brainstorming
brainstorm-bead.formula.toml assess step body replaced with thin shim: invokes bead-assess skill as first action
brainstorm-bead.formula.toml write-spec step body replaced with thin shim: invokes bead-write-spec skill as first action
brainstorm-bead.formula.toml discuss step is unchanged (main-agent interactive, no skill)
brainstorm-bead.formula.toml ralf-spec-review step pre-fetches spec via bd show in main-agent context then dispatches subagent_type: bead-reviewer with spec text, review criteria, context, and max-cycles all in Agent tool prompt body
docs/specs/bead-pipeline-architecture.md §6 catalog updated: bead-assessor entry changed to skill-form (bead-assess), bead-specwriter entry changed to skill-form (bead-write-spec), bead-reviewer agent entry preserved; design rationale cross-referenced
scripts/smoke/verify-artifacts.sh extended with file-existence checks for bead-assess skill, bead-write-spec skill, and bead-reviewer agent; script passes
No bead-assessor.md or bead-specwriter.md agent files created
Build passes. Typecheck passes. Tests pass.

title: Review karpathy-guidelines and other external skills for formula/skill value-adds
description: Audit the karpathy-guidelines skill and any other externally-sourced skills (e.g. andrej-karpathy-skills) for patterns or principles that could strengthen our bead formulas (brainstorm-bead, implement-feature, fix-bug, etc.) or warrant updates to our own skills. Produce a short list of candidate improvements with rationale. No implementation — discovery and recommendation only.
close_reason: Deferred (2026-05-17). karpathy-guidelines is out of scope for cx6's named-four-source contract (pocock, superpowers, beads-plugin, claude-plugins-official). Revisit later if karpathy/andrej-karpathy-skills earn a place in a future epic.

title: [Follow-up] Soft-warning UX for open bead carrying produced-bead-* label (anomaly diagnostic)
description:
Discovered during cycle-2 ralf-review of agents-config-12q7.2 (Route Z spec). When an open bead carries a produced-bead-<Y-id> label (which is unusual — suggests stuck finalize replay or manual mis-application), Route Z's Step 1.5 short-circuits at the status check (status != 'closed' exits the preflight before label evaluation). The bead proceeds through Step 2/3 normally, losing a potentially-useful diagnostic signal. Goal: emit a soft warning to the user when this anomaly is detected, without changing routing behavior.

Out of scope for the parent (12q7.4). Filed per cycle-2 MINOR-3.
close_reason: Duplicate of agents-config-hhou (existing follow-up bead for same cycle-2 MINOR-3 anomaly)

title: start-bead: soft-warning UX when open bead carries produced-bead-* (finalize-replay anomaly)
description:
When an open bead carries a produced-bead-<Y-id> label, it indicates a finalize-replay anomaly: the brainstorm-bead formula started to close X and produce Y but didn't complete the X-close step. start-bead's Step 1.5 (Route Z) short-circuits on status != closed, so this anomaly silently falls through to Routes A/B/C — losing diagnostic-signal traceability.

Add a soft-warning to start-bead Step 1.5 (or as a separate Step 1.6 'open-bead anomaly check') that emits an agent reply note when an open bead carries produced-bead-*, then continues normal routing. Non-gating; pure visibility.

Surfaced by ralf-review cycle 2 of agents-config-12q7.2 (Route Z spec) as MINOR-3.

Out of scope for 12q7.2 by design — that bead is the Route Z preflight only.

title: Research bead gates: applicability and workflow integration
description:
Investigate whether 'bead gates' exist as a concept in the beads system and what utility they might provide for our workflows.

## Questions to answer

- Does bd have a native gate or pre-condition mechanism (e.g. required labels, status checks, or hooks before transitions)?
- If not, what patterns exist or could be introduced to enforce gates (e.g. label-based guards, molecule step preconditions, custom bd hooks)?
- What workflow pain points could gates address? (e.g. prevent close while human label is present, block implementation-ready without brainstormed, require verified-by-human before close on flagged beads)
- Are there any existing formula steps or skill checks already acting as informal gates that could be formalized?
- What is the implementation cost — configuration, scripting, skill updates?

## Deliverable

A research report with findings and a recommendation: adopt a gate pattern, defer, or close as not applicable.

title: Add 'work isolation mandatory before edits' rule to AGENTS.md
notes:
Captured from uncommitted edit found in stale worktree .claude/worktrees/feat-jyb2-dynamic-include during hft housekeeping (session 66272d4a, 2026-05-06). Worktree was scheduled for removal; this bead preserves the policy text before discard.

## Proposed addition

Add a bullet under the relevant constraints section of AGENTS.md (or the analogous constraints/workflow doc — the user's intent was the root AGENTS.md):

  **Work isolation is mandatory before edits** — before the first file edit, run `git rev-parse --abbrev-ref HEAD`; if it returns `main`, create/switch to a dedicated feature branch in a worktree and only then proceed

## Why

Reinforces the existing 'Isolate work' orchestration rule mechanically — gives agents a concrete pre-flight check (`git rev-parse --abbrev-ref HEAD`) instead of just 'use a worktree'. Catches the failure mode of starting edits on main without realizing it.

## Open questions for routing

- Where exactly does this belong? Candidates:
  - Top-level project AGENTS.md (which file the uncommitted edit was made to)
  - User-scoped INSTRUCTIONS.md.template under <constraints> or <orchestration>
  - A new entry in worktrees.md
- Is this a standalone rule, or should it merge with existing 'Isolate work' guidance?
- Does it need to apply to BOTH user-level and project-level AGENTS files (i.e., goes in INSTRUCTIONS.md.template so it propagates everywhere)?

## Source

Original uncommitted edit on root AGENTS.md in worktree feat-jyb2-dynamic-include, never committed.

title: brainstorm-bead writes spec to notes field but rules/beads.md says specs go in description — resolve inconsistency
description:
# Spec: Migrate brainstorm-bead spec field from notes to description

## Summary

Migrate the brainstorm-bead formula's spec-writing steps from `--notes=` to `--description=`, and update the beads.md documentation to reflect the upstream-confirmed design intent: notes accumulate, specifications are written once to description.

## Background / motivation

The beads project confirmed that field-update semantics are intentional by design:
- `--notes` / `--append-notes`: accumulation field — use for lifecycle context, run breadcrumbs, discovered constraints
- `--description`, `--design`, `--acceptance`: replace-semantics only — write once, completely, at the step that owns them
- No `--append-{description,design,acceptance}` flags exist by design (filed as f298, closed as upstream-wontfix)

The brainstorm-bead formula currently writes the spec to `--notes=`. This is the wrong field: the spec is a single authoritative document written once (by write-spec) and revised once (by finalize). That is description territory. Meanwhile, `src/plugins/beads/.claude/rules/beads.md` says "specs may be written directly into the bead description" — accurate once this migration lands.

## Requirements

### R1. brainstorm-bead write-spec step — migrate to --description=

In `src/plugins/beads/.beads/formulas/brainstorm-bead.formula.toml`, the write-spec step's spec-write line:
  `bd update {{bead-id}} --notes="<full spec document>"`
→
  `bd update {{bead-id}} --description="<full spec document>"`

The migration MUST use the inline `--description=` form (not `--body-file` or `--stdin`) for symmetry with the existing inline `--acceptance=` invocation immediately above and to keep the formula's TOML triple-quoted description block straightforward.

Update the guard preamble immediately above this line. The current text reads (paraphrased): "--notes= REPLACES the entire notes field, and --acceptance= REPLACES the entire acceptance-criteria field. ... If the bead has prior investigation context (notes from create-bead, user discussion, or earlier brainstorming), you MUST either: (a) merge ... (b) preserve via bd comments add ...".

The post-migration guard MUST:
- Reference `--description=` REPLACES "the entire description field" (verbatim phrase, for greppability)
- Drop the "--notes= REPLACES the entire notes field" reference (now obsolete here)
- Reword the prior-context source list to reflect what description actually contains pre-write-spec: e.g. "prior context (the placeholder description from `bd create`, manual `--description` updates, or notes from create-bead / user discussion that should be folded into the spec)". The point is to STOP saying "notes from create-bead" as if those are at risk of overwrite — they aren't, they live in the notes field; but they MAY contain context the spec author should incorporate.
- Keep the same (a) merge / (b) preserve via `bd comments add` escape hatches
- Keep the existing greppable sentinel structure so the AC's positive grep on "REPLACES the entire description field" matches reliably

### R2. brainstorm-bead finalize step — migrate to --description=

In the same file, the finalize step's spec-revise line:
  `bd update {{bead-id}} --notes="<revised spec>"`
→
  `bd update {{bead-id}} --description="<revised spec>"`

Also update the prose header IMMEDIATELY above this line. Currently reads:
  "1. Update bead acceptance + notes with final spec revisions:"
→
  "1. Update bead acceptance + description with final spec revisions:"

The finalize step does NOT get its own destructive-write guard preamble — by the time finalize runs, write-spec has already authored the canonical spec into description. Finalize legitimately overwrites that freshly-written content with the revised version, never "prior investigation context". This is the same logic that protected revise from receiving a guard in the original hft fix.

The `--acceptance=` lines in both write-spec and finalize remain unchanged.

### R3. brainstorm-bead ralf-spec-review step — clarify open-questions sink

In the same file, Phase 4 (ralf-spec-review) has a sentence:
  "If concerns remain after cycle cap, document them explicitly as open questions in the bead notes."

Post-migration this is intentional and correct: open questions discovered during review are accumulating breadcrumbs and belong in notes. Update this line to make the mechanism explicit:
  "If concerns remain after cycle cap, document them explicitly via `bd update {{bead-id}} --append-notes \"open question: <text>\"`. (Open questions discovered during review are lifecycle accumulation, distinct from the spec body which lives in description.)"

This removes any reading where "in the bead notes" might be misinterpreted post-migration.

### R4. rules/beads.md — update Notes vs Comments table

In `src/plugins/beads/.claude/rules/beads.md`, the "Notes vs Comments" table row for `bd update <id> --notes "..."`:

Current text ("When to use" column): "Initial bead creation, or `brainstorm-bead` spec writes — intentional overwrites only"
→
New text: "Initial bead creation where notes need seeding; intentional full-replacement of accumulated notes (rare — prefer --append-notes)"

After the table, add a callout that mentions ALL THREE flag names individually:

  **Append asymmetry**: `--append-notes` exists; `--append-description`, `--append-design`, and `--append-acceptance` do not — these fields are replace-semantics by design. If you need to accumulate content across formula steps, use `--append-notes`. If the information belongs in description/design/acceptance, restructure so one step owns the complete value.

The exact strings `append-description`, `append-design`, and `append-acceptance` MUST each appear at least once in the file post-edit (each individually verified by AC).

## Out of scope

- Bulk-migrating pre-existing beads that have specs in notes (leave alone; `bd show` displays both fields, consumers are unaffected; manual migration recipes are NOT in scope)
- Changing `--acceptance=` semantics (stays as-is in both steps)
- Changing the brainstorm-bead revise behavior (write-spec owns the field; revise loops back to write-spec to overwrite)
- Adding a guard preamble to the finalize step (explicitly NOT in scope per R2 rationale)
- Updating implement-feature, fix-bug, implement-bead, or run-queue (they consume via `bd show` which displays all fields)
- Changing how `create-bead` populates notes (orthogonal; the create-bead skill writes lifecycle context to notes via --append-notes, which is correct per design intent)

## Design notes

- Downstream consumers (implement-feature, fix-bug, implement-bead) read the spec via `bd show {{bead-id}}` which displays both description and notes. No coordinated downstream changes required.
- Pre-existing beads with specs in notes are not affected operationally — `bd show` still displays them. Mixed-state coexistence is benign and intentional.
- The write-spec guard preamble (added by agents-config-hft) protects against destructive overwrite of prior context. Post-migration the protection applies to the description field; the prior-context inventory changes accordingly (see R1).
- The finalize step is asymmetric on purpose: by the time it runs, write-spec has already written the canonical spec, so there is no "prior investigation context" to protect.
- ralf-spec-review's "open questions to notes" instruction (R3) is now explicitly aligned with the accumulation-vs-spec design: open questions are lifecycle breadcrumbs, --append-notes territory.
- Inline `--description="..."` is valid for the spec lengths brainstorm-bead produces. `--body-file` would require additional shell ceremony inside the formula's triple-quoted TOML description block; not worth it.

## Open questions

None. Scope confirmed in session 0413407c. RALF spec-review cycles 1 (Codex) and 2 (Gemini) raised five MODERATE findings total, all addressed. No third cycle (formula cap = 2).

Meanwhile rules/beads.md says 'specs may be written directly into the bead description'. title: Quote unquoted <path> in git -C invocations across pre-existing formulas
description:
Source: surfaced by Copilot's PR #40 round-2 review (comment 3196388664) on agents-config-ooun. Copilot flagged the unquoted git -C <path> command in docs-only.formula.toml apply-edits step (line 119); fixed in PR #40. The same anti-pattern exists in two PRE-EXISTING locations not in scope for PR #40:

- src/plugins/beads/.beads/formulas/implement-feature.formula.toml line 148
- src/plugins/beads/.beads/formulas/fix-bug.formula.toml line 148

Both were last touched in commit 4a1e4455 (Scott Hamilton, 2026-05-05) — predating PR #40. Per the project's minimal-edits rule, scope-creeping the fix into PR #40 was rejected; tracking here for separate disposition.

Risk: paths with spaces or shell-special characters (e.g. /Users/Scott Hamilton/foo) misparse and either fail the rev-parse check or — worse — validate the wrong directory.

Fix: change

  git -C <path> rev-parse --is-inside-work-tree

to

  git -C "<path>" rev-parse --is-inside-work-tree

in both locations, mirroring the pattern already used by implement-bead/SKILL.md line 51 (which Scott authored correctly the first time).

Provisional ACs:
- [m] git diff at HEAD shows the two lines flipped to quoted form.
- [m] No other unquoted git -C <path> patterns remain in src/plugins/beads/.beads/formulas/*.toml or src/plugins/beads/.agents/skills/*/SKILL.md (verify with: grep -rE 'git -C <path>' src/plugins/beads/).
- [h] None — pure mechanical fix.

Trivial enough that start-bead Route B (inline) is appropriate.

title: Extract repeated formula shell blocks into helper scripts
description:
Source: PR #40 review comment 3196066711 by @scotthamilton77 on src/plugins/beads/.agents/skills/implement-bead/SKILL.md line 42. Scott noted: 'Same with other code blocks in this PR' — the pattern is broader than the one block.

Origin: PR #40 (bead agents-config-ooun) introduces several formula-internal shell blocks that are duplicated across files and call sites. Each agent has to reconstruct the logic at runtime instead of invoking a stable command.

Precedent: the wait-for-pr-comments skill already follows the helper-script convention internally with write-inventory.sh and validate-inventory.sh co-located in the skill directory — invoked via shell from within the skill's prose, not reconstructed by the agent.

Goal: design a convention for extracting reusable formula shell logic into helper scripts that:
- the skill/formula prose references via a stable command line (not by re-pasting source);
- agents invoke without parsing the body;
- have unit-testable contracts (input args, stdin/stdout, exit codes);
- live somewhere agents can discover.

Open design questions (brainstorm before implementing):
- Where should cross-formula helpers live? (Skill-internal works for skill-private logic; formula-shared logic crosses skills — possibly src/plugins/beads/scripts/ or .beads/scripts/.)
- Discovery: how does an agent find the right helper? By name convention? By manifest?
- Contract format: shell-script-with-flags? Or callable bash functions sourced into the formula step?
- Compatibility: existing in-prose blocks need a deprecation path; formulas already in liquid molecules must keep working.

Specific extraction targets (initial set, expand during brainstorm):
- formula-* label collision detector (currently inlined in implement-bead SKILL.md sections 1 and 2, plus brainstorm-bead.formula.toml finalize step)
- [gates].test extractor from project-config.toml (currently inlined in implement-feature/fix-bug/brainstorm-bead — flag-based awk parser)
- parent-chain claim walk (currently inlined in beads.md I1)
- parent-chain close walk (currently inlined in beads.md I2)
- merge-gate-child idempotent creator (currently inlined in reroute step 4 of implement-feature/fix-bug)

Provisional ACs (finalize during brainstorm):
- [m] At least one helper extracted as a working script with tests (the highest-value duplicate).
- [m] Formula prose references the script by command line, not by source paste.
- [m] Convention documented in src/plugins/beads/AGENTS.md (or equivalent location decided during brainstorm).
- [h] Convention is ergonomic enough that a reviewer reading a formula understands what the helper does without opening it (clear command name + brief in-prose summary of contract).

Dependencies: blocked by PR #40 merge (the duplicated blocks land in PR #40 and become extraction targets).

title: Architecture doc §5.3/§5.4/§5.8 cross-cutting tables don't acknowledge docs-only stages
description:
Discovered during ooun review (PR-pending). The new docs-only formula introduces stage names 'apply-edits' and 'review' that the cross-cutting tables in bead-pipeline-architecture.md don't enumerate:

- §5.3 line 509 enumerates allowed stage-role-name values for session-id derivation: preflight, diagnose, red-tests, green-loop, quality-sweep, verify-ac, create-pr, review-cycle, merge-or-handoff. The docs-only stages 'apply-edits' and 'review' are missing — the session-id convention is undefined for them.
- §5.4 line 521-525 cwd table doesn't mention 'apply-edits' or 'review' (though the formula header comments specify cwd: worktree, so the implicit convention covers it).
- §5.8 line 572-582 state-channels table omits docs-only transitions (apply-edits → review, review → verify-ac).

Risk: medium. The formula's apply-edits and review steps will work without §5.3 listing them (UUIDv5 derivation works from any string), but spec drift means future readers can't tell which stage names are sanctioned.

Recommended fix: extend §5.3's enumeration to include apply-edits, review. Add docs-only rows to §5.4 and §5.8 (or a single note that docs-only stages cwd:worktree throughout, mirroring implement-feature except for preflight at repo root).

Source: ooun final quality review M4 finding.

title: Beads 2.0 future proofing: support BD_JSON_ENVELOPE=1

title: Replace generic routing path references (A, B, C, etc.) with functional names in create-bead and implement-bead skills
description:
The create-bead and implement-bead skills (and related documentation like start-bead) use generic routing path nomenclature such as 'Route A', 'Route B', 'Route C', 'Route D' when describing decision trees and workflow branching logic. These references lack semantic meaning and become confusing when cross-referenced or discussed in other contexts.

Replace all generic routing path identifiers with functional names that clearly describe what each path does. Examples of functional naming conventions to consider:
  - 'Route A (brainstorm)' → 'brainstorm-path' or 'spec-needed-path'
  - 'Route B (implement-ready)' → 'implementation-ready-path' or 'ready-to-code-path'
  - 'Route C (trivial)' → 'inline-implementation-path' or 'quick-fix-path'
  - 'Route D (human-flagged)' → 'human-intervention-path' or 'resolution-needed-path'

Files in scope (initial scan):
  - src/plugins/beads/.agents/skills/create-bead/SKILL.md
  - src/plugins/beads/.agents/skills/implement-bead/SKILL.md
  - src/plugins/beads/.agents/skills/start-bead/SKILL.md
  - src/plugins/beads/.agents/skills/resolve-human-bead/SKILL.md (if it references routing)
  - Any AGENTS.md or documentation that cross-references these paths

This is a documentation clarity and maintainability task. The logic and behavior remain unchanged; only the nomenclature is refined for clarity.

title: Refactor install.sh for modularity, testability, and maintainability
description: install.sh has grown into a monolithic script that handles multiple concerns (tool detection, file copying, template rendering, plugin installation). Break it into modular, independently-testable functions. Improve maintainability by separating concerns: detection logic, file operations, template processing, and error handling. Add test coverage for critical paths.

title: Empirical A/B testing of per-stage model/effort allocations against quality/cost outcomes
description:
Empirical A/B testing of the per-stage model/effort defaults (per 7bk.15) to validate cost vs quality outcomes with real bead processing. The F-04 default table shipped with 7bk.15 was set by argument, not data. This bead defines a methodology to gather data so future tuning is data-driven.

## Why

Several decisions in the F-04 table are calibration-by-intuition:
- preflight at sonnet-high (vs sonnet-medium): is the "architectural gate" framing right or paranoid?
- green-loop controller at opus-medium (vs opus-high): is medium effort enough for synthesis decisions?
- four haiku stages (quality-sweep, verify-ac, create-pr, merge-or-handoff): do they break down on edge cases, or stay mechanical?
- review-cycle at sonnet-medium: does it cope with high Copilot comment volume?
- diagnose (bug-class) at sonnet-high: is the cost justified by quality lift?
- red-tests Codex at gpt-5.5 vs gpt-5.4-mini: cost-vs-quality call (per TR5 reconsideration in 7bk.15)

Without empirical data, every adjustment to the table is argument-based; we may be silently sacrificing quality/reliability for cost or vice versa.

## Methodology sketch (refine at brainstorm)

1. Pick 5-10 representative beads from history (small/medium/large scope; feature/bug/chore mix; varying AC complexity).
2. For each bead, process through the pipeline N times with different stage-defaults variants:
   - Variant A: F-04 baseline (per 7bk.15 amendment 2026-05-04)
   - Variant B: cheaper (downgrade preflight + diagnose to sonnet-medium; opus-medium → sonnet-high in green-loop)
   - Variant C: expensive (upgrade four haiku stages to sonnet-medium)
3. Capture per-run metrics:
   - Total token cost (Anthropic + foreign-CLI)
   - Wall-time per stage and total
   - # of RALF iterations to converge
   - # of Copilot comments (FIX-class)
   - # of human escapes (flag-human invocations)
   - # of reverts/regressions in subsequent commits
4. Quality scoring: human (or independent reviewer agent) reviews each variant's PR, scores 1-5 on:
   - (a) test correctness
   - (b) implementation quality
   - (c) AC coverage
   - (d) idiomatic / non-hacky
5. Output: a calibration report with per-stage recommendations to amend the F-04 table.

## Dependencies

- agents-config-7bk.9 (per-stage architecture functional)
- agents-config-7bk.14 (Model:/Effort: directives mechanism shipped — required to vary defaults per run)
- agents-config-7bk.15 (initial defaults applied as baseline)

## Out of scope

- Changing the F-04 table without supporting data from this exercise.
- Building automated A/B harness in v1 (manual comparison runs are acceptable for first pass).
- Cross-project generalization (this is project-specific calibration).

## Acceptance Criteria

- A/B test methodology documented and approved
- Test fixture beads selected (5-10 representative)
- Comparison runs executed for at least 3 variants
- Quality scoring rubric agreed and applied
- Calibration report produced with specific recommendations to amend the F-04 table (or confirm it as-is)
- Build passes. Typecheck passes. Tests pass.
